spanish_meaning_en: The armpit is below the junction of the arm and torso.
spanish_example_en: I put deodorant in my armpit.

spanish_meaning: La espalda es la parte posterior del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Me duele la espalda.
spanish_meaning_en: The back is the back part of the body.
spanish_example_en: My back hurts.

spanish_meaning: La cadera une la pelvis con la pierna.
spanish_example: Mi cadera cruje al caminar.
spanish_meaning_en: The hip connects the pelvis to the leg.
spanish_example_en: My hip creaks when I walk.

spanish_meaning: La parte inferior es la zona más baja o trasera.
spanish_example: Se sentó en el fondo de la silla.
spanish_meaning_en: The bottom is the lowest or rear area.
spanish_example_en: He sat at the bottom of the chair.

spanish_meaning: El talón es la parte trasera del pie.
spanish_example: Me golpeé el talón en la mesa.
spanish_meaning_en: The heel is the back part of the foot.
spanish_example_en: I hit my heel on the table.

spanish_meaning: El tobillo une pie y pierna.
spanish_example: Me torcí el tobillo.
spanish_meaning_en: The ankle joins the foot and leg.
spanish_example_en: I sprained my ankle.

spanish_meaning: La pantorrilla es la parte trasera de la pierna.
spanish_example: Me dolió la pantorrilla.
spanish_meaning_en: The calf is the back part of the leg.
spanish_example_en: My calf hurt.

spanish_meaning: La uña de la mano cubre y protege la punta de los dedos.
spanish_example: Tengo la uña de la mano rota.
spanish_meaning_en: The fingernail covers and protects the tips of the fingers.
spanish_example_en: I have a broken fingernail.

spanish_meaning: El nudillo es la articulación visible de los dedos.
spanish_example: Me dolía el nudillo.
spanish_meaning_en: The knuckle is the visible joint of the fingers.
spanish_example_en: My knuckle hurt.

spanish_meaning: La cintura es la parte media entre pecho y caderas.
spanish_example: La cintura se me ensanchó.
spanish_meaning_en: The waist is the middle part between the chest and hips.
spanish_example_en: My waist widened.

spanish_meaning: El antebrazo está entre muñeca y codo.
spanish_example: Me apreté el antebrazo del perro.
spanish_meaning_en: The forearm is between the wrist and the elbow.
spanish_example_en: I squeezed the dog's forearm.

spanish_meaning: El codo es la articulación que une brazo y antebrazo.
spanish_example: Me dolía el codo al moverlo.
spanish_meaning_en: The elbow is the joint that joins the arm and forearm.
spanish_example_en: My elbow hurt when I moved it.

spanish_meaning: El brazo superior es la parte alta del brazo.
spanish_example: Entreno el brazo superior.
spanish_meaning_en: The upper arm is the upper part of the arm.
spanish_example_en: I train the upper arm.

spanish_meaning: El hombro une el brazo con el tórax.
spanish_example: Me pesa la mochila sobre el hombro.
spanish_meaning_en: The shoulder joins the arm to the thorax.
spanish_example_en: My backpack weighs on my shoulder.

spanish_meaning: Hermano significa varón de la familia o mismo padre/madre.
spanish_example: Mi hermano estudia música.
spanish_meaning_en: Brother means male of the family or same father/mother.
spanish_example_en: My brother studies music.

spanish_meaning: Hermana es la persona femenina con la misma familia.
spanish_example: Mi hermana cocina bien.
spanish_meaning_en: Sister is the female person with the same family.
spanish_example_en: My sister cooks well.

spanish_meaning: Padre es quien engendra o cría a un hijo.
spanish_example: Mi padre llega tarde.
spanish_meaning_en: Father is one who begets or raises a child.
spanish_example_en: My father is late.

spanish_meaning: Madre es la persona que da vida y cuida a su hijo.
spanish_example: Mi madre prepara café.
spanish_meaning_en: Mother is the person who gives life and cares for her child.
spanish_example_en: My mother makes coffee.

spanish_meaning: El padrastro es el marido de tu madre sin parentesco biológico.
spanish_example: Mi padrastro me llevó al cine.
notes: Ambigüedad: depende de si tu madre se volvió a casar.
spanish_meaning_en: The stepfather is your mother's husband without biological relationship.
spanish_example_en: My stepfather took me to the movies.

spanish_meaning: Madrastra es la esposa de tu padre sin ser tu madre biológica.
spanish_example: La madrastra me llevó al médico.
spanish_meaning_en: Stepmother is your father's wife without being your biological mother.
spanish_example_en: The stepmother took me to the doctor.

spanish_meaning: Padres son la mamá y el papá de una persona.
spanish_example: Mis padres viven en Madrid.
spanish_meaning_en: Parents are a person's mom and dad.
spanish_example_en: My parents live in Madrid.

spanish_meaning: Abuelo es el padre del padre o de la madre.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo cuenta buenas historias.
spanish_meaning_en: Grandfather is the father of the father or mother.
spanish_example_en: My grandfather tells good stories.

spanish_meaning: Abuela es la madre del padre o de la madre.
spanish_example: La abuela vive cerca.
spanish_meaning_en: Grandmother is the mother of the father or mother.
spanish_example_en: Grandma lives nearby.

spanish_meaning: Abuelos son el abuelo y la abuela de alguien.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos nos visitan los domingos.
spanish_meaning_en: Grandparents are someone's grandfather and grandmother.
spanish_example_en: My grandparents visit us on Sundays.

spanish_meaning: Tío es el hermano de tu padre o madre.
spanish_example: Mi tío vive en otra ciudad.
spanish_meaning_en: Uncle is your father or mother's brother.
spanish_example_en: My uncle lives in another city.

spanish_meaning: La tía es la hermana de tu padre o de tu madre.
spanish_example: Mi tía vive lejos.
spanish_meaning_en: The aunt is the sister of your father or mother.
spanish_example_en: My aunt lives far away.

spanish_meaning: Primo o prima es el hijo de los hermanos de tus padres.
spanish_example: Mi primo vino ayer.
spanish_meaning_en: Cousin or cousin is the child of your parents' siblings.
spanish_example_en: My cousin came yesterday.

spanish_meaning: La sobrina de los padres es un primo o prima para ti.
spanish_example: La sobrina de mis padres vive conmigo.
notes: La fórmula es más literal y ambigua.
spanish_meaning_en: The parents' niece is a cousin for you.
spanish_example_en: My parents' niece lives with me.

spanish_meaning: Primo o prima es el hijo de los hermanos de tus padres.
spanish_example: Mi primo vino ayer.
spanish_meaning_en: Cousin or cousin is the child of your parents' siblings.
spanish_example_en: My cousin came yesterday.

spanish_meaning: El sobrino de los padres también se considera primo o prima.
spanish_example: Su sobrino de mis padres estudia ingeniería.
notes: La fórmula es más literal y ambigua.
spanish_meaning_en: The parents' nephew is also considered a cousin.
spanish_example_en: My parents' nephew studies engineering.

spanish_meaning: Rojo es un color de la rueda cromática.
spanish_example: La manzana es roja.
spanish_meaning_en: Red is a color of the color wheel.
spanish_example_en: The apple is red.

spanish_meaning: Rosa es un color claro entre rojo y blanco.
spanish_example: Me gusta el vestido rosa.
spanish_meaning_en: Pink is a light color between red and white.
spanish_example_en: I like the pink dress.

spanish_meaning: Verde es el color de la naturaleza.
spanish_example: La hierba es verde.
spanish_meaning_en: Green is the color of nature.
spanish_example_en: The grass is green.

spanish_meaning: Verde lima es un tono de verde brillante.
spanish_example: Quiero una camisa verde lima.
spanish_meaning_en: Lime green is a bright shade of green.
spanish_example_en: I want a lime green shirt.

spanish_meaning: Verde azulado es un color entre verde y azul.
spanish_example: El sofá es verde azulado.
spanish_meaning_en: Teal is a color between green and blue.
spanish_example_en: The sofa is teal.

spanish_meaning: Azul es un color del cielo y del mar.
spanish_example: El cielo está azul.
spanish_meaning_en: Blue is a color of the sky and the sea.
spanish_example_en: The sky is blue.

spanish_meaning: Azul marino es un azul muy oscuro.
spanish_example: Llevaba un abrigo azul marino.
spanish_meaning_en: Navy blue is a very dark blue.
spanish_example_en: He was wearing a navy blue coat.

spanish_meaning: Amarillo es el color del sol.
spanish_example: El plátano está amarillo.
spanish_meaning_en: Yellow is the color of the sun.
spanish_example_en: The banana is yellow.

spanish_meaning: Morado es un color entre azul y rojo.
spanish_example: La flor es morada.
spanish_meaning_en: Purple is a color between blue and red.
spanish_example_en: The flower is purple.

spanish_meaning: Marrón es un color parecido al de la tierra.
spanish_example: Sus botas son marrones.
spanish_meaning_en: Brown is a color similar to that of the earth.
spanish_example_en: His boots are brown.

spanish_meaning: Beige es un color crema-grisáceo neutro.
spanish_example: El sofá es color beige.
spanish_meaning_en: Beige is a neutral grayish-cream color.
spanish_example_en: The sofa is beige.

spanish_meaning: Naranja es el color entre rojo y amarillo.
spanish_example: La pelota es naranja.
spanish_meaning_en: Orange is the color between red and yellow.
spanish_example_en: The ball is orange.

spanish_meaning: Blanco es el color de la luz sin tinte.
spanish_example: La nieve es blanca.
spanish_meaning_en: White is the color of light without tint.
spanish_example_en: The snow is white.

spanish_meaning: Negro es la ausencia de color visible.
spanish_example: El auto es negro.
spanish_meaning_en: Black is the absence of visible color.
spanish_example_en: The car is black.

spanish_meaning: Gris es un color entre negro y blanco.
spanish_example: El cielo está gris.
spanish_meaning_en: Gray is a color between black and white.
spanish_example_en: The sky is gray.

english_meaning: To agree is to have the same opinion or belief as another person.
english_example: The students agree they have too much homework.
spanish_meaning: Estar de acuerdo es compartir la misma opinión o creencia.
spanish_example: Estoy de acuerdo contigo.

english_meaning: Alcohol is a type of drink that can make people drunk.
english_example: A person should not drive a car after he or she has been drinking alcohol.
spanish_meaning: El alcohol es una bebida fermentada o destilada que puede embriagar.
spanish_example: No conduzcas si bebes alcohol.

english_meaning: To arrive is to get somewhere.
english_example: They arrived at school at 7 a.m.
spanish_meaning: Llegar significa alcanzar un lugar.
spanish_example: Ella llegó temprano a casa.

english_meaning: August is the eighth month of the year.
english_example: Is your birthday in August?
spanish_meaning: Agosto es el octavo mes del año.
spanish_example: Mi cumpleaños es en agosto.

english_meaning: A boat is a vehicle that moves across water.
english_example: There is a small boat on the lake.
spanish_meaning: Un barco es un vehículo que navega sobre el agua.
spanish_example: El barco cruza el lago.

english_meaning: Breakfast is the morning meal.
english_example: I ate eggs for breakfast.
spanish_meaning: El desayuno es la comida de la mañana.
spanish_example: Tomé café en el desayuno.

english_meaning: A camera is a piece of equipment that takes pictures.
english_example: I brought my camera on my vacation.
spanish_meaning: La cámara es un equipo para tomar fotografías.
spanish_example: Traje mi cámara al viaje.

english_meaning: A capital is a city where a country’s government is based.
english_example: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.
spanish_meaning: La capital es la ciudad principal de un país.
spanish_example: La capital de Francia es París.

english_meaning: To catch is to grab or get something.
english_example: Did you catch the ball during the baseball game?
spanish_meaning: Atrapar es coger algo que se mueve.
spanish_example: ¿Atrapaste la pelota?

english_meaning: A duck is a small water bird.
english_example: People feed ducks at the lake.
spanish_meaning: El pato es un ave acuática pequeña.
spanish_example: Alimenté al pato en el lago.

english_meaning: To enjoy is to like something.
english_example: The woman enjoys riding her bicycle.
spanish_meaning: Disfrutar es tener placer en algo.
spanish_example: Disfruto caminar por la tarde.

english_meaning: To invite is to ask someone to come to a place or event.
english_example: I will invite my friends to my birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Invitar es pedir a alguien que asista a algo.
spanish_example: Invitaré a mis amigos a cenar.

english_meaning: To love is to like something or someone a lot.
english_example: I love my family very much.
spanish_meaning: Amar es sentir afecto intenso.
spanish_example: Amo a mi familia.

english_meaning: A month is one of 12 periods of time in one year.
english_example: January is the first month of the year.
spanish_meaning: El mes es una división de tiempo de aproximadamente 30 días.
spanish_example: Enero es el primer mes del año.

english_meaning: To travel is to go to a faraway place on vacation or business.
english_example: They will travel to Argentina this summer.
spanish_meaning: Viajar es ir a otro lugar por ocio o trabajo.
spanish_example: Viajamos al sur en verano.

english_meaning: If something is typical, it is normal, or something that usually happens.
english_example: My typical breakfast is toast and eggs.
spanish_meaning: Típico significa normal o frecuente.
spanish_example: Mi desayuno típico es café y pan.

english_meaning: To visit is to go and spend time in another place or see another person.
english_example: She wants to visit her grandmother.
spanish_meaning: Visitar es ir a ver a alguien o un lugar.
spanish_example: Voy a visitar a mi abuela.

english_meaning: Weather is the temperature and the state of the outdoors.
english_example: Today’s weather is rainy and cloudy.
spanish_meaning: El clima describe temperatura y condiciones del aire.
spanish_example: Hoy el clima está frío y nublado.

english_meaning: A week is a period of time that is seven days long.
english_example: What are you doing next week?
spanish_meaning: La semana dura siete días.
spanish_example: ¿Qué vas a hacer la próxima semana?

english_meaning: Wine is an alcoholic drink made from grapes.
english_example: The store carried both red and white wine.
spanish_meaning: El vino es una bebida alcohólica de uvas fermentadas.
spanish_example: Compré una botella de vino.

english_meaning: An adventure is a fun or exciting thing that you do.
english_example: Riding in the rough water was an adventure.
spanish_meaning: Aventura es una experiencia emocionante.
spanish_example: La excursión fue una gran aventura.

english_meaning: To approach something means to move close to it.
english_example: The boy approached his school.
spanish_meaning: Acercarse es moverse hacia algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Ella se acercó al escenario.

english_meaning: Carefully means with great attention, especially to detail or safety.
english_example: The baby carefully climbed down the stairs.
spanish_meaning: Cuidadosamente significa con mucha atención.
spanish_example: La niña subió las escaleras cuidadosamente.

english_meaning: A chemical is something that scientists use in chemistry.
english_example: The scientist mixed the chemicals.
spanish_meaning: Un químico es una sustancia usada en procesos químicos.
spanish_example: El laboratorio usó varios químicos.

english_meaning: To create means to make something new.
english_example: She created an igloo from blocks of snow.
spanish_meaning: Crear es producir algo que no existía antes.
spanish_example: Ella creó un juego nuevo.

english_meaning: Evil describes something or someone bad or cruel, not good.
english_example: They felt a strange, evil presence as they got closer to the house.
spanish_meaning: Malvado describe algo cruel o dañino.
spanish_example: Sus actos fueron malvados.

english_meaning: An experiment is a test that you do to see what will happen.
english_example: The student did an experiment in science class.
spanish_meaning: Un experimento prueba una idea o hipótesis.
spanish_example: Hicimos un experimento en clase.

english_meaning: To kill someone or something is to make them die.
english_example: I killed the fly.
spanish_meaning: Matar es causar la muerte a un ser vivo.
spanish_example: Matar insectos no siempre es lo mejor.

english_meaning: A laboratory is a room where a scientist works.
english_example: My mother works in a laboratory.
spanish_meaning: El laboratorio es un lugar para hacer pruebas científicas.
spanish_example: La química se hace en el laboratorio.

english_meaning: Laugh is the sound made when someone is happy or a funny thing occurs.
english_example: The sound of her laugh filled the room.
spanish_meaning: Reír es expresar alegría con sonidos de voz.
spanish_example: Ella ríe cuando ve una broma.

english_meaning: If a sound is loud, it is strong and very easy to hear.
english_example: The man’s voice was so loud that we all could hear him.
spanish_meaning: Fuerte es lo que se oye con mucha intensidad.
spanish_example: El audio suena fuerte.

english_meaning: When a person is nervous, they think something bad will happen.
english_example: The boy became nervous when he heard the news.
spanish_meaning: Nervioso describe a alguien inquieto o con ansiedad.
spanish_example: Estoy nervioso antes del examen.

english_meaning: A noise is an unpleasant sound.
english_example: The crying baby made a loud noise.
spanish_meaning: Ruido es un sonido, a veces molesto.
spanish_example: El ruido del motor despierta al bebé.

english_meaning: A project is a type of work that you do for school or a job.
english_example: His afternoon work project was to paint the room green.
spanish_meaning: Un proyecto es una tarea con objetivo y tiempo.
spanish_example: Presenté mi proyecto hoy.

english_meaning: To scare someone is to make them feel afraid.
english_example: My uncle was scared by what he saw in the room.
spanish_meaning: Asustar es provocar miedo en alguien.
spanish_example: El ruido me asustó.

english_meaning: A secret is something that you do not tell other people.
english_example: The two boys were sharing a secret.
spanish_meaning: Un secreto es algo que se guarda en silencio.
spanish_example: Guardamos el secreto entre dos.

english_meaning: To shout is to say something loudly.
english_example: My boss shouted at me because I was late for work.
spanish_meaning: Gritar es hablar muy fuerte.
spanish_example: Gritó por ayuda.

english_meaning: To smell something means to use your nose to sense it.
english_example: The two friends smelled the flower.
spanish_meaning: Oler es detectar un olor con la nariz.
spanish_example: La niña olió la flor.

english_meaning: If something is terrible, it is very bad.
english_example: The way he treated his classmate was terrible.
spanish_meaning: Terrible significa muy malo o grave.
spanish_example: La noticia fue terrible para todos.

english_meaning: If something is worse, it is of poorer quality than another thing.
english_example: Business was worse this month than last month.
spanish_meaning: Peor es una calidad o situación más mala.
spanish_example: Este mes fue peor que el anterior.

english_meaning: An alien is a creature from a different world.
english_example: The alien came in peace.
spanish_meaning: Un extraterrestre viene de otro mundo.
spanish_example: El extraterrestre llegó en silencio.

english_meaning: If you are among certain things, they are all around you.
english_example: There was a red apple among the green ones.
spanish_meaning: Entre significa dentro de un grupo o rodeado por.
spanish_example: Hay una manzana entre las frutas.

english_meaning: A chart is a list of information.
english_example: We used a chart to see how we had improved.
spanish_meaning: Un gráfico organiza información visualmente.
spanish_example: Mira el gráfico de ventas.

english_meaning: A cloud is a group of water drops in the sky.
english_example: The sky was filled with white clouds.
spanish_meaning: La nube es una masa de vapor en el cielo.
spanish_example: Las nubes cubrían el cielo.

english_meaning: To describe is to say or write what someone or something is like.
english_example: They described their tree as colorful, with gold ribbon and a star.
spanish_meaning: Describir es decir cómo es algo.
spanish_example: La niña describió su casa.

english_meaning: Ever means at any time.
english_example: Going skiing last winter was the most fun I’ve ever had.
spanish_meaning: Alguna vez indica "en algún momento".
spanish_example: Ir a esquiar el invierno pasado fue lo más divertido que he hecho alguna vez.

english_meaning: To fail means you do not succeed in what you try to do.
english_example: Since he failed to get the job, he was sad.
spanish_meaning: Fallar es no lograr lo que se intenta.
spanish_example: Fallé el examen.

english_meaning: A grade is a score or mark given to someone’s work.
english_example: I managed to get good grades on my report card.
spanish_meaning: La calificación es la nota de una evaluación.
spanish_example: Saqué buena calificación.

english_meaning: Instead means in place of.
english_example: He ate the carrot instead of the ice cream.
spanish_meaning: En lugar de significa sustituyendo algo por otra cosa.
spanish_example: Tomó té en lugar de café.

english_meaning: A library is a place where you go to read books.
english_example: The library at school is full of books.
spanish_meaning: La biblioteca es un lugar para leer y prestar libros.
spanish_example: La biblioteca abre a las nueve.

english_meaning: I like taking photographs.
english_example: I took this photograph with my cell phone.
spanish_meaning: Una fotografía es una imagen fija tomada con una cámara.
spanish_example: Tomé una fotografía del atardecer.

english_meaning: A planet is a large round thing in space.
english_example: Saturn is the planet with the ring around it.
spanish_meaning: Un planeta es un cuerpo celeste que gira alrededor de una estrella.
spanish_example: Marte es un planeta.

english_meaning: A report is something someone writes for school or work.
english_example: Karen had trouble writing her report.
spanish_meaning: Un informe describe información o resultados.
spanish_example: Entregué el informe de hoy.

english_meaning: Several is more than two but not many.
english_example: He had to read several books for class.
spanish_meaning: Varios significa más de dos.
spanish_example: Leí varios libros.

english_meaning: An object’s shape is the arrangement of its sides and surfaces.
english_example: Even with your eyes closed you can feel the shape of it.
spanish_meaning: La forma es el contorno o aspecto de algo.
spanish_example: La forma del vaso es redonda.

english_meaning: To solve something is to find an answer to it.
english_example: All the students could easily solve the math problem.
spanish_meaning: Resolver es encontrar la respuesta correcta.
spanish_example: Resolver el problema fue fácil.

english_meaning: If something happens suddenly, it happens quickly and unexpectedly.
english_example: I was surprised when my friends suddenly shouted, “Happy birthday!”
spanish_meaning: De repente significa inesperadamente y sin aviso.
spanish_example: De pronto cambió el plan.

english_meaning: To suppose is to guess.
english_example: I suppose I should go home now.
spanish_meaning: Suponer es adivinar o pensar probable.
spanish_example: Supongo que lloverá.

english_meaning: To understand something you need to know what it means.
english_example: Henry could not understand the message.
spanish_meaning: Comprender es entender el sentido de algo.
spanish_example: Comprendo el mensaje.

english_meaning: To view is to look at something.
english_example: Michael likes to view himself in the mirror.
spanish_meaning: Ver es mirar con la vista.
spanish_example: Me gusta ver documentales.

english_meaning: When a thing is appropriate, it is right or normal.
english_example: It’s appropriate to wear a suit when you go to the office.
spanish_meaning: Apropiado es lo que conviene para una situación.
spanish_example: Ese gesto fue apropiado.

english_meaning: To avoid something is to stay away from it.
english_example: Avoid the broken bottle on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Evitar es mantenerse alejado de algo.
spanish_example: Evita la botella rota.

english_meaning: To behave is to act in a particular way, especially to be good.
english_example: She always behaves well when her father is around.
spanish_meaning: Comportarse es actuar de cierta forma.
spanish_example: Ella se comportó bien en clase.

english_meaning: When someone is calm, they do not get excited or upset.
english_example: A nice warm bath makes me feel so calm.
spanish_meaning: Tranquilo significa sin nervios ni agitación.
spanish_example: Ahora estoy tranquilo.

english_meaning: Concern is a feeling of worry.
english_example: I was filled with concern after reading the newspaper.
spanish_meaning: Preocupación es sentir inquietud por algo.
spanish_example: Tengo preocupación por mi perro.

english_meaning: To be content is to be happy and not want more.
english_example: The baby looked very content sitting on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Satisfecho significa contento o tranquilo.
spanish_example: Estoy satisfecho con mi desayuno.

english_meaning: If you expect something to happen, you believe it will happen.
english_example: I expect the bus to be here very soon.
spanish_meaning: Esperar es prever que algo ocurrirá.
spanish_example: Espero que lleguen pronto.

english_meaning: When something happens frequently, it happens often.
english_example: We meet frequently, either at the beginning or end of the week.
spanish_meaning: Frecuentemente significa muchas veces.
spanish_example: Nos vemos frecuentemente.

english_meaning: A habit is a thing that you do often.
english_example: Smoking is a bad habit that can kill you.
spanish_meaning: Un hábito es una conducta repetida.
spanish_example: Fumar es un mal hábito.

english_meaning: To instruct is to teach.
english_example: My teacher instructs us in several subjects.
spanish_meaning: Instruir es enseñar de forma clara.
spanish_example: El profesor me instruyó hoy.

english_meaning: An issue is an important topic.
english_example: The men spoke about issues that were important to the people.
spanish_meaning: Un problema es un tema importante que requiere solución.
spanish_example: Hablaron del problema del agua.

english_meaning: None means not any of something.
english_example: He spent all his money. There is none left.
spanish_meaning: Ninguno significa ausencia total de algo.
spanish_example: No queda ninguno.

english_meaning: If people are patient, they don’t become angry or upset easily.
english_example: I had to be patient and wait until 5 o’clock to leave.
spanish_meaning: Paciente describe calma y tolerancia.
spanish_example: Ella fue paciente en la fila.

english_meaning: If something is positive, it is good.
english_example: She has a positive future ahead of her after finishing college.
spanish_meaning: Positivo significa favorable o optimista.
spanish_example: Pensamos con visión positiva.

english_meaning: To punish means to make someone suffer for breaking the rules or laws.
english_example: To punish me, my teacher had me stand in the corner.
spanish_meaning: Castigar es aplicar una sanción por una falta.
spanish_example: Me castigaron por llegar tarde.

english_meaning: To represent is to speak or act for a person or group.
english_example: My lawyer will represent me in court.
spanish_meaning: Representar es hablar en nombre de alguien.
spanish_example: El abogado me representa en juicio.

english_meaning: To shake is to move back and forth or up and down quickly.
english_example: When people shake hands, it usually means they agree.
spanish_meaning: Agitar es mover algo o alguien con movimientos leves o rápidos.
spanish_example: Ella agitó la mano al despedirse.

english_meaning: To spread is to move out to cover a larger area.
english_example: I like to spread butter on my toast.
spanish_meaning: Extender es repartir algo sobre una superficie.
spanish_example: Extendí mantequilla en el pan.

english_meaning: To stroll means to walk slowly and calmly.
english_example: My dog and I strolled through the park today.
spanish_meaning: Pasear es caminar sin prisa.
spanish_example: Paseamos por el parque.

english_meaning: A village is a very small town.
english_example: There are only a few houses in my village.
spanish_meaning: Un pueblo es una localidad pequeña.
spanish_example: Vivimos en un pueblo.

english_meaning: When someone is active, they move a lot or have a lot of things to do.
english_example: It is important to be active and not sit around all the time.
spanish_meaning: Activo significa en movimiento o participando.
spanish_example: Haz ejercicio para estar activo.

english_meaning: An adult is a person who is more than 18 years old.
english_example: My mom and dad are adults.
spanish_meaning: Adulto es una persona mayor de edad.
spanish_example: Es adulto y puede conducir.

english_meaning: Age is how many years someone has lived.
english_example: She died at the age of 80.
spanish_meaning: La edad es el número de años vividos.
spanish_example: Mi edad es treinta.

english_meaning: When something is bad, it is not good.
english_example: She is unhappy because she had a bad day.
spanish_meaning: Malo significa de mala calidad o desagradable.
spanish_example: Hoy es un día malo.

english_meaning: Balance is when two or more things are equal.
english_example: A good balance between work and fun helps keep you healthy.
spanish_meaning: El equilibrio es la proporción o estabilidad entre partes.
spanish_example: Mantén equilibrio en tu vida.

english_meaning: A bike is a vehicle with two wheels powered by a human.
english_example: He rides his bike to school every day.
spanish_meaning: La bicicleta se mueve por pedaleo humano.
spanish_example: Voy en bicicleta al trabajo.

english_meaning: To choose is to pick something or make a decision.
english_example: I have to choose between taking art classes and sports lessons.
spanish_meaning: Elegir es seleccionar una opción.
spanish_example: Elijo café o té.

english_meaning: A doctor is a person who studies medicine and helps sick people.
english_example: You should go to the doctor when you are sick.
spanish_meaning: El médico atiende personas enfermas.
spanish_example: Voy al médico mañana.

english_meaning: During an event means while the event was happening.
english_example: Did you sleep during the movie?
spanish_meaning: Durante indica que algo pasa en el tiempo de otra acción.
spanish_example: Dormimos durante la película.

english_meaning: Football is a sport with eleven members and an oval-shaped ball.
english_example: Football is a popular sport in the United States.
spanish_meaning: El fútbol es un deporte de equipo con balón.
spanish_example: Jugamos fútbol los domingos.

english_meaning: When something is fun, it is enjoyable.
english_example: We had a fun time at the birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Divertido significa que produce placer.
spanish_example: La fiesta fue divertida.

english_meaning: A game is an activity where people compete against each other.
english_example: Let’s play a board game tonight.
spanish_meaning: Un juego es una actividad con reglas y participación.
spanish_example: Jugamos un juego de mesa.

english_meaning: A heart is an organ that keeps the body alive.
english_example: My heart beats fast when I am nervous.
spanish_meaning: El corazón bombea la sangre por el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Mi corazón late rápido cuando estoy nervioso.

english_meaning: Golf is a sport with clubs and a small white ball.
english_example: People play golf in nice weather.
spanish_meaning: El golf es un deporte con palos y una pelota pequeña.
spanish_example: Jugamos golf los fines de semana.

english_meaning: To increase is to make something larger.
english_example: I will increase my score if I study for the test.
spanish_meaning: Aumentar es hacer crecer una cantidad o intensidad.
spanish_example: Aumentaré la velocidad.

english_meaning: Life is the time when a person is alive.
english_example: My grandfather had a long life.
spanish_meaning: Vida es el tiempo de existir de los seres vivos.
spanish_example: La vida puede cambiar de un día a otro.

english_meaning: A kilometer is a unit of measurement that is 1,000 meters.
english_example: A marathon is 42.2 kilometers.
spanish_meaning: Un kilómetro es una medida de distancia igual a mil metros.
spanish_example: Un maratón mide 42,2 kilómetros.

english_meaning: Often is when something happens many times.
english_example: He often goes to bed early during the week.
spanish_meaning: A menudo significa repetido o frecuente.
spanish_example: A menudo leo por la noche.

english_meaning: If you have plenty of something, there is a lot of it.
english_example: We have plenty of fruit, so help yourself.
spanish_meaning: Mucho indica una cantidad abundante.
spanish_example: Tengo mucha fruta en casa.

english_meaning: Weight is how heavy something or someone is.
english_example: I gained weight because I ate a lot of pizza.
spanish_meaning: Peso es la medida de la fuerza de gravedad sobre un cuerpo.
spanish_example: Subió mi peso está semana.

english_meaning: When people or things are apart, they are not next to each other.
english_example: They moved apart and then came back together.
spanish_meaning: Apart significa distantes o no juntos.
spanish_example: Se sentaron separados en la fila.

english_meaning: An attribute is a characteristic of a person or thing.
english_example: He isn’t very clever, but he does have some other positive attributes.
spanish_meaning: Atributo es una cualidad o característica.
spanish_example: Su mayor atributo es la paciencia.

english_meaning: A bilingual person can speak two languages.
english_example: Since you already know English, after learning French you’ll be bilingual.
spanish_meaning: Bilíngüe describe a alguien que habla dos idiomas.
spanish_example: Mi hermana es bilíngüe.

english_meaning: Something completely different is very, very different from before.
english_example: I was completely wrong.
spanish_meaning: Totalmente significa de forma absoluta.
spanish_example: Estoy totalmente seguro.

english_meaning: To dash means to run or move quickly.
english_example: Helen dashed up the stairs so she wouldn’t be late for her appointment.
spanish_meaning: Correr es moverse con rapidez usando piernas.
spanish_example: Corrió hacia la salida.

english_meaning: Disgust is a feeling of distaste and anger caused by something rude or unpleasant.
english_example: He felt disgust toward his date because she had such terrible eating habits.
spanish_meaning: Asco es una sensación de rechazo.
spanish_example: Sentí asco al ver eso.

english_meaning: Something fashionable is what people like to wear and do now.
english_example: It is very fashionable to wear a hat.
spanish_meaning: Moderno o de moda es lo que hoy se considera popular.
spanish_example: Ese estilo es muy moderno.

english_meaning: Something foreign is something we are not used to.
english_example: He has travelled widely in foreign countries.
spanish_meaning: Extranjero significa de otro país o no habitual aquí.
spanish_example: Probé comida extranjera ayer.

english_meaning: A gulf is a gap between people who do not understand each other.
english_example: There has been a gulf between James and Tony since their parents died.
spanish_meaning: Brecha es una separación grande en opinión o relación.
spanish_example: Hay una brecha entre ellos.

english_meaning: A mirror is usually made of glass, and you can see yourself in it.
english_example: He used the mirror to shine light in their eyes.
spanish_meaning: El espejo refleja la imagen de personas y objetos.
spanish_example: Usó el espejo para dirigir luz hacia sus ojos.

english_meaning: Things that are natural are not made by people.
english_example: We need to take care of the natural world.
spanish_meaning: Natural significa que no está hecho por humanos.
spanish_example: El paisaje natural está protegido.

english_meaning: Nowadays means at the present time.
english_example: Nowadays, not so many people smoke.
spanish_meaning: Hoy en día significa en la época actual.
spanish_example: Hoy en día casi no fumo.

english_meaning: A participant is someone who joins in a social event or competition.
english_example: There were thousands of participants in this year’s marathon.
spanish_meaning: Participante es quien toma parte en algo.
spanish_example: Hay muchos participantes hoy.

english_meaning: A ritual is a formal custom that people do regularly.
english_example: Ken was very interested to learn about the religious rituals of the natives.
spanish_meaning: Un ritual es una práctica repetida con significado simbólico.
spanish_example: Ken tenía mucho interés en aprender sobre los rituales religiosos de los nativos.

english_meaning: Spoken language is what we say.
english_example: We cannot take back what has been spoken.
spanish_meaning: Hablado es el lenguaje que se expresa con voz.
spanish_example: Hablado y escrito son formas distintas.

english_meaning: Sport involves games like running, jumping, football, and tennis.
english_example: She loves watching sport on TV.
spanish_meaning: Deporte es una actividad física reglada.
spanish_example: Me gusta ver deporte en casa.

english_meaning: You are surprised when something unexpected happens.
english_example: He didn’t appear at all surprised when he read the article.
spanish_meaning: Sorprendido es mostrar asombro inesperado.
spanish_example: Me sorprendió su regalo.

english_meaning: When you are tense, you are not comfortable and feel unhappy.
english_example: I felt very tense waiting to hear the result of the vote.
spanish_meaning: Tenso significa preocupado y con mucha tensión.
spanish_example: Estoy tenso antes del examen.

english_meaning: If you totally agree, you agree with everything just said.
english_example: I am totally against that.
spanish_meaning: Totalmente significa por completo.
spanish_example: Estoy totalmente de acuerdo.

english_meaning: If something is vague, it is not clear and gives very few details.
english_example: I asked him about his mother’s health, but he was very vague about it.
spanish_meaning: Vago significa impreciso o poco claro.
spanish_example: Habló de forma vaga.

english_meaning: To allow something to happen means to let it happen.
english_example: Having a ticket will allow you to enter the show.
spanish_meaning: Permitir es dejar que algo ocurra.
spanish_example: El permiso permite entrar.

english_meaning: To announce something is to make it known.
english_example: He announced to everyone his new idea for the company.
spanish_meaning: Anunciar es comunicar públicamente algo.
spanish_example: Anunció la reunión.

english_meaning: Someone or something beside you is next to you.
english_example: The two brothers stood beside each other.
spanish_meaning: Al lado de significa junto o próximo.
spanish_example: Estoy al lado de mi amigo.

english_meaning: A challenge is something difficult to complete.
english_example: It was a challenge to climb to the top of the mountain.
spanish_meaning: Un desafío es una tarea difícil.
spanish_example: Escalar esa montaña fue un desafío.

english_meaning: To claim means to say that something is true.
english_example: He claimed to know why the country’s laws were weak.
spanish_meaning: Afirmar es declarar algo como verdadero.
spanish_example: Afirmó que llegaría temprano.

english_meaning: The condition of someone or something is the state that they are in.
english_example: The patient’s condition was very good.
spanish_meaning: La condición describe el estado de algo o alguien.
spanish_example: La condición del paciente mejoró.

english_meaning: To contribute to something means to do something to make it successful.
english_example: We decided to contribute money to the new hospital.
spanish_meaning: Contribuir es ayudar para que algo salga bien.
spanish_example: Contribuí con mis horas de voluntariado.

english_meaning: A difference is a way that something is not like other things.
english_example: The biggest difference between the birds is the color of their feathers.
spanish_meaning: Diferencia es lo que distingue a una cosa de otra.
spanish_example: La diferencia es pequeña.

english_meaning: To divide something is to split it into smaller parts.
english_example: We divided the pizza.
spanish_meaning: Dividir es separar en partes.
spanish_example: Dividí la pizza en tres.

english_meaning: An expert is someone who is very good at doing something.
english_example: The wizard was an expert at magic.
spanish_meaning: Persona muy competente o especialista en algo.
spanish_example: El mago era un experto en magia.

english_meaning: A famous person or thing is well known.
english_example: The Eiffel Tower in Paris is very famous.
spanish_meaning: Muy conocido por muchas personas.
spanish_example: La Torre Eiffel en París es muy famosa.

english_meaning: Force is a person’s strength or power.
english_example: He used all his force to try and open the door.
spanish_meaning: Potencia o energía que una persona puede ejercer.
spanish_example: Usó toda su fuerza para intentar abrir la puerta.

english_meaning: To cause harm is to hurt someone or damage something.
english_example: A hot iron can cause great harm if you are not careful.
spanish_meaning: Dañar es herir a alguien o perjudicar algo.
spanish_example: Una plancha caliente puede dañar mucho si no tienes cuidado.

english_meaning: To lay means to put or place in a horizontal or flat position.
english_example: Don’t lay your socks on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Poner o colocar algo en posición horizontal o plana.
spanish_example: No pongas tus calcetines en el suelo.
notes: En este contexto equivale a «colocar»; no al verbo «acostarse».

english_meaning: Peace is a time without war.
english_example: A white dove is a symbol of peace.
spanish_meaning: Situación sin guerra ni violencia.
spanish_example: La paloma blanca es símbolo de la paz.

english_meaning: A prince is the son of a king.
english_example: The prince and the princess were married.
spanish_meaning: Hijo de un rey.
spanish_example: El príncipe y la princesa se casaron.

english_meaning: To protect someone is to stop them from getting hurt.
english_example: Firemen protect us from fires.
spanish_meaning: Evitar que alguien o algo salga lastimado.
spanish_example: Los bomberos nos protegen de los incendios.

english_meaning: To sense something is to know about it without being told.
english_example: I could sense that he was watching me.
spanish_meaning: Darse cuenta o notar algo sin que te lo hayan dicho.
spanish_example: Pude percibir que él me estaba observando.

english_meaning: When something is sudden, it happens very quickly.
english_example: He felt a sudden pain in his chest.
spanish_meaning: Que sucede de forma rápida e inesperada.
spanish_example: Sintió un dolor repentino en el pecho.

english_meaning: Therefore means for this reason.
english_example: He is fat. Therefore, he should go on a diet.
spanish_meaning: Por está razón; en consecuencia.
spanish_example: Es obeso; por lo tanto, debería hacer dieta.

english_meaning: To accept something that is offered is to take it.
english_example: I accepted the girl’s very nice gift.
spanish_meaning: Recibir o tomar algo que te ofrecen.
spanish_example: Acepté el bonito regalo de la chica.

english_meaning: To arrange things is to put them in the right place.
english_example: Please arrange the words in order from A to Z.
spanish_meaning: Colocar las cosas en el lugar correcto.
spanish_example: Por favor, organiza las palabras en orden de la A a la Z.

english_meaning: To attend something is to go to it.
english_example: My sister and I attend the same school.
spanish_meaning: Ir a un lugar o evento.
spanish_example: Mi hermana y yo asistimos al mismo instituto.

english_meaning: You chase people when you run after them and try to catch them.
english_example: I was chased by a dog.
spanish_meaning: Correr detrás de alguien o algo para alcanzarlo.
spanish_example: Un perro me persiguió.

english_meaning: A contrast is a sharp difference between two things.
english_example: The contrast between my parents is very noticeable.
spanish_meaning: Diferencia clara entre dos cosas.
spanish_example: El contraste entre mis padres es muy evidente.

english_meaning: To encourage people is to make them want to do something.
english_example: My football coach will encourage us when we are losing.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que alguien quiera o se sienta capaz de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Nuestro entrenador nos anima cuando vamos perdiendo.

english_meaning: People or things familiar to you are those you know well.
english_example: The two friends were very familiar with each other.
spanish_meaning: Que una persona o cosa te resulta bien conocida.
spanish_example: Los dos amigos eran muy conocidos entre sí.

english_meaning: To grab is to take a hold of someone or something suddenly.
english_example: I grabbed a pear from the tree.
spanish_meaning: Tomar algo de golpe o sujetarlo rápidamente.
spanish_example: Agarré una pera del árbol.

english_meaning: To hang something is to keep it above the ground.
english_example: I drew a picture of my family, and my mother hung it on the wall.
spanish_meaning: Poner algo suspendido o por encima del suelo.
spanish_example: Mi madre colgó el retrato en la pared.

english_meaning: If something is huge, it is very big.
english_example: At work, my father drives a huge truck.
spanish_meaning: Muy grande o de gran tamaño.
spanish_example: Mi padre conduce un camión enorme.

english_meaning: If something is necessary, you must do it.
english_example: A passport is necessary if you travel to other countries.
spanish_meaning: Que es imprescindible y se debe hacer.
spanish_example: El pasaporte es necesario si viajas a otro país.

english_meaning: A pattern is a way in which something is done or organized.
english_example: My pattern of brushing my teeth is the same as most people’s.
spanish_meaning: Manera habitual de hacer o organizar algo.
spanish_example: Mi patrón para cepillarme los dientes es igual al de la mayoría.

english_meaning: To propose something is to say that it should be done.
english_example: My grandmother proposed that we have our picture taken with Santa.
spanish_meaning: Sugerir que algo se haga.
spanish_example: Mi abuela propuso que nos tomáramos una foto con Santa.

english_meaning: A purpose is the reason that you do something.
english_example: The purpose of exercising is to get into shape.
spanish_meaning: Motivo o razón para hacer algo.
spanish_example: El propósito de hacer ejercicio es ponerse en forma.

english_meaning: To release something is to stop holding it.
english_example: She released the bird from her hands.
spanish_meaning: Dejar de sujetar o retener algo.
spanish_example: Ella soltó al pajarito de su mano.

english_meaning: To require something is to say that it is necessary.
english_example: We require teachers to have a university degree.
spanish_meaning: Decir que algo es necesario.
spanish_example: Requerimos que los profesores tengan un título universitario.

english_meaning: You are satisfied when you have what you wanted.
english_example: I am very satisfied with your work.
spanish_meaning: Contento por haber conseguido lo que se quería.
spanish_example: Estoy muy satisfecho con tu trabajo.

english_meaning: If something is single, then there is only one.
english_example: I have a single key in my hand.
spanish_meaning: Que es uno solo o no está acompañado.
spanish_example: Tengo una sola llave en la mano.

english_meaning: To tear something means to pull it apart.
english_example: It is easy to tear paper.
spanish_meaning: Separar algo tirando o rasgándolo.
spanish_example: Es fácil romper el papel.

english_meaning: A theory is an idea about how something works.
english_example: We talked about Einstein’s theory of relativity in class.
spanish_meaning: Idea o explicación de cómo funciona algo.
spanish_example: En clase hablamos de la teoría de la relatividad de Einstein.

english_meaning: An animal is a living thing that can move.
english_example: My favorite animal is the panda.
spanish_meaning: Ser vivo que se mueve por sí mismo.
spanish_example: Mi animal favorito es el panda.

english_meaning: A bus is a large vehicle that people travel on.
english_example: My father takes the bus to work.
spanish_meaning: Vehículo grande para transportar personas.
spanish_example: Mi padre toma el autobús para ir al trabajo.

english_meaning: A cat is a small animal related to lions and tigers that is kept as a pet.
english_example: This cat is playing with a ball.
spanish_meaning: Pequeño animal doméstico relacionado con leones y tigres.
spanish_example: Este gato está jugando con una pelota.

english_meaning: A command is an order given to a person or animal to do something.
english_example: Joe gave the students a command to stand up.
spanish_meaning: Instrucción dada para que alguien haga algo.
spanish_example: Joe dio la orden de ponerse de pie a los alumnos.

english_meaning: To depend on something is to need it for support or help.
english_example: My grandfather depends on a cane when he walks.
spanish_meaning: Necesitar de algo para tener apoyo o ayuda.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo depende de un bastón para caminar.

english_meaning: A dog is an animal with four legs and a tail that is kept as a pet or trained to work.
english_example: This woman’s best friend is her dog.
spanish_meaning: Animal de cuatro patas con cola que suele ser mascota.
spanish_example: El mejor amigo de está mujer es su perro.

english_meaning: A door is an object that swings or slides open and shut.
english_example: Can you close the door, please?
spanish_meaning: Objeto que se abre y cierra para entrar o salir.
spanish_example: ¿Puedes cerrar la puerta, por favor?

english_meaning: A friend is someone a person knows and likes spending time with.
english_example: Brian’s friend is very sad.
spanish_meaning: Persona que conoces y te gusta pasar tiempo.
spanish_example: El amigo de Brian está muy triste.

english_meaning: To hear is to be aware of sound through your ears.
english_example: Michelle cannot hear what you are saying.
spanish_meaning: Percibir sonidos con los oídos.
spanish_example: Michelle no puede oír lo que estás diciendo.

english_meaning: To help is to do something that makes something easier for someone else.
english_example: Jane can help Nathan climb up the rock.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo que facilita a otra persona.
spanish_example: Jane puede ayudar a Nathan a subir la roca.

english_meaning: A horse is a big, strong animal that people ride and use for pulling heavy things.
english_example: I went to a farm and saw a horse.
spanish_meaning: Animal grande y fuerte que la gente monta o usa para tirar.
spanish_example: Fui a una granja y vi un caballo.

english_meaning: A hospital is where sick or hurt people receive care or treatment.
english_example: The doctor talks to a patient at the hospital.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde enfermos o heridos reciben atención médica.
spanish_example: El médico habla con un paciente en el hospital.

english_meaning: A leg is a body part used for standing and walking.
english_example: She hurt her leg.
spanish_meaning: Parte del cuerpo para apoyar y caminar.
spanish_example: Ella se lesionó la pierna.

english_meaning: Medical means related to the treatment of an injury or disease.
english_example: Tyler is a medical student studying to become a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con el tratamiento de enfermedades o lesiones.
spanish_example: Tyler es un estudiante de medicina.
notes: Como adjetivo, también puede usarse «médica/médico». No confundir con «médico» como sustantivo «doctor».

english_meaning: To open is to move something so that an opening is not covered.
english_example: Amy likes to open the window to let fresh air in.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que algo no esté cubierto.
spanish_example: A Amy le gusta abrir la ventana para que entre aire fresco.

english_meaning: To pull is to hold onto something to move it toward you.
english_example: The children pull the rope.
spanish_meaning: Sujetar algo para moverlo hacia ti.
spanish_example: Los niños tiran de la cuerda.

english_meaning: A rabbit is a small animal with long ears that lives in a hole in the ground.
english_example: Look at the rabbit in the park.
spanish_meaning: Animal pequeño con orejas largas que vive en madrigueras.
spanish_example: Mira el conejo en el parque.

english_meaning: A school is a place where children go to learn.
english_example: The students study at school.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde los niños van a aprender.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes estudian en la escuela.

english_meaning: To see is to use your eyes to look at something or someone.
english_example: Steven does not want to see the scary picture.
spanish_meaning: Usar los ojos para mirar algo o a alguien.
spanish_example: A Steven no le gusta ver la foto de miedo.

english_meaning: Service is the act of helping or serving someone.
english_example: This coffee shop has excellent service.
spanish_meaning: Acción de ayudar o atender a alguien.
spanish_example: Esta cafetería tiene excelente servicio.

english_meaning: A benefit is a good thing.
english_example: Being able to fly is a benefit to birds.
spanish_meaning: Algo bueno o ventajoso.
spanish_example: Poder volar es un gran beneficio para las aves.

english_meaning: If you are certain about something, you know it is true.
english_example: I am certain that zebras have stripes.
spanish_meaning: Seguro de que una cosa es verdadera.
spanish_example: Estoy seguro de que las cebras tienen rayas.

english_meaning: A chance is an opportunity to do something.
english_example: I had a chance to see the Coliseum in Rome last summer.
spanish_meaning: Posibilidad de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Tuve la oportunidad de ver el Coliseo de Roma el verano pasado.

english_meaning: An effect is a change made by something else.
english_example: The medicine had a good effect on the boy.
spanish_meaning: Cambio producido por algo.
spanish_example: La medicina tuvo un buen efecto en el niño.

english_meaning: If something is essential, it is very important and necessary.
english_example: It is essential to have oxygen when you scuba dive.
spanish_meaning: Muy importante y necesario.
spanish_example: Es esencial tener oxígeno cuando haces buceo.

english_meaning: If something is far, it is not close.
english_example: It’s far from the east coast to the west coast of America.
spanish_meaning: No cerca.
spanish_example: Es muy lejos desde la costa este a la costa oeste de América.

english_meaning: To focus on something is to think about it and pay attention to it.
english_example: My mom always helps me to focus on my school work.
spanish_meaning: Prestar atención o centrar la mente en algo.
spanish_example: Mi mamá siempre me ayuda a concentrarme en mis tareas.

english_meaning: The function of something is what it does.
english_example: The function of a flashlight is to help you see in the dark.
spanish_meaning: Lo que algo hace o para qué sirve.
spanish_example: La función de una linterna es ayudar a ver en la oscuridad.

english_meaning: Grass is the green leaves that cover the ground.
english_example: The grass looked so soft and green.
spanish_meaning: Hojas verdes que cubren el suelo.
spanish_example: La hierba se veía muy suave y verde.

english_meaning: To guard something is to take care of it.
english_example: The police officer will guard us from any harm.
spanish_meaning: Proteger y vigilar algo para que no se dañe.
spanish_example: El policía nos cuidará de cualquier daño.

english_meaning: The image of something is a picture of it.
english_example: The image of her eye was very clear.
spanish_meaning: Representación o retrato de algo.
spanish_example: La imagen de su ojo estaba muy clara.

english_meaning: If something is immediate, it happens quickly.
english_example: An immediate response came from the pizza place.
spanish_meaning: Que ocurre rápido o enseguida.
spanish_example: Recibimos una respuesta inmediata de la pizzería.

english_meaning: If something is primary, it is the most important thing.
english_example: His primary thoughts are about money.
spanish_meaning: Lo más importante o prioritario.
spanish_example: Sus pensamientos principales son sobre el dinero.

english_meaning: People who feel proud are happy about what they have done.
english_example: She is proud of the picture she drew of her house.
spanish_meaning: Sentir satisfacción por lo que se ha hecho.
spanish_example: Ella está orgullosa del dibujo que hizo de su casa.

english_meaning: To remain somewhere is to stay there.
english_example: My sister had to remain home since she was sick.
spanish_meaning: Permanecer en un lugar.
spanish_example: Mi hermana tuvo que quedarse en casa porque estaba enferma.

english_meaning: To rest is to stop being active while the body gets back its strength.
english_example: I rested on the couch after work.
spanish_meaning: Detener la actividad para recuperar fuerza.
spanish_example: Descansé en el sofá después del trabajo.

english_meaning: If two things are separate, they are not together.
english_example: New York and Los Angeles are in two separate parts of America.
spanish_meaning: No unido o no junto.
spanish_example: Nueva York y Los Ángeles están en dos partes separadas de Estados Unidos.

english_meaning: A site is a place.
english_example: We found the perfect site for our picnic.
spanish_meaning: Espacio o sitio donde está algo.
spanish_example: Encontramos el lugar perfecto para nuestro picnic.

english_meaning: A tail is a part of an animal’s body that sticks out from its rear or back.
english_example: Our dog wags its tail when it’s happy.
spanish_meaning: Parte trasera que sobresale de un animal.
spanish_example: Nuestro perro mueve su cola cuando está feliz.

english_meaning: Trouble is a problem or a difficulty.
english_example: I have trouble working with my boss.
spanish_meaning: Dificultad o situación problemática.
spanish_example: Tengo problemas para trabajar con mi jefe.

english_meaning: To advertise is to describe or draw attention to a product or event by using a public medium.
english_example: The woman started a new company and needed to advertise her products.
spanish_meaning: Dar a conocer algo para llamar la atención pública.
spanish_example: La mujer creó una empresa y necesitaba anunciar sus productos.

english_meaning: If someone is aware, they know that something or a situation exists.
english_example: The student became aware that the teacher was watching him.
spanish_meaning: Saber que algo existe o que pasa.
spanish_example: El estudiante se dio cuenta de que el profesor lo estaba mirando.

english_meaning: A battery is an object placed inside something to supply it with electricity.
english_example: My brother needs a battery for his clock.
spanish_meaning: Objeto que proporciona electricidad.
spanish_example: Mi hermano necesita una batería para su reloj.

english_meaning: Black is the darkest color.
english_example: Our street is black at night because there are no street lights.
spanish_meaning: Color más oscuro.
spanish_example: Nuestra calle está oscura de noche porque no hay farolas.

english_meaning: A city is a place where a lot of people live.
english_example: Tokyo is a very big city in Japan.
spanish_meaning: Lugar con mucha población.
spanish_example: Tokio es una ciudad muy grande de Japón.

english_meaning: To clean is to make something neat and tidy.
english_example: I clean our kitchen every Saturday.
spanish_meaning: Quitar suciedad para dejar algo ordenado.
spanish_example: Limpio nuestra cocina todos los sábados.

english_meaning: A country is an area of land with the same government and laws.
english_example: France is a country in Europe.
spanish_meaning: Zona de tierra con gobierno y leyes propias.
spanish_example: Francia es un país de Europa.

english_meaning: To develop is to make something larger or more advanced.
english_example: This practice will help you develop your math skills.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo más grande o avanzado.
spanish_example: Esta práctica te ayudará a desarrollar tus habilidades matemáticas.

english_meaning: If something is electric, it uses electricity.
english_example: Some car companies make electric cars.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con la electricidad.
spanish_example: Algunas marcas hacen autos eléctricos.

english_meaning: When something happens at a later time or in the end, it happens eventually.
english_example: He will eventually move to London to find a new job.
spanish_meaning: Al final o en un momento posterior.
spanish_example: Finalmente, se mudará a Londres para buscar un nuevo trabajo.

english_meaning: A fact is a piece of information that is true.
english_example: A fact about South America is that it is a continent.
spanish_meaning: Información o dato verdadero.
spanish_example: Un hecho sobre Sudamérica es que es un continente.

english_meaning: Glass is a transparent, breakable material.
english_example: Windows are made of glass.
spanish_meaning: Material transparente y frágil.
spanish_example: Las ventanas están hechas de vidrio.

english_meaning: History is the study of the past.
english_example: History was my favorite subject in school.
spanish_meaning: Estudio o relato del pasado.
spanish_example: La historia fue mi asignatura favorita en la escuela.

english_meaning: Nature is everything in the physical world that is not made by people.
english_example: On the weekends, I love to walk in nature among the trees.
spanish_meaning: Todo lo físico del mundo no hecho por humanos.
spanish_example: Los fines de semana me encanta caminar por la naturaleza.

english_meaning: Never is when something is impossible or will not happen.
english_example: They never eat meat because they are vegetarians.
spanish_meaning: No ha ocurrido y no ocurre hasta ahora.
spanish_example: Nunca comen carne porque son vegetarianos.

english_meaning: People are humans.
english_example: How many people live in China?
spanish_meaning: Seres humanos.
spanish_example: ¿Cuántas personas viven en China?

english_meaning: Plastic is a material made by people.
english_example: Many drinks are sold in plastic bottles.
spanish_meaning: Material sintético fabricado por personas.
spanish_example: Muchas bebidas se venden en botellas de plástico.

english_meaning: A problem is a situation when something goes wrong.
english_example: My problem is that I lost my dog.
spanish_meaning: Situación en la que algo sale mal.
spanish_example: Mi problema es que perdí a mi perro.

english_meaning: A street is a road in a city or village.
english_example: What is the name of the street you live on?
spanish_meaning: Camino dentro de una ciudad o pueblo.
spanish_example: ¿Cómo se llama la calle donde vives?

english_meaning: To think is to have an opinion or thought about something.
english_example: I think summer is the best season.
spanish_meaning: Tener una opinión o idea sobre algo.
spanish_example: Pienso que el verano es la mejor estación.

english_meaning: A person who is alone is not with another person.
english_example: The boy wanted to be alone to think.
spanish_meaning: Sin compañía.
spanish_example: El niño quería estar solo para pensar.

english_meaning: An apartment is a set of rooms in a building where people live.
english_example: She has a nice apartment in the city.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de habitaciones en un edificio donde vive gente.
spanish_example: Ella tiene un buen apartamento en la ciudad.

english_meaning: An article is a story in a newspaper or magazine.
english_example: Did you read the article in the newspaper about the soccer game?
spanish_meaning: Texto informativo en un periódico o revista.
spanish_example: ¿Leíste el artículo del periódico sobre el partido de fútbol?

english_meaning: An artist is a person who paints, draws, or makes sculptures.
english_example: He went to Paris to become an artist.
spanish_meaning: Persona que pinta, dibuja o esculpe.
spanish_example: Se fue a París para convertirse en artista.

english_meaning: Someone’s attitude is the way they feel and think.
english_example: John has a bad attitude. He’s always angry.
spanish_meaning: Forma de pensar o de sentirse de alguien.
spanish_example: John tiene una mala actitud. Siempre está enojado.

english_meaning: Beauty is the state or quality of being beautiful.
english_example: I heard my mother was a great beauty when she was young.
spanish_meaning: Cualidad de ser o parecer bello.
spanish_example: Escuché que mi madre era muy bella cuando era joven.

english_meaning: To compare means to say how two things are the same and different.
english_example: If you compare cats and dogs, you’ll see that they’re both good pets.
spanish_meaning: Decir en qué se parecen y se diferencian dos cosas.
spanish_example: Si comparas gatos y perros, verás que ambos son buenas mascotas.

english_meaning: To judge something is to say if it is good or bad.
english_example: The boy was going to judge how his mother’s turkey tasted.
spanish_meaning: Decidir si algo es bueno o malo.
spanish_example: El niño iba a juzgar cómo sabía el pavo de su madre.

english_meaning: A magazine is a regular publication with news, stories, and articles.
english_example: She likes to read fashion magazines.
spanish_meaning: Publicación periódica con noticias y artículos.
spanish_example: Le gusta leer revistas de moda.

english_meaning: A material is what is used to make something.
english_example: Brick is a good material for building houses.
spanish_meaning: Lo que se usa para fabricar algo.
spanish_example: El ladrillo es un buen material para construir casas.

english_meaning: A meal is a time when food is eaten, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
english_example: Breakfast is my favorite meal because I enjoy cereal.
spanish_meaning: Momento en que se come (desayuno, almuerzo o cena).
spanish_example: El desayuno es mi comida favorita porque me gusta el cereal.

english_meaning: A method is the way to do something.
english_example: One method to remember things is to tie a string around your finger.
spanish_meaning: Manera de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Un método para recordar cosas es atar un hilo en el dedo.

english_meaning: A neighbor is a person who lives near you.
english_example: I like my neighbor because he’s very friendly.
spanish_meaning: Persona que vive cerca de ti.
spanish_example: Me gusta mi vecino porque es muy amable.

english_meaning: If something is professional, it deals with work that uses special skills.
english_example: If you want to be a pilot, you must have professional training.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con un trabajo que requiere habilidades especiales.
spanish_example: Si quieres ser piloto, necesitas formación profesional.

english_meaning: A profit is the extra money you make when you sell something.
english_example: I made a small profit from selling my old clothes.
spanish_meaning: Dinero extra que se obtiene al vender algo.
spanish_example: Saqué una pequeña ganancia vendiendo mi ropa vieja.

english_meaning: The quality of something is how good it is.
english_example: The quality of his car is very good.
spanish_meaning: Qué tan bueno es algo.
spanish_example: La calidad de su coche es muy buena.

english_meaning: A space is an empty area.
english_example: I don’t have much space for things in my small house.
spanish_meaning: Área vacía o lugar libre.
spanish_example: No tengo mucho espacio para las cosas en mi casa pequeña.

english_meaning: Stairs are the things that are used to walk up or down in a building.
english_example: You can take the stairs to the second floor.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de peldaños para subir o bajar en un edificio.
spanish_example: Puedes tomar las escaleras hasta el segundo piso.

english_meaning: A symbol is a thing that stands for something else.
english_example: This symbol tells us that we cannot smoke in this area.
spanish_meaning: Objeto o signo que representa otra cosa.
spanish_example: Este símbolo nos indica que aquí no se puede fumar.

english_meaning: If someone or something is thin, they are not fat.
english_example: The man was thin because he didn’t eat much.
spanish_meaning: No grueso ni con mucha grasa.
spanish_example: El hombre era delgado porque comía poco.

english_meaning: People study accounting so they can deal with money and finance properly.
english_example: He works for an accounting firm.
spanish_meaning: Estudio para llevar y controlar el dinero.
spanish_example: Estudia contabilidad en una empresa.

english_meaning: To appeal to someone is to be interesting or attractive.
english_example: Sleeping all day appeals to me, but I have to go to school.
spanish_meaning: Resultar interesante o atractivo para alguien.
spanish_example: Dormir todo el día me atrae, pero tengo que ir a la escuela.

english_meaning: To assume something is to think that it is true, even with no proof.
english_example: I assume you are both familiar with this plan.
spanish_meaning: Creer que algo es cierto sin pruebas.
spanish_example: Supongo que ambos conocen bien este plan.

english_meaning: To borrow something is to take it and then give it back later.
english_example: Can I borrow a pencil to use today? I’ll give it back to you tomorrow.
spanish_meaning: Tomar algo de alguien para luego devolverlo.
spanish_example: ¿Puedo pedirte prestado un lápiz? Te lo devuelvo mañana.

english_meaning: A client is a person or business that pays another to do a service.
english_example: She has many clients who enjoy coming to her salon.
spanish_meaning: Persona que paga por un servicio.
spanish_example: Ella tiene muchos clientes que vienen a su salón.

english_meaning: The downtown area is the center of most cities.
english_example: The downtown area is filled with many tall buildings.
spanish_meaning: Zona principal y céntrica de una ciudad.
spanish_example: El centro de la ciudad está lleno de edificios altos.

english_meaning: If something is dull, it is not exciting.
english_example: The movie was very dull. I fell asleep watching it.
spanish_meaning: Que no resulta emocionante o interesante.
spanish_example: La película fue tan aburrida que me dormí viéndola.

english_meaning: To embarrass people is to make them feel ashamed or foolish.
english_example: He was embarrassed when he couldn’t remember her name.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que alguien se sienta cohibido o tonto.
spanish_example: Se avergonzó cuando no pudo recordar su nombre.

english_meaning: A fare is an amount of money paid to use a bus, train, or taxi.
english_example: Since he is a senior, my grandfather pays a low fare for the bus.
spanish_meaning: Monto de dinero que se paga por usar transporte.
spanish_example: Como jubilado, mi abuelo paga una tarifa baja en el autobús.

english_meaning: Former describes something that used to be but is not anymore.
english_example: The hotel, a former castle, was built over 200 years ago.
spanish_meaning: Que antes existía así y ya no.
spanish_example: El hotel, un castillo anterior, se construyó hace más de 200 años.

english_meaning: To found a company or organization means to start it.
english_example: The Pilgrims founded one of the first colonies in the United States.
spanish_meaning: Crear o establecer una empresa u organización.
spanish_example: Los peregrinos fundaron una de las primeras colonias de EE. UU.

english_meaning: To invest means to use money in a way that will bring a profit later.
english_example: I invested money in a new building that should bring me a profit.
spanish_meaning: Usar dinero para que produzca beneficio más tarde.
spanish_example: Invertí dinero en un nuevo edificio para obtener ganancias.

english_meaning: A loan is something, usually money, that one person lends to another.
english_example: I got a loan from the bank.
spanish_meaning: Dinero que una persona presta a otra.
spanish_example: Pedí un préstamo al banco.

english_meaning: If something is practical, it is useful.
english_example: Learning English is practical; you can use it in many places.
spanish_meaning: Útil y aplicable.
spanish_example: Aprender inglés es práctico; te sirve en muchos lugares.

english_meaning: A quarter is 1/4 or 25% of something.
english_example: He paid a quarter for the candy.
spanish_meaning: 25%, un cuarto.
spanish_example: Pagó una cuarta parte por el caramelo.

english_meaning: A salary is how much money a person makes at his or her job.
english_example: He got a new job with a better salary.
spanish_meaning: Dinero que gana una persona en su trabajo.
spanish_example: Conseguí un trabajo nuevo con mejor sueldo.

english_meaning: A scholarship is money given so someone can go to school.
english_example: I got a scholarship to help me pay for university.
spanish_meaning: Ayuda económica para estudiar.
spanish_example: Obtuve una beca para pagar la universidad.

english_meaning: If something is temporary, it exists for a short time.
english_example: This car is only temporary; I’ll get a new one soon.
spanish_meaning: Que dura poco tiempo.
spanish_example: Este coche es temporal; pronto compraré uno nuevo.

english_meaning: A treasure is a collection of valuable things, especially jewels or gold.
english_example: They became very rich when they found the buried treasure.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de objetos valiosos, como oro o joyas.
spanish_example: Se hicieron ricos al encontrar el tesoro enterrado.

english_meaning: To urge someone is to try very hard to get them to do something.
english_example: He urged them to believe his story.
spanish_meaning: Animar con fuerza para que alguien haga algo.
spanish_example: Él insistió para que creyeran su historia.

english_meaning: A coach is a person who teaches sports.
english_example: My coach gets very excited during games.
spanish_meaning: Persona que entrena equipos o atletas.
spanish_example: Mi entrenador se entusiasma mucho durante los partidos.

english_meaning: To control something is to make it do what you want.
english_example: To control the TV, just push the buttons.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que algo haga lo que quieres.
spanish_example: Para controlar la televisión, pulsa los botones.

english_meaning: A description of someone or something says what they are like.
english_example: I gave a description of the man with the gun and hat to the police.
spanish_meaning: Texto que dice cómo es alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Di una descripción del hombre con el sombrero y el arma a la policía.

english_meaning: If something is direct, it goes straight between two places.
english_example: The green path is a direct route to my house.
spanish_meaning: Que va sin rodeos.
spanish_example: El camino verde es una ruta directa a mi casa.

english_meaning: An exam is a test.
english_example: I did some practice questions for the math exam on the board.
spanish_meaning: Prueba para evaluar conocimientos.
spanish_example: Hice preguntas de práctica para el examen de matemáticas.

english_meaning: An example of something is a thing that is typical of it.
english_example: Cola is an example of a soft drink.
spanish_meaning: Algo típico o modelo de algo.
spanish_example: La Coca-Cola es un ejemplo de refresco.

english_meaning: A limit is the largest or smallest amount of something that you allow.
english_example: My mother put a limit on how much I could use the phone.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad máxima o mínima permitida.
spanish_example: Mi madre puso un límite al tiempo de móvil.

english_meaning: If something is local, it is nearby.
english_example: The local market in my neighborhood sells all the food we need.
spanish_meaning: Propio de una zona cercana; del barrio.
spanish_example: El mercado local de mi barrio vende toda la comida que necesitamos.

english_meaning: Magical describes a quality that makes someone or something special.
english_example: The fireworks made the night sky look so magical.
spanish_meaning: Que parece especial o extraordinario.
spanish_example: Los fuegos artificiales hicieron que el cielo se viera mágico.

english_meaning: Mail is letters and other things sent to people.
english_example: I get a lot of mail because I have friends all over the world.
spanish_meaning: Cartas y paquetes enviados a personas.
spanish_example: Recibo mucho correo porque tengo amigos en todo el mundo.

english_meaning: A novel is a book that tells a story.
english_example: He wrote a great novel about ancient China.
spanish_meaning: Libro que cuenta una historia.
spanish_example: Escribió una novela magnífica sobre la China antigua.

english_meaning: An outline is the plan for a story or essay.
english_example: Before I wrote my essay, I made an outline.
spanish_meaning: Borrador o plan de un texto.
spanish_example: Antes de escribir mi ensayo, hice un esquema.

english_meaning: A poet is a person who writes poems.
english_example: William Shakespeare was one of the greatest poets.
spanish_meaning: Persona que escribe poemas.
spanish_example: William Shakespeare fue uno de los mejores poetas.

english_meaning: To print something is to put it onto paper.
english_example: Make sure that you print your name clearly.
spanish_meaning: Poner algo sobre papel.
spanish_example: Asegúrate de imprimir tu nombre claramente.

english_meaning: A scene is one part of a book or movie.
english_example: A movie is made up of many short pieces or scenes.
spanish_meaning: Parte de una película o de un libro.
spanish_example: Una película se compone de muchas escenas.

english_meaning: A sheet is a thin flat piece of paper.
english_example: I only needed a single sheet of paper to do my homework.
spanish_meaning: Papel delgado y plano.
spanish_example: Solo necesité una hoja para hacer mi tarea.

english_meaning: People who are silly show a lack of serious thought.
english_example: I made a silly mistake of dropping mom’s vase.
spanish_meaning: Que carece de seriedad.
spanish_example: Cometí un error tonto al dejar caer el jarrón de mamá.

english_meaning: A store is a place where you can buy things.
english_example: I picked up a few things at the grocery store.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde se compran cosas.
spanish_example: Compré algunas cosas en la tienda de comestibles.

english_meaning: To suffer is to feel pain.
english_example: Her headache made her suffer all day.
spanish_meaning: Sentir dolor o pasar por dificultades.
spanish_example: El dolor de cabeza le hizo sufrir todo el día.

english_meaning: Technology is new things made by using science.
english_example: He loves technology such as laptop computers.
spanish_meaning: Nuevos avances creados con ciencia.
spanish_example: Le encanta la tecnología, como los ordenadores portátiles.

english_meaning: To go across something is to go to the other side of it.
english_example: He walked across the board to the other side.
spanish_meaning: Ir desde un lado al otro de algo.
spanish_example: Caminó a través del tablero hasta el otro lado.

english_meaning: To breathe means to let air go in and out of your body.
english_example: We need strong healthy lungs to help us breathe well.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que el aire entre y salga del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Necesitamos pulmones sanos para respirar bien.

english_meaning: A characteristic is something that shows what a person or a thing is like.
