english_example: One characteristic of tigers is their black stripes.
spanish_meaning: Cualidad que muestra cómo es algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Una característica de los tigres es tener rayas negras.

english_meaning: To consume something means to eat or drink it.
english_example: Jack consumed a whole plate of spaghetti.
spanish_meaning: Comer o beber algo.
spanish_example: Jack consumió un plato entero de espagueti.

english_meaning: To excite someone means to make them happy and interested.
english_example: I heard about the school dance on Friday. This excited me.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que alguien se sienta feliz e interesado.
spanish_example: Cuando supe del baile de la escuela, me emocioné.

english_meaning: If something is extremely good, it is very, very good.
english_example: I was extremely worried about him.
spanish_meaning: De forma muy intensa o exagerada.
spanish_example: Estaba extremadamente preocupado por él.

english_meaning: Fear is the feeling of being afraid.
english_example: I have a great fear of skateboarding.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de temor.
spanish_example: Tengo mucho miedo al monopatín.

english_meaning: If you are fortunate, you are lucky.
english_example: I was fortunate to get a seat.
spanish_meaning: Que tiene suerte.
spanish_example: Tuve la suerte de conseguir un asiento.

english_meaning: If someone happens to do something, he or she does it by chance.
english_example: I happened to meet some new friends at school today.
spanish_meaning: Ocurrir, a veces por casualidad.
spanish_example: Me pasé encontrando hoy a nuevos amigos en la escuela.

english_meaning: The length of something is how long it is from one end to the other.
english_example: The length of the floor is three meters.
spanish_meaning: Qué tan largo es algo de un extremo al otro.
spanish_example: La longitud del suelo es de tres metros.

english_meaning: A mistake is something you do wrong.
english_example: My boss always yells at me when I make a mistake.
spanish_meaning: Cosa que se hace mal.
spanish_example: Mi jefe siempre me regaña cuando cometo un error.

english_meaning: To observe something is to watch it.
english_example: Brian observed the sun rising over the mountains.
spanish_meaning: Mirar algo con atención.
spanish_example: Brian observó la salida del sol sobre las montañas.

english_meaning: An opportunity is a chance to do something.
english_example: I had an opportunity to take pictures in the jungle.
spanish_meaning: Posibilidad de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Tuve la oportunidad de tomar fotos en la selva.

english_meaning: A prize is something of value that is given to the winner.
english_example: I won a prize for getting the best grade on my science test.
spanish_meaning: Objeto o dinero que se da al ganador.
spanish_example: Gané un premio por sacar la mejor nota en ciencias.

english_meaning: A race is a contest to see who is the fastest.
english_example: Paul is a fast runner, so he easily won the race.
spanish_meaning: Competencia para ver quién es más rápido.
spanish_example: Paul es un corredor rápido y ganó la carrera.

english_meaning: To realize is to suddenly understand.
english_example: After I left my house, I realized that I left the light on.
spanish_meaning: Entender de repente la verdad de algo.
spanish_example: Cuando salí de casa, me di cuenta de que había dejado la luz encendida.

english_meaning: To respond is to give an answer to what someone else said.
english_example: When the teacher asked the question, we all responded.
spanish_meaning: Dar una respuesta a lo que alguien dijo.
spanish_example: Cuando el profesor hizo la pregunta, respondimos todos.

english_meaning: A risk is a chance of something bad happening.
english_example: I took a risk and climbed the snowy mountain.
spanish_meaning: Posibilidad de que ocurra algo malo.
spanish_example: Asumí un riesgo y subí la montaña nevada.

english_meaning: To wonder is to ask yourself questions or have a need to know.
english_example: The young mother wondered if she’d have a boy or a girl.
spanish_meaning: Sentir curiosidad o querer saber algo.
spanish_example: La madre se preguntaba si tendría un niño o una niña.

english_meaning: Yet is used to say something has not happened up to now.
english_example: We can’t go out yet; we’re still eating.
spanish_meaning: Todavía no ha ocurrido hasta el momento presente.
spanish_example: No podemos salir todavía; todavía estamos comiendo.

english_meaning: Art is something creative that expresses ideas and feelings.
english_example: My sister likes to look at the art in the museum.
spanish_meaning: Actividad creativa que expresa ideas y emociones.
spanish_example: A mi hermana le gusta ver el arte del museo.

english_meaning: A book is a set of printed sheets of paper that are held together inside a cover.
english_example: The man smiles as he reads his favorite book.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de hojas impresas encuadernadas.
spanish_example: El hombre sonríe mientras lee su libro favorito.

english_meaning: Clothes are what people wear to cover their bodies.
english_example: I bought warm clothes for the cold winter.
spanish_meaning: Lo que las personas usan para vestirse.
spanish_example: Compré ropa abrigada para el invierno.

english_meaning: A community is a group of people who share the same area or interests.
english_example: The community got together to plant trees.
spanish_meaning: Grupo de personas con un entorno o intereses comunes.
spanish_example: La comunidad se reunió para plantar árboles.

english_meaning: December is the twelfth month of the year.
english_example: The last day of the year is December 31st.
spanish_meaning: Duodécimo mes del año.
spanish_example: El último día del año es el 31 de diciembre.

english_meaning: Dinner is the main meal eaten in the evening.
english_example: I had a hamburger for dinner.
spanish_meaning: Comida principal que se hace por la noche.
spanish_example: Cené una hamburguesa.

english_meaning: To end is to stop or finish.
english_example: They are happy because the teacher wanted to end the class early.
spanish_meaning: Detener o finalizar algo.
spanish_example: Están contentos porque el profesor quiso terminar la clase temprano.

english_meaning: To exchange is to give one thing in return for another.
english_example: The men exchange business cards.
spanish_meaning: Dar una cosa a cambio de otra.
spanish_example: Los hombres intercambiaron tarjetas de visita.

english_meaning: A family is a group of people who are related to each other.
english_example: There are four people in my family.
spanish_meaning: Grupo de personas unidas por parentesco.
spanish_example: Hay cuatro personas en mi familia.

english_meaning: From shows a starting place or position.
english_example: Chris studies from 10 o’clock to noon every morning.
spanish_meaning: Indica un punto de origen o inicio.
spanish_example: Chris estudia de las 10 a las 12 cada mañana.

english_meaning: Green is the color of growing grass or leaves.
english_example: I don’t like green apples.
spanish_meaning: Color de la hierba o las hojas.
spanish_example: No me gustan las manzanas verdes.

english_meaning: A person’s home is the place where that person lives.
english_example: The family likes to stay at home on the weekends.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde vive una persona.
spanish_example: La familia gusta de estar en casa los fines de semana.

english_meaning: January is the first month of the year.
english_example: My birthday is in January.
spanish_meaning: Primer mes del año.
spanish_example: Mi cumpleaños es en enero.

english_meaning: Red is the color of blood.
english_example: Jack is wearing a red shirt.
spanish_meaning: Color de la sangre.
spanish_example: Jack lleva una camisa roja.

english_meaning: Seven is the word for the number 7.
english_example: I have seven colored pencils on my desk.
spanish_meaning: Número 7.
spanish_example: Tengo siete lápices de color en mi mesa.

english_meaning: To start is to begin doing something.
english_example: Catherine has to start work at 8 o’clock in the morning.
spanish_meaning: Iniciar algo.
spanish_example: Catherine tiene que empezar a trabajar a las 8 en punto.

english_meaning: Together shows an action is done with another person or thing.
english_example: Let’s work together to finish the project.
spanish_meaning: Realizar una acción con otra persona.
spanish_example: Trabajemos juntos para terminar el proyecto.

english_meaning: A university is a school where people study for a degree.
english_example: My dream is to go to a good university.
spanish_meaning: Centro donde se estudia para obtener un título.
spanish_example: Mi sueño es ir a una buena universidad.

english_meaning: To wear is to have clothing on your body.
english_example: The man has to wear a suit to work.
spanish_meaning: Llevar puesta una prenda de vestir.
spanish_example: El hombre tiene que usar traje para ir a trabajar.

english_meaning: A year is a period of 365 days or twelve months.
english_example: You become one year older every birthday.
spanish_meaning: período de doce meses o 365 días.
spanish_example: Cada cumpleaños te hace un año mayor.

english_meaning: To appreciate something is to understand its good qualities.
english_example: I can appreciate the lovely scenery.
spanish_meaning: Reconocer el valor o mérito de algo.
spanish_example: Puedo apreciar el paisaje precioso.

english_meaning: If something is available, it means you can get it.
english_example: There were many seats available in the room.
spanish_meaning: Que se puede obtener o está libre.
spanish_example: Había muchos asientos disponibles en la sala.

english_meaning: To beat someone means to do better than they do.
english_example: I managed to beat everyone in the race.
spanish_meaning: Salir vencedor de alguien.
spanish_example: Logré ganar a todos en la carrera.

english_meaning: If something is bright, it gives off a lot of light.
english_example: The bright light from the explosion hurt my eyes.
spanish_meaning: Que da mucha luz.
spanish_example: La luz brillante de la explosión me dolió los ojos.

english_meaning: To celebrate is to do something to show that an event is special.
english_example: We all celebrated when we heard the great news.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo para mostrar que algo es especial.
spanish_example: Todos celebramos cuando supimos la buena noticia.

english_meaning: To decide is to make a definite choice.
english_example: He tried to decide which one to eat first.
spanish_meaning: Tomar una decisión firme.
spanish_example: Intentó decidir cuál comer primero.

english_meaning: To disappear means to go away or not be seen.
english_example: The top of the building is disappearing in the clouds.
spanish_meaning: Irse o dejar de verse.
spanish_example: La cima del edificio está desapareciendo entre las nubes.

english_meaning: If you talk about something else, you talk about something different.
english_example: I wanted a bike for my birthday, but I got something else.
spanish_meaning: Diferente o adicional.
spanish_example: Quería una bici para mi cumpleaños, pero recibí otra cosa.

english_meaning: Fair means reasonable or right.
english_example: He sold me his car for a fair price.
spanish_meaning: Razonable o equitativo.
spanish_example: Él me vendió su coche a un precio justo.

english_meaning: To flow is to move easily and continuously in one direction.
english_example: The water flowed over the rocks and into the lake.
spanish_meaning: Moverse de manera continua en una dirección.
spanish_example: El agua fluyó entre las rocas y entró en el lago.

english_meaning: If you move forward, you move in the direction in front of you.
english_example: When he saw his mother, the baby crawled forward to her.
spanish_meaning: Hacia la parte delantera o del frente.
spanish_example: Al verlo, el bebé se arrastró hacia adelante hacia ella.

english_meaning: A hill is a raised area of land. It is higher than the land around it.
english_example: The sun was rising above the green hills.
spanish_meaning: Una elevación de terreno más alta que su alrededor.
spanish_example: El sol se elevaba sobre las colinas verdes.

english_meaning: A level is a point on a scale that measures something.
english_example: Please check the level of the temperature.
spanish_meaning: Un punto de una escala de medición.
spanish_example: Por favor, comprueba el nivel de la temperatura.

english_meaning: A lone person or thing is the only one in a given place.
english_example: A lone man walked along the street.
spanish_meaning: Que está solo y sin compañía.
spanish_example: Un hombre solo caminó por la calle.

english_meaning: A puddle is a pool of liquid on the ground.
english_example: When the ice melted, it formed a puddle.
spanish_meaning: Pequeña acumulación de líquido en el suelo.
spanish_example: Cuando se derritió el hielo, se formó un charco.

english_meaning: A response is the answer to a question.
english_example: He asked if I was sad. My response was “No.”
spanish_meaning: Contestación o réplica a una pregunta.
spanish_example: Le preguntó si estaba triste. Mi respuesta fue “No”.

english_meaning: A season is a time of the year: spring, summer, fall, or winter.
english_example: Fall is a warm season, while winter is very cold.
spanish_meaning: período del año como primavera, verano, otoño o invierno.
spanish_example: El otoño es una estación cálida, mientras que el invierno es muy frío.

english_meaning: A solution is a way to solve a problem.
english_example: There are many problems. We need solutions!
spanish_meaning: Una forma de resolver un problema.
spanish_example: Hay muchos problemas; necesitamos soluciones.

english_meaning: To waste means to use something carelessly and unnecessarily.
english_example: Turn off the water so you don’t waste it.
spanish_meaning: Usar algo en exceso o innecesariamente.
spanish_example: Cierra el agua para no desperdiciarla.

english_meaning: You use whether when you must choose between two things.
english_example: I could not decide whether to go left or right.
spanish_meaning: Conjunción para indicar dos alternativas.
spanish_example: No podía decidir si ir a la izquierda o a la derecha.

english_meaning: Always means that something happens all the time.
english_example: They always brush their teeth in the morning.
spanish_meaning: En todo momento, siempre.
spanish_example: Siempre se cepillan los dientes por la mañana.

english_meaning: To ask is to say or write something to get an answer.
english_example: Please ask questions if you do not understand.
spanish_meaning: Pedir información o solicitar algo por una pregunta.
spanish_example: Por favor, pregunta si no entiendes.

english_meaning: A banana is a long yellow fruit with soft white flesh inside.
english_example: Did you eat a banana for breakfast?
spanish_meaning: Fruta alargada y amarilla de pulpa blanda.
spanish_example: ¿Comiste un plátano en el desayuno?

english_meaning: Bread is a food made from flour and water.
english_example: You need two pieces of bread to make a sandwich.
spanish_meaning: Alimento hecho con harina y agua, y a menudo se cuece para comer.
spanish_example: Necesitas dos rebanadas de pan para hacer un sándwich.

english_meaning: Cake is a sweet dessert made from flour, water, sugar, and eggs.
english_example: What a beautiful birthday cake!
spanish_meaning: Postre dulce y esponjoso hecho con harina y azúcar.
spanish_example: ¡Qué linda tarta de cumpleaños!

english_meaning: A carrot is an orange vegetable.
english_example: I put a carrot in my salad.
spanish_meaning: Verdura naranja de raíz.
spanish_example: Puse una zanahoria en mi ensalada.

english_meaning: Chicken is a bird that is often used for food.
english_example: Chicken is his favorite kind of meat.
spanish_meaning: Ave domesticada usada como alimento.
spanish_example: El pollo es su tipo favorito de carne.

english_meaning: Chocolate is a sweet food made from cacao beans.
english_example: I made a chocolate cake for my mom’s party.
spanish_meaning: Alimento dulce hecho de cacao y azúcar.
spanish_example: Hice un pastel de chocolate para la fiesta de mamá.

english_meaning: To contain is to hold or have something.
english_example: I have to find something to contain these apples.
spanish_meaning: Guardar o incluir algo dentro.
spanish_example: Necesito encontrar algo para contener estas manzanas.

english_meaning: If a food is delicious, it is tasty.
english_example: I loved the delicious fried chicken I ate for dinner!
spanish_meaning: Que sabe muy bien; sabroso.
spanish_example: Me encantó el pollo frito delicioso que comí anoche.

english_meaning: A diet is the food regularly eaten by a person.
english_example: His diet mostly consists of fruits and vegetables.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto habitual de alimentos que una persona consume.
spanish_example: Su dieta consiste sobre todo en frutas y verduras.

english_meaning: To eat is to chew and swallow food.
english_example: You should eat breakfast every day.
spanish_meaning: Ingerir alimentos masticando y tragándolos.
spanish_example: Deberías comer desayuno todos los días.

english_meaning: Food is things people and animals eat.
english_example: Pasta is a famous food in Italy.
spanish_meaning: Alimentos que se comen.
spanish_example: La pasta es una comida famosa en Italia.

english_meaning: Fruit is a type of healthy food that grows on trees and plants.
english_example: Apples, pears, and oranges are types of fruit.
spanish_meaning: Parte comestible dulce o ácida de plantas y árboles.
spanish_example: Las manzanas, peras y naranjas son frutas.

english_meaning: When something is great, it is very good.
english_example: It was a great, exciting game!
spanish_meaning: Muy bueno o excelente.
spanish_example: Fue un juego excelente y emocionante.

english_meaning: Health is the state of a person’s body.
english_example: People who want good health should not smoke.
spanish_meaning: Estado de bienestar físico y funcional del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Las personas que quieren buena salud no deberían fumar.

english_meaning: A recipe is a set of instructions for cooking a certain type of food.
english_example: Do you use a recipe when you make that sauce?
spanish_meaning: Instrucciones para preparar un alimento.
spanish_example: ¿Usas una receta cuando haces esa salsa?

english_meaning: A restaurant is a business where people sit and eat food.
english_example: Let’s eat at the Indian restaurant.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde se sirve comida para comer.
spanish_example: Vamos a comer al restaurante indio.

english_meaning: If something is special, it is unique and different.
english_example: The cupcake was special because it had blue frosting.
spanish_meaning: Diferente, único o destacado.
spanish_example: El cupcake era especial porque tenía glaseado azul.

english_meaning: Water is a clear liquid that people need to survive.
english_example: Drink eight cups of water every day.
spanish_meaning: Líquido transparente esencial para vivir.
spanish_example: bebé ocho vasos de agua cada día.

english_meaning: Someone or something that is alive is not dead.
english_example: My grandparents are still alive even though they are over 90.
spanish_meaning: Que tiene vida.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos siguen vivos aunque tienen más de 90 años.

english_meaning: A bone is a hard part of the body.
english_example: I brought home a nice bone for my dog.
spanish_meaning: Parte dura del cuerpo animal o humano.
spanish_example: Le llevé un hueso bonito a mi perro.

english_meaning: To bother is to make the effort to do something.
english_example: No one bothered to wash the dishes today.
spanish_meaning: Causar fastidio o incomodidad, o dar trabajo extra.
spanish_example: Nadie se molestó en lavar los platos hoy.

english_meaning: A captain is the person who controls a ship or airplane.
english_example: The captain sailed his ship to Australia.
spanish_meaning: Persona que dirige un barco o avión.
spanish_example: El capitán llevó su barco hacia Australia.

english_meaning: The conclusion of something is the final part of it.
english_example: At the conclusion of the race, the spectators cheered for the winner.
spanish_meaning: Parte final de un proceso o evento.
spanish_example: Al final de la carrera, los espectadores aplaudieron al ganador.

english_meaning: Doubt is a feeling of not being sure.
english_example: I have doubt that the story is true.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de inseguridad sobre algo.
spanish_example: Tengo dudas de que la historia sea verdadera.

english_meaning: To explore is to look for new places.
english_example: He wants to explore the world and see new things.
spanish_meaning: Buscar y conocer lugares nuevos.
spanish_example: Quiere explorar el mundo y ver cosas nuevas.

english_meaning: If you are glad, you are happy.
english_example: I am glad you came to my party.
spanish_meaning: Sentirse contento o satisfecho.
spanish_example: Me alegra que hayas venido a mi fiesta.

english_meaning: However means despite or not being influenced by something.
english_example: She is a great cook. However, she never had professional lessons.
spanish_meaning: No obstante, a pesar de lo anterior.
spanish_example: Ella es una gran cocinera; sin embargo, nunca tuvo clases profesionales.

english_meaning: Injustice is a lack of fairness or justice.
english_example: Putting an innocent person in jail is an act of injustice.
spanish_meaning: Falta de justicia o trato justo.
spanish_example: Poner a una persona inocente en prisión es un acto de injusticia.

english_meaning: If something is international, it involves more than one country.
english_example: The United Nations is a powerful international organization.
spanish_meaning: Que involucra a varios países.
spanish_example: La ONU es una organización internacional poderosa.

english_meaning: A lawyer works with the law and represents people in court.
english_example: The lawyer left the courthouse after the judge made her decision.
spanish_meaning: Profesional que ejerce de representación legal.
spanish_example: El abogado salió del tribunal tras la decisión del juez.

english_meaning: To mention something is to talk about it.
english_example: The doctors mentioned the problems that the patient was having.
spanish_meaning: Hablar o citar algo.
spanish_example: Los médicos mencionaron los problemas que tenía la paciente.

english_meaning: Someone who is old has lived for many years.
english_example: My mother is seventy years old now.
spanish_meaning: Que tiene muchos años de vida.
spanish_example: Mi madre tiene ahora setenta años de edad.

english_meaning: A policy is a rule.
english_example: He told us that his policy was to put customers first.
spanish_meaning: Norma o principio para actuar.
spanish_example: Dijo que su política era poner primero al cliente.

english_meaning: If something is social, it is about many people in a community.
english_example: People should come together and fix the world’s social problems.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con la sociedad y las personas.
spanish_example: La gente debería unirse para resolver problemas sociales.

english_meaning: A speech is something said to a group of people.
english_example: She gave a speech to the class.
spanish_meaning: Texto hablado ante un grupo.
spanish_example: Ella dio un discurso a la clase.

english_meaning: A staff is a group of people working together in a company.
english_example: My dad has a staff of four people to help him at the office.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de trabajadores de una organización.
spanish_example: Mi padre tiene una plantilla de cuatro personas para ayudarle en la oficina.

english_meaning: If you go toward something, you go closer to it.
english_example: Santa walked toward my house with a bag of gifts.
spanish_meaning: En la dirección de un punto.
spanish_example: Santa caminó hacia mi casa con una bolsa de regalos.

english_meaning: Wood is the thing that trees are made of.
english_example: I put the pieces of wood in a pile.
spanish_meaning: Material duro de los árboles.
spanish_example: Puse los trozos de madera en una pila.

english_meaning: To achieve something is to successfully do it after trying hard.
english_example: I was happy that I could achieve my goal.
spanish_meaning: Conseguir algo con esfuerzo.
spanish_example: Me alegré de poder lograr mi objetivo.

english_meaning: To advise someone is to tell them what to do.
english_example: My mother often advises people about their money.
spanish_meaning: Dar recomendaciones sobre qué hacer.
spanish_example: Mi madre suele aconsejar a la gente sobre su dinero.

english_meaning: If something happens already, it happens before a certain time.
english_example: It is already time for the movie to start. Let’s go in.
spanish_meaning: Antes o en ese momento, no después.
spanish_example: Ya es hora de que empiece la película. Entremos.

english_meaning: If something is basic, it is very simple or easy.
english_example: I learned some basic English skills in school today.
spanish_meaning: Lo más simple o fundamental.
spanish_example: Hoy aprendí algunas habilidades básicas de inglés en la escuela.

english_meaning: A bit is a small amount of something.
english_example: I ate a bit of chocolate before I went to bed.
spanish_meaning: Una pequeña cantidad.
spanish_example: Comí un poco de chocolate antes de dormir.

english_meaning: To consider something means to think about it.
english_example: Pete didn’t like his job. He considered getting a new one.
spanish_meaning: Pensar seriamente sobre algo.
spanish_example: Pete no le gustó su trabajo y consideró buscar uno nuevo.

english_meaning: To destroy means to damage something so badly that it cannot be used.
english_example: The glass was destroyed.
spanish_meaning: Dañar algo hasta dejarlo inutilizable.
spanish_example: El vaso fue destruido.

english_meaning: To entertain someone is to do something that they enjoy.
english_example: The clown entertained the kids at the party.
spanish_meaning: Divertir o hacer pasar un buen rato.
spanish_example: El payaso entretuvo a los niños en la fiesta.

english_meaning: If something is extra, it is more than what is needed.
english_example: The squirrel had extra nuts for the winter.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad superior a lo necesario.
spanish_example: La ardilla tenía nueces extra para el invierno.

english_meaning: A goal is something you work toward.
english_example: Her goal was to become a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Meta o propósito al que se apunta.
spanish_example: Su objetivo era convertirse en médico.

english_meaning: To lie is to say or write something untrue to deceive someone.
english_example: Whenever Pinocchio lied to his father, his nose grew.
spanish_meaning: Decir una mentira.
spanish_example: Cada vez que Pinocchio mentía, su nariz crecía.

english_meaning: Meat is food made of animals.
english_example: This piece of meat I’m eating tastes very good.
spanish_meaning: Alimento de origen animal.
spanish_example: Este trozo de carne que como sabe muy bien.

english_meaning: An opinion is a thought about a person or a thing.
english_example: Meg told me her opinion of my story. She said it was not funny.
spanish_meaning: Juicio personal sobre algo.
spanish_example: Meg me dijo su opinión de mi historia y dijo que no era graciosa.

english_meaning: If something is real, it actually exists.
english_example: Some people don’t believe in aliens from space, but I think they’re real.
spanish_meaning: Que existe de verdad.
spanish_example: Algunas personas no creen en extraterrestres, pero yo creo que son reales.

english_meaning: To reflect is when a surface sends back light, heat, sound or an image.
english_example: Her face was reflected in the smooth glass.
spanish_meaning: Devolver luz, sonido o imagen.
spanish_example: Su cara se reflejó en el vidrio liso.

english_meaning: To regard people is to think of them in a certain way.
english_example: The boy regarded the girl as a good friend.
spanish_meaning: Pensar de alguien de cierta manera.
spanish_example: El niño consideraba a la niña como una buena amiga.

english_meaning: To serve someone is to give them food or drinks.
english_example: He served us our drinks quickly.
spanish_meaning: Entregar comida o bebida a alguien.
spanish_example: Nos sirvió las bebidas rápidamente.

english_meaning: A vegetable is a plant used as food.
english_example: Carrots are my favorite vegetable.
spanish_meaning: Planta comestible no dulce usada para comer.
spanish_example: Las zanahorias son mi verdura favorita.

english_meaning: A war is a big fight between two groups of people.
english_example: Many young men died in the war.
spanish_meaning: Conflicto armado entre grupos grandes.
spanish_example: Muchos jóvenes murieron en la guerra.

english_meaning: If something is worth an amount of money, it costs that amount.
english_example: Our house is worth a lot of money.
spanish_meaning: Tener un precio o valor.
spanish_example: Nuestra casa vale mucho dinero.

english_meaning: To appear is to seem.
english_example: She appeared to be sad. She was crying.
spanish_meaning: Darse la impresión de ser algo.
spanish_example: Parecía estar triste; estaba llorando.

english_meaning: The base is the bottom of something.
english_example: The base of the table has three legs.
spanish_meaning: Parte inferior o fundamento.
spanish_example: La base de la mesa tiene tres patas.

english_meaning: The brain is the organ in your head that lets you think.
english_example: You must use your brain to solve the problem.
spanish_meaning: Órgano cerebral donde se piensa.
spanish_example: Debes usar el cerebro para resolver el problema.

english_meaning: A career is a job that you do for a large part of your life.
english_example: He was in the hospitality business for most of his career.
spanish_meaning: Trayectoria profesional prolongada.
spanish_example: Estuvo en el sector hotelero durante la mayor parte de su carrera.

english_meaning: A clerk is a type of worker. Clerks in a store help customers.
english_example: The clerk added up her bill for the groceries.
spanish_meaning: Empleado que atiende al público en una tienda/oficina.
spanish_example: El cajero sumó su cuenta para las compras de comestibles.

english_meaning: Effort is hard work or an attempt to do something.
english_example: He always puts a lot of effort into his studies.
spanish_meaning: Energía física o mental puesta para lograr algo.
spanish_example: Él siempre pone mucho esfuerzo en sus estudios.

english_meaning: To enter a place is to go into it.
english_example: Two guards greeted me as I entered the front door.
spanish_meaning: Pasar al interior de un lugar.
spanish_example: Dos guardias me saludaron cuando entré por la puerta principal.

english_meaning: When something is excellent, it is very good.
english_example: I got an excellent score on my school test.
spanish_meaning: De muy buena calidad.
spanish_example: Saqué una puntuación excelente en mi examen escolar.

english_meaning: A hero is a brave person who does things to help others.
english_example: To children, the man in the blue and red costume was a real hero.
spanish_meaning: Persona valiente que ayuda a otros.
spanish_example: Para los niños, el hombre con traje azul y rojo era un héroe de verdad.

english_meaning: To hurry is to do something quickly.
english_example: I hurried home on my bike.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo con prisa.
spanish_example: Me apresuré a casa en bicicleta.

english_meaning: To inform someone is to tell them about something.
english_example: I called and informed her about my idea.
spanish_meaning: Comunicar información a alguien.
spanish_example: Llamé e informé a ella sobre mi idea.

english_meaning: Later means after the present, expected, or usual time.
english_example: She missed the train, so she’ll arrive a little later than expected.
spanish_meaning: En un momento posterior.
spanish_example: Perdió el tren, así que llegará un poco más tarde de lo esperado.

english_meaning: To leave means to go away from someone or something.
english_example: He packed his bag and was ready to leave for home.
spanish_meaning: Salir o partir de un lugar.
spanish_example: Empacó su bolsa y estuvo listo para irse a casa.

english_meaning: To locate something is to find it.
english_example: I could not locate my keys in the house.
spanish_meaning: Encontrar o situar algo.
spanish_example: No pude localizar mis llaves en la casa.

english_meaning: A nurse is a person who helps sick people in the hospital.
english_example: A nurse helped me get better.
spanish_meaning: Profesional de salud que cuida pacientes.
spanish_example: Una enfermera me ayudó a recuperarme.

english_meaning: An operation is when a doctor replaces or removes something in the body.
english_example: The operation on my arm was a success.
spanish_meaning: Intervención médica quirúrgica.
spanish_example: La operación de mi brazo fue un éxito.

english_meaning: Pain is the feeling that you have when you are hurt.
english_example: His head was full of pain.
spanish_meaning: Sensación de molestia o sufrimiento físico.
spanish_example: Tenía dolor de cabeza.

english_meaning: To refuse something is to say “no” to it.
english_example: The dog refused to play with the cat.
spanish_meaning: Decir que no; no aceptar.
spanish_example: El perro rechazó jugar con el gato.

english_meaning: Though is used when one idea makes another seem surprising.
english_example: Though he was overweight, he liked to be active.
spanish_meaning: Conectores: aunque, a pesar de.
spanish_example: Aunque estaba con sobrepeso, le gustaba estar activo.

english_meaning: If something is various, there are many types of it.
english_example: She owned shoes of various styles.
spanish_meaning: Diversos o de varios tipos.
spanish_example: Tenía zapatos de varios estilos.

english_meaning: Actual means real or true.
english_example: This is the actual sword that the king owned, not a fake one.
spanish_meaning: Que existe y no es falso.
spanish_example: Esta es la espada real que tuvo el rey, no una falsa.

english_meaning: To amaze people is to surprise them very much.
english_example: The news in the paper amazed Jack.
spanish_meaning: Sorprender profundamente.
spanish_example: Las noticias del periódico sorprendieron a Jack.

english_meaning: A charge is the price to pay for something.
english_example: The charge for the shirts was $15.
spanish_meaning: Precio que se paga por un producto o servicio.
spanish_example: La tarifa de las camisas fue de 15 dólares.

english_meaning: To comfort someone means to make them feel better.
english_example: I wanted to comfort my friend after I heard the bad news.
spanish_meaning: Dar alivio o ánimo a alguien.
spanish_example: Quería consolar a mi amiga tras la mala noticia.

english_meaning: To contact people is to speak or write to them.
english_example: I contacted Sue about my party.
spanish_meaning: Ponerse en contacto con alguien.
spanish_example: Contacté a Sue para invitarla a mi fiesta.

english_meaning: A customer is a person who buys something at a store.
english_example: The customer put a few items in a bag.
spanish_meaning: Persona que compra productos o servicios.
spanish_example: El cliente guardó unos artículos en una bolsa.

english_meaning: To deliver something is to take it from one place to another.
english_example: The man delivered Chinese food to my house.
spanish_meaning: Llevar o hacer llegar algo a destino.
spanish_example: El hombre entregó comida china en mi casa.

english_meaning: To earn means to get money for the work you do.
english_example: He earns his living as a chef in a great restaurant.
spanish_meaning: Obtener dinero a cambio de trabajo.
spanish_example: Gana su sustento como chef en un gran restaurante.

english_meaning: A gate is a type of door. Gates are usually made of metal or wood.
english_example: We want to put up a wooden gate around our house.
spanish_meaning: Acceso o cerco con estructura de apertura.
spanish_example: Queremos poner una puerta de madera alrededor de nuestra casa.

english_meaning: To include something means to have it as part of a group.
english_example: Does this meal include a soft drink?
spanish_meaning: Formar parte de un conjunto.
spanish_example: ¿Esta comida incluye una bebida refrescante?

english_meaning: To manage something means to control or be in charge of it.
english_example: I had to manage the meeting myself.
spanish_meaning: Dirigir o administrar algo.
spanish_example: Tuve que gestionar la reunión yo solo.

english_meaning: A mystery is something that is difficult to understand or explain.
english_example: The path on the map was a complete mystery to me.
spanish_meaning: Algo enigmático o difícil de comprender.
spanish_example: El camino en el mapa fue un misterio para mí.

english_meaning: To occur means to happen.
english_example: When did the thunderstorm occur?
spanish_meaning: Suceder o pasar.
spanish_example: ¿Cuándo ocurrió la tormenta?

english_meaning: If A is the opposite of B, A is completely different from B.
english_example: The opposite of black is white.
spanish_meaning: Lo contrario.
spanish_example: Lo opuesto al negro es el blanco.

english_meaning: A plate is a flat round thing that you put food on.
english_example: I put my plate down so I could put some food on it.
spanish_meaning: Recipiente plano para servir comida.
spanish_example: Puse mi plato para ponerle comida.

english_meaning: To receive something is to get it.
english_example: I received a present on my birthday.
spanish_meaning: Obtener algo.
spanish_example: Recibí un regalo en mi cumpleaños.

english_meaning: A reward is something given in exchange for good behavior or work.
english_example: He was given a reward for his excellent performance.
spanish_meaning: Premio por un buen desempeño o conducta.
spanish_example: Le dieron una recompensa por su excelente trabajo.

english_meaning: To set something is to put it somewhere.
english_example: Please set the dice down on the table.
spanish_meaning: Colocar o situar algo.
spanish_example: Por favor, pon los dados sobre la mesa.

english_meaning: To steal is to take something that is not yours.
english_example: The men tried to steal money from the bank.
spanish_meaning: Tomar lo ajeno de forma ilegal.
spanish_example: Los hombres intentaron robar dinero del banco.

english_meaning: A thief is someone who quietly takes things that do not belong to them.
english_example: A thief broke into our home and took my mother’s jewelry.
spanish_meaning: Persona que roba.
spanish_example: Un ladrón entró en nuestra casa y se llevó las joyas de mi madre.

english_meaning: To advance is to go forward.
english_example: He advanced across the bridge slowly.
spanish_meaning: Progresar o pasar hacia delante.
spanish_example: Avanzó lentamente por el puente.

english_meaning: An athlete is a person who plays sports.
english_example: Some athletes can play many sports very well.
spanish_meaning: Persona que practica deportes de forma competitiva.
spanish_example: Algunos atletas pueden jugar muchos deportes muy bien.

english_meaning: If something is average, it is at a normal level.
english_example: I’m not rich or poor; I’m average.
spanish_meaning: Normal o típico.
spanish_example: No soy rico ni pobre; soy promedio.

english_meaning: Your behavior is the way you act.
english_example: Their behavior was good this semester. They didn’t cause trouble.
spanish_meaning: Manera de actuar o comportarse.
spanish_example: Su comportamiento fue bueno este semestre; no causaron problemas.

english_meaning: Behind means to be at the back of something.
english_example: The little girl was hiding behind a tree.
spanish_meaning: En la parte posterior de algo.
spanish_example: La niña se escondía detrás de un árbol.

english_meaning: A course is a class in school.
english_example: I took a P.E. course in school this year.
spanish_meaning: Asignatura o materia.
spanish_example: Este año tomé un curso de educación física.

english_meaning: To lower something is to make it go down.
english_example: The chart shows how his production has lowered over the year.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que algo disminuya o descienda.
spanish_example: El gráfico muestra cómo bajó su producción durante el año.

english_meaning: To match is to be the same or similar.
english_example: The two shoes matched. They looked the same.
spanish_meaning: Ser parecido o corresponder.
spanish_example: Los dos zapatos coincidían.

english_meaning: A member is a person who is part of a group.
english_example: Julie is the newest member of our team.
spanish_meaning: Persona que pertenece a un grupo.
spanish_example: Julie es la nueva miembro de nuestro equipo.

english_meaning: If something is mental, it has to do with your mind.
english_example: I made a mental picture of the room.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con la mente o la inteligencia.
spanish_example: Me hice una imagen mental de la habitación.

english_meaning: A passenger is a person who rides in a car, train, or airplane.
english_example: One passenger was standing near the subway train.
spanish_meaning: Persona que viaja en transporte.
spanish_example: Un pasajero estaba de pie cerca del tren del metro.

english_meaning: Your personality is what you are like and how you behave.
english_example: John has a bad personality.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de rasgos de carácter y conducta.
spanish_example: John tiene una mala personalidad.

english_meaning: A poem is a short kind of writing.
english_example: William Shakespeare wrote many poems.
spanish_meaning: Texto literario breve con ritmo y emociones.
spanish_example: William Shakespeare escribió muchos poemas.

english_meaning: A pole is a long thin stick made of wood or metal that supports things.
english_example: The flag was hanging from the flag pole.
spanish_meaning: Varilla larga que sirve de soporte.
spanish_example: La bandera colgaba del poste.

english_meaning: To remove something is to take it away.
english_example: I removed the nail from the board.
spanish_meaning: Eliminar o sacar algo de su lugar.
spanish_example: Quité el clavo del tablero.

english_meaning: Safety means the condition of being safe and free from danger.
english_example: For his own safety, he was placed in a car seat.
spanish_meaning: Estado de protección ante riesgos.
spanish_example: Por su seguridad, lo pusieron en una silla del coche.

english_meaning: To shoot is to fire something like a bullet at someone or something.
english_example: The hunter raised his gun to shoot at the target.
spanish_meaning: Lanzar un disparo con arma o lanzar algo.
spanish_example: El cazador levantó su arma para disparar al blanco.

english_meaning: To sound means to make a noise.
english_example: The alarm clock sounded and woke us all up.
spanish_meaning: Producir ruido.
spanish_example: El despertador sonó y nos despertó a todos.

english_meaning: To swim is to move through water.
english_example: I love to swim in the ocean.
spanish_meaning: Moverse por el agua con el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Me encanta nadar en el océano.

english_meaning: A web is a home made by a spider.
english_example: Mom cleaned the spider webs out of the garage.
spanish_meaning: Red tejida por una araña.
spanish_example: Mi mamá limpió las telarañas de la cochera.

english_meaning: A block is a solid piece of wood, stone, or ice.
english_example: I saw a block of ice on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Bloque sólido de material.
spanish_example: Vi un bloque de hielo en el suelo.

english_meaning: When you bury something, you put it under the ground or under a lot of other things.
english_example: My father was buried in his hometown when he died.
spanish_meaning: Cubrir con tierra u ocultar debajo.
spanish_example: Mi padre fue enterrado en su ciudad natal cuando murió.

english_meaning: To cheer is to give a loud shout of approval or encouragement.
english_example: The crowd all cheered when the home team won.
spanish_meaning: Aplaudir o alentar en voz alta.
spanish_example: La multitud animó cuando el equipo local ganó.

english_meaning: If something is complex, it has many small parts. It is hard to understand.
english_example: A jigsaw puzzle can be complex because it has so many pieces.
spanish_meaning: Compuesto y difícil de entender.
spanish_example: Un rompecabezas puede ser complejo porque tiene muchas piezas.

english_meaning: A critic is someone who gives opinions about movies, books, plays, etc.
english_example: The wine critic tasted the wine so he could give his opinion.
spanish_meaning: Persona que evalúa y opina sobre obras o productos.
spanish_example: El crítico de vinos degustó el vino para dar su opinión.

english_meaning: A direction is the way to go.
english_example: Go in this direction for 10 minutes, and you will get to the river.
spanish_meaning: Camino o rumbo a seguir.
spanish_example: Ve en está dirección durante 10 minutos y llegarás al río.

english_meaning: An event is something that happens, especially something important.
english_example: Finishing high school was a major event in his life.
spanish_meaning: Suceso importante.
spanish_example: Terminar la secundaria fue un gran evento en su vida.

english_meaning: To exercise is to run or play sports so that you can be healthy.
english_example: You should exercise every day.
spanish_meaning: Hacer actividad física para mantener la salud.
spanish_example: Debes hacer ejercicio todos los días.

english_meaning: Friendship is the relationship between people who are friends.
english_example: Michael and Lisa have a very strong friendship with each other.
spanish_meaning: Relación de compañerismo entre amigos.
spanish_example: Michael y Lisa tienen una amistad muy fuerte entre ellos.

english_meaning: A guide is someone who shows you where to go.
english_example: We followed a guide at the park.
spanish_meaning: Persona o recurso que orienta el camino.
spanish_example: Seguimos a una guía en el parque.

english_meaning: If there is a lack of something, there is not enough of it.
english_example: His only problem is a lack of money.
spanish_meaning: Ausencia o insuficiencia de algo.
spanish_example: Su único problema es la falta de dinero.

english_meaning: To perform is to do something in front of people who watch.
english_example: He will perform a song for the class.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo ante una audiencia.
spanish_example: Él interpretará una canción para la clase.

english_meaning: Pressure is what you apply to make someone do something.
english_example: They put pressure on him to change his mind.
spanish_meaning: Fuerza o influencia para que alguien actúe.
spanish_example: Pusieron presión para que cambiara de opinión.

english_meaning: If something will probably happen, it is likely to happen.
english_example: You will probably get a good grade if you study for the test.
spanish_meaning: Con alta probabilidad de ocurrir.
spanish_example: Probablemente sacarás buena nota si estudias para el examen.

english_meaning: If something is public, it is meant for everyone to use.
english_example: I went to the public park to play with my friends.
spanish_meaning: Que es de uso o acceso común.
spanish_example: Fui al parque público a jugar con mis amigos.

english_meaning: Smart means intelligent.
english_example: Mary is a smart student.
spanish_meaning: Que posee capacidad de aprender bien.
spanish_example: María es una estudiante inteligente.

english_meaning: To strike people or things is to hit them.
english_example: She struck the other girl in the face.
spanish_meaning: Dar un golpe a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Ella golpeó a la otra niña en la cara.

english_meaning: To support something is to like it and help it be successful.
english_example: Everyone at work supports the new plan.
spanish_meaning: Respaldar o favorecer una idea o persona.
spanish_example: Todos en el trabajo apoyan el nuevo plan.

english_meaning: A term is a word for something.
english_example: I often use the term “oops” when I make a mistake.
spanish_meaning: Palabra o expresión de una lengua.
spanish_example: A menudo uso el término “uy” cuando cometo un error.

english_meaning: To unite is to get together to do something.
english_example: If we unite, we can finish our project faster.
spanish_meaning: Unirse o juntar fuerzas.
spanish_example: Si nos unimos, terminaremos el proyecto más rápido.

english_meaning: To associate means to connect something with a person or thing.
english_example: Most people associate birthday parties with having fun.
spanish_meaning: Vincular una idea con otra.
spanish_example: La mayoría asocia las fiestas de cumpleaños con la diversión.

english_meaning: The environment is the place where people work or live.
english_example: Keeping our environment clean is important to our health.
spanish_meaning: Ambiente o medio donde vive la gente.
spanish_example: Mantener limpio nuestro entorno es importante para la salud.

english_meaning: A factory is a building where things are made or put together.
english_example: We have only one factory in our town.
spanish_meaning: Lugar industrial donde se producen bienes.
spanish_example: Tenemos solo una fábrica en nuestra ciudad.

english_meaning: A feature is an important part of something.
english_example: The cell phone has many features.
spanish_meaning: Atributo o cualidad destacada.
spanish_example: El teléfono móvil tiene muchas características.

english_meaning: An instance is an example of something.
english_example: I have never experienced an instance of hate. Have you?
spanish_meaning: Un ejemplo o caso particular.
spanish_example: Nunca he vivido una instancia de odio. ¿La has vivido?

english_meaning: To involve means to include as an active participant.
english_example: The whole family was involved in playing the game.
spanish_meaning: Incluir o hacer participar activamente.
spanish_example: Toda la familia estaba involucrada en el juego.

english_meaning: Medicine is something you take to feel better or treat an illness.
english_example: The doctor gave me medicine for my cold.
spanish_meaning: Medicamento para tratar una dolencia.
spanish_example: El médico me dio medicina para el resfriado.

english_meaning: A mix is different things put together.
english_example: The green mix we made in science class spilled onto the table.
spanish_meaning: Combinación de elementos distintos.
spanish_example: La mezcla verde que hicimos en clase se derramó en la mesa.

english_meaning: To organize is to plan or get ready for an event.
english_example: Make a list to help you organize the things you need.
spanish_meaning: Planificar o poner orden en algo.
spanish_example: Haz una lista para organizar lo que necesitas.

english_meaning: A period is an amount of time when something happens.
english_example: In one period in Europe, there were many knights.
spanish_meaning: Lapso o fase de tiempo.
spanish_example: En una época de Europa había muchos caballeros.

english_meaning: If people populate an area, they live there.
english_example: Billions of people populate the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Habitar o llenar un lugar con población.
spanish_example: Miles de millones de personas habitan la Tierra.

english_meaning: To produce something is to make or grow it.
english_example: This tree produces apples every year.
spanish_meaning: Crear o cosechar bienes o productos.
spanish_example: Este árbol produce manzanas cada año.

english_meaning: A range is a number or a set of similar things.
english_example: I saw a range of cars to choose from.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de valores o variedad de opciones.
spanish_example: Vi una gama de coches para elegir.

english_meaning: To recognize something is to know it because you have seen it before.
english_example: I recognized an old friend from many years ago.
spanish_meaning: Identificar algo o a alguien por experiencia previa.
spanish_example: Reconocí a un viejo amigo de hace muchos años.

english_meaning: If something is regular, it happens often and in equal amounts of time.
english_example: Our regular lunch time is around noon.
spanish_meaning: Habitual o constante.
spanish_example: Nuestra hora de almuerzo regular es alrededor del mediodía.

english_meaning: A sign is an indication giving information, directions, a warning, etc.
english_example: The sign indicated that today would be a bad day.
spanish_meaning: Indicación visual o aviso.
spanish_example: La señal indicó que hoy sería un mal día.

english_meaning: A tip is a pointed end of something.
english_example: The tip of his pen was very sharp.
spanish_meaning: Extremo puntiagudo.
spanish_example: La punta de su bolígrafo era muy afilada.

english_meaning: A tradition is something people have been doing for a long time.
english_example: Marriage is a tradition all over the world.
spanish_meaning: Práctica repetida históricamente en una cultura.
spanish_example: El matrimonio es una tradición en todo el mundo.

english_meaning: Trash is waste material or unwanted or worthless things.
english_example: Please take out the trash; it smells bad.
spanish_meaning: Desechos o residuos sin uso.
spanish_example: Por favor saca la basura; huele mal.

english_meaning: If something is wide, it is large from side to side.
english_example: The door was as wide as my arms.
spanish_meaning: Con gran medida de lado a lado.
spanish_example: La puerta era tan ancha como mis brazos.

english_meaning: Advice is an opinion about what to do.
english_example: I don’t know how to study for my exams. Can you give me some advice?
spanish_meaning: Sugerencia para actuar bien.
spanish_example: No sé cómo estudiar para mis exámenes. ¿Me das un consejo?

english_meaning: Along means down the length of a road, river, etc.
english_example: Walk along this tunnel for ten minutes, and you’ll see a door on the left.
spanish_meaning: Recorrer la extensión de un lugar.
spanish_example: Camina a lo largo de este túnel durante diez minutos y verás una puerta a la izquierda.

english_meaning: Attention is the notice, thought, or consideration of someone.
english_example: His work got the attention of two of his co-workers.
spanish_meaning: Concentración o interés en algo.
spanish_example: Su trabajo llamó la atención de dos compañeros.

english_meaning: To attract means to make a person or thing come closer or be interested.
english_example: The magnet attracted the metal.
spanish_meaning: Llevar algo o alguien hacia uno por interés o fuerza.
spanish_example: El imán atrajo al metal.

english_meaning: To climb means to use your hands and feet to go up something.
english_example: The girls climbed to the top of the mountain.
spanish_meaning: Subir usando manos y pies.
spanish_example: Las niñas subieron a la cima de la montaña.

english_meaning: To drop is to fall or allow something to fall.
english_example: A small amount of water dropped from the bottle.
spanish_meaning: Descender o dejar caer algo.
spanish_example: Cayó un poco de agua de la botella.

english_meaning: If something is final, it is the last part.
english_example: In the final part of the film, the man and the woman got married.
spanish_meaning: Último o definitivo.
spanish_example: En la parte final de la película, el hombre y la mujer se casaron.

english_meaning: Further means at or from a greater distance or time.
english_example: The escalator is further than I thought.
spanish_meaning: Más lejos o además, en mayor grado.
spanish_example: La escalera mecánica está más lejos de lo que pensé.

english_meaning: To imply something is to suggest it without saying it.
english_example: The man implied that he wanted the job, but he didn’t say so.
spanish_meaning: Dar a entender algo sin decirlo claramente.
spanish_example: El hombre insinuó que quería el trabajo, pero no lo dijo abiertamente.

english_meaning: To maintain means to make something stay the same.
english_example: The balls maintain constant movement.
spanish_meaning: Conservar sin cambiar.
spanish_example: Las bolas mantienen un movimiento constante.

english_meaning: You use neither to connect two negative possibilities.
english_example: Neither the path on the left nor the path on the right will lead us home.
spanish_meaning: Negación de ambas opciones.
spanish_example: Ni el camino de la izquierda ni el de la derecha nos llevarán a casa.

english_meaning: Otherwise means in another way if you don’t do this.
english_example: It’s good to stay active; otherwise, you’ll gain weight.
spanish_meaning: En caso contrario.
spanish_example: Es bueno mantenerse activo; de lo contrario, subirás de peso.

english_meaning: If something is physical, it is related to your body and not your mind.
english_example: Biking is good for your physical health.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Andar en bicicleta es bueno para la salud física.

english_meaning: To prove something is to show that it is true.
english_example: My teacher proved the answer on the board.
spanish_meaning: Mostrar evidencia de la verdad de algo.
spanish_example: Mi profesora demostró la respuesta en la pizarra.

english_meaning: To react is to respond by acting in a certain way.
english_example: James reacted badly to the news.
spanish_meaning: Responder con una acción o emoción.
spanish_example: James reaccionó mal a la noticia.

english_meaning: To ride something is to travel on it. You can ride an animal, a bike, etc.
english_example: I will ride a roller coaster for the first time today.
spanish_meaning: Subirse y desplazarse sobre algo.
spanish_example: Hoy montaré en montaña rusa por primera vez.

english_meaning: If something is situated somewhere, it is in that place.
english_example: The whiteboard is situated between the two men.
spanish_meaning: Estar ubicado en un lugar.
spanish_example: La pizarra está situada entre los dos hombres.

english_meaning: Society is a group of people who interact and share a culture.
english_example: Society expects people to be good and honest.
spanish_meaning: Comunidad organizada de personas.
spanish_example: La sociedad espera que la gente sea buena y honesta.

english_meaning: A standard is what people consider normal or good.
english_example: This older model TV is below our store’s standards.
spanish_meaning: Lo que se considera normal o aceptable.
spanish_example: Este televisor antiguo está por debajo de nuestros estándares.

english_meaning: To suggest something means to give an idea or plan about it.
english_example: He suggested that we go to see his boss.
spanish_meaning: Proponer una idea o plan.
spanish_example: Sugirió que fuéramos a ver a su jefe.

english_meaning: Actually means in fact or really.
english_example: My dad looks a little mean, but actually he’s very kind.
spanish_meaning: En realidad, de verdad.
spanish_example: Mi papá parece un poco severo, pero en realidad es muy amable.

english_meaning: Bite is the act of using your teeth to cut and tear into something.
english_example: The boy took a big bite of his hamburger.
spanish_meaning: Cortar con los dientes.
spanish_example: El niño dio un gran mordisco a su hamburguesa.

english_meaning: The coast is the land by an ocean.
english_example: I stayed on the southern coast of Australia.
spanish_meaning: Faja costera junto al mar.
spanish_example: Me quedé en la costa sur de Australia.

english_meaning: A deal is an agreement that you have with another person.
english_example: I made a deal with the other company to give us some money.
spanish_meaning: Convenio entre dos o más partes.
spanish_example: Hice un acuerdo con la otra empresa para que nos dieran dinero.

english_meaning: The desert is an area of land without many plants or water.
english_example: Not many plants grow in the desert.
spanish_meaning: Zona árida con poca agua y vegetación.
spanish_example: No crecen muchas plantas en el desierto.

english_meaning: If something is effective, it works well.
english_example: Swimming is an effective way to stay healthy.
spanish_meaning: Que produce el efecto esperado.
spanish_example: Nadar es una forma efectiva de mantenerse saludable.

english_meaning: To examine something is to look at it carefully.
english_example: The doctor examined my eyes today.
spanish_meaning: Observar con atención para evaluar.
spanish_example: El médico examinó mis ojos hoy.

english_meaning: If something is false, it is not correct.
english_example: If you think the answer is false, press the red button.
spanish_meaning: Que no es cierto o auténtico.
spanish_example: Si crees que la respuesta es falsa, pulsa el botón rojo.

english_meaning: When you figure something out, you come to understand it and find an answer.
english_example: I couldn’t figure out what he wanted me to do.
spanish_meaning: Comprender y hallar una solución o respuesta.
spanish_example: No pude resolver qué quería que hiciera.

english_meaning: A gift is something you give someone.
english_example: Dave received many gifts for Christmas.
spanish_meaning: Una cosa que se da a alguien.
spanish_example: Dave recibió muchos regalos por Navidad.

english_meaning: Hunger is the feeling that you get when you need to eat.
english_example: After playing all day long, he was filled with hunger.
spanish_meaning: La necesidad de comer.
spanish_example: Después de jugar todo el día, él tenía mucha hambre.

english_meaning: To imagine something is to think of it in your mind.
english_example: Sally imagined herself winning lots of money.
spanish_meaning: Formar una imagen o idea en la mente.
spanish_example: Sally se imaginó ganando mucho dinero.

english_meaning: A journey is a long trip.
english_example: I went on a journey across the country with my parents.
spanish_meaning: Un desplazamiento largo.
spanish_example: Hice un viaje por todo el país con mis padres.

english_meaning: A puzzle is something that is hard to understand.
english_example: The question was a puzzle to him.
spanish_meaning: Algo difícil de comprender.
spanish_example: La pregunta fue un enigma para él.

english_meaning: Quite means that something is a certain way completely or very much.
english_example: I think typing on a keyboard is quite easy.
spanish_meaning: Totalmente o en gran medida.
spanish_example: Creo que escribir en un teclado es bastante fácil.

english_meaning: Rather is used when you want to do one thing but not the other.
english_example: I would rather have the red one than the blue one.
spanish_meaning: Elegir una cosa en lugar de otra.
spanish_example: Prefiero el rojo en lugar del azul.

english_meaning: If something is specific, it is precise or exact.
english_example: Please choose a specific place on the map.
spanish_meaning: Preciso o exacto.
spanish_example: Por favor elige un lugar específico en el mapa.

english_meaning: A spider is a small creature with eight legs.
english_example: Some adults have a fear of little creatures such as spiders.
spanish_meaning: Un pequeño animal de ocho patas.
spanish_example: Algunos adultos tienen miedo de criaturas como las arañas.

english_meaning: A tour is a short trip in which you see many sights.
english_example: I took a tour of Asia and Europe.
spanish_meaning: Viaje corto para ver muchos lugares.
spanish_example: Hice una excursión por Asia y Europa.

english_meaning: A trip is a journey to a certain place.
english_example: Ken took a trip to the city yesterday.
spanish_meaning: Un desplazamiento a un lugar.
spanish_example: Ken hizo un viaje a la ciudad ayer.

english_meaning: A band is a group of people who play music.
english_example: My brother is in a rock band.
spanish_meaning: Grupo de personas que tocan música.
spanish_example: Mi hermano está en una banda de rock.

english_meaning: Barely means by the smallest amount, almost not.
english_example: I barely had enough money to pay for my bus ticket.
spanish_meaning: De manera mínima; casi no.
spanish_example: Apenas tenía dinero suficiente para pagar mi boleto de autobús.

english_meaning: If something is boring, it is not fun.
english_example: I think the Internet is boring.
spanish_meaning: No divertido.
spanish_example: Creo que Internet es aburrido.

english_meaning: To cancel means to decide that an event or a request will not happen.
english_example: She canceled the rest of her plans because of the rain.
spanish_meaning: Decidir que algo no se hará.
spanish_example: Ella canceló el resto de sus planes por la lluvia.

english_meaning: A driveway is a short private road that leads to a person’s home.
english_example: The long driveway led us to their new house.
spanish_meaning: Un acceso privado corto hacia una casa.
spanish_example: El largo camino de entrada nos llevó hasta su casa.

english_meaning: Garbage is waste material like unwanted or spoiled food, bottles, paper, etc.
english_example: The boy cleaned up the garbage around his house.
spanish_meaning: Residuos o desechos.
spanish_example: El niño limpió la basura alrededor de su casa.

english_meaning: An instrument is something designed to do a certain task like play music.
english_example: My favorite musical instrument is the piano.
spanish_meaning: Objeto diseñado para una tarea.
spanish_example: Mi instrumento musical favorito es el piano.

english_meaning: A list is a record of information printed with an item on each line.
english_example: My mom makes a list of groceries to buy.
spanish_meaning: Un registro de información.
spanish_example: Mi mamá hace una lista de provisiones para comprar.

english_meaning: Magic is the power to do impossible things.
english_example: The magician used magic to pull a rabbit out of his hat.
spanish_meaning: Capacidad de hacer cosas imposibles.
spanish_example: El mago usó magia para sacar un conejo de su sombrero.

english_meaning: A message is a set of words that you send to someone.
english_example: I left a message for you in the envelope.
spanish_meaning: Un texto corto que se envía a alguien.
spanish_example: Dejé un mensaje para ti en el sobre.

english_meaning: To notice something is to see it for the first time.
english_example: Did you notice the view?
spanish_meaning: Ver o percibir algo por primera vez.
spanish_example: ¿Notaste el paisaje?

english_meaning: To own something means to have it. That thing belongs to you.
english_example: My grandfather owns that house.
spanish_meaning: Tener algo o que te pertenezca.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo posee esa casa.

english_meaning: To predict something is to say that it will happen.
english_example: She predicted that I would get married next year.
spanish_meaning: Decir que algo va a suceder.
spanish_example: Ella predijo que me casaría el próximo año.

english_meaning: A professor is a person who teaches in college.
english_example: Mike’s science professor knows a lot about physics.
spanish_meaning: Persona que enseña en la universidad.
spanish_example: El profesor de ciencias de Mike sabe mucho de física.

english_meaning: To rush is to go somewhere or do something very quickly.
english_example: Nancy rushed to finish her homework.
spanish_meaning: Ir o hacer algo muy rápido.
spanish_example: Nancy se apresuró para terminar su tarea.

english_meaning: A schedule is a plan that tells you when to do things.
english_example: What is your class schedule for today?
spanish_meaning: Plan que indica cuándo hacer cosas.
spanish_example: ¿Cuál es tu horario de clases para hoy?

english_meaning: To share something is to give some of it to another person.
english_example: Jimmy shared his apple with me.
spanish_meaning: Dar una parte de algo a otra persona.
spanish_example: Jimmy compartió su manzana conmigo.

english_meaning: A stage is a place where actors or musicians act or sing.
english_example: A large screen was on the stage.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde actúan actores o músicos.
spanish_example: Había una gran pantalla en el escenario.

english_meaning: A storm is very bad weather. There is a lot of rain or snow.
english_example: Did that storm wake you up last night?
spanish_meaning: Clima muy malo con lluvia o nieve.
spanish_example: ¿La tormenta te despertó anoche?

english_meaning: You use within to say that something is inside another thing.
english_example: Within the box, there was a pizza.
spanish_meaning: En el interior de algo.
spanish_example: Dentro de la caja había una pizza.

english_meaning: A burden is a serious or difficult responsibility.
english_example: Children who do not behave are a burden to their parents.
spanish_meaning: Responsabilidad seria o difícil.
spanish_example: Los niños que no se portan bien son una carga para sus padres.

english_meaning: To compromise is to agree to something that is not exactly what you want.
english_example: We both compromised about the game we decided to play.
spanish_meaning: Aceptar algo que no es exactamente lo que quieres.
spanish_example: Ambos hicimos concesiones sobre el juego que decidimos.

english_meaning: To craft something is to make it using skill.
english_example: She crafted the bookcase out of solid pine wood and then painted it.
spanish_meaning: Crear algo usando habilidad.
spanish_example: Ella fabricó una estantería de madera de pino y la pintó.

english_meaning: When you dive into water, you go down head first into the water.
english_example: I like sky-diving from an airplane.
spanish_meaning: Entrar en el agua de golpe y de cabeza.
spanish_example: Me gusta zambullirme al agua desde un avión.

english_meaning: When people or things are fragile, they are not strong and can be damaged easily.
english_example: The fragile glassware was carefully packed into boxes.
spanish_meaning: Que se daña con facilidad.
spanish_example: La vajilla frágil se empaquetó con cuidado.

english_meaning: If you cut something into two parts that are the same size, you cut it half.
english_example: The machine can divide the book in half.
spanish_meaning: La parte que divide algo en dos.
spanish_example: La máquina puede dividir el libro en dos mitades.

english_meaning: Innocence is a lack of experience of difficult or complex things in life.
english_example: Everyone who met her found her innocence to be charming.
spanish_meaning: Falta de experiencia de cosas difíciles.
spanish_example: Todos encontraron su inocencia encantadora.

english_meaning: When you lead something, you go first and it follows you.
english_example: I will lead you to the right place.
spanish_meaning: Ir primero para que otros te sigan.
spanish_example: Te guiaré hacia el lugar correcto.

english_meaning: When you have a meeting, you are with other people.
english_example: We have lots of meetings at our school to plan what to do.
spanish_meaning: Encuentro con otras personas.
spanish_example: Tenemos muchas reuniones en la escuela para planear qué hacer.

english_meaning: To merge two things is to combine them into one whole thing.
english_example: The storm clouds merged into one large menacing cloud that filled the sky.
spanish_meaning: Unir dos cosas en una sola.
spanish_example: Las nubes de tormenta se fusionaron en una nube grande.

english_meaning: When something is moderate, it is not too big or too small in size or amount.
english_example: It takes a moderate amount of patience to be around small children all day.
spanish_meaning: No demasiado intenso ni extremo.
spanish_example: Se necesita paciencia moderada para estar con niños todo el día.

english_meaning: To overwhelm is to exist in such a large amount that someone cannot deal with it.
english_example: The amount of homework her teacher assigned has overwhelmed her.
spanish_meaning: Afectar en exceso a alguien.
spanish_example: La cantidad de tarea le abrumó.

english_meaning: When you make a payment, you give someone some money for what you get.
english_example: I made the last payment on my car yesterday. Now I am really the owner.
spanish_meaning: Entregar dinero por algo.
spanish_example: Hice el último pago de mi coche ayer.

english_meaning: A perception of a situation is a way of thinking about it or understanding it.
english_example: Since he couldn’t see, his perception of life was much different than mine.
spanish_meaning: Manera de entender una situación.
spanish_example: Como no podía ver, su percepción de la vida era distinta a la mía.

english_meaning: When you settle something, you reach an agreement.
english_example: We settled the argument by looking up the answer on the web.
spanish_meaning: Llegar a un acuerdo.
spanish_example: Arreglamos la discusión mirando la respuesta en la web.

english_meaning: A shiver is a shaking movement the body makes when someone is cold or scared.
english_example: I got shivers on my way home because it was so cold.
spanish_meaning: Temblor del cuerpo por frío o miedo.
spanish_example: Tuve escalofríos al regresar a casa porque hacía frío.

english_meaning: When someone is sociable, they are friendly.
english_example: Many of my good friends are sociable, but I am shy.
spanish_meaning: Amigable y de trato fácil.
spanish_example: Muchos de mis buenos amigos son sociables, pero yo soy tímido.

english_meaning: When you speed, you go very fast.
english_example: Speeding is the cause of most car accidents.
spanish_meaning: Ir muy rápido.
spanish_example: Conducir rápido es la causa de la mayoría de accidentes.

english_meaning: When someone is talkative, he or she talks a lot.
english_example: My aunt is very talkative whenever she is on the phone.
spanish_meaning: Que habla mucho.
spanish_example: Mi tía es muy habladora cuando está al teléfono.

english_meaning: When something is usual, it is what we expect.
english_example: It was just the usual people who came.
spanish_meaning: Lo que se espera o ocurre seguido.
spanish_example: Fue la gente habitual la que vino.

english_meaning: If something is above, it is at a higher level than something else.
english_example: He straightened the sign that was above the crowd.
spanish_meaning: En un nivel más alto.
spanish_example: Arregló el cartel que estaba encima de la multitud.

english_meaning: If something is ahead of something else, it is in front of it.
english_example: The blue car drove on ahead of us.
spanish_meaning: Por delante o más adelantado.
spanish_example: El coche azul iba delante de nosotros.

english_meaning: An amount is how much there is of something.
english_example: Can I use my card to pay for the entire amount?
spanish_meaning: La cantidad total de algo.
spanish_example: ¿Puedo pagar el importe total con mi tarjeta?

english_meaning: A belief is a strong feeling that something is correct or true.
english_example: A preacher or priest should have a strong belief in God.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de que algo es verdadero.
spanish_example: Un predicador debe tener una fuerte creencia en Dios.

english_meaning: The center of something is the middle of it.
english_example: The center of a dart board is the most important spot.
spanish_meaning: La parte media.
spanish_example: El centro de un tablero de dardos es el punto más importante.

english_meaning: If something is common, it happens often or there is much of it.
english_example: It is common for snow to fall in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Que ocurre a menudo o hay mucho.
spanish_example: Es común que nieve en invierno.

english_meaning: To cost is to require payment.
english_example: These designer shoes cost more than the regular ones.
spanish_meaning: Requerir dinero.
spanish_example: Estos zapatos de diseñador cuestan más que los normales.

english_meaning: To demonstrate something is to show how it is done.
english_example: She demonstrated her plan to her co-workers.
spanish_meaning: Mostrar cómo se hace algo.
spanish_example: Ella mostró su plan a sus compañeras de trabajo.

english_meaning: Different describes someone or something that is not the same as others.
english_example: Each of my sisters has a different hair style.
spanish_meaning: No igual a otro.
spanish_example: Cada una de mis hermanas tiene un peinado diferente.

english_meaning: Evidence is a fact or thing that you use to prove something.
english_example: He used the pictures as evidence that UFOs are real.
spanish_meaning: Prueba o hecho que demuestra algo.
spanish_example: Usó las fotos como evidencia de que los OVNIs son reales.

english_meaning: Honesty means the quality of being truthful or honest.
english_example: A courtroom should be a place of honesty.
spanish_meaning: Cualidad de ser sincero.
spanish_example: Una corte debe ser un lugar de honestidad.

english_meaning: An idiom is a phrase with a meaning different from its words.
english_example: The idiom “when pigs fly” means that something will never happen.
spanish_meaning: Expresión con significado distinto a su literal.
spanish_example: El modismo “cuando vuelan los cerdos” significa que algo nunca pasará.

english_meaning: If something is independent, it is not controlled by something else.
english_example: She chose to live an independent life in the country.
spanish_meaning: Que no depende de otro.
spanish_example: Ella eligió vivir de forma independiente en el campo.

english_meaning: Inside means the inner part, space or side of something.
english_example: The inside of the box was empty.
spanish_meaning: Parte o espacio interno.
spanish_example: El interior de la caja estaba vacío.

english_meaning: A jail is a place to keep bad people.
english_example: He was sent to jail for taking other people’s cars.
spanish_meaning: Lugar para encerrar personas castigadas.
spanish_example: Lo enviaron a la cárcel por robar coches.

english_meaning: A master is a person who is very good at something.
english_example: My brother is a master of taekwondo.
spanish_meaning: Persona muy experta en algo.
spanish_example: Mi hermano es maestro de taekwondo.

english_meaning: A memory is something you remember.
english_example: The memory of my first time in the city will always be the best.
spanish_meaning: Capacidad de recordar.
spanish_example: Mi memoria de la primera vez en la ciudad será la mejor siempre.

english_meaning: A pocket is a part of your clothing where you can keep things.
english_example: She always had her hands in her pockets.
spanish_meaning: Parte de la ropa para guardar cosas.
spanish_example: Ella siempre tenía las manos en los bolsillos.

english_meaning: If something is proper, it is right.
english_example: It is not proper to throw your garbage on the road.
spanish_meaning: Lo que es correcto o adecuado.
spanish_example: No es apropiado tirar la basura en la carretera.

english_meaning: If something is for sale, you can buy it.
english_example: Everything for sale here is the same price.
spanish_meaning: Que puede comprarse.
spanish_example: Todo lo que está en venta aquí tiene el mismo precio.

english_meaning: Because introduces a reason for something.
english_example: We need to study because we have a test tomorrow.
spanish_meaning: Introduce una causa o motivo.
spanish_example: Tenemos que estudiar porque tenemos examen mañana.

english_meaning: East is the direction the sun rises from.
english_example: My window looks to the east, so I can watch the sunrise every morning.
spanish_meaning: Dirección por donde sale el sol.
spanish_example: Mi ventana mira al este, así que veo el amanecer.

english_meaning: Expensive things cost a lot of money.
english_example: My friend drives an expensive sports car.
spanish_meaning: Que cuesta mucho.
spanish_example: Mi amigo conduce un coche deportivo caro.

english_meaning: A flower is the colored part of a plant.
english_example: She gave pink flowers to her grandmother.
spanish_meaning: Parte coloreada de una planta.
spanish_example: Le dio flores rosas a su abuela.

english_meaning: A garden is an area where people grow plants.
