english_example: The garden is very bright and colorful in the spring.
spanish_meaning: Espacio donde se cultivan plantas.
spanish_example: El jardín es muy colorido en primavera.

english_meaning: A holiday is a special day of celebration.
english_example: Monday was a holiday, so there was no school or work.
spanish_meaning: Día de celebración.
spanish_example: El lunes fue día festivo, así que no había escuela.

english_meaning: Many shows that there is a large number of something.
english_example: There are many people on the street.
spanish_meaning: Un número grande de cosas o personas.
spanish_example: Hay mucha gente en la calle.

english_meaning: A million is another way to write the number 1,000,000.
english_example: Almost 19 million people live in Delhi, India.
spanish_meaning: Número uno seguido de seis ceros.
spanish_example: Casi 19 millones de personas viven en Delhi.

english_meaning: A mountain is a very high hill.
english_example: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.
spanish_meaning: Una colina muy alta.
spanish_example: El Everest es la montaña más alta del mundo.

english_meaning: A place is a space or area.
english_example: A library is a place where people can read books.
spanish_meaning: Espacio o sitio.
spanish_example: Una biblioteca es un lugar donde se leen libros.

english_meaning: A popular thing is liked by many people.
english_example: These people are listening to a popular man speak.
spanish_meaning: Que es querido por mucha gente.
spanish_example: Las personas escuchaban a un conferencista popular.

english_meaning: To ski is to glide on long pieces of wood or metal over snow.
english_example: The man likes to ski and goes every weekend.
spanish_meaning: Bajar sobre nieve con esquís.
spanish_example: Al hombre le gusta esquiar y va cada fin de semana.

english_meaning: Such means “like this”.
english_example: I have never seen such a beautiful sunset before.
spanish_meaning: De ese tipo o de está manera.
spanish_example: Nunca había visto un atardecer tan hermoso.

english_meaning: Total shows that everyone or everything has been counted.
english_example: The total cost of the items she bought was $52.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad o suma completa.
spanish_example: El costo total de los artículos fue 52 dólares.

english_meaning: A tower is a tall, narrow building.
english_example: This is a very famous tower in Italy.
spanish_meaning: Edificio muy alto y estrecho.
spanish_example: Esta es una torre famosa en Italia.

english_meaning: A town is a place where people live and work, and is smaller than a city.
english_example: I come from a small town, and everyone there knows each other.
spanish_meaning: Lugar pequeño de población, más pequeño que una ciudad.
spanish_example: Vengo de un pueblo pequeño y todos se conocen.

english_meaning: A train is a group of railway cars connected together.
english_example: The train is very fast, so we can get home in one hour.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de vagones ferroviarios.
spanish_example: El tren es muy rápido, así que llegamos a casa en una hora.

english_meaning: To walk is to move forward using legs, but it is slower than running.
english_example: The children walk to school in the morning.
spanish_meaning: Moverse hacia adelante con las piernas.
spanish_example: Los niños caminan a la escuela por la mañana.

english_meaning: To watch is to look at someone or something for a period of time.
english_example: My friend came over to watch a movie with me.
spanish_meaning: Mirar algo durante un tiempo.
spanish_example: Mi amigo vino para ver una película conmigo.

english_meaning: The world is the Earth and all the people and things in it.
english_example: What are the names of the five oceans of the world?
spanish_meaning: La Tierra y lo que hay en ella.
spanish_example: ¿Cuáles son los cinco océanos del mundo?

english_meaning: Anxious means feeling worried or nervous.
english_example: She was anxious about not making her appointment on time.
spanish_meaning: Preocupado o nervioso.
spanish_example: Ella estaba ansiosa por no llegar a tiempo.

english_meaning: An awful thing is very bad.
english_example: Her performance last night was awful.
spanish_meaning: Muy malo.
spanish_example: Su actuación anoche fue horrible.

english_meaning: To consist of certain is to be made of parts or things them.
english_example: Today’s choices for lunch consisted of pizza, hamburgers, and hot dogs.
spanish_meaning: Formar parte de algo.
spanish_example: Las opciones de almuerzo consistían en pizza, hamburguesas y hot dogs.

english_meaning: To desire is to want something.
english_example: My sister desires a big house and lots of money.
spanish_meaning: Querer algo con fuerza.
spanish_example: Mi hermana desea una casa grande y mucho dinero.

english_meaning: Eager shows excitement about something.
english_example: The man was eager to talk about the good news.
spanish_meaning: Entusiasmado por algo.
spanish_example: El hombre estaba ansioso por hablar de las buenas noticias.

english_meaning: A household is all the people who live in one house.
english_example: Our household is made up of my father, my mother, and me.
spanish_meaning: Todas las personas que viven en una casa.
spanish_example: Nuestro hogar está formado por mi padre, mi madre y yo.

english_meaning: An intent is a plan to do something.
english_example: Her intent is to visit Italy next summer.
spanish_meaning: Plan de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Su intención es visitar Italia el próximo verano.

english_meaning: A landscape is how an area of land looks.
english_example: The landscape of the country is very green.
spanish_meaning: Apariencia de una zona.
spanish_example: El paisaje del país es muy verde.

english_meaning: To lift something is to move it higher.
english_example: The man tried to lift the box.
spanish_meaning: Subir algo hacia arriba.
spanish_example: El hombre intentó levantar la caja.

english_meaning: To load is to put objects into something.
english_example: The man loaded the boxes into a truck.
spanish_meaning: Poner objetos dentro de otro.
spanish_example: El hombre cargó las cajas en un camión.

english_meaning: A lung is a part of the body that fills with air when breathing.
english_example: Having strong lungs is necessary for a healthy life.
spanish_meaning: Órgano que se llena de aire al respirar.
spanish_example: Tener pulmones fuertes es necesario para una vida sana.

english_meaning: A motion is a movement that someone makes.
english_example: The police officer made a motion with his hand.
spanish_meaning: Un movimiento corporal.
spanish_example: El policía hizo un gesto con su mano.

english_meaning: The pace of something is the speed at which it happens.
english_example: I ran the race at a slower pace than my friend.
spanish_meaning: La velocidad a la que avanza algo.
spanish_example: Corrí la carrera a un ritmo más lento que mi amigo.

english_meaning: Polite shows a thoughtful and kind behavior.
english_example: The boy was very polite; he behaved very thoughtfully.
spanish_meaning: Amable y considerado.
spanish_example: El niño fue muy cortés y se comportó bien.

english_meaning: To possess something is to have it or own it.
english_example: My uncle possesses three sheep, a chicken, a cow, and a dog.
spanish_meaning: Tener o ser dueño de algo.
spanish_example: Mi tío posee tres ovejas, una gallina, una vaca y un perro.

english_meaning: Rapidly means happening very fast.
english_example: The train moved rapidly on the tracks.
spanish_meaning: De forma muy rápida.
spanish_example: El tren se movió rápidamente sobre los rieles.

english_meaning: To remark is to say something.
english_example: The teacher remarked on how quickly the students were learning.
spanish_meaning: Decir algo.
spanish_example: El profesor comentó lo rápido que aprendían los estudiantes.

english_meaning: To seek is to look for something.
english_example: If I have a problem, I seek my sister’s advice.
spanish_meaning: Intentar encontrar algo.
spanish_example: Si tengo un problema, busco el consejo de mi hermana.

english_meaning: To shine is to make a bright light.
english_example: The candles are shining in the dark room.
spanish_meaning: Producir luz brillante.
spanish_example: Las velas brillaban en la habitación oscura.

english_meaning: To spill is to accidentally make something fall out of its container.
english_example: I spilled the coffee on the table.
spanish_meaning: Hacer salir algo de un recipiente.
spanish_example: Derramé el café sobre la mesa.

english_meaning: An arrow is a thin, straight stick shot from a bow.
english_example: The arrow flew through the air and hit the target.
spanish_meaning: Estrella pequeña lanzada con un arco.
spanish_example: La flecha voló por el aire y alcanzó el blanco.

english_meaning: A battle is a fight between two armies during a war.
english_example: The battle lasted for many days.
spanish_meaning: Pelea entre ejércitos en guerra.
spanish_example: La batalla duró muchos días.

english_meaning: A bow is a weapon made of curved wood and string that shoots arrows.
english_example: He went hunting with a bow and arrow.
spanish_meaning: Arma con cuerda para lanzar flechas.
spanish_example: Él fue a cazar con arco y flechas.

english_meaning: A brave person is not afraid to face pain or danger.
english_example: The brave firefighter saved the girl from the burning building.
spanish_meaning: Que no tiene miedo.
spanish_example: El valiente bombero salvó a la niña del edificio en llamas.

english_meaning: A chief is the leader of a group of people.
english_example: The chief led the people through the mountains.
spanish_meaning: La persona que lidera un grupo.
spanish_example: El jefe guió al pueblo por las montañas.

english_meaning: A disadvantage is a situation that makes it hard to do something.
english_example: Mike had a disadvantage in the race since he hurt his knee.
spanish_meaning: Situación que dificulta algo.
spanish_example: Mike tenía desventaja en la carrera por lesionarse la rodilla.

english_meaning: An enemy is a country that is fighting another country during a war.
english_example: The enemy prepared to attack the kingdom.
spanish_meaning: País o persona en guerra con otro.
spanish_example: El enemigo se preparó para atacar el reino.

english_meaning: An entrance is a place where someone can enter an area.
english_example: The gate was locked, so Bill had to find a different entrance.
spanish_meaning: Lugar de acceso a un sitio.
spanish_example: La puerta estaba cerrada, así que Bill buscó otra entrada.

english_meaning: Hardly shows that something happens in a very small way.
english_example: I hardly saw the concert since I had to leave early.
spanish_meaning: Casi no, en muy poca medida.
spanish_example: Apenas vi el concierto, porque tuve que irme temprano.

english_meaning: To intend to do something means to plan to do it.
english_example: I intend to finish college in three years.
spanish_meaning: Planear hacer algo.
spanish_example: Pretendo terminar la universidad en tres años.

english_meaning: Laughter is the sound produced by laughing about something funny.
english_example: Susan’s joke made her classmates burst into laughter.
spanish_meaning: Sonido de reír.
spanish_example: El chiste de Susan hizo reír a la clase.

english_meaning: A log is a thick piece of wood that is cut from a tree.
english_example: The fire was too small, so we added another log to it.
spanish_meaning: Trozo grueso de madera de un árbol.
spanish_example: El fuego era pequeño, así que agregamos otra leña.

english_meaning: The military is the armed forces of a country.
english_example: I joined the military after I finished high school.
spanish_meaning: Fuerzas armadas de un país.
spanish_example: Me uní al ejército tras terminar la secundaria.

english_meaning: To obey means to follow what a law or a person says to do.
english_example: My little sister did not obey my mother. Now, she is in trouble.
spanish_meaning: Hacer lo que dice una ley o una persona.
spanish_example: Mi hermana pequeña no obedeció a mi madre y se metió en problemas.

english_meaning: To secure something means to get it after a lot of effort.
english_example: I was able to secure a good grade on my test after weeks of studying.
spanish_meaning: Lograr algo con mucho esfuerzo.
spanish_example: Pude asegurar una buena nota en mi examen tras estudiar mucho.

english_meaning: Steady shows that someone or something does not change much.
english_example: The problem was hard, but she remained steady and solved it.
spanish_meaning: No cambia mucho; firme.
spanish_example: El problema era difícil, pero ella se mantuvo estable y lo resolvió.

english_meaning: To trust is to believe that someone is honest and will do what is right.
english_example: I trust my friends; they don’t tell my secrets to other people.
spanish_meaning: Creer en la sinceridad de alguien.
spanish_example: Confío en mis amigos; no cuentan mis secretos.

english_meaning: When we twist something we turn it around and around.
english_example: She twisted the spaghetti around her fork.
spanish_meaning: Girar algo repetidamente.
spanish_example: Ella retorció los espaguetis con el tenedor.

english_meaning: Unless means if not or except when.
english_example: Unless you clean your room, you cannot play with your friends.
spanish_meaning: Si no.
spanish_example: A menos que limpies tu cuarto, no puedes jugar con tus amigos.

english_meaning: A weapon is an object used to hurt people.
english_example: Swords have been used as weapons for thousands of years.
spanish_meaning: Objeto usado para herir.
spanish_example: Las espadas han sido usadas como armas por miles de años.

english_meaning: The chest is the front part of a body between the neck and stomach.
english_example: The water in the lake was as high as my chest.
spanish_meaning: Parte del cuerpo entre cuello y estómago.
spanish_example: El agua del lago estaba a la altura de mi pecho.

english_meaning: Confidence is a feeling of certainty or ability.
english_example: I have confidence that I did well on the test.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de certeza o seguridad.
spanish_example: Tengo confianza en que hice bien el examen.

english_meaning: A consequence is a result of a choice or action.
english_example: As a consequence of missing my bus, I had to find another way to work.
spanish_meaning: Resultado de una acción.
spanish_example: Como consecuencia de perder el autobús, tuve que buscar otra forma de ir al trabajo.

english_meaning: A disaster is a really bad thing that happens.
english_example: When the car crashed, it was a disaster.
spanish_meaning: Un hecho muy malo.
spanish_example: Cuando el coche chocó, fue un desastre.

english_meaning: To disturb someone means to upset that person.
english_example: The loud noise disturbed me while I was working.
spanish_meaning: Interrumpir o inquietar.
spanish_example: El ruido fuerte me molestó mientras trabajaba.

english_meaning: To estimate something means to make a guess about it.
english_example: The boy estimated that he was one meter tall.
spanish_meaning: Hacer una suposición.
spanish_example: El chico estimó que medía un metro.

english_meaning: To honor is to show respect for someone or something.
english_example: Each year we honor those who died fighting for their country.
spanish_meaning: Mostrar respeto por alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Cada año honramos a quienes murieron defendiendo su país.

english_meaning: To impress someone means to make that person proud or amazed.
english_example: He was able to impress the girls with his new dance.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que alguien se asombre o admire.
spanish_example: Logró impresionar a las chicas con su nuevo baile.

english_meaning: A narrow object or space is thin, not wide.
english_example: The bridge is too narrow for a car to drive across it.
spanish_meaning: Delgado, con poca anchura.
spanish_example: El puente es demasiado estrecho para pasar un coche.

english_meaning: Pale means that a color or thing is not bright.
english_example: The girl’s skin was very pale.
spanish_meaning: Sin mucho color.
spanish_example: La piel de la chica era muy pálida.

english_meaning: A rough thing is not even or smooth.
english_example: The rough ground hurt my feet.
spanish_meaning: No liso o no suave.
spanish_example: El suelo áspero me lastimó los pies.

english_meaning: To satisfy someone means to make that person happy.
english_example: It will satisfy my teacher if I finish all my homework.
spanish_meaning: Hacer feliz o cumplir a alguien.
spanish_example: Eso me satisfará a mi maestra si terminó toda mi tarea.

english_meaning: To scream means to make a loud noise with your mouth.
english_example: The girl saw a spider and screamed.
spanish_meaning: Hacer un ruido fuerte con la voz.
spanish_example: La chica vio una araña y gritó.

english_meaning: A sensitive person or thing is easily hurt.
english_example: My teeth are sensitive to cold things.
spanish_meaning: Que se afecta con facilidad.
spanish_example: Mis dientes son sensibles al frío.

english_meaning: Shade is a dark area that something makes when it blocks the sun.
english_example: It was hot outside, so the boy sat in the shade of a tree.
spanish_meaning: Área oscura que crea una sombra.
spanish_example: Hacía calor, así que el niño se sentó a la sombra de un árbol.

english_meaning: Strength is the ability to do hard work or exercise.
english_example: Eating good food builds up your strength.
spanish_meaning: Capacidad de hacer esfuerzo.
spanish_example: Comer bien aumenta tu fuerza.

english_meaning: To supplement something is to add something else to it in a good way.
english_example: He supplements his diet with fresh fruits.
spanish_meaning: Suplementar algo es añadirle otra cosa para completarlo o mejorarlo.
spanish_example: Él suplementa su dieta con frutas frescas.

english_meaning: Terror is a feeling of very strong fear.
english_example: I felt a sense of terror when the tiger chased me.
spanish_meaning: Miedo muy intenso.
spanish_example: Sentí terror cuando el tigre me persiguió.

english_meaning: A threat is something bad that might happen.
english_example: Due to the dark clouds, there was a threat of a bad storm.
spanish_meaning: Algo peligroso que puede pasar.
spanish_example: Debido a las nubes oscuras, había amenaza de tormenta.

english_meaning: A victim is a person who is hurt by a bad action.
english_example: I was a victim of a robbery.
spanish_meaning: Persona dañada por una acción mala.
spanish_example: Fui víctima de un robo.

english_meaning: An ancestor is a family member from the past.
english_example: My ancestors came from Germany.
spanish_meaning: Familiar de generaciones pasadas.
spanish_example: Nuestros antepasados vinieron de Alemania.

english_meaning: An angle is the direction from which something is looked at.
english_example: The giraffe turned its head to see from another angle.
spanish_meaning: La dirección desde la que se mira.
spanish_example: La jirafa giró la cabeza para ver desde otro ángulo.

english_meaning: A boot is a heavy shoe that goes up above a person’s ankle.
english_example: He wore boots so that his feet wouldn’t get wet.
spanish_meaning: Calzado pesado que sube al tobillo.
spanish_example: Llevaba botas para que sus pies no se mojaran.

english_meaning: A border is the edge of an area.
english_example: The postcard had a pretty green border of pine needles.
spanish_meaning: Borde o límite.
spanish_example: La postal tenía un borde verde de agujas de pino.

english_meaning: To congratulate someone is to tell them that you are happy for them.
english_example: Bill and Angela congratulated each other on a job well done.
spanish_meaning: Decir a alguien que te alegra su logro.
spanish_example: Bill y Angela se felicitaron por el buen trabajo.

english_meaning: A frame is a border for a picture or mirror.
english_example: I have to get a frame for my friend’s picture.
spanish_meaning: Borde para foto o espejo.
spanish_example: Tengo que comprar un marco para la foto de mi amigo.

english_meaning: Heaven is the place that some believe people go to after they die.
english_example: When I die, I hope that I go to heaven.
spanish_meaning: Lugar donde algunos creen que se va tras morir.
spanish_example: Cuando muera, espero ir al cielo.

english_meaning: An incredible thing is so amazing that it is hard to believe.
english_example: I have an incredible story to tell you about my vacation.
spanish_meaning: Muy asombroso, difícil de creer.
spanish_example: Tengo una historia increíble sobre mis vacaciones.

english_meaning: A legend is a story from the past.
english_example: There is a well-known legend about a king and his queen.
spanish_meaning: Historia del pasado.
spanish_example: Hay una leyenda famosa sobre un rey y su reina.

english_meaning: To praise is to show that you like someone or something.
english_example: The coach praised the athletes after a good practice.
spanish_meaning: Decir que te gusta o admiras algo.
spanish_example: El entrenador elogió a los atletas después del entrenamiento.

english_meaning: To proceed is to go somewhere or to continue doing something.
english_example: My son and I proceeded to the beach so we could go fishing.
spanish_meaning: Seguir haciéndose o avanzar.
spanish_example: Mi hijo y yo continuamos hacia la playa para pescar.

english_meaning: A pure thing is very clear and beautiful.
english_example: The rose was pure. It had no dirt or imperfections.
spanish_meaning: Muy limpio o sin mezcla.
spanish_example: La rosa era pura, sin suciedad ni manchas.

english_meaning: A relative is a family member.
english_example: My relatives came by to see the new baby.
spanish_meaning: Miembro de una familia.
spanish_example: Mis parientes vinieron a ver al bebé.

english_meaning: A senior person is older or more experienced.
english_example: Because he got his job first, Bob is the senior chef.
spanish_meaning: Persona más vieja o con más experiencia.
spanish_example: Como llegó primero, Bob es el jefe chef.

english_meaning: A silent person or thing makes no sound.
english_example: Since no one was home, the house was silent.
spanish_meaning: Que no hace ruido.
spanish_example: Como no había nadie en casa, la casa estaba silenciosa.

english_meaning: To sink into something is to slowly fall into it.
english_example: The boat had a hole in it, and it sank into the ocean.
spanish_meaning: Caer o ir hacia abajo dentro de algo.
spanish_example: El barco tenía un agujero y se hundió en el océano.

english_meaning: A superior person or thing is better than another.
english_example: I think cooking outdoors is superior to cooking indoors.
spanish_meaning: Mejor o más alto de calidad.
spanish_example: Cocinar al aire libre es superior a cocinar dentro de casa.

english_meaning: To surround something is to close in on it from all sides.
english_example: We surrounded the suspect on all four sides.
spanish_meaning: Cerrar alrededor desde todos lados.
spanish_example: Rodeamos al sospechoso por los cuatro lados.

english_meaning: A thick thing is wide and solid.
english_example: The fog was so thick that I couldn’t see through it.
spanish_meaning: Muy denso o ancho.
spanish_example: La niebla era tan espesa que no veía nada.

english_meaning: To wrap is to cover something on all sides.
english_example: I wrapped his gift and put a bow on it.
spanish_meaning: Cubrir algo por completo.
spanish_example: Envolví su regalo y le puse un lazo.

english_meaning: Also means in addition to or too.
english_example: I like blue, and I also like yellow.
spanish_meaning: Además de algo.
spanish_example: Me gusta el azul y también el amarillo.

english_meaning: If an action happens automatically, it happens without thinking or planning.
english_example: The man automatically smiled when he thought about his friend.
spanish_meaning: Sin pensar o planear.
spanish_example: El hombre sonrió automáticamente al pensar en su amigo.

english_meaning: A busy person has a lot of things to do.
english_example: Everyone is busy at the office today.
spanish_meaning: Tener muchas cosas por hacer.
spanish_example: Todos están ocupados en la oficina hoy.

english_meaning: Can shows that a person or thing has the ability to do an action.
english_example: Sad news can make her cry.
spanish_meaning: Tener capacidad de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Una mala noticia puede hacerla llorar.

english_meaning: To clear is to remove everything from a place.
english_example: I need to clear my desk because it is too messy.
spanish_meaning: Quitar cosas de un lugar.
spanish_example: Necesito despejar mi escritorio porque está desordenado.

english_meaning: To close is to shut something or cover up an opening.
english_example: The man wanted to close the door tightly.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que algo quede cerrado.
spanish_example: El hombre quiso cerrar bien la puerta.

english_meaning: To discuss is to talk about something with another person.
english_example: James began to discuss his report with his teacher.
spanish_meaning: Hablar con otra persona sobre algo.
spanish_example: James empezó a discutir su informe con su maestra.

english_meaning: To feel is to experience an emotion or feeling.
english_example: The girl must feel happy because it is her birthday today.
spanish_meaning: Experimentar una emoción o sensación.
spanish_example: La niña debe sentir felicidad porque hoy es su cumpleaños.

english_meaning: To listen is to pay attention to a sound that you can hear.
english_example: Lisa wanted to listen carefully to her friend.
spanish_meaning: Prestar atención a un sonido.
spanish_example: Lisa quería escuchar con atención a su amiga.

english_meaning: To meet is to come together so that you can talk or do something together.
english_example: Ken’s mother wanted to meet his teacher today.
spanish_meaning: Venir juntos para hablar o hacer algo.
spanish_example: La madre de Ken quiso reunirse con su maestra hoy.

english_meaning: Music is the sound made by singing or playing musical instruments.
english_example: The boy makes music by playing a guitar.
spanish_meaning: Sonido de canciones o instrumentos.
spanish_example: El chico hace música tocando la guitarra.

english_meaning: A normal thing is usual and not strange.
english_example: It is normal to wear school uniforms in private schools.
spanish_meaning: Lo que es habitual.
spanish_example: Es normal llevar uniforme escolar en escuelas privadas.

english_meaning: If something is quiet, it does not make much sound.
english_example: The man told the children to be quiet.
spanish_meaning: Que hace poco ruido.
spanish_example: El hombre pidió a los niños que estuvieran callados.

english_meaning: To relax is to rest or do something enjoyable.
english_example: Nicole likes to relax by reading books.
spanish_meaning: Descansar o hacer algo agradable.
spanish_example: A Nicole le gusta relajarse leyendo libros.

english_meaning: To sleep is to rest your mind and body, usually at night in bed.
english_example: The child goes to sleep in her bedroom at night.
spanish_meaning: Descansar cuerpo y mente durante la noche.
spanish_example: La niña se va a dormir a su habitación por la noche.

english_meaning: Stress is a strong feeling of worry caused by problems in life, work, etc.
english_example: Dan has a lot of stress at work.
spanish_meaning: Preocupación fuerte por problemas.
spanish_example: Dan tiene mucho estrés en el trabajo.

english_meaning: To study is to learn something by reading, memorizing, or going to school.
english_example: The woman needed a quiet place to study for a big test.
spanish_meaning: Aprender algo leyendo o practicando.
spanish_example: La mujer necesitaba un lugar tranquilo para estudiar.

english_meaning: To talk is to say words to express your thoughts, opinions, etc.
english_example: They went someplace to talk to each other.
spanish_meaning: Decir palabras para expresar ideas.
spanish_example: Fueron a un lugar para hablar entre ellos.

english_meaning: To work is to do a job that you get paid for.
english_example: They need to work together to finish an important project.
spanish_meaning: Realizar un trabajo remunerado.
spanish_example: Necesitan trabajar juntos para terminar un proyecto importante.

english_meaning: To write is to use a pen or keyboard to make letters and numbers on paper or a screen.
english_example: I need to write a story for my homework.
spanish_meaning: Usar pluma o teclado para poner palabras.
spanish_example: Necesito escribir una historia para mi tarea.

english_meaning: The basis of something is the main part of amount of it.
english_example: My grandfather gets his hearing checked on a yearly basis.
spanish_meaning: Fundamento o parte principal.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo se revisa la audición cada año.

english_meaning: Biology is the study of living things.
english_example: We learned about the human heart in biology class.
spanish_meaning: Estudio de los seres vivos.
spanish_example: Aprendimos sobre el corazón humano en biología.

english_meaning: A cage is something that holds an animal so it cannot leave.
english_example: We put the parrots in their cage at night.
spanish_meaning: Lugar que encierra un animal.
spanish_example: Pusimos a los loros en su jaula por la noche.

english_meaning: A colleague is somebody you work with.
english_example: My colleague helped me finish the job.
spanish_meaning: Persona con quien trabajas.
spanish_example: Mi colega me ayudó a terminar el trabajo.

english_meaning: A colony is a country controlled by another country.
english_example: The USA was at one time a colony of Great Britain.
spanish_meaning: País controlado por otra nación.
spanish_example: EE. UU. fue alguna vez una colonia de Gran Bretaña.

english_meaning: To debate is to seriously discuss something with someone.
english_example: The husband and wife debated which TV to buy.
spanish_meaning: Discutir algo seriamente.
spanish_example: El marido y la esposa debatieron qué tele comprar.

english_meaning: To depart is to leave some place so you can go to another place.
english_example: The plane departed for Italy at 3:00 this afternoon.
spanish_meaning: Abandonar un lugar para ir a otro.
spanish_example: El avión partió a Italia a las 3:00 de la tarde.

english_meaning: To depress someone is to make that person sad.
english_example: The bad news from work depressed the man.
spanish_meaning: Entristecer a alguien.
spanish_example: Las malas noticias de trabajo deprimieron al hombre.

english_meaning: A factual report or message includes true details.
english_example: John learns about history from factual books.
spanish_meaning: Basado en hechos reales.
spanish_example: John aprende historia con libros de hechos verdaderos.

english_meaning: To fascinate someone is to make that person very interested.
english_example: The kitten was fascinated by the ball of yarn.
spanish_meaning: Fascinar significa despertar mucho interés o asombro.
spanish_example: El gatito se fascinó con la pelota de estambre.

english_meaning: A mission is an important job that is sometimes far away.
english_example: The woman’s mission was to help sick people.
spanish_meaning: Una misión es una tarea importante, a veces en otra parte.
spanish_example: La misión de la mujer era ayudar a los enfermos.

english_meaning: Nevertheless shows a difference to what is expected or known.
english_example: He is usually friendly. Nevertheless, he wasn’t friendly this afternoon.
spanish_meaning: Sin embargo se usa para introducir una idea contraria a la anterior.
spanish_example: Suele ser amistoso; sin embargo, está tarde no lo fue.

english_meaning: An occupation is a person’s job.
english_example: My father’s occupation is a dentist.
spanish_meaning: La ocupación es el trabajo o profesión de una persona.
spanish_example: La ocupación de mi padre es dentista.

english_meaning: Overseas shows an action happens in another country, across an ocean.
english_example: John often goes overseas for vacations.
spanish_meaning: En el extranjero significa en otro país, separado por mar u océano.
spanish_example: John viaja al extranjero para vacacionar.

english_meaning: To persuade someone is to make that person agree to do something.
english_example: The children persuaded their parents to buy them gifts.
spanish_meaning: Convencer es lograr que alguien acepte una idea o haga algo.
spanish_example: Los niños convencieron a sus padres de comprarles regalos.

english_meaning: A route is the way you go from one place to another.
english_example: I saw many new houses along the route to the city.
spanish_meaning: La ruta es el camino o recorrido entre dos lugares.
spanish_example: Vi muchas casas nuevas a lo largo de la ruta hacia la ciudad.

english_meaning: Ruins are old buildings that are not used anymore.
english_example: I visited some interesting ruins in Greece.
spanish_meaning: Las ruinas son restos antiguos de edificios en desuso.
spanish_example: Visitamos ruinas muy interesantes en Grecia.

english_meaning: A scholar is a person who studies something and knows a lot about it.
english_example: The scholar knew much about art history.
spanish_meaning: El erudito es una persona que estudia mucho y conoce profundamente un tema.
spanish_example: El erudito sabía mucho de historia del arte.

english_meaning: A significant person or thing is important.
english_example: I read many significant novels as a literature major in university.
spanish_meaning: Significativo significa importante o de gran relevancia.
spanish_example: Leí muchas novelas significativas en la universidad.

english_meaning: A volcano is a mountain with a hole on top where hot liquid comes out.
english_example: When the volcano erupted, smoke and heat filled the air.
spanish_meaning: Un volcán es una montaña con cráter que puede expulsar lava y gases.
spanish_example: Cuando el volcán hizo erupción, se llenó de humo y calor el aire.

english_meaning: Broad means that something is wide, not narrow.
english_example: The river is very long and broad.
spanish_meaning: Amplio significa ancho o extenso.
spanish_example: El río es muy largo y amplio.

english_meaning: A bush is a woody plant that is smaller than a tree.
english_example: My dad and I planted some small bushes around the house.
spanish_meaning: Un arbusto es una planta leñosa más pequeña que un árbol.
spanish_example: Mi papá y yo plantamos algunos arbustos alrededor de la casa.

english_meaning: A capable person or thing can do an action.
english_example: The Olympic athlete is capable of lifting a lot of weight.
spanish_meaning: Capaz significa con habilidad o aptitud para hacer algo.
spanish_example: El atleta olímpico es capaz de levantar mucho peso.

english_meaning: To cheat is to be dishonest in order to win or do well.
english_example: They cheated on the test by sharing answers.
spanish_meaning: Engañar es actuar de forma deshonesta para lograr ventaja.
spanish_example: Engañaron en el examen al compartir respuestas.

english_meaning: To concentrate is to give one’s full attention to something.
english_example: I could not concentrate on my homework because the room was so loud.
spanish_meaning: Concentrarse es prestar atención plena a una tarea.
spanish_example: No pude concentrarme en la tarea porque la habitación estaba muy ruidosa.

english_meaning: To conclude is to arrive at a logical end by looking at evidence.
english_example: I saw crumbs on my dog’s face, so I concluded that he ate my cookie.
spanish_meaning: Concluir es llegar a una decisión final mediante razonamiento.
spanish_example: Vi migas en la cara de mi perro, así que concluí que se comió mi galleta.

english_meaning: Confident people believe that they can do something without failing.
english_example: She was confident she could climb the mountain due to her training.
spanish_meaning: Seguro significa tener confianza para hacerlo.
spanish_example: Ella estaba segura de que podía subir la montaña.

english_meaning: Considerable means large in size, amount, or extent.
english_example: They paid a considerable amount of money for that car.
spanish_meaning: Considerable significa bastante grande en cantidad o grado.
spanish_example: Pagaron una suma considerable por ese coche.

english_meaning: To convey is to communicate or make ideas known.
english_example: That picture of a crying child conveys a feeling of sadness.
spanish_meaning: Transmitir es comunicar una idea, emoción o mensaje.
spanish_example: La foto de ese niño llorando transmite tristeza.

english_meaning: A definite thing is certain or sure to be true.
english_example: There is a definite connection between hard work and success.
spanish_meaning: en contexto formal: que es seguro o cierto.
spanish_example: Existe una conexión definitiva entre esfuerzo y éxito.

english_meaning: Delight is a feeling of being very happy with something.
english_example: He felt such delight after getting a promotion at work.
spanish_meaning: Placer es la sensación de gran alegría o gusto.
spanish_example: Sintió un gran placer al recibir un ascenso.

english_meaning: A destination is the place where someone or something is going.
english_example: The destination of this plane is Munich, Germany.
spanish_meaning: El destino es el lugar de llegada de un viaje.
spanish_example: El destino de este avión es Múnich, Alemania.

english_meaning: The edge is the furthest part or side of something.
english_example: He ran to the edge of the cliff.
spanish_meaning: El borde es el extremo de algo.
spanish_example: Corrió hasta el borde del acantilado.

english_meaning: A set of instructions explains how to do something.
english_example: Just follow the instructions and you will be OK.
spanish_meaning: Las instrucciones son pasos para hacer algo correctamente.
spanish_example: Solo sigue las instrucciones y estarás bien.

english_meaning: A path is a way from one place to another that people can walk along.
english_example: We followed a path through the woods.
spanish_meaning: Un sendero es un camino para caminar.
spanish_example: Seguimos un sendero por el bosque.

english_meaning: To resort to something is to depend on it in order to solve a problem.
english_example: I hope they don’t resort to violence to end the argument.
spanish_meaning: Recurrir a algo es usarlo como medida o salida.
spanish_example: Espero que no recurran a la violencia para terminar la discusión.

english_meaning: A shadow is the dark area that is made when something blocks light.
english_example: The man’s shadow was taller than he was.
spanish_meaning: La sombra es la zona oscura que aparece al bloquearse la luz.
spanish_example: La sombra del hombre era más alta que él.

english_meaning: To succeed is to complete something as planned.
english_example: He will continue to work on the robot until he succeeds.
spanish_meaning: Triunfar es alcanzar el objetivo con éxito.
spanish_example: Él seguirá trabajando en el robot hasta que triunfe.

english_meaning: To suspect something is to believe that it might be true.
english_example: I suspect that those kids stole the money.
spanish_meaning: Sospechar es pensar que algo es posible, aunque no probado.
spanish_example: Sospecho que esos niños robaron el dinero.

english_meaning: A valley is a low area of land between two mountains or hills.
english_example: We looked at the valley below from the top of the mountain.
spanish_meaning: El valle es una zona baja entre montañas o colinas.
spanish_example: Desde lo alto vimos el valle de abajo.

english_meaning: To be against something is to be touching it or opposed to it.
english_example: They both leaned against the wall.
spanish_meaning: en contra significa pegado a, o en oposición a.
spanish_example: Se apoyaron contra la pared.

english_meaning: The beach is a sandy or rocky place by the ocean.
english_example: The little girl built a sandcastle on the beach.
spanish_meaning: La playa es la franja de arena o roca junto al mar.
spanish_example: La niña construyó un castillo de arena en la playa.

english_meaning: To damage something is to break it.
english_example: The car was damaged in the accident.
spanish_meaning: Dañar algo significa estropearlo o perjudicarlo.
spanish_example: El coche se dañó en el accidente.

english_meaning: To discover something is to find it for the first time.
english_example: I discovered some new information in this book.
spanish_meaning: Descubrir es encontrar o revelar algo por primera vez.
spanish_example: Descubrí información nueva en este libro.

english_meaning: An emotion is how you feel.
english_example: Anger is a common emotion that we all feel.
spanish_meaning: La emoción es un estado de sentimiento.
spanish_example: La rabia es una emoción muy común.

english_meaning: To fix something is to make it work.
english_example: My dad has many tools to help him fix broken things.
spanish_meaning: Reparar significa arreglar algo averiado.
spanish_example: Mi papá tiene muchas herramientas para reparar cosas rotas.

english_meaning: To identify something is to find out what it is.
english_example: I used the file to identify his name.
spanish_meaning: Identificar es averiguar qué o quién es algo.
spanish_example: Usé el expediente para identificar su nombre.

english_meaning: An island is land in the middle of water.
english_example: Japan is a group of islands.
spanish_meaning: Una isla es un territorio rodeado de agua.
spanish_example: Japón es un grupo de islas.

english_meaning: The ocean is all of the salt water that surrounds land.
english_example: The ocean can make powerful waves.
spanish_meaning: El océano es la gran extensión de agua salada de la Tierra.
spanish_example: El océano puede producir olas muy fuertes.

english_meaning: Perhaps is used when you say that something could happen.
english_example: Perhaps I will eat an apple for lunch.
spanish_meaning: Quizá se usa para señalar posibilidad.
spanish_example: Quizá comeré una manzana para almuerzo.

english_meaning: If something is pleasant, you enjoy it.
english_example: The character had a pleasant look on its face.
spanish_meaning: Agradable significa agradable y grato.
spanish_example: El personaje tenía una expresión agradable.

english_meaning: To prevent something is to stop it from happening.
english_example: The handcuffs prevented me from moving my hands.
spanish_meaning: Prevenir es evitar que algo suceda.
spanish_example: Las esposas me impidieron mover las manos.

english_meaning: A rock is a hard thing in the dirt.
english_example: I stacked rocks on top of one another.
spanish_meaning: La roca es una piedra dura.
spanish_example: Apilé rocas una encima de otra.

english_meaning: To save something is to keep it from being hurt.
english_example: I want to help save the world.
spanish_meaning: Salvar es proteger o preservar de un daño.
spanish_example: Quiero ayudar a salvar el mundo.

english_meaning: To smile is to show happiness with your mouth.
english_example: The baby smiled at me.
spanish_meaning: Sonreír es mostrar felicidad con el rostro.
spanish_example: El bebé sonrió al verme.

english_meaning: To step is to walk.
english_example: Be careful where you step.
spanish_meaning: Dar un paso o avanzar significa mover el pie para ir a otra posición.
spanish_example: Ten cuidado de dónde pisas al avanzar.

english_meaning: Still is used when you say that a situation keeps going on.
english_example: They are still waiting in line to get tickets.
spanish_meaning: Aún indica continuidad en el tiempo.
spanish_example: Todavía siguen esperando en la fila para comprar entradas.

english_meaning: A taste is the flavor something makes in your mouth.
english_example: The taste of the fruit was sweet.
spanish_meaning: El sabor es la percepción de sabor de un alimento o bebida.
spanish_example: El sabor de la fruta era dulce.

english_meaning: To throw something is to use your hand to make it go through the air.
english_example: The pitcher can throw the baseball very fast.
spanish_meaning: Lanzar algo es usar la mano para hacerlo ir por el aire.
spanish_example: El lanzador puede lanzar la pelota de béisbol muy rápido.

english_meaning: A wave is a line of water that moves higher than the rest of the water.
english_example: The water was filled with large blue waves.
spanish_meaning: La ola es un movimiento de agua en la superficie marina.
spanish_example: El agua estaba llena de grandes olas azules.

english_meaning: A citizen is someone who lives in a certain place.
english_example: Carlos was born in Spain. He is a Spanish citizen.
spanish_meaning: Un ciudadano es una persona con derechos y deberes en un país.
spanish_example: Carlos nació en España. Es ciudadano español.

english_meaning: A council is a group of people who run a city or town.
english_example: The council met to discuss the new laws for the city.
spanish_meaning: El consejo es un organismo que gobierna asuntos locales.
spanish_example: El consejo se reunió para discutir las nuevas normas de la ciudad.

english_meaning: To declare is to say something officially.
english_example: I declared my love for him.
spanish_meaning: Declarar es expresar algo de forma formal.
spanish_example: Declaré mi amor por él.

english_meaning: Enormous people or things are very large.
english_example: My dog looks enormous next to yours.
spanish_meaning: Enorme significa muy grande.
spanish_example: Mi perro parece enorme junto al tuyo.

english_meaning: Extraordinary things are amazing.
english_example: The fireman who rescued the girl was extraordinary.
spanish_meaning: Extraordinario significa extraordinariamente bueno o notable.
spanish_example: El bombero que rescató a la niña fue extraordinario.

english_meaning: Fog is a thick cloud that is near the ground or water.
english_example: I did not want to drive in the thick fog.
spanish_meaning: La niebla es una nube baja de humedad y poca visibilidad.
spanish_example: No quise conducir en la niebla espesa.

english_meaning: A funeral is a ceremony that takes place after a person dies.
english_example: They had a funeral for the soldier who died during the war.
spanish_meaning: El funeral es el servicio en recuerdo de un fallecimiento.
spanish_example: Tuvieron un funeral por el soldado que murió en la guerra.

english_meaning: Giant means very big.
english_example: The giant truck got in my way.
spanish_meaning: Gigante significa muy grande.
spanish_example: El camión gigante se metió en mi camino.

english_meaning: An impression is the way of thinking about someone or something.
english_example: Most people’s first impression of Dr. Giani is that he is mean.
spanish_meaning: La impresión es la idea inicial sobre alguien o algo.
spanish_example: La primera impresión de la gente fue que era antipático.

english_meaning: An intention is what a person plans to do.
english_example: Do you have good intentions?
spanish_meaning: La intención es la voluntad de hacer algo.
spanish_example: ¿Tienes buenas intenciones?

english_meaning: A mad person or animal is angry.
english_example: Mother got mad when I didn’t listen to her.
spanish_meaning: Enojado/a significa furioso o irritado.
spanish_example: Mi madre se enojó cuando no la escuché.

english_meaning: If you ought to do an action, it is the right thing to do.
english_example: I ought to take my library books back.
spanish_meaning: Deber es tener obligación moral o lógica de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Debo devolver mis libros de la biblioteca.

english_meaning: To resist something is to fight against it.
english_example: He resisted the treatment at the hospital.
spanish_meaning: Resistir es oponerse o no ceder ante algo.
spanish_example: Él resistió el tratamiento en el hospital.

english_meaning: To reveal is to show something.
english_example: I will reveal where I hid the candy bar.
spanish_meaning: Revelar es mostrar u ocultar algo.
spanish_example: Revelaré dónde escondí la barra de chocolate.

english_meaning: To rid is to make a place free from something or someone.
english_example: We rid our home of mice by using traps.
spanish_meaning: significa quitar o eliminar algo de un lugar.
spanish_example: Nos libramos de los ratones usando trampas.

english_meaning: A sword is a long sharp weapon.
english_example: They used to use swords in battles in ancient times.
spanish_meaning: La espada es un arma larga y afilada.
spanish_example: Antes se usaban espadas en batallas antiguas.

english_meaning: A tale is a story.
english_example: She told her two friends about the wild tale of her day.
spanish_meaning: Un cuento o relato que describe eventos.
spanish_example: Les contó un relato salvaje de su día.

english_meaning: To trap people or animals is to capture them so they cannot get away.
english_example: We trapped butterflies in a net.
spanish_meaning: Atrapar es encerrar o capturar algo para que no pueda escapar.
spanish_example: Atrapamos mariposas con una red.

english_meaning: A trial is the way a court discovers if a person is guilty or innocent.
english_example: He went on trial for robbing the bank.
spanish_meaning: El juicio es el proceso legal para resolver si alguien es culpable o inocente.
spanish_example: Él fue juzgado por robar en el banco.

english_meaning: A violent person or animal uses force to hurt others.
english_example: The man was put into jail because he was violent.
spanish_meaning: Violento significa que usa fuerza o daño contra otros.
spanish_example: El hombre fue encarcelado porque era violento.

english_meaning: Admission is the act of allowing to enter a place.
english_example: The admission ticket to the movie was $5.
spanish_meaning: La admisión es el acto de permitir la entrada.
spanish_example: La entrada a la película costó 5 dólares.

english_meaning: Astronomy is the study of the stars and planets.
english_example: Harold loved looking at the stars, so he decided to study astronomy.
spanish_meaning: La astronomía estudia las estrellas, planetas y el universo.
spanish_example: A Harold le encantaba mirar estrellas y estudió astronomía.

english_meaning: To blame someone for something bad is to say they did it.
english_example: My mom blamed me for something I didn’t do.
spanish_meaning: Culpar es responsabilizar a alguien por un hecho.
spanish_example: Mi mamá me culpó por algo que no hice.

english_meaning: Chemistry is the study of substances and reactions between them.
english_example: In chemistry class, the professor taught us about chemical reactions.
spanish_meaning: La química estudia las sustancias y sus reacciones.
spanish_example: En la clase de química aprendimos sobre reacciones químicas.

english_meaning: Despite shows a difference from what is expected.
english_example: We still played the game despite the cold weather.
spanish_meaning: a pesar de se usa para contrastar una dificultad o excepción.
spanish_example: Seguimos jugando a pesar del frío.

english_meaning: A dinosaur is a very big animal that lived millions of years ago.
english_example: I like to see the dinosaur bones at the museum.
spanish_meaning: El dinosaurio es un gran animal prehistórico.
spanish_example: Me gusta ver los huesos de dinosaurios en el museo.

english_meaning: To exhibit is to show something so that people can go look at it.
english_example: My painting will be exhibited at the fair.
spanish_meaning: Exhibir significa mostrar algo al público.
spanish_example: Mi pintura se exhibirá en la feria.

english_meaning: Fame is a reputation one has gained among the public.
english_example: He had fame and fortune, but he was not happy.
spanish_meaning: La fama es reconocimiento público de una persona.
spanish_example: Tenía fama y fortuna, pero no era feliz.

english_meaning: A forecast is an idea about what the weather will be like in the future.
english_example: The forecast says that it will rain all week.
spanish_meaning: El pronóstico predice cómo será el tiempo.
spanish_example: El pronóstico dice que lloverá toda la semana.

english_meaning: A genius is a very smart person.
english_example: Since she was a genius, she easily passed all of her school exams.
spanish_meaning: Un genio es una persona con gran inteligencia.
spanish_example: Como era un genio, aprobó todos los exámenes con facilidad.

english_meaning: Someone who is gentle is kind and calm.
english_example: He is very gentle with the baby.
spanish_meaning: Tierno significa amable y cuidadoso.
spanish_example: Es muy tierno con el bebé.

english_meaning: Geography is the study of the Earth, its land, weather, etc.
english_example: I had to draw a map for geography class.
spanish_meaning: La geografía estudia la Tierra, su clima y territorio.
spanish_example: En geografía tuve que dibujar un mapa.

english_meaning: To interfere is to cause problems and keep something from happening.
english_example: My little sister always interferes when I’m trying to study.
spanish_meaning: Interferir es meterse y obstaculizar algo.
spanish_example: Mi hermana siempre interfiere cuando intento estudiar.

english_meaning: To do something lightly is to not push very hard.
english_example: Draw lightly so you do not tear your paper.
spanish_meaning: Leve significa con poca fuerza.
spanish_example: Dibuja con cuidado, sin presión.

english_meaning: A principal is a person in charge of a school.
english_example: My school’s principal can be very strict with the rules.
spanish_meaning: El director o directora dirige un colegio.
spanish_example: El director de mi escuela puede ser muy estricto con las reglas.

english_meaning: A row is a line of things.
english_example: James put all of his toy soldiers into neat rows.
spanish_meaning: Una fila es una línea ordenada de objetos o personas.
spanish_example: James puso sus soldados de juguete en filas ordenadas.

english_meaning: A shelf is a place on a wall where you put things.
english_example: I keep my clothes on a shelf in my closet.
spanish_meaning: Una estantería es el lugar para poner cosas encima.
spanish_example: Guardo mis ropa en una estantería del armario.

english_meaning: Spite is the desire to be mean.
english_example: He snuck into his sister’s room and stole her bag out of spite.
spanish_meaning: Actuar por despecho es hacerlo con mala voluntad.
spanish_example: Entró y le robó el bolso a su hermana por despecho.

english_meaning: Super means really good.
english_example: My dad said I did a super job cleaning the house.
spanish_meaning: “Súper” en España se usa como intensificador informal.
spanish_example: Mi papá dijo que hice un súper trabajo limpiando la casa.

english_meaning: A wet thing has water on it.
english_example: Since my dog was wet, he tried to shake all the water off his body.
spanish_meaning: Húmedo es estar con agua o humedad.
spanish_example: Como mi perro estaba húmedo, intentó sacudirse el agua.

english_meaning: To abuse means to hurt someone or something on purpose.
english_example: The mean man abused his dog when it barked too loudly.
spanish_meaning: Abusar significa maltratar o hacer daño intencionadamente.
spanish_example: El hombre abusó de su perro cuando ladró demasiado.

english_meaning: To afford something means to have enough money to pay for it.
english_example: I’ve been saving my money so I can afford to buy a new bike.
spanish_meaning: Poder permitirse algo es tener dinero suficiente para pagarlo.
spanish_example: He estado ahorrando para poder comprarme una bici nueva.

english_meaning: To bake means to cook food in an oven.
english_example: My sister is a good cook. She bakes delicious cakes.
spanish_meaning: Hornear es cocinar con calor en el horno.
spanish_example: Mi hermana hornea tartas deliciosas.

english_meaning: A bean is a plant seed that is good to eat.
english_example: There are many different kinds of beans to eat.
spanish_meaning: El frijol es una semilla comestible de varias variedades.
spanish_example: Hay muchos tipos de frijoles para comer.

english_meaning: A candle is a stick of wax that is lit on fire for light or heat.
english_example: When the lights went out, we lit some candles.
spanish_meaning: Una vela es un cilindro de cera con mecha que arde para dar luz.
spanish_example: Cuando se fue la luz, encendimos velas.

english_meaning: To convert something means to change it into something else.
english_example: The man converted his messy field into a garden of flowers.
spanish_meaning: Convertir es transformar algo en otra cosa.
spanish_example: El hombre convirtió su campo desordenado en un jardín de flores.

english_meaning: A debt is an amount of money that a person owes.
english_example: I have not paid my gas bill. I owe a debt to the gas company.
spanish_meaning: La deuda es dinero que se debe pagar a otra persona.
spanish_example: Debo el pago del gas porque no he liquidado la factura.

english_meaning: To decrease something is to make it less than it was before.
english_example: Hiring more police officers has decreased crime in the city.
spanish_meaning: Disminuir es hacer que algo baje o sea menor.
spanish_example: La contratación de más policías ha disminuido el delito en la ciudad.

english_meaning: A fault is responsibility for a mistake.
english_example: It is my fault that the cat ran away. I left the door open.
spanish_meaning: La culpa es la responsabilidad por un error.
spanish_example: Es mi culpa que el gato se escapó: dejé la puerta abierta.

english_meaning: A fund is an amount of money that people have.
english_example: We all put money into our club’s fund.
spanish_meaning: Un fondo es una reserva de dinero.
spanish_example: Todos aportamos dinero al fondo del club.

english_meaning: A generous person likes to give things to people.
english_example: The generous man donated several new computers to our school.
spanish_meaning: Generoso significa dispuesto a dar sin esperar nada a cambio.
spanish_example: El hombre generoso donó varias computadoras nuevas.

english_meaning: An ingredient is something that is part of a food dish.
english_example: The main ingredients in cake are eggs, sugar, and flour.
spanish_meaning: Un ingrediente es un componente de una receta.
spanish_example: Los ingredientes principales del pastel son huevos, azúcar y harina.

english_meaning: To insist means to be firm in telling people what to do.
english_example: I insist that you try some of these cookies.
spanish_meaning: Insistir es mantener una petición de forma reiterada.
spanish_example: Insisto en que pruebes estas galletas.

english_meaning: A mess is a condition that is not clean or neat.
english_example: Heather’s room was a complete mess.
spanish_meaning: Un desorden es una situación sucia o desorganizada.
spanish_example: La habitación de Heather estaba hecha un desastre.

english_meaning: Metal is a strong material people use to build things.
english_example: Steel is a common metal that is used to build buildings.
spanish_meaning: El metal es un material resistente para construir.
spanish_example: El acero es un metal común para construir edificios.

english_meaning: To monitor people or things is to watch them closely.
english_example: The teacher monitors the students when they take tests.
spanish_meaning: Monitorear es vigilar o supervisar de cerca.
spanish_example: El profesor monitorea a los estudiantes en los exámenes.

english_meaning: To oppose something means to dislike it or act against it.
english_example: I want to be a police officer because I oppose crime.
spanish_meaning: Oponerse es estar en contra de una idea o acción.
spanish_example: Quiero ser policía porque me opongo al crimen.

english_meaning: A passive person does not take action to solve problems.
english_example: Marcie is so passive that she never solves her own problems.
spanish_meaning: Pasivo significa poco activo o poco participante.
spanish_example: Marcie es tan pasiva que nunca resuelve sus problemas.

english_meaning: A quantity is a certain amount of something.
english_example: I have a small quantity of milk in my glass.
spanish_meaning: La cantidad es una medida o número de algo.
spanish_example: Tengo una pequeña cantidad de leche en el vaso.

english_meaning: To sue is to take someone to court for some harmful action.
english_example: I sued the company after I slipped on a banana peel in their hallway.
spanish_meaning: Demandar es llevar un caso a los tribunales.
spanish_example: Demandé a la empresa después de resbalar con la cáscara.

english_meaning: Something adequate is good enough.
english_example: This is adequate for my needs.
spanish_meaning: Adecuado significa suficiente para la necesidad.
spanish_example: Esto es adecuado para mis necesidades.

english_meaning: Anxiety is a feeling of worry and fear.
english_example: When I have to climb to high places, I’m filled with anxiety.
spanish_meaning: La ansiedad es una sensación de preocupación o miedo.
spanish_example: Cuando tengo que subir alto, me lleno de ansiedad.

english_meaning: An army is a large group of people who fight in wars.
english_example: The army protects all the people in the country.
spanish_meaning: El ejército es una fuerza armada organizada para la defensa.
spanish_example: El ejército protege a la población del país.

english_meaning: A billion is a very large number: 1,000,000,000.
english_example: There are billions of stars in outer space.
spanish_meaning: Mil millones es la cifra 1,000,000,000.
spanish_example: Hay miles de millones de estrellas en el espacio.

english_meaning: To carve means to cut into something.
english_example: My father usually carves the turkey for Thanksgiving.
spanish_meaning: Tallar es cortar o esculpir una forma.
spanish_example: Mi padre suele tallar el pavo en acción de gracias.

english_meaning: To consult means to ask someone for help.
english_example: I will consult my accountant to find a way to pay my bills.
spanish_meaning: Consultar es pedir consejo o ayuda profesional.
spanish_example: Consultaré a mi contador para pagar mis facturas.

english_meaning: An emergency is a time when someone needs help right away.
english_example: There is a huge fire in my house! This is an emergency!
spanish_meaning: Una emergencia es una situación urgente que requiere ayuda inmediata.
spanish_example: ¡Hay un fuego enorme en mi casa! Esto es una emergencia.

english_meaning: Fortune means the things that happen but are not controlled by a person.
english_example: I have good fortune when I play cards.
spanish_meaning: La fortuna son las condiciones de suerte o destino.
spanish_example: Tengo buena fortuna cuando juego cartas.

english_meaning: I will guarantee that the loan will be repaid.
english_example: I guarantee that the sun will come up in the morning.
spanish_meaning: Garantizar es asegurar que algo se cumplirá.
spanish_example: Garanto que el sol saldrá mañana.

english_meaning: Initial shows that something is first.
english_example: The initial step when writing a paper is to find a good topic.
spanish_meaning: Inicial significa primeramente o al comienzo.
spanish_example: El primer paso inicial al escribir un trabajo es elegir un buen tema.

english_meaning: An intense thing is very strong.
english_example: The skunk made an intense odor that filled the air.
spanish_meaning: Intenso significa fuerte o de gran intensidad.
spanish_example: El zorrillo hizo un olor intenso que llenó el aire.

english_meaning: To lend something is to give it to someone for a short time.
english_example: My sister lost her pen, so I will lend her mine.
spanish_meaning: Prestar es dar algo temporalmente para que otra persona lo use.
spanish_example: Prestaré mi bolígrafo a mi hermana.

english_meaning: The peak is the very top of a mountain.
english_example: There is snow on the peaks of those mountains.
spanish_meaning: La cima es la parte más alta de una montaña.
spanish_example: En las cimas de esas montañas había nieve.

english_meaning: Potential means capable of being but not yet actual or real.
english_example: I’ve thought of some potential problems with your idea.
spanish_meaning: Potencial significa posible de realizarse en el futuro.
spanish_example: He pensado en algunos problemas potenciales de tu idea.

english_meaning: Pride is a feeling of happiness about oneself or one’s things.
english_example: I take pride in getting good grades.
spanish_meaning: El orgullo es satisfacción positiva por logros propios o propios.
spanish_example: Me da orgullo sacar buenas notas.

english_meaning: Proof is a fact that shows something is real.
english_example: They used his fingerprint for proof that he committed the crime.
spanish_meaning: La prueba es una evidencia que demuestra un hecho.
spanish_example: Usaron su huella dactilar como prueba del delito.

english_meaning: To quit something means to stop doing it.
english_example: I quit running because I got tired.
spanish_meaning: Dejar de algo significa detenerlo por completo.
spanish_example: Dejé de correr porque me cansé.

english_meaning: To spin is to turn around in circles.
english_example: The boy kept spinning until he fell down.
spanish_meaning: Girar es girar alrededor de un eje.
spanish_example: El niño siguió girando hasta caer.

english_meaning: A tiny thing is very small.
english_example: A baby’s hand is tiny.
spanish_meaning: Muy pequeño significa de tamaño diminuto.
spanish_example: La mano de un bebé es muy pequeña.

english_meaning: A tutor is someone who gives lessons to one student.
english_example: My sister is bad at math. So my mother hired a tutor to help her.
spanish_meaning: Un tutor es quien da clases particulares.
spanish_example: Mi madre contrató un tutor para ayudarla en matemáticas.

english_meaning: Apparent means clear or easy to see.
english_example: Her happiness was apparent from the smile on her face.
spanish_meaning: Aparente significa visible u obvio.
spanish_example: Su felicidad era aparente por la sonrisa.

english_meaning: A blind person or animal cannot see.
english_example: The blind man didn’t see the hole and almost fell in.
spanish_meaning: Ciego significa sin vista.
spanish_example: El hombre ciego no vio el agujero y casi cae.

english_meaning: To calculate is to find an answer using math.
english_example: I calculated how much money I would need to buy the car.
spanish_meaning: Calcular es obtener un resultado con operaciones.
spanish_example: Calculé cuánto dinero necesitaría para comprar el coche.

english_meaning: To chat is to talk with someone.
english_example: Even though they were far apart, the couple chatted every day.
spanish_meaning: Charlar es conversar de manera informal.
spanish_example: Aunque estaban lejos, la pareja charlaba todos los días.

english_meaning: To commit to something is to promise to do it.
english_example: Seth wanted to go home, but he had committed to finishing the job.
spanish_meaning: Comprometerse es asumir una responsabilidad o promesa.
spanish_example: Seth quería irse, pero se había comprometido a terminar el trabajo.

english_meaning: To compose something is to make it from smaller parts.
english_example: Tony composed his report using many sources of information.
spanish_meaning: Componer es crear algo uniendo partes.
spanish_example: Tony compuso su informe con varias fuentes de información.

english_meaning: A dormitory is a school building where students live.
english_example: I will move into the dormitory at the beginning of the school year.
spanish_meaning: El dormitorio es un edificio residencial estudiantil.
spanish_example: Me mudaré al dormitorio al comenzar el año escolar.

english_meaning: To exhaust someone is to make that person tired.
english_example: John exhausted himself by swimming all day.
spanish_meaning: Agotar significa dejar sin energía.
spanish_example: John se agotó nadando todo el día.

english_meaning: A greenhouse is a small glass building that is used to grow plants.
english_example: We have a small greenhouse in our backyard where we grow plants.
spanish_meaning: Un invernadero es una estructura de vidrio para cultivar plantas.
spanish_example: Tenemos un pequeño invernadero en el patio trasero.

english_meaning: To ignore something is to act like you do not see or hear it.
english_example: I ignored the message he was making and kept studying.
spanish_meaning: Ignorar es fingir que no se ve o escucha algo.
spanish_example: Ignoré su mensaje y seguí estudiando.

english_meaning: Obvious means clear or easy to see.
english_example: It was obvious that he was tired. He kept falling asleep.
spanish_meaning: Obvio significa evidente y fácil de percibir.
spanish_example: Era obvio que estaba cansado porque se quedaba dormido.

english_meaning: Physics is a science that deals with energy and how it affects things.
english_example: In physics class, we used Newton’s Cradle to learn about energy.
spanish_meaning: La física estudia la energía y el movimiento.
spanish_example: En la clase de física usamos el péndulo de Newton para aprender sobre energía.

english_meaning: A portion of something is a part of it.
english_example: I only ate a small portion of the pizza.
spanish_meaning: Una porción es una parte de un todo.
spanish_example: Solo comí una porción pequeña de pizza.

english_meaning: To remind is to tell someone to remember to do something.
english_example: Nick’s dad reminded him to do his homework.
spanish_meaning: Recordar aquí equivale a hacerle a alguien una indicación de memoria.
spanish_example: El padre de Nick le recordó que hiciera la tarea.

english_meaning: A secretary is a person who works in an office.
english_example: Rebecca asked her secretary to type a report.
spanish_meaning: La secretaria trabaja en tareas administrativas de oficina.
spanish_example: Rebecca pidió a su secretaria que tipeara un informe.

english_meaning: Severe means very bad or serious.
english_example: After hitting his hand with the hammer, Sam was in severe pain.
spanish_meaning: Muy grave o severo en intensidad.
spanish_example: Después de golpearse con el martillo, Sam tuvo un dolor severo.

english_meaning: A talent is a natural ability to do something well.
english_example: Maria has a talent for playing the piano.
spanish_meaning: El talento es capacidad innata para hacer algo bien.
spanish_example: María tiene talento para tocar el piano.

english_meaning: A thesis is an idea that needs to be proved.
english_example: She did not support her thesis very well.
spanish_meaning: La tesis es una idea o argumento que se intenta demostrar.
spanish_example: Ella no defendió bien su tesis.

english_meaning: A uniform is a piece of clothing worn by people of the same group.
english_example: All the members of our marching band wear matching uniforms.
spanish_meaning: El uniforme es la ropa similar de un grupo.
spanish_example: Todos en la banda usan uniforme igual.

english_meaning: Vision is the ability to see.
english_example: The eye doctor tested my vision.
spanish_meaning: La visión es la capacidad de ver.
spanish_example: El oftalmólogo comprobó mi visión.

english_meaning: To absorb a liquid means to take it inside.
english_example: He used a sponge to absorb the water on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Absorber es tomar un líquido u otra cosa en su interior.
spanish_example: Usó una esponja para absorber el agua del piso.

english_meaning: A boss is a person in charge of other people at work.
english_example: My boss is a nice person.
spanish_meaning: El jefe dirige y coordina al equipo de trabajo.
spanish_example: Mi jefe es una persona amable.

english_meaning: A charitable organization aims to help people.
english_example: I give money each year to a charitable foundation.
spanish_meaning: Benéfico significa orientado a ayudar a los demás.
spanish_example: Doy dinero cada año a una fundación benéfica.

english_meaning: A committee is a group of people who meet together to make decisions.
english_example: The school’s committee agreed on a new dress code for students.
spanish_meaning: Un comité es un grupo que toma decisiones.
spanish_example: El comité escolar aprobó un nuevo código de vestimenta.

english_meaning: A contract is a written agreement between two people.
english_example: The woman signed a contract when she bought the house.
spanish_meaning: Un contrato es un acuerdo escrito con obligaciones.
spanish_example: La mujer firmó un contrato al comprar la casa.

english_meaning: A crew is a group of workers.
english_example: My father has a crew that helps him build houses.
spanish_meaning: El equipo es un grupo de personas que trabajan juntas.
spanish_example: Mi padre tiene un equipo que lo ayuda a construir casas.

english_meaning: To devote time to something means to spend a lot of time doing it.
english_example: She devotes two hours a day to playing the piano.
spanish_meaning: Dedicar es aplicar tiempo o esfuerzo con constancia.
spanish_example: Ella dedica dos horas diarias a tocar piano.

english_meaning: To dig is to make a hole in the ground.
english_example: My dog digs in the yard so he can hide his bones.
spanish_meaning: Cavar es hacer hoyos excavando la tierra.
spanish_example: Mi perro cava en el patio para esconder sus huesos.

english_meaning: To dine means to eat dinner.
english_example: The young couple dined at their home.
spanish_meaning: Cenar significa comer la cena.
spanish_example: La pareja joven cenó en su casa.

english_meaning: To donate is to give something to a charity or organization.
english_example: We donate money to charities every year.
spanish_meaning: Donar es aportar bienes para una causa.
spanish_example: Damos dinero a organizaciones benéficas cada año.

english_meaning: Double means twice as much or twice as many.
english_example: I paid almost double the amount for that shirt.
spanish_meaning: Doble significa por dos veces o duplicado.
spanish_example: Pagué casi el doble por esa camisa.

english_meaning: A flavor is the taste of food or drinks.
english_example: The flavor of the ice cream was very good.
spanish_meaning: El sabor es la percepción gustativa de un alimento.
spanish_example: El sabor del helado era muy bueno.

english_meaning: A foundation is a group that provides money for research.
english_example: The foundation raised money to give scholarships to students.
spanish_meaning: La fundación es una entidad que financia proyectos.
spanish_example: La fundación reunió fondos para becas.

english_meaning: A generation is a group of people who live at the same time.
english_example: My grandparents are from a different generation than me.
spanish_meaning: Una generación es un grupo de personas de una misma época.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos pertenecen a otra generación que yo.

english_meaning: A handle is the part of an object people hold while using it.
english_example: The pot is very hot, so pick it up by the handle.
spanish_meaning: El asa es la parte para tomar un objeto.
spanish_example: La olla está muy caliente, tómala por el asa.

english_meaning: A layer covers over something or is one of several pieces lying on top of each other.
english_example: There was a layer of snow on the tops of the houses this morning.
spanish_meaning: Una capa es una franja o nivel superpuesto.
spanish_example: Esta mañana había una capa de nieve en los tejados.

english_meaning: Mud is soft, wet dirt.
english_example: My brother played rugby in the mud. Now, he’s dirty.
spanish_meaning: El lodo es tierra blanda y húmeda.
spanish_example: Mi hermano jugó rugby en el barro y luego estuvo sucio.

english_meaning: A smooth thing has no bumps or rough parts.
english_example: The baby’s skin felt very smooth.
spanish_meaning: Liso significa sin asperezas.
spanish_example: La piel del bebé se sentía muy lisa.

english_meaning: Soil is the top layer of land on the Earth.
english_example: The boy planted flowers in the soil and watered them every day.
spanish_meaning: La tierra o suelo fértil donde se plantan las cosas.
spanish_example: El chico plantó flores en la tierra y las regó cada día.

english_meaning: A unique person or thing is not like others.
english_example: Her dog is unique. I’ve never seen one quite like it.
spanish_meaning: Único significa que no se parece a los demás.
spanish_example: Su perro es único; no he visto uno igual.

english_meaning: An academy is a special type of school.
english_example: There are many courses taught at the academy that I go to.
spanish_meaning: Una academia es un centro educativo o de formación.
spanish_example: En la academia donde estudio hay muchos cursos.

english_meaning: If something is ancient, it is very old.
english_example: I want to see the ancient buildings in Rome.
spanish_meaning: Antiguo significa muy viejo o histórico.
spanish_example: Quiero ver los edificios antiguos de Roma.

english_meaning: A board is a flat piece of wood.
english_example: The sign was made of a few wooden boards.
spanish_meaning: Una tabla es una pieza de madera plana.
spanish_example: El letrero estaba hecho con unas tablas de madera.

english_meaning: A century is one hundred years.
english_example: Our company is celebrating a century of business in London.
spanish_meaning: Un siglo equivale a cien años.
spanish_example: Nuestra empresa celebra un siglo de negocio en Londres.

english_meaning: A clue is a fact or object that helps solve a mystery or crime.
english_example: The detective found some clues on the sidewalk.
spanish_meaning: Una pista es una evidencia o indicio útil para resolver un caso.
spanish_example: El detective encontró pistas en la acera.

english_meaning: A concert is an event where you listen to people play music.
english_example: I enjoyed the concert last night. The band was very good.
spanish_meaning: El concierto es un evento musical en vivo.
spanish_example: Disfruté el concierto de anoche; la banda fue muy buena.

english_meaning: A county is the largest division of a state in a country.
english_example: He wanted to represent the citizens of his county.
spanish_meaning: Un condado es una división administrativa dentro de un estado.
spanish_example: Él quería representar a los ciudadanos de su condado.

english_meaning: A dictionary is a book that tells you what words mean.
english_example: I use the dictionary to learn new words.
spanish_meaning: El diccionario es un libro que explica el significado de palabras.
spanish_example: Uso el diccionario para aprender vocabulario nuevo.

english_meaning: To exist is to be real.
english_example: Do you really think that unicorns ever existed?
spanish_meaning: Existir significa estar o ser real.
spanish_example: ¿De verdad crees que los unicornios existieron alguna vez?

english_meaning: Flat describes something that is level and smooth with no curved parts.
english_example: My parents bought a new flat-screen TV on the weekend.
spanish_meaning: Plano significa sin relieve, liso y nivelado.
spanish_example: Mis padres compraron un televisor de pantalla plana.

english_meaning: A gentleman is a nice man.
english_example: My grandfather is a kind and helpful gentleman.
spanish_meaning: Un caballero es un hombre educado y de buenas maneras.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo es un caballero amable y servicial.

english_meaning: Hidden means not easily noticed or too hard to find.
english_example: The hidden camera recorded everything in the parking lot.
spanish_meaning: Oculto significa que no se ve fácilmente.
spanish_example: La cámara oculta grabó todo en el estacionamiento.

english_meaning: Maybe is used to show that something is possible or may be true.
english_example: If I focus hard enough, maybe I can come up with the right answer.
spanish_meaning: Tal vez expresa posibilidad.
spanish_example: Si me concentro, tal vez encuentre la respuesta correcta.

english_meaning: An officer is a leader in the army.
