english_example: The soldiers followed the orders of the officer.
spanish_meaning: Un oficial es un miembro con rango en la fuerza armada.
spanish_example: Los soldados siguieron las órdenes del oficial.

english_meaning: If something is original, it is the first one of that thing.
english_example: This is the original painting of the Mona Lisa.
spanish_meaning: Original significa primigenio o primer ejemplar.
spanish_example: Este es el cuadro original de la Mona Lisa.

english_meaning: To pound something is to hit it many times with a lot of force.
english_example: He pounded the nail with the hammer.
spanish_meaning: Pound significa golpear repetidamente con fuerza.
spanish_example: Martilleó el clavo con el martillo.

english_meaning: A process is the steps to take to do something.
english_example: Making a cake is a long process.
spanish_meaning: Un proceso es el conjunto de pasos ordenados para lograr algo.
spanish_example: Hacer un pastel es un proceso largo.

english_meaning: To publish a book is to get it printed and ready to sell.
english_example: That company publishes daily newspapers.
spanish_meaning: Publicar es imprimir y difundir una obra.
spanish_example: Esa compañía publica periódicos diarios.

english_meaning: A theater is a building where you watch plays, shows, and movies.
english_example: We went to the theater to see a play.
spanish_meaning: El teatro es el lugar donde se presentan obras y proyecciones.
spanish_example: Fuimos al teatro para ver una obra.

english_meaning: Wealth is the total of one’s possessions (money, land, etc.).
english_example: One of the most important things for some people is wealth.
spanish_meaning: La riqueza es el conjunto de bienes o dinero acumulado.
spanish_example: Para algunas personas, la riqueza es muy importante.

english_meaning: An aim is a goal someone wants to make happen.
english_example: My aim is to become a helicopter pilot.
spanish_meaning: La meta es el objetivo a alcanzar.
spanish_example: Mi meta es convertirme en piloto de helicóptero.

english_meaning: To attach is to put two things together.
english_example: I attached the socks to the clothesline to dry.
spanish_meaning: Unir o fijar algo a otra cosa.
spanish_example: Colgué los calcetines en la cuerda para secarlos.

english_meaning: To bet is to risk money on the result of a game or a business.
english_example: How much will you bet that your horse will win?
spanish_meaning: Apostar es arriesgar dinero por un resultado.
spanish_example: ¿Cuánto apostarás a que tu caballo gana?

english_meaning: A carriage is a vehicle pulled by a horse.
english_example: We took a carriage ride in the park.
spanish_meaning: La carroza o carruaje se mueve tirado por caballos.
spanish_example: Dimos un paseo en carroza por el parque.

english_meaning: A classic thing is something that is common from the past.
english_example: The athlete made a classic mistake-he started running too soon.
spanish_meaning: Clásico significa muy típico o típico de otra época.
spanish_example: El atleta cometió un error clásico: empezó a correr demasiado pronto.

english_meaning: To commute is to travel a long distance to get to work.
english_example: I usually commute to work on the train.
spanish_meaning: Commutar es desplazarse habitualmente al trabajo.
spanish_example: Normalmente hago el trayecto al trabajo en tren.

english_meaning: To confirm is to make sure something is correct.
english_example: Winning the game confirmed that James was a good player.
spanish_meaning: Confirmar es asegurar que algo es correcto.
spanish_example: Ganar el juego confirmó que James era un buen jugador.

english_meaning: To criticize is to say bad things about someone or something.
english_example: He criticized his wife for spending too much money.
spanish_meaning: Criticar es expresar desaprobación.
spanish_example: Criticó a su esposa por gastar demasiado.

english_meaning: To differ is to not be the same as another person or thing.
english_example: I differ from my brother: he is short, while I am tall.
spanish_meaning: Diferir es no coincidir o no ser igual.
spanish_example: Yo difiero de mi hermano: él es bajito y yo soy alto.

english_meaning: An expense is the money that people spend on something.
english_example: She wrote down all the expenses for her trip.
spanish_meaning: El gasto es el dinero que la gente paga en algo.
spanish_example: Escribió todos los gastos de su viaje.

english_meaning: A formal thing is official or serious.
english_example: It was a formal dinner, so we wore our best clothes.
spanish_meaning: Formal significa oficial o serio.
spanish_example: Fue una cena formal, así que llevamos ropa elegante.

english_meaning: Height is how tall someone or something is.
english_example: My height is 168 centimeters.
spanish_meaning: La altura es la medida de lo alto de una persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Mido 168 centímetros de altura.

english_meaning: To invent is to create something that never existed before.
english_example: My grandfather has invented some interesting things.
spanish_meaning: Inventar es crear algo que antes no existía.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo ha inventado cosas interesantes.

english_meaning: A junior person is younger or less experienced.
english_example: When she started at the company, she was only a junior manager.
spanish_meaning: Junior significa más joven o con menos experiencia.
spanish_example: Cuando empezó en la empresa, era una gerente junior.

english_meaning: Labor is the act of doing or making something.
english_example: Building the house took a lot of labor.
spanish_meaning: El trabajo es la acción de hacer o fabricar algo.
spanish_example: Construir la casa exigió mucho trabajo.

english_meaning: A mechanic is someone who fixes vehicles or machines.
english_example: We took the car to the mechanic to be fixed.
spanish_meaning: Un mecánico es una persona que arregla vehículos o máquinas.
spanish_example: Llevamos el coche al mecánico para que lo repararan.

english_meaning: Prime shows that something is the most important one.
english_example: Dirty air is a prime cause of illness.
spanish_meaning: Principal indica lo más importante.
spanish_example: El aire contaminado es una causa principal de enfermedad.

english_meaning: To shift is to move into a different place or direction.
english_example: He shifted to the other side of the table to eat his breakfast.
spanish_meaning: Cambiar es mover algo a otro sitio o dirección.
spanish_example: Se cambió al otro lado de la mesa para desayunar.

english_meaning: A signal is a sound or action that tells someone to do something.
english_example: The coach blew his whistle as a signal to begin the game.
spanish_meaning: Una señal es un sonido o gesto que indica hacer algo.
spanish_example: El entrenador tocó el silbato como señal para empezar.

english_meaning: A sincere person is honest, especially about emotions or opinions.
english_example: He sounded sincere when he apologized to me.
spanish_meaning: Sincero describe a alguien honesto.
spanish_example: Sonó sincero cuando me pidió perdón.

english_meaning: Ability is the quality of a person being able to do something well.
english_example: His swimming abilities let him cross the entire lake.
spanish_meaning: La habilidad es la capacidad para hacer algo bien.
spanish_example: Su habilidad para nadar le permitió cruzar todo el lago.

english_meaning: Agriculture is the growing of plants and raising of animals for food.
english_example: The farmer studied agriculture in college.
spanish_meaning: La agricultura es el cultivo de plantas y crianza de animales para alimento.
spanish_example: El agricultor estudió agricultura en la universidad.

english_meaning: A cartoon is a funny drawing.
english_example: Sometimes, people draw cartoons for the newspaper.
spanish_meaning: Una caricatura es un dibujo gracioso.
spanish_example: A veces, las personas dibujan caricaturas para el periódico.

english_meaning: The ceiling is the top of a room.
english_example: He painted the ceiling with a special roller.
spanish_meaning: El techo es la parte superior de una habitación.
spanish_example: Pintó el techo con un rodillo especial.

english_meaning: To convince someone means to make that person sure of something.
english_example: She convinced me to buy the house.
spanish_meaning: Convencer a alguien es lograr que esté seguro de algo.
spanish_example: Ella me convenció para comprar la casa.

english_meaning: A curious person or animals wants to know about something.
english_example: I opened up the clock because I was curious about how it worked.
spanish_meaning: Curioso significa querer saber cómo funciona algo.
spanish_example: Abrí el reloj porque estaba curioso sobre cómo funcionaba.

english_meaning: To delay means to wait to do something.
english_example: I was delayed at the airport for over two hours.
spanish_meaning: Retrasar es esperar para hacer algo.
spanish_example: Tuve un retraso de más de dos horas en el aeropuerto.

english_meaning: A diary is a book in which people write their personal experiences.
english_example: I do not let anybody read my diary.
spanish_meaning: Un diario es un libro para escribir experiencias personales.
spanish_example: No dejo que nadie lea mi diario.

english_meaning: An element of something is a particular part of it.
english_example: Tackling an opponent is Johnny’s favorite element of American football.
spanish_meaning: Un elemento es una parte concreta de algo.
spanish_example: Atacar a un rival es su elemento favorito del fútbol americano.

english_meaning: Faith is trust or belief without proof.
english_example: The sick girl had faith in doctors. She knew they would make her better.
spanish_meaning: La fe es confianza o creencia sin pruebas.
spanish_example: La niña enferma tenía fe en los médicos.

english_meaning: A grain is a food crop such as wheat, corn, rice, or oats.
english_example: The farmer planted two fields of grain this year.
spanish_meaning: Un grano es un cultivo como trigo, maíz o arroz.
spanish_example: El agricultor sembró dos campos de grano este año.

english_meaning: To greet someone means to meet and welcome that person.
english_example: When my friend came over, I greeted him at the door.
spanish_meaning: Saludar a alguien es recibirlo y darle la bienvenida.
spanish_example: Cuando mi amigo llegó, lo saludé en la puerta.

english_meaning: To investigate means to search for something or learn about it.
english_example: The detective went to investigate the crime.
spanish_meaning: Investigar significa buscar información sobre algo.
spanish_example: El detective fue a investigar el crimen.

english_meaning: Joy is a feeling of great happiness.
english_example: I love baseball. I feel joy when I play.
spanish_meaning: La alegría es un sentimiento de gran felicidad.
spanish_example: Me encanta el béisbol. Siento alegría cuando juego.

english_meaning: A label is a tag that tells about something.
english_example: The label on the back of your shirt will tell you what size it is.
spanish_meaning: Una etiqueta es un distintivo que informa sobre algo.
spanish_example: La etiqueta de atrás de tu camiseta dice tu talla.

english_meaning: A monk is a religious person who lives a simple life.
english_example: The monks knew a lot about religion.
spanish_meaning: Un monje es una persona religiosa que vive de forma sencilla.
spanish_example: Los monjes sabían mucho sobre la religión.

english_meaning: Something odd is unusual.
english_example: Her cat is odd. It walks on two feet.
spanish_meaning: Algo extraño es inusual.
spanish_example: Su gato es extraño. Camina sobre dos patas.

english_meaning: To pause means to stop doing something for a while.
english_example: Since she was so hungry, she paused to make a snack.
spanish_meaning: Hacer una pausa significa parar algo un momento.
spanish_example: Como tenía mucha hambre, se hizo una pausa para preparar un tentempié.

english_meaning: A priest is a person trained to perform religious duties.
english_example: The priest taught us about God.
spanish_meaning: Un sacerdote es alguien que realiza tareas religiosas.
spanish_example: El sacerdote nos enseñó sobre Dios.

english_meaning: A profession is a person’s job.
english_example: He loved sailing, so he chose to work on ships as a profession.
spanish_meaning: La profesión es el trabajo de una persona.
spanish_example: Le encantaba navegar, así que eligió esa profesión.

english_meaning: A ball is a round object that is thrown, kicked, or hit in a game or sport.
english_example: Seth bought a new soccer ball.
spanish_meaning: Una pelota es un objeto redondo que se lanza, patea o golpea en juegos.
spanish_example: Seth compró una pelota de fútbol nueva.

english_meaning: The bottom is the lowest part, point, or level of something.
english_example: Sarah is so tall that her feet can touch the bottom of the swimming pool.
spanish_meaning: El fondo es la parte o nivel más bajo de algo.
spanish_example: Sarah es tan alta que puede tocar el fondo de la piscina.

english_meaning: A company is a business or organization that makes or sells goods or services.
english_example: I want to work for a small software company.
spanish_meaning: Una empresa es un negocio u organización que fábrica o vende bienes o servicios.
spanish_example: Quiero trabajar en una empresa de software pequeña.

english_meaning: To drink is to take liquid into the body through the mouth.
english_example: The woman likes to drink water after she exercises.
spanish_meaning: Beber es tomar líquido por la boca.
spanish_example: La mujer bebé agua después de hacer ejercicio.

english_meaning: A few things is a small number of them.
english_example: I have only a few coins.
spanish_meaning: Pocos significa una cantidad pequeña.
spanish_example: Solo tengo unas pocas monedas.

english_meaning: A line is a row of people or things.
english_example: This is the longest line I have ever seen.
spanish_meaning: Una fila es una fila de personas o cosas.
spanish_example: Esta es la fila más larga que he visto.

english_meaning: A pet is an animal such as a cat or dog that people keep and care for.
english_example: Tyler likes to spend time with his pet dog.
spanish_meaning: Una mascota es un animal que las personas cuidan.
spanish_example: Tyler pasa tiempo con su perro mascota.

english_meaning: A product is something grown or made in a factory in order to be sold.
english_example: There’s no room for even one more product in Melissa’s bag.
spanish_meaning: Un producto es algo producido o cultivado para venderlo.
spanish_example: No hay espacio para otro producto en la bolsa de Melissa.

english_meaning: A responsible person is in charge of someone or something.
english_example: Peter is responsible for leading his department.
spanish_meaning: Responsable significa estar a cargo de alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Peter es responsable de dirigir su departamento.

english_meaning: To sell is to give something to someone in exchange for money.
english_example: This man’s job is to sell houses.
spanish_meaning: Vender es dar algo a cambio de dinero.
spanish_example: El trabajo de este hombre es vender casas.

english_meaning: A snake is an animal with a long, thin body and no legs.
english_example: Be careful of the snake in the tree.
spanish_meaning: Una serpiente es un animal con cuerpo largo y delgado sin patas.
spanish_example: Cuidado con la serpiente en el árbol.

english_meaning: To stand is to use the legs and feet to hold the body upright.
english_example: Allan prefers to stand and work at his desk.
spanish_meaning: Estar de pie es sostener el cuerpo con las piernas y pies.
spanish_example: Allan prefiere estar de pie y trabajar en su escritorio.

english_meaning: A strange thing is unusual or surprising.
english_example: They are wearing strange masks.
spanish_meaning: Extraño significa inusual o sorprendente.
spanish_example: Llevan máscaras extrañas.

english_meaning: Tea is a drink made by pouring boiling water onto dried leaves.
english_example: Many people drink green tea because it has many health benefits.
spanish_meaning: El té es una bebida hecha al verter agua hirviendo sobre hojas secas.
spanish_example: Muchas personas toman té verde por sus beneficios.

english_meaning: To test is to examine something to see if its quality is good.
english_example: His job is to test the electricity to make sure it works correctly.
spanish_meaning: Probar significa comprobar algo para ver su calidad.
spanish_example: Su trabajo es probar la electricidad para asegurar que funcione.

english_meaning: A tongue is the movable muscle inside the mouth that is used to speak, eat, and drink.
english_example: The cat uses its tongue to drink water.
spanish_meaning: La lengua es el músculo de la boca usado para hablar y comer.
spanish_example: El gato usa su lengua para beber agua.

english_meaning: They refers to two or more people or things.
english_example: They are playing a fun game.
spanish_meaning: Ellos/ellas se refieren a dos o más personas o cosas.
spanish_example: Ellos están jugando un juego divertido.

english_meaning: A type is a particular kind or group of things or people.
english_example: Tulips are a type of flower.
spanish_meaning: Un tipo es una clase o especie de cosas o personas.
spanish_example: Los tulipanes son un tipo de flor.

english_meaning: Very is used to emphasize an adjective or adverb.
english_example: An elephant is a very big animal.
spanish_meaning: Muy se usa para enfatizar un adjetivo o adverbio.
spanish_example: Un elefante es un animal muy grande.

english_meaning: To wait is to stay in a place until an expected event happens.
english_example: She has to wait for the airplane to arrive.
spanish_meaning: Esperar es quedarse en un lugar hasta que ocurra algo.
spanish_example: Tiene que esperar la llegada del avión.

english_meaning: To accomplish something means to finish it.
english_example: He accomplished his goal of running ten miles.
spanish_meaning: Lograr algo significa terminarlo.
spanish_example: Logró su meta de correr diez millas.

english_meaning: To approve of something means you like or agree with that thing.
english_example: Her co-workers approved her new plan.
spanish_meaning: Aprobar algo significa estar de acuerdo o gustar.
spanish_example: Sus compañeros aprobaron su nuevo plan.

english_meaning: Approximate means close to an exact amount, number, or time.
english_example: My approximate height is two meters.
spanish_meaning: Aproximado significa cerca de una cantidad, número o tiempo exacto.
spanish_example: Mi altura aproximada es de dos metros.

english_meaning: A barrier is something that blocks a path or way.
english_example: The Great Wall was a barrier between China and its enemies.
spanish_meaning: Una barrera es algo que bloquea un camino.
spanish_example: La Gran Muralla fue una barrera entre China y sus enemigos.

english_meaning: To detect means to notice or find something.
english_example: The boy ran to the kitchen when he detected the smell of cookies.
spanish_meaning: Detectar significa notar o encontrar algo.
spanish_example: El niño corrió a la cocina cuando detectó el olor de galletas.

english_meaning: A duty is something that a person has to do.
english_example: It is parents’ duty to take care of their children.
spanish_meaning: Un deber es algo que alguien tiene que hacer.
spanish_example: Es deber de los padres cuidar de sus hijos.

english_meaning: An elementary thing is the first or most simple thing.
english_example: Children go to elementary school before high school.
spanish_meaning: Básico es lo más simple o inicial.
spanish_example: Los niños van a la escuela primaria antes de la secundaria.

english_meaning: A failure happens when something is not done right.
english_example: My cooking ended in failure because I burned the food.
spanish_meaning: Un fracaso ocurre cuando algo no sale bien.
spanish_example: Mi cocina terminó en fracaso porque quemé la comida.

english_meaning: Something gradual happens slowly.
english_example: Children learn to read at a gradual pace. They do not learn right away.
spanish_meaning: Gradual significa que algo sucede despacio.
spanish_example: Los niños aprenden a leer a un ritmo gradual.

english_meaning: An immigrant is a person who moves to a different country.
english_example: My parents were immigrants. They came from Poland.
spanish_meaning: Un inmigrante es una persona que se muda a otro país.
spanish_example: Mis padres eran inmigrantes; vinieron de Polonia.

english_meaning: To insert something means to put it in something else.
english_example: He inserted an extra sentence into the story.
spanish_meaning: Insertar algo significa ponerlo dentro de otra cosa.
spanish_example: Insertó una frase extra en la historia.

english_meaning: An instant is a very short amount of time.
english_example: A microwave oven cooks food in an instant.
spanish_meaning: Un instante es una cantidad de tiempo muy corta.
spanish_example: El microondas cocina la comida en un instante.

english_meaning: Poverty is the state of being poor.
english_example: Poverty is a problem in many countries around the world.
spanish_meaning: La pobreza es la situación de ser pobre.
spanish_example: La pobreza es un problema en muchos países del mundo.

english_meaning: To pretend means to make believe something is real.
english_example: The boy liked to pretend he was a king.
spanish_meaning: Fingir significa hacer creer algo que no es real.
spanish_example: Al chico le gustaba fingir que era un rey.

english_meaning: A rank is a person’s place in an order of people.
english_example: The man got to the rank of captain in the navy.
spanish_meaning: El rango es el puesto que ocupa alguien en una jerarquía.
spanish_example: El hombre alcanzó el rango de capitán en la marina.

english_meaning: Recognition is the act of getting praise from other people.
english_example: The hero got recognition for his brave deed.
spanish_meaning: El reconocimiento es recibir aprobación o elogios de otros.
spanish_example: El héroe recibió reconocimiento por su acto valiente.

english_meaning: To refrigerate something means to make it cold.
english_example: Supermarkets refrigerate fruit to make it last long.
spanish_meaning: Refrigerar algo significa hacerlo frío.
spanish_example: Los supermercados refrigeran frutas para que duren más.

english_meaning: Rent is the money people pay to live in a certain place.
english_example: To live in this house, I have to pay rent at the start of each month.
spanish_meaning: El alquiler es el dinero que se paga para vivir en un lugar.
spanish_example: Para vivir en está casa, tengo que pagar el alquiler al comienzo de cada mes.

english_meaning: To retire is to leave a job, usually because of old age.
english_example: My father is sixty-five years old. He is about to retire from work.
spanish_meaning: Jubilarse es dejar el trabajo, normalmente por la edad.
spanish_example: Mi padre tiene 65 años y está por jubilarse.

english_meaning: A statistic is a number that tells a fact about something.
english_example: The statistics showed that we did just as well this year as last year.
spanish_meaning: Una estadística es un número que muestra un hecho.
spanish_example: Las estadísticas mostraron que estuvimos igual de bien este año.

english_meaning: An accident is unexpected, and may cause some trouble.
english_example: He damaged the car in an accident.
spanish_meaning: Un accidente es un suceso inesperado que puede causar problemas.
spanish_example: Dañó el coche en un accidente.

english_meaning: An astronaut is a person who goes into outer space.
english_example: The astronaut was walking on the moon.
spanish_meaning: Un astronauta es una persona que viaja al espacio.
spanish_example: El astronauta caminaba sobre la luna.

english_meaning: A person who is awake is not asleep.
english_example: Sometimes, I lay awake in bed because I am not tired.
spanish_meaning: Despierto significa no estar dormido.
spanish_example: A veces me quedó despierto en la cama porque no tengo sueño.

english_meaning: Courage is the feeling of not being afraid.
english_example: The man had the courage to touch the lion.
spanish_meaning: El coraje es sentir valentía y no tener miedo.
spanish_example: El hombre tuvo coraje para tocar al león.

english_meaning: To float is to move on top of water without sinking.
english_example: The boy’s toy boat floated in the pool.
spanish_meaning: Flotar es moverse sobre el agua sin hundirse.
spanish_example: El barco de juguete del niño flotó en la piscina.

english_meaning: To grant something is to allow someone to have it.
english_example: The teacher granted us a break after studying hard all day.
spanish_meaning: Conceder algo es permitir que alguien lo tenga.
spanish_example: El profesor nos concedió un descanso después de estudiar todo el día.

english_meaning: Gravity is the force that makes things fall to Earth.
english_example: There is no gravity in space.
spanish_meaning: La gravedad es la fuerza que hace caer las cosas en la Tierra.
spanish_example: No hay gravedad en el espacio.

english_meaning: A jewel is a beautiful stone that is worth a lot of money.
english_example: A diamond is one of the most expensive jewels in the world.
spanish_meaning: Una joya es una piedra preciosa valiosa.
spanish_example: Un diamante es una de las joyas más caras del mundo.

english_meaning: A miner is a person who works in a mine.
english_example: The miner was looking for gold.
spanish_meaning: Un minero es una persona que trabaja en una mina.
spanish_example: El minero buscaba oro.

english_meaning: A mineral is a type of substance found in the Earth.
english_example: Rocks are made up of different kinds of minerals.
spanish_meaning: Un mineral es un tipo de sustancia que se encuentra en la Tierra.
spanish_example: Las rocas están formadas por distintos minerales.

english_meaning: To participate is to be active and do something.
english_example: The students participated in the school play.
spanish_meaning: Participar es estar involucrado y actuar.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes participaron en la obra de la escuela.

english_meaning: Permission means the act of allowing some action.
english_example: I have permission to drive my mom’s car.
spanish_meaning: Permiso significa permitir una acción.
spanish_example: Tengo permiso para conducir el coche de mi mamá.

english_meaning: To pour a liquid means to make it come out of a container.
english_example: I poured some milk into my sister’s cup.
spanish_meaning: Verter un líquido es hacerlo salir de un recipiente.
spanish_example: Vertí un poco de leche en la taza de mi hermana.

english_meaning: A raw material is natural and has not been processed.
english_example: The company dumped raw sewage into the river.
spanish_meaning: Crudo significa natural y sin procesar.
spanish_example: La empresa vertía aguas residuales crudas al río.

english_meaning: A satellite is a machine sent into space to get information.
english_example: The satellite was traveling around the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Un satélite es un aparato enviado al espacio para obtener información.
spanish_example: El satélite viajaba alrededor de la Tierra.

english_meaning: The scale of something is its size, especially when it is very large.
english_example: I was surprised by the scale of the buildings in the downtown area.
spanish_meaning: La escala de algo es su tamaño, sobre todo cuando es muy grande.
spanish_example: Me sorprendió la escala de los edificios del centro.

english_meaning: To skip something is to not do it.
english_example: He skipped work to get more sleep.
spanish_meaning: Saltar algo es no hacerlo.
spanish_example: Se saltó el trabajo para dormir más.

english_meaning: To stretch is to make your arms or legs reach out.
english_example: She stretched her body before exercising.
spanish_meaning: Estirar es extender brazos o piernas.
spanish_example: Ella estiró su cuerpo antes de hacer ejercicio.

english_meaning: A telescope is a tool people use to look at the stars.
english_example: With a telescope, you can see the moon and stars easily.
spanish_meaning: Un telescopio es un instrumento para mirar las estrellas.
spanish_example: Con un telescopio se pueden ver bien la luna y las estrellas.

english_meaning: An underground action happens below the surface of the Earth.
english_example: Subway trains travel underground.
spanish_meaning: Subterráneo significa debajo de la superficie.
spanish_example: Los trenes viajan por debajo de tierra.

english_meaning: An alarm is something that warns people of danger.
english_example: When the students heard the fire alarm, they left the building.
spanish_meaning: Una alarma es algo que advierte a la gente de peligro.
spanish_example: Cuando los alumnos oyeron la alarma de incendio, salieron del edificio.

english_meaning: To arrest someone means to catch that person for doing something bad.
english_example: The man was arrested for breaking the law.
spanish_meaning: Detener a alguien es capturarlo por haber hecho algo malo.
spanish_example: El hombre fue detenido por infringir la ley.

english_meaning: An award is a prize for doing something well.
english_example: He got an award for having the best grades in class.
spanish_meaning: Un premio es un reconocimiento por hacer algo bien.
spanish_example: Recibió un premio por tener las mejores notas.

english_meaning: A breed is a group of animals within a species.
english_example: I like small dog breeds, such as terriers.
spanish_meaning: La raza es un grupo de animales de una misma especie.
spanish_example: Me gustan las razas pequeñas de perro, como los terriers.

english_meaning: A bucket is a round container to put things in.
english_example: I filled the bucket with water.
spanish_meaning: Un cubo es un recipiente redondo para guardar cosas.
spanish_example: Llené el cubo con agua.

english_meaning: A contest is a game or a race.
english_example: The girls had a contest to see who could jump higher.
spanish_meaning: Un concurso es una carrera o juego.
spanish_example: Las niñas tuvieron un concurso para ver quién saltaba más alto.

english_meaning: To convict means to prove that someone did a bad thing.
english_example: He was convicted of the crime and sent to jail.
spanish_meaning: Condenar significa probar que alguien hizo algo malo.
spanish_example: Fue condenado por el delito y enviado a prisión.

english_meaning: A festival is an event that is held to celebrate a particular thing.
english_example: I heard the song at the music festival in London.
spanish_meaning: Un festival es un evento para celebrar algo.
spanish_example: Escuché la canción en el festival de música de Londres.

english_meaning: A garage is the part of a house where people put their cars.
english_example: My car does not get dirty because I keep it in the garage.
spanish_meaning: Un garaje es el lugar de la casa para guardar coches.
spanish_example: Mi coche no se ensucia porque lo guardo en el garaje.

english_meaning: A journalist is a person who writes news stories.
english_example: The journalist took notes for a story he was writing.
spanish_meaning: Un periodista es una persona que escribe noticias.
spanish_example: El periodista tomó notas para una historia.

english_meaning: A pup is a young dog.
english_example: All the girl wanted for her birthday was a pup.
spanish_meaning: Un cachorro es un perro joven.
spanish_example: Todo lo que la niña quería de cumpleaños era un cachorro.

english_meaning: To qualify is to have or do things that are needed for something.
english_example: He qualified to go to the final match by beating the opponent.
spanish_meaning: Calificar es cumplir lo necesario para algo.
spanish_example: Se clasificó para la final al vencer a su rival.

english_meaning: To repair something is to fix it.
english_example: I repaired the flat tire on my car.
spanish_meaning: Reparar algo es arreglarlo.
spanish_example: Reparé la llanta desinflada de mi coche.

english_meaning: To resume something means to start it again after taking a break.
english_example: I put the newspaper down to eat breakfast. Then, I resumed reading.
spanish_meaning: Reanudar algo significa empezar otra vez tras una pausa.
spanish_example: Dejé el periódico para desayunar y luego lo reanudé.

english_meaning: To rob is to take property by using force.
english_example: A thief has robbed me of my passport.
spanish_meaning: Robar es llevarse algo usando fuerza.
spanish_example: Un ladrón me robó el pasaporte.

english_meaning: To slip means to slide and fall down.
english_example: The man slipped on the wet floor.
spanish_meaning: Resbalar significa deslizarse y caer.
spanish_example: El hombre resbaló en el suelo mojado.

english_meaning: Somewhat means to some degree, but not to a large degree.
english_example: James was somewhat upset when he had to move heavy boxes.
spanish_meaning: Algo significa en cierta medida.
spanish_example: James estaba algo molesto al tener que mover cajas pesadas.

english_meaning: A stable thing will not move, change, or fall over.
english_example: The chair is stable. Its legs are strong.
spanish_meaning: Algo estable no se mueve ni cambia con facilidad.
spanish_example: La silla es estable; sus patas son fuertes.

english_meaning: A tissue is a soft piece of paper people use to wipe their noses.
english_example: There was a box of tissue on the table.
spanish_meaning: tissue es un papel suave para limpiarse la nariz.
spanish_example: Había una caja de pañuelos en la mesa.

english_meaning: A yard is the ground just outside of a house.
english_example: The girls jumped rope in the yard.
spanish_meaning: El patio es el terreno justo fuera de una casa.
spanish_example: Las niñas saltaban la cuerda en el patio.

english_meaning: The best person or thing is better than all the others.
english_example: I got the best score on the math test.
spanish_meaning: El mejor es mejor que los demás.
spanish_example: Saqué la mejor nota en el examen de matemáticas.

english_meaning: A card is a small piece of plastic or paper used to buy or use things.
english_example: Adam used his library card to borrow a book.
spanish_meaning: Una tarjeta es una pequeña pieza de plástico o papel para comprar o usar cosas.
spanish_example: Adam usó su tarjeta de la biblioteca para pedir un libro.

english_meaning: A crowd is a large group of people who are together in one place.
english_example: The crowd waved to the camera.
spanish_meaning: Un multitud es un grupo grande de personas en un mismo lugar.
spanish_example: La multitud agitó los brazos hacia la cámara.

english_meaning: A day is a period of twenty-four hours, beginning at midnight.
english_example: There are two more days until the weekend.
spanish_meaning: Un día es un período de veinticuatro horas.
spanish_example: Faltan dos días para el fin de semana.

english_meaning: A dish is a type of food that is cooked in a particular way.
english_example: My favorite dish at the restaurant is chicken curry.
spanish_meaning: Un plato es un tipo de comida preparada de una manera concreta.
spanish_example: Mi plato favorito en el restaurante es el curry de pollo.

english_meaning: An easy action is not difficult to do.
english_example: Karen is happy because her English homework is easy.
spanish_meaning: Algo fácil no es difícil de hacer.
spanish_example: Karen está feliz porque su tarea de inglés es fácil.

english_meaning: To experience is to do or see something or have something happen to you.
english_example: Going to the concert was the best thing I’ve ever experienced.
spanish_meaning: Experimentar es vivir o hacer algo personalmente.
spanish_example: Ir al concierto fue la mejor experiencia que he tenido.

english_meaning: A hotel is a place where people stay overnight when they are traveling.
english_example: This family is staying at their favorite hotel.
spanish_meaning: Un hotel es un lugar donde la gente se queda al viajar.
spanish_example: Esta familia se hospeda en su hotel favorito.

english_meaning: An hour is sixty minutes.
english_example: The man waited for the train for over an hour.
spanish_meaning: Una hora tiene sesenta minutos.
spanish_example: El hombre esperó más de una hora para el tren.

english_meaning: Light is a form of energy or brightness that makes it possible to see something.
english_example: I will turn on the light so that you can see.
spanish_meaning: La luz es la energía o brillo que permite ver.
spanish_example: Encenderé la luz para que puedas ver.

english_meaning: A market is a place where people buy and sell products or food.
english_example: I go to the market every weekend to buy vegetables.
spanish_meaning: Un mercado es un lugar donde la gente compra y vende productos o comida.
spanish_example: Voy al mercado cada fin de semana para comprar verduras.

english_meaning: To plan is to think about and arrange the details of something you want to do.
english_example: It’s Sam’s turn to plan the company party.
spanish_meaning: Planificar es pensar y organizar detalles de algo que quieres hacer.
spanish_example: Es turno de Sam de planificar la fiesta de la empresa.

english_meaning: The price is the amount of money needed to pay for something.
english_example: Julie is checking the price of a sweater.
spanish_meaning: El precio es la cantidad de dinero necesaria para pagar algo.
spanish_example: Julie está buscando el precio de un suéter.

english_meaning: A short thing is not long or not tall.
english_example: The days are short in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Algo corto no es largo o no es alto.
spanish_example: Los días son cortos en invierno.

english_meaning: To shop is to visit places where goods are sold in order to look at and buy things.
english_example: Tom decided to shop for groceries on his way home.
spanish_meaning: Comprar es ir a lugares de venta para mirar o adquirir cosas.
spanish_example: Tom decidió comprar víveres de camino a casa.

english_meaning: A station is a place where buses and trains stop for passengers.
english_example: This man is waiting at the train station.
spanish_meaning: Una estación es un lugar donde paran buses o trenes.
spanish_example: Este hombre espera en la estación de tren.

english_meaning: To surprise is to cause something that is unexpected.
english_example: His parents decided to surprise him with a puppy.
spanish_meaning: Sorprender es causar algo inesperado.
spanish_example: Sus padres decidieron sorprenderlo con un cachorro.

english_meaning: A system is a group of related parts that move or work together.
english_example: This device controls the building’s heating system.
spanish_meaning: Un sistema es un conjunto de partes que trabajan juntas.
spanish_example: Este dispositivo controla el sistema de calefacción del edificio.

english_meaning: A taxi is a car and driver that you pay to take you somewhere.
english_example: Gary drives a taxi, so he knows the roads very well.
spanish_meaning: Un taxi es un coche con conductor al que se paga por llevarte.
spanish_example: Gary conduce un taxi, así que conoce muy bien las carreteras.

english_meaning: Two is the word for the number 2.
english_example: Two friends study together at the coffee shop.
spanish_meaning: Dos es el número 2.
spanish_example: Dos amigas estudian juntas en la cafetería.

english_meaning: A bath is the act of sitting in a tub of water in order to get clean.
english_example: After playing in the dirt, the boy took a bath.
spanish_meaning: Un baño es la acción de sentarse en un recipiente de agua para limpiarse.
spanish_example: Después de ensuciarse, el niño se dio un baño.

english_meaning: To bend is to move something so it is not straight.
english_example: Lee bent over and picked up the paper on the ground.
spanish_meaning: Doblar es mover algo para que no quede recto.
spanish_example: Lee se dobló y recogió el papel del suelo.

english_meaning: To chew is to break up food by using the mouth and teeth.
english_example: I always chew my food carefully before swallowing it.
spanish_meaning: Masticar es descomponer la comida con la boca y los dientes.
spanish_example: Yo mastico mi comida bien antes de tragarla.

english_meaning: A disabled person has a physical problem that makes some activities difficult.
english_example: The disabled man used a wheelchair to move around.
spanish_meaning: Discapacitado significa tener una dificultad física que complica algunas actividades.
spanish_example: El hombre discapacitado usó una silla de ruedas para moverse.

english_meaning: A fantastic thing is really good.
english_example: The student did a fantastic job on his project and got an award.
spanish_meaning: Fantástico significa realmente muy bueno.
spanish_example: El estudiante hizo un trabajo fantástico y ganó un premio.

english_meaning: Fiction is a story that is not true.
english_example: I enjoy reading works of fiction because they are very entertaining.
spanish_meaning: La ficción es una historia que no es real.
spanish_example: Disfruto leer obras de ficción porque son entretenidas.

english_meaning: A flag is a piece of colored cloth that represents something.
english_example: Our country has a beautiful flag.
spanish_meaning: Una bandera es una tela de colores que representa algo.
spanish_example: Nuestro país tiene una bandera hermosa.

english_meaning: To inspect is to look at something carefully.
english_example: The mechanic inspected our car to see if it had any problems.
spanish_meaning: Inspeccionar significa mirar algo con atención.
spanish_example: El mecánico inspeccionó nuestro coche para ver si tenía fallas.

english_meaning: A journal is a type of magazine that deals with an academic subject.
english_example: Mi-young was busy working on an article for an art journal.
spanish_meaning: Una revista es una publicación especializada en un tema.
spanish_example: Mi-young trabajaba en un artículo para una revista de arte.

english_meaning: A liquid is a substance that is neither solid nor gas.
english_example: Water is the most important liquid for life.
spanish_meaning: Un líquido no es sólido ni gas.
spanish_example: El agua es el líquido más importante para la vida.

english_meaning: To marvel at something is to feel surprise and interest in it.
english_example: We marveled at her excellent piano playing.
spanish_meaning: Maravillarse es sentir sorpresa e interés.
spanish_example: Se maravillaron de su excelente ejecución del piano.

english_meaning: To overcome a problem is to successfully fix it.
english_example: She overcame her shyness and spoke in front of the class.
spanish_meaning: Superar un problema significa resolverlo con éxito.
spanish_example: Venció su timidez y habló delante de la clase.

english_meaning: To recall something is to remember it.
english_example: She was trying to recall what she had told her friend.
spanish_meaning: Recordar algo es traerlo a la mente.
spanish_example: Intentaba recordar lo que le había dicho a su amiga.

english_meaning: To regret something is to wish that it hadn’t happened.
english_example: I regret that I was mean to my sister.
spanish_meaning: Arrepentirse es desear que algo no hubiera pasado.
spanish_example: Lamento haber sido grosero con mi hermana.

english_meaning: A soul is a person’s spirit.
english_example: Some people believe that the soul lives after the body dies.
spanish_meaning: El alma es el espíritu de una persona.
spanish_example: Algunas personas creen que el alma vive después de la muerte.

english_meaning: Sufficient shows that something is enough, in quality or quantity.
english_example: After eating a sufficient amount of food, I left the table.
spanish_meaning: Suficiente significa que algo es bastante en calidad o cantidad.
spanish_example: Cuando comí una cantidad suficiente de comida, me levanté de la mesa.

english_meaning: Surgery is medical treatment involving a doctor cutting into a body.
english_example: I needed surgery to repair my leg after the accident.
spanish_meaning: La cirugía es un tratamiento médico que implica incisión.
spanish_example: Necesité cirugía para reparar mi pierna tras el accidente.

english_meaning: A tough thing is difficult.
english_example: The man passed his driving test even though it was very tough.
spanish_meaning: Algo difícil es complicado o exigente.
spanish_example: El hombre aprobó su examen de conducir aunque fue muy difícil.

english_meaning: A tube is a pipe through which water or air passes.
english_example: The pile of tubes was going to be put in the ground.
spanish_meaning: Un tubo es una caña o manguera por donde pasa agua o aire.
spanish_example: El montón de tubos iba a colocarse bajo tierra.

english_meaning: The value of something is what it is worth.
english_example: Your love for me has greater value than gold.
spanish_meaning: El valor de algo es lo que vale.
spanish_example: Tu amor por mí vale más que el oro.

english_meaning: An atom is the smallest unit of a substance.
english_example: A molecule consists of a combination of two or more atoms.
spanish_meaning: Un átomo es la unidad mínima de una sustancia.
spanish_example: Una molécula consiste en dos o más átomos.

english_meaning: A beautiful thing is good to look at.
english_example: There was a beautiful sunset.
spanish_meaning: Algo hermoso es agradable de ver.
spanish_example: Había una puesta de sol hermosa.

english_meaning: Breadth is the distance from one side to the other side of something.
english_example: The breadth of the northern wall of the house is twenty meters.
spanish_meaning: La amplitud es la distancia de un lado a otro.
spanish_example: La amplitud del muro norte de la casa es de veinte metros.

english_meaning: A comet is an object in space made of ice and rock with a tail of glowing dust.
english_example: Comets take many decades to complete an orbit around a star.
spanish_meaning: Un cometa es un objeto de hielo y roca con una cola luminosa.
spanish_example: Los cometas tardan décadas en dar una vuelta alrededor de una estrella.

english_meaning: To cover something is to put things over it.
english_example: The Earth was covered with clouds.
spanish_meaning: Cubrir algo es poner algo encima de ello.
spanish_example: La Tierra estaba cubierta de nubes.

english_meaning: Despair is the feeling of having no hope.
english_example: After we lost the big account, our salespeople were filled with despair.
spanish_meaning: La desesperación es un sentimiento de no tener esperanza.
spanish_example: Después de perder la gran cuenta, los vendedores se llenaron de desesperación.

english_meaning: To form is to make or to shape something.
english_example: They formed a new government.
spanish_meaning: Formar es crear o dar forma a algo.
spanish_example: Formaron un nuevo gobierno.

english_meaning: A fragment is a small part of something.
english_example: After the light broke, there were fragments of glass to clean up.
spanish_meaning: Un fragmento es una parte pequeña de algo.
spanish_example: Después de romper la luz, hubo fragmentos de vidrio para recoger.

english_meaning: A galaxy is an extremely large collection of star systems.
english_example: Our solar system is located in the outer area of our galaxy.
spanish_meaning: Una galaxia es una gran colección de sistemas estelares.
spanish_example: Nuestro sistema solar está en la parte externa de nuestra galaxia.

english_meaning: Gloom is a state of almost complete darkness or sadness.
english_example: In the gloom of the morning, it was difficult to see the boat on the lake.
spanish_meaning: La penumbra es una oscuridad casi total o tristeza.
spanish_example: En la penumbra de la mañana era difícil ver la barca en el lago.

english_meaning: Something large is very big.
english_example: I was frightened by a large bird.
spanish_meaning: Algo grande es muy grande.
spanish_example: Me asustó un ave grande.

english_meaning: The moon is an object that travels around our Earth.
english_example: The moon looks beautiful tonight.
spanish_meaning: La luna es un cuerpo que gira alrededor de la Tierra.
spanish_example: La luna se ve hermosa está noche.

english_meaning: To radiate means to send out energy or heat.
english_example: The heat from the fireplace radiated throughout the room.
spanish_meaning: Radiar significa emitir energía o calor.
spanish_example: El calor de la chimenea radiaba por toda la sala.

english_meaning: To roam means to move around without a plan or purpose.
english_example: All day, the cows roamed around the field eating grass.
spanish_meaning: Vagar significa moverse sin rumbo fijo.
spanish_example: Todo el día, las vacas vagaron por el campo comiendo pasto.

english_meaning: A solitary thing is lonely or the only one.
english_example: The only thing in the room was a solitary chair.
spanish_meaning: Algo solitario es el único o algo aislado.
spanish_example: La única cosa en la habitación era una silla solitaria.

english_meaning: The spectrum is the full range of color ranging from red to violet.
english_example: You can see the entire spectrum in a rainbow.
spanish_meaning: El espectro es el conjunto completo de colores.
spanish_example: Puedes ver todo el espectro en un arcoíris.

english_meaning: A sphere is a three-dimensional round shape, like a ball.
english_example: The balloons were inflated into a variety of colorful spheres.
spanish_meaning: Una esfera es una forma redonda tridimensional, como una pelota.
spanish_example: Se inflaron balones en forma de esferas de muchos colores.

english_meaning: A star is a bright shining thing in the night sky.
english_example: The stars come out at night.
spanish_meaning: Una estrella es un objeto brillante en el cielo nocturno.
spanish_example: Las estrellas salen por la noche.

english_meaning: Status is the position of something or someone in relation to others.
english_example: She had achieved the status of being the smartest girl in the class.
spanish_meaning: El estatus es la posición o rango de alguien.
spanish_example: Había alcanzado el estatus de ser la niña más inteligente de la clase.

english_meaning: Something ugly is not good to look at.
english_example: It was an ugly sight.
spanish_meaning: Algo feo no es agradable de mirar.
spanish_example: Era una visión fea.

english_meaning: To accuse someone of something is to blame them for doing it.
english_example: She accused her brother of breaking her computer.
spanish_meaning: Acusar a alguien de algo es culparlo por ello.
spanish_example: Ella acusó a su hermano de romper su computadora.

english_meaning: To adjust something means to change it so it is better.
english_example: He adjusted the old guitar to make it sound better.
spanish_meaning: Ajustar algo significa modificarlo para que esté mejor.
spanish_example: Ajustó la vieja guitarra para que sonara mejor.

english_meaning: To amuse someone means to do something that is funny or entertaining.
english_example: The singer was very good. She amused the crowd.
spanish_meaning: Entretener a alguien es hacer algo divertido.
spanish_example: La cantante era muy buena; entretuvo a la multitud.

english_meaning: Coral is the hard, colorful material formed by the shells of animals.
english_example: The diver admired the beautiful coral under the water.
spanish_meaning: El coral es la sustancia dura y colorida formada por conchas de animales.
spanish_example: El buzo admiró el hermoso coral bajo el agua.

english_meaning: Cotton is a cloth made from the fibers of the cotton plant.
english_example: I like to wear clothes made from cotton in the summer.
spanish_meaning: El algodón es una tela hecha de fibras de la planta del algodón.
spanish_example: Me gusta vestir en verano con ropa de algodón.

english_meaning: To crash means to hit and break something.
english_example: There was a loud noise when the car crashed into the tree.
spanish_meaning: Estrellar significa chocar y romper algo.
spanish_example: Hubo un ruido fuerte cuando el coche se estrelló contra el árbol.

english_meaning: A deck is a wooden floor built outside of a house or the floor of a ship.
english_example: A ship will store many supplies below its deck.
spanish_meaning: La cubierta es el piso exterior de una casa o de un barco.
spanish_example: Un barco guarda muchas provisiones debajo de la cubierta.

english_meaning: To engage in something means to do it.
english_example: Dad was engaged in sawing a piece of wood in half.
spanish_meaning: Involucrarse en algo significa hacerlo.
spanish_example: Papá estaba involucrado en cortar un madero por la mitad.

english_meaning: A firm thing is solid but not too hard.
english_example: He sleeps better on a firm bed.
spanish_meaning: Una cosa firme es sólida y resistente.
spanish_example: Duerme mejor en una cama firme.

english_meaning: Fuel is something that creates heat or energy.
english_example: Wood is the fuel that burns to make heat in this fire.
spanish_meaning: El combustible es lo que produce calor o energía.
spanish_example: La leña es el combustible que arde para dar calor en este fuego.

english_meaning: Something grand is big and liked by people.
english_example: The grand mountain rose high into the sky.
spanish_meaning: Algo grandioso es grande y admirado por la gente.
spanish_example: La montaña grandiosa se elevaba alto hacia el cielo.

english_meaning: A hurricane is a bad storm that happens over the ocean.
english_example: The wind from the hurricane bent the palm tree.
spanish_meaning: Un huracán es una tormenta fuerte sobre el océano.
spanish_example: El viento del huracán dobló la palmera.

english_meaning: A loss is the act or instance of losing something.
english_example: I suffered a big loss while I was gambling.
spanish_meaning: La pérdida es el hecho de perder algo.
spanish_example: Tuve una gran pérdida mientras apostaba.

english_meaning: A plain thing is simple and not decorated.
english_example: He bought a pair of plain white shoes over the weekend.
spanish_meaning: Algo llano es simple y sin adornos.
spanish_example: Compró un par de zapatos blancos sencillos el fin de semana.

english_meaning: A reef is a group of rocks or coral in the ocean.
english_example: He walked along the reef and looked at the water below.
spanish_meaning: Un arrecife es un grupo de rocas o coral en el mar.
spanish_example: Caminó por el arrecife y miró el agua abajo.

english_meaning: To shut something means to close it tightly.
english_example: Please shut the door; the air outside is cold.
spanish_meaning: Cerrar algo significa dejarlo bien cerrado.
spanish_example: Por favor, cierra la puerta; el aire de afuera está frío.

english_meaning: A strict person makes sure others follow rules.
english_example: The teacher is strict. She does not let students talk in class.
spanish_meaning: Una persona estricta se asegura de que se sigan las reglas.
spanish_example: La maestra es estricta y no deja que los alumnos hablen.

english_meaning: To surf means to use a special board to ride on waves in the ocean.
english_example: The students went to the beach to surf during their vacation.
spanish_meaning: Hacer surf significa deslizarse en una tabla sobre las olas.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes fueron a la playa a surfear durante las vacaciones.

english_meaning: A task is a piece of work to be done that is usually difficult.
english_example: My task for the weekend was to clean the entire back yard.
spanish_meaning: Una tarea es un trabajo que se debe hacer.
spanish_example: Mi tarea del fin de semana era limpiar todo el patio trasero.

english_meaning: A zone is an area that has different qualities from the ones around it.
english_example: Firefighters often work in danger zones.
spanish_meaning: Una zona es un área con características distintas.
spanish_example: Los bomberos suelen trabajar en zonas de peligro.

english_meaning: An apology is what people say to show that they are sorry.
english_example: After arguing with her teacher, the girl wrote the teacher an apology.
spanish_meaning: Una disculpa es lo que se dice para mostrar que uno lamenta algo.
spanish_example: Después de discutir con su maestra, la niña escribió una disculpa.

english_meaning: A bold person is not afraid of doing something.
english_example: The bold man climbed the high mountain.
spanish_meaning: Alguien valiente no tiene miedo de hacer algo.
spanish_example: El hombre valiente escaló la montaña alta.

english_meaning: A bug is a small insect.
english_example: Birds like eating bugs.
spanish_meaning: Un bicho es un insecto pequeño.
spanish_example: Las aves comen bichos.

english_meaning: To capture something is to catch and hold it.
english_example: James tried to capture the bubbles in his hands.
spanish_meaning: Capturar algo es atraparlo y retenerlo.
spanish_example: James intentó capturar las burbujas en sus manos.

english_meaning: A duke is a man of high social rank but below a king or queen.
english_example: The duke ruled over the land.
spanish_meaning: Un duque es un noble de alto rango debajo de un rey.
spanish_example: El duque gobernaba la tierra.

english_meaning: To expose is to make known something that is hidden.
english_example: He took off his shirt to expose his costume.
spanish_meaning: Exponer es mostrar algo que estaba oculto.
spanish_example: Se quitó la camisa para exponer su disfraz.

english_meaning: Guilty people feel bad for something they did.
english_example: I felt guilty for taking my sister’s cookies.
spanish_meaning: Una persona culpable siente remordimiento por algo que hizo.
spanish_example: Me sentí culpable por tomar las galletas de mi hermana.

english_meaning: To hire someone is to pay that person money to work for you.
english_example: We hired a man to paint our house.
spanish_meaning: Contratar a alguien es pagarle para que trabaje para ti.
spanish_example: Contratamos a un hombre para pintar nuestra casa.

english_meaning: An innocent person is not guilty of a crime.
english_example: The judge said that the woman was innocent of the crime.
spanish_meaning: Una persona inocente no es culpable de un delito.
spanish_example: El juez dijo que la mujer era inocente del crimen.

english_meaning: A language is a system of communication.
english_example: The reporter spoke a language Sally had never heard before.
spanish_meaning: El idioma es un sistema de comunicación.
spanish_example: El reportero habló en un idioma que Sally nunca había oído antes.

english_meaning: A minister is an important person in government with many duties.
english_example: The minister of education controls the country’s schools.
spanish_meaning: Un ministro es una persona importante del gobierno con muchas responsabilidades.
spanish_example: El ministro de Educación dirige las escuelas del país.

english_meaning: Ordinary means normal, or not special in any way.
english_example: Today was just an ordinary day. Nothing unusual happened.
spanish_meaning: Ordinario significa normal o nada especial.
spanish_example: Hoy fue un día ordinario y no pasó nada extraño.

english_meaning: Something permanent lasts for a long time or forever.
english_example: We don’t know if Aunt Mildred’s visit will be a permanent one.
spanish_meaning: Algo permanente dura mucho tiempo o para siempre.
spanish_example: No sabemos si la visita de la tía Mildred será permanente.

english_meaning: To preserve is to protect something from harm.
english_example: Dad sprayed a chemical on the house to help preserve the walls.
spanish_meaning: Preservar es proteger algo de dañarse.
spanish_example: Papá roció un químico en la casa para preservar las paredes.

english_meaning: To pronounce is to say the sounds of letters or words.
english_example: Young children often have trouble pronouncing words right.
spanish_meaning: Pronunciar es decir los sonidos de letras o palabras.
spanish_example: A los niños pequeños a menudo les cuesta pronunciar bien las palabras.

english_meaning: To resemble someone is to look like that person.
english_example: The baby really resembles his father.
spanish_meaning: Parecerse significa verse parecido a alguien.
spanish_example: El bebé se parece mucho a su padre.

english_meaning: A symptom of a bad condition or illness is a sign that it is happening.
english_example: Sneezing and a high fever are symptoms of the common cold.
spanish_meaning: Un síntoma es una señal de que algo está ocurriendo.
spanish_example: Estornudar y tener fiebre alta son síntomas del resfriado.

english_meaning: Tobacco is a plant whose leaves are smoked, such as in cigarettes.
english_example: The tobacco in cigarettes is bad for you.
spanish_meaning: El tabaco es la planta cuyas hojas se fuman en los cigarrillos.
spanish_example: El tabaco de los cigarrillos es malo para la salud.

english_meaning: Twins are two children born at the same time.
english_example: My sister and I are twins. We look exactly the same.
spanish_meaning: Los gemelos son dos niños nacidos al mismo tiempo.
spanish_example: Mi hermana y yo somos gemelas. Nos parecemos mucho.

english_meaning: A witch is a woman with magical powers.
english_example: People think that witches fly around on broomsticks.
spanish_meaning: Una bruja es una mujer con poderes mágicos.
spanish_example: La gente cree que las brujas vuelan en escobas.

english_meaning: To accompany other people means to join them or go with them.
english_example: My brothers accompanied me to the movie.
spanish_meaning: Acompañar a alguien es unirse o ir con otras personas.
spanish_example: Mis hermanos me acompañaron al cine.

english_meaning: A bare thing is plain and not covered.
english_example: He likes to walk around in his bare feet.
spanish_meaning: Algo descubierto es una cosa sin cubrir.
spanish_example: A él le gusta caminar con los pies descalzos.
notes: nota: también puede significar «desnudo», según el contexto.

english_meaning: A branch is the part of a tree with leaves.
english_example: The monkey was hanging from a branch on the tree.
spanish_meaning: Una rama es la parte de un árbol con hojas.
spanish_example: El mono colgaba de una rama del árbol.

english_meaning: A breath is the air that goes into and out of one’s lungs.
english_example: You can’t take a breath under water.
spanish_meaning: La respiración es el aire que entra y sale de los pulmones.
spanish_example: No puedes tomar aire bajo el agua.

english_meaning: A bridge is something that is built over a river so people can cross it.
english_example: The old bridge fell into the river.
spanish_meaning: Un puente se construye sobre un río para cruzarlo.
spanish_example: El viejo puente se cayó al río.

english_meaning: To cast something means to throw it.
english_example: The fisherman cast his line into the water.
spanish_meaning: Lanzar algo es tirarlo.
spanish_example: El pescador lanzó su caña al agua.

english_meaning: To dare means to be brave enough to try something.
english_example: He dared to jump out of the airplane and skydive.
spanish_meaning: Atreverse significa tener valor para intentarlo.
spanish_example: Se atrevió a saltar del avión y hacer paracaidismo.

english_meaning: An electronic thing uses electricity to work.
english_example: I like having electronic devices such as an MP3 player.
spanish_meaning: Lo electrónico usa electricidad para funcionar.
spanish_example: Me gustan los dispositivos electrónicos, como un reproductor de música.

english_meaning: An inn is a place where travelers can rest and eat.
english_example: The visitor got a room at the inn.
spanish_meaning: Una posada es un lugar donde los viajeros pueden descansar y comer.
spanish_example: El visitante se alojó en una habitación de la posada.

english_meaning: A net is a bag made of strong thread. It is used to catch animals.
english_example: The boy caught butterflies in his net.
spanish_meaning: Una red es una malla para atrapar animales.
spanish_example: El chico atrapó mariposas con su red.

english_meaning: A philosophy is a way to think about truth and life.
english_example: My philosophy is “live and let live.”
spanish_meaning: La filosofía es una forma de pensar sobre la verdad y la vida.
spanish_example: Mi filosofía es “vive y deja vivir”.

english_meaning: A pot is a deep, round metal container used for cooking.
english_example: Don’t touch the pot on the stove. It’s hot.
spanish_meaning: Una olla es un recipiente profundo y redondo usado para cocinar.
spanish_example: No toques la olla del fuego. Está caliente.

english_meaning: A seed is the hard part of a plant or fruit that trees grow from.
english_example: I planted the seed in the dirt, hoping that it would grow into a tree.
spanish_meaning: La semilla es la parte dura de una planta o fruto de donde nace.
spanish_example: Sembré la semilla en la tierra, esperando que creciera un árbol.

english_meaning: A sharp object has a thin edge that cuts things easily.
english_example: That knife is very sharp. Be careful not to hurt yourself.
spanish_meaning: Algo afilado tiene un filo delgado que corta con facilidad.
spanish_example: Ese cuchillo está muy afilado. Ten cuidado.

english_meaning: A sort of something is a type of it.
english_example: What sort of instrument do you want to learn to play?
spanish_meaning: Un tipo es una clase o especie de algo.
spanish_example: ¿Qué tipo de instrumento quieres aprender a tocar?

english_meaning: To subtract means to take something away.
english_example: We learned how to subtract numbers in class.
spanish_meaning: Restar es quitar una cantidad.
spanish_example: Aprendimos cómo restar números en clase.

english_meaning: A tight thing is hard to move because it is firmly in place.
english_example: The knots were too tight to untie.
spanish_meaning: Algo ajustado está duro de mover porque está firme.
spanish_example: Los nudos estaban demasiado ajustados para desatarlos.

english_meaning: A virtual thing is very close to being true or accurate.
english_example: Because he’s popular, Joe is the virtual leader of the group.
spanish_meaning: Virtual significa casi o prácticamente real.
spanish_example: Como es popular, Joe es el líder virtual del grupo.

english_meaning: To weigh something means to measure how heavy it is.
english_example: The little dog weighed exactly 3 kilograms.
spanish_meaning: Pesar algo es medir su peso.
spanish_example: El perrito pesaba exactamente 3 kilos.

english_meaning: To whisper means to say very quietly.
english_example: We have to whisper in the library so people can focus on reading.
spanish_meaning: Susurrar es decir algo muy bajito.
spanish_example: Tenemos que susurrar en la biblioteca.

english_meaning: An abstract thing is an idea or thought, not a physical thing.
english_example: The idea of beauty is abstract and changes over time.
spanish_meaning: Algo abstracto es una idea, no una cosa física.
spanish_example: La idea de belleza es abstracta y cambia con el tiempo.

english_meaning: An annual event happens once a year.
english_example: The only time I see my aunts and uncles is at our annual family picnic.
spanish_meaning: Un evento anual ocurre una vez al año.
spanish_example: La única vez que veo a mis tías y tíos es en el picnic familiar anual.

english_meaning: Clay is a type of heavy, wet soil used to make pots.
english_example: She made a bowl out of the clay.
spanish_meaning: La arcilla es una tierra pesada y húmeda usada para hacer vasijas.
spanish_example: Ella hizo un cuenco con arcilla.

english_meaning: Cloth is material used to make clothes.
english_example: His shirt is made of a very soft type of cloth.
spanish_meaning: La tela es un material usado para hacer ropa.
spanish_example: Su camisa está hecha de una tela muy suave.

english_meaning: A curtain is a cloth hung over a window or used to divide a room.
english_example: She opened the curtains to let light into the room.
spanish_meaning: Una cortina es una tela sobre una ventana o para separar un espacio.
spanish_example: Abrió las cortinas para dejar entrar la luz.

english_meaning: To deserve is to be worthy of something as a result of one’s actions.
english_example: The dog deserved a bone for behaving very well.
spanish_meaning: Merecer es ser digno de algo por lo que se ha hecho.
spanish_example: El perro merecía un hueso por portarse tan bien.

english_meaning: Feathers are the things covering a bird’s bodies.
english_example: That bird has orange feathers on its chest.
spanish_meaning: Las plumas cubren el cuerpo de los pájaros.
spanish_example: Ese pájaro tiene plumas naranjas en el pecho.

english_meaning: Fertile land is able to produce good crops and plants.
english_example: The farmer grew many vegetables in the fertile soil.
spanish_meaning: Una tierra fértil puede producir buenas cosechas.
spanish_example: El agricultor cultivó muchas verduras en el suelo fértil.

english_meaning: A flood is an event in which water covers an area that is usually dry.
english_example: After three days of rain, there was a flood in the city.
spanish_meaning: Una inundación es cuando el agua cubre un área que suele estar seca.
spanish_example: Después de tres días de lluvia, hubo una inundación en la ciudad.

english_meaning: Furniture means the things used in a house such as tables and chairs.
english_example: His living room only had a few simple pieces of furniture.
spanish_meaning: El mobiliario es el conjunto de muebles de una casa.
spanish_example: Su sala sólo tenía unas pocas piezas de mobiliario.

english_meaning: A grave is the place where a dead person is buried.
english_example: We visit our grandfather’s grave each year.
spanish_meaning: La tumba es el lugar donde se entierra a una persona muerta.
spanish_example: Visito la tumba de mi abuelo cada año.

english_meaning: An ideal thing is the best that it can possibly be.
english_example: This house is an ideal place for my family. It has everything we need.
spanish_meaning: Un ideal es lo mejor que algo puede ser.
spanish_example: Esta casa es un lugar ideal para mi familia.

english_meaning: Intelligence is the ability to learn and understand things.
english_example: Because of his high intelligence, he finished school early.
spanish_meaning: La inteligencia es la capacidad de aprender y comprender.
spanish_example: Por su alta inteligencia, terminó la escuela temprano.

english_meaning: To obtain is to get something you want or need.
english_example: After I passed the test, I obtained my driver’s license.
spanish_meaning: Obtener es conseguir algo que se quiere o necesita.
spanish_example: Después de aprobar el examen, obtuve mi licencia de conducir.

english_meaning: Religious means related to or about religion.
english_example: The holy man spoke about religious topics.
spanish_meaning: Religioso significa relacionado con la religión.
spanish_example: El hombre santo habló sobre temas religiosos.

english_meaning: Romantic means related to or about love.
english_example: The young couple went to see a romantic movie.
spanish_meaning: Romántico significa relacionado con el amor.
spanish_example: La joven pareja fue a ver una película romántica.

english_meaning: A shell is a hard covering that protects the body of some sea creatures.
english_example: There were many pretty shells on the beach.
spanish_meaning: La concha es una cubierta dura que protege a ciertos animales marinos.
spanish_example: Había muchas conchas bonitas en la playa.

english_meaning: A shore is the edge of a large body of water.
english_example: All of the boats were floating near the shore.
spanish_meaning: La orilla es el borde de un gran cuerpo de agua.
spanish_example: Todos los botes flotaban cerca de la orilla.

english_meaning: A wheel is a round thing on a vehicle that turns when it moves.
english_example: A car has four wheels.
spanish_meaning: Una rueda es una parte redonda de un vehículo que gira al moverse.
spanish_example: Un coche tiene cuatro ruedas.

english_meaning: Wooden objects are made of wood.
english_example: My mother gave me a wooden spoon.
spanish_meaning: Las cosas de madera están hechas con madera.
spanish_example: Mi madre me regaló una cuchara de madera.

english_meaning: An appliance is a piece of equipment used for jobs in the home.
english_example: Many homes have appliances like ovens, toasters, and refrigerators.
spanish_meaning: Un electrodoméstico es equipo para tareas del hogar.
spanish_example: Muchas casas tienen electrodomésticos como hornos y refrigeradores.

english_meaning: A basin is large bowl for washing things. A sink is sometimes called a basin.
english_example: She filled the basin with water and washed her face.
spanish_meaning: Un cuenco grande se usa para lavar cosas; a veces también se llama lavabo.
spanish_example: Llenó el cuenco con agua y se lavó la cara.

english_meaning: A broom is a brush with a long handle used for cleaning floors.
english_example: My father usually uses a broom to sweep away dust in the basement.
spanish_meaning: La escoba es un cepillo con mango largo para limpiar pisos.
spanish_example: Mi padre usa una escoba para barrer el polvo del sótano.

english_meaning: A caterpillar is a small insect that looks like a worm and eats plants.
english_example: After eating a lot of leaves, caterpillars change into butterflies.
spanish_meaning: La oruga es un insecto pequeño que parece un gusano y come plantas.
spanish_example: Después de comer muchas hojas, las orugas se convierten en mariposas.

english_meaning: A cupboard is a piece of furniture that is used to store food or household items.
english_example: We put all of our dishes and food in the cupboards.
spanish_meaning: Un armario guarda comida o utensilios del hogar.
spanish_example: Pusimos nuestros platos y comida en los armarios.

english_meaning: Delicate things are easy to break or harm.
english_example: You should hold the baby carefully because she’s very delicate.
spanish_meaning: Algo delicado se rompe o se daña con facilidad.
spanish_example: Debes sostener al bebé con cuidado porque es muy delicada.

english_meaning: To emerge from something means to come out of it.
english_example: A groundhog emerged from a snow covered hole.
spanish_meaning: Emerger es salir de algo.
spanish_example: Una marmota emergió de un agujero cubierto de nieve.

english_meaning: A handicap is a condition that limits someone’s mental or physical abilities.
english_example: Joe has a slight handicap, so he uses a walker to get around.
spanish_meaning: La discapacidad limita capacidades mentales o físicas.
spanish_example: Joe tiene una ligera discapacidad, así que usa un andador.

english_meaning: A hole is a hollow space in something solid.
english_example: They made a big hole in the wall.
spanish_meaning: Un agujero es un espacio hueco en algo sólido.
spanish_example: Hicieron un agujero grande en la pared.

english_meaning: A hook is a sharp curved piece of metal used for catching or holding things.
english_example: The fish went after the sharp hook.
spanish_meaning: Un anzuelo es una pieza curva y metálica para pescar.
spanish_example: El pez picó el anzuelo.

english_meaning: To hop means to jump a short distance.
english_example: The kangaroo quickly hopped away from danger.
spanish_meaning: Brincar significa dar saltos cortos.
spanish_example: El canguro se alejó de inmediato dando brincos.

english_meaning: Laundry is clothes that have been or need to be washed.
english_example: He folded the clean laundry and put the dirty laundry in a basket.
spanish_meaning: La ropa es la ropa que se lava o está sucia.
spanish_example: Dobló la ropa limpia y puso la sucia en una canasta.

english_meaning: To pursue is to chase or follow someone or something.
english_example: The mother pursued her young child down the hill.
spanish_meaning: Perseguir es cazar o seguir a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: La madre persiguió a su hijo niño por la colina.

english_meaning: Reluctant means not wanting to do something.
english_example: She was reluctant to say that she saw the robbery.
spanish_meaning: Una persona reacia no quiere hacer algo.
spanish_example: Ella fue reacia a decir que había visto el robo.

english_meaning: Sleeves are the part of a shirt in which arms go.
english_example: Ryan bought a new shirt with long sleeves to keep his arms warm.
spanish_meaning: Las mangas son la parte de la camisa por donde van los brazos.
spanish_example: Ryan compró una camisa nueva con mangas largas.

english_meaning: The spine is the group of bones that run up and down the middle of the back.
english_example: Our spine helps us to stand up nice and straight.
spanish_meaning: La columna vertebral es el grupo de huesos que va por la espalda.
spanish_example: Nuestra columna nos ayuda a mantenernos derechos.

english_meaning: A stain is a dirty mark that is difficult to clean.
english_example: He had a red stain on the collar of his shirt.
spanish_meaning: Una mancha es una marca sucia difícil de limpiar.
spanish_example: Él tenía una mancha roja en el cuello de la camisa.

english_meaning: A strip is a long, narrow piece of material or land.
english_example: He had long strips of film that held images of his trip abroad.
spanish_meaning: Una franja es una pieza larga y estrecha de material o terreno.
spanish_example: Tenía largas tiras de película de su viaje.

english_meaning: To swear means to promise to do something.
english_example: I will put my hand on the Bible and swear to do my best for the country.
spanish_meaning: Jurar es prometer hacer algo.
spanish_example: Pondré la mano sobre la Biblia y juraré hacer lo mejor por mi país.

english_meaning: To swing something means to move it back and forth or from side to side.
english_example: He can swing a golf club very powerfully.
spanish_meaning: Mecer algo es moverlo de un lado a otro.
spanish_example: Puede mecer con fuerza un palo de golf.

english_meaning: An acre is a unit for measuring area.
english_example: They lived on a 150-acre farm.
spanish_meaning: Un acre es una unidad para medir superficie.
spanish_example: Vivían en una granja de 150 acres.

english_meaning: The afterlife is a life that some people believe begins when a person dies.
english_example: I believe that there is an afterlife.
spanish_meaning: La vida después de la muerte es lo que algunas personas creen que comienza al morir.
spanish_example: Creo que existe una vida después de la muerte.

english_meaning: Archaeology is the study of the remains left by ancient societies.
english_example: He enjoyed visiting the Great Pyramids of Egypt because he loves archaeology.
spanish_meaning: La arqueología estudia los restos de sociedades antiguas.
spanish_example: Disfrutaba visitar las pirámides de Egipto porque ama la arqueología.

english_meaning: A chamber is a closed space or room used for a special purpose.
english_example: The meeting was held in the faculty chamber.
spanish_meaning: Una sala es un espacio cerrado usado para un propósito especial.
spanish_example: La reunión se hizo en la sala de la facultad.

english_meaning: A channel is a long, deep space between two edges.
english_example: The river cut a channel through the rocks.
spanish_meaning: Un canal es un espacio alargado entre dos márgenes.
spanish_example: El río abrió un canal entre las rocas.

english_meaning: A core is the main or central part of something.
english_example: Earth has a solid inner core.
spanish_meaning: El núcleo es la parte central y principal de algo.
spanish_example: La Tierra tiene un núcleo sólido interno.

english_meaning: A corridor is a narrow passage that leads into other areas.
english_example: He took the corridor on the left to go to his office.
spanish_meaning: Un pasillo es un espacio estrecho que conduce a otras áreas.
spanish_example: Tomó el pasillo de la izquierda para ir a su oficina.
