english_meaning: A distinct thing is different or stands out.
english_example: He has a distinct accent.
spanish_meaning: Algo distinto es diferente o se nota claramente.
spanish_example: Tiene un acento muy distintivo.

english_meaning: Elite means of or from a high-level group.
english_example: Only an elite group was allowed membership into the club.
spanish_meaning: La élite es un grupo de alto nivel.
spanish_example: Sólo un grupo élite podía entrar al club.

english_meaning: To engineer something is to skillfully plan out how to make that thing.
english_example: After engineering the robot, they needed to find the correct parts to build it.
spanish_meaning: Ingeniar algo es planear hábilmente cómo construirlo.
spanish_example: Ingeniaron el robot y luego buscaron las piezas correctas.

english_meaning: To found something means to start, organize, or establish that thing.
english_example: My grandfather founded the City Bank.
spanish_meaning: Fundar algo es iniciar o establecerlo.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo fundó City Bank.

english_meaning: A gap is a space between two things.
english_example: There is a small gap between the blocks of wood.
spanish_meaning: Una brecha es el espacio entre dos cosas.
spanish_example: Hay una pequeña brecha entre los bloques de madera.

english_meaning: Glory is the importance, magnificence, or specialness of something.
english_example: They enjoyed the glory of the beautiful sunset.
spanish_meaning: La gloria es grandeza o importancia especial.
spanish_example: Disfrutaron de la gloria del hermoso atardecer.

english_meaning: An interior is the inside of something.
english_example: They looked at the interior of the box.
spanish_meaning: El interior es la parte de adentro de algo.
spanish_example: Miraron el interior de la caja.

english_meaning: A lion is a large animal in the cat family.
english_example: We went to see the lion at the zoo.
spanish_meaning: El león es un gran animal de la familia de los felinos.
spanish_example: Fuimos a ver al león en el zoológico.

english_meaning: A role is a job, position, or part in something.
english_example: Her role in the office is to sell products to customers.
spanish_meaning: Un rol es un puesto o parte en algo.
spanish_example: Su rol en la oficina es vender productos a clientes.

english_meaning: Royal means related to a king or queen.
english_example: The Duchess was part of the royal family.
spanish_meaning: Real significa relacionado con un rey o una reina.
spanish_example: La duquesa formaba parte de la familia real.

english_meaning: A sole person or thing is the only one.
english_example: She was the sole woman in the room.
spanish_meaning: Solo o único significa el único.
spanish_example: Ella fue la única mujer de la habitación.

english_meaning: Stairs are a set of steps built to go from one level of a building to another.
english_example: He took the stairs instead of the elevator.
spanish_meaning: Las escaleras son una serie de escalones para pasar de un nivel a otro.
spanish_example: Tomó las escaleras en lugar del ascensor.

english_meaning: The surface is the top layer of something.
english_example: She wiped the surface of the table.
spanish_meaning: La superficie es la capa superior de algo.
spanish_example: Limpié la superficie de la mesa.

english_meaning: An agreement is a formal decision about future action.
english_example: I think you’ll get Tom’s agreement to this proposal.
spanish_meaning: Un acuerdo es una decisión formal sobre una acción futura.
spanish_example: Creo que obtendrás el acuerdo de Tom para está propuesta.

english_meaning: To arise is to happen.
english_example: Difficulties arose with his computer because it was old.
spanish_meaning: Surgir significa que algo sucede.
spanish_example: Surgieron dificultades con su computadora vieja.

english_meaning: A benefactor is a person who gives money to help someone.
english_example: The student’s benefactor gave him money to spend on his studies.
spanish_meaning: Un benefactor es quien da dinero para ayudar a alguien.
spanish_example: El benefactor del estudiante le dio dinero para estudiar.

english_meaning: A blacksmith is a person who makes things out of metal.
english_example: The blacksmith pounded the piece of metal until it was flat.
spanish_meaning: Un herrero es una persona que trabaja el metal.
spanish_example: El herrero martilleó el metal hasta dejarlo plano.

english_meaning: A chimney is a tall pipe used to carry smoke out of a building.
english_example: The cat was sitting on the roof next to the chimney.
spanish_meaning: La chimenea es una pipa alta para sacar humo de un edificio.
spanish_example: El gato se sentó en el tejado junto a la chimenea.

english_meaning: To compensate is to pay someone for the time they spent doing something.
english_example: Her boss compensated her for the extra work she did last week.
spanish_meaning: Compensar es pagar a alguien por el tiempo dedicado.
spanish_example: Su jefa la compensó por el trabajo extra de la semana pasada.

english_meaning: To encounter is to find or meet a person or thing.
english_example: I encountered a sea turtle while I was swimming.
spanish_meaning: Encontrarse con alguien o algo es topar con ello.
spanish_example: Encontré una tortuga marina mientras nadaba.

english_meaning: To exceed is to be more than something.
english_example: Since I exceeded my limit, I decided to get rid of my credit cards.
spanish_meaning: Exceder es ser más que algo.
spanish_example: Como excedí mi límite, decidí deshacerme de mis tarjetas.

english_meaning: To forge is to make or produce, especially with difficulty.
english_example: Stacy and Heather forged their friendship when they were teenagers.
spanish_meaning: Forjar es fabricar o crear, a veces con dificultad.
spanish_example: Stacy y Heather forjaron su amistad cuando eran adolescentes.

english_meaning: People who are humble do not believe that they are better than other people.
english_example: Even though Bob is the smartest boy in his class, he is humble.
spanish_meaning: Una persona humilde no se cree mejor que los demás.
spanish_example: Aunque Bob sea el más inteligente, es humilde.

english_meaning: Iron is a strong metal that is used to make many objects.
english_example: The horse had shoes made of iron.
spanish_meaning: El hierro es un metal fuerte usado para muchos objetos.
spanish_example: El caballo tenía herraduras de hierro.

english_meaning: A ladder is an object that is used to climb up and down things.
english_example: He used a ladder to climb to the top of his tree house.
spanish_meaning: Una escalera se usa para subir y bajar.
spanish_example: Usó una escalera para subir al fuerte de su casa en el árbol.

english_meaning: If people are modest, they do not think that they are too important.
english_example: Derek is very modest for someone who is so rich.
spanish_meaning: Una persona modesta no se cree muy importante.
spanish_example: Derek es muy modesto para alguien tan rico.

english_meaning: To occupy a place is to live, work, or be there.
english_example: Kevin and Alice occupied the chairs and had a long discussion.
spanish_meaning: Ocupar un lugar es vivir, trabajar o estar allí.
spanish_example: Kevin y Alice ocuparon las sillas y hablaron mucho.

english_meaning: A penny is a coin worth one cent.
english_example: US President Abraham Lincoln is on the penny.
spanish_meaning: Un centavo es una moneda de un céntimo.
spanish_example: El rostro de Abraham Lincoln está en el centavo.

english_meaning: To preach is to talk about and promote a religious idea.
english_example: Aaron often preached about living an honest life.
spanish_meaning: Predicar es hablar sobre una idea religiosa.
spanish_example: Aaron solía predicar sobre vivir honestamente.

english_meaning: To prosper is to be successful or make a lot of money.
english_example: Frank’s new business finally prospered after many years of hard work.
spanish_meaning: Prosperar es tener éxito o prosperar económicamente.
spanish_example: El negocio nuevo de Frank prosperó tras muchos años.

english_meaning: A province is an area that is controlled by a country.
english_example: Canada is divided into several different provinces.
spanish_meaning: Una provincia es un área controlada por un país.
spanish_example: Canadá se divide en varias provincias.

english_meaning: Satisfaction is the feeling of having done or received something good.
english_example: Brad was filled with satisfaction when he saw what was for dinner.
spanish_meaning: La satisfacción es el sentimiento de haber hecho o recibido algo bueno.
spanish_example: Brad se llenó de satisfacción al ver qué había para cenar.

english_meaning: To sustain something is to keep it going.
english_example: Wind power is a clean way to sustain a city with energy.
spanish_meaning: Mantener algo es mantenerlo en marcha.
spanish_example: La energía eólica permite sostener una ciudad con energía.

english_meaning: To acquire something is to get or gain possession of that thing.
english_example: Tina acquired a strange package yesterday.
spanish_meaning: Adquirir algo es obtener la posesión de eso.
spanish_example: Tina adquirió un paquete raro ayer.

english_meaning: An awkward thing is embarrassing and uncomfortable.
english_example: After dropping his coffee cup, Robbie felt awkward.
spanish_meaning: Algo incómodo es embarazoso y pone en una situación difícil.
spanish_example: Tras dejar caer su taza de café, Robbie se sintió incómodo.

english_meaning: A caretaker is a person who takes care of very young, old, or sick people.
english_example: My grandmother’s caretaker helps her get around the house.
spanish_meaning: Un cuidador atiende a personas muy jóvenes, viejas o enfermas.
spanish_example: El cuidador de mi abuela la ayuda a moverse por la casa.

english_meaning: To deceive is to make someone believe something that is not true.
english_example: He tried to deceive his friends with a card trick.
spanish_meaning: Engañar es hacer que alguien crea algo falso.
spanish_example: Intentó engañar a sus amigos con un truco de cartas.

english_meaning: To discourage is to make someone feel less excited about something.
english_example: Mr. Perry discouraged the students from quitting school.
spanish_meaning: Desalentar es quitarle ganas a alguien.
spanish_example: El Sr. Perry desalentó a los estudiantes de dejar la escuela.

english_meaning: A fake thing is made to look real in order to trick people.
english_example: The model was wearing fake hair.
spanish_meaning: Algo falso está hecho para parecer real y engañar.
spanish_example: El modelo llevaba un pelo falso.

english_meaning: Hatred is a strong feeling of not liking someone or something.
english_example: I have a hatred for the taste of medicine.
spanish_meaning: El odio es un fuerte sentimiento de no gustar a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Tengo odio al sabor de la medicina.

english_meaning: A hut is a house made of wood, grass, or mud that has only one or two rooms.
english_example: We all went into the hut to sleep.
spanish_meaning: Una choza es una casa pequeña de una o dos habitaciones.
spanish_example: Entramos a la choza para dormir.

english_meaning: An inferior thing is not as good as something else.
english_example: Cars built a hundred years ago are inferior to ones built today.
spanish_meaning: Un objeto inferior no es tan bueno como otro.
spanish_example: Los coches de hace cien años son inferiores a los actuales.

english_meaning: A lodge is a house in the mountains used by people who hunt or fish.
english_example: During our ski trip, we stayed at a lodge.
spanish_meaning: Una cabaña es una casa de montaña para cazadores o pescadores.
spanish_example: Durante el viaje de esquí, nos quedamos en una cabaña.

english_meaning: To neglect someone or something is to not take care of it properly.
english_example: William neglected his room, so it is a complete mess.
spanish_meaning: Descuidar a alguien o algo es no cuidarlo bien.
spanish_example: William descuidó su habitación, por eso está hecha un desastre.

english_meaning: A newcomer is a person who has recently arrived at a place or a group.
english_example: The students happily welcomed the newcomer to the school.
spanish_meaning: Un recién llegado es alguien que llega recientemente.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes recibieron con alegría al recién llegado al colegio.

english_meaning: An offense is behavior that is wrong or breaks a law.
english_example: Stealing a car is a very serious offense.
spanish_meaning: Una ofensa es una conducta indebida o delictiva.
spanish_example: Robar un coche es una ofensa muy grave.

english_meaning: To overlook something is to not notice it or to not realize that it is important.
english_example: Brenda overlooked the last step and had a bad fall.
spanish_meaning: Pasar por alto algo es no darse cuenta de su importancia.
spanish_example: Brenda pasó por alto el último paso y tuvo una mala caída.

english_meaning: To repay is to pay back or to reward someone or something.
english_example: She repaid her friend for all of his hard work with a small gift.
spanish_meaning: Reembolsar es pagar de vuelta o devolver algo.
spanish_example: Ella le reembolsó a su amigo todo su esfuerzo con un detalle.

english_meaning: A ridiculous thing is silly or strange.
english_example: Steve looked ridiculous with those huge blue sunglasses.
spanish_meaning: Ridículo significa tonto o extraño.
spanish_example: Steve se veía ridículo con esas gafas azules enormes.

english_meaning: A satisfactory thing is good enough.
english_example: Mina often received satisfactory grades since she studied so hard.
spanish_meaning: Algo satisfactorio es lo suficientemente bueno.
spanish_example: Mina sacaba calificaciones satisfactorias.

english_meaning: A shepherd is a person who protects and cares for sheep.
english_example: The shepherd moved the sheep to another field.
spanish_meaning: Un pastor protege y cuida a las ovejas.
spanish_example: El pastor llevó las ovejas a otro campo.

english_meaning: To venture is to go to a place that may be dangerous.
english_example: Even though it was dangerous, they ventured up the mountain.
spanish_meaning: Aventurarse es ir a un lugar que puede ser peligroso.
spanish_example: Aunque era peligroso, se aventuraron por la montaña.

english_meaning: Wheat is a plant from which we get the grain used to make bread.
english_example: The field of golden wheat was ready to be harvested.
spanish_meaning: El trigo es una planta de la que se obtiene grano para hacer pan.
spanish_example: El campo de trigo dorado estaba listo para cosechar.

english_meaning: An alley is a narrow road between houses or buildings.
english_example: The alley behind my house looks dirty.
spanish_meaning: Un callejón es un camino estrecho entre casas o edificios.
spanish_example: El callejón detrás de mi casa se ve sucio.

english_meaning: An ax is a tool used to cut wood.
english_example: She used an ax to cut some wood for the fire.
spanish_meaning: Un hacha es una herramienta para cortar madera.
spanish_example: Usó un hacha para cortar leña para la chimenea.

english_meaning: A bunch is a group of the same things.
english_example: She was hungry, so she ate the entire bunch of grapes.
spanish_meaning: Un racimo es un grupo de cosas iguales.
spanish_example: Tenía hambre y se comió un racimo de uvas.

english_meaning: A chore is an unpleasant job that must be done.
english_example: It’s Nikki’s chore to do the dishes every Tuesday and Wednesday night.
spanish_meaning: Una tarea doméstica es un trabajo desagradable que hay que hacer.
spanish_example: El trabajo de hacer los platos todos los martes y miércoles es una tarea de Nikki.

english_meaning: A decent person or thing is OK or good enough.
english_example: Eric did a decent job painting the fence.
spanish_meaning: Una persona o cosa decente es suficiente o adecuada.
spanish_example: Eric hizo un trabajo decente pintando la cerca.

english_meaning: Disgraceful behavior is behavior that is very bad.
english_example: This is a disgraceful waste of money.
spanish_meaning: Una conducta desgraciada o deshonrosa es muy mala.
spanish_example: Esto es un gasto vergonzoso de dinero.

english_meaning: The elbow is the middle part of an arm, where it bends.
english_example: She pointed to her elbow to show me where she hurt herself.
spanish_meaning: El codo es la parte media de un brazo.
spanish_example: Señaló su codo para mostrarme dónde se había lastimado.

english_meaning: A grateful person feels thankful about something.
english_example: The girls were grateful for the chance to visit their grandfather.
spanish_meaning: Una persona agradecida se siente agradecida.
spanish_example: Las niñas estaban agradecidas por la oportunidad de ver a su abuelo.

english_meaning: To irritate means to annoy someone.
english_example: She was irritated when her brother told her that he had lost her camera.
spanish_meaning: Irritar es molestar o enfadar a alguien.
spanish_example: Se irritó cuando su hermano le dijo que había perdido su cámara.

english_meaning: To kid is to say something that is not true as a joke.
english_example: I am not really mad. I was kidding when I said I was angry.
spanish_meaning: Bromear es decir algo falso como broma.
spanish_example: No estaba enojado de verdad. Bromee al decir que estaba molesto.

english_meaning: A loose thing is not held in place tightly.
english_example: The bolt was loose, so I tightened it with the wrench.
spanish_meaning: Algo flojo no está firmemente sujeto.
spanish_example: El perno estaba flojo, así que lo apreté con la llave.

english_meaning: To offend is to make someone angry or upset.
english_example: They were both offended by what they had said to each other.
spanish_meaning: Ofender es hacer enojar o herir a alguien.
spanish_example: Ambos se ofendieron por lo que se dijeron.

english_meaning: An overnight action happens during the night.
english_example: The campers stayed in the tent overnight.
spanish_meaning: Una acción overnight ocurre durante la noche.
spanish_example: Los campistas se quedaron en la tienda durante la noche.

english_meaning: To persist means to keep doing something even when it is hard.
english_example: Even though the lesson was difficult, he persisted until he understood.
spanish_meaning: Persistir significa seguir haciéndolo aunque sea difícil.
spanish_example: Aunque la lección era difícil, persistió hasta entenderla.

english_meaning: A pine is a type of tall, thin tree with needles instead of leaves.
english_example: There were many beautiful pine trees in the forest.
spanish_meaning: El pino es un árbol alto y delgado con agujas.
spanish_example: Había muchos pinos hermosos en el bosque.

english_meaning: A scar is a mark on the skin after a wound heals.
english_example: The man had horrible scars on his cheek and forehead.
spanish_meaning: Una cicatriz es una marca en la piel tras sanar una herida.
spanish_example: El hombre tenía cicatrices terribles en la mejilla y la frente.

english_meaning: A sensation is a feeling that people get from their senses.
english_example: Mom got a painful sensation in her head from the loud noise.
spanish_meaning: Una sensación es una percepción que surge de los sentidos.
spanish_example: Mamá sintió una sensación dolorosa en la cabeza por el ruido.

english_meaning: A sled is a small vehicle used on snow.
english_example: Marvin likes to ride his sled down the hill in winter.
spanish_meaning: Un trineo es un pequeño vehículo usado sobre nieve.
spanish_example: A Marvin le gusta bajar la colina en trineo en invierno.

english_meaning: To tease means to laugh at or make fun of someone.
english_example: Jake teased Charlie because he was the new student.
spanish_meaning: Burlarse es hacer bromas o reírse de alguien.
spanish_example: Jake se burló de Charlie porque era el nuevo alumno.

english_meaning: A valentine is someone loved or admired with great affection.
english_example: Harry wanted Molly to be his valentine.
spanish_meaning: Un novio o novia es alguien amado con gran cariño.
spanish_example: Harry quería que Molly fuera su novio.

english_meaning: To acquaint is to get to know something or someone.
english_example: Nancy acquainted herself with the new computer.
spanish_meaning: Familiarizarse con algo o alguien es llegar a conocerlo.
spanish_example: Nancy se familiarizó con la nueva computadora.

english_meaning: A cemetery is a place where people are buried when they die.
english_example: Some people are scared of cemeteries.
spanish_meaning: Un cementerio es un lugar donde se entierran personas.
spanish_example: Algunas personas tienen miedo de los cementerios.

english_meaning: A creature is an animal or person.
english_example: Those creatures live in Africa.
spanish_meaning: Una criatura es un animal o persona.
spanish_example: Esas criaturas viven en África.

english_meaning: To curse someone is to hope that bad things happen to that person.
english_example: The witch cursed the village.
spanish_meaning: Maldecir a alguien es desearle cosas malas.
spanish_example: La bruja maldijo el pueblo.

english_meaning: A disguise is something you wear so people cannot tell who you are.
english_example: Everyone knew that it was Dad in the Santa disguise.
spanish_meaning: Un disfraz es algo que se usa para que no reconozcan a alguien.
spanish_example: Todos sabían que era papá con el disfraz de Papá Noel.

english_meaning: A fancy thing is nicer or more detailed than normal.
english_example: Their table was all set for a fancy dinner.
spanish_meaning: Algo lujoso es más refinado o detallado que lo normal.
spanish_example: Pusieron la mesa para una cena lujosa.

english_meaning: A flashlight is a small electric light that people carry in their hands.
english_example: We took a flashlight when we went camping.
spanish_meaning: Una linterna es una luz portátil eléctrica.
spanish_example: Llevamos una linterna cuando fuimos de campamento.

english_meaning: A hood is part of a coat that goes over a person’s head.
english_example: She put on her hood to keep her head warm.
spanish_meaning: La capucha es la parte de un abrigo que cubre la cabeza.
spanish_example: Ella se puso la capucha para mantener su cabeza caliente.

english_meaning: An inhabitant is a person who lives in a certain place.
english_example: The number of inhabitants in the countryside is increasing.
spanish_meaning: Un habitante es una persona que vive en un lugar.
spanish_example: El número de habitantes en el campo está aumentando.

english_meaning: To nourish is to give someone or something the food needed to live.
english_example: A good mother will nourish her baby every day.
spanish_meaning: Nutrir es dar el alimento necesario para vivir.
spanish_example: Una buena madre nutre a su bebé todos los días.

english_meaning: A pirate is a sailor who steals things from other boats.
english_example: Pirates are very scary characters.
spanish_meaning: Un pirata es un marinero que roba de otros barcos.
spanish_example: Los piratas son personajes muy temibles.

english_meaning: A publication is something printed, like a newspaper or book.
english_example: She’s been a subscriber to that publication for over ten years.
spanish_meaning: Una publicación es algo impreso como un periódico o un libro.
spanish_example: Ella es suscriptora de esa publicación desde hace más de diez años.

english_meaning: A riddle is a question that is difficult to answer but meant to be funny.
english_example: I could not answer Wendy’s riddle, but it made me laugh.
spanish_meaning: Un acertijo es una pregunta difícil pero graciosa.
spanish_example: No pude responder el acertijo de Wendy, pero me hizo reír.

english_meaning: To rot is to slowly get softer and become destroyed.
english_example: The old log began to rot in the forest.
spanish_meaning: Pudrir significa descomponerse lentamente.
spanish_example: El tronco viejo comenzó a pudrirse en el bosque.

english_meaning: An action that happens shortly happens very soon.
english_example: My workday will end shortly.
spanish_meaning: En breve significa en poco tiempo.
spanish_example: Mi jornada terminará en breve.

english_meaning: A skeleton is all the bones of a body.
english_example: There is a skeleton in the science classroom.
spanish_meaning: El esqueleto es el conjunto de huesos de un cuerpo.
spanish_example: Hay un esqueleto en el aula de ciencias.

english_meaning: To spoil is to rot or to make bad.
english_example: We left the fruit out too long, and it spoiled.
spanish_meaning: Estropear algo es hacerlo malo o dañado.
spanish_example: Dejamos la fruta fuera demasiado tiempo y se estropeó.

english_meaning: To starve is to not get enough food for a long period of time.
english_example: During the war, many people starved.
spanish_meaning: Morir de hambre significa no tener comida durante mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: Durante la guerra, muchas personas murieron de hambre.

english_meaning: A thrill is an exciting feeling.
english_example: The surfer enjoys the thrill of surfing a big wave.
spanish_meaning: La emoción es un fuerte sentimiento de excitación.
spanish_example: El surfista disfruta la emoción de surfear una gran ola.

english_meaning: A wicked person is very bad or evil.
english_example: My boss is a very wicked man.
spanish_meaning: Malvado significa muy malo o cruel.
spanish_example: Mi jefe es un hombre muy malvado.

english_meaning: To alert someone is to tell or warn that person about something.
english_example: The fire alarm alerted us that there was a problem.
spanish_meaning: Alerta/avisar a alguien sobre algo.
spanish_example: La alarma de incendio nos alertó de que había un problema.

english_meaning: A broadcast is a television or radio show.
english_example: We watched the broadcast of the local news on TV.
spanish_meaning: La transmisión es un programa o emisión de radio/TV.
spanish_example: Vimos la transmisión de las noticias locales por televisión.

english_meaning: A bulletin is a news report about very recent and important events.
english_example: There was a live bulletin reporting on the economy of the city.
spanish_meaning: Un boletín informa noticias recientes e importantes.
spanish_example: Hubo un boletín en vivo sobre la economía de la ciudad.

english_meaning: A bump is a small raised area on a surface.
english_example: The monkey got a bump on his head because he was hit by a rock.
spanish_meaning: Un bulto es una protuberancia pequeña en una superficie.
spanish_example: El mono se hizo un bulto en la cabeza al ser golpeado por una roca.

english_meaning: To chop something means to cut it into pieces with a tool.
english_example: Mom chopped some vegetables to put into the stew.
spanish_meaning: Picar algo es cortarlo en pedazos con una herramienta.
spanish_example: Mi mamá picó algunas verduras para el guiso.

english_meaning: A closet is a small room used to store things.
english_example: Marie has many clothes inside of her closet.
spanish_meaning: Un armario es un mueble para guardar cosas.
spanish_example: Marie tiene mucha ropa dentro de su armario.

english_meaning: To console is to give comfort to a person who feels sad.
english_example: When my dog ran away, my dad consoled me.
spanish_meaning: Consolar es dar consuelo a alguien triste.
spanish_example: Cuando mi perro se perdió, mi padre me consoló.

english_meaning: A district is a small part of a city, county, state, or country.
english_example: I live in a residential district of Seattle, Washington.
spanish_meaning: Un distrito es una parte de una ciudad, condado o estado.
spanish_example: Vivo en un distrito residencial de Seattle, Washington.

english_meaning: A drawer is a small part in furniture that is used to store things.
english_example: I put my clothes into the empty drawers.
spanish_meaning: Un cajón es una parte de un mueble para guardar cosas.
spanish_example: Puse mi ropa en los cajones vacíos.

english_meaning: To endure means to experience and survive something difficult.
english_example: She had to endure her husband shouting all day long.
spanish_meaning: Resistir significa soportar y sobrevivir algo difícil.
spanish_example: Tuvo que resistir los gritos de su marido todo el día.

english_meaning: To execute means to kill someone as a legal punishment.
english_example: Some people are executed for serious crimes.
spanish_meaning: Ejecutar es matar legalmente a alguien.
spanish_example: A algunas personas las ejecutan por crímenes graves.

english_meaning: To grasp something means to hold it.
english_example: He grasped the bag of money tightly.
spanish_meaning: Agarrar es tomar firmemente algo.
spanish_example: Apretó firmemente la bolsa de dinero.

english_meaning: The rear of something is the back part of that thing.
english_example: The man loaded the rear of his truck with boxes.
spanish_meaning: La parte trasera es la parte de atrás.
spanish_example: El hombre cargó la parte trasera de su camión con cajas.

english_meaning: A senator is someone who makes laws for a state.
english_example: The young senator promised to make laws that would help the people.
spanish_meaning: Un senador hace leyes para un estado.
spanish_example: El joven senador prometió hacer leyes que ayudarían a la gente.

english_meaning: A skull is the hard head bone that protects the brain.
english_example: The brain is protected by the skull.
spanish_meaning: El cráneo es el hueso duro que protege el cerebro.
spanish_example: El cráneo protege al cerebro.

english_meaning: To stir something means to mix it using something small, like a spoon.
english_example: Mom stirred the batter until it was smooth.
spanish_meaning: Revolver es mezclar algo con algo pequeño, como una cuchara.
spanish_example: Mi mamá revolvió la mezcla hasta que quedó suave.

english_meaning: To tap something is to hit it lightly.
english_example: He tapped the keys of the keyboard to write a short word.
spanish_meaning: Tocar algo es golpearlo suavemente.
spanish_example: Golpeó suavemente las teclas del teclado para escribir una palabra.

english_meaning: A tremendous thing is very large or very good.
english_example: The Earth’s oceans are filled with a tremendous amount of water.
spanish_meaning: Tremendo significa muy grande o muy bueno.
spanish_example: Los océanos de la Tierra están llenos de una cantidad tremenda de agua.

english_meaning: Underneath means below or under.
english_example: The roots of a tree are located underneath the ground.
spanish_meaning: Debajo significa por debajo.
spanish_example: Las raíces de un árbol están debajo de la tierra.

english_meaning: A worm is a small animal with a long, thin body.
english_example: Worms are often used to help catch fish.
spanish_meaning: Un gusano es un animal pequeño de cuerpo largo y delgado.
spanish_example: Se usan gusanos para ayudar a pescar.

english_meaning: To abandon something is to leave it forever or for a long time.
english_example: The old room had been abandoned years before.
spanish_meaning: Abandonar es dejar algo para siempre o por mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: La habitación vieja había sido abandonada años antes.

english_meaning: An ambitious person wants to be rich or successful.
english_example: Kendra had to be ambitious to get into medical school.
spanish_meaning: Una persona ambiciosa quiere ser rica o exitosa.
spanish_example: Kendra tuvo que ser ambiciosa para entrar en medicina.

english_meaning: To bark is to make a short, loud noise, like a dog.
english_example: The dog barked loudly and frighteningly.
spanish_meaning: Ladrar es emitir un ruido corto y fuerte, como un perro.
spanish_example: El perro ladró fuerte y de forma aterradora.

english_meaning: A bay is an area near the ocean where the land goes inward.
english_example: The Golden Gate Bridge crosses San Francisco Bay.
spanish_meaning: Una bahía es una zona costera en la que la tierra se adentra en el mar.
spanish_example: El puente Golden Gate cruza la bahía de San Francisco.

english_meaning: A brilliant person is very bright or smart.
english_example: My younger sister is brilliant for someone her age.
spanish_meaning: Brillante significa muy inteligente.
spanish_example: Mi hermana menor es brillante para su edad.

english_meaning: A chin is the hard part at the bottom of a person’s face.
english_example: Luke pointed to the hair on his chin.
spanish_meaning: El mentón es la parte dura inferior de la cara.
spanish_example: Luke señaló el vello de su mentón.

english_meaning: A complaint is an expression of unhappiness about something.
english_example: Mom said she didn’t want to hear my brother’s complaints.
spanish_meaning: Una queja es una expresión de descontento.
spanish_example: Mamá dijo que no quería oír las quejas de mi hermano.

english_meaning: A deaf person or animal cannot hear.
english_example: Susan uses sign language because she is deaf.
spanish_meaning: Una persona sorda no puede oír.
spanish_example: Susan usa lengua de señas porque es sorda.

english_meaning: An enthusiastic person is excited by or interested in something.
english_example: The man was enthusiastic about his job.
spanish_meaning: Una persona entusiasta está interesada y con mucha energía.
spanish_example: El hombre estaba entusiasta por su trabajo.

english_meaning: An expedition is a long trip, usually to a place very far away.
english_example: They got into their spaceship to begin their expedition.
spanish_meaning: Una expedición es un viaje largo, normalmente a un lugar muy lejano.
spanish_example: Subieron a su nave espacial para comenzar su expedición.

english_meaning: The horizon is where the sky looks like it meets the ground.
english_example: The sun dipped below the horizon.
spanish_meaning: El horizonte es el lugar donde el cielo parece unirse al suelo o al mar.
spanish_example: El sol se ocultó bajo el horizonte.

english_meaning: Loyal people always help or support a certain person or thing.
english_example: The three friends are very loyal to each other.
spanish_meaning: Una persona leal siempre ayuda y apoya a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Los tres amigos son muy leales entre sí.

english_meaning: The mayor is the person in charge of a city.
english_example: The mayor of my hometown is quite a powerful speaker.
spanish_meaning: El alcalde es la persona que dirige y representa a una ciudad.
spanish_example: El alcalde de mi ciudad natal es un orador muy buen hablante.

english_meaning: A mutual thing is felt in the same way by two or more people.
english_example: Robert likes Sarah, and she likes him. The feeling is mutual.
spanish_meaning: Mutuo significa que la misma cosa la sienten dos o más personas.
spanish_example: Robert ama a Sarah y ella le ama a él; ese sentimiento es mutuo.

english_meaning: Overweight people or animals are heavier than is healthy.
english_example: Lisa eats too much, so now she’s overweight.
spanish_meaning: Se dice de una persona o animal que tiene más peso de lo saludable.
spanish_example: Lisa come demasiado, por eso ahora tiene sobrepeso.

english_meaning: A refuge is a place of safety.
english_example: When it started to rain, she found refuge in the house.
spanish_meaning: Un refugio es un lugar seguro.
spanish_example: Cuando empezó a llover, ella encontró refugio en la casa.

english_meaning: To restore something is to put it back the way it was.
english_example: Victor restored the old car.
spanish_meaning: Restaurar es devolver algo a su estado anterior.
spanish_example: Víctor restauró el coche viejo.

english_meaning: To rub something is to push on it and move your hand back and forth.
english_example: Mom’s feet were sore, so she rubbed them.
spanish_meaning: Frotar es presionar y mover la mano sobre algo de forma repetida.
spanish_example: Como le dolían los pies, mamá se los frotó.

english_meaning: The senses are how living things experience the world: sight, taste, smell, hearing and touch.
english_example: It would be hard to live without your five senses.
spanish_meaning: Los sentidos son los sentidos físico que usan los seres vivos para conocer el mundo: vista, gusto, olfato, oído y tacto.
spanish_example: Sería difícil vivir sin tus cinco sentidos.

english_meaning: A veterinarian is a doctor who takes care of animals.
english_example: Wanda became a veterinarian because she loves dogs.
spanish_meaning: Un veterinario es un médico que cuida la salud de los animales.
spanish_example: Wanda se volvió veterinaria porque ama a los perros.

english_meaning: An anniversary is a day that celebrates something from the past.
english_example: My parents went out to a restaurant for their wedding anniversary.
spanish_meaning: Un aniversario es un día para celebrar algo ocurrido en el pasado.
spanish_example: Mis padres salieron a comer para su aniversario de bodas.

english_meaning: Arithmetic is math.
english_example: I like to study arithmetic at school.
spanish_meaning: La aritmética es la matemática de los números y operaciones básicas.
spanish_example: Me gusta estudiar aritmética en la escuela.

english_meaning: Ashamed means feeling upset and embarrassed because of a bad action.
english_example: He was ashamed when he forgot his teacher’s name.
spanish_meaning: Avergonzado significa sentirse mal o pudoroso por una mala acción.
spanish_example: Él se sintió avergonzado cuando olvidó el nombre de su profesor.

english_meaning: To burst is to suddenly break open or apart.
english_example: The bomb burst over the city.
spanish_meaning: Estallar es romperse o abrirse de forma repentina y violenta.
spanish_example: La bomba estalló sobre la ciudad.

english_meaning: A carpenter is a person who builds things with wood.
english_example: We hired a carpenter to make a cupboard.
spanish_meaning: Un carpintero es una persona que construye cosas con madera.
spanish_example: Contratamos a un carpintero para hacer un armario.

english_meaning: Coal is a hard black material that people burn for heat.
english_example: Many power stations burn coal to produce energy.
spanish_meaning: El carbón es una sustancia negra y dura que se quema para dar calor.
spanish_example: Muchas centrales eléctricas queman carbón para producir energía.

english_meaning: A couch is a long, soft seat that many people can sit on.
english_example: Kim and Martin’s new couch was very expensive.
spanish_meaning: Un sofá es un asiento largo y blando donde pueden sentarse varias personas.
spanish_example: El nuevo sofá de Kim y Martin fue muy caro.

english_meaning: To drip is to fall a little bit at a time.
english_example: I heard water dripping from the faucet.
spanish_meaning: Gotear significa caer en pequeñas gotas o poco a poco.
spanish_example: Escuché agua gotear del grifo.

english_meaning: Something elegant is very fancy and pleasing.
english_example: In Japan, women wear elegant kimonos on special occasions.
spanish_meaning: Algo elegante es muy bonito y refinado.
spanish_example: En Japón, las mujeres llevan kimonos elegantes en ocasiones especiales.

english_meaning: Fabric is cloth used to make clothes, furniture, etc.
english_example: The towels were made from a soft fabric.
spanish_meaning: El tejido es el material con el que se hacen ropas, muebles y más.
spanish_example: Las toallas estaban hechas con un tejido suave.

english_meaning: Highlands are high areas of land, usually with mountains.
english_example: The man had a small home in the highlands.
spanish_meaning: Las tierras altas son zonas altas del terreno, normalmente con montañas.
spanish_example: El hombre tenía una casa pequeña en las tierras altas.

english_meaning: Ivory is a hard, white substance that comes from elephants.
english_example: The elephant’s long ivory tusks looked very impressive.
spanish_meaning: El marfil es una sustancia dura y blanca que viene de los elefantes.
spanish_example: Los largos colmillos de marfil del elefante se veían impresionantes.

english_meaning: A mill is a building in which wheat is ground into flour.
english_example: The farmer took his wheat to the mill to make it into flour.
spanish_meaning: Un molino es un edificio donde se muele trigo para hacer harina.
spanish_example: El granjero llevó su trigo al molino para convertirlo en harina.

english_meaning: A needle is a small, sharp piece of metal used to make or fix clothes.
english_example: I used a needle to fix the hole in my pants.
spanish_meaning: La aguja es una pequeña pieza metálica, puntiaguda, usada para coser o reparar ropa.
spanish_example: Usé una aguja para arreglar el agujero de mis pantalones.

english_meaning: To polish something is to rub it in order to make it shiny.
english_example: Mark spent all morning polishing his shoes for the wedding.
spanish_meaning: Pulir es frotar para dejar algo brillante.
spanish_example: Mark pasó toda la mañana puliendo sus zapatos para la boda.

english_meaning: To sew means to put pieces of cloth together using string or thread.
english_example: I learned to sew when I was a little girl.
spanish_meaning: Coser significa unir telas con hilo mediante puntadas.
spanish_example: Aprendí a coser cuando era niña.

english_meaning: A shed is a small building in which people store things like tools.
english_example: We have a small shed in the backyard for storage.
spanish_meaning: Un cobertizo es un edificio pequeño para guardar herramientas y objetos.
spanish_example: Tenemos un cobertizo pequeño en el patio para guardar cosas.

english_meaning: A thread is a thin piece of string.
english_example: I have many different colors of thread at home.
spanish_meaning: Un hilo es una hebra delgada usada para coser.
spanish_example: Tengo muchos colores de hilo en casa.

english_meaning: To trim something is to cut it a little bit.
english_example: I had my hair trimmed this afternoon.
spanish_meaning: Recortar es cortar un poco algo para darle forma o hacerlo más corto.
spanish_example: Me recortaron el cabello está tarde.

english_meaning: An upwards action moves vertically towards the direction above.
english_example: The kite went upwards further and further.
spanish_meaning: Hacia arriba describe un movimiento en dirección de lo alto o superior.
spanish_example: La cometa voló hacia arriba cada vez más.

english_meaning: An ally is someone who agrees to help or support another person or group.
english_example: I was happy to find many allies who shared my opinion.
spanish_meaning: Un aliado es una persona que ayuda o apoya a otra.
spanish_example: Me alegré de encontrar muchos aliados que compartían mi opinión.

english_meaning: To boast is to talk about how great one is.
english_example: We all became tired of listening to him boast about himself all day.
spanish_meaning: Jactarse es hablar de uno mismo con orgullo exagerado.
spanish_example: Ya nos cansamos de escucharle jactarse de sí mismo todo el día.

english_meaning: To bounce is to move up and away from a surface after hitting it.
english_example: Owen bounced the ball on the ground.
spanish_meaning: Rebotar es botar un objeto sobre una superficie y que vuelve hacia arriba.
spanish_example: Owen rebota la pelota en el suelo.

english_meaning: A bully is a person who is mean to others.
english_example: The two bullies always picked on the smaller, weaker kids.
spanish_meaning: Un matón es una persona que intimida o maltrata a otros.
spanish_example: Los dos matones siempre molestaban a los niños más pequeños y débiles.

english_meaning: Carbohydrates are substances in foods like bread that give energy.
english_example: Carbohydrates like rice are a good source of energy for active people.
spanish_meaning: Los carbohidratos son sustancias de alimentos como el arroz o el pan que dan energía.
spanish_example: Los carbohidratos como el arroz son una buena fuente de energía para personas activas.

english_meaning: To crawl is to move slowly on hands and knees.
english_example: The baby crawled across the floor.
spanish_meaning: Gatear es moverse despacio con las manos y las rodillas.
spanish_example: El bebé gateó por el piso.

english_meaning: To defeat someone is to beat that person in a game or battle.
english_example: The champion defeated the challenger in the boxing match.
spanish_meaning: Derrotar significa vencer o ganar contra alguien en una competencia.
spanish_example: El campeón derrotó al retador en el combate.

english_meaning: A dial is a circular tool, like the front of a clock.
english_example: I looked at the dial to see what time it was.
spanish_meaning: La esfera es la parte circular de un reloj que muestra la hora.
spanish_example: Miró la esfera para ver qué hora era.

english_meaning: A dominant person or thing is stronger than others.
english_example: The gorilla is one of the dominant animals in the jungle.
spanish_meaning: Dominante significa más fuerte o con más poder que otros.
spanish_example: El gorila es uno de los animales dominantes de la selva.

english_meaning: Entire means the whole thing or group.
english_example: The entire table was covered with food.
spanish_meaning: Entero significa completo, sin faltar ninguna parte.
spanish_example: La mesa entera estaba cubierta de comida.

english_meaning: Mercy is a feeling or act of kindness.
english_example: He asked his boss for mercy and to not fire him.
spanish_meaning: La misericordia es compasión y clemencia hacia alguien.
spanish_example: Pidió misericordia a su jefe para no ser despedido.

english_meaning: To nod is to move your head up and down.
english_example: I nodded my head as I listened to the song.
spanish_meaning: Asentir es mover la cabeza hacia arriba y abajo para mostrar aprobación o atención.
spanish_example: Asentí mi cabeza mientras escuchaba la canción.

english_meaning: An opponent is a person or group trying to defeat another person or group.
english_example: On the field, our opponents were too good for us.
spanish_meaning: Un oponente es la persona o grupo que intenta vencer a otro.
spanish_example: En la cancha, nuestros oponentes eran demasiado buenos para nosotros.

english_meaning: To quarrel is to argue or fight.
english_example: Billy quarreled with his wife about buying a new house.
spanish_meaning: Discutir es pelearse con palabras o discutir un problema.
spanish_example: Billy discutió con su esposa por la compra de una casa nueva.

english_meaning: A rival is someone trying to achieve the same thing as another.
english_example: The three rivals were all competing for the same job.
spanish_meaning: Un rival es alguien que compite por lo mismo que otro.
spanish_example: Los tres rivales competían por el mismo trabajo.

english_meaning: Sore means feeling or causing pain.
english_example: After lifting the heavy box, Mona’s back was sore.
spanish_meaning: Dolorido significa doloroso o con dolor.
spanish_example: Después de levantar la caja pesada, la espalda de Mona estaba dolorida.

english_meaning: To sting is to cause pain by pushing a sharp part into the skin.
english_example: The needle stung my arm and made me say, “Ouch!”
spanish_meaning: Picar es causar dolor al introducir algo puntiagudo en la piel.
spanish_example: La aguja me picó en el brazo y dije “¡Ay!”.

english_meaning: To strain is to try very hard.
english_example: Casey strained to lift the heavy box.
spanish_meaning: Esforzarse significa intentar con mucha fuerza o energía.
spanish_example: Casey se esforzó por levantar la caja pesada.

english_meaning: Torture is action that causes physical or mental pain.
english_example: Greg thought that writing the essay was torture.
spanish_meaning: Tortura es causar dolor físico o mental intenso.
spanish_example: Greg pensó que escribir el ensayo era tortura.

english_meaning: To wrestle is to try to push another competitor to the floor.
english_example: My dad loved to wrestle when he was in high school.
spanish_meaning: Luchar o forcejear significa intentar derribar a un rival.
spanish_example: A mi padre le encantaba luchar en la secundaria.

english_meaning: Absence is the state of something being away.
english_example: There is an absence of sand in the hourglass.
spanish_meaning: Ausencia es la condición de estar lejos o no estar presente.
spanish_example: Hay una ausencia de arena en el reloj de arena.

english_meaning: An action done aloud is done so that people can hear it.
english_example: My father often reads stories aloud to me and my sister.
spanish_meaning: En voz alta significa de manera audible para que otros puedan oír.
spanish_example: Mi padre suele leer cuentos en voz alta para mí y mi hermana.

english_meaning: A bald person or animal has no hair.
english_example: My oldest brother is bald.
spanish_meaning: Calvo significa no tener pelo en la cabeza.
spanish_example: Mi hermano mayor es calvo.

english_meaning: A blanket is a piece of cloth that people use to keep warm or to sit upon.
english_example: I laid a blanket on the ground so that we could have a picnic.
spanish_meaning: Una manta es una tela para cubrirse y entrar en calor o sentarse.
spanish_example: Puse una manta en el suelo para hacer picnic.

english_meaning: To creep is to move quietly and slowly.
english_example: The cat slowly crept down the tree.
spanish_meaning: Arrastrarse es moverse con sigilo y despacio.
spanish_example: El gato bajó lentamente de la escalera arrastrándose.

english_meaning: Divorce is the process of ending a marriage.
english_example: Divorce rates have increased in the past twenty years.
spanish_meaning: El divorcio es el proceso legal de terminar un matrimonio.
spanish_example: En los últimos veinte años, las tasas de divorcio han aumentado.

english_meaning: To imitate someone or something is to act in the exact same way.
english_example: He imitated his favorite superhero by putting on a costume.
spanish_meaning: Imitar es hacer algo como otra persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Él imitó a su superhéroe favorito poniéndose un disfraz.

english_meaning: An infant is a baby.
english_example: The infant cried all night.
spanish_meaning: Un bebé o infante es un niño muy pequeño.
spanish_example: El bebé lloró toda la noche.

english_meaning: To kidnap someone is to take that person illegally.
english_example: She was terrified to find out her son had been kidnapped.
spanish_meaning: Secuestrar es llevarse a alguien por la fuerza e ilegalmente.
spanish_example: Ella se aterrorizó al saber que su hijo había sido secuestrado.

english_meaning: A nap is a short sleep, usually during the day.
english_example: I took a short nap because I had stayed up late last night.
spanish_meaning: Una siesta es una breve siesta durante el día.
spanish_example: Me tomé una siesta corta porque me acosté tarde anoche.

english_meaning: Nowhere means no place or not existing.
english_example: Unfortunately, water was nowhere to be found.
spanish_meaning: En ninguna parte significa que no existe en ningún lugar.
spanish_example: Lamentablemente, no había agua en ninguna parte.

english_meaning: To pat something is to hit it softly with your hand.
english_example: I patted some lotion onto my face.
spanish_meaning: Palmear es tocar algo suavemente con la mano.
spanish_example: Me di una palmada con crema en la cara.

english_meaning: Relief is a good feeling after something bad or challenging ends.
english_example: I felt a sense of relief when I heard the good news.
spanish_meaning: Alivio es la sensación de descanso tras algo difícil o doloroso.
spanish_example: Sentí un gran alivio cuando oí la buena noticia.

english_meaning: To reproduce is to make something exactly how someone else did it.
english_example: The children tried to reproduce their house using toy blocks.
spanish_meaning: Reproducir es crear de nuevo algo que hizo alguien.
spanish_example: Los niños intentaron reproducir su casa con bloques de juguete.

english_meaning: A rhyme is writing or speech that has words with the same ending sounds.
english_example: “Humpty Dumpty” is an old rhyme that children learn in school.
spanish_meaning: La rima es un verso o expresión con sonidos finales parecidos.
spanish_example: “Humpty Dumpty” es una rima antigua que los niños aprenden en la escuela.

english_meaning: To suck is to put something in your mouth and try to get something out of it.
english_example: The baby sucked milk from her bottle.
spanish_meaning: Chupar es tomar algo en la boca haciendo succión.
spanish_example: El bebé chupó leche de su botella.

english_meaning: An urgent thing is important and needs to be done now.
english_example: He had to leave at that moment; it was urgent.
spanish_meaning: Urgente es algo importante que necesita hacerse de inmediato.
spanish_example: Tuvo que irse en ese momento porque era urgente.

english_meaning: To vanish is to go away suddenly.
english_example: All the passengers vanished from the train station.
spanish_meaning: Desaparecer significa irse de repente y sin dejar rastro.
spanish_example: Todos los pasajeros desaparecieron de la estación de tren.

english_meaning: A wagon is a cart used to carry heavy things.
english_example: He used his wagon to carry some of his gifts.
spanish_meaning: Un carro es un vehículo con ruedas usado para transportar cosas pesadas.
spanish_example: Usó su carro para llevar algunos regalos.

english_meaning: A wrinkle is a line on a person’s face that appears as they get old.
english_example: My grandfather has some wrinkles on his face.
spanish_meaning: Una arruga es una marca o pliegue en la piel, especialmente en la cara.
spanish_example: Mi abuelo tiene algunas arrugas en la cara.

english_meaning: Architecture is the design and form of a building.
english_example: The Sydney Opera House is a good example of modern architecture.
spanish_meaning: La arquitectura es el diseño y forma de un edificio.
spanish_example: El edificio de la Ópera de Sídney es un buen ejemplo de arquitectura moderna.

english_meaning: A basket is a container made of woven materials that is used to carry things.
english_example: He put the vegetables in a basket.
spanish_meaning: Una canasta es un recipiente tejido para llevar objetos.
spanish_example: Puso las verduras en una canasta.

english_meaning: To bloom is to produce an open flower.
english_example: The white daisies were about to bloom.
spanish_meaning: Florecer significa abrirse y salir una flor.
spanish_example: Las margaritas blancas estaban a punto de florecer.

english_meaning: A canoe is a long, light boat with pointed ends.
english_example: He paddled his canoe to the edge of the lake.
spanish_meaning: La canoa es una embarcación pequeña y alargada con extremos puntiagudos.
spanish_example: Remó su canoa hasta la orilla del lago.

english_meaning: A carpet is a thick, heavy, woven fabric used to cover the floor.
english_example: His white living room carpet was soft.
spanish_meaning: Una alfombra es una tela gruesa para cubrir el suelo.
spanish_example: La alfombra blanca de su sala era suave.

english_meaning: A cousin is the child of one’s aunt and uncle.
english_example: My cousin looks just like me.
spanish_meaning: Un primo o prima es el hijo de tu tío o tu tía.
spanish_example: Mi prima se parece mucho a mí.

english_meaning: A desk is a piece of furniture that people sit at to do work.
english_example: She put the books on her desk.
spanish_meaning: El escritorio es un mueble para escribir, estudiar o trabajar.
spanish_example: Puso los libros en su escritorio.

english_meaning: A gallery is a large space where people can see works of art.
english_example: The art gallery displayed beautiful paintings.
spanish_meaning: Una galería es un espacio amplio para mostrar obras de arte.
spanish_example: La galería de arte mostraba cuadros hermosos.

english_meaning: A guest is someone who is invited to an event, occasion, or location.
english_example: Alice invited a special guest for dinner.
spanish_meaning: Un invitado es alguien al que se invita a un evento o a una casa.
spanish_example: Alicia invitó a un invitado especial para cenar.

english_meaning: A host is someone who invites a guest someplace.
english_example: He was a gracious host.
spanish_meaning: Un anfitrión es quien recibe o invita a invitados a un lugar.
spanish_example: Él fue un anfitrión muy amable.

english_meaning: The seventh month of the year is July.
english_example: Her birthday is in July.
spanish_meaning: Julio es el séptimo mes del año.
spanish_example: Su cumpleaños es en julio.

english_meaning: A modern thing belongs to the current time.
english_example: The kitchen had a modern look.
spanish_meaning: Moderno significa propio de la época actual.
spanish_example: La cocina tenía un aspecto muy moderno.

english_meaning: A museum is a building that displays cultural, social, and scientific objects.
english_example: The Louvre is a famous museum in France.
spanish_meaning: Un museo es un edificio donde se muestran objetos culturales, sociales y científicos.
spanish_example: El Louvre es un museo muy famoso en Francia.

english_meaning: Pink is a pale shade of red.
english_example: She chose a pink lipstick.
spanish_meaning: Rosa es un color claro, entre el rojo y el blanco.
spanish_example: Eligió un lápiz labial rosado.

english_meaning: A plane is a vehicle that has an engine and wings and flies in the air.
english_example: They took a plane across the ocean to India.
spanish_meaning: Un avión es una aeronave con alas y motor que vuela.
spanish_example: Tomaron un avión sobre el océano hacia la India.

english_meaning: A refrigerator is a large electrical machine used to keep food cold.
english_example: He kept milk, eggs, and butter in the refrigerator.
spanish_meaning: El refrigerador es un electrodoméstico grande para mantener la comida fría.
spanish_example: Guardó leche, huevos y mantequilla en el refrigerador.

english_meaning: A temperature is a measure of how hot or cold something is.
english_example: In the winter, the temperature can drop below 0 degrees.
spanish_meaning: La temperatura es la medida de calor o frío de algo.
spanish_example: En invierno, la temperatura puede bajar de 0 grados.

english_meaning: A theater is a place where people can watch movies or live shows.
english_example: He took her to see a comedy show at the theater.
spanish_meaning: Un teatro es un lugar para ver cine o espectáculos en vivo.
spanish_example: La llevó a ver un espectáculo de comedia en el teatro.

english_meaning: An upper position is a higher position.
english_example: He took the elevator to the upper floor.
spanish_meaning: Superior significa que está en una posición más alta.
spanish_example: Tomó el ascensor al piso superior.

english_meaning: To welcome is to greet someone or something with pleasure.
english_example: She greeted her friends with a warm welcome.
spanish_meaning: Dar la bienvenida es recibir a alguien con afecto y agrado.
spanish_example: Recibió a sus amigos con una bienvenida cálida.

english_meaning: To anticipate something is to think that it will happen.
english_example: Carrie anticipated the arrival of her baby.
spanish_meaning: Anticipar es pensar o esperar que algo sucederá.
spanish_example: Carrie anticipaba la llegada de su bebé.

english_meaning: A barrel is a round thing that you can keep liquids in.
english_example: There was an empty barrel outside the house.
spanish_meaning: Un barril es un recipiente cilíndrico para guardar líquidos.
spanish_example: Había un barril vacío fuera de la casa.

english_meaning: A beam is a heavy bar.
english_example: Modern skyscrapers are made with many beams.
spanish_meaning: Una viga es una barra resistente que sostiene una construcción.
spanish_example: Los rascacielos modernos se construyen con muchas vigas.

english_meaning: A casual thing is relaxed or simple.
english_example: You can wear casual clothes, like jeans, to the party.
spanish_meaning: Casual significa relajado y poco formal.
spanish_example: Puedes vestir ropa casual, como vaqueros, para la fiesta.

english_meaning: Caution is care and attention in order to avoid danger.
english_example: Please use the power saw with caution. It is very dangerous.
spanish_meaning: Precaución es actuar con cuidado para evitar peligro.
spanish_example: Usa la sierra eléctrica con precaución; es muy peligrosa.

english_meaning: A contrary thing is the opposite to another thing.
english_example: It isn’t warm outside at all. On the contrary, it is quite cold.
spanish_meaning: Contrario significa opuesto o opuesta a algo.
spanish_example: No hace calor afuera. Por el contrario, hace bastante frío.

english_meaning: A deliberate action is one done on purpose, not by accident.
english_example: Bernie made a deliberate attempt to injure Andy.
spanish_meaning: Deliberado significa hecho a propósito y con intención.
spanish_example: Bernie hizo un intento deliberado de lastimar a Andy.

english_meaning: To dissolve something is to mix it into a liquid until it disappears.
english_example: I dissolved the pill in a glass of water.
spanish_meaning: Disolver es mezclar una sustancia en líquido hasta que desaparece.
spanish_example: Disolví la pastilla en un vaso de agua.

english_meaning: To explode is to suddenly move apart in many smaller pieces.
english_example: The old TV exploded when I plugged it in.
spanish_meaning: Explotar es abrirse con fuerza y romperse en piezas o expandirse abruptamente.
spanish_example: El televisor viejo explotó cuando lo conecté.

english_meaning: To fasten something is to close it or attach it to something.
english_example: Elizabeth fastened her seat belt.
spanish_meaning: Abrochar significa cerrar o unir algo con un broche o hebilla.
spanish_example: Elizabeth se abrochó el cinturón de seguridad.

english_meaning: A germ is a very small living thing that can make people sick.
english_example: Germs are on everything that you touch.
spanish_meaning: Un germen es un organismo muy pequeño que puede causar enfermedad.
spanish_example: Hay gérmenes en todo lo que tocas.

english_meaning: A kit is a set of all the things needed to do something.
english_example: Is there a first aid kit in your office?
spanish_meaning: Un kit es un conjunto de objetos necesarios para hacer algo.
spanish_example: ¿Tienes un kit de primeros auxilios en la oficina?

english_meaning: A puff is a little bit of smoke or steam.
english_example: A puff of smoke came from the burnt match.
spanish_meaning: Una bocanada es una pequeña nube de humo o vapor.
spanish_example: Salió una bocanada de humo del fósforo quemado.

english_meaning: A rag is a small towel.
english_example: Please use a rag to clean the dust off the table.
spanish_meaning: Un trapo es una tela pequeña para limpiar o fregar.
spanish_example: Usa un trapo para quitar el polvo de la mesa.

english_meaning: To scatter something is to make it go in many places.
english_example: I accidentally scattered all of my pills.
spanish_meaning: Dispersar es repartir algo en muchos lugares.
spanish_example: Accidentalmente dispersé todas mis pastillas.

english_meaning: A scent is a smell.
english_example: Julie enjoyed the scent of the flowers.
spanish_meaning: El aroma es un olor agradable.
spanish_example: A Julie le encantaba el aroma de las flores.

english_meaning: Steel is a strong gray metal.
english_example: The new apartment building was made with steel.
spanish_meaning: El acero es un metal resistente de color gris.
spanish_example: El nuevo edificio de apartamentos se construyó con acero.

english_meaning: A swift person or animal is fast.
english_example: The swift horse easily jumped over the hurdle.
spanish_meaning: Ágil significa muy rápido y ligero en movimiento.
spanish_example: El caballo ágil saltó fácilmente la valla.

english_meaning: To toss something is to throw it softly.
english_example: He tossed a coin into the air.
spanish_meaning: Lanzar es tirar algo suavemente por el aire o hacia un lugar.
spanish_example: Lanzó una moneda al aire.

english_meaning: Triumph is the act or feeling of winning.
english_example: He raised the award in triumph at the end of his speech.
spanish_meaning: Triunfo es ganar o la emoción de una victoria.
spanish_example: Al final de su discurso, levantó el premio en señal de triunfo.

english_meaning: Someone aboard a ship or plane is in or on it.
english_example: They climbed aboard the kayak and paddled through the river.
spanish_meaning: A bordo significa en un barco o avión.
spanish_example: Subieron a bordo de la canoa y remaron río abajo.

english_meaning: A bitter person feels upset or angry about something.
english_example: He was extremely bitter when his computer crashed.
spanish_meaning: Amargo también describe una actitud resentida o enojada por algo.
spanish_example: Se puso muy amargo cuando se rompió su computadora.

english_meaning: A bullet is a small metal object that is shot out of a gun.
english_example: Bullets come in different sizes for different guns.
spanish_meaning: Una bala es un proyectil pequeño de metal disparado de un arma.
spanish_example: Las balas tienen distintos tamaños según el arma.

english_meaning: The devil is a powerful evil spirit in some religions.
english_example: The church promised protection from the devil.
spanish_meaning: El diablo es un espíritu maligno muy poderoso en algunas religiones.
spanish_example: La iglesia prometió protección contra el diablo.

english_meaning: To drift means to be moved slowly by wind or water.
english_example: The large chunk of ice drifted in the water.
spanish_meaning: Derivar es moverse lentamente impulsado por el viento o el agua.
spanish_example: El gran bloque de hielo derivó por el agua.

english_meaning: To enforce means to make a person follow a rule.
english_example: Police enforce traffic laws to keep everyone safe.
spanish_meaning: Hacer cumplir significa obligar a respetar una norma.
spanish_example: La policía hace cumplir las leyes de tránsito para mantener la seguridad.

english_meaning: A fountain is a source of water made by people.
english_example: There was a beautiful fountain in the middle of the park.
spanish_meaning: Una fuente es una estructura creada por el ser humano que lanza agua.
spanish_example: Había una hermosa fuente en medio del parque.

english_meaning: A harbor is an area of water along a shore where boats land.
english_example: There were a few small boats in the harbor.
spanish_meaning: Un puerto es una zona costera protegida donde atracan los barcos.
spanish_example: Había algunos barcos pequeños en el puerto.

english_meaning: To inhabit means to live in a certain place.
english_example: No one inhabits the ancient city.
spanish_meaning: Habitar significa vivir en un lugar.
spanish_example: Nadie habita la ciudad antigua.

english_meaning: To march means to walk at a steady pace together with others.
english_example: The soldiers marched in straight rows.
spanish_meaning: Marchar significa caminar ordenadamente con un paso constante junto a otros.
spanish_example: Los soldados marcharon en filas rectas.

english_meaning: A millionaire is a person who has at least a million dollars.
english_example: He became a millionaire because he was smart with his money.
spanish_meaning: Un millonario es una persona que posee al menos un millón de dólares.
spanish_example: Se volvió millonario porque manejó bien su dinero.

english_meaning: A port is a place where ships stop to load and unload things.
english_example: The ship was being loaded with materials at the port.
spanish_meaning: Un puerto es un lugar donde los barcos atracan para cargar o descargar.
spanish_example: El barco se estaba cargando con materiales en el puerto.

english_meaning: A sheriff is a police officer who is in charge of a large area.
english_example: It was the sheriff’s job to make the city safe.
spanish_meaning: Un alguacil o sheriff es un agente que supervisa la seguridad de una zona amplia.
spanish_example: Era tarea del alguacil mantener la ciudad segura.

english_meaning: To startle means to scare someone suddenly.
english_example: The loud crash startled the sleeping woman.
spanish_meaning: Sorprender de golpe es asustar bruscamente a alguien.
spanish_example: El fuerte ruido sorprendió a la mujer dormida.

english_meaning: To sweat means to lose liquid from the body through the skin.
english_example: Whenever I go to the gym, I sweat quite a bit.
spanish_meaning: Sudar es perder agua por la piel durante el esfuerzo o el calor.
spanish_example: Cuando voy al gimnasio, sudo bastante.

english_meaning: A trigger is the part of a gun that a person pulls to make it fire.
english_example: The man had his finger on the trigger of the gun.
spanish_meaning: El gatillo es la pieza de un arma que se aprieta para disparar.
spanish_example: El hombre tenía el dedo en el gatillo del arma.

english_meaning: To unify means to bring people or things together.
english_example: With their good deeds, the children tried to unify the world.
spanish_meaning: Unificar significa unir personas o cosas para hacerlas una sola parte.
spanish_example: Con sus buenas acciones, los niños intentaron unificar al mundo.

english_meaning: A vessel is a large ship or boat.
english_example: We toured the area aboard a luxury vessel.
spanish_meaning: Un buque es una embarcación grande como un barco.
spanish_example: Recorrimos la zona a bordo de un buque de lujo.

english_meaning: A voyage is a long journey made on a boat or an aircraft.
english_example: The astronauts took off on a long voyage to the moon.
spanish_meaning: Un viaje es un trayecto largo por barco o avión.
spanish_example: Los astronautas partieron en un largo viaje a la luna.

english_meaning: To worship means to like and honor a person, thing, or religious figure.
english_example: Many people around the world worship in a church.
spanish_meaning: Adorar es venerar o rendir honor a alguien o a Dios.
spanish_example: Muchas personas en el mundo adoran en una iglesia.

english_meaning: An apprentice is a person who learns how to do a job from a skilled person.
english_example: Mark is an apprentice chef at the restaurant.
spanish_meaning: Un aprendiz aprende un oficio bajo la guía de un experto.
spanish_example: Mark es aprendiz de chef en el restaurante.

english_meaning: To assure people is to tell them something is true to make them less worried.
english_example: He assured the boss that the building would be done on time.
spanish_meaning: Asegurar significa decir algo con certeza para calmar preocupaciones.
spanish_example: Aseguró al jefe que el edificio estaría listo a tiempo.

english_meaning: A bandage is a piece of cloth used to stop bleeding.
english_example: If you cut yourself, please get a bandage from the first-aid kit.
spanish_meaning: La venda es una tela que se usa para detener el sangrado.
spanish_example: Si te cortas, busca una venda en el botiquín.

english_meaning: To bleed is to lose blood.
english_example: If you are not careful, you will cut your finger and bleed.
spanish_meaning: Sangrar es perder sangre por el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Si no tienes cuidado, te cortarás el dedo y sangrarás.

english_meaning: To bond with someone is to become friends with that person.
english_example: The women bonded after several hours of conversation.
spanish_meaning: Un vínculo es una relación de cercanía o amistad entre personas.
spanish_example: Las mujeres hicieron vínculo tras varias horas de conversación.

english_meaning: A chef is a person who cooks in a restaurant.
english_example: Tom is a chef at the restaurant near my house.
spanish_meaning: El chef es la persona que cocina en un restaurante.
spanish_example: Tom es chef en el restaurante de cerca de mi casa.

english_meaning: A crown is the hat worn by a king or queen.
english_example: The crown is made of gold.
spanish_meaning: La corona es la tiara o adorno real de rey o reina.
spanish_example: La corona está hecha de oro.

english_meaning: A departure is the act of leaving a place.
english_example: They were excited about their departure to go to go back home.
spanish_meaning: La partida es el acto de partir o irse de un lugar.
spanish_example: Estaban emocionados por su partida de regreso a casa.

english_meaning: A diligent person works hard and is careful.
english_example: Craig has always been a very diligent person at work.
spanish_meaning: Diligente significa trabajador y cuidadoso.
spanish_example: Craig siempre ha sido una persona muy diligente en el trabajo.

english_meaning: An emperor is the leader of a group of countries.
english_example: The emperor Julius Caesar was in control of ancient Rome.
spanish_meaning: El emperador es el gobernante supremo de un imperio o varias tierras.
spanish_example: El emperador Julio César controlaba la antigua Roma.

english_meaning: A fiber is a thread of a substance used to make clothes or rope.
english_example: The mat was made from tiny fibers.
spanish_meaning: La fibra es un hilo fino de un material usado para tejer o hacer cuerda.
spanish_example: La esterilla estaba hecha con fibras diminutas.

english_meaning: A horrible thing is very bad.
english_example: The assignment was horrible. I hated it.
spanish_meaning: Horrible significa muy malo, desagradable o terrible.
spanish_example: La tarea fue horrible; la odié.

english_meaning: An impolite person is rude, or not polite.
english_example: Roger is impolite to everyone he meets.
spanish_meaning: Descortés significa falto de educación o grosero.
spanish_example: Roger es descortés con todo el que conoce.

english_meaning: To kneel is to put one or both knees on the ground.
english_example: Daryl kneeled down on one knee and asked Nina to marry him.
spanish_meaning: Arrodillarse es ponerse de rodillas, normalmente para suplicar o rezar.
spanish_example: Daryl se arrodilló en una rodilla y le pidió matrimonio a Nina.

english_meaning: A luxury is an expensive thing that is nice but not needed.
english_example: Our honeymoon resort was a luxury we really enjoyed.
spanish_meaning: Un lujo es algo caro, cómodo y no esencial.
spanish_example: Nuestro resort de luna de miel fue un verdadero lujo que disfrutamos mucho.

english_meaning: A massive thing is very big.
english_example: The wheels on his truck were massive.
spanish_meaning: Masivo significa de gran tamaño o escala.
spanish_example: Las ruedas de su camión eran masivas.

english_meaning: To panic is to feel so nervous or afraid that one cannot think clearly.
english_example: Everyone panicked when the house caught on fire.
spanish_meaning: El pánico es un miedo intenso que impide pensar con claridad.
spanish_example: Todos entraron en pánico cuando la casa se incendió.

english_meaning: A priority is something that is more important than other things.
english_example: My priority is to get good grades in school.
spanish_meaning: Prioridad es aquello que se considera más importante que otras cosas.
spanish_example: Mi prioridad es sacar buenas notas en la escuela.

english_meaning: A robe is a long, loose piece of clothing.
english_example: The monks at the temple all wear robes.
spanish_meaning: Una túnica es una prenda larga y suelta.
spanish_example: Los monjes del templo todos llevan túnicas.

english_meaning: To scold means to criticize someone angrily for doing something wrong.
english_example: Jesse was scolded by the teacher for not paying attention.
spanish_meaning: Regañar es reprender con firmeza a alguien por una falta.
spanish_example: El profesor regañó a Jesse por no prestar atención.

english_meaning: An affair is an event or a thing that happened.
english_example: My wife and I attended a formal affair over the weekend.
spanish_meaning: Un asunto o evento es una actividad o hecho importante ocurrido.
spanish_example: Mi esposa y yo asistimos a un evento formal el fin de semana.

english_meaning: An assembly is a group gathered together for the same reason.
english_example: The students had an assembly to talk about their interests.
spanish_meaning: Una asamblea es un grupo reunido para tratar un tema.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes tuvieron una asamblea para hablar de sus intereses.

english_meaning: To bless is to ask God for protection or help.
english_example: The angel blessed the newborn baby to keep it safe.
spanish_meaning: Bendecir es pedir protección o pedir por alguien en un sentido religioso.
spanish_example: El ángel bendijo al recién nacido para mantenerlo seguro.

english_meaning: Cereal is a breakfast food made from grains that is eaten with milk.
english_example: Cereal is a fast and common breakfast food enjoyed in the US.
spanish_meaning: El cereal es un alimento de desayuno hecho con granos y se come con leche.
spanish_example: El cereal es un desayuno rápido y común en EE UU.

english_meaning: A cheerful person is happy and pleasant.
english_example: The children were cheerful because they didn’t have to go to school.
spanish_meaning: Alegre es una persona feliz y de buen ánimo.
spanish_example: Los niños estaban alegres porque no tenían que ir a la escuela.

english_meaning: The diameter of a round thing is the length across its center.
english_example: The diameter of the tree was about 60 centimeters.
spanish_meaning: El diámetro de un objeto redondo es la distancia entre dos lados opuestos pasando por el centro.
spanish_example: El diámetro del árbol era de unos 60 centímetros.

english_meaning: To exploit something is to use it for greedy reasons rather than good reasons.
english_example: The company exploits their workers and makes them work 12 hours a day.
spanish_meaning: Explotar significa usar algo o a alguien en beneficio propio de forma abusiva.
spanish_example: La empresa explota a sus trabajadores y les hace trabajar 12 horas diarias.

english_meaning: A famine is a long time with little or no food.
english_example: The farmers couldn’t grow any food in the dry soil, so there was a famine.
spanish_meaning: Hambruna es un período prolongado de escasez de alimentos.
spanish_example: Los agricultores no pudieron cultivar nada en el suelo seco, así que hubo una hambruna.

english_meaning: A harvest is the act of collecting food from farming.
english_example: They had a lot of wheat from the last harvest.
spanish_meaning: La cosecha es el acto de recolectar productos agrícolas.
spanish_example: Tuvieron mucho trigo tras la última cosecha.

english_meaning: A merry person is happy and pleasant.
english_example: They felt merry because the weather was great.
spanish_meaning: Alegre significa contento y animado.
spanish_example: Se sintieron alegres porque el tiempo estaba muy bueno.

english_meaning: A nut is a hard seed or fruit that comes from some trees and bushes.
english_example: To eat a nut, you must first crack its shell.
spanish_meaning: Una nuez es la semilla dura de ciertos árboles o arbustos comestible.
spanish_example: Para comer una nuez, primero debes romper su cáscara.

english_meaning: To pardon is to not be angry at someone for asking a question or for making a mistake.
english_example: Pardon me teacher, but could you repeat what you just said?
spanish_meaning: El perdón es la acción de excusar o no guardar rencor por una falta o pregunta.
spanish_example: ¿Perdón, profesora, pero podría repetir lo que dijo?

english_meaning: A pharaoh was a king in ancient Egypt.
english_example: The pharaohs ruled Egypt for thousands of years.
spanish_meaning: El faraón era el rey del antiguo Egipto.
spanish_example: Los faraones gobernaron Egipto durante miles de años.

english_meaning: A ripe fruit or vegetable is ready to be eaten.
english_example: The cherries were nice and ripe.
spanish_meaning: Maduro significa listo para comer por su grado de maduración.
spanish_example: Las cerezas estaban dulces y maduras.

english_meaning: To roast something is to cook it in an oven or over a fire.
english_example: Mom roasted a turkey for the holiday dinner.
