spanish_meaning: Asar es cocinar con calor seco, en horno o fuego.
spanish_example: Mamá asó un pavo para la cena de Navidad.

english_meaning: A routine is a way of doing things that is the same every time.
english_example: My father’s daily routine includes shaving right before breakfast.
spanish_meaning: La rutina es una secuencia de acciones que se repite cada día.
spanish_example: La rutina diaria de mi padre incluye afeitarse antes del desayuno.

english_meaning: A scheme is a plan or design.
english_example: Jason and Mark came up with a scheme to solve the problem.
spanish_meaning: Un plan o esquema es una estrategia para lograr algo.
spanish_example: Jason y Mark idearon un plan para resolver el problema.

english_meaning: A slim person or thing is thin, not thick.
english_example: Look at my new cell phone. It’s very slim.
spanish_meaning: Delgado significa delgado y de poca grasa o volumen.
spanish_example: Mira mi celular nuevo. Es muy delgado.

english_meaning: A stove is a device used to cook food.
english_example: Our new stove helps us to cook food much faster than before.
spanish_meaning: Una estufa es un artefacto para cocinar alimentos.
spanish_example: Nuestra nueva estufa nos ayuda a cocinar mucho más rápido.

english_meaning: A theft is a criminal act that involves someone stealing something.
english_example: The theft of his TV took place when he was at work.
spanish_meaning: El robo es el acto ilegal de quitar algo de propiedad de otro.
spanish_example: El robo de su televisor ocurrió mientras estaba en el trabajo.

english_meaning: An adolescent is a young person or a teenager.
english_example: The adolescent was excited about getting a skateboard for his birthday.
spanish_meaning: Un adolescente es un joven en la etapa de la adolescencia.
spanish_example: El adolescente estaba feliz por recibir un monopatín de cumpleaños.

english_meaning: Aptitude is a natural ability or skill.
english_example: He has a natural aptitude for water skiing.
spanish_meaning: La aptitud es una habilidad natural para hacer algo con facilidad.
spanish_example: Tiene una aptitud natural para practicar esquí acuático.

english_meaning: To compliment is to say a nice thing about someone or something.
english_example: Her co-worker complimented her for doing a good job.
spanish_meaning: Un elogio es decir algo bueno sobre alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Su compañera de trabajo le dio un elogio por hacer un buen trabajo.

english_meaning: To hinder is to stop someone or something from doing something.
english_example: All the traffic hindered me from getting to work on time.
spanish_meaning: Obstaculizar es dificultar que alguien haga algo.
spanish_example: Todo el tráfico me obstaculizó para llegar al trabajo a tiempo.

english_meaning: Journalism is the work of collecting the news to put in newspapers or on TV.
english_example: Before becoming a teacher, she worked in journalism.
spanish_meaning: El periodismo es la actividad de recoger y difundir noticias en medios de comunicación.
spanish_example: Antes de ser maestra, ella trabajaba en el periodismo.

english_meaning: A jury is a group of people that listen to a trial and say if someone is guilty.
english_example: The jury listened closely to the attorney before they made their decision.
spanish_meaning: El jurado es el grupo que evalúa un juicio y emite un veredicto.
spanish_example: El jurado escuchó atentamente al abogado antes de decidir.

english_meaning: Justice is fairness in the way that people are treated.
english_example: People turn to the court system when they are seeking justice.
spanish_meaning: La justicia es el trato justo y equitativo hacia las personas.
spanish_example: La gente acude a los tribunales para buscar justicia.

english_meaning: Liberty is freedom to do what one wants.
english_example: To many people, the Statue of Liberty is a symbol of freedom.
spanish_meaning: La libertad es poder hacer lo que se desea dentro de la ley.
spanish_example: Para mucha gente, la Estatua de la Libertad es símbolo de libertad.

english_meaning: A literary person or thing is involved with literature in some way.
english_example: He worked hard to create a successful literary career.
spanish_meaning: Literario se refiere a lo relacionado con la literatura.
spanish_example: Trabajó duro para crear una carrera literaria exitosa.

english_meaning: A pharmacy is a place where medicine is sold.
english_example: My mother sells medicine to people at the pharmacy.
spanish_meaning: Una farmacia es un establecimiento donde se venden medicamentos.
spanish_example: Mi madre vende medicamentos en la farmacia.

english_meaning: A pill is a small object that has medicine inside.
english_example: She took a pill for her headache.
spanish_meaning: Una pastilla es un pequeño comprimido con medicamento.
spanish_example: Se tomó una pastilla para el dolor de cabeza.

english_meaning: To presume is to believe something is true without being certain.
english_example: Since the girl raised her hand, the teacher presumed she knew the answer.
spanish_meaning: Presumir significa pensar que algo es cierto sin tener prueba suficiente.
spanish_example: Como la niña levantó la mano, el profesor presumió que conocía la respuesta.

english_meaning: Privacy is the state of being happily away from other people.
english_example: Please hang the sign on the door so we can have some privacy.
spanish_meaning: La privacidad es estar libre de la mirada de otros.
spanish_example: Pon el cartel en la puerta para que tengamos algo de privacidad.

english_meaning: A punishment is something that one must endure for any wrongdoing.
english_example: He was given a punishment for being rude to the teacher.
spanish_meaning: El castigo es una sanción por una mala conducta o falta.
spanish_example: Le dieron un castigo por ser grosero con el profesor.

english_meaning: A sensible person or thing is good and smart.
english_example: It was sensible for her to save some money each month.
spanish_meaning: Sensato significa razonable y práctico.
spanish_example: Fue sensato de su parte ahorrar algo de dinero cada mes.

english_meaning: A slice is a piece from something larger, such as a cake.
english_example: The girl enjoyed a slice of cake at her birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Una rebanada es un trozo delgado de un alimento.
spanish_example: La niña disfrutó de una rebanada de tarta en su cumpleaños.

english_meaning: Sorrow is a very sad feeling.
english_example: The girl felt sorrow after her best friend moved away.
spanish_meaning: La tristeza es un sentimiento de dolor emocional intenso.
spanish_example: La niña sintió mucha tristeza cuando su mejor amiga se mudó.

english_meaning: A straw is a thin tube that is used to suck liquid into the mouth.
english_example: I drank the orange juice through a straw.
spanish_meaning: Una pajita es un tubito para sorber líquido con la boca.
spanish_example: Tomé el jugo de naranja con una pajita.

english_meaning: To swell is to become larger and rounder.
english_example: My sister’s stomach began to swell after she got pregnant.
spanish_meaning: Hinchase significa aumentar de tamaño o volumen.
spanish_example: El estómago de mi hermana empezó a hincharse cuando quedó embarazada.

english_meaning: A tidy place or person is clean and in order.
english_example: Leon has always been a very tidy boy.
spanish_meaning: Ordenado significa limpio y con todo en su lugar.
spanish_example: León siempre ha sido un chico muy ordenado.

english_meaning: Affection is a feeling of liking someone or something.
english_example: Amanda has a lot of affection for her little sister, Sarah.
spanish_meaning: El afecto es el sentimiento de cariño hacia alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Amanda tiene mucho afecto por su hermanita Sarah.

english_meaning: An agency is a business or service set up to act for others.
english_example: I went to a travel agency to help me arrange a flight home.
spanish_meaning: Una agencia es una empresa que presta un servicio en nombre de otros.
spanish_example: Fui a una agencia de viajes para organizar mi vuelo de regreso.

english_meaning: Ash is the grey or black powder created when something is burned.
english_example: The end of his cigar was full of ashes.
spanish_meaning: La ceniza es un polvo gris o negro que queda al quemar algo.
spanish_example: La boquilla de su cigarro estaba llena de ceniza.

english_meaning: To confine something is to keep it in one place.
english_example: The elephant is confined to a cage in the zoo.
spanish_meaning: Confinar significa mantener o limitar algo en un lugar determinado.
spanish_example: El elefante está confinado a una jaula en el zoológico.

english_meaning: To dismiss something is to say it is not important.
english_example: He quickly dismissed my idea about the new project.
spanish_meaning: Rechazar una idea es no aceptarla o tomarla por insignificante.
spanish_example: Rechazó rápidamente mi idea sobre el nuevo proyecto.

english_meaning: To erupt is to explode or blow apart, especially a volcano.
english_example: The volcano erupted for the first time in ten years.
spanish_meaning: Erupcionar es expulsar material o energía de forma violenta, como un volcán.
spanish_example: El volcán erupcionó por primera vez en diez años.

english_meaning: Fate is a power that causes things to happen.
english_example: Some people believe that the lines on a person’s hand can tell his or her fate.
spanish_meaning: El destino es la fuerza o camino que determina lo que sucederá.
spanish_example: Algunas personas creen que las líneas de la mano revelan el destino.

english_meaning: Lava is the hot substance made of melted rock that comes out of volcanoes.
english_example: The red-hot lava poured from the volcano.
spanish_meaning: La lava es roca fundida muy caliente que sale de un volcán.
spanish_example: La lava ardiente salió del volcán.

english_meaning: A miserable person is very unhappy.
english_example: He was miserable after his dog died.
spanish_meaning: Miserable significa muy triste y con poco ánimo.
spanish_example: Él estaba miserable después de que su perro murió.

english_meaning: To navigate something is to control the way it moves or goes.
english_example: She navigated the ship across the ocean.
spanish_meaning: Controlar la dirección o el rumbo de algo.
spanish_example: Ella navegó el barco por el océano.

english_meaning: To originate somewhere is to start there.
english_example: The idea of democracy originated in Ancient Greece.
spanish_meaning: Proceder o comenzar en un lugar u origen.
spanish_example: La idea de la democracia se originó en la Antigua Grecia.

english_meaning: The remainder of something is what is left.
english_example: He took a bite of the apple and then gave me the remainder of it.
spanish_meaning: Lo que queda después de que se ha quitado algo.
spanish_example: Tomó un bocado de la manzana y me dio el resto.

english_meaning: To retrieve something is to find it and get it back.
english_example: She retrieved her mail from the mailbox.
spanish_meaning: Encontrar algo y traerlo de vuelta.
spanish_example: Ella recuperó su correo del buzón.

english_meaning: A shallow thing is not deep.
english_example: The kids were playing in the shallow water.
spanish_meaning: Poco profundo, en poca profundidad.
spanish_example: Los niños jugaban en agua somera.

english_meaning: A slope is ground that is not flat.
english_example: The slope to the top of the mountain was very steep.
spanish_meaning: Terreno que no es plano, que sube o baja.
spanish_example: La pendiente hacia la cima de la montaña era muy empinada.

english_meaning: To span is to spread across an amount of time or space.
english_example: His work at the bakery has spanned twenty years.
spanish_meaning: Extenderse por un período de tiempo o espacio.
spanish_example: Su trabajo en la panadería abarcó veinte años.

english_meaning: A superstition is something magical that people believe is real.
english_example: It is a superstition that Friday the 13th is an unlucky day.
spanish_meaning: Creencia irracional de que algo mágico es real.
spanish_example: Creen que el viernes 13 es un día de mala suerte.

english_meaning: Sympathy is a feeling of sadness for another person who feels bad.
english_example: I felt sympathy for my sister, so I got her a balloon to cheer her up.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de pena o compasión por alguien.
spanish_example: Sentí simpatía por mi hermana y le compré un globo para animarla.

english_meaning: To vibrate is to shake very hard.
english_example: The machine made his whole body vibrate as he broke up the ground.
spanish_meaning: Sacudirse o moverse rápidamente.
spanish_example: La máquina hizo que todo su cuerpo vibrara.

english_meaning: To wander is to walk without going to a certain place.
english_example: The boys like to wander in the woods and look at birds.
spanish_meaning: Caminar sin rumbo o objetivo fijo.
spanish_example: A los niños les gusta deambular por el bosque.

english_meaning: Armor is metal worn by soldiers to protect the body.
english_example: The soldier wore armor to protect his body.
spanish_meaning: Protección de metal que llevan algunos soldados.
spanish_example: El soldado llevaba armadura para protegerse.

english_meaning: To blaze means to burn brightly or powerfully.
english_example: The small fire soon blazed into a large, dangerous one.
spanish_meaning: Arder con gran intensidad y brillo.
spanish_example: El fuego pequeño se convirtió en una gran llama brillante.

english_meaning: To boom means to make a loud, deep sound.
english_example: The firecrackers made a loud boom when they exploded.
spanish_meaning: Sonido fuerte y profundo.
spanish_example: Los petardos hicieron un gran estruendo al explotar.

english_meaning: A cliff is a high and often flat wall of rock.
english_example: The wolf stood at the cliff and howled.
spanish_meaning: Gran pared de roca alta y casi vertical.
spanish_example: El lobo se quedó junto al acantilado y aulló.

english_meaning: A flame is part of a fire.
english_example: The torch was filled with yellow and orange flames.
spanish_meaning: Parte de un fuego que brilla.
spanish_example: La antorcha tenía llamas amarillas y anaranjadas.

english_meaning: Independence is the state of being free from the control of others.
english_example: After leaving home, Sophia had a great feeling of independence.
spanish_meaning: Estado de ser libre de control de otros.
spanish_example: Después de mudarse, Sophia sintió una gran independencia.

english_meaning: An invasion is an attack by a group from another country.
english_example: In Korea, walls were built around cities to protect them from invasions.
spanish_meaning: Ataque de un grupo desde otro país o lugar.
spanish_example: En Corea construían muros para protegerse de invasiones.

english_meaning: A knight is a soldier of high rank and skill who usually serves a king.
english_example: He was the best soldier, so the king made him a knight.
spanish_meaning: Soldado de alto rango que suele servir a un rey.
spanish_example: El rey lo convirtió en un caballero.

english_meaning: Lightning is the bright light seen during a storm.
english_example: The lightning flashed above the water.
spanish_meaning: Luz intensa que aparece durante una tormenta.
spanish_example: El relámpago brilló sobre el agua.

english_meaning: A rebel is a person who fights the government in order to change it.
english_example: The rebel was angry about the government’s unfair policies.
spanish_meaning: Persona que se opone al gobierno para cambiarlo.
spanish_example: El rebelde estaba enojado por las políticas injustas del gobierno.

english_meaning: To retreat means to run away because of losing a fight.
english_example: The army retreated because they were losing the battle.
spanish_meaning: Alejarse o retroceder en una pelea.
spanish_example: El ejército se retiró porque perdía la batalla.

english_meaning: A revolution is a change to the political system by a group of people.
english_example: The revolution in Russia led to the creation of the Soviet Union.
spanish_meaning: Cambio político profundo realizado por un grupo de personas.
spanish_example: La revolución en Rusia llevó a la creación de la Unión Soviética.

english_meaning: A spear is a long stick with a blade on one end that is used as a weapon.
english_example: The soldier was holding a spear in his hand.
spanish_meaning: Arma larga con hoja en un extremo.
spanish_example: El soldado llevaba una lanza en la mano.

english_meaning: A steep surface or place has a slope or angle that rises or falls sharply.
english_example: He rode his bike up the steep hill to reach the top.
spanish_meaning: Con fuerte inclinación o desnivel.
spanish_example: Él subió su bici por la colina empinada.

english_meaning: A summit is the highest part of a hill or mountain.
english_example: Snow covered the summit of the mountain even during the summer.
spanish_meaning: Parte más alta de una colina o montaña.
spanish_example: La nieve cubría la cumbre de la montaña.

english_meaning: Thunder is the loud noise heard during a storm.
english_example: The sound of the thunder startled me.
spanish_meaning: Ruido fuerte de una tormenta.
spanish_example: El sonido del trueno me sorprendió.

english_meaning: Troops are soldiers that fight in groups in a battle.
english_example: The troops were all prepared to go into battle.
spanish_meaning: Grupo de soldados que luchan juntos.
spanish_example: Las tropas estaban preparadas para ir a la batalla.

english_meaning: A warrior is a brave soldier or fighter.
english_example: The samurai were some of the most skilled warriors in the ancient world.
spanish_meaning: Soldado o luchador valiente.
spanish_example: Los samuráis eran guerreros muy hábiles.

english_meaning: To withdraw means to leave a place, usually during war.
english_example: After losing the battle, the enemy withdrew back to its own country.
spanish_meaning: Apartarse o regresar de un lugar, especialmente en guerra.
spanish_example: El enemigo se retiró a su propio país.

english_meaning: To yield something means to give up control of it or to give it away.
english_example: He had to yield his turn because he was in checkmate.
spanish_meaning: Dar el control o entregar algo.
spanish_example: Tuvo que ceder su turno.

english_meaning: A bench is a long seat for two or more people.
english_example: Most parks have benches for people to relax upon.
spanish_meaning: Asiento largo para dos o más personas.
spanish_example: La mayoría de los parques tienen bancos para descansar.

english_meaning: A daisy is a small flower with white petals and a yellow center.
english_example: There were a few daisies growing in the field.
spanish_meaning: Flor pequeña de pétalos blancos y centro amarillo.
spanish_example: Había algunas margaritas creciendo en el campo.

english_meaning: A dispute is an argument or disagreement that people have.
english_example: Karen and Brian often have disputes about silly things.
spanish_meaning: Desacuerdo o discusión entre personas.
spanish_example: Karen y Brian suelen tener disputas por cosas tontas.

english_meaning: An empty container is one that has no things in it.
english_example: The teacher showed the students an empty bottle.
spanish_meaning: Que no tiene nada en su interior.
spanish_example: La maestra mostró a los alumnos una botella vacía.

english_meaning: Horror is a feeling of being very afraid or shocked.
english_example: The audience screamed in horror when the ghost appeared in the movie.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de miedo o terror intenso.
spanish_example: El público gritó de horror cuando apareció el fantasma.

english_meaning: An incident is an event that is usually not pleasant.
english_example: Mr. Wilson had an incident; he became sick and had to leave.
spanish_meaning: Suceso, generalmente desagradable, que ocurre de forma aislada.
spanish_example: El señor Wilson tuvo un incidente: se enfermó y tuvo que irse.

english_meaning: Mist is water that can be seen in the air or on a surface.
english_example: The forest was covered with mist.
spanish_meaning: Agua visible en el aire o sobre una superficie.
spanish_example: El bosque estaba cubierto de niebla.

english_meaning: An object is a non-living thing that you can see or touch.
english_example: The shopping cart was filled with objects.
spanish_meaning: Cosa no viva que se puede ver o tocar.
spanish_example: El carro estaba lleno de objetos.

english_meaning: An orphan is a child who does not have parents.
english_example: The orphan frequently cried during the night.
spanish_meaning: Niño o niña sin padres.
spanish_example: El huérfano lloraba mucho por la noche.

english_meaning: To plot is to make a secret plan to do something that is wrong or mean.
english_example: The group was plotting to ruin the company’s financial reports.
spanish_meaning: Planear en secreto algo malo o dañino.
spanish_example: El grupo tramaba arruinar los informes financieros de la empresa.

english_meaning: A pregnant woman or animal is going to have a baby.
english_example: The pregnant woman was shopping for baby clothes.
spanish_meaning: Mujer o animal que espera un bebé.
spanish_example: La mujer embarazada compraba ropa para el bebé.

english_meaning: Rage is a very angry feeling.
english_example: The chef was filled with rage when his helpers ruined the meal.
spanish_meaning: Sentimiento de enojo intenso.
spanish_example: El chef estaba lleno de furia cuando sus ayudantes arruinaron la comida.

english_meaning: Revenge is the act of hurting someone who has hurt you.
english_example: He broke his sister’s doll as revenge after she lost his favorite book.
spanish_meaning: Acción de hacer daño a quien te lastimó.
spanish_example: Le rompió la muñeca a su hermana por venganza.

english_meaning: Shame is a bad feeling about things one has done wrong.
english_example: The boy felt shame about misplacing his clothes.
spanish_meaning: Mal sentimiento por algo hecho mal.
spanish_example: El niño sintió vergüenza por perder su ropa.

english_meaning: To sigh is to breathe out loudly to show tiredness, boredom, or sadness.
english_example: Molly sighed when she looked at all the information she had to research.
spanish_meaning: Expirar fuerte para mostrar cansancio, tedio o tristeza.
spanish_example: Molly suspiró al ver toda la información que debía investigar.

english_meaning: To sneak is to move quietly in order not to be seen or heard.
english_example: The thief snuck out of the house without anyone noticing him.
spanish_meaning: Entrar o moverse en silencio para no ser visto o oído.
spanish_example: El ladrón se escabulló de la casa sin que nadie lo notara.

english_meaning: To spare something is to give it away because it is not needed.
english_example: I wanted to help him but I couldn’t spare a tire.
spanish_meaning: Ceder algo que no se necesita.
spanish_example: Quería ayudarlo, pero no pude regalarle una llanta.

english_meaning: The stem of a plant is the stick that grows leaves or flowers.
english_example: The rose had a long thin stem.
spanish_meaning: Parte de una planta que sostiene hojas o flores.
spanish_example: La rosa tenía un tallo largo y delgado.

english_meaning: Supper is a meal that is eaten in the evening.
english_example: We usually have supper around 6 o’clock at my house.
spanish_meaning: Comida que se toma por la tarde-noche.
spanish_example: Solemos cenar alrededor de las seis.

english_meaning: A tender food is soft and easy to chew.
english_example: The meat was so tender they didn’t need knives to cut it.
spanish_meaning: Suave y fácil de masticar.
spanish_example: La carne estaba tan tierna que no necesitaban cuchillos para cortarla.

english_meaning: Beneath means under or lower than.
english_example: The largest part of an iceberg lies beneath the waterline.
spanish_meaning: En posición inferior o bajo algo.
spanish_example: La parte más grande del iceberg está debajo de la línea del agua.

english_meaning: A cub is a baby animal, such as a bear or lion.
english_example: The lion cub was crying for its mother.
spanish_meaning: Joven de un animal como un león o un oso.
spanish_example: El cachorro de león lloraba por su madre.

english_meaning: Dawn is the time of day when the sun rises.
english_example: At dawn, the sun gently rose over the farm.
spanish_meaning: Hora en que sale el sol.
spanish_example: Al amanecer, el sol apareció suavemente sobre la granja.

english_meaning: Dissatisfied means not happy with something.
english_example: I was dissatisfied with their decision to work on Sunday.
spanish_meaning: No estar contento con algo.
spanish_example: Estoy insatisfecho con su decisión de trabajar el domingo.

english_meaning: Ease is a condition without difficulty or hard work.
english_example: The monkey climbed the tree with ease.
spanish_meaning: Estado sin dificultad o esfuerzo.
spanish_example: El mono subió al árbol con facilidad.

english_meaning: Evident means easy to see or understand.
english_example: It was evident from the look on his face that he was unhappy.
spanish_meaning: Fácil de ver o comprender.
spanish_example: Era evidente por su expresión que estaba descontento.

english_meaning: Hail is ice that falls from the sky when rain freezes.
english_example: The hail from the storm was the size of golf balls.
spanish_meaning: Hielo que cae del cielo cuando la lluvia se congela.
spanish_example: El granizo de la tormenta tenía tamaño de bolas de golf.

english_meaning: To howl means to make a long, loud sound like a wolf or a dog.
english_example: The wolf howled at the moon.
spanish_meaning: Lanzar un sonido largo y fuerte como un lobo.
spanish_example: El lobo aulló a la luna.

english_meaning: To leap means to jump a long distance.
english_example: He had to leap over the gap to reach the other side of the hill.
spanish_meaning: Dar un salto largo.
spanish_example: Tuvo que saltar el hueco para llegar al otro lado.

english_meaning: Magnificent means beautiful and grand.
english_example: The man gave his wife a pair of magnificent diamond earrings.
spanish_meaning: Muy bello o grandioso.
spanish_example: Le dio a su esposa unos aretes de diamantes magníficos.

english_meaning: A necessity is something that is needed.
english_example: Fresh water is a necessity for life.
spanish_meaning: Cosa necesaria o imprescindible.
spanish_example: El agua dulce es una necesidad para la vida.

english_meaning: An outcome is the end result of an action or event.
english_example: The outcome of his latest business plan was a complete failure.
spanish_meaning: Conclusión o fin de una acción o evento.
spanish_example: El resultado de su último plan de negocios fue un fracaso total.

english_meaning: A pile is a large group of things on top of one another.
english_example: The pile of cups was beginning to lean.
spanish_meaning: Acopio grande de cosas una sobre otra.
spanish_example: La pila de tazas empezaba a inclinarse.

english_meaning: Profound means deep or very intelligent.
english_example: For a young man, Jeremy has some profound thoughts.
spanish_meaning: Muy intenso o reflexivo.
spanish_example: Para un joven, Jeremy tiene pensamientos profundos.

english_meaning: To seize something means to grab it quickly or strongly.
english_example: The man seized as much money as he could before anyone could see him.
spanish_meaning: Tomar algo con fuerza y rapidez.
spanish_example: El hombre agarró tanto dinero como pudo antes de que alguien lo viera.

english_meaning: To squeeze something means to press it together and hold it tightly.
english_example: When Clara saw her cat, she squeezed it to her face.
spanish_meaning: Presionar algo fuertemente hasta compactarlo.
spanish_example: Cuando Clara vio a su gato, lo apretó contra su cara.

english_meaning: Supreme means of the highest level or best quality.
english_example: The supreme officer was in charge of keeping the citizens calm.
spanish_meaning: De nivel o calidad más alta.
spanish_example: El oficial supremo estaba a cargo de calmar a los ciudadanos.

english_meaning: Something terrific is very good.
english_example: My youngest daughter is a terrific painter.
spanish_meaning: Muy bueno o excelente.
spanish_example: Mi hija menor es una pintora estupenda.

english_meaning: A trait is part of someone’s personality.
english_example: One trait of Salvador’s personality is his cheerfulness.
spanish_meaning: Característica de la personalidad.
spanish_example: Un rasgo de la personalidad de Salvador es su alegría.

english_meaning: Something vital is necessary for life.
english_example: The heart is a vital organ.
spanish_meaning: Esencial para la vida.
spanish_example: El corazón es un órgano vital.

english_meaning: To attack something is to hurt or damage it.
english_example: She watched the lizard attack its prey.
spanish_meaning: Hacer daño o dañar algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Ella vio al lagarto atacar a su presa.

english_meaning: A breast is one of the two soft parts on a woman’s chest.
english_example: She was diagnosed with breast cancer.
spanish_meaning: Parte blanda del torso de la mujer; también glándula mamaria.
spanish_example: Fue diagnosticada con cáncer de pecho.

english_meaning: Cancer is a serious disease that causes cells to grow abnormally.
english_example: He was a leading scientist in finding a cure for cancer.
spanish_meaning: Enfermedad grave por crecimiento celular anormal.
spanish_example: Era un científico líder buscando una cura para el cáncer.

english_meaning: Cancerous cells are the result of cancer.
english_example: The exam found cancerous cells in the patient’s blood.
spanish_meaning: Que está relacionado con el cáncer o lo contiene.
spanish_example: El examen halló células cancerígenas en la sangre del paciente.

english_meaning: A cell is the smallest part of a living thing that can live by itself.
english_example: A red blood cell carries oxygen.
spanish_meaning: Unidad mínima de los seres vivos.
spanish_example: Un glóbulo rojo es una célula que lleva oxígeno.

english_meaning: A cigarette is a thinly wrapped paper tube filled with tobacco that is smoked.
english_example: Cigarette smoking is terrible for your health.
spanish_meaning: Tubo de papel fino lleno de tabaco que se fuma.
spanish_example: Fumar cigarrillos es terrible para la salud.

english_meaning: A code is a set of symbols used to hide or read a message.
english_example: She used the code to solve the puzzle.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de símbolos para ocultar o leer un mensaje.
spanish_example: Ella usó el código para resolver el rompecabezas.

english_meaning: A cure is a medical treatment to make a sickness go away.
english_example: Scientists are still working on finding a cure for diabetes.
spanish_meaning: Tratamiento médico para eliminar una enfermedad.
spanish_example: Los científicos buscan una cura para la diabetes.

english_meaning: To damage something is to cause its destruction.
english_example: The wildfires left the forest in a state of destruction.
spanish_meaning: Estado de algo dañado o arruinado.
spanish_example: Los incendios dejaron el bosque en ruinas.

english_meaning: DNA is the short form of deoxyribonucleic acid.
english_example: The police sometimes use DNA to solve crime.
spanish_meaning: Sigla de ácido desoxirribonucleico.
spanish_example: La policía a veces usa ADN para resolver crímenes.

english_meaning: Extensive means large in size or amount.
english_example: The game drew extensive media coverage.
spanish_meaning: Grande o amplio en tamaño o cantidad.
spanish_example: El juego obtuvo una cobertura mediática extensa.

english_meaning: Female refers to women or girls.
english_example: She is the only female student in her class with short hair.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con mujeres o niñas.
spanish_example: Es la única mujer de su clase con el pelo corto.

english_meaning: Furthermore means more information will be added.
english_example: She’s clever, and furthermore, she is not afraid of work.
spanish_meaning: Además, se añade más información.
spanish_example: Es inteligente y, además, no le teme al trabajo.

english_meaning: A gene controls what it looks like, how an organism grows, and how it develops.
english_example: Gene therapy is sometimes used to treat an illness.
spanish_meaning: Parte de la información genética que determina rasgos.
spanish_example: La terapia genética a veces se usa para tratar una enfermedad.

english_meaning: To inherit is to receive something that is passed down from a relative.
english_example: All the girls in my family inherit red hair from my great-grandmother.
spanish_meaning: Recibir algo que se recibe de un familiar.
spanish_example: Todas las chicas de mi familia heredaron el pelo rojo de mi bisabuela.

english_meaning: A link is a connection to something else.
english_example: The two lines in the poem share a common link.
spanish_meaning: Conexión con otra cosa.
spanish_example: Los dos versos del poema comparten un mismo enlace.

english_meaning: Male refers to men or boys.
english_example: He was the only male in the store.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con hombres o niños.
spanish_example: Era el único masculino en la tienda.

english_meaning: A population is all the people living in an area.
english_example: The population of our city is steadily growing.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de personas que viven en un lugar.
spanish_example: La población de nuestra ciudad crece de forma constante.

english_meaning: A result happens because of something else.
english_example: The result of her experiment was surprising.
spanish_meaning: Algo que ocurre como consecuencia de otra cosa.
spanish_example: El resultado de su experimento fue sorprendente.

english_meaning: Sugar comes from plants and is used to make food taste sweet.
english_example: She added extra sugar to her tea.
spanish_meaning: Sustancia dulce usada para dar sabor a los alimentos.
spanish_example: Añadió más azúcar a su té.

english_meaning: To accommodate is to have enough room.
english_example: The meeting room can accommodate nine people.
spanish_meaning: Tener espacio suficiente para algo o alguien.
spanish_example: La sala de reuniones puede acomodar a nueve personas.

english_meaning: A circus is a traveling show with animals and people.
english_example: I like to go to the circus to see the animals do tricks.
spanish_meaning: Espectáculo ambulante con animales y artistas.
spanish_example: Me gusta ir al circo a ver cómo hacen trucos los animales.

english_meaning: To coincide means to happen at the same time.
english_example: My birthday coincides with Christmas.
spanish_meaning: Suceder al mismo tiempo.
spanish_example: Mi cumpleaños coincide con Navidad.

english_meaning: To commission someone is to pay that person to do some job.
english_example: The artist was commissioned to create a picture.
spanish_meaning: Pagar a alguien para que haga un trabajo.
spanish_example: Al artista se le encomendó crear una pintura.

english_meaning: A dose is a certain amount of medicine taken at one time.
english_example: My mother gave me a dose of medicine before I went to bed.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad de medicina tomada de una vez.
spanish_example: Mi madre me dio una dosis de medicina antes de acostarme.

english_meaning: To dye something is to make it a certain color by using a special chemical.
english_example: Valery got her hair dyed at the salon yesterday.
spanish_meaning: Dar color a algo mediante un químico.
spanish_example: Ayer Valery se tiñó el pelo en el salón.

english_meaning: The extent of something is how large, important, or serious it is.
english_example: He ate to such an extent that he became overweight.
spanish_meaning: Qué tan grande, importante o serio es algo.
spanish_example: Comió hasta tal extensión que engordó.

english_meaning: Gender is a category that describes being either a boy or a girl.
english_example: Do you know the gender of her new baby?
spanish_meaning: Categoría que describe si alguien es niño o niña.
spanish_example: ¿Sabes el género de su nuevo bebé?

english_meaning: A headline is the title of a newspaper story.
english_example: The headline on the front page was about the economy.
spanish_meaning: Título principal de una noticia.
spanish_example: El titular de portada era sobre la economía.

english_meaning: An informal thing is casual and relaxed, not official.
english_example: They had an informal meeting to talk about their experiences.
spanish_meaning: Casual y relajado, no oficial.
spanish_example: Tuvieron una reunión informal para hablar de sus experiencias.

english_meaning: To inquire about something is to ask about it.
english_example: Dad called to inquire about the price of tickets for the show.
spanish_meaning: Solicitar información sobre algo.
spanish_example: Papá llamó para preguntar el precio de las entradas del espectáculo.

english_meaning: A messenger is one who carries information from one place to another.
english_example: The messenger delivered an important document to the office.
spanish_meaning: Persona que lleva información de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: El mensajero llevó un documento importante a la oficina.

english_meaning: To peer at something is to watch it carefully.
english_example: She peered at people through the window.
spanish_meaning: Observar algo con atención.
spanish_example: Ella miró atentamente a la gente por la ventana.

english_meaning: A portrait is a painting or photograph of someone.
english_example: I saw many religious portraits when I went to the museum.
spanish_meaning: Pintura o foto de una persona.
spanish_example: Vi muchos retratos religiosos en el museo.

english_meaning: To pose is to stay in one place without moving.
english_example: The kids and their dog posed for a picture.
spanish_meaning: Permanecer en una posición sin moverse.
spanish_example: Los niños y su perro posaron para una foto.

english_meaning: A ranch is a large farm where animals are kept.
english_example: My uncle has many horses on his ranch.
spanish_meaning: Granja grande donde se crían animales.
spanish_example: Mi tío tiene muchos caballos en su rancho.

english_meaning: To steer something is to control where it goes.
english_example: He steered the go-cart around the track.
spanish_meaning: Controlar hacia dónde va algo.
spanish_example: Condujo el carrito alrededor de la pista.

english_meaning: A stripe is a thick line.
english_example: The flag of the United States has red and white stripes.
spanish_meaning: Línea ancha de color o tono.
spanish_example: La bandera de Estados Unidos tiene franjas rojas y blancas.

english_meaning: A tame animal is not afraid to be near people.
english_example: The tame bird rested in his hand.
spanish_meaning: Animal al que no le da miedo estar cerca de personas.
spanish_example: El ave mansa descansaba en su mano.

english_meaning: To tempt people is to offer them something they want but shouldn’t have.
english_example: I wasn’t hungry, but she tempted me with a piece of my favorite cake.
spanish_meaning: Ofrecer algo atractivo que alguien no debería tener.
spanish_example: No tenía hambre, pero me tentó con un trozo de mi pastel favorito.

english_meaning: To ban something is to not let people do it.
english_example: Smoking is banned in this building.
spanish_meaning: Impedir que la gente haga algo.
spanish_example: Está prohibido fumar en este edificio.

english_meaning: A cautious person is careful to avoid danger.
english_example: Be very cautious as you stack those boxes.
spanish_meaning: Cuidado para evitar peligros.
spanish_example: Sé muy cauteloso al apilar esas cajas.

english_meaning: To confess is to admit a bad or embarrassing truth.
english_example: The woman confessed that she had stolen the money.
spanish_meaning: Reconocer una verdad mala o embarazosa.
spanish_example: La mujer confesó que había robado el dinero.

english_meaning: A cottage is a small, old house in the countryside.
english_example: My aunt lives in a pretty cottage in the mountains.
spanish_meaning: Casa pequeña y vieja del campo.
spanish_example: Mi tía vive en una linda cabaña en las montañas.

english_meaning: Daytime is the time of the day when the sky is light.
english_example: The kids are only allowed to play outside in the daytime.
spanish_meaning: Horas del día con luz; tiempo de día.
spanish_example: Los niños solo pueden jugar afuera de día.

english_meaning: A desperate person will try anything to do or change something.
english_example: I’m desperate to find a new job.
spanish_meaning: Persona que intenta cualquier cosa para cambiar algo.
spanish_example: Estoy desesperado por encontrar un nuevo trabajo.

english_meaning: An exhausting activity is very tiring.
english_example: After an exhausting journey, we finally arrived.
spanish_meaning: Muy cansador o fatigante.
spanish_example: Después de un viaje agotador, al fin llegamos.

english_meaning: To fade is to become quieter or less bright.
english_example: The piece of cloth I found was old and faded.
spanish_meaning: Volverse más débil o menos brillante.
spanish_example: La tela que encontré era vieja y estaba desvaída.

english_meaning: A fierce person or animal is angry or violent.
english_example: Wolves are fierce animals. Do not disturb them.
spanish_meaning: Muy agresivo o violento.
spanish_example: Los lobos son animales feroces.

english_meaning: To gamble means to play a game that involves winning or losing money.
english_example: Many people like to go to casinos to gamble.
spanish_meaning: Jugar a juegos con dinero que se puede ganar o perder.
spanish_example: A mucha gente le gusta ir a casinos para apostar.

english_meaning: A lawn is an area covered in grass.
english_example: My dad keeps the lawn in front of our house very neat.
spanish_meaning: Superficie cubierta de pasto.
spanish_example: Mi papá mantiene el césped de la casa muy prolijo.

english_meaning: To mow grass is to cut it to make it short.
english_example: I mow our lawn every weekend.
spanish_meaning: Podar o segar el pasto para que quede corto.
spanish_example: Yo corto el césped cada fin de semana.

english_meaning: An outlaw is a criminal who hides from the police.
english_example: The police passed out posters of the outlaw to all the people.
spanish_meaning: Delincuente que se oculta de la policía.
spanish_example: La policía repartió carteles del forajido a todo el pueblo.

english_meaning: A prospect is a possibility that something will happen.
english_example: He wakes up every morning with the prospect of having a good day.
spanish_meaning: Posibilidad de que algo ocurra.
spanish_example: Despierta cada mañana con la posibilidad de tener un buen día.

english_meaning: A purse is a bag in which women keep money, makeup, keys, etc.
english_example: My sister likes to buy designer purses.
spanish_meaning: Bolsa pequeña donde se guardan dinero y objetos personales.
spanish_example: A mi hermana le gusta comprar bolsos de diseñador.

english_meaning: A rod is a thin stick made of wood or metal.
english_example: I bought a new fishing rod to use while on vacation.
spanish_meaning: Palito delgado de madera o metal, por ejemplo para pescar.
spanish_example: Compré una caña nueva para pescar en vacaciones.

english_meaning: Seldom shows that an action doesn’t happen very often.
english_example: It seldom rains in southern Arizona.
spanish_meaning: Que no ocurre a menudo.
spanish_example: Raramente llueve en el sur de Arizona.

english_meaning: To shave means to cut the hairs on one’s face with a sharp tool.
english_example: My father shaves his face every day because he doesn’t want a beard.
spanish_meaning: Cortar el vello de la cara con una herramienta.
spanish_example: Mi padre se afeita la barba todos los días para no tener barba.

english_meaning: A terrified person or animal is extremely scared.
english_example: When I saw the ghost, I was absolutely terrified!
spanish_meaning: Persona o animal con mucho miedo.
spanish_example: Cuando vi al fantasma, me aterrorizó por completo.

english_meaning: A wizard is a man who can do magic.
english_example: The wizard made gold fall from the sky.
spanish_meaning: Persona que puede hacer magia.
spanish_example: El mago hizo caer oro del cielo.

english_meaning: An action done abroad is done in a different country.
english_example: My friend is going on a summer trip abroad.
spanish_meaning: En otro país.
spanish_example: Mi amigo va de viaje al extranjero en verano.

english_meaning: An airline is a company that takes people to different places by plane.
english_example: The airline has excellent customer service.
spanish_meaning: Empresa que transporta personas por avión.
spanish_example: La aerolínea tiene excelente atención al cliente.

english_meaning: An audience is a group of people who gather to watch someone do something.
english_example: He was excited to be part of the audience.
spanish_meaning: Grupo de personas que observa un espectáculo o acto.
spanish_example: Estaba feliz de formar parte de la audiencia.

english_meaning: A bargain is a very good price paid for a product.
english_example: She was looking forward to getting a good bargain at the supermarket.
spanish_meaning: Precio muy bueno o trato económico.
spanish_example: Ella esperaba encontrar una ganga buena en el supermercado.

english_meaning: A brief action lasts a short time.
english_example: The lawyers took a brief break.
spanish_meaning: Que dura poco tiempo.
spanish_example: Los abogados tomaron un breve descanso.

english_meaning: The currency of a country is the type of money used in that country.
english_example: The currency in China is different from that in Australia.
spanish_meaning: Dinero oficial que se usa en un país.
spanish_example: La moneda en China es distinta a la de Australia.

english_meaning: Data is a collection of information and facts.
english_example: He saved all the data on his computer.
spanish_meaning: Información y hechos recopilados.
spanish_example: Guardó todos los datos en su computadora.

english_meaning: Domestic refers to something that happens within a particular country.
english_example: The airline has fifty domestic flights daily.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con lo que ocurre dentro de un país.
spanish_example: La aerolínea tiene cincuenta vuelos domésticos diarios.

english_meaning: A draft is a piece of written work that is not in its final form.
english_example: He was writing his first draft.
spanish_meaning: Texto escrito que aún no está en versión final.
spanish_example: Él estaba escribiendo su primer borrador.

english_meaning: To gather is to form a group or bring together.
english_example: We will gather the children before the meeting.
spanish_meaning: Formar un grupo o juntar cosas/ personas.
spanish_example: Reuniremos a los niños antes de la reunión.

english_meaning: A hobby is a fun and creative activity people do in their free time.
english_example: She liked to paint as a hobby.
spanish_meaning: Actividad recreativa y creativa en el tiempo libre.
spanish_example: Le gustaba pintar como pasatiempo.

english_meaning: An income is the money you earned from work.
english_example: She was saving her income to buy a house.
spanish_meaning: Dinero ganado por el trabajo.
spanish_example: Ahorraba su ingreso para comprar una casa.

english_meaning: A jet is a fast plane with a big engine.
english_example: Her company owns a private jet.
spanish_meaning: Avión a reacción muy rápido.
spanish_example: Su empresa tiene un jet privado.

english_meaning: Maximum is the highest amount of anything allowed.
english_example: The maximum driving speed here is 80 km/h.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad más alta permitida.
spanish_example: La velocidad máxima de manejo aquí es de 80 km/h.

english_meaning: An official thing is approved by someone in authority.
english_example: Her official degree was being transferred.
spanish_meaning: Aprobado o validado por una autoridad.
spanish_example: Su título oficial se estaba tramitando.

english_meaning: To recommend means to give advice based on experience.
english_example: His doctor will recommend the best medicine for her cough.
spanish_meaning: Dar un consejo basado en experiencia.
spanish_example: Su médico recomendará el mejor medicamento para la tos.

english_meaning: To refer to something means to mention or call attention to it.
english_example: Mr. Cid referred to the time to show Bill that he was late for class.
spanish_meaning: Mencionar o llamar la atención sobre algo.
spanish_example: El señor Cid se refirió a la hora para mostrarle a Bill que llegaba tarde.

english_meaning: A remote place is distant or far away.
english_example: He lived in a remote village in the Himalayas.
spanish_meaning: Lejos o distante de lugares cercanos.
spanish_example: Vivía en un pueblo remoto en el Himalaya.

english_meaning: Sleepless describes a time period in which someone does not sleep.
english_example: Jill had a sleepless night because she had so much on her mind.
spanish_meaning: Período en el que alguien no duerme.
spanish_example: Jill pasó una noche en vela porque tenía muchas preocupaciones.

english_meaning: The volume is the total amount of something.
english_example: The volume of cars in the parking lot was steadily increasing.
spanish_meaning: Cantidad total de algo.
spanish_example: El volumen de autos en el estacionamiento aumentaba constantemente.

english_meaning: To circulate is to spread something around, especially in a circular way.
english_example: The fan helped to circulate cool air through the room.
spanish_meaning: Hacer que algo se distribuya, especialmente en torno a un espacio.
spanish_example: El ventilador ayudó a circular aire fresco en la sala.

english_meaning: Consequent means happening because of a different situation.
english_example: Her consequent rash came after she touched the poisonous plant.
spanish_meaning: Que ocurre a causa de otra situación.
spanish_example: El sarpullido resultante apareció después de tocar la planta venenosa.

english_meaning: To derive means to come, or originate, from a thing or place.
english_example: Red’s nickname was derived from the color of her hair.
spanish_meaning: Proceder o venir de algo o de un lugar.
spanish_example: El apodo de Red se derivó del color de su cabello.

english_meaning: To drown is to die from not being able to breathe underwater.
english_example: He would have drowned if the sailors had not rescued him.
spanish_meaning: Morir por no poder respirar bajo el agua.
spanish_example: Se habría ahogado si los marineros no lo hubieran rescatado.

english_meaning: A dynasty is a series of rulers who are all from the same family.
english_example: The ancient Egyptians had a dynasty that lasted for many years.
spanish_meaning: Serie de gobernantes de una misma familia.
spanish_example: Los antiguos egipcios tuvieron una dinastía que duró muchos años.

english_meaning: A fraction is a small part of something.
english_example: Only a fraction of the cake was gone.
spanish_meaning: Parte pequeña de un todo.
spanish_example: Sólo se había ido una fracción del pastel.

english_meaning: Frost is a white layer of ice that forms during very cold weather.
english_example: In the morning, the trees were all covered with frost.
spanish_meaning: Capa blanca de hielo en tiempo muy frío.
spanish_example: Por la mañana, los árboles estaban cubiertos de escarcha.

english_meaning: An illusion is something that looks real, but doesn’t actually exist.
english_example: Some pictures create an illusion for the eyes.
spanish_meaning: Algo que parece real pero no existe realmente.
spanish_example: Algunas imágenes crean una ilusión para los ojos.

english_meaning: To invade is to take over a place by force.
english_example: The enemy forces tried to invade our country from the sky.
spanish_meaning: Tomar una zona por la fuerza.
spanish_example: Las fuerzas enemigas intentaron invadir nuestro país desde el cielo.

english_meaning: A lieutenant is a rank in the military or police, or a person with that rank.
english_example: The lieutenant was a good leader, and his soldiers respected him.
spanish_meaning: Rango militar o policial; persona con ese rango.
spanish_example: El teniente era un buen líder, y sus soldados lo respetaban.

english_meaning: Marine describes something related to the sea.
english_example: A healthy ocean is full of marine animals.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con el mar o la vida marina.
spanish_example: Un océano sano está lleno de animales marinos.

english_meaning: A merit is a positive or good quality.
english_example: The actor received an award for his merits in the movie.
spanish_meaning: Cualidad positiva o buena.
spanish_example: El actor recibió un premio por su mérito en la película.

english_meaning: A navy is the part of a country’s military that fights at sea.
english_example: My country is known for our strong navy.
spanish_meaning: Fuerza militar que combate por mar.
spanish_example: Mi país es conocido por su armada fuerte.

english_meaning: Polar relates to the cold places on Earth’s north and south ends.
english_example: Only a few people live in the Earth’s northern polar region.
spanish_meaning: Relacionado con las regiones frías del norte y sur de la Tierra.
spanish_example: Pocas personas viven en la región polar del norte.

english_meaning: A ray is a line of light that comes from a bright object.
english_example: The sun’s warm rays covered the beach.
spanish_meaning: Haz de luz que sale de un objeto brillante.
spanish_example: Los cálidos rayos del sol cubrieron la playa.

english_meaning: To resign means to quit a job.
english_example: After I officially resigned from work, I said goodbye to my boss.
spanish_meaning: Dejar voluntariamente un trabajo.
spanish_example: Después de renunciar oficialmente, me despedí de mi jefe.

english_meaning: Suicide is the act of killing oneself.
english_example: Some people feel so sad that they think suicide is the only answer.
spanish_meaning: Acto de quitarse la propia vida.
spanish_example: Algunas personas se sienten tan tristes que piensan que el suicidio es la única salida.

english_meaning: To tremble is to shake as a result of excitement or cold weather.
english_example: Harry was not used to the cold, so he trembled most of the day.
spanish_meaning: Sacudirse por emoción o frío.
spanish_example: A Harry no le gustaba el frío, así que tembló casi todo el día.

english_meaning: An underlying thing is a hidden cause or reason.
english_example: Her underlying fear of flying reduced her traveling options.
spanish_meaning: Causa o motivo oculto.
spanish_example: Su miedo subyacente a volar redujo sus opciones de viaje.

english_meaning: Via introduces a route or means of travel.
english_example: We arrived in the city from the airport via the train.
spanish_meaning: Por medio de; a través de.
spanish_example: Llegamos a la ciudad desde el aeropuerto vía tren.

english_meaning: To alter something means to change it.
english_example: I altered the color of my nails to match my hair.
spanish_meaning: Cambiar algo.
spanish_example: Cambié el color de mis uñas para que coincidiera con mi pelo.

english_meaning: An action done aside is done toward the side of something or someplace.
english_example: The man stood aside and opened the door for me.
spanish_meaning: Hacia el costado o apartado.
spanish_example: El hombre se quedó a un lado y abrió la puerta para mí.

english_meaning: Autumn is the season of the year between summer and winter.
english_example: I love when the leaves fall in autumn because I can play in them.
spanish_meaning: Estación entre verano e invierno.
spanish_example: Me encanta que caigan hojas en otoño porque puedo jugar con ellas.

english_meaning: To blend is to mix two or more things together so that they become one thing.
english_example: My wife blended together all of the ingredients to make a delicious stew.
spanish_meaning: Unir dos o más cosas para que sean una sola cosa.
spanish_example: Mi esposa mezcló todos los ingredientes para preparar un guiso delicioso.

english_meaning: To collapse is to fall down suddenly.
english_example: The tree collapsed right in front of our house.
spanish_meaning: Caer de repente y derrumbarse.
spanish_example: El árbol se colapsó justo frente a nuestra casa.

english_meaning: To crush something is to press it together so its shape is destroyed.
english_example: Selena’s new car was crushed when the tree fell on top of it.
spanish_meaning: Presionar algo hasta deformarlo o destruirlo.
spanish_example: El coche nuevo de Selena fue aplastado cuando el árbol cayó encima.

english_meaning: To curve is to move in a line that bends and does not go straight.
english_example: The road curves to the left and to the right.
spanish_meaning: Moverse en una línea que dobla y no es recta.
spanish_example: La carretera curvaba hacia la izquierda y la derecha.

english_meaning: A disgusting thing is very unpleasant.
english_example: After running all day, Greg’s feet had a disgusting odor.
spanish_meaning: Muy desagradable.
spanish_example: Después de correr todo el día, Greg tenía un olor repugnante en los pies.

english_meaning: A drain is a pipe that carries away water from a building, such as in a kitchen
english_example: .The water in the sink goes down the drain as you wash your hands.
spanish_meaning: Tubería que evacua agua de un edificio.
spanish_example: El agua del fregadero baja por el desagüe al lavarse las manos.

english_meaning: To embrace is to hug.
english_example: When they saw each other again, the happy couple embraced.
spanish_meaning: Dar un abrazo, tomar en brazos y abrazar.
spanish_example: Cuando se vieron de nuevo, la pareja feliz se abrazó.

english_meaning: To envy someone is to wish that you had something that other person has.
english_example: Sally envied the happy couple.
spanish_meaning: Deseo por algo que tiene otra persona.
spanish_example: Sally envidiaba a la pareja feliz.

english_meaning: Fireworks are objects that create colored lights when they are lit.
english_example: The display of fireworks was so beautiful.
spanish_meaning: Objetos que producen luces de colores al encenderse.
spanish_example: El espectáculo de fuegos artificiales fue precioso.

english_meaning: Flour is a powder made from plants that is used to make foods like bread.
english_example: I wanted to bake a pie, but I needed flour.
spanish_meaning: Polvo de plantas usado para hacer pan o repostería.
spanish_example: Quería hornear una tarta, pero necesitaba harina.

english_meaning: A fuse is a string on fireworks that burns to make them explode.
english_example: The boy lit the fuse on the rocket and waited for it to burst in the sky.
spanish_meaning: Cuerda de fuego que arde para provocar la explosión.
spanish_example: El niño encendió la mecha del cohete y esperó a que explotara.

english_meaning: Ginger is a root of a plant that is used to make food spicy and sweet.
english_example: Ginger is a common ingredient in many dishes from India.
spanish_meaning: Raíz usada para dar sabor dulce y picante a la comida.
spanish_example: El jengibre es un ingrediente común en muchos platos de la India.

english_meaning: A jealous person might take something from you.
english_example: Miriam was jealous because Sue was paying too much attention to Jim.
spanish_meaning: Persona que desea lo que otro posee.
spanish_example: Miriam estaba celosa porque Sue prestaba mucha atención a Jim.

english_meaning: A paste is a thick and smooth substance.
english_example: My son needed some paste for a school project.
spanish_meaning: Sustancia espesa y suave.
spanish_example: Mi hijo necesitó pasta para un proyecto escolar.

english_meaning: A receipt is a paper that proves that something was received or bought.
english_example: After looking at my receipt, I realized that I had spent too much money.
spanish_meaning: Documento que prueba una compra o recepción.
spanish_example: Al ver mi recibo, me di cuenta de que había gastado demasiado dinero.

english_meaning: To wipe something is to slide a piece of cloth over it to clean it.
english_example: She wiped the dust from the windows.
spanish_meaning: Frotar con un paño para quitar suciedad.
spanish_example: Ella limpió el polvo de las ventanas.

english_meaning: A wire is a thin string made out of metal.
english_example: The wires were connected to towers that brought electricity to the city.
spanish_meaning: Hilo delgado y flexible de metal.
spanish_example: Los cables estaban conectados a torres que traían electricidad a la ciudad.

english_meaning: To acknowledge something is to accept that it is true or that it exists.
english_example: The teacher acknowledged that the young student was hungry.
spanish_meaning: Aceptar que algo es cierto o existe.
spanish_example: La maestra reconoció que la estudiante joven tenía hambre.

english_meaning: An ambassador is a government worker who works in another country.
english_example: The ambassador from Korea was in charge of the conference.
spanish_meaning: Funcionario que representa a un gobierno en otro país.
spanish_example: El embajador de Corea dirigía la conferencia.

english_meaning: A blonde is a person with light-colored hair.
english_example: My cousin is a blonde with blue eyes.
spanish_meaning: Persona de cabello claro.
spanish_example: Mi prima es rubia y tiene ojos azules.

english_meaning: To conquer a place means to attack and take control of it.
english_example: The soldiers were trying to conquer the world.
spanish_meaning: Atacar y tomar control de un lugar.
spanish_example: Los soldados intentaban conquistar el mundo.

english_meaning: To drag something means to pull it across the ground.
english_example: The dog was dragging his owner down the street.
spanish_meaning: Tirar algo sobre el suelo.
spanish_example: El perro arrastraba a su dueño por la calle.

english_meaning: To exaggerate is to say that something is bigger or better than it really is.
english_example: Jimmy wasn’t exaggerating about the seriousness of his injury.
spanish_meaning: Decir algo más grande o mejor de lo real.
spanish_example: Jimmy no exageraba sobre la gravedad de su lesión.

english_meaning: Heritage is the collection of features of a society, such as language and religion.
english_example: Teepees are part of the heritage of the Native Americans of the plains.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto de rasgos culturales de una sociedad.
spanish_example: Las tipis forman parte del patrimonio de los nativos de las llanuras.

english_meaning: To insult someone is to say things that will hurt the person’s feelings.
english_example: The girls insulted each other all afternoon.
spanish_meaning: Decir cosas para herir sentimientos.
spanish_example: Las niñas se insultaban toda la tarde.

english_meaning: An action that happens meanwhile happens at the same time as another action.
english_example: He wants to be a doctor in the future, but meanwhile, he works a regular job.
spanish_meaning: Al mismo tiempo que otra acción ocurre.
spanish_example: Quiere ser médico en el futuro, pero mientras tanto trabaja en un empleo común.

english_meaning: A necklace is a piece of jewelry that people wear around their necks.
english_example: Joyce received a lovely pearl necklace for her wedding anniversary.
spanish_meaning: Un collar es una pieza de joyería que se lleva alrededor del cuello.
spanish_example: Joyce recibió un hermoso collar de perlas por su aniversario de bodas.

english_meaning: A noble is a rich and powerful person.
english_example: The Queen invited a noble from a nearby country to dinner.
spanish_meaning: Un noble es una persona rica y poderosa.
spanish_example: La reina invitó a un noble de un país cercano a cenar.

english_meaning: A precious thing is valuable and important.
english_example: In a desert, water can be more precious than money.
spanish_meaning: Algo precioso es valioso e importante.
spanish_example: En un desierto, el agua puede ser más preciosa que el dinero.

english_meaning: A prejudice is an unfair opinion about people based on the group they belong to.
english_example: The company’s rules against gender prejudice must be enforced.
spanish_meaning: Un prejuicio es una opinión injusta sobre personas basada en el grupo al que pertenecen.
spanish_example: Las reglas contra el prejuicio de género de la empresa deben aplicarse.

english_meaning: A rumor is a story that may not be true.
english_example: Carla was spreading rumors around the office.
spanish_meaning: Un rumor es una historia que puede no ser cierta.
spanish_example: Carla estaba difundiendo rumores en la oficina.

english_meaning: A sin is something that is wrong for religious reasons.
english_example: Taking something that doesn’t belong to you is a sin.
spanish_meaning: Un pecado es algo que se considera incorrecto por razones religiosas.
spanish_example: Tomar algo que no te pertenece es un pecado.

english_meaning: A spectacle is an amazing sight.
english_example: Niagara Falls is quite a spectacle.
spanish_meaning: Un espectáculo es algo increíble de contemplar.
spanish_example: Las cataratas del Niágara son un espectáculo impresionante.

english_meaning: A stack is a pile of different things.
english_example: There was a stack of paperwork on his desk to complete.
spanish_meaning: Una pila es una cantidad de cosas una encima de otra.
spanish_example: Había una pila de papeles en su escritorio por completar.

english_meaning: A suspicious person does not trust others, or is not trusted by others.
english_example: Dad was suspicious of the caller on the line.
spanish_meaning: Una persona sospechosa no confía en otros, o no se la considera confiable.
spanish_example: Papá desconfiaba de la persona que llamaba.

english_meaning: Tin is an inexpensive metal.
english_example: Soup is a food that is often sold in tin cans.
spanish_meaning: El estaño es un metal económico.
spanish_example: La sopa suele venderse en latas de estaño.

english_meaning: A vase is an attractive container in which people keep flowers.
english_example: The vase was filled with such lovely flowers.
spanish_meaning: Un jarrón es un recipiente bonito para poner flores.
spanish_example: El jarrón estaba lleno de flores hermosas.

english_meaning: To ache is to hurt, or cause pain.
english_example: Mindy’s head ached because she bumped it against the wall.
spanish_meaning: Doler significa sentir o causar dolor.
spanish_example: La cabeza de Mindy le dolía porque se golpeó contra la pared.

english_meaning: Arctic shows that something is of or from the cold, far-north part of the Earth.
english_example: It is cold when the arctic winds blow.
spanish_meaning: Lo ártico se refiere a algo del frío extremo del norte de la Tierra.
spanish_example: Hace frío cuando soplan los vientos árticos.

english_meaning: A canal is a path for water to travel through.
english_example: The city of Venice, Italy, has many canals.
spanish_meaning: Un canal es un camino por el que viaja el agua.
spanish_example: Venecia tiene muchos canales.

english_meaning: A chemist is a scientist who works with chemicals.
english_example: My mother is a successful chemist who helps invent new products.
spanish_meaning: Un químico es un científico que trabaja con químicos.
spanish_example: Mi madre es una química que ayuda a inventar nuevos productos.

english_meaning: A chill is a feeling of cold.
english_example: The chill from the cold wind made Bill shiver.
spanish_meaning: El frío intenso se siente como escalofríos.
spanish_example: El frío del viento helado hizo que Bill temblara.

english_meaning: Congress is a group of leaders in a government.
english_example: Congress makes laws for people to follow.
spanish_meaning: Un congreso es un grupo de líderes de un gobierno.
spanish_example: El congreso crea leyes que la gente debe seguir.

english_meaning: Dairy shows that something is made from milk.
english_example: Things like milk and cheese are dairy foods.
spanish_meaning: Lácteo significa que algo está hecho de leche.
spanish_example: Leche y queso son alimentos lácteos.

english_meaning: To descend is to go downward.
english_example: I descended the stairs to get out of the building.
spanish_meaning: Descender es ir hacia abajo.
spanish_example: Bajé las escaleras para salir del edificio.

english_meaning: A grocer is a person who sells food.
english_example: Our grocer, Mr. Smith, is a very kind man.
spanish_meaning: Un tendero es una persona que vende comida.
spanish_example: Nuestro tendero, el Sr. Smith, es muy amable.

english_meaning: To hesitate is to wait for a short time before doing something.
english_example: He hesitated for a moment before he decided which one to choose.
spanish_meaning: Dudar es esperar un momento antes de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Él dudó un momento antes de decidir qué elegir.

english_meaning: An institution is an organization that works to help a city or group of people.
english_example: Banks are vital institutions that businesses and people use every day.
spanish_meaning: Una institución es una organización que ayuda a una ciudad o a un grupo de personas.
spanish_example: Los bancos son instituciones que la gente y las empresas usan cada día.

english_meaning: To jog is to run slowly.
english_example: He jogs every day so he can be healthier.
spanish_meaning: Trotar es correr despacio.
spanish_example: Él trota todos los días para estar más sano.

english_meaning: A merchant is a person who sells things.
english_example: The merchant was selling used cars.
spanish_meaning: Un comerciante es una persona que vende cosas.
spanish_example: El comerciante estaba vendiendo autos usados.

english_meaning: To poke something is to push something quickly with your finger or a pointed object.
english_example: I poked my finger on a thumb tack.
spanish_meaning: Pinchar algo es empujarlo rápido con el dedo o un objeto puntiagudo.
spanish_example: Me pinché el dedo con una chincheta.

english_meaning: To postpone something is to make it happen later than planned.
english_example: They postponed the meeting until Elena arrived.
spanish_meaning: Posponer algo es hacerlo más tarde de lo previsto.
spanish_example: Pospusieron la reunión hasta que llegó Elena.

english_meaning: To splash is to crash into something so that liquid spreads out.
english_example: Some green paint splashed upon the floor.
spanish_meaning: Salpicar es chocar y hacer que el líquido se extienda.
spanish_example: La pintura verde se salpicó por el suelo.

english_meaning: Stubborn people don’t change their minds easily.
english_example: Both of my parents are stubborn. They never give in to each other.
spanish_meaning: Una persona terco no cambia de opinión fácilmente.
spanish_example: Ambos de mis padres son tercos; nunca ceden.

english_meaning: A suburb is a small part of a large city.
english_example: I grew up in the suburbs, only a few minutes outside of the city.
spanish_meaning: Un suburbio es una zona de una ciudad grande.
spanish_example: Crecí en los suburbios, a pocos minutos de la ciudad.

english_meaning: The tide is the level of the water in the sea.
english_example: The tide continued to crawl upon the beach little by little.
spanish_meaning: La marea es el nivel del agua en el mar.
spanish_example: La marea seguía subiendo en la playa poco a poco.

english_meaning: A tragedy is a very sad event.
english_example: It was a tragedy when his house was struck by lightning and burned.
spanish_meaning: Una tragedia es un suceso muy triste.
spanish_example: Fue una tragedia cuando su casa fue alcanzada por un rayo y se quemó.

english_meaning: A bomb is an object that explodes and destroys large areas.
english_example: The bomb will destroy anything that is nearby.
spanish_meaning: Una bomba es un objeto que explota y destruye áreas grandes.
spanish_example: La bomba destruirá cualquier cosa cercana.

english_meaning: A certificate is a document that says that something is true or happened.
english_example: I was given a certificate for completing the computer course.
spanish_meaning: Un certificado es un documento que dice que algo es verdad o ocurrió.
spanish_example: Recibí un certificado por completar el curso de informática.

english_meaning: A circumstance is an event that makes a situation what it is.
english_example: There were many circumstances behind their success, like hard work.
spanish_meaning: Una circunstancia es un hecho o situación que influye en algo.
spanish_example: Hubo muchas circunstancias detrás de su éxito, como el trabajo duro.

english_meaning: A coffin is a box used to bury dead people.
english_example: When people pass away, they are usually buried inside of a coffin.
spanish_meaning: Un ataúd es una caja en la que se entierran personas muertas.
spanish_example: Cuando alguien fallece, suele ser enterrado en un ataúd.

english_meaning: To cope with a difficult or stressful situation means to deal with it.
english_example: He copes with work stress by exercising three or four times a week.
spanish_meaning: Lidiar con algo difícil significa enfrentarlo y sobrellevarlo.
spanish_example: Él afronta el estrés del trabajo haciendo ejercicio varias veces por semana.

english_meaning: Criticism is the act of saying that something is not correct or good.
english_example: She had a lot of criticism about their new plan.
spanish_meaning: La crítica es decir que algo no está bien o no es correcto.
spanish_example: Ella recibió mucha crítica sobre su nuevo plan.

english_meaning: To devastate something is to cause great damage or pain to that thing.
english_example: The entire wall was devastated.
spanish_meaning: Devastar algo significa causar gran daño.
spanish_example: El muro entero quedó devastado.

english_meaning: To frown is to make an unhappy look with one’s face.
english_example: Melissa frowned when she found out that the party had been cancelled.
spanish_meaning: Fruncir el ceño significa poner una expresión de disgusto en el rostro.
spanish_example: Melissa frunció el ceño cuando supo que la fiesta se canceló.

english_meaning: To gaze at something means to look at it for a long time.
english_example: We used a telescope to gaze at the stars for over an hour.
spanish_meaning: Contemplar algo es mirarlo durante bastante tiempo.
spanish_example: Usamos un telescopio para contemplar las estrellas durante una hora.

english_meaning: To glance at something means to look at it quickly.
english_example: She glanced behind her to see if he was looking at her.
spanish_meaning: Mirar de un vistazo es hacerlo rápido y de forma breve.
spanish_example: Ella miró detrás de sí para ver si él la miraba.

english_meaning: Grief is the feeling of deep sadness, usually when a person dies.
english_example: The grief caused by losing her parents was very difficult for her.
spanish_meaning: La pena es una sensación profunda de tristeza.
spanish_example: La pena causada por la muerte de sus padres le resultó muy dura.

english_meaning: A groom is a man who is going to be married.
english_example: The groom looked happy as he walked with his new wife.
spanish_meaning: Un novio es el hombre que va a casarse.
spanish_example: El novio parecía feliz mientras caminaba con su nueva esposa.

english_meaning: A license is an official document that gives one permission to do something.
english_example: In the United States, you need a driver’s license to drive legally.
spanish_meaning: Una licencia es un documento oficial que da permiso para hacer algo.
spanish_example: En Estados Unidos necesitas una licencia de conducir para manejar legalmente.

english_meaning: A microscope is a device that makes small objects look bigger.
english_example: Germs cannot be seen without a microscope.
spanish_meaning: Un microscopio es un aparato que agranda objetos pequeños.
spanish_example: Los gérmenes no se ven sin un microscopio.

english_meaning: A nuclear thing relates to the division or joining of atoms.
english_example: Nuclear power plants provide inexpensive energy to cities.
spanish_meaning: Nuclear se relaciona con la división o unión de átomos.
spanish_example: Las plantas nucleares proporcionan energía económica a las ciudades.

english_meaning: To portray something means to describe it or show it in a picture.
english_example: Her picture portrayed the house she grew up in when she was little.
spanish_meaning: Retratar algo significa describirlo o mostrarlo en una imagen.
spanish_example: Su retrato mostró la casa en la que creció de niña.

english_meaning: To rotate something means to turn it around in a circle.
english_example: An airplane’s propellers rotate quickly to help it fly.
spanish_meaning: Rotar algo significa hacerlo girar en círculo.
spanish_example: Las hélices de un avión rotan rápido para que pueda volar.

english_meaning: A souvenir is something bought to remember of a place or event.
english_example: I bought a Russian doll as a souvenir on my trip to Moscow.
spanish_meaning: Un recuerdo es algo comprado para recordar un lugar o evento.
spanish_example: Compré una muñeca rusa como recuerdo en mi viaje a Moscú.

english_meaning: A submarine is a boat that can go underwater for long periods of time.
english_example: The submarine dove under the sea so the enemy couldn’t see it.
spanish_meaning: Un submarino es un barco que puede estar bajo el agua mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: El submarino se sumergió para que el enemigo no lo viera.

english_meaning: To trace something means to follow over it with the eyes or a finger.
english_example: He traced over the graph with his finger.
spanish_meaning: Trazar algo significa seguir su forma con la vista o el dedo.
spanish_example: Trazó el gráfico con su dedo.

english_meaning: A coastline is the outline of a country’s coast.
english_example: He noticed that most of the cities in Australia are on the coastline.
spanish_meaning: La línea de costa es el contorno de una costa.
spanish_example: Notó que la mayoría de las ciudades de Australia están en la costa.

english_meaning: To deter means to prevent or discourage someone from doing something.
english_example: Icy roads deter people from driving their cars.
spanish_meaning: Disuadir significa prevenir o desalentar a alguien de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Las carreteras heladas disuaden a la gente de conducir.

english_meaning: To devise something means to come up with an idea or plan about it.
english_example: The thieves devised a plan to steal the diamonds.
spanish_meaning: Idear algo significa inventar una idea o plan.
spanish_example: Los ladrones idearon un plan para robar los diamantes.

english_meaning: A distance is how far it is between two points.
english_example: The distance between America and Russia is about 8,000 km.
spanish_meaning: La distancia es lo lejos que hay entre dos puntos.
spanish_example: La distancia entre América y Rusia es de unos 8.000 km.

english_meaning: Expertise is the knowledge and skills to do something well.
english_example: John has a lot of advertising expertise. He can sell anything!
spanish_meaning: La experiencia es el conocimiento y las habilidades para hacer algo bien.
spanish_example: Juan tiene mucha experiencia en publicidad. Puede vender cualquier cosa.

english_meaning: A fracture is a crack or break in something.
english_example: Don’t stand on that leg because there is a fracture. It might get worse.
spanish_meaning: Una fractura es una grieta o rotura en algo.
spanish_example: No te apoyes en esa pierna: tiene una fractura. Puede empeorar.

english_meaning: A headache is a pain in one’s head.
english_example: My sister gets a headache every time she has a lot of stress.
