spanish_meaning: Un dolor de cabeza es dolor en la cabeza.
spanish_example: Mi hermana tiene dolor de cabeza cuando está muy estresada.

english_meaning: To implement something means to ensure that what has been planned is done.
english_example: The school decided to implement a new teaching strategy.
spanish_meaning: Implementar algo significa hacer realidad lo que se planeó.
spanish_example: La escuela decidió implementar una nueva estrategia de enseñanza.

english_meaning: Insight is a deep and accurate understanding of something.
english_example: The physics textbook gave the student a new insight about gravity.
spanish_meaning: La perspicacia es una comprensión profunda y precisa de algo.
spanish_example: El libro de física le dio al estudiante una nueva idea sobre la gravedad.

english_meaning: A limb is a large branch on a tree.
english_example: The monkey sat on the tree limb and enjoyed a piece of fruit.
spanish_meaning: Un miembro grande de un árbol es una rama.
spanish_example: El mono se sentó en una rama y disfrutó de una fruta.

english_meaning: Might means strength or power.
english_example: Try with all your might to lift the heavy tire.
spanish_meaning: La fuerza es poder o energía para hacer algo.
spanish_example: Usa toda tu fuerza para levantar la llanta pesada.

english_meaning: Optimism is the state of being hopeful about the future.
english_example: The mother had optimism about her children’s futures.
spanish_meaning: El optimismo es estar esperanzado sobre el futuro.
spanish_example: La madre tenía optimismo sobre el futuro de sus hijos.

english_meaning: Proficient means able to do something well.
english_example: Secretaries are proficient at typing quickly.
spanish_meaning: Proficiente significa capaz de hacer algo bien.
spanish_example: Las secretarias son muy competentes escribiendo rápido.

english_meaning: A raft is a flat kind of boat.
english_example: He sailed across the Pacific Ocean on a raft.
spanish_meaning: Una balsa es un tipo de barco plano.
spanish_example: Él cruzó el océano Pacífico en una balsa.

english_meaning: A ridge is a long, narrow piece of raised land.
english_example: The brown bear walked along the edge of the mountain ridge.
spanish_meaning: Una cresta es una franja alta y estrecha de tierra.
spanish_example: El oso marrón caminó por el borde de la cresta de la montaña.

english_meaning: A shoulder is the body part between the top of the arm and the neck.
english_example: He carried his bike on his shoulder.
spanish_meaning: El hombro es la parte del cuerpo entre el brazo y el cuello.
spanish_example: Llevó su bicicleta al hombro.

english_meaning: To shove something is to push it with a lot of power.
english_example: She shoved him out of the way.
spanish_meaning: Empujar algo es moverlo con mucha fuerza.
spanish_example: Ella empujó a él para quitarle el paso.

english_meaning: A spouse is the person to whom someone is married.
english_example: I live in a home with my spouse and our two children.
spanish_meaning: Un cónyuge es la persona con la que alguien está casado.
spanish_example: Vivo con mi cónyuge y nuestros dos hijos.

english_meaning: To thrust means to push or move something quickly with a lot of force.
english_example: The boxer thrust his fist into the punching bag.
spanish_meaning: Impulsar significa empujar algo con mucha fuerza.
spanish_example: El boxeador impulsó su puño al saco de boxeo.

english_meaning: To tolerate something means to be able to accept it even when it is unpleasant.
english_example: When you are in a hurry, it can be hard to tolerate traffic signals.
spanish_meaning: Tolerar algo es poder aceptarlo aunque sea desagradable.
spanish_example: Cuando tienes prisa, es difícil tolerar los semáforos.

english_meaning: When someone is accountable, he or she is responsible for the effects of his or her actions.
english_example: Political leaders are accountable to the people they represent.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es responsable, responde por los efectos de sus acciones.
spanish_example: Los líderes políticos son responsables ante la gente que representan.

english_meaning: If something is addictive, it is hard to stop doing.
english_example: I think this game is very addictive.
spanish_meaning: Algo adictivo es difícil de dejar.
spanish_example: Pienso que este juego es muy adictivo.

english_meaning: An agenda is a plan or goal that guides someone’s behavior.
english_example: The man is pushing his questionable agenda on his colleagues.
spanish_meaning: Una agenda es un plan o meta que guía la conducta.
spanish_example: El hombre insiste en empujar su agenda dudosa entre colegas.

english_meaning: To coin is to create a new word or phrase that other people begin to use.
english_example: The coach coined the phrase, “There is no ‘I’ in team.”
spanish_meaning: Acuñar es crear una palabra o frase nueva que otros adoptan.
spanish_example: El entrenador acuñó la frase “No hay un yo en equipo”.

english_meaning: To correlate is to have a close connection to something.
english_example: Scientists believe that brain size might correlate with intelligence.
spanish_meaning: Correlacionar es tener una conexión cercana con algo.
spanish_example: Los científicos creen que el tamaño del cerebro podría correlacionar con la inteligencia.

english_meaning: Depression is a medical condition that makes a person very unhappy for long periods of time.
english_example: Ron decided to get help for his depression.
spanish_meaning: La depresión es una condición médica que vuelve a una persona muy infeliz por mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: Ron decidió buscar ayuda por su depresión.

english_meaning: When something is digital, it is characterized by computer technology.
english_example: Students prefer to take notes on their laptops and other digital devices.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es digital, está relacionado con la tecnología informática.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes prefieren tomar apuntes en laptops y otros dispositivos digitales.

english_meaning: Importance is the quality or state of being important, valuable, or significant.
english_example: The professor lectured the students on the importance of communication strategies.
spanish_meaning: Importancia es la calidad o estado de ser importante o valioso.
spanish_example: El profesor habló de la importancia de las estrategias de comunicación.

english_meaning: A keyboard has buttons marked with letters and numbers that are pressed to put information into a computer.
english_example: Paul spilled coffee all over his keyboard.
spanish_meaning: Un teclado tiene botones con letras y números para introducir datos.
spanish_example: Pablo se derramó el café sobre su teclado.

english_meaning: Loneliness is the unhappiness that is felt by someone if they do not have any friends.
english_example: Many people join social clubs to escape loneliness.
spanish_meaning: La soledad es la tristeza de no tener amigos.
spanish_example: Muchas personas se unen a clubes sociales para evitar la soledad.

english_meaning: Media is the different ways of entertaining and giving information to the public.
english_example: Many people like to express their opinions online using social media.
spanish_meaning: Los medios son formas de entretenimiento e información pública.
spanish_example: Muchas personas expresan opiniones en línea usando los medios sociales.

english_meaning: A platform is something that allows someone to tell a large number of people about an idea, product, etc.
english_example: The band uses their music as a platform to express their political beliefs.
spanish_meaning: Una plataforma permite hablar de ideas o productos a mucha gente.
spanish_example: La banda usa su música como plataforma para expresar sus ideas políticas.

english_meaning: When something is poor, it is not as good as it could or should be.
english_example: The condition of the sidewalk is very poor.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es pobre, no es tan bueno como debería ser.
spanish_example: El estado de la acera es muy pobre.

english_meaning: Presence is when someone or something is present in a particular place.
english_example: The researcher discovered the presence of bacteria in the water sample.
spanish_meaning: La presencia es cuando alguien o algo está en un lugar.
spanish_example: La investigadora descubrió la presencia de bacterias en la muestra de agua.

english_meaning: To subject is to cause or force someone or something to experience something harmful or unpleasant.
english_example: The location of the trees will subject them to strong winds from the hurricane.
spanish_meaning: Someter significa hacer que alguien o algo sufra algo.
spanish_example: La ubicación de los árboles los someterá a fuertes vientos del huracán.

english_meaning: A surge is a sudden, large increase in something.
english_example: All at once, there was a surge of runners at the finish line.
spanish_meaning: Una oleada es un aumento repentino y grande.
spanish_example: De repente hubo una oleada de corredores en la meta.

english_meaning: A survey is a set of questions that are asked to a large number of people in order to find out about opinions.
english_example: I filled out a survey about my shopping experience at the department store.
spanish_meaning: Una encuesta es un conjunto de preguntas para muchas personas.
spanish_example: Completé una encuesta sobre mi experiencia de compra en la tienda.

english_meaning: To target is to aim an attack at someone or something.
english_example: Scientists are looking for new drugs to target cancer cells.
spanish_meaning: Apuntar es dirigir un ataque a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Los científicos buscan nuevos fármacos para apuntar a las células cancerosas.

english_meaning: A trend is a general tendency in the way a situation is changing or developing.
english_example: Lucy likes to think she started this fashion trend.
spanish_meaning: Una tendencia es una tendencia general en cómo cambia una situación.
spanish_example: Lucy cree que ella inició está moda.

english_meaning: To validate is to make someone feel that his or her ideas and feelings are respected.
english_example: Winning first prize will validate all of Tony’s hard work.
spanish_meaning: Validar es hacer que alguien sienta que sus ideas y emociones son respetadas.
spanish_example: Ganar primer premio validará el esfuerzo de Tony.

english_meaning: Carbon is a chemical element.
english_example: Carbon is found in coal.
spanish_meaning: El carbono es un elemento químico.
spanish_example: El carbono se encuentra en el carbón.

english_meaning: Carbon dioxide is a gas made from one carbon atom and two oxygen atoms.
english_example: There is carbon dioxide in the smoke from coal power plants.
spanish_meaning: El dióxido de carbono es un gas formado por un átomo de carbono y dos de oxígeno.
spanish_example: Hay dióxido de carbono en el humo de las plantas de carbón.

english_meaning: Dust is very small, dry particles of earth or sand.
english_example: Some people wear medical face masks to avoid breathing in fine dust and germs.
spanish_meaning: El polvo es polvo de tierra o arena muy pequeño y seco.
spanish_example: Algunas personas usan mascarillas médicas para no respirar polvo y gérmenes finos.

english_meaning: To emit is to send out gas, heat, light, sound, etc.
english_example: This iron emits steam.
spanish_meaning: Emitir significa soltar gas, calor, luz o sonido.
spanish_example: Esta plancha emite vapor.

english_meaning: To enable is to make it possible for something to happen.
english_example: Studying hard will enable the girl to get a good job.
spanish_meaning: Permitir es hacer posible que algo ocurra.
spanish_example: Estudiar mucho permitirá que ella consiga un buen trabajo.

english_meaning: To grind is to break something into small pieces or powder.
english_example: We grind wheat to make flour.
spanish_meaning: Moler significa romper algo en piezas pequeñas o polvo.
spanish_example: Molimos trigo para hacer harina.

english_meaning: To heal is to become healthy or well again.
english_example: This ointment will help heal your wounds.
spanish_meaning: Sanar significa volverse saludable de nuevo.
spanish_example: Esta pomada ayudará a sanar tus heridas.

english_meaning: To integrate is to make something a part of another larger thing.
english_example: More women are being integrated into the military.
spanish_meaning: Integrar significa hacer que algo sea parte de algo más grande.
spanish_example: Cada vez más mujeres se integran en el ejército.

english_meaning: When something is leading, it is the most advanced or best.
english_example: Teresa is the leading expert on brain surgery for dogs.
spanish_meaning: Algo líder es lo más avanzado o mejor.
spanish_example: Teresa es la experta líder en cirugía cerebral de perros.

english_meaning: Moreover is used to introduce information that adds to or supports what has previously been said.
english_example: Marianne likes to walk to work to save money; moreover, it is good exercise.
spanish_meaning: Además se usa para añadir información que apoya lo dicho.
spanish_example: A Marianne le gusta caminar al trabajo para ahorrar dinero; además, es buen ejercicio.

english_meaning: To object is to feel or say that you oppose or disapprove of something.
english_example: The students object to raising the tuition.
spanish_meaning: Objetar es oponerse o desaprobar algo.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes objetan el aumento de la matrícula.

english_meaning: A pollutant is a substance that makes the air or water not safe for use.
english_example: A pollutant in the water killed this fish.
spanish_meaning: Un contaminante es una sustancia que vuelve el agua o el aire inseguros.
spanish_example: Un contaminante en el agua mató a estos peces.

english_meaning: Powder is a dry substance in the form of very small grains.
english_example: Turmeric is a yellow powder used in cooking.
spanish_meaning: El polvo es una sustancia seca en granos muy pequeños.
spanish_example: La cúrcuma es un polvo amarillo usado para cocinar.

english_meaning: To prompt is to cause someone to do something.
english_example: The tight deadline will prompt the employee to work very hard.
spanish_meaning: Provocar significa hacer que alguien haga algo.
spanish_example: La fecha límite estricta provocará que el trabajador trabaje muy duro.

english_meaning: To reject is to refuse to accept, believe in, or agree with something.
english_example: I’m afraid the teacher will reject my new idea.
spanish_meaning: Rechazar es negarse a aceptar, creer o estar de acuerdo con algo.
spanish_example: Me temo que el profesor rechazará mi nueva idea.

english_meaning: A skeptic is a person who disagrees with particular claims that are generally thought to be true.
english_example: Kevin is a skeptic because he never believes anyone or anything.
spanish_meaning: Un escéptico es alguien que no cree una afirmación sin pruebas.
spanish_example: Kevin es escéptico porque nunca cree en nada o en nadie.

english_meaning: A substance is a particular type of solid, liquid, or gas.
english_example: Arsenic is a poisonous substance.
spanish_meaning: Una sustancia es un tipo de sólido, líquido o gas.
spanish_example: El arsénico es una sustancia venenosa.

english_meaning: If something changes substantially, it changes a lot.
english_example: The price of the apartment went up substantially.
spanish_meaning: Si algo cambia mucho, cambia sustancialmente.
spanish_example: El precio del apartamento subió sustancialmente.

english_meaning: A ton is a unit for measuring weight equal to 2000 pounds or 907.2 kilograms.
english_example: This dump truck can carry up to 25 tons.
spanish_meaning: Una tonelada es una unidad de peso de 2000 libras o 907.2 kilos.
spanish_example: Ese camión puede cargar hasta 25 toneladas.

english_meaning: Transport is the system or method of carrying passengers or goods from one place to another.
english_example: Cargo ships are used for the transport of manufactured goods.
spanish_meaning: El transporte es el método para mover personas o mercancías de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: Los barcos de carga se usan para el transporte de bienes.

english_meaning: To align is to support a political group, country, or person that you agree with.
english_example: The crowd seemed to align themselves with the protestors.
spanish_meaning: Alinear significa apoyar un grupo o país con quien estás de acuerdo.
spanish_example: La multitud parecía alinearse con los manifestantes.

english_meaning: An authority is someone who has the power to make decisions and enforce rules and laws.
english_example: The local police department is the authority whenever large crowds gather.
spanish_meaning: Una autoridad es alguien que puede tomar decisiones y hacer cumplir leyes.
spanish_example: La policía local es la autoridad cuando se reúne mucha gente.

english_meaning: A barn is a large farm building that houses crops, equipment, and animals.
english_example: My grandparents keep hay in their red barn.
spanish_meaning: Un granero es un gran edificio de granja para guardar cultivos, equipos y animales.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos guardan heno en su granero rojo.

english_meaning: To betray is to be disloyal to someone who trusts you.
english_example: The four friends would never betray each other’s trust.
spanish_meaning: Traicionar es ser desleal con alguien que confía en ti.
spanish_example: Los cuatro amigos nunca traicionarían su confianza.

english_meaning: The Bible is the book of sacred writing in the Christian religion.
english_example: I read the Bible every night before I go to sleep.
spanish_meaning: La Biblia es el libro sagrado del cristianismo.
spanish_example: Leo la Biblia cada noche antes de dormir.

english_meaning: A Catholic thing or person is one that is connected with the Roman Catholic Church.
english_example: Rosanne is Catholic, so she goes to church every Sunday.
spanish_meaning: Católico se relaciona con la Iglesia Católica Romana.
spanish_example: Rosanne es católica, así que va a misa cada domingo.

english_meaning: To cooperate is to work with someone to achieve something that you both want.
english_example: The members of the team agreed to cooperate in order to get the project done.
spanish_meaning: Cooperar es trabajar con alguien para lograr algo que ambos quieren.
spanish_example: Los miembros del equipo acordaron cooperar para terminar el proyecto.

english_meaning: To detain is to officially prevent someone from leaving a place.
english_example: The police had to detain the thief for shoplifting.
spanish_meaning: Detener es impedir oficialmente que alguien se vaya de un lugar.
spanish_example: La policía tuvo que detener al ladrón por robar en la tienda.

english_meaning: An exit is something that is used as a way to get out of a place.
english_example: The green sign shows where the exit is located.
spanish_meaning: Una salida es una forma de salir de un lugar.
spanish_example: El letrero verde muestra dónde está la salida.

english_meaning: An exceptional thing or person is one that is outstanding.
english_example: Holly is an exceptional student.
spanish_meaning: Algo o alguien excepcional es sobresaliente.
spanish_example: Holly es una estudiante excepcional.

english_meaning: To flee is to leave somewhere very quickly in order to escape from danger.
english_example: When a predator approaches a deer, the deer will flee.
spanish_meaning: Huir es salir rápido para escapar de peligro.
spanish_example: Cuando un depredador se acerca a un ciervo, este huye.

english_meaning: A network is a group of people or organizations that are in some way connected to each other.
english_example: Lesley is part of a volunteer network that plants trees in the city.
spanish_meaning: Una red es un grupo de personas u organizaciones conectadas entre sí.
spanish_example: Lesley pertenece a una red de voluntarios que plantan árboles.

english_meaning: To outrage is to make someone feel very angry or shocked.
english_example: He was outraged when his proposal was turned down.
spanish_meaning: Indignar significa hacer que alguien se enfade o choque mucho.
spanish_example: Se indignó cuando rechazaron su propuesta.

english_meaning: A parish is the area for which a priest in some Christian churches is responsible.
english_example: Greg’s parish has grown over the past year.
spanish_meaning: Una parroquia es el área de responsabilidad de un sacerdote.
spanish_example: La parroquia de Greg ha crecido el último año.

english_meaning: A passage is a narrow space that people can move through.
english_example: Follow the passage down the stairs.
spanish_meaning: Un pasaje es un espacio estrecho por el que pasan personas.
spanish_example: Sigue el pasaje hasta las escaleras.

english_meaning: A pastor is a minister in charge of a parish or church.
english_example: The pastor speaks to the members of the church.
spanish_meaning: Un pastor es un ministro a cargo de una iglesia o parroquia.
spanish_example: El pastor habla con los miembros de la iglesia.

english_meaning: A patrol is a group of people or vehicles that go through an area to make sure that it is free of trouble or danger.
english_example: The military patrol is responsible for keeping the area safe.
spanish_meaning: Una patrulla es un grupo de personas o vehículos que revisa una zona para mantenerla segura.
spanish_example: La patrulla militar se encarga de la seguridad del área.

english_meaning: To raid is to attack a place in a short time in order to cause damage.
english_example: The soldiers are looking for the people who continue to raid the jungle villages.
spanish_meaning: Asaltar es atacar un lugar de golpe para causar daño.
spanish_example: Los soldados buscan a los que siguen asaltando las aldeas de la selva.

english_meaning: A rail is a system of tracks on which trains travel.
english_example: I prefer traveling by rail because it’s more convenient than driving through traffic.
spanish_meaning: Los rieles son las vías por donde viajan los trenes.
spanish_example: Prefiero viajar por rieles porque evita el tráfico.

english_meaning: A tunnel is an underground passage for cars, trains, etc. to go through.
english_example: The tunnel will lead you to the next city.
spanish_meaning: Un túnel es un paso subterráneo para autos, trenes, etc.
spanish_example: El túnel te llevará a la próxima ciudad.

english_meaning: When something is comfortable, it makes you feel relaxed, confident, and not worried.
english_example: Will loves his comfortable new sofa.
spanish_meaning: Cómodo significa que algo te hace sentir relajado y seguro.
spanish_example: Will le encanta su nuevo sofá cómodo.

english_meaning: Education is the process of teaching and learning, usually at a school, college, or university.
english_example: These students are receiving their education at a private school.
spanish_meaning: La educación es el proceso de enseñar y aprender, normalmente en escuela, colegio o universidad.
spanish_example: Estos estudiantes reciben su educación en una escuela privada.

english_meaning: Encouragement is something that makes someone more determined, hopeful, or confident.
english_example: The mother gives constant encouragement to her son.
spanish_meaning: El apoyo es algo que hace que alguien esté más decidido, esperanzado o seguro.
spanish_example: La madre da apoyo constante a su hijo.

english_meaning: To endorse is to express formal support or approval for someone or something.
english_example: The committee will endorse their leader for the upcoming election.
spanish_meaning: Respaldar es expresar apoyo o aprobación oficial.
spanish_example: El comité respaldará a su líder en las próximas elecciones.

english_meaning: When something is equal, it is the same in size, number, amount, or value as something else.
english_example: The apple and orange are equal in weight.
spanish_meaning: Igual significa el mismo tamaño, número, cantidad o valor.
spanish_example: La manzana y la naranja tienen igual peso.

english_meaning: To face is to deal with something in a direct way.
english_example: Justin will face many challenges at work.
spanish_meaning: Enfrentar algo es tratarlo de forma directa.
spanish_example: Justin enfrentará muchos retos en el trabajo.

english_meaning: A field is a subject that people study or an area of activity that they are involved in as part of their work.
english_example: Jane works in the healthcare field.
spanish_meaning: Un campo es una materia que la gente estudia o un área de trabajo.
spanish_example: Jane trabaja en el campo de salud.

english_meaning: To foster is to help a skill, feeling, or idea develop over a period of time.
english_example: The afterschool program is designed to foster a sense of community at school.
spanish_meaning: Fomentar es ayudar a que una habilidad, sentimiento o idea se desarrolle.
spanish_example: El programa extraescolar está diseñado para fomentar el sentido de comunidad en la escuela.

english_meaning: Guidance is help and advice that is given to someone about their work, education, or personal life.
english_example: Ms. Smith provided valuable guidance to all her students.
spanish_meaning: La orientación es ayuda y consejo sobre trabajo, estudios o vida personal.
spanish_example: La profesora Smith dio orientación valiosa a todos sus alumnos.

english_meaning: To hold is to have a particular degree, title, record, job, or position.
english_example: Damian will soon hold a degree in business administration.
spanish_meaning: Tener o mantener un título, puesto o récord es mantener la posición.
spanish_example: Damian pronto obtendrá un título en administración de empresas.

english_meaning: Inclusion is the act of including someone or something in a larger group or set.
english_example: The school promotes the inclusion of parents in their students’ education.
spanish_meaning: Incluir a alguien en un grupo más grande es inclusión.
spanish_example: La escuela promueve la inclusión de los padres en la educación.

english_meaning: To inspire is to encourage someone by making them feel confident and eager to do something.
english_example: Jim’s father likes to inspire his son by encouraging him to follow his dream.
spanish_meaning: Inspirar es alentar a alguien haciéndole sentirse confiado.
spanish_example: Jim inspira a su hijo para que siga sus sueños.

english_meaning: Mere is used to emphasize how small or unimportant something or someone is.
english_example: Scott lost the race by a mere two seconds.
spanish_meaning: Mero se usa para enfatizar algo pequeño o poco importante.
spanish_example: Scott perdió la carrera por meros dos segundos.

english_meaning: A position is a rank or role of someone in an organization or company.
english_example: Kyle is interviewing for a marketing position.
spanish_meaning: Un puesto es un rango o función en una organización o empresa.
spanish_example: Kyle hace entrevista para un puesto de marketing.

english_meaning: Power is the ability to influence people or give them strong feelings.
english_example: The coach has the power to keep the players from playing if they don’t show up to practice.
spanish_meaning: Poder es la capacidad de influir en personas o provocar fuertes sentimientos.
spanish_example: El entrenador tiene el poder de impedir que jueguen si faltan al entrenamiento.

english_meaning: Responsibility is a task or duty that you are expected or required to do.
english_example: Parents have the responsibility to raise their children with love.
spanish_meaning: La responsabilidad es una tarea o deber que se espera que hagas.
spanish_example: Los padres tienen la responsabilidad de criar a sus hijos con amor.

english_meaning: To soar is to increase quickly to a high level.
english_example: The temperature today will soar to a high of 100 degrees Fahrenheit.
spanish_meaning: Elevarse significa subir rápidamente hasta una altura alta.
spanish_example: La temperatura hoy subirá a un máximo de 38 grados.

english_meaning: A tool is a piece of equipment or a skill that is useful for doing your job.
english_example: Presentation skills are an important tool for communication.
spanish_meaning: Una herramienta es un equipo o una habilidad útil para tu trabajo.
spanish_example: Las habilidades de presentación son una herramienta importante para comunicarse.

english_meaning: When someone is vocal, he or she expresses a strong opinion publicly.
english_example: Shawn gets vocal when someone disagrees with his opinions.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien es vocal, expresa una opinión fuerte en público.
spanish_example: Shawn es vocal cuando alguien no está de acuerdo con sus ideas.

english_meaning: A workplace is the room or building where you work.
english_example: Diversity is important in the workplace.
spanish_meaning: El lugar de trabajo es la sala o edificio donde trabajas.
spanish_example: La diversidad es importante en el lugar de trabajo.

english_meaning: When something is academic, it relates to schools and education.
english_example: ˌækəˈdemɪk
spanish_meaning: Académico se refiere a escuelas y educación.
spanish_example: Su enfoque académico le ayudó a entrar en la universidad.
notes: La celda de ejemplo en inglés es solo un símbolo fonético.

english_meaning: Acceptance is when people agree that an idea, statement, explanation, etc., is right or true.
english_example: əkˈseptəns
spanish_meaning: La aceptación es cuando la gente acuerda que una idea o explicación es correcta.
spanish_example: La persona mostró aceptación de la solución propuesta.

english_meaning: An array is a large group or number of things.
english_example: The toy cars are available in an array of colors.
spanish_meaning: Un arreglo es un gran grupo o número de cosas.
spanish_example: Los carros de juguete están disponibles en un arreglo de colores.

english_meaning: To blur is to make something unclear or difficult to see or remember.
english_example: The photographer can blur the bright lights into the background.
spanish_meaning: Desenfocar significa hacer algo difícil de ver o recordar con claridad.
spanish_example: El fotógrafo puede desenfocar las luces brillantes en el fondo.

english_meaning: A café is a small restaurant where you can buy drinks and simple meals.
english_example: Monica works part-time at a café.
spanish_meaning: Un café es un pequeño restaurante donde puedes comprar bebidas y comidas sencillas.
spanish_example: Monica trabaja medio tiempo en un café.

english_meaning: A canvas is a thick piece of cloth that artists use to paint on.
english_example: The artist paints bright colors on a canvas.
spanish_meaning: Un lienzo es una tela gruesa que usan los artistas para pintar.
spanish_example: La artista pinta colores vivos en un lienzo.

english_meaning: A cinema is a building in which films are shown.
english_example: My sister enjoys watching movies at the cinema.
spanish_meaning: El cine es un edificio donde se proyectan películas.
spanish_example: Mi hermana disfruta ver películas en el cine.

english_meaning: Class is the way people in society are divided into different social and economic groups.
english_example: This upper-class family travels on a private jet.
spanish_meaning: Clase es la forma en que la sociedad divide a las personas en grupos sociales y económicos.
spanish_example: Esa familia de clase alta viaja en un jet privado.

english_meaning: When something is distinctive, it has a special quality, character, or appearance that is different and easy to recognize.
english_example: Olives have a distinctive flavor.
spanish_meaning: Algo distintivo tiene una cualidad fácil de reconocer.
spanish_example: Las aceitunas tienen un sabor distintivo.

english_meaning: When something is key, it is extremely important.
english_example: Marco is a key player on the country’s national team.
spanish_meaning: Algo clave es sumamente importante.
spanish_example: Marco es un jugador clave del equipo nacional.

english_meaning: A movement is a series of organized activities in which people work together to do or achieve something.
english_example: Booker T. Washington was a member of the civil rights movement.
spanish_meaning: Un movimiento es una serie de actividades organizadas en la que la gente trabaja junta.
spanish_example: Booker T. Washington fue miembro del movimiento de derechos civiles.

english_meaning: A practice is something that is done often or regularly.
english_example: Samuel has made it a practice to read the newspaper every morning.
spanish_meaning: Una práctica es algo que se hace a menudo o con regularidad.
spanish_example: Samuel hizo de la lectura del periódico una práctica diaria.

english_meaning: When something is realistic, it is shown as it is in real life.
english_example: Dorothy is painting realistic images of fish.
spanish_meaning: Realista significa mostrado como en la vida real.
spanish_example: Dorothy pinta imágenes realistas de peces.

english_meaning: A reflection is an image that is seen in a mirror or other shiny surface.
english_example: The man stared at his reflection in the mirror.
spanish_meaning: Un reflejo es una imagen vista en un espejo o superficie brillante.
spanish_example: El hombre se miró su reflejo en el espejo.

english_meaning: A rule is an official instruction that says how things must be done or what is allowed.
english_example: Wearing a helmet while riding a bicycle is a rule in some cities.
spanish_meaning: Una regla es una instrucción oficial que indica cómo deben hacerse las cosas.
spanish_example: Usar casco al montar bicicleta es una regla en algunas ciudades.

english_meaning: A stroke is a single movement of a pen or brush when you are writing or painting.
english_example: The artist began with a yellow stroke on his painting.
spanish_meaning: Un trazo es un movimiento de pluma o pincel al escribir o pintar.
spanish_example: El artista comenzó con un trazo amarillo en su pintura.

english_meaning: Sunlight is the natural light that comes from the sun.
english_example: The sunlight shines through the trees.
spanish_meaning: La luz solar es la luz natural que viene del sol.
spanish_example: La luz solar entra entre los árboles.

english_meaning: When something is traditional, it is based on old-fashioned ideas.
english_example: I prefer traditional furniture.
spanish_meaning: Tradicional significa basado en ideas antiguas.
spanish_example: Prefiero muebles tradicionales.

english_meaning: Unlike is used in place of “completely different from a particular person or thing.”
english_example: Unlike his older brother, Jim is short.
spanish_meaning: A diferencia de significa completamente distinto de alguien o algo.
spanish_example: A diferencia de su hermano mayor, Jim es bajo.

english_meaning: Width is the distance from one side of something to the other side.
english_example: The man is measuring the width of the box.
spanish_meaning: El ancho es la distancia entre un lado y el otro de algo.
spanish_example: El hombre mide el ancho de la caja.

english_meaning: An anthem is a formal or religious song.
english_example: Everyone sings the national anthem before the baseball game starts.
spanish_meaning: Un himno es una canción formal o religiosa.
spanish_example: Todos cantan el himno nacional antes de que empiece el partido.

english_meaning: Beyond is used to say that one thing is more than another.
english_example: The job is beyond the man’s ability.
spanish_meaning: Más allá significa que algo sobrepasa otra cosa.
spanish_example: El trabajo está más allá de la capacidad de ese hombre.

english_meaning: Determination is what you have when you try to do something even when it is difficult.
english_example: Chris is the youngest competitor, but he has the most determination.
spanish_meaning: La determinación es insistencia al intentar algo aunque sea difícil.
spanish_example: Chris es el competidor más joven, pero tiene más determinación.

english_meaning: Fighting is physical conflict between people or groups in a war, in the street, etc.
english_example: Heavy fighting continued for days.
spanish_meaning: El combate es el conflicto físico entre personas o grupos en guerra o en la calle.
spanish_example: El combate fue intenso durante días.

english_meaning: When something is joint, it is done by or involves two or more people or groups.
english_example: Mike and Joanna are joint owners of a coffee shop.
spanish_meaning: Conjunto significa hecho por dos o más personas o grupos.
spanish_example: Mike y Joanna son dueños conjuntos de una cafetería.

english_meaning: A landing is when you return to the ground or another surface after a flight or a boat ride.
english_example: The man is in charge of leading the landing in the warzone.
spanish_meaning: Aterrizaje es volver al suelo o a una superficie tras un vuelo o viaje en bote.
spanish_example: El hombre dirige el aterrizaje en la zona de guerra.

english_meaning: To mark is to celebrate an important event or time by doing something.
english_example: My grandparents are having a party to mark their 50th anniversary.
spanish_meaning: Marcar significa señalar o celebrar una fecha o momento importante.
spanish_example: Mis abuelos hicieron una fiesta para marcar su aniversario número 50.

english_meaning: To mobilize is to prepare an army to fight in a war.
english_example: Thousands of soldiers were ready to mobilize for the fight.
spanish_meaning: Móvilizar significa preparar al ejército para la guerra.
spanish_example: Miles de soldados estaban listos para móvilizarse para la lucha.

english_meaning: A nation is a large area of land that is controlled by its own government.
english_example: India is a nation that is developing rapidly.
spanish_meaning: Una nación es una gran área de tierra con gobierno propio.
spanish_example: India es una nación que se desarrolla rápidamente.

english_meaning: Originally is used in place of “in the beginning” or “when something first happened or began.”
english_example: This building was originally a bank.
spanish_meaning: Originalmente significa en los inicios, cuando algo comenzó.
spanish_example: Este edificio fue originalmente un banco.

english_meaning: An outbreak is a sudden start or increase of fighting or disease.
english_example: The scientists prepared for an outbreak of a dangerous virus.
spanish_meaning: Un brote es un inicio repentino de lucha o enfermedad.
spanish_example: Los científicos se prepararon para un brote de un virus peligroso.

english_meaning: To pray is to speak to God in order to ask for help or to give thanks.
english_example: The girl likes to pray for world peace.
spanish_meaning: Rezar es hablar con Dios para pedir ayuda o dar gracias.
spanish_example: La niña gusta de rezar por la paz del mundo.

english_meaning: A prisoner is someone who is taken by force and kept somewhere.
english_example: He has been a prisoner for many years.
spanish_meaning: Un prisionero es alguien retenido por la fuerza en un lugar.
spanish_example: Ha sido prisionero durante muchos años.

english_meaning: To sacrifice is to give up something in order to get something you want or do something else for someone.
english_example: Karen has had to sacrifice a lot for her children.
spanish_meaning: Sacrificar es renunciar a algo para obtener algo o hacer algo por otro.
spanish_example: Karen ha tenido que sacrificar mucho por sus hijos.

english_meaning: Silence is the complete absence of sound or noise.
english_example: I can only sleep in complete silence.
spanish_meaning: Silencio es ausencia total de sonido.
spanish_example: Solo puedo dormir en silencio total.

english_meaning: Thus is used in place of “as a result of something that was just mentioned.”
english_example: Victor wants to lose weight; thus, he’ll go on a diet and start exercising.
spanish_meaning: Así se usa para decir “como resultado de lo mencionado”.
spanish_example: Victor quiere bajar de peso; así irá en dieta y empezará a ejercitarse.

english_meaning: A tribute is something that you say, do, or give in order to express respect for someone.
english_example: In some countries, people wear poppies as a tribute to veterans.
spanish_meaning: Un tributo es algo dicho, hecho o dado para mostrar respeto.
spanish_example: En algunos países, la gente lleva amapolas como tributo a los veteranos.

english_meaning: A victory is a situation in which you win a battle, game, election, or dispute.
english_example: The man was thrilled with his victory.
spanish_meaning: La victoria es cuando ganas una batalla, juego, elección o disputa.
spanish_example: El hombre estaba feliz con su victoria.

english_meaning: To be wild is to be without control.
english_example: Dandelions grow wild in the cracks of the streets.
spanish_meaning: Salvaje significa sin control.
spanish_example: Las diente de león crecen salvajes en las grietas de las calles.

english_meaning: To wound is to injure someone or something by cutting or breaking the skin.
english_example: The boy knew he might wound his knees if he didn’t wear pads on them.
spanish_meaning: Herir es causar daño cortando o rompiendo la piel.
spanish_example: El niño sabía que podría herir sus rodillas si no llevaba rodilleras.

english_meaning: To assimilate is to adopt the ways of a new culture and fully become a part of it.
english_example: When the family moves to the US they will assimilate into American culture.
spanish_meaning: Asimilar es adoptar las costumbres de una nueva cultura e integrarse.
spanish_example: Cuando la familia se mudó a EE. UU., asimiló la cultura americana.

english_meaning: An attempt is an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult.
english_example: This is Ben’s second attempt to climb Mt. Everest.
spanish_meaning: Un intento es un acto de tratar de hacer algo, sobre todo algo difícil.
spanish_example: Este es el segundo intento de Ben por escalar el Everest.

english_meaning: A case is an example of a particular situation or of something happening.
english_example: A deposit of $1,000 is required, but in this case, we will accept half.
spanish_meaning: Un caso es un ejemplo de una situación particular.
spanish_example: Se requiere un depósito de $1,000, pero en este caso aceptaremos la mitad.

english_meaning: To cease is to stop doing something or to stop something happening.
english_example: The rain will cease in the afternoon.
spanish_meaning: Cesar significa detenerse o hacer que algo deje de pasar.
spanish_example: La lluvia cesará por la tarde.

english_meaning: Danger is the possibility that someone or something will be harmed, destroyed, or killed.
english_example: Smoking cigarettes is a danger to health.
spanish_meaning: Peligro es la posibilidad de daño, destrucción o muerte.
spanish_example: Fumar cigarrillos es un peligro para la salud.

english_meaning: If something is devastating, it causes great harm or damage to something or someone.
english_example: The tsunami had devastating effects on the seaside village.
spanish_meaning: Devastador significa que causa mucho daño o destrucción.
spanish_example: El tsunami tuvo efectos devastadores en el pueblo costero.

english_meaning: Diversity is the quality or state of having many different forms, types, ideas, etc.
english_example: My company puts an emphasis on the importance on having cultural diversity.
spanish_meaning: La diversidad es la variedad de formas, tipos o ideas.
spanish_example: Mi empresa enfatiza la importancia de la diversidad cultural.

english_meaning: An earthquake is a sudden shaking of a part of the Earth’s surface that often causes a lot of damage.
english_example: The earthquake caused the road to crack in multiple places.
spanish_meaning: Un terremoto es un fuerte temblor de la Tierra que causa daño.
spanish_example: El terremoto hizo que la carretera se rompiera en muchos lugares.

english_meaning: If something is endangered, it has become very rare and may eventually die out altogether.
english_example: The whooping crane is an endangered species.
spanish_meaning: Amenazado significa que se ha vuelto muy raro y puede desaparecer.
spanish_example: El grulla cornuda es una especie en peligro de extinción.

english_meaning: Extinction is when a particular animal, plant, type of person, custom, skill, etc. stops existing.
english_example: The extinction of the dodo bird occurred in the 1600s.
spanish_meaning: La extinción es cuando un animal, planta, tipo de persona, costumbre o habilidad deja de existir.
spanish_example: La extinción del dodo ocurrió en el siglo XVII.

english_meaning: We use forever in place of “for all future time” or “for a very long time.”
english_example: Michelle and Tanya want to be best friends forever.
spanish_meaning: Usamos “para siempre” para decir “para todo el tiempo que queda” o “por muchísimo tiempo”.
spanish_example: Michelle y Tanya quieren ser mejores amigas para siempre.

english_meaning: We use generally in place of “in most cases” or “to most people.”
english_example: The suburbs are generally viewed as a good place to raise children.
spanish_meaning: Generalmente significa “en la mayoría de los casos” o “para la mayoría de la gente”.
spanish_example: En general, los suburbios se ven como un buen lugar para criar hijos.

english_meaning: Genocide is the deliberate murder of a whole group or race of people.
english_example: As a result of the genocide, many children have been orphaned.
spanish_meaning: El genocidio es el asesinato deliberado de todo un grupo o una etnia de personas.
spanish_example: A causa del genocidio, muchos niños quedaron huérfanos.

english_meaning: To hit is to affect someone or something in a harmful or dangerous way.
english_example: The ship was hit by a violent storm.
spanish_meaning: Golpear o afectar a alguien o algo de forma dañina o peligrosa.
spanish_example: El barco fue impactado por una tormenta violenta.

english_meaning: To lose is to have something that is important or necessary taken from you or destroyed.
english_example: The family never thought they’d lose everything to fire.
spanish_meaning: Perder significa que algo importante se te quita o se destruye.
spanish_example: La familia nunca pensó que perdería todo en el incendio.

english_meaning: If something is native, it refers to the place someone was born and raised.
english_example: Carlos is a hero in his native country of Brazil.
spanish_meaning: Nativo se refiere al lugar donde alguien nació y creció.
spanish_example: Carlos es un héroe en su país nativo, Brasil.

english_meaning: A percent is an amount that is equal to one one-hundredth of something.
english_example: Liz needs to get 80 percent on her test to pass the course.
spanish_meaning: El porcentaje es una cantidad igual a una parte de cada cien.
spanish_example: Liz necesita sacar un 80 por ciento para aprobar la prueba.

english_meaning: A reason is a statement or fact that explains why something is the way it is, or why someone does, thinks, or says something.
english_example: The reason Eric is late for work is that he forgot to set his alarm.
spanish_meaning: Una razón es un hecho o explicación de por qué algo sucede o de por qué alguien actúa.
spanish_example: La razón por la que Eric llega tarde es que se olvidó de poner la alarma.

english_meaning: A reference is the act of referring to something or someone.
english_example: My supervisor made a reference to the data I’d collected.
spanish_meaning: Una referencia es la acción de aludir a algo o alguien.
spanish_example: El supervisor hizo una referencia a los datos que había recopilado.

english_meaning: If something is urban, it is related to towns and cities.
english_example: It is common to see tall buildings in urban areas.
spanish_meaning: Lo urbano se relaciona con las ciudades y los pueblos.
spanish_example: Es común ver edificios altos en zonas urbanas.

english_meaning: To burn is to use something as a source of energy.
english_example: Running is good exercise for people who want to burn fat and lose weight.
spanish_meaning: Quemar significa usar algo como fuente de energía.
spanish_example: Correr es un buen ejercicio para quemar grasa y bajar de peso.

english_meaning: To check is to ask someone whether something is correct, true, or allowed.
english_example: Peter wants to check with the teacher to see if he is doing the assignment right.
spanish_meaning: Comprobar significa preguntar o verificar si algo es correcto, verdadero o permitido.
spanish_example: Peter quiere comprobar con el profesor si hace bien la tarea.

english_meaning: A coconut is a large brown fruit that has a hard shell and white flesh.
english_example: A coconut produces a healthy oil.
spanish_meaning: El coco es una fruta grande y marrón con cáscara dura y pulpa blanca.
spanish_example: Un coco produce un aceite saludable.

english_meaning: Epilepsy is a medical condition that affects the brain and can make someone become unconscious or unable to control their movement for a short time.
english_example: The doctor diagnosed the patient with epilepsy.
spanish_meaning: La epilepsia es una enfermedad del cerebro que puede causar desmayos o falta de control de movimiento por poco tiempo.
spanish_example: El médico diagnosticó al paciente con epilepsia.

english_meaning: Fat is an oily solid or liquid substance in food.
english_example: The fat contained in salmon, avocados, and nuts is healthy.
spanish_meaning: La grasa es una sustancia aceitosa sólida o líquida de los alimentos.
spanish_example: La grasa del salmón, aguacate y nueces es saludable.

english_meaning: To gain is to gradually get more and more of a quality, feeling, etc.
english_example: Winning the award will help Steven gain confidence.
spanish_meaning: Ganar significa obtener de forma gradual más cantidad de una cualidad, emoción o cosa.
spanish_example: Ganar ese premio le ayudará a Steven a ganar confianza.

english_meaning: Glucose is a natural form of sugar that exists in plants and fruit.
english_example: People with diabetes need to check their glucose levels.
spanish_meaning: La glucosa es una forma natural de azúcar que hay en plantas y frutas.
spanish_example: Las personas con diabetes deben controlar sus niveles de glucosa.

english_meaning: When something is high, it is greater than usual in amount, number, or degree.
english_example: The thermometer shows a high body temperature.
spanish_meaning: Alto significa mayor de lo normal en cantidad, número o intensidad.
spanish_example: El termómetro muestra una temperatura corporal alta.

english_meaning: To keep is to stay in a particular state, condition, or position.
english_example: Brian needs to keep working in order to finish his project on time.
spanish_meaning: Mantener significa permanecer en un estado, condición o posición específica.
spanish_example: Brian debe mantenerse trabajando para terminar su proyecto a tiempo.

english_meaning: If something is known, it is something that people are aware of or have discovered.
english_example: Bob is known throughout the neighborhood as a burglars.
spanish_meaning: Conocido significa que algo o alguien es sabido por muchas personas.
spanish_example: Bob es conocido en todo el vecindario como el ladrón.

english_meaning: The liver is a large organ in the body that produces bile and cleans blood.
english_example: The professor showed us what the human liver looks like.
spanish_meaning: El hígado es un órgano grande que produce bilis y limpia la sangre.
spanish_example: El profesor nos mostró cómo se ve el hígado humano.

english_meaning: If something is low, it is smaller than usual in amount, number, or degree.
english_example: The temperature gets very low in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Bajo significa menor que lo normal en cantidad, número o grado.
spanish_example: La temperatura baja mucho en invierno.

english_meaning: Management is the act or process of controlling and dealing with something.
english_example: Practicing yoga is good for stress management.
spanish_meaning: La gestión es el acto de controlar y manejar algo.
spanish_example: Practicar yoga ayuda a la gestión del estrés.

english_meaning: When something is metabolic, it relates to the chemical process in which plants or animals use food and water to make energy.
english_example: Fish have a very high metabolic rate.
spanish_meaning: Metabólico se refiere al proceso químico con el que plantas y animales usan alimentos y agua para obtener energía.
spanish_example: Los peces tienen una tasa metabólica muy alta.

english_meaning: Oil is a smooth, thick liquid made from plants or some animals, and is especially in cooking.
english_example: The chef put the oil in the pan and heated it up.
spanish_meaning: El aceite es un líquido espeso y viscoso de origen vegetal o animal, usado mucho en cocina.
spanish_example: El cocinero puso el aceite en la sartén y lo calentó.

english_meaning: An olive is a small egg-shaped black or green fruit.
english_example: I prepared a bowl of olives for my guests.
spanish_meaning: La aceituna es una fruta pequeña, negra o verde, con forma ovalada.
spanish_example: Preparé un tazón de aceitunas para mis invitados.

english_meaning: Performance is how well someone or something functions, works, etc.
english_example: Liam’s performance in the race was not his best.
spanish_meaning: El rendimiento es la manera en que alguien o algo funciona o trabaja.
spanish_example: El rendimiento de Liam en la carrera no fue su mejor.

english_meaning: If something is powerful, it has a strong effect.
english_example: This perfume has a powerful scent.
spanish_meaning: Algo poderoso tiene un efecto fuerte.
spanish_example: Este perfume tiene un aroma muy poderoso.

english_meaning: Starch is a substance that provides your body with energy and is found in foods such as wheat, potatoes, and rice.
english_example: Pasta is high in starch.
spanish_meaning: El almidón es una sustancia que aporta energía y se encuentra en alimentos como trigo, patata y arroz.
spanish_example: La pasta tiene mucho almidón.

english_meaning: A summary is a short statement that contains the main information about something.
english_example: Cindy concluded the meeting with a summary of the project.
spanish_meaning: Un resumen es una declaración breve con la información principal de algo.
spanish_example: Cindy concluyó la reunión con un resumen del proyecto.

english_meaning: If something is analytic, it is related to logic and reasoning.
english_example: The analytic article criticized the new plan and presented one of its own.
spanish_meaning: Analítico se refiere a la lógica y el razonamiento.
spanish_example: El artículo analítico criticó el nuevo plan y presentó otro.

english_meaning: An area is a reasonably large place.
english_example: He was very familiar with the local area.
spanish_meaning: Un área es un lugar de tamaño considerable.
spanish_example: Conocía muy bien el área local.

english_meaning: A bachelor is an unmarried man.
english_example: Since he was a bachelor, Jason did his shopping by himself.
spanish_meaning: Un soltero es un hombre que no está casado.
spanish_example: Como era soltero, Jason hizo sus compras solo.

english_meaning: If something is cognitive, it is related to learning and knowing things.
english_example: After her physical examination, her cognitive skills were tested.
spanish_meaning: Cognitivo está relacionado con aprender y comprender cosas.
spanish_example: Después del examen físico, probaron sus habilidades cognitivas.

english_meaning: A collision is the act of two things hitting into each another.
english_example: The collision between the two cars created a loud noise.
spanish_meaning: La colisión es el choque entre dos cosas que se golpean.
spanish_example: La colisión entre los dos autos produjo un ruido fuerte.

english_meaning: If someone is competent, they are able to think or act successfully.
english_example: Competent employees are much better than unknowledgeable ones.
spanish_meaning: Competente significa capaz de actuar o pensar con eficacia.
spanish_example: Los empleados competentes son mucho mejores que los que no saben nada.

english_meaning: A diploma is a certificate proving that someone has completed their studies.
english_example: After four years of college, Mary finally had a diploma.
spanish_meaning: Un diploma es un certificado que demuestra que alguien terminó sus estudios.
spanish_example: Después de cuatro años de universidad, Mary por fin obtuvo un diploma.

english_meaning: To excel at a subject or activity means to be very good at it.
english_example: Jenny excels at playing the piano.
spanish_meaning: Sobresalir significa ser muy bueno en una materia o actividad.
spanish_example: Jenny sobresale mucho al tocar el piano.

english_meaning: Geology is the study of the Earth’s natural structures and how they change.
english_example: Because he studied geology, he knew how the mountains were formed.
spanish_meaning: La geología es el estudio de las estructuras naturales de la Tierra y su cambio.
spanish_example: Como estudió geología, sabía cómo se formaron las montañas.

english_meaning: Something that is integral is an important part of the whole.
english_example: She is an integral member of our team.
spanish_meaning: Integral significa parte esencial de un conjunto.
spanish_example: Ella es un miembro integral de nuestro equipo.

english_meaning: An intellect is a person’s ability to understand things easily.
english_example: She was known for her quick and strong intellect as well as her beauty.
spanish_meaning: El intelecto es la capacidad de entender con facilidad.
spanish_example: La conocían por su rápido e inteligente intelecto, además de su belleza.

english_meaning: Your job is the work you do to earn money.
english_example: He now has a high-paying job.
spanish_meaning: Un trabajo es la actividad con la que se gana dinero.
spanish_example: Ahora tiene un trabajo bien pago.

english_meaning: If someone is keen, they are intelligent.
english_example: Only a keen student could have solved that math problem.
spanish_meaning: Alguien que es inteligente capta y comprende con facilidad.
spanish_example: Solo un estudiante inteligente pudo resolver ese problema de matemáticas.

english_meaning: The minimum is the smallest amount.
english_example: What is the minimum temperature in winter in this area?
spanish_meaning: El mínimo es la cantidad más pequeña posible.
spanish_example: ¿Cuál es la temperatura mínima en invierno en está zona?

english_meaning: Psychology involves the study of how the brain affects our thought and actions.
english_example: The psychology of seeing is a fascinating subject.
spanish_meaning: La psicología estudia cómo el cerebro afecta nuestros pensamientos y acciones.
spanish_example: La psicología de la percepción es un tema fascinante.

english_meaning: If something is radioactive, then it lets out, or is related to, radiation.
english_example: Radioactive states can be very bad for anyone’s health.
spanish_meaning: Algo radiactivo emite radiación o está relacionado con ella.
spanish_example: Los estados radiactivos pueden ser muy peligrosos para la salud.

english_meaning: Relativity is a set of ideas about time and space developed by Albert Einstein.
english_example: Relativity teaches that light travels at the same speed throughout the universe.
spanish_meaning: La relatividad es un conjunto de ideas sobre el tiempo y el espacio creadas por Einstein.
spanish_example: La relatividad enseña que la luz viaja a la misma velocidad en todo el universo.

english_meaning: Sociology is the study of human society, its organizations, and problems.
english_example: Sociology teaches that people’s problems are a result of their society.
spanish_meaning: La sociología estudia la sociedad humana, sus organizaciones y problemas.
spanish_example: La sociología muestra que muchos problemas vienen de la sociedad.

english_meaning: If something is theoretical, it is based on theory rather than experience.
english_example: His conclusion was only theoretical and not meant to be publicized.
spanish_meaning: Teórico significa basado en ideas o teorías, no en experiencia directa.
spanish_example: Su conclusión era solo teórica y no debía hacerse pública.

english_meaning: If someone is weak, they are not strong and healthy.
english_example: She is still weak after the illness.
spanish_meaning: Alguien débil no está fuerte ni sano.
spanish_example: Ella sigue débil después de la enfermedad.

english_meaning: An administrator is a person who controls a business, company, or organization.
english_example: Everyone in the store did whatever the administrator asked them to do.
spanish_meaning: Un administrador es la persona que dirige una empresa u organización.
spanish_example: Todos en la tienda hacían lo que les pedía el administrador.

english_meaning: If someone is affluent, they are wealthy.
english_example: People in the city are usually more affluent than people in the country.
spanish_meaning: Adinerado significa una persona con mucha riqueza.
spanish_example: La gente de la ciudad suele ser más adinerada que la del campo.

english_meaning: To audit means to inspect financial records from a person or business.
english_example: The government usually audits companies that report lower than usual incomes.
spanish_meaning: Auditar significa revisar los registros financieros de una persona o empresa.
spanish_example: El gobierno suele auditar a empresas con ingresos menores de lo habitual.

english_meaning: To automate something means to have machines or computers do the work.
english_example: When the bank automated, it started installing ATM machines.
spanish_meaning: Automatizar significa hacer que las máquinas o computadores realicen el trabajo.
spanish_example: Cuando el banco automatizó sus procesos, instaló cajeros automáticos.

english_meaning: To bribe someone means to illegally persuade them for a favor with money.
english_example: The judge was bribed so that she would set the suspect free.
spanish_meaning: Sobornar significa convencer a alguien ilegalmente con dinero para obtener un favor.
spanish_example: El juez fue sobornado para dejar libre al sospechoso.

english_meaning: If someone is corrupt, they break the law for money or fame.
english_example: The corrupt policemen didn’t arrest the man because he gave them money.
spanish_meaning: Corrupto significa que alguien rompe la ley por dinero o fama.
spanish_example: Los policías corruptos no detuvieron al hombre porque le dieron dinero.

english_meaning: To dispose of something means to get rid of it.
english_example: He disposed of the can by throwing it into the recycle bin.
spanish_meaning: Deshacerse o eliminar algo de forma definitiva.
spanish_example: Él desechó la lata echándola al cubo de reciclaje.

english_meaning: A headquarters is a building where the bosses of a company work.
english_example: He took the long route to the headquarters because it was a nice day.
spanish_meaning: La sede es el edificio desde donde dirige una empresa.
spanish_example: Él tomó la ruta larga hasta la sede porque hacía buen día.

english_meaning: An incentive is what makes a person want to do something.
english_example: The chance of winning a prize was an incentive to get people to play the game.
spanish_meaning: Un incentivo es lo que motiva a alguien a actuar.
spanish_example: La posibilidad de ganar un premio fue un incentivo para jugar.

english_meaning: An infrastructure is a collection of services needed to run a society or business.
english_example: Power lines are important parts of a city’s infrastructure.
spanish_meaning: La infraestructura es el conjunto de servicios necesarios para que funcione una sociedad o negocio.
spanish_example: Las líneas eléctricas son parte importante de la infraestructura de la ciudad.

english_meaning: To legislate means to make laws.
english_example: Senators have to legislate fairly so most people will enjoy the benefits.
spanish_meaning: Legislar significa crear leyes.
spanish_example: Los senadores deben legislar con justicia para que la mayoría disfrute de los beneficios.

english_meaning: If something is legitimate, then it is acceptable according to the law.
english_example: She found a legitimate plan to raise extra funds for her vacation.
spanish_meaning: Legítimo significa aceptable según la ley.
spanish_example: Ella encontró un plan legítimo para conseguir fondos extra para su viaje.

english_meaning: To manipulate something means to skillfully or unfairly control or affect it.
english_example: The doctor manipulated the data to make it look like the cure was working.
spanish_meaning: Manipular significa controlar o influir en algo de forma hábil o injusta.
spanish_example: El médico manipuló los datos para que el tratamiento pareciera funcionar.

english_meaning: Merchandise is goods ready to be purchased or sold.
english_example: The store added more merchandise because there were more shoppers.
spanish_meaning: La mercancía son bienes que se pueden comprar o vender.
spanish_example: La tienda añadió más mercancía porque llegaron más compradores.

english_meaning: Retail is the activity of selling goods to the public, often for personal use.
english_example: Although cheap to make, once a t-shirt reaches retail, it costs ten times as much.
spanish_meaning: El comercio minorista es la venta de bienes al público para uso personal.
spanish_example: Aunque se produce barato, cuando una camiseta llega al comercio minorista cuesta diez veces más.

english_meaning: Revenue is the income made by a company.
english_example: The new products really increased the business’s monthly revenue.
spanish_meaning: Los ingresos son el dinero que obtiene una empresa.
spanish_example: Los nuevos productos aumentaron mucho los ingresos mensuales del negocio.

english_meaning: Rubbish is trash or waste.
english_example: The floor around the garbage can was covered with all kinds of rubbish.
spanish_meaning: La basura es el desecho o residuo.
spanish_example: El suelo junto al basurero estaba cubierto de todo tipo de basura.

english_meaning: A subsidy is money given by the government to companies to assist them.
english_example: The official gave the company a subsidy so it could open two new factories.
spanish_meaning: Un subsidio es dinero del gobierno para ayudar a una empresa.
spanish_example: La autoridad dio un subsidio a la empresa para que abriera dos fábricas nuevas.

english_meaning: A transaction is an act of buying or selling something.
english_example: Because the clerk was new at the job, the simple transaction took a long time.
spanish_meaning: Una transacción es el acto de comprar o vender algo.
spanish_example: Como el dependiente era nuevo, la transacción simple tardó mucho.

english_meaning: To violate a law, rule, or agreement means to break it.
english_example: I was given a ticket because the policeman said I violated a traffic law.
spanish_meaning: Violar una ley, regla o acuerdo significa romperla.
spanish_example: Me dieron una multa porque el policía dijo que violé una norma de tránsito.

english_meaning: To assess something means to judge the structure, purpose, or quality of it.
english_example: She assessed the condition of the toy car before buying it.
spanish_meaning: Evaluar significa juzgar la calidad, estructura o finalidad de algo.
spanish_example: Ella evaluó la condición del coche de juguete antes de comprarlo.

english_meaning: To astonish someone means to greatly surprise them.
english_example: The number of people that came to her party astonished her.
spanish_meaning: Asombrar significa sorprender mucho a alguien.
spanish_example: La cantidad de gente que asistió a su fiesta la asombró.

english_meaning: To commence something means to begin it.
english_example: His speech commenced with a “thank you” to all who had helped him succeed.
spanish_meaning: Comenzar significa iniciar algo.
spanish_example: El discurso comenzó con un “gracias” a todos los que lo ayudaron a tener éxito.

english_meaning: The essence of something is its important qualities or basic characteristics.
english_example: The essence of the argument was that both sides felt they had lost money.
spanish_meaning: La esencia es la cualidad importante o rasgo básico de algo.
spanish_example: La esencia del argumento era que ambos equipos sentían que habían perdido dinero.

english_meaning: To extract something means to remove it.
english_example: The dentist extracted the woman’s damaged tooth and put in a fake one.
spanish_meaning: Extraer significa quitar algo.
spanish_example: El dentista extrajo el diente dañado de la mujer y le colocó uno artificial.

english_meaning: If something is fabulous, it is extremely good.
english_example: This strawberry is the best I’ve ever had. It’s fabulous.
spanish_meaning: Fabuloso significa extraordinariamente bueno.
spanish_example: Esta fresa es la mejor que he probado; es fabulosa.

english_meaning: Haste is speed in movement or action.
english_example: In order to get to the meeting in time, he proceeded with haste.
spanish_meaning: La prisa es rapidez en el movimiento o la acción.
spanish_example: Para llegar a tiempo a la reunión, salió con prisa.

english_meaning: An impulse is a sudden thoughtless urge to do something.
english_example: Because of the scary noise, she had an impulse to run somewhere and hide.
spanish_meaning: Un impulso es un deseo repentino de actuar sin pensar.
spanish_example: A causa del ruido, tuvo el impulso de correr y esconderse.

english_meaning: When you inhibit something, you stop it from developing.
english_example: Poor reading skills will inhibit learning in a wide range of subject areas.
spanish_meaning: Inhibir significa frenar o impedir que algo se desarrolle.
spanish_example: Las pocas habilidades de lectura pueden inhibir el aprendizaje en muchas materias.

english_meaning: Latter describes something last in a series or the second choice of two things.
english_example: In the latter minutes of the game, the visitors scored the winning goal.
spanish_meaning: Último se usa para lo que va al final de una serie o la segunda opción.
spanish_example: En los últimos minutos del juego, los visitantes marcaron el gol ganador.

english_meaning: A molecule is the smallest basic unit that makes up a physical substance.
english_example: A tiny drop of water is made up of many billions of molecules of water.
spanish_meaning: Una molécula es la unidad física más pequeña de una sustancia.
spanish_example: Una gota de agua tiene miles de millones de moléculas de agua.

english_meaning: If something is ongoing, then it is still happening or still growing.
english_example: The development of plants is ongoing because it takes time for them to mature.
spanish_meaning: Sigue en curso cuando algo todavía está ocurriendo o progresando.
spanish_example: El desarrollo de las plantas está en curso porque tardan tiempo en crecer.

english_meaning: If someone is precise, then they are exact and careful about their work.
english_example: The builder was very precise about where he placed the nails.
spanish_meaning: Preciso significa exacto y detallista en el trabajo.
spanish_example: El constructor fue muy preciso al poner los clavos.

english_meaning: Proximity is closeness in time, space, or relationships.
english_example: All the trees in the proximity of the beach had been cut down.
spanish_meaning: La proximidad es la cercanía en tiempo, espacio o relaciones.
spanish_example: Todos los árboles en las proximidades de la playa habían sido talados.

english_meaning: Publicity is public attention given to someone or something by the media.
english_example: She received a lot of publicity after her performance in the film.
spanish_meaning: La publicidad es la atención pública que alguien o algo recibe por los medios.
spanish_example: Recibió mucha publicidad tras su actuación en la película.

english_meaning: A remedy is a cure for a disease, argument, or problem.
english_example: A good remedy for a headache is an aspirin and a glass of water.
spanish_meaning: Un remedio es una cura para una enfermedad, discusión o problema.
spanish_example: Un buen remedio para el dolor de cabeza es una aspirina y un vaso de agua.

english_meaning: The significance of something is the quality that makes it important.
english_example: The significance of the snowy weather was that we didn’t have to go to school.
spanish_meaning: La importancia o significación es la cualidad que hace algo valioso.
spanish_example: Lo importante de que lloviera y nevara fue que no tuvimos que ir a la escuela.

english_meaning: If something is subsequent, then it comes after something else in time.
english_example: The flood and the subsequent rescue of those caught in the flood were on TV.
spanish_meaning: Subsecuente/subsecuente significa que ocurre después de algo.
spanish_example: La inundación y el rescate posterior de los atrapados se vieron en la televisión.

english_meaning: If something is synthetic, then it is made to be like something natural.
english_example: Clothing made out of synthetic fabrics is very effective at keeping people warm.
spanish_meaning: Sintético significa fabricado para parecerse a algo natural.
spanish_example: La ropa hecha con telas sintéticas conserva mejor el calor.

english_meaning: If something is terminal, then it causes or results in death.
english_example: Since his condition was not terminal, he felt a great sense of relief.
spanish_meaning: Terminal significa que causa o resulta en muerte.
spanish_example: Como su condición no era terminal, se sintió mucho aliviado.

english_meaning: When a bad thing is acute, it is very severe and intense.
english_example: When she fell out of the tree, the girl felt an acute pain in her arm.
spanish_meaning: Agudo significa muy intenso o severo.
spanish_example: Cuando cayó del árbol, la niña sintió un dolor agudo en el brazo.

english_meaning: Aggression is behavior that is mean or violent to others.
english_example: The problem was only made worse by Mark’s aggression.
spanish_meaning: La agresión es un comportamiento cruel o violento hacia otros.
spanish_example: El problema empeoró por la agresión de Mark.

english_meaning: A biography is an account of someone’s life that is written by someone else.
english_example: We read a biography about Charles Darwin in science class.
spanish_meaning: Una biografía es la historia de la vida de una persona escrita por otra.
spanish_example: Leímos una biografía de Charles Darwin en clase de ciencias.

english_meaning: To boost something means to increase or improve it.
english_example: Lowering prices boosts customers’ interest in shopping.
spanish_meaning: Impulsar significa aumentar o mejorar algo.
spanish_example: Bajar los precios impulsa el interés de los clientes por comprar.

english_meaning: To clap means to hit one’s hands together to express pleasure or get attention.
english_example: After the speech, everyone in the crowd clapped their hands for the speaker.
spanish_meaning: Aplausar es juntar las manos para mostrar gusto o llamar la atención.
spanish_example: Después del discurso, todos aplaudieron al orador.

english_meaning: Classical music is more formal and serious than popular music.
english_example: Beethoven is a composer of classical music.
spanish_meaning: Clásico describe música formal y seria, distinta de la popular.
spanish_example: Beethoven es un compositor de música clásica.

english_meaning: To compel someone to do something means to force them to do it.
english_example: Traffic signs compel drivers to drive safely.
spanish_meaning: Obligar significa forzar a alguien a hacer algo.
spanish_example: Las señales de tránsito obligan a los conductores a conducir con seguridad.

english_meaning: A creation is something original that is made.
english_example: We saw the creation of a new political party.
spanish_meaning: La creación es algo original que se produce.
spanish_example: Vimos la creación de un nuevo partido político.

english_meaning: The dominance of a person is their state of being more powerful than others.
english_example: Large gorillas hit their chests to express their dominance over others.
spanish_meaning: La dominancia es el estado de tener más poder que otros.
spanish_example: Los gorilas adultos golpean su pecho para mostrar su dominancia.

english_meaning: When something is gorgeous, it is very pleasing and attractive.
english_example: The girl picked out a gorgeous dress to wear to the dance.
spanish_meaning: Algo hermoso o deslumbrante, muy bonito y atractivo.
spanish_example: La chica eligió un vestido precioso para ir al baile.

english_meaning: When something is inevitable, it is certain to happen or cannot be avoided.
english_example: It is inevitable that the days will get longer in the summer.
spanish_meaning: Inevitable significa que algo seguro ocurre o no se puede evitar.
spanish_example: Es inevitable que en verano los días se alarguen.

english_meaning: A legacy is an effect that exists because of a person or thing in the past.
english_example: The legacy of the ancient Egyptians can be seen in their monuments.
spanish_meaning: Un legado es el efecto o herencia que deja una persona o cosa del pasado.
spanish_example: El legado de los antiguos egipcios se ve en sus monumentos.

english_meaning: A masterpiece is a very good painting, novel, movie, or other work of art.
english_example: The Arc de Triomphe is considered a masterpiece in the world of architecture.
spanish_meaning: Una obra maestra es una obra de arte de gran calidad.
spanish_example: El Arco del Triunfo se considera una obra maestra de la arquitectura.

english_meaning: If there are multiple things, there are many of them.
english_example: When the stunt went wrong, the man suffered multiple injuries.
spanish_meaning: Múltiple significa que hay muchas cosas.
spanish_example: Al fallar la acrobacia, el hombre sufrió múltiples lesiones.

english_meaning: To narrate a story means to write about it or read it aloud.
english_example: This story was written by John, but Aaron is narrating it to the crowd.
spanish_meaning: Narrar es contar una historia por escrito o en voz alta.
spanish_example: John escribió está historia, pero Aaron la está narrando al público.

english_meaning: When something is notorious, it is well known because of something bad.
english_example: This area of town is notorious for gang activity.
spanish_meaning: Notorio significa bien conocido por algo negativo.
spanish_example: Este barrio es notorio por la actividad de las pandillas.

english_meaning: When a thing is talked about overall, the whole thing is considered.
english_example: Overall, the party was a huge success.
spanish_meaning: En general significa considerando el conjunto total.
spanish_example: En general, la fiesta fue un gran éxito.

english_meaning: A partiality is a tendency to prefer one thing to another.
english_example: She has a partiality for walking to school instead of driving.
spanish_meaning: La parcialidad es la tendencia a preferir una cosa sobre otra.
spanish_example: Ella tiene parcialidad por caminar al colegio en vez de conducir.

english_meaning: When an act is spontaneous, it is not planned. It happens suddenly.
english_example: My wife made a spontaneous decision to buy a new sofa while I was at work.
spanish_meaning: Algo espontáneo no está planificado y ocurre de golpe.
spanish_example: Mi esposa tomó una decisión espontánea de comprar un sofá nuevo mientras estaba en el trabajo.

english_meaning: A virtue is a good quality or way of behaving.
english_example: My best virtue is forgiveness.
spanish_meaning: La virtud es una cualidad buena o una forma correcta de comportarse.
