spanish_example: Mi mejor virtud es el perdón.

english_meaning: An aspect is one part or feature of something.
english_example: I thought about the different aspects of owning two dogs.
spanish_meaning: Un aspecto es una parte o característica de algo.
spanish_example: Pensé en los distintos aspectos de tener dos perros.

english_meaning: An asset is a skill or quality that is useful or valuable.
english_example: The coach realized the boy’s speed was an asset to the team.
spanish_meaning: Un recurso es una habilidad o cualidad útil y valiosa.
spanish_example: El entrenador se dio cuenta de que la velocidad del chico era un recurso para el equipo.

english_meaning: An assignment is task that is given to you to do.
english_example: He was given a difficult assignment but he felt he could succeed in doing it.
spanish_meaning: Una asignación es una tarea que se te da para hacer.
spanish_example: Le asignaron una tarea difícil, pero sintió que podía cumplirla.

english_meaning: An awareness is knowledge or perception or a situation or fact.
english_example: She has little awareness of what needs to be done.
spanish_meaning: Conciencia significa conocimiento o percepción de una situación.
spanish_example: Ella tiene poca conciencia de lo que hay que hacer.

english_meaning: A bud is a part of a plant that turns into a flower or a leaf.
english_example: Two weeks after planting the seed, a small bud appeared.
spanish_meaning: Una yema es la parte de la planta que luego se transforma en flor o hoja.
spanish_example: Dos semanas después de plantar la semilla, apareció una pequeña yema.

english_meaning: To coordinate things is to make different parts work together.
english_example: Each skating team had to coordinate their movements for the show.
spanish_meaning: Coordinar significa hacer que diferentes partes trabajen juntas.
spanish_example: Cada equipo de patinaje tuvo que coordinar sus movimientos para el show.

english_meaning: Credit is something good in your favour.
english_example: His bank account is in credit, so he has money he can spend.
spanish_meaning: Crédito es algo favorable a tu favor.
spanish_example: Su cuenta bancaria está en crédito, así que tiene dinero para gastar.

english_meaning: To disprove something means to show that it is not true.
english_example: The scientist disproved the theory that the sun moved around the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Refutar significa demostrar que algo no es cierto.
spanish_example: El científico refutó la teoría de que el sol giraba alrededor de la Tierra.

english_meaning: If something is humanitarian, it is connected to helping people’s lives.
english_example: After the flood, several humanitarian organizations offered help.
spanish_meaning: Humanitario se refiere a ayudar y proteger la vida de las personas.
spanish_example: Después de la inundación, varias organizaciones humanitarias ofrecieron ayuda.

english_meaning: A hypothesis is an idea about something that has not been proved yet.
english_example: The teacher did an experiment to prove whether his hypothesis was right.
spanish_meaning: Una hipótesis es una idea sobre algo que aún no se ha demostrado.
spanish_example: El profesor hizo un experimento para comprobar si su hipótesis era correcta.

english_meaning: When something is informative, it provides a lot of information.
english_example: The travel guide had a lot of informative facts about the region.
spanish_meaning: Informativo significa que aporta mucha información.
spanish_example: La guía de viaje tenía muchos datos informativos sobre la región.

english_meaning: When something is optic, it relates to the eyes or light.
english_example: Her blindness was caused by a problem with her optic nerve.
spanish_meaning: Óptico se relaciona con los ojos o la luz.
spanish_example: Su ceguera fue causada por un problema en el nervio óptico.

english_meaning: A premise is an idea on which something is based.
english_example: The premise of the movie that Bobbi and I watched was unrealistic.
spanish_meaning: La premisa es la idea en la que se basa algo.
spanish_example: La premisa de la película que vimos era poco realista.

english_meaning: A rack is an object with shelves that holds things.
english_example: He stored his tools on a rack.
spanish_meaning: Una estantería es un mueble con estantes para guardar cosas.
spanish_example: Guardó sus herramientas en una estantería.

english_meaning: The Renaissance was a period between the 14th and 17th centuries.
english_example: Leonardo da Vinci was a popular artist of the Renaissance.
spanish_meaning: El Renacimiento fue un período entre los siglos XIV y XVII.
spanish_example: Leonardo da Vinci fue un artista destacado del Renacimiento.

english_meaning: When something is spatial, it relates to the position and size of things.
english_example: He was asked where the books were located to test his spatial awareness.
spanish_meaning: Lo espacial se refiere a la posición y tamaño de las cosas.
spanish_example: Le pidieron que ubicara los libros para evaluar su sentido espacial.

english_meaning: To specify is to describe something clearly.
english_example: The poster didn’t specify where the concert was taking place.
spanish_meaning: Especificar es describir algo con claridad.
spanish_example: El cartel no especificaba dónde sería el concierto.

english_meaning: If you tie two things together, you fasten them with rope, string, etc.
english_example: The dog is used to being tied up.
spanish_meaning: Atar significa unir dos cosas sujetándolas con cuerda u otro medio.
spanish_example: El perro está acostumbrado a ir atado.

english_meaning: To undertake a particular task is to take on the responsibility of doing it.
english_example: We undertook to make sure everybody was well informed of current progress.
spanish_meaning: Emprender significa asumir la responsabilidad de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Nosotros emprendimos la tarea de informar bien a todos del progreso actual.

english_meaning: Wax is a substance that is slightly shiny and melts when heated.
english_example: The candles are made of wax.
spanish_meaning: La cera es una sustancia algo brillante que se derrite al calentarse.
spanish_example: Las velas están hechas de cera.

english_meaning: An addict is a person who cannot stop doing something.
english_example: She was a coffee addict. She had more than three cups each day.
spanish_meaning: Un adicto es una persona que no puede dejar de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Ella era adicta al café y tomaba más de tres tazas al día.

english_meaning: When something is archeological, it relates to archeology.
english_example: They found archeological evidence of an ancient species of man.
spanish_meaning: Arqueológico se relaciona con la arqueología.
spanish_example: Encontraron evidencia arqueológica de una especie humana antigua.

english_meaning: Archeology is the study of ancient people through their artifacts.
english_example: He studied archeology to learn more about ancient Egyptian culture.
spanish_meaning: La arqueología estudia a las personas antiguas a través de sus objetos.
spanish_example: Estudió arqueología para aprender más sobre la cultura del antiguo Egipto.

english_meaning: To brainstorm is to have a lot of ideas about a certain topic.
english_example: The students met after school to brainstorm ideas for their assignments.
spanish_meaning: Hacer una lluvia de ideas es generar muchas ideas sobre un tema.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes se reunieron después de clase para hacer una lluvia de ideas para sus tareas.

english_meaning: A budget is the amount of money available to spend on something.
english_example: His budget for food was very tight.
spanish_meaning: El presupuesto es la cantidad de dinero disponible para gastar en algo.
spanish_example: Su presupuesto para alimentos era muy ajustado.

english_meaning: When something is chaotic, it is crazy, confused, and hectic.
english_example: The first day of school can be chaotic for a new student.
spanish_meaning: Algo caótico es desordenado, confuso y agitado.
spanish_example: El primer día de clases puede ser caótico para un estudiante nuevo.

english_meaning: To cite something is to mention it as an example or as proof of something.
english_example: She cited six reasons that the school needed to build new classrooms.
spanish_meaning: Citar significa mencionar algo como ejemplo o evidencia.
spanish_example: Ella citó seis razones por las que la escuela necesitaba construir más aulas.

english_meaning: To correspond is to match or to be similar to something.
english_example: The boy’s story didn’t correspond with his mother’s version.
spanish_meaning: Corresponder significa coincidir o ser similar a algo.
spanish_example: La versión del chico no coincidía con la versión de su madre.

english_meaning: A courtyard is an outdoor area that is surrounded by the walls of a building.
english_example: During the summer, the courtyard is a nice place to have lunch.
spanish_meaning: Un patio es un área abierta rodeada por un edificio.
spanish_example: En verano, el patio es un buen lugar para almorzar.

english_meaning: An estate is a large area of land owned by a family or organization.
english_example: He lived on his father’s estate in the country.
spanish_meaning: Una finca es una gran propiedad de tierra de una familia u organización.
spanish_example: Vivía en la finca de su padre en el campo.

english_meaning: Fraud is the crime of gaining money by lying or by tricking people.
english_example: Experts say that credit card fraud increases around the holidays.
spanish_meaning: El fraude es el delito de obtener dinero engañando o mintiendo.
spanish_example: Los expertos dicen que el fraude con tarjetas de crédito aumenta en las fiestas.

english_meaning: Hydrogen is a gas that has no taste, color, or smell.
english_example: Airships used to be filled with hydrogen, but it exploded easily.
spanish_meaning: El hidrógeno es un gas sin sabor, color ni olor.
spanish_example: Antes se llenaban los dirigibles con hidrógeno, pero explotaba fácilmente.

english_meaning: Integrity is honesty and good morals.
english_example: The principal had a lot of integrity.
spanish_meaning: La integridad es honestidad y buenas costumbres morales.
spanish_example: El director tenía mucha integridad.

english_meaning: An outlook is a person’s opinion or way of thinking about something.
english_example: He changed his outlook about rats after he read a book about them.
spanish_meaning: El punto de vista es la opinión o forma de pensar sobre algo.
spanish_example: Cambió su punto de vista sobre las ratas tras leer un libro sobre ellas.

english_meaning: A parachute is a device that helps people and things fall to the ground safely.
english_example: They used parachutes dropped from airplanes to send supplies to the civilians.
spanish_meaning: Un paracaídas es un dispositivo que permite caer con seguridad desde el aire.
spanish_example: Los civiles recibieron suministros que se lanzaron desde aviones con paracaídas.

english_meaning: When something is prehistoric, it is from a time before written history.
english_example: The scientists found prehistoric pots used by the people in the area.
spanish_meaning: Prehistórico significa anterior a la historia escrita.
spanish_example: Los científicos encontraron ollas prehistóricas usadas por la gente de la zona.

english_meaning: A proponent is a person who supports an idea or a plan.
english_example: He was a proponent of using environmentally-friendly products.
spanish_meaning: Quien apoya una idea o un plan se llama proponente.
spanish_example: Fue un proponente del uso de productos ecológicos.

english_meaning: To refine something is to make it better by making changes.
english_example: The principal refined his method of controlling students over the years.
spanish_meaning: Refinar significa mejorar algo mediante cambios.
spanish_example: Con los años, el director fue refinando su forma de controlar a los estudiantes.

english_meaning: To restrict something is to limit it and prevent it from getting bigger.
english_example: The club restricted the amount of members.
spanish_meaning: Restringir significa limitar o impedir que algo crezca.
spanish_example: El club restringió el número de miembros.

english_meaning: A team is a group of people who work closely together.
english_example: The All Blacks are my favorite sports team.
spanish_meaning: Un equipo es un grupo de personas que trabajan juntas.
spanish_example: Los All Blacks son mi equipo deportivo favorito.

english_meaning: When something is aggregate, it is made up of smaller amounts added together.
english_example: The company totaled its aggregate sales for the entire year.
spanish_meaning: Agregado significa formado por partes menores sumadas.
spanish_example: La empresa totalizó sus ventas agregadas de todo el año.

english_meaning: An antibiotic is a medical drug used to kill bacteria and treat infections.
english_example: The doctor gave me a shot of an antibiotic when I got the flu.
spanish_meaning: Un antibiótico es un medicamento médico para matar bacterias y tratar infecciones.
spanish_example: El médico me puso una inyección de antibiótico cuando tuve gripe.

english_meaning: A circuit is a piece of an electronic device that allows electricity to flow.
english_example: Be very careful not to shock yourself when fixing an electrical circuit.
spanish_meaning: Un circuito es una parte de un dispositivo eléctrico que permite que pase la electricidad.
spanish_example: Al reparar un circuito eléctrico, ten cuidado para no electrocutarte.

english_meaning: To complement something or someone is to make them better.
english_example: The wool scarf complemented her lovely eyes.
spanish_meaning: Complementar significa mejorar algo o alguien.
spanish_example: El pañuelo de lana complementó sus hermosos ojos.

english_meaning: To compress something means to press or squeeze it so that it takes up less space.
english_example: I compressed my clothes to fit into a single suitcase.
spanish_meaning: Comprimir significa presionar o apretar para que ocupe menos espacio.
spanish_example: Comprimí mi ropa para que cupiera en una sola maleta.

english_meaning: A database is a collection of data that is stored in a computer.
english_example: The company has a database of all the names and accounts of their customers.
spanish_meaning: Una base de datos es un conjunto de datos guardados en un ordenador.
spanish_example: La empresa tiene una base de datos con todos los nombres y cuentas de sus clientes.

english_meaning: An equivalent is an amount or value that is the same as another amount or value.
english_example: I worked the equivalent of sixty hours this week.
spanish_meaning: Un equivalente es una cantidad o valor igual a otro.
spanish_example: Trabajé el equivalente a sesenta horas está semana.

english_meaning: When someone is immune to a disease, they cannot be affected by it.
english_example: Children usually get shots to make them immune to certain diseases.
spanish_meaning: Inmune significa que no se puede contraer una enfermedad.
spanish_example: Los niños se vacunan para hacerse inmunes a ciertas enfermedades.

english_meaning: Input is information that is put into a computer.
english_example: Type the input into the computer program.
spanish_meaning: La entrada es la información que se introduce en un ordenador.
spanish_example: Escribe la entrada en el programa informático.

english_meaning: When a relationship is intimate, the two things are very closely connected.
english_example: I only tell my secrets to my most intimate friends.
spanish_meaning: Íntimo significa muy cercano o estrechamente conectado.
spanish_example: Sólo comparto mis secretos con mis amigos más íntimos.

english_meaning: A magnet is a piece of iron or other material which attracts iron toward it.
english_example: I used a magnet to pick up the nails that were scattered on the floor.
spanish_meaning: Un imán es un material que atrae hierro.
spanish_example: Usé un imán para recoger los clavos que estaban tirados en el suelo.

english_meaning: A person’s metabolism is the way chemical processes in their body use energy.
english_example: If you exercise every day, your metabolism speeds up.
spanish_meaning: El metabolismo es el proceso químico por el que el cuerpo usa energía.
spanish_example: Si haces ejercicio todos los días, tu metabolismo se acelera.

english_meaning: A microchip is a small device in a computer that holds information.
english_example: I can put more data on my computer if I buy a more powerful microchip.
spanish_meaning: Un microchip es un dispositivo pequeño que guarda información en un dispositivo tecnológico.
spanish_example: Puedo guardar más datos en mi ordenador si compro un microchip más potente.

english_meaning: A phase is a stage in a process or the gradual development of something.
english_example: The first phase in the recycling project involves finding volunteers to help out.
spanish_meaning: Una fase es una etapa en el desarrollo de algo.
spanish_example: La primera fase del proyecto de reciclaje consiste en buscar voluntarios.

english_meaning: To pinch means to take a piece of skin between one’s fingers and squeeze.
english_example: I pinched my nose so I couldn’t smell the odor from the garbage.
spanish_meaning: Pellizcar es tomar piel entre los dedos y apretar.
spanish_example: Me pellizqué la nariz para no oler el olor de la basura.

english_meaning: When something is prevalent, it is common.
english_example: Growing a beard is more prevalent in some countries than in others.
spanish_meaning: Algo prevalente es algo común o frecuente.
spanish_example: En algunos países es más prevalente llevar barba que en otros.

english_meaning: When something is quantum, it relates to the behavior of atomic particles.
english_example: The physics student studied quantum mechanics.
spanish_meaning: Cuántico se relaciona con el comportamiento de partículas atómicas.
spanish_example: El alumno de física estudió mecánica cuántica.

english_meaning: A ratio is a relationship between two things expressed in numbers or amounts.
english_example: The boy-to-girl ratio is one to three.
spanish_meaning: La proporción es la relación entre dos cosas expresada en números.
spanish_example: La proporción de niños y niñas es de uno a tres.

english_meaning: A spiral is a shape which winds round and round as if around a cylinder.
english_example: A strand of DNA looks like two interlocking spirals.
spanish_meaning: Una espiral es una forma en espiral alrededor de un eje.
spanish_example: Una hebra de ADN parece dos espirales entrelazadas.

english_meaning: When something is viral, it is a disease or infection that is caused by a virus.
english_example: The girl was in bed for a week when she had a viral infection.
spanish_meaning: Viral se refiere a una infección causada por un virus.
spanish_example: La chica estuvo en cama una semana por una infección viral.

english_meaning: An acid is a chemical that can burn or dissolve other substances.
english_example: In chemistry class, we mixed two acids together and watched the reaction.
spanish_meaning: Un ácido es una sustancia química que puede quemar o disolver otras sustancias.
spanish_example: En química mezclamos dos ácidos y observamos la reacción.

english_meaning: An administration is the group of people who manage a company or organization.
english_example: She hoped she could be promoted to a job in administration.
spanish_meaning: La administración es el grupo de personas que gestionan una empresa.
spanish_example: Ella esperaba ascender a un puesto en administración.

english_meaning: Administrative describes anything related to managing a company or organization.
english_example: I work as an administrative assistant to the owner of the company.
spanish_meaning: Administrativo se relaciona con la gestión de una empresa o organización.
spanish_example: Trabajo como asistente administrativo del dueño de la empresa.

english_meaning: Biotechnology is the use of living parts, such as cells, in industry and technology.
english_example: Researchers at the biotechnology company use bacteria to make medicine.
spanish_meaning: La biotecnología usa componentes vivos, como células, en la industria y tecnología.
spanish_example: Los investigadores de la compañía de biotecnología usan bacterias para fabricar medicina.

english_meaning: Cholesterol is a substance in fat, tissues, and blood of all animals.
english_example: When people have too much cholesterol, they are at a high risk for heart problems.
spanish_meaning: El colesterol es una sustancia grasa presente en tejidos y sangre.
spanish_example: Al tener demasiado colesterol, aumenta el riesgo de problemas del corazón.

english_meaning: A coalition is a group of people or organizations working for a common purpose.
english_example: The companies formed a coalition to make trade less expensive.
spanish_meaning: Una coalición es un grupo que trabaja junto por un objetivo común.
spanish_example: Las empresas formaron una coalición para abaratar el comercio.

english_meaning: When something is deceptive, it encourages one to believe something that is false.
english_example: The scary-looking man’s appearance is deceptive; he is actually very nice.
spanish_meaning: Engañoso significa inducir a creer algo falso.
spanish_example: La apariencia de ese hombre era engañosa; en realidad era muy amable.

english_meaning: Diabetes is a medical condition in which a person’s body cannot control the level of sugar in their blood.
english_example: Overweight people are more likely to suffer from diabetes than slimmer ones.
spanish_meaning: La diabetes es una enfermedad en la que el cuerpo no controla bien el azúcar en la sangre.
spanish_example: Las personas con sobrepeso tienen más riesgo de sufrir diabetes.

english_meaning: To eliminate something that is unwanted means to completely remove it.
english_example: Wearing a seatbelt eliminates some of the dangers of driving a car.
spanish_meaning: Eliminar significa quitar algo por completo.
spanish_example: Usar cinturón de seguridad elimina algunos riesgos al conducir.

english_meaning: Erosion is the destruction of rock or soil due to flowing water or weather.
english_example: Canyons are formed because rivers of fast-moving water caused erosion.
spanish_meaning: La erosión es la destrucción de rocas o suelo por agua o clima.
spanish_example: Los cañones se forman porque el agua rápida causa erosión.

english_meaning: Ethics are moral beliefs or rules about right or wrong.
english_example: The act of stealing certainly doesn’t go against some people’s ethics.
spanish_meaning: La ética son las creencias o normas sobre lo correcto e incorrecto.
spanish_example: Para algunas personas, robar claramente va contra la ética.

english_meaning: If something is explicit, it is very clear, open, and truthful.
english_example: The man gave a very explicit account of the car accident.
spanish_meaning: Explícito significa claro, abierto y directo.
spanish_example: El hombre dio un relato muy explícito del accidente de coche.

english_meaning: A framework is a set of rules or ideas that people use to solve problems.
english_example: His ideas fit into the framework of a successful business plan.
spanish_meaning: Un marco es un conjunto de reglas o ideas para resolver problemas.
spanish_example: Sus ideas encajaban en el marco de un plan de negocios exitoso.

english_meaning: To manufacture something means to make it in a factory.
english_example: My father’s company manufactures steel building materials.
spanish_meaning: Fabricar significa hacer algo en una fábrica.
spanish_example: La empresa de mi padre fábrica materiales de construcción de acero.

english_meaning: A mechanism is a part of a machine that performs a certain function.
english_example: I can’t open my car door because the locking mechanism is broke.
spanish_meaning: Un mecanismo es una parte de una máquina que realiza una función.
spanish_example: No puedo abrir la puerta del coche porque el mecanismo de cierre está roto.

english_meaning: To minimize means to reduce something to the lowest possible level.
english_example: I checked my homework twice to minimize errors I might have made.
spanish_meaning: Minimizar significa reducir algo al nivel más bajo posible.
spanish_example: Revisé la tarea dos veces para minimizar los errores.

english_meaning: Nectar is a sweet liquid produced by flowers that bees and other insects collect.
english_example: Bees use nectar to make their honey.
spanish_meaning: El néctar es un líquido dulce producido por flores.
spanish_example: Las abejas usan néctar para hacer miel.

english_meaning: A notion is an idea or belief about something.
english_example: I had a notion that this route would get us to the beach.
spanish_meaning: Una noción es una idea o creencia sobre algo.
spanish_example: Tenía la noción de que este camino nos llevaría a la playa.

english_meaning: A regime is a system of government or management.
english_example: He was a member of the old regime that had been overthrown in the election.
spanish_meaning: Un régimen es un sistema de gobierno o de manejo.
spanish_example: Fue miembro del antiguo régimen que fue derrocado en la elección.

english_meaning: When something is straightforward, it is good because it is easy to understand.
english_example: The teacher’s grading system was straightforward and fair.
spanish_meaning: Sencillo o claro significa fácil de entender.
spanish_example: El sistema de calificación del profesor era sencillo y justo.

english_meaning: If someone is bankrupt, then they are unable to pay their debts.
english_example: The store had few customers and soon went bankrupt.
spanish_meaning: Quedarse en quiebra significa no poder pagar las deudas.
spanish_example: La tienda tuvo pocos clientes y pronto se fue a la quiebra.

english_meaning: To conform to rules or laws is to obey them.
english_example: The new student had to conform to the school’s dress code.
spanish_meaning: Conformarse significa obedecer normas o reglas.
spanish_example: El nuevo estudiante tuvo que cumplir el código de vestimenta de la escuela.

english_meaning: To employ someone means to give work to them.
english_example: The bookstore employed two full-time clerks.
spanish_meaning: Emplear significa dar trabajo a alguien.
spanish_example: La librería emplea a dos dependientes a tiempo completo.

english_meaning: To expel someone means to force them to leave a place.
english_example: Since he would not follow the rules, the principal had to expel the student.
spanish_meaning: Expulsar significa obligar a alguien a salir de un lugar.
spanish_example: Como no seguía las reglas, el director tuvo que expulsar al estudiante.

english_meaning: An extension is a part added to something to give it more time or space.
english_example: My parents decided to add an extension to our house for the new baby.
spanish_meaning: Una extensión es una parte añadida para tener más espacio o tiempo.
spanish_example: Mis padres decidieron añadir una extensión a nuestra casa para el bebé nuevo.

english_meaning: If something is forthcoming, then it is about to happen in the future.
english_example: Some economists predicted that the forthcoming world economy would be severe.
spanish_meaning: Próximo significa inminente o que ocurrirá pronto.
spanish_example: Algunos economistas predijeron que la próxima economía mundial sería grave.

english_meaning: To furnish means to put furniture in a house or room.
english_example: Most homes are furnished with tables, chairs, and beds.
spanish_meaning: Amueblar significa poner muebles en una casa o habitación.
spanish_example: La mayoría de las casas están amuebladas con mesas, sillas y camas.

english_meaning: Hygiene is the conditions or methods needed for health and cleanliness.
english_example: People who brush their teeth at least twice a day are practicing good hygiene.
spanish_meaning: La higiene son los hábitos y condiciones para conservar la salud y la limpieza.
spanish_example: Quienes se cepillan los dientes al menos dos veces al día practican buena higiene.

english_meaning: If something is hygienic, then it is clean and unlikely to cause disease.
english_example: My sister works very hard to keep her entire home as hygienic as possible.
spanish_meaning: Higiénico significa limpio y que no provoca enfermedad.
spanish_example: Mi hermana se esfuerza mucho para que toda su casa sea lo más higiénica posible.

english_meaning: A landlord is a man who rents property to a person.
english_example: The landlord collected everyone’s rent money on the first day of every month.
spanish_meaning: Alquilar significa ser el propietario que renta una propiedad.
spanish_example: El arrendador cobró la renta de todos el primer día de cada mes.

english_meaning: To lease means to rent property, usually an apartment or land.
english_example: When the family first leased the apartment, the rent was very low.
spanish_meaning: Alquilar significa rentar una propiedad, normalmente un departamento o terreno.
spanish_example: Cuando la familia alquiló el apartamento, la renta era muy baja.

english_meaning: If something is mandatory, then it is required by law.
english_example: It’s mandatory that everyone be at least 16 to drive a car in the US.
spanish_meaning: Algo obligatorio es requerido por ley.
spanish_example: Es obligatorio que todos tengan al menos 16 años para conducir en EE. UU.

english_meaning: To mend something means to fix it when it is broken or damaged.
english_example: Mother mended the rip in my pants with a piece of cloth.
spanish_meaning: Reparar significa arreglar algo roto o dañado.
spanish_example: Mi madre reparó la rotura de mis pantalones con un pedazo de tela.

english_meaning: A mortgage is a loan for property, especially a home or a business.
english_example: When they bought their new home, the married couple had to sign a mortgage.
spanish_meaning: La hipoteca es un préstamo para una propiedad, especialmente una casa o un negocio.
spanish_example: Cuando compraron su casa, la pareja firmó una hipoteca.

english_meaning: Personnel are employees in a business.
english_example: When business increased, we had to hire more personnel.
spanish_meaning: El personal es el conjunto de empleados de un negocio.
spanish_example: Cuando el negocio creció, tuvimos que contratar más personal.

english_meaning: Plumbing is the system of pipes used in a home to supply water.
english_example: When the plumbing stopped working, no one was allowed to use the toilets.
spanish_meaning: La fontanería es el sistema de tuberías de una casa para suministrar agua.
spanish_example: Cuando la fontanería dejó de funcionar, nadie podía usar los baños.

english_meaning: A quote is what someone has said, including saying how much a piece of work will cost.
english_example: His writing was full of quotes from Shakespeare.
spanish_meaning: Una cita es lo que alguien ha dicho o el costo de un trabajo.
spanish_example: Su texto estaba lleno de citas de Shakespeare.

english_meaning: A tenant is a person who rents property from a landlord.
english_example: The new tenants moved into the house across the street.
spanish_meaning: Un inquilino es la persona que renta una propiedad de un arrendador.
spanish_example: Los nuevos inquilinos se mudaron a la casa de enfrente.

english_meaning: A utility is a business that supplies services such as water or electricity.
english_example: If you don’t pay the utilities, you may have your electricity turned off.
spanish_meaning: Un servicio público es una empresa que suministra agua o electricidad.
spanish_example: Si no pagas los servicios, te pueden cortar la electricidad.

english_meaning: Whereby means by which or through which.
english_example: The mayor had a new bridge built whereby the citizens could cross the river.
spanish_meaning: Mediante significa por medio de o a través de algo.
spanish_example: El alcalde construyó un puente mediante el cual los ciudadanos cruzaban el río.

english_meaning: To adapt means to change in order to deal with a new situation.
english_example: When he went to the new town, he had to adapt to all the weather changes.
spanish_meaning: Adaptarse significa cambiar para enfrentarse a una nueva situación.
spanish_example: Al llegar a la nueva ciudad, tuvo que adaptarse a los cambios de clima.

english_meaning: Biological describes the process of life and living things.
english_example: In science class, we learned about the biological process of bacterial growth.
spanish_meaning: Biológico describe los procesos de la vida y los seres vivos.
spanish_example: En clase de ciencias aprendimos el proceso biológico del crecimiento bacteriano.

english_meaning: When something is cellular, it relates to the cells of animals or plants.
english_example: She used a microscope to see the activity at a cellular level.
spanish_meaning: Celular se refiere a las células de animales o plantas.
spanish_example: Vio con un microscopio la actividad a nivel celular.

english_meaning: When people are dynamic, they are lively and have creative ideas.
english_example: The new, dynamic employee came up with a good way to juggle his workload.
spanish_meaning: Dinámico describe a alguien enérgico y con ideas creativas.
spanish_example: El nuevo empleado, dinámico, inventó una buena forma de organizar su trabajo.

english_meaning: A fantasy is a pleasant situation that people think about but is unlikely to happen.
english_example: Becoming an astronaut is a fantasy shared by many children.
spanish_meaning: Una fantasía es una situación agradable que parece poco probable.
spanish_example: Ser astronauta es una fantasía compartida por muchos niños.

english_meaning: Heredity is the process of passing on features from parents to children.
english_example: The boy’s face is similar to his father’s because of heredity.
spanish_meaning: La herencia es el proceso de transmitir rasgos de padres a hijos.
spanish_example: La cara del niño es similar a la de su padre por la herencia.

english_meaning: When something is internal, it exists or happens inside a person, object, or place.
english_example: We removed the outer case to reveal the computer’s internal wires.
spanish_meaning: Interno significa que existe o sucede dentro de una persona, objeto o lugar.
spanish_example: Quitamos la carcasa externa para mostrar los cables internos de la computadora.

english_meaning: When something is minimal, it is very small.
english_example: My lazy husband does a minimal amount of work around the house.
spanish_meaning: Mínimo significa muy poco.
spanish_example: Mi esposo perezoso hace una cantidad mínima de trabajo en casa.

english_meaning: A pioneer is a person who is the first to discover or be involved in something.
english_example: He was a pioneer of computer programming.
spanish_meaning: Un pionero es la primera persona en descubrir o participar en algo.
spanish_example: Él fue un pionero de la programación informática.

english_meaning: To prescribe medicine means to tell someone to take it.
english_example: When I was sick, the doctor prescribed me flu medicine for me to take.
spanish_meaning: Prescribir medicina significa indicar a alguien que la tome.
spanish_example: Cuando estuve enfermo, el médico me prescribió un medicamento para la gripe.

english_meaning: When things are respective, they relate separately to each person just mentioned.
english_example: The boxers were told to return to their respective corners.
spanish_meaning: Respectivo significa que corresponde por separado a cada persona mencionada.
spanish_example: Los boxeadores fueron enviados a sus respectivas esquinas.

english_meaning: When rules or systems are rigid, they are severe because they cannot be changed.
english_example: Societies often have rigid rules about the way that people are supposed to act.
spanish_meaning: Rígido significa severo porque no puede cambiarse.
spanish_example: Muchas sociedades tienen reglas rígidas sobre cómo actuar.

english_meaning: A sequence is a number of events or things that come one after another.
english_example: The dominos fell in a sequence of one after another.
spanish_meaning: Una secuencia es una serie de eventos o cosas que vienen uno tras otro.
spanish_example: Las fichas de dominó cayeron en secuencia, una tras otra.

english_meaning: Things which are similar are almost the same.
english_example: I have had a similar experience to the one you described.
spanish_meaning: Similar significa casi igual.
spanish_example: Tuve una experiencia similar a la que describiste.

english_meaning: To substitute something or someone means to have them take the place of another.
english_example: When I ran out of juice, I had to substitute water to drink in the morning.
spanish_meaning: Sustituir algo o alguien significa ocupar el lugar de otro.
spanish_example: Cuando me quedé sin zumo, sustituí agua para beber por la mañana.

english_meaning: A surgeon is a doctor who is trained to do surgery.
english_example: The surgeon operated on the old man’s heart.
spanish_meaning: Un cirujano es un médico entrenado para hacer cirugía.
spanish_example: El cirujano operó el corazón del anciano.

english_meaning: Therapy is treatment for a particular physical or mental illness or condition.
english_example: After she broke her legs, she used physical therapy to learn how to walk again.
spanish_meaning: La terapia es tratamiento para una enfermedad física o mental.
spanish_example: Después de romperse las piernas, usó terapia física para aprender a caminar de nuevo.

english_meaning: To transfer something means to move it from one place to another.
english_example: The family transferred the groceries from the shopping cart to the car.
spanish_meaning: Transferir algo significa moverlo de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: La familia transfirió las compras del carrito al coche.

english_meaning: A transition is a process where there is a change from one form to another.
english_example: The weather gets colder during the transition from summer to autumn.
spanish_meaning: Una transición es un proceso de cambio de una forma a otra.
spanish_example: El clima se enfría durante la transición del verano al otoño.

english_meaning: A transplant is an operation in which a damaged part of one’s body is replaced.
english_example: The sick child needed a heart transplant to live.
spanish_meaning: Un trasplante es una operación para reemplazar una parte dañada del cuerpo.
spanish_example: El niño enfermo necesitó un trasplante de corazón para sobrevivir.

english_meaning: Astrology is the study of the stars in the belief that they influence people’s lives.
english_example: Jack, who studies astrology, believes that the stars can predict the future.
spanish_meaning: La astrología es el estudio de las estrellas y la creencia de que influyen en la vida.
spanish_example: Jack, que estudia astrología, cree que las estrellas pueden predecir el futuro.

english_meaning: A couple is made of two things that go together.
english_example: There were a couple of problems I had to solve.
spanish_meaning: Una pareja o par son dos cosas que van juntas.
spanish_example: Había un par de problemas que tenía que resolver.

english_meaning: To deviate is to move away from your proper course.
english_example: Very few deviate much from the average.
spanish_meaning: Desviarse es alejarse de la ruta correcta.
spanish_example: Pocos se desvían mucho del promedio.

english_meaning: To differentiate things or people is to show the difference between them.
english_example: It was hard to differentiate between the identical twins.
spanish_meaning: Diferenciar cosas o personas es mostrar la diferencia entre ellas.
spanish_example: Fue difícil diferenciar a los gemelos idénticos.

english_meaning: To disrupt something or someone is to prevent them from working.
english_example: The loud crash disrupted the class lecture.
spanish_meaning: Interrumpir algo o a alguien es evitar que funcione.
spanish_example: El fuerte estruendo interrumpió la clase.

english_meaning: An equation is a math operation to determine the value of something.
english_example: I used the Pythagorean theorem to solve the equation.
spanish_meaning: Una ecuación es una operación matemática para determinar un valor.
spanish_example: Usé el teorema de Pitágoras para resolver la ecuación.

english_meaning: To err means to make a mistake.
english_example: The pilot erred in his estimate of the time it would take to make the trip.
spanish_meaning: Errar significa cometer un error.
spanish_example: El piloto erró al estimar cuánto tiempo tomaría el viaje.

english_meaning: When something is erroneous, it is incorrect or only partly correct.
english_example: The child held the erroneous belief that time machines were real.
spanish_meaning: Erróneo significa incorrecto o solo parcialmente correcto.
spanish_example: El niño tenía la creencia errónea de que las máquinas del tiempo eran reales.

english_meaning: If people or things are frantic, they behave in a wild way because they are frightened.
english_example: The cat became frantic when I tried to give it a bath.
spanish_meaning: Frenético es comportarse de forma descontrolada por miedo.
spanish_example: El gato se puso frenético cuando intenté bañarlo.

english_meaning: When an action is inadvertent, it is done without realizing what you are doing.
english_example: She made an inadvertent error when she knocked over the nail polish.
spanish_meaning: Inadvertido es una acción hecha sin darse cuenta.
spanish_example: Ella cometió un error inadvertido al derribar el esmalte de uñas.

english_meaning: To improvise something is to do it with whatever is available or without planning.
english_example: There was no meat for the pizza, so we improvised with what was in the fridge.
spanish_meaning: Improvisar algo es hacerlo con lo disponible, sin planificar.
spanish_example: No había carne para la pizza, así que improvisamos con lo que había en la nevera.

english_meaning: The latitude of a place is its distance from the equator.
english_example: The device was able to tell the traveler his exact latitude.
spanish_meaning: La latitud de un lugar es su distancia al ecuador.
spanish_example: El dispositivo podía decir al viajero su latitud exacta.

english_meaning: A mariner is a sailor.
english_example: The old mariner used his telescope to find the shore.
spanish_meaning: Un marinero es una persona que navega en el mar.
spanish_example: El viejo marinero usó su telescopio para encontrar la costa.

english_meaning: A multitude of things or people is a very large number of them.
english_example: A multitude of people were waiting at the airport.
spanish_meaning: Una multitud es un número muy grande de cosas o personas.
spanish_example: Una multitud de gente esperaba en el aeropuerto.

english_meaning: A nuisance is a person or thing that is annoying or causes a lot of problems.
english_example: The teenager considered her noisy little brothers to be quite a nuisance.
spanish_meaning: Una molestia es una persona o cosa que resulta irritante o problemática.
spanish_example: La hermana adolescente veía a sus hermanos ruidosos como una gran molestia.

english_meaning: The permanence of something is its ability to last forever.
english_example: Poor results threaten the permanence of the new system.
spanish_meaning: La permanencia de algo es su capacidad de durar para siempre.
spanish_example: Malos resultados amenazan la permanencia del nuevo sistema.

english_meaning: To revolve around something is to keep it as the main feature or focus.
english_example: My life revolves around sports.
spanish_meaning: Revolverse en torno a algo es tomarlo como tema central.
spanish_example: Mi vida gira en torno al deporte.

english_meaning: To soothe means to calm someone who is angry or upset.
english_example: The mother soothed her crying baby by rocking him in her arms.
spanish_meaning: Calmar significa tranquilizar a alguien enojado o alterado.
spanish_example: La madre calmó a su bebé llorando al mecerlo en sus brazos.

english_meaning: If someone is stranded, they are prevented from leaving a place.
english_example: When the plane left, my sister and I were stranded in China.
spanish_meaning: Estar varado es no poder salir de un lugar.
spanish_example: Cuando el avión despegó, mi hermana y yo quedamos varados en China.

english_meaning: A technique is a way of doing something.
english_example: There are a lot of teaching techniques that are well supported by research.
spanish_meaning: Una técnica es una forma de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Hay muchas técnicas de enseñanza bien respaldadas por la investigación.

english_meaning: If something or someone is absurd, they are ridiculous.
english_example: That group of people making animal noises sounds completely absurd.
spanish_meaning: Absuro significa ridículo.
spanish_example: Ese grupo haciendo ruidos de animales suena completamente absurdo.

english_meaning: The aristocracy is the highest class of people in certain societies.
english_example: Most members of the aristocracy were very well fed.
spanish_meaning: La aristocracia es la clase más alta de personas en ciertas sociedades.
spanish_example: La mayoría de la aristocracia estaba bien alimentada.

english_meaning: An aristocrat is a person who is of the highest class in certain societies.
english_example: The aristocrat did not need a job because his family was wealthy.
spanish_meaning: Un aristócrata es una persona de la clase más alta en ciertas sociedades.
spanish_example: El aristócrata no necesitaba un trabajo porque su familia era rica.

english_meaning: Attire is nice or special clothing.
english_example: Everyone wore their best attire to the president’s daughter’s wedding.
spanish_meaning: El atuendo es ropa bonita o especial.
spanish_example: Todos vestían su mejor atuendo para la boda de la hija del presidente.

english_meaning: A craze is a brief and popular activity or object.
english_example: Wearing bright red socks was a craze when I was in high school.
spanish_meaning: Una moda es una actividad u objeto de gran popularidad pero breve.
spanish_example: Llevar calcetines rojos era una moda cuando estaba en la secundaria.

english_meaning: When one thing denotes another, it stands as a sign or substitute for that thing.
english_example: Quotation marks are used to denote speech.
spanish_meaning: Denotar significa servir como signo o sustituto de otra cosa.
spanish_example: Las comillas se usan para denotar el habla.

english_meaning: To enlarge something means to make it bigger.
english_example: The classrooms were enlarged over the summer to make room for more students.
spanish_meaning: Agrandar algo significa hacerlo más grande.
spanish_example: Las aulas se agrandaron en verano para caber más estudiantes.

english_meaning: An excess is an amount of something that is more than needed or wanted.
english_example: Because it never got cold that winter, many stores had an excess of coats.
spanish_meaning: Un exceso es una cantidad mayor de la necesaria o deseada.
spanish_example: Ese invierno muchas tiendas tuvieron un exceso de abrigos.

english_meaning: If something is feminine, then it has qualities that are commonly related to women.
english_example: Many of the older people thought his long hair made him look too feminine.
spanish_meaning: Femenino significa tener cualidades que se asocian comúnmente con mujeres.
spanish_example: Muchos pensaban que su cabello largo lo hacía parecer demasiado femenino.

english_meaning: A hierarchy is a system of things or people ranked one above the other.
english_example: Everyone knows where they fit in the hierarchy.
spanish_meaning: Una jerarquía es un sistema de personas u objetos ordenados de mayor a menor.
spanish_example: Todos saben en qué lugar están dentro de la jerarquía.

english_meaning: A pad is a thick piece of soft material used to protect or clean things.
english_example: Football players wear shoulder pads to keep them safe.
spanish_meaning: Una almohadilla es una pieza blanda y gruesa para proteger o limpiar.
spanish_example: Los jugadores de fútbol usan hombreras para protegerse.

english_meaning: Your partner is someone who lives or works with you as an equal.
english_example: Ladies and gentlemen, take your partners for the next dance.
spanish_meaning: Tu pareja es alguien que vive o trabaja contigo como igual.
spanish_example: Buenas noches, tomen a sus parejas para el próximo baile.

english_meaning: If something is predominant, then it is the most important, common, or strongest.
english_example: Before cars were invented, horses were the predominant method of travel.
spanish_meaning: Predominante significa lo más importante, común o fuerte.
spanish_example: Antes de que inventaran los autos, los caballos eran el método predominante de viaje.

english_meaning: Protocol is a system of rules and customs that guides how things are done.
english_example: The introduction of the guests followed the normal protocol.
spanish_meaning: El protocolo es un sistema de reglas y costumbres que guía cómo se hacen las cosas.
spanish_example: La llegada de los huéspedes siguió el protocolo normal.

english_meaning: If someone or something is reputable, then they have a good reputation.
english_example: The service from the less-than-reputable company made her angry.
spanish_meaning: Alguien o algo de buena reputación es respetado y confiable.
spanish_example: El servicio de la empresa de mala reputación la enfadó.

english_meaning: To signify means to be a symbol of something.
english_example: A red octagon is used to signify a place to stop.
spanish_meaning: Significar significa ser símbolo o representación de algo.
spanish_example: Un octágono rojo se usa para significar una zona de alto.

english_meaning: A strap is a thin long piece of fabric used to fasten, carry, or hold something.
english_example: She put the strap of her purse over her shoulder and walked out the door.
spanish_meaning: Una correa es una tira fina de tela o material para sujetar o sostener algo.
spanish_example: Ella puso la correa de su bolso sobre el hombro y salió.

english_meaning: Your style is the way you do things.
english_example: Her clothes were all in the latest style.
spanish_meaning: El estilo es la manera en que haces las cosas.
spanish_example: Su ropa estaba en el estilo más moderno.

english_meaning: A tangle is something or many things twisted together.
english_example: The laces of his shoes were in such a tangle that he could not untie them.
spanish_meaning: Un enredo es algo o muchas cosas enredadas juntas.
spanish_example: Los cordones de sus zapatos estaban en un gran enredo.

english_meaning: Vanity is excessive pride or love of one’s own appearance or things one has done.
english_example: Her vanity won’t allow her to pass a mirror without looking at herself.
spanish_meaning: La vanidad es orgullo excesivo por la propia apariencia o logros.
spanish_example: Su vanidad no la deja pasar ante un espejo sin mirarse.

english_meaning: If something goes ashore, it goes from the water to the land.
english_example: After a long day of fishing, Glen pulled his boat ashore, so it wouldn’t float away.
spanish_meaning: A la orilla significa pasar del agua a tierra firme.
spanish_example: Tras un día de pesca, Glen llevó su bote a la orilla para que no se fuera.

english_meaning: To contradict means to state the opposite of what someone else has said.
english_example: Ken was always fighting with his little sister because she kept contradicting him.
spanish_meaning: Contradecir significa decir lo contrario de lo que otra persona dijo.
spanish_example: Ken siempre peleaba con su hermana porque ella lo contradijo.

english_meaning: A counterpart is something that is very similar to something else in what it does.
english_example: Our manager will meet our rival company’s counterpart later today.
spanish_meaning: Un homólogo es algo muy similar a algo más en función.
spanish_example: Más tarde hoy se reunirá con el homólogo de la empresa rival.

english_meaning: When something or someone is devoid of a thing, they are missing it.
english_example: The movie was devoid of any violence, so it was a perfect movie for the family.
spanish_meaning: Cuando alguien o algo está carente de algo, le falta ese algo.
spanish_example: La película estaba carente de violencia, así que era ideal para la familia.

english_meaning: To diverge is to become different or to follow a different direction.
english_example: The road diverged into two paths that led to our houses.
spanish_meaning: Divergir es alejarse de una dirección apropiada.
spanish_example: El camino se dividió en dos rutas que conducían a nuestras casas.

english_meaning: An embryo is a human or animal that is still growing inside its mother.
english_example: Some doctors say that what a mother eats has a big effect on her embryo.
spanish_meaning: Un embrión es un ser humano o animal que todavía crece en la madre.
spanish_example: Algunos médicos dicen que lo que come la madre afecta mucho a su embrión.

english_meaning: When something finally happens, it happens at the end of a series of events.
english_example: We finally reached our destination.
spanish_meaning: Finalmente significa que algo ocurre al final de una serie de eventos.
spanish_example: Finalmente llegamos a nuestro destino.

english_meaning: A gazette is a newspaper.
english_example: Alice wants to write for a gazette when she’s older.
spanish_meaning: Una gaceta es un periódico.
spanish_example: Alice quiere escribir para una gaceta cuando sea mayor.

english_meaning: If something is homogeneous, it is made up of things which are all the same.
english_example: All of the houses on Victor’s block were boring and homogenous.
spanish_meaning: Homogéneo significa que está hecho de cosas todas iguales.
spanish_example: Todas las casas de la calle de Victor eran aburridas y homogéneas.

english_meaning: An individual is a single member of a group.
english_example: Each individual is responsible for themselves.
spanish_meaning: Un individuo es un miembro de un grupo.
spanish_example: Cada individuo es responsable de sí mismo.

english_meaning: A major event is a very important event.
english_example: There had been a major shift in government policy.
spanish_meaning: Algo mayor es muy importante o grande.
spanish_example: Hubo un cambio mayor en la política gubernamental.

english_meaning: To obstruct something means to get in its way.
english_example: The car broke down on the road and obstructed traffic for hours.
spanish_meaning: Obstruir algo significa estorbar su camino.
spanish_example: El coche se descompuso y obstruyó el tráfico durante horas.

english_meaning: To plunge means to move down into something very quickly.
english_example: The water in the pool was very cold, but the boy plunged in anyway.
spanish_meaning: Sumergirse es moverse rápidamente hacia abajo o dentro de algo.
spanish_example: El agua de la piscina estaba muy fría, pero el niño se sumergió de todas formas.

english_meaning: To prolong means to make something last for a longer time.
english_example: Sandy walked slowly across the beach, trying to prolong her lunch break.
spanish_meaning: Prolongar significa hacer que algo dure más tiempo.
spanish_example: Sandy caminó lento por la playa para prolongar su receso.

english_meaning: To publicize is to make something get a lot of attention.
english_example: The company publicized the job positions in the newspaper.
spanish_meaning: Publicitar es hacer que algo reciba mucha atención.
spanish_example: La empresa publicitó las vacantes de trabajo en el periódico.

english_meaning: A surplus is an extra amount of something.
english_example: The store sold their surplus items on sale.
spanish_meaning: Un excedente es una cantidad extra de algo.
spanish_example: La tienda vendió sus productos excedentes con descuento.

english_meaning: When something survives, it manages to continue to live in spite of difficult circumstances.
english_example: She survived a heart attack.
spanish_meaning: Sobrevivir significa continuar viviendo pese a dificultades.
spanish_example: Ella sobrevivió a un infarto.

english_meaning: To theorize means to develop ideas about something.
english_example: They enjoyed theorizing about how things worked.
spanish_meaning: Teorizar significa desarrollar ideas sobre algo.
spanish_example: Disfrutaron teorizar sobre cómo funcionaban las cosas.

english_meaning: To verify means to find out if something is true.
english_example: Julian called the movie theater to verify that the movie started at nine.
spanish_meaning: Verificar significa averiguar si algo es cierto.
spanish_example: Julio llamó al cine para verificar que la función empezaba a las nueve.

english_meaning: If something or someone is vigorous, they use a lot of energy.
english_example: Henry uses vigorous exercise to keep himself in shape.
spanish_meaning: Vigoroso significa usar mucha energía.
spanish_example: Henry hace ejercicio vigoroso para mantenerse en forma.

english_meaning: An altar is a table used in churches.
english_example: The altar had many lit candles on it.
spanish_meaning: Un altar es una mesa usada en iglesias.
spanish_example: El altar tenía muchas velas encendidas.

english_meaning: Arthritis is an illness causing pain and swelling in a person’s joints.
english_example: Agatha couldn’t play the piano anymore because of her arthritis.
spanish_meaning: La artritis es una enfermedad que causa dolor y hinchazón en las articulaciones.
spanish_example: Agatha no pudo tocar el piano por su artritis.

english_meaning: Botany is the study of plants.
english_example: Gardens are the best places for botany.
spanish_meaning: La botánica es el estudio de las plantas.
spanish_example: Los jardines son los mejores lugares para la botánica.

english_meaning: If something or someone is credible, they can be believed or trusted.
english_example: Dick gave a credible reason for being late and didn’t get in any trouble.
spanish_meaning: Creíble significa que puede creerse o confiarse.
spanish_example: Dick dio un motivo creíble por llegar tarde y no recibió problemas.

english_meaning: If someone is deceased, they are dead.
english_example: We visited the graves of our deceased grandparents.
spanish_meaning: Fallecido significa que alguien está muerto.
spanish_example: Visitamos las tumbas de nuestros abuelos fallecidos.

english_meaning: Deception is the act of lying or tricking someone.
english_example: The magic looked very real, but it was only deception.
spanish_meaning: El engaño es el acto de mentir o engañar a alguien.
spanish_example: La magia parecía real, pero era solo un engaño.

english_meaning: A document is a written text, usually not in the form of a book.
english_example: Please scan this document so we have an electronic copy.
spanish_meaning: Un documento es un texto escrito, que generalmente no es un libro.
spanish_example: Escanea ese documento para que tengamos copia electrónica.

english_meaning: Dung is solid waste material produced by animals.
english_example: There was cow dung all over the field.
spanish_meaning: El estiércol es material sólido de desecho producido por animales.
spanish_example: Había estiércol de vaca en todo el campo.

english_meaning: Dusk is the time in the evening when it begins to get dark.
english_example: After dusk, Hannah went to catch fireflies in the park.
spanish_meaning: El anochecer es la hora de la tarde en que empieza a oscurecer.
spanish_example: Después del anochecer, Hannah salió a cazar luciérnagas al parque.

english_meaning: To gratify someone means to please them.
english_example: Bonnie was gratified after receiving her gift from her parents.
spanish_meaning: Gratificar a alguien significa darle satisfacción.
spanish_example: Bonnie quedó gratificada al recibir su regalo de los padres.

english_meaning: To hone something is to improve it and make it very good.
english_example: Lisa honed her chess skills through hours and hours of practice.
spanish_meaning: Pulir algo significa mejorarlo y hacerlo excelente.
spanish_example: Lisa pulió sus habilidades de ajedrez durante muchas horas de práctica.

english_meaning: When you interpret something, you find its meaning, often by changing it into a different language.
english_example: It is difficult to interpret while someone is speaking.
spanish_meaning: Interpretar algo significa encontrar su significado, a menudo cambiándolo de idioma.
spanish_example: Es difícil interpretar mientras alguien esté hablando.

english_meaning: Your motive is your reason for doing something.
english_example: We can show he had a motive to commit the crime, but we cannot show he did it.
spanish_meaning: El motivo es la razón para hacer algo.
spanish_example: Podemos mostrar que tenía un motivo para cometer el crimen, pero no probar que lo hizo.

english_meaning: Pneumonia is a dangerous illness causing the lungs to fill with liquid.
english_example: Elaine got pneumonia after playing outside in the rain without a coat.
spanish_meaning: La neumonía es una enfermedad grave que llena los pulmones de líquido.
spanish_example: Elaine contrajo neumonía tras jugar bajo la lluvia sin abrigo.

english_meaning: When someone is psychic, they know what will happen or what people think.
english_example: I think my grandmother has psychic abilities because she can predict anything.
spanish_meaning: Psíquico es alguien que sabe lo que ocurrirá o piensa la gente.
spanish_example: Creo que mi abuela tiene capacidades psíquicas porque predice mucho.

english_meaning: If someone is psychotic, they have a very serious mental illness.
english_example: The psychotic patient believed he saw things that weren’t real.
spanish_meaning: Psicótico se refiere a una enfermedad mental muy grave.
spanish_example: El paciente psicótico creía ver cosas que no eran reales.

english_meaning: The scope of something is how many people or things it relates to.
english_example: Modern history has a wide scope.
spanish_meaning: El alcance de algo es a cuántas personas o cosas afecta.
spanish_example: La historia moderna tiene un alcance amplio.

english_meaning: If something or someone is sinister, they are evil.
english_example: Bad guys in most movies have sinister laughs.
spanish_meaning: Siniestro significa malvado o amenazador.
spanish_example: Los villanos suelen tener risas siniestras en las películas.

english_meaning: Strife is disagreement or fighting between people or groups.
english_example: There was a lot of strife between Jim and Lisa about what TV show to watch.
spanish_meaning: El conflicto es el desacuerdo o pelea entre personas o grupos.
spanish_example: Había mucha riña entre Jim y Lisa por qué programa ver.

english_meaning: Text is language used in communicating messages.
english_example: Spoken text has many features which are different from written text.
spanish_meaning: El texto es el lenguaje usado para comunicarse en mensajes.
spanish_example: El texto hablado tiene características distintas al texto escrito.

english_meaning: An artery is a tube that takes blood from the heart to the rest of the body.
english_example: Eating healthily keeps your arteries clean so blood can flow with ease.
spanish_meaning: Una arteria es un conducto que lleva sangre del corazón al resto del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Comer sano mantiene las arterias limpias para que la sangre fluya con facilidad.

english_meaning: Deterioration is the act of becoming worse.
english_example: The lack of care led to the deterioration of the house.
spanish_meaning: Deterioro es el proceso de empeorar algo.
spanish_example: La falta de cuidado llevó al deterioro de la casa.

english_meaning: If things or people are elusive, they are hard to find.
english_example: The elusive fish only came out at night.
spanish_meaning: Esquivo significa difícil de encontrar.
spanish_example: El pez esquivo solo salía de noche.

english_meaning: When you ensure something, you make sure it happens.
english_example: Saving now will ensure we have enough money for our retirement.
spanish_meaning: Asegurar algo significa hacer que suceda.
spanish_example: Ahorrar ahora asegurará tener suficiente dinero para la jubilación.

english_meaning: To induce something means to make it happen.
english_example: Running without good shoes may induce leg pain.
spanish_meaning: Inducir algo significa hacer que ocurra.
spanish_example: Correr sin buenos zapatos puede inducir dolor de piernas.

english_meaning: If two things are inseparable, they can’t be separated.
english_example: Denise and Diana have been inseparable since they first met.
spanish_meaning: Inseparable significa que no se puede separar.
spanish_example: Denise y Diana han sido inseparables desde que se conocieron.

english_meaning: If something is invalid, it is not correct.
english_example: The way to winning debates is to avoid invalid arguments.
spanish_meaning: Inválido significa no correcto.
spanish_example: Usar argumentos inválidos hace que pierdas una discusión.

english_meaning: To magnify something means to make it look bigger than it really is.
english_example: Astronomers use telescopes to magnify faraway stars.
spanish_meaning: Magnificar algo significa hacerlo ver más grande de lo que es.
spanish_example: Los astrónomos usan telescopios para magnificar estrellas lejanas.

english_meaning: The mainstream is what is considered normal and accepted by most people.
english_example: Before punk rock music became part of the mainstream, only a few people liked it.
spanish_meaning: La corriente principal es lo que se considera normal y aceptado por la mayoría.
spanish_example: Antes de que el punk rock fuera mainstream, pocos lo escuchaban.

english_meaning: A microbe is a very small living thing that often makes people sick.
english_example: The first step in curing a disease is finding the microbe that causes it.
spanish_meaning: Un microbio es un ser vivo muy pequeño que suele enfermar a las personas.
spanish_example: El primer paso para curar una enfermedad es encontrar el microbio causante.

english_meaning: A minor problem is a very small and unimportant problem.
english_example: This is a minor problem.
spanish_meaning: Un problema menor es pequeño e importante poco.
spanish_example: Este es un problema menor.

english_meaning: If something is negligible, it is extremely small and not important.
english_example: The amount she owed was negligible. It was less than ten cents.
spanish_meaning: Insignificante significa extremadamente pequeño y sin importancia.
spanish_example: Lo que debía no era significativo: era menos de diez centavos.

english_meaning: Paralysis is the loss of the ability to move all or part of your body.
english_example: His paralysis meant that he had to use a wheelchair to get around.
spanish_meaning: La parálisis es la pérdida de la capacidad de mover todo o parte del cuerpo.
spanish_example: Su parálisis hizo que tuviera que usar silla de ruedas.

english_meaning: A pest is an animal or insect that hurts plants or food.
english_example: Many farmers put up scarecrows to keep pests of their corn.
spanish_meaning: Una plaga es un animal o insecto que daña plantas o alimentos.
spanish_example: Muchos granjeros ponen espantapájaros para alejar plagas de maíz.

english_meaning: To prevail means to be accepted or very common.
english_example: Unfortunately, injustice still prevails in the country’s court system.
spanish_meaning: Prevalecer significa ser aceptado o muy común.
spanish_example: Por desgracia, la injusticia todavía prevalece en el sistema judicial del país.

english_meaning: When you are in pursuit of something, you are chasing it.
english_example: The pursuit of happiness is a basic human right.
spanish_meaning: La persecución es perseguir algo o alguien.
spanish_example: La búsqueda de la felicidad es un derecho humano básico.

english_meaning: To rupture means to tear or burst open.
english_example: Sarah put too much water into the balloon, and it ruptured.
spanish_meaning: Ruptura significa desgarrar o romper de forma brusca.
spanish_example: Sara metió demasiada agua en el globo y éste se rompió.

english_meaning: If things or people are savage, they are very violent or cruel.
english_example: Wild animals are very savage when defending their babies.
spanish_meaning: Algo salvaje es muy violento o cruel.
spanish_example: Los animales son muy salvajes al defender a sus crías.

english_meaning: To stun someone means to make them unable to speak due to surprise or shock.
english_example: Tim fell of the swing and was too stunned to cry.
spanish_meaning: Aturdir a alguien significa dejarlo sin hablar por sorpresa o impacto.
spanish_example: Tim se cayó del columpio y quedó tan aturdido que no pudo llorar.

english_meaning: If one is susceptible to something like a disease, they are easily harmed by it.
english_example: Not eating enough vegetables makes you more susceptible to getting sick.
spanish_meaning: Susceptible a algo como una enfermedad significa ser afectado fácilmente.
spanish_example: No comer suficientes verduras te hace más susceptible a enfermarte.

english_meaning: To advocate a plan or idea is to support or suggest it in public.
english_example: The group advocated increased spending on education and schools.
spanish_meaning: Abogar por un plan o idea es apoyarlo públicamente.
spanish_example: El grupo abogó por más presupuesto para educación y escuelas.

english_meaning: Albeit means although. (It is made of three parts – al(though), be, it.
english_example: She was happy, albeit briefly.
spanish_meaning: Aunque significa pese a ello.
spanish_example: Ella estaba feliz, aunque de forma breve.

english_meaning: To authorize something means to give permission for it.
english_example: The mayor authorized the construction of a new statue in the park.
spanish_meaning: Autorizar significa dar permiso.
spanish_example: El alcalde autorizó la construcción de una nueva estatua en el parque.

english_meaning: A civilian is someone who is not in the military.
english_example: It was Todd’s job to keep civilians from entering the army facility.
spanish_meaning: Civil es alguien que no pertenece al ejército.
spanish_example: El trabajo de Todd fue mantener a los civiles fuera de la base militar.

english_meaning: A commodity is something that can be bought or sold.
english_example: Fran’s uncle made most of his money trading commodities.
spanish_meaning: Una mercancía es algo que se puede comprar o vender.
spanish_example: El tío de Fran ganó mucho dinero comerciando mercancías.

english_meaning: When we communicate with others, we talk or write to them.
english_example: He was unable to communicate his feelings clearly.
spanish_meaning: Comunicar con otros significa hablarles o escribirles.
spanish_example: No pudo comunicar claramente sus sentimientos.

english_meaning: A conquest is an event by which one country takes over another country.
english_example: After the conquest, the people of the small city had no freedom.
spanish_meaning: Una conquista es cuando un país toma control de otro.
spanish_example: Tras la conquista, la gente de la pequeña ciudad perdió la libertad.

english_meaning: To disclose something means to tell it to someone else.
english_example: John came home late, so he had to disclose his activities to his wife.
spanish_meaning: Revelar algo significa contarlo a alguien más.
spanish_example: Al llegar tarde, John tuvo que revelar sus actividades a su esposa.

english_meaning: The dynamics of a situation are the way that parts of it affect each other.
english_example: A good psychologist needs to know a lot about the dynamics of brain disorders.
spanish_meaning: La dinámica de una situación es la forma en que sus partes se afectan entre sí.
spanish_example: Un buen psicólogo debe conocer la dinámica de trastornos mentales.

english_meaning: To enroll in something is to put one’s name on a list as a member of a group.
english_example: The students had to enroll in the class one semester in advance.
spanish_meaning: Inscribirse en algo es poner el nombre en una lista como miembro.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes tenían que inscribirse en la clase un semestre antes.

english_meaning: When someone is envious, they want something that another person has.
english_example: They were envious of their neighbor’s front yard.
spanish_meaning: Envidioso significa querer lo que otra persona tiene.
spanish_example: Estaban envidiosos del jardín delantero de su vecino.

english_meaning: When something is festive, it is happy and related to a party or celebration.
english_example: Dean’s favorite part of Christmas is the festive clothing that people wear.
spanish_meaning: Festivo es alegre y relacionado con una fiesta o celebración.
spanish_example: Lo mejor de la Navidad para Dean es la ropa festiva que la gente usa.

english_meaning: When we indicate something, we show or point out our thoughts or plans.
english_example: They indicated that things would be likely to improve soon.
spanish_meaning: Indicar algo significa mostrar o señalar pensamientos o planes.
spanish_example: Indicaron que las cosas probablemente mejorarían pronto.

english_meaning: Lentils are very small beans that people cook and eat.
english_example: Danni made her special soup with lentils when her husband was sick.
spanish_meaning: La lenteja es un grano muy pequeño que la gente cocina y come.
spanish_example: Danni hizo su sopa especial con lentejas cuando su esposo estaba enfermo.

english_meaning: To marshal a group means to assemble them in order.
english_example: The students were marshaled outside and put into groups.
spanish_meaning: Organizar un grupo significa reunirlo de forma ordenada.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes fueron organizados afuera y separados en grupos.

english_meaning: Morale is the amount of hope that people have during a difficult situation.
english_example: To improve our family’s morale, mom suggested going on a picnic.
spanish_meaning: La moral es la cantidad de esperanza que la gente tiene en una situación difícil.
spanish_example: Para mejorar la moral de la familia, mamá sugirió ir de picnic.

english_meaning: Your mood is the way you are feeling.
english_example: My boss is in a really bad mood today.
spanish_meaning: El estado de ánimo es la manera en que te sientes.
spanish_example: Mi jefe está hoy de muy mal humor.

english_meaning: A prophecy is a prediction about what will happen in the future.
english_example: Some people believe that prophecies are actually true.
spanish_meaning: Una profecía es una predicción de lo que ocurrirá en el futuro.
spanish_example: Algunas personas creen que las profecías son ciertas.

english_meaning: A senate is a part of the government in some countries.
english_example: Everybody liked Caroline and chose her to speak for them in the senate.
spanish_meaning: El senado es parte del gobierno en algunos países.
spanish_example: Todo el mundo quería que Caroline hablara por ellos en el senado.

english_meaning: Unrest is a state of anger about something among the people in a place.
english_example: High taxes caused much of the unrest that led to the Revolutionary War.
spanish_meaning: La agitación es un estado de enojo entre la gente de un lugar.
spanish_example: Los altos impuestos causaron mucha agitación y la guerra revolucionaria.

english_meaning: To certify something means to confirm that its results are true.
english_example: The photograph on her passport certified that she was indeed Jolene Sawyer.
spanish_meaning: Certificar algo significa confirmar que sus resultados son verdaderos.
spanish_example: La foto de su pasaporte certificaba que ella era Jolene Sawyer.

english_meaning: The parts of a coherent plan fit well together.
english_example: This was a coherent program for solving the flooding problem.
spanish_meaning: Las partes de un programa coherente encajan bien entre sí.
spanish_example: Era un programa coherente para resolver el problema de inundaciones.

english_meaning: To collaborate means to work together on something.
english_example: When they collaborated, they managed to finish their chores early.
spanish_meaning: Colaborar significa trabajar juntos en algo.
spanish_example: Cuando colaboraron, lograron terminar sus tareas pronto.

english_meaning: To compile things means to collect a variety of them into a group.
english_example: She compiled a list of people who she wanted to attend her birthday party.
spanish_meaning: Compilar cosas significa reunir una variedad de elementos en un conjunto.
spanish_example: Ella compiló una lista de personas que quería en su cumpleaños.

english_meaning: To curb something means to prevent it from happening or increasing.
english_example: She curbed her anger by listening to a relaxing song.
spanish_meaning: Frenar algo significa prevenir que ocurra o aumente.
spanish_example: Ella frenó su enojo al escuchar una canción relajante.

english_meaning: To diagnose someone means to identify the medical condition they have.
english_example: Several of the children were diagnosed with the flu.
spanish_meaning: Diagnosticar a alguien significa identificar su condición médica.
spanish_example: Varios niños fueron diagnosticados con gripe.

english_meaning: To enact something means to make it into a law.
english_example: The council enacted a law that would only allow buses to drive downtown.
spanish_meaning: Promulgar algo significa convertirlo en ley.
spanish_example: El consejo promulgó una ley que solo permitía buses en el centro.

english_meaning: A federation is a group of states or businesses working for a common cause.
english_example: The United Nations is a federation designed to prevent war, disease, and famine.
spanish_meaning: Una federación es un grupo de estados o negocios que trabaja por un fin común.
spanish_example: Las Naciones Unidas es una federación creada para prevenir guerras y hambrunas.

english_meaning: If something is gross, then it is disgusting.
english_example: The food was so gross that the dog couldn’t eat it without feeling sick.
spanish_meaning: Repugnante significa asqueroso o desagradable.
spanish_example: La comida era tan repugnante que el perro no pudo comerla.

english_meaning: If something is humane, then it is good and kind.
english_example: Helping build homes for poor people is very humane.
spanish_meaning: Humano es bueno y compasivo con las personas.
spanish_example: Ayudar a construir casas para pobres es muy humano.

english_meaning: If someone is needy, they are very poor.
english_example: After he lost his job, he became very needy.
spanish_meaning: Necesitado significa pobre o con carencias económicas.
spanish_example: Después de perder su trabajo, se volvió muy necesitado.

english_meaning: The onset of something unpleasant is the beginning of it.
english_example: At the onset of the battle, the enemy wasn’t prepared for such a large attack.
spanish_meaning: El inicio de algo desagradable es el comienzo de ese algo.
spanish_example: Al inicio de la batalla, el enemigo no estaba preparado para un ataque tan grande.

english_meaning: To pledge means to make a promise to do something.
english_example: Her mother pledged that she would find her daughter’s lost kitten.
spanish_meaning: Prometer significa hacer una promesa de hacer algo.
spanish_example: Su madre prometió encontrar el gatito perdido de ella.

english_meaning: To prohibit something means to not allow it.
english_example: She prohibited the students from speaking until their work was done.
