english_meaning: If a person or animal is sly, they are sneaky or good at tricking people.
english_example: The sly fox stole the eggs from the nest.
spanish_meaning: Una persona astuta es taimada o lista.
spanish_example: El zorro astuto robo los huevos del nido.

english_meaning: To tend to do something is to be likely to do it or to do it often.
english_example: My mom tends to buy me the perfect gift each Christmas.
spanish_meaning: Soler significa hacerlo con frecuencia.
spanish_example: Mi madre suele comprarme un regalo perfecto cada Navidad.

english_meaning: When something is valid, it is correct or based on good reasoning.
english_example: The expert’s opinion on the subject was more valid than others.
spanish_meaning: Algo válido es correcto o está bien fundamentado.
spanish_example: La opinión del experto fue más válida que la de otros.

english_meaning: A version is an account of something that differs slightly from the original.
english_example: She read the students British version of the Chinese fairy tale.
spanish_meaning: Una versión es un relato o forma que difiere un poco del original.
spanish_example: Ella leyó la versión británica del cuento chino.

english_meaning: You use whatsoever after a noun to emphasize that there is nothing of that thing.
english_example: The boy had no idea whatsoever how to finish the story.
spanish_meaning: No significa nada la presencia de esa cosa.
spanish_example: El chico no tenía idea en absoluto de cómo terminar la historia.

english_meaning: If something is alongside another thing, then it is next to it.
english_example: We work alongside each other in the office.
spanish_meaning: Al lado de significa junto a algo.
spanish_example: Trabajamos al lado uno del otro en la oficina.

english_meaning: Your appetite is your hunger for food.
english_example: Tom has a big appetite. He eats all the time.
spanish_meaning: El apetito es hambre o deseo de comer.
spanish_example: Tom tiene mucho apetito.

english_meaning: To assist someone is to help them.
english_example: Andrew assisted me with my homework.
spanish_meaning: Ayudar significa asistir o prestar apoyo.
spanish_example: Andrew me ayudo con mi tarea.

english_meaning: A breeze is a soft wind.
english_example: The breeze caused the leaves to fall off the tree.
spanish_meaning: La brisa es un viento suave.
spanish_example: La brisa hizo caer las hojas del árbol.

english_meaning: To defy someone is to work against them or refuse to do what they say.
english_example: The students got into trouble for defying their teacher’s rules.
spanish_meaning: Desafiar significa negarse a obedecer.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes se metieron en problemas por desafiar las reglas.

english_meaning: To display something is to show it, especially by putting it in a certain place.
english_example: The museum displayed many wonderful paintings.
spanish_meaning: Mostrar significa exponer o poner a la vista.
spanish_example: El museo mostró muchas pinturas maravillosas.

english_meaning: If something or someone is efficient, they do not waste energy.
english_example: My car is very efficient. I rarely have to buy gas.
spanish_meaning: Algo eficiente consigue resultados sin perder energia.
spanish_example: Mi coche es muy eficiente.

english_meaning: If someone is feeble, they are small or weak.
english_example: The boy was too feeble to carry the object very far.
spanish_meaning: Debil significa pequeño o con poca fuerza.
spanish_example: El niño era demasiado debil para llevar ese objeto.

english_meaning: To forgive someone is to stop being angry with them.
english_example: Sandra forgave Peter after he said he was sorry.
spanish_meaning: Perdonar es dejar de estar enojado con alguien.
spanish_example: Sandra perdono a Peter después de que pidio perdon.

english_meaning: If someone is lively, they have a lot of energy.
english_example: Jennifer is very lively: she’s always running and playing.
spanish_meaning: Vivaz significa lleno de energia.
spanish_example: Jennifer es muy vivaz.

english_meaning: If something is majestic, it is large and impressive.
english_example: The rich people lived in a big, majestic house.
spanish_meaning: Majestuoso significa grande e impresionante.
spanish_example: Vivian tuvo una casa majestuosa.

english_meaning: You use nor to connect two negative ideas.
english_example: I eat neither apples nor oranges. I don’t like either one.
spanish_meaning: Ni une dos ideas negativas.
spanish_example: No como manzanas ni naranjas.

english_meaning: If someone is outraged, they are very angry.
english_example: My father was outraged when he saw that I had crashed his car.
spanish_meaning: Indignado significa sentirse muy enojado y ofendido.
spanish_example: Mi padre se indignó cuando vio que habían chocado su coche.

english_meaning: If someone is pessimistic, they believe that the worst will happen.
english_example: John is pessimistic. He always thinks something bad will happen.
spanish_meaning: Pesimista significa esperar que pase lo peor.
spanish_example: John es pesimista y siempre piensa que pasa algo malo.

english_meaning: The relationship between two or more people is the way in which they behave toward each other.
english_example: The teacher was proud of her good relationship with the students.
spanish_meaning: Una relación es la forma de trato entre personas.
spanish_example: La maestra estaba orgullosa de su buena relación con los estudiantes.

english_meaning: To slap someone means to hit them with the palm of the hand.
english_example: Out of anger, Helen slapped Eunice on the face.
spanish_meaning: Abofetear es golpear con la mano abierta.
spanish_example: Helen abofeteo a Eunice en la cara.

english_meaning: To smash something is to break it into many small pieces.
english_example: Jacob smashed the window with a rock.
spanish_meaning: Derribar o romper en pedazos.
spanish_example: Jacob rompio la ventana con una roca.

english_meaning: Someone who is successful achieves what they intended to achieve.
english_example: My father is a successful businessman.
spanish_meaning: Exitoso significa lograr lo que se quería.
spanish_example: Mi padre es un hombre de negocios exitoso.

english_meaning: A wage is the money that a person gets for doing a job.
english_example: The wages I receive from my job are really great!
spanish_meaning: El salario es dinero por el trabajo.
spanish_example: Las ganancias que recibo son muy grandes.

english_meaning: You use whereas to show how two things are different.
english_example: My sister loves horror movies, whereas I prefer comedies.
spanish_meaning: Mientras que conecta dos ideas contrastantes.
spanish_example: Mi hermana ama peliculas de terror, mientras que yo prefiero comedias.

english_meaning: When something is animate, it has life.
english_example: Rocks and machines are not animate things.
spanish_meaning: Animar significa dar vida o energia.
spanish_example: Las rocas y maquinas no son cosas animadas.

english_meaning: To classify things is to put them into groups based on their type.
english_example: The biologist classified the plant as a completely new species.
spanish_meaning: Clasificar significa separar por tipo.
spanish_example: El biólogo clasifico la planta como especie nueva.

english_meaning: To concede is to admit against your wish that something is true.
english_example: The student conceded that he had cheated on the test.
spanish_meaning: Conceder significa aceptar o admitir.
spanish_example: El estudiante concedió que había hecho trampa.

english_meaning: A concept is an idea about something.
english_example: I learned some concepts about molecules before working in the science lab.
spanish_meaning: Un concepto es una idea.
spanish_example: Aprendi conceptos sobre moleculas.

english_meaning: To construct something means to make or build it.
english_example: The men used wood and metal to construct a house.
spanish_meaning: Construir significa fabricar o levantar.
spanish_example: Los hombres usaron madera y metal para construir una casa.

english_meaning: A decade is a period of ten years.
english_example: She celebrated her three decades of work with the company.
spanish_meaning: Una decada es un período de diez años.
spanish_example: Ella celebro sus tres decadas de trabajo.

english_meaning: A diagram is a simple drawing that explains what something is or how it works.
english_example: By following the diagram, I was able to put the desk together.
spanish_meaning: Un diagrama es un dibujo explicativo.
spanish_example: Siguiendo el diagrama pude armar el escritorio.

english_meaning: A ferry is a boat that carries passengers over short distances.
english_example: The ferry took the people across the lake.
spanish_meaning: Un ferry es un barco que cruza personas y coches por el agua.
spanish_example: El ferry cruzó el lago.

english_meaning: If something is handy, it is useful.
english_example: An eraser is handy if you make a lot of mistakes.
spanish_meaning: Útil significa práctico.
spanish_example: Un borrador es útil.

english_meaning: To isolate is to separate one person or thing from a group.
english_example: The teacher isolated the bad child from the class before talking with her.
spanish_meaning: Aislar significa separar de un grupo.
spanish_example: La maestra aislo a la niña antes de hablar con ella.

english_meaning: A longing is a strong feeling of wanting.
english_example: Since he skipped breakfast, he had a longing for food all morning.
spanish_meaning: Anhelo o deseo intenso.
spanish_example: Al saltar desayuno, tuvo un fuerte deseo de comida.

english_meaning: If something is numerous, there are many of those things.
english_example: It was hard to drive fast since there were numerous holes in the road.
spanish_meaning: Numeroso significa muy grande en cantidad.
spanish_example: Había numerosos hoyos en el camino.

english_meaning: A particle is a very small piece of something.
english_example: The bottles of wine were covered in a layer of dust particles.
spanish_meaning: Una partícula es un pedazo muy pequeño.
spanish_example: Había una capa de partículas de polvo.

english_meaning: A plea is a request that is urgent or emotional.
english_example: The poor, hungry man made a plea for food.
spanish_meaning: Una peticion/petición es un pedido emocional.
spanish_example: El hombre hambriento hizo una peticion de comida.

english_meaning: To refrain from something is to avoid doing it.
english_example: The doctor asked Mary to refrain from eating fast food as part of her diet.
spanish_meaning: Abstenerse es evitar hacerlo.
spanish_example: La doctora pidio que se abstuviera de comida rápida.

english_meaning: A review of something is a formal inspection of it by people in authority.
english_example: The government ordered a careful review of the economic situation.
spanish_meaning: Una revision es una inspeccion formal.
spanish_example: El gobierno ordeno una revision de la economia.

english_meaning: If someone is sophisticated, they know many things about the world.
english_example: Jake is one of the most sophisticated people I’ve ever met.
spanish_meaning: Sophisticado significa refinado o con experiencia.
spanish_example: Jake es una persona muy sofisticada.

english_meaning: To surrender something is to give it up.
english_example: The thief surrendered the money to the police when he was caught.
spanish_meaning: Rendirse significa entregar o darse por vencido.
spanish_example: El ladrón se rindo y entrego el dinero.

english_meaning: If something is upright, it is standing up straight.
english_example: Meerkats can’t walk like humans, but they can stand upright.
spanish_meaning: Erguido significa de pie y derecho.
spanish_example: Las mearcas no caminan como humanos, pero pueden ponerse erguidas.

english_meaning: If something is worthwhile, it is important or useful.
english_example: On his visit to Canada, he realized studying English was worthwhile.
spanish_meaning: Vale la pena es provechoso o importante.
spanish_example: Estudiar ingles fue totalmente valioso.

english_meaning: An alliance is a group of people who work together.
english_example: The junior executives formed an alliance with each other.
spanish_meaning: Una alianza es un grupo que trabaja junto.
spanish_example: Los ejecutivos formaron una alianza.

english_meaning: Applause is the noise made when people clap their hands to show approval.
english_example: At the end of his speech, everyone burst into loud applause.
spanish_meaning: El aplauso es el ruido que hace la gente al aplaudir para mostrar aprobación.
spanish_example: Al final de su discurso, todos estallaron en fuertes aplausos.

english_meaning: If you are armed, you are carrying a weapon.
english_example: The robber was armed with a gun.
spanish_meaning: Armar significa portar un arma.
spanish_example: El ladrón estaba armado con una pistola.

english_meaning: If something is authoritative, it uses the best information available.
english_example: This book is an authoritative source on ancient Egypt’s Queen Nefertiti.
spanish_meaning: Autoritativo significa de autoridad o fiable.
spanish_example: Este libro es una fuente autoritaria.

english_meaning: Bravery is brave behavior.
english_example: The books commended the general’s bravery.
spanish_meaning: La valentía es un comportamiento valiente.
spanish_example: Los libros elogiaron la valentía del general.

english_meaning: A ceremony is an event that happens on special occasions.
english_example: Tom and Amy’s marriage ceremony is in June.
spanish_meaning: Una ceremonia es un evento formal.
spanish_example: La ceremonia de matrimonio fue en junio.

english_meaning: Culture is the music, art, and writings of a certain place or group of people.
english_example: Ancient Mexican culture is renowned for its architecture.
spanish_meaning: La cultura es el arte y las costumbres de un pueblo.
spanish_example: La cultura mexicana es reconocida.

english_meaning: A defense is something that is done to protect another thing.
english_example: The air force works in defense of its country.
spanish_meaning: La defensa es protección.
spanish_example: La fuerza aérea lucha en defensa de su país.

english_meaning: A detail is a small piece of information.
english_example: Jane told Ben all the details of her trip to China.
spanish_meaning: Un detalle es una pieza de informacion.
spanish_example: Jane conto todos los detalles de su viaje.

english_meaning: When a group of things is diverse, it is made up of a wide variety of things.
english_example: Big cities have diverse populations with people of different races and ages.
spanish_meaning: Diverso significa variado.
spanish_example: La ciudad tiene una poblacion diversa.

english_meaning: To enchant someone is to make them feel very interested or happy.
english_example: The beautiful woman enchanted everybody in the room.
spanish_meaning: Encantar significa agradar mucho.
spanish_example: La mujer encanto a todos.

english_meaning: To equip someone is to give them the things needed to do something.
english_example: Steve was equipped with the tools needed to do the job.
spanish_meaning: Equipar significa dar lo necesario.
spanish_example: Steve estaba equipado para el trabajo.

english_meaning: An exception is someone or something not conforming to a rule.
english_example: Most students thought the test was hard, but Tim was the exception.
spanish_meaning: Una excepción es lo que no sigue una regla.
spanish_example: Todos pensaron que era difícil, pero Tim fue una excepción.

english_meaning: A genre is a type of literature, art, or music characterized by its style.
english_example: She is considered a master in the comedy genre.
spanish_meaning: Un genero es un tipo de arte o musica.
spanish_example: Ella es experta en el genero comedia.

english_meaning: An impact is the effect someone or something has.
english_example: My grandmother had a great impact on my life.
spanish_meaning: Impacto es el efecto.
spanish_example: Mi abuela tuvo un gran impacto en mi vida.

english_meaning: To lure someone is to convince them to do something by using a trick.
english_example: The store lures people in with big signs that say “Sale!”
spanish_meaning: Atraer es atraer con algun truco.
spanish_example: El cartel de "Oferta" atrae a las personas.

english_meaning: An obstacle is an object or a problem that stops you from doing something.
english_example: Climbing over the giant rock was the biggest obstacle for the hikers.
spanish_meaning: Un obstaculo es algo que impide.
spanish_example: La roca gigante fue un gran obstaculo.

english_meaning: A shelter is a place to go that is safe from danger or bad weather.
english_example: The frog took shelter from the storm in a nearby hold.
spanish_meaning: Un refugio es un lugar seguro.
spanish_example: La rana se refugio durante la tormenta.

english_meaning: To supply something means to give people what they need or want.
english_example: The water heater supplied the house with warm water.
spanish_meaning: Suministrar significa dar a alguien lo que necesita.
spanish_example: El calefactor suministró agua caliente a la casa.

english_meaning: If people are vain, they are only concerned with how they look.
english_example: Rebecca is so vain that she looks at herself in every mirror.
spanish_meaning: Vanidoso significa preocuparce por la apariencia.
spanish_example: Rebecca es muy vanidosa.

english_meaning: An alternative is something that you can choose instead of your first choice.
english_example: Her first plan to make extra money seemed weak, so she chose an alternative.
spanish_meaning: Alternativa significa otra opcion.
spanish_example: Eligio una alternativa.

english_meaning: An avenue is a road, often a large one with buildings on each side.
english_example: Drive down this avenue and then turn left to get to the park.
spanish_meaning: Avenida es una via ancha urbana.
spanish_example: Baja por está avenida y gira a la izquierda.

english_meaning: The belly is the stomach of a person or animal.
english_example: His belly was full because he had eaten a lot of food.
spanish_meaning: El vientre es la zona del abdomen.
spanish_example: Su vientre estaba lleno porque había comido demasiado.

english_meaning: A bid to do something is an attempt to do it.
english_example: He made a bid to become the university’s next president.
spanish_meaning: Una puja es oferta o apuesta para algo.
spanish_example: Hizo una puja para ser presidente de la universidad.

english_meaning: To blow means to move air or move something through the air, as in the wind.
english_example: The wind is blowing very hard today. We should stay inside.
spanish_meaning: Soplar significa mover aire.
spanish_example: El viento soplaba muy fuerte.

english_meaning: A conflict is a fight between different people or groups.
english_example: The two nations had a conflict over which one could use the water in the river.
spanish_meaning: Conflicto es pelea entre grupos.
spanish_example: Las dos naciones tuvieron un conflicto.

english_meaning: A continent is one of the seven large areas of land on the Earth.
english_example: Asia is the largest continent.
spanish_meaning: Un continente es una gran región de tierra.
spanish_example: Asia es el continente más grande.

english_meaning: A current is a steady and constant flow of air or water in a river or ocean.
english_example: The ocean currents took the ship far off into the sea.
spanish_meaning: Una corriente es flujo continuo de agua o aire.
spanish_example: Las corrientes marinas desviaron el barco.

english_meaning: Disrespect is rudeness or behavior that shows a lack of respect.
english_example: He showed disrespect by arguing with his boss during a meeting.
spanish_meaning: La falta de respeto es rudeza o comportamiento que muestra poco respeto.
spanish_example: Mostró falta de respeto al discutir con su jefe durante una reunión.

english_meaning: Enthusiasm is a very strong good feeling about something.
english_example: The crowd showed their enthusiasm for the soccer team by cheering loudly.
spanish_meaning: El entusiasmo es una alegría o interés muy fuerte por algo.
spanish_example: El público mostró entusiasmo por el equipo de fútbol animándolo con fuerza.

english_meaning: When something is harsh, it is very unpleasant.
english_example: The desert can be a very harsh environment.
spanish_meaning: Duro significa severo o desagradable.
spanish_example: El desierto es un ambiente muy duro.

english_meaning: To lean is to bend in a particular direction.
english_example: The woman leaned against the counter because she was tired.
spanish_meaning: Inclinarse es inclinar el cuerpo.
spanish_example: La mujer se inclino contra el mostrador.

english_meaning: The meantime is the time between two events.
english_example: Ted began setting the table. In the meantime, I began preparing the food.
spanish_meaning: Mientras tanto significa durante ese tiempo.
spanish_example: Tom puso la mesa; mientras tanto, yo prepare la comida.

english_meaning: Mischief is behavior that is meant to trick or cause trouble for people.
english_example: Ben was up to mischief when he persuaded Ken to paint his face.
spanish_meaning: La travesura es conducta de hacer bromas.
spanish_example: Ben hizo travesuras al pintar la cara de alguien.

english_meaning: Muscle is a mass of tissue attached to bone that helps you move.
english_example: She went to the gym in order to make her muscles stronger.
spanish_meaning: Un musculo es tejido que mueve el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Ella fue al gimnasio para fortalecer sus musculos.

english_meaning: To rescue someone means to remove them from danger.
english_example: The firefighter rescued the man from the burning building.
spanish_meaning: Rescatar significa salvar a alguien de peligro.
spanish_example: El bombero rescató al hombre del edificio en llamas.

english_meaning: A succession is a number of things that follow one after the other.
english_example: The student said the letters of the alphabet in succession.
spanish_meaning: Sucesion es serie ordenada.
spanish_example: Repitio las letras del alfabeto en sucesion.

english_meaning: The terrain is the land and all of its physical features or parts.
english_example: The terrain below was rocky and full of hills.
spanish_meaning: El terreno es la superficie de la tierra con su relieve.
spanish_example: El terreno era rocoso y lleno de colinas.

english_meaning: If someone is timid, they are afraid, shy, or nervous.
english_example: The timid child hides behind her mother whenever she sees a stranger.
spanish_meaning: Tímido significa miedoso, vergonzoso o nervioso.
spanish_example: La niña tímida se escondió detrás de su madre cuando vio a un desconocido.

english_meaning: Violence is forceful action that is meant to injure or kill people.
english_example: The boy hit his brother in an act of violence.
spanish_meaning: La violencia es una acción de fuerza destinada a herir o matar.
spanish_example: El niño golpeó a su hermano en un acto de violencia.

english_meaning: To affect someone or something is to have an influence over them.
english_example: The student’s poor attitude affected the other students in the class.
spanish_meaning: Afectar es causar influencia.
spanish_example: La mala actitud de un alumno afecto a los otros.

english_meaning: An autograph is the written name of a famous person.
english_example: Everybody wanted the movie star’s autograph.
spanish_meaning: Un autografo es una firma escrita.
spanish_example: Todo el mundo quería el autografo de la estrella.

english_meaning: A bead is a drop of liquid.
english_example: Beads of water collected on the outside of the glass.
spanish_meaning: Una gota es una pequeña pieza de liquido.
spanish_example: Se formaron gotas de agua en el vidrio.

english_meaning: To brew coffee or tea means to pour hot water over it.
english_example: Please brew a fresh pot of coffee.
spanish_meaning: Preparar café o té significa verter agua caliente sobre él.
spanish_example: Por favor, prepara una cafetera de café fresco.

english_meaning: To charm someone is to please them with your personality.
english_example: Gail charmed everyone with her humorous stories.
spanish_meaning: Encanto es atraccion o gracia.
spanish_example: Gail encanto a todos con historias.

english_meaning: A destiny is all the things that happen or will happen to a person in their life.
english_example: It was his destiny to become a great singer.
spanish_meaning: El destino es lo que está escrito en la vida.
spanish_example: Era su destino ser un gran cantante.

english_meaning: Equipment consists of the things which are used for a specific purpose.
english_example: The factory produces a wide range of high-tech equipment.
spanish_meaning: El equipo son las herramientas para un fin.
spanish_example: La fábrica produce mucho equipo.

english_meaning: A horn is a device that makes a loud noise.
english_example: The boy honked his horn as he rode his bicycle past the house.
spanish_meaning: La bocina es un dispositivo que hace un ruido fuerte.
spanish_example: El niño tocó la bocina mientras pasaba en bicicleta frente a la casa.

english_meaning: When someone is irritable, they become annoyed or angry very easily.
english_example: She is irritable when she doesn’t get enough sleep.
spanish_meaning: Una persona irritable se molesta con facilidad.
spanish_example: Se volvió irritable cuando tenía hambre.

english_meaning: To lag behind is to move slowly behind other moving objects.
english_example: The girl on rollerblades lagged behind the little girl on the bicycle.
spanish_meaning: Quedarse atrás significa ir más lento.
spanish_example: La chica en patines quedó atrás.

english_meaning: A nightmare is a bad or scary dream.
english_example: The girl was scared to go back to sleep because she had had a nightmare.
spanish_meaning: Una pesadilla es un sueño terrible y angustiante.
spanish_example: La niña no quería dormir porque había tenido una pesadilla.

english_meaning: When something is nutritious, it helps the body stay healthy.
english_example: Mangoes are one of the most nutritious fruits in the world.
spanish_meaning: Nutritivo mantiene la salud.
spanish_example: Las mangostas son frutas nutritivas.

english_meaning: Protein is a substance that is necessary for the body to grow and be strong.
english_example: Fish and beef are good sources of protein.
spanish_meaning: La proteina es sustancia de crecimiento.
spanish_example: El pescado y la carne son buenas fuentes de proteina.

english_meaning: A signature is your name, when you write it in your own way.
english_example: I put my signature at the end of the letter.
spanish_meaning: Firma es el nombre escrito.
spanish_example: Puse mi firma al final de la carta.

english_meaning: Stuff is a word used to refer to things without mentioning the things by name.
english_example: We gave a lot of stuff to the charity.
spanish_meaning: Cosas son objetos o material.
spanish_example: Dimos muchas cosas a la caridad.

english_meaning: When something is subconscious, it is done without thinking about it.
english_example: Breathing is one of the subconscious things that the body does.
spanish_meaning: Subconsciente es sin pensar conscientemente.
spanish_example: Respirar es una accion del subconsciente.

english_meaning: A van is a vehicle that is used for carrying things. It is smaller than a truck.
english_example: The delivery company uses large vans to deliver packages.
spanish_meaning: Una furgoneta es un vehículo de carga.
spanish_example: La empresa usa vans grandes para entrega.

english_meaning: To warn someone is to make them know of possible danger in the future.
english_example: The lifeguard warned people to stay away from the rough ocean.
spanish_meaning: Advertir significa avisar de peligro.
spanish_example: El salvavidas advirtio no acercarse al mar.

english_meaning: A workout is an exercise routine that helps improve health.
english_example: She doesn’t do her workout on the weekends.
spanish_meaning: Entrenamiento es rutina de ejercicio.
spanish_example: Ella no hace su entrenamiento los fines de semana.

english_meaning: To zoom is to move quickly.
english_example: The cars zoomed along the road.
spanish_meaning: Acelerar es moverse rapido.
spanish_example: Los autos aceleraron por la carretera.

english_meaning: A brick is a block of hard clay that is used for building things, such as walls.
english_example: There were several bricks scattered on the ground.
spanish_meaning: Un ladrillo es bloque de arcilla.
spanish_example: Había varios ladrillos en el suelo.

english_meaning: To crumble means to break or fall apart into small pieces.
english_example: The old house’s walls crumbled into a pile of rock and wood.
spanish_meaning: Desmoronarse significa romperse o caerse en trozos pequeños.
spanish_example: Las paredes de la casa vieja se desmoronaron en una pila de roca y madera.

english_meaning: Dough is a mixture of flour and water that becomes bread when baked.
english_example: I made heart-shaped cookies from the dough.
spanish_meaning: Masa es mezcla de harina para cocinar.
spanish_example: Hice galletas con esa masa.

english_meaning: To express a feeling or idea means to show others how one thinks or feels.
english_example: The nurse expressed her sympathy for the sick patient.
spanish_meaning: Expresar es mostrar una idea o sentimiento.
spanish_example: La enfermera expreso simpatia por el paciente.

english_meaning: A fist is a hand with fingers bent in toward the palm.
english_example: The bully made a fist and threatened to hit the small boy.
spanish_meaning: Un puño es el puño cerrado de la mano.
spanish_example: El maton hizo un puño.

english_meaning: If something is flexible, then it can bend easily without breaking.
english_example: The tree branch was so flexible it could be bent into a circle and not break.
spanish_meaning: Flexible significa que se dobla.
spanish_example: La rama podia doblarse en círculo.

english_meaning: To flush means the face becomes red due to heat, illness, or emotion.
english_example: After the long race, the runner’s face was flushed.
spanish_meaning: Enrojecer es que la cara se pone roja.
spanish_example: después de la carrera su cara estaba enrojecida.

english_meaning: To injure someone means to damage a part of their body.
english_example: The car crash injured two people.
spanish_meaning: Herir es dañar el cuerpo.
spanish_example: El choque hirio a dos personas.

english_meaning: A lump is a small piece of something that is solid.
english_example: The artist took a lump of clay and turned it into a beautiful pot.
spanish_meaning: Un bulto es una masa pequeña y sólida.
spanish_example: El artista hizo un bulto de barro.

english_meaning: A mixture is something that is made by mixing other things together.
english_example: The walls were built using a mixture of water, rock, and dirt.
spanish_meaning: Una mezcla combina cosas.
spanish_example: Construyeron paredes con mezcla de agua y tierra.

english_meaning: To reconcile means to return to a friendly relationship.
english_example: After arguing, the two friends were reconciled with each other.
spanish_meaning: Reconciliar significa volver a la amistad.
spanish_example: Tras discutir, los dos amigos se reconciliaron.

english_meaning: To ruin something means to harm or damage it greatly.
english_example: Our walk in the park was ruined by the sudden rain.
spanish_meaning: Arruinar significa causar daño.
spanish_example: La lluvia arruinó nuestro paseo.

english_meaning: To shatter something means to break it suddenly into many tiny pieces.
english_example: When the ball hit the window, the glass shattered.
spanish_meaning: Destruir significa romper en pedazos.
spanish_example: El golpe destrozo la ventana por completo.

english_meaning: Shutters are wooden or metal covers in front of a window.
english_example: Mr. Smith closed the shutters every night to make his bedroom dark.
spanish_meaning: Una persiana cubre la ventana.
spanish_example: Mi hermano cerró las persianas.

english_meaning: To sift something means to remove all the large pieces.
english_example: The baker sifted the flour into a large bowl.
spanish_meaning: Tamizar significa pasar con cedazo.
spanish_example: El panadero tamizo la harina.

english_meaning: If something is slight, then it is small or minor.
english_example: There was only a slight change in the little boy’s height.
spanish_meaning: Ligero significa pequeño o menor.
spanish_example: Solo hubo un ligero cambio en su altura.

english_meaning: To sparkle means to shine brightly with quick fashes of light.
english_example: The stars sparkled in the winter night’s sky.
spanish_meaning: Brillar es destellar.
spanish_example: Las estrellas brillaban anoche.

english_meaning: To sprinkle means to scatter something all over something else.
english_example: He sprinkled the pasta with salt and black pepper.
spanish_meaning: Espolvorear es esparcir algo sobre otra cosa.
spanish_example: Espolvoreó la pasta con sal y pimienta negra.

english_meaning: If food is stale, then it is not fresh but dry, hard, and not good to eat.
english_example: The cookies sat on the table so long that they became stale.
spanish_meaning: Un alimento duro o pasado no está fresco y no sabe bien.
spanish_example: Las galletas estuvieron tanto tiempo en la mesa que se pusieron duras.

english_meaning: To utter a word or a sound means to say it.
english_example: The lost boy was so scared that he could barely utter a single word.
spanish_meaning: Pronunciar es decir una palabra o sonido.
spanish_example: El niño perdido tenía tanto miedo que apenas podía pronunciar una palabra.

english_meaning: You use although to say that one thing is contrasted by another.
english_example: Although she was late, her friends gave her a warm welcome.
spanish_meaning: Aunque significa contraste entre dos ideas.
spanish_example: Aunque llego tarde, todos la recibieron.

english_meaning: To apply something means to put it on.
english_example: She always applies makeup to her face before going outside.
spanish_meaning: Aplicar significa poner sobre.
spanish_example: Ella se aplica maquillaje antes de salir.

english_meaning: To await something means to wait for it.
english_example: The players awaited the referee’s decision.
spanish_meaning: Esperar significa aguardar.
spanish_example: Los jugadores esperaron la decisión del árbitro.

english_meaning: When something is beloved, it is very special and you like it very much.
english_example: The boy took a nap next to his beloved cat.
spanish_meaning: Querido significa muy amado o muy especial.
spanish_example: El niño durmió la siesta junto a su querido gato.

english_meaning: A climate is the usual weather in a place.
english_example: The climate in the desert is very hot.
spanish_meaning: El clima es el tiempo habitual.
spanish_example: El clima del desierto es muy caluroso.

english_meaning: When you complain, you say that you are unhappy about something.
english_example: The workers complained that they were being treated unfairly.
spanish_meaning: Quejarse es expresar descontento.
spanish_example: Los trabajadores se quejaron de ser injustamente tratados.

english_meaning: To confuse someone means to make them feel like they are unsure.
english_example: The sign confused the traveler because it pointed in two directions.
spanish_meaning: Confundir significa hacer que alguien no entienda.
spanish_example: El cartel confunde al viajero.

english_meaning: When something is due, it is expected to happen or be done at that time.
english_example: The papers were due on the 19th.
spanish_meaning: Debido significa que algo está previsto en cierto tiempo.
spanish_example: Los papeles estaban vencidos el 19.

english_meaning: To establish something means to create it.
english_example: He wanted to establish a club for people to help the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Establecer significa crear o fundar.
spanish_example: Quiere establecer un club para ayudar al planeta.

english_meaning: A furnace is a place where heat is made.
english_example: Mr. Jones came to fix the furnace.
spanish_meaning: La caldera produce calor.
spanish_example: El técnico reparó la caldera.

english_meaning: A leash is a rope or chain that is used to lead an animal.
english_example: Dogs must wear a leash to keep them from running away.
spanish_meaning: Una correa sirve para llevar un animal.
spanish_example: Los perros llevan correa.

english_meaning: To mature means to grow up to become an adult.
english_example: When they matured, they became as tall as their parents.
spanish_meaning: Madurar significa crecer hasta convertirse en adulto.
spanish_example: Cuando maduraron, llegaron a ser tan altos como sus padres.

english_meaning: To measure something means to find out the quality, value, or effect of it.
english_example: The scientists carefully measured the amount of chemicals in the tubes.
spanish_meaning: Medir es encontrar cantidad o efecto.
spanish_example: Los científicos midieron la cantidad de químicos.

english_meaning: The midst of something is the middle of it.
english_example: She was in the midst of cleaning when the telephone rang.
spanish_meaning: Mitad o centro de algo.
spanish_example: Ella estaba en el medio de la limpieza cuando sonó el telefono.

english_meaning: Misery is extreme suffering.
english_example: There was a lot of misery after Sam lost his dog.
spanish_meaning: La miseria o el sufrimiento extremo es un gran dolor.
spanish_example: Hubo mucho sufrimiento después de que Sam perdió a su perro.

english_meaning: Powerless means without power or authority.
english_example: I fear my father is powerless now.
spanish_meaning: Sin poder o autoridad.
spanish_example: Temo que mi padre ahora no tenga poder.

english_meaning: When something happened prior to something else, it happened earlier.
english_example: Ron had to wait since he arrived prior to the scheduled meeting time.
spanish_meaning: Anterior significa que ocurrió antes.
spanish_example: Ron tuvo que esperar porque llegó antes de la hora programada de la reunión.

english_meaning: Research is close and careful study to discover new things.
english_example: Scientists did a lot of research on the subject of blood type.
spanish_meaning: La investigación es un estudio cuidadoso para descubrir cosas nuevas.
spanish_example: Los científicos hicieron mucha investigación sobre el tipo de sangre.

english_meaning: A spaceship is a craft designed for spaceflight.
english_example: Many people want to travel into space by spaceship.
spanish_meaning: Nave espacial es un vehiculo para el espacio.
spanish_example: Mucha gente quiere viajar en nave espacial.

english_meaning: A variety of something is a group of many different kinds of it.
english_example: There are a variety of flowers at the shop.
spanish_meaning: Variedad es muchas diferencias.
spanish_example: Hay mucha variedad de flores.

english_meaning: If something happens altogether, it happens completely.
english_example: The company stopped using sugar altogether in its food.
spanish_meaning: Completamente.
spanish_example: La empresa dejo de usar azucar por completo.

english_meaning: To bind people is to make them feel united together.
english_example: The victims of the flood were bound by their need to help each other.
spanish_meaning: Vincular significa unir a otros.
spanish_example: Los victimas quedaron unidas por la necesidad de ayudar.

english_meaning: A bruise is a dark mark caused by being hit by something.
english_example: She got a bruise on her knee from falling down.
spanish_meaning: Un moretón es una marca oscura en la piel por un golpe.
spanish_example: Ella se hizo un moretón en la rodilla al caer.

english_meaning: A custom is a way of doing things that has been the same for a long time.
english_example: It is a custom that the bride and groom have the first dance.
spanish_meaning: Costumbre es forma de actuar tradicional.
spanish_example: Es costumbre bailar en bodas.

english_meaning: When someone is disobedient, they do not follow the rules or instructions.
english_example: The disobedient children didn’t listen to their mother and had an accident.
spanish_meaning: Desobediente es quien no sigue reglas.
spanish_example: Los niños desobedientes no escuchaban a la madre.

english_meaning: To foresee something is to know about it before it happens.
english_example: The teacher didn’t foresee any problems with his large class.
spanish_meaning: Prever es adelantar un suceso.
spanish_example: El profesor no pudo prever problemas.

english_meaning: To glimpse something is to see it for a short time.
english_example: She glimpsed outside the window as the plane was about to land.
spanish_meaning: Vislumbrar es ver de rapido.
spanish_example: Ella vislumbro el paisaje al aterrizar.

english_meaning: A hoop is a ring that is made of plastic, metal, or wood.
english_example: The boys tried to toss the ball through the basketball hoop.
spanish_meaning: Un aro es un anillo.
spanish_example: Intentaron meter la pelota en el aro.

english_meaning: Misfortune is bad luck or an unlucky event.
english_example: His family helped him when he encountered misfortune.
spanish_meaning: Mala suerte es un infortunio.
spanish_example: Su familia lo ayudó en la desgracia.

english_meaning: When something is negative, it is unpleasant or sad.
english_example: She doesn’t like to say negative things about her friends.
spanish_meaning: Negativo es no favorable o desfavorable.
spanish_example: No le gusta hablar en terminos negativos.

english_meaning: Per is used to mean “each” when giving a price, size, or amount.
english_example: It costs eight dollars per person to watch the baseball game.
spanish_meaning: Se usa por para indicar cantidad por unidad.
spanish_example: Cuesta ocho dolares por persona.

english_meaning: To plead is to ask for something you want very badly.
english_example: He pleaded for his parents to let him go to the soccer game.
spanish_meaning: Suplicar es pedir con insistencia.
spanish_example: El chico suplico que lo dejaran ir.

english_meaning: To rip something means to pull it apart.
english_example: She ripped the paper in half by accident.
spanish_meaning: Desgarrar es romper con fuerza.
spanish_example: Ella desgarró el papel en dos.

english_meaning: The sake of something is the reason for doing it.
english_example: The parents worked hard for their children’s sake.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo por el bien de alguien significa para ayudarle o protegerle.
spanish_example: Los padres trabajaron mucho por el bien de sus hijos.

english_meaning: To scrape something is to rub it very hard with something sharp.
english_example: I accidentally scraped the paint of the side of the car.
spanish_meaning: Raspar es frotar fuerte.
spanish_example: Raspo la pintura del coche.

english_meaning: A source of something is the place that it comes from.
english_example: The river was the source of drinking water for the village.
spanish_meaning: Fuente es el origen de algo.
spanish_example: El río era la fuente de agua.

english_meaning: When somebody is stern, they are very serious.
english_example: The stern teacher didn’t allow the students to speak during class.
spanish_meaning: Severo es duro y serio.
spanish_example: La maestra severa no permitio hablar.

english_meaning: To stitch is to use a needle and thread to join pieces of cloth together.
english_example: My grandmother stitched the pieces together to make a big blanket.
spanish_meaning: Coser es unir piezas con aguja y hilo.
spanish_example: Mi abuela cosio las piezas.

english_meaning: A thump is the sound of a heavy object hitting something.
english_example: They heard the thump when the bowling ball hit the floor.
spanish_meaning: Un golpeteo es sonido fuerte de impacto.
spanish_example: Oyieron un golpe cuando la bola tocó la pista.

english_meaning: When somebody is vehement, they are angry and emotional.
english_example: Gina was vehement when she found out that Liz was bad in school.
spanish_meaning: Vehemente significa intenso y enojado.
spanish_example: Gina fue muy vehemente al recibir la noticia.

english_meaning: If something is ailing, it is sickly or not doing well.
english_example: Antonio’s ailing health caused his head to hurt.
spanish_meaning: Enfermo significa no sentirse bien de salud.
spanish_example: La salud de Antonio estaba mal y le dolía la cabeza.

english_meaning: If two people or things are alike, they are similar to each other.
english_example: The brothers looked so much alike that it was impossible to tell one from the other.
spanish_meaning: Parecido significa parecido.
spanish_example: Los hermanos se parecen mucho.

english_meaning: To appall means to disgust someone.
english_example: Minerva was appalled when she read the email from her enemy.
spanish_meaning: Horrorizar significa causar una gran reacción de temor o disgusto.
spanish_example: Minerva se horrorizó al leer el correo de su enemigo.

english_meaning: To bathe means to wash oneself with water.
english_example: The mermaid bathed herself in the ocean.
spanish_meaning: Bañarse es lavarse con agua.
spanish_example: La sirena se baño en el oceano.

english_meaning: Bounds is the area in a game in which plays are legal.
english_example: If your ball lands outside the lines, it’s out of bounds.
spanish_meaning: Limite es frontera del juego.
spanish_example: Si la pelota cae fuera de las lineas, está fuera de limites.

english_meaning: To critique means express an opinion about the good and the bad parts of something.
english_example: My job is to critique the food at various restaurants.
spanish_meaning: Critica es opinión evaluativa.
spanish_example: Mi trabajo es criticar comida en restaurantes.

english_meaning: Demography is the study of people and populations.
english_example: If you study demography, you’ll see huge population growth in some areas of the world.
spanish_meaning: Demografia estudia poblaciones.
spanish_example: La demografia muestra crecimiento rapido.

english_meaning: A diagnosis is a doctor’s decision about the cause of an illness.
english_example: The vet’s diagnosis was that our dog had a broken leg.
spanish_meaning: Un diagnóstico es la decisión médica sobre la causa de una enfermedad.
spanish_example: El diagnóstico del veterinario fue que nuestro perro tenía una pata rota.

english_meaning: A dip is a decline or a worsening in condition.
english_example: The economy experienced a dip in the 1930s, leading many workers to lose their jobs.
spanish_meaning: Un decaimiento o caida temporal.
spanish_example: La economia tuvo una decaida en los años 30.

english_meaning: Distress is a feeling of sadness and anxiousness.
english_example: We were in great distress when our home was destroyed during the last hurricane.
spanish_meaning: La angustia es una sensación de tristeza y ansiedad.
spanish_example: Nuestra casa fue destruida y estuvimos en una gran angustia.

english_meaning: A fever is when a body’s temperature is higher than normal.
english_example: He had to miss school because he was sick with a fever.
spanish_meaning: La fiebre es cuando la temperatura corporal es más alta de lo normal.
spanish_example: Tuvo que faltar a la escuela porque estaba enfermo de fiebre.

english_meaning: Something that is grim is worrying, serious, and scary.
english_example: The boy made the grim discovery that he had accidentally deleted his homework.
spanish_meaning: Algo sombrío es preocupante, serio y puede dar miedo.
spanish_example: El chico hizo un descubrimiento sombrío cuando se dio cuenta de que había borrado su tarea.

english_meaning: Someone’s gut is their intestines, where food is processed after leaving the stomach.
english_example: Thousands of bacteria live in your gut and help you digest food.
spanish_meaning: El intestino es la parte del cuerpo donde se procesa la comida después del estómago.
spanish_example: Miles de bacterias viven en tus intestinos y ayudan a digerir la comida.

english_meaning: A helmet is a type of hard hat that protects your head.
english_example: When exploring underground caves, it is important to always wear a helmet.
spanish_meaning: Un casco es un tipo de protección dura para la cabeza.
spanish_example: Al explorar cuevas subterráneas, es importante usar siempre un casco.

english_meaning: An herb is a plant used for cooking or medicine.
english_example: I couldn’t decide which herb I wanted to buy at the market.
spanish_meaning: Una hierba es una planta usada para cocinar o para medicina.
spanish_example: No pude decidir qué hierba quería comprar en el mercado.

english_meaning: If someone is militant, they are angry and aggressive, and willing to fight easily.
english_example: The woman delivered an angry, militant speech on the floor of the legislature.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien es militante, está enojado, agresivo y dispuesto a pelear.
spanish_example: La mujer dio un discurso militante en la legislatura.

english_meaning: A miracle is an unexpected blessing which seems almost impossible, like a gift from heaven.
english_example: A miracle happened when Rosalita won over 60 million dollars in the lottery.
spanish_meaning: Un milagro es una bendición inesperada que parece casi imposible.
spanish_example: Fue un milagro que Rosalita ganara más de sesenta millones de dólares.

english_meaning: To mourn means to show or feel great sadness when you lose someone.
english_example: The entire family mourned their grandfather’s death together.
spanish_meaning: Llorar significa mostrar o sentir gran tristeza al perder a alguien.
spanish_example: Toda la familia lloró a su abuelo en la muerte.

english_meaning: Nonsense are words or ideas that are silly or foolish.
english_example: The baby was talking nonsense into the phone.
spanish_meaning: La tontería son palabras o ideas absurdas.
spanish_example: El bebé decía tonterías por el teléfono.

english_meaning: A physician is a doctor.
english_example: The physician was a friendly man who did his best with all of his patients.
spanish_meaning: Un médico es una persona que trata a los pacientes.
spanish_example: El médico fue un hombre amable y trató de ayudar a todos.

english_meaning: An accent is a certain way of speaking that shows where a person is from.
english_example: The new teacher’s accent was clearly a German one.
spanish_meaning: Un acento es una forma de hablar que muestra de dónde viene una persona.
spanish_example: El acento del nuevo profesor era claramente alemán.

english_meaning: A barber is a person whose job is to cut men’s hair.
english_example: My hair is getting much too long. I’d better go to the barber’s shop.
spanish_meaning: Un barbero es una persona cuyo oficio es cortar el cabello masculino.
spanish_example: Mi pelo estaba muy largo y mejor iré a la peluquería del barbero.

english_meaning: A basement of a house or building is a room that is built underground.
english_example: They turned their basement into a game room.
spanish_meaning: Un sótano es una habitación construida bajo tierra.
spanish_example: Convirtieron su sótano en una sala de juegos.

english_meaning: When something is blank, it does not have anything on it.
english_example: She got a blank piece of paper to draw on.
spanish_meaning: Algo en blanco no tiene nada escrito o dibujado.
spanish_example: Recibió una hoja en blanco para dibujar.

english_meaning: To blink means to shut the eyes and quickly open them again.
english_example: I blinked many times so that my eyes could adjust to the bright light.
spanish_meaning: Parpadear significa cerrar y abrir los ojos rápidamente.
spanish_example: Parpadeé varias veces para que mis ojos se acostumbraran a la luz brillante.

english_meaning: A choir is a group of people who sing together.
english_example: He had choir practice every day after school.
spanish_meaning: Un coro es un grupo de personas que canta junto.
spanish_example: Él tenía práctica de coro cada día.

english_meaning: When something is comic, it is funny.
english_example: The comic actor was famous for his jokes.
spanish_meaning: Algo cómico es gracioso.
spanish_example: El actor cómico era famoso por sus chistes.

english_meaning: To complicate something means to make it harder than necessary.
english_example: The bad weather complicated finishing the job quickly.
spanish_meaning: Complicar algo es hacerlo más difícil de lo necesario.
spanish_example: El mal tiempo complicó terminar el trabajo pronto.

english_meaning: To decline an offer or invitation means to say no to it.
english_example: She declined his offer to pay for her dinner.
spanish_meaning: Rechazar una oferta o invitación significa decir que no.
spanish_example: Ella rechazó su oferta de pagarle la cena.

english_meaning: An errand is a trip taken to do a specific activity.
english_example: He couldn’t go to practice because he had several errands to do.
spanish_meaning: Un recado es un viaje para hacer una tarea concreta.
spanish_example: No pudo entrenarse porque tenía varios recados por hacer.

english_meaning: A glove is a piece of clothing that covers your fingers and hand.
english_example: When it gets cold, I always put on a pair of gloves.
spanish_meaning: Un guante es una prenda que cubre dedos y manos.
spanish_example: Cuando hace frío, siempre uso un par de guantes.

english_meaning: A hermit is one who lives alone and does not spend time with others.
english_example: The hermit lived a simple life in a small cave in the forest.
spanish_meaning: Un ermitaño vive solo y no pasa tiempo con otros.
spanish_example: El ermitaño vivía una vida simple en una pequeña cueva del bosque.

english_meaning: If something is done justly, then it is fair.
english_example: We justly decided to give the prize to him.
spanish_meaning: Si algo se hace justamente, se hace con justicia.
spanish_example: Fue justamente la decisión darle el premio.

english_meaning: Leather is a material made from animal skin that is used to make clothing.
english_example: He got a new leather jacket for his birthday.
spanish_meaning: El cuero es un material de piel animal usado para ropa.
spanish_example: Se compró una chaqueta nueva de cuero.

english_meaning: To ponder something is to think about it carefully.
english_example: She sat in the park and pondered her problem.
spanish_meaning: Reflexionar significa pensar cuidadosamente en algo.
spanish_example: Ella se sentó en el parque y reflexionó sobre su problema.

english_meaning: To reserve something means to keep it for a certain person or time.
english_example: He reserved a table at the busy restaurant.
spanish_meaning: Reservar algo es apartarlo para una persona o tiempo.
spanish_example: Reservó una mesa en un restaurante lleno.

english_meaning: A script is the words of a film or play.
english_example: He read the script of the play three times.
spanish_meaning: Un guion es el texto de una película o una obra.
spanish_example: Leyó el guion de la obra tres veces.

english_meaning: To search for something or someone means to look for them carefully.
english_example: I searched the newspaper for a new job.
spanish_meaning: Buscar es mirar con cuidado para encontrar algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Busqué en el periódico un nuevo trabajo.

english_meaning: To slam is to close something hard.
english_example: She slammed the book shut after she finished reading it.
spanish_meaning: Cerrar algo de golpe significa presionar y dejarlo rápidamente.
spanish_example: Cerró el libro con fuerza cuando terminó de leerlo.

english_meaning: A staircase is a set of stairs found inside a building.
english_example: The staircase leads directly into the kitchen.
spanish_meaning: Una escalera es una estructura de escalones dentro de un edificio.
spanish_example: La escalera lleva directamente a la cocina.

english_meaning: Afflicted means to suffer physically or mentally.
english_example: He was afflicted by pain in his right arm.
spanish_meaning: Alguien afectado es quien sufre física o mentalmente.
spanish_example: Él estaba afectado por dolor en su brazo derecho.

english_meaning: An aisle is a space between two things that people use to walk.
english_example: They were told to clear the aisle because the plane was about to land.
spanish_meaning: Un pasillo es un espacio para caminar entre dos filas de asientos o sillas.
spanish_example: Pidieron vaciar el pasillo porque el avión iba a aterrizar.

english_meaning: The atmosphere is the air around the Earth where weather conditions form.
english_example: Scientists worry that harmful substances are hurting the atmosphere.
spanish_meaning: La atmósfera es el aire alrededor de la Tierra donde se forma el clima.
spanish_example: Temen que sustancias nocivas dañen la atmósfera.

english_meaning: An author is a person who wrote a certain piece of writing.
english_example: The author was hard at work on his next novel.
spanish_meaning: Un autor es la persona que escribió una obra.
spanish_example: El autor estaba muy concentrado en su próxima novela.

english_meaning: A breakdown is a failure to work correctly.
english_example: Her car had a breakdown, and she wasn’t sure how to fix it.
spanish_meaning: Una avería es una falla que impide que algo funcione.
spanish_example: El coche de ella tuvo una avería y no sabía cómo repararlo.

english_meaning: Cargo is the items carried by a ship or an airplane.
english_example: The cargo of the ship got wet when it started raining.
spanish_meaning: La carga es lo que se transporta en un barco o avión.
spanish_example: La carga del barco se mojó cuando comenzó a llover.

english_meaning: A chapter is a part of a book that usually has a number or a title.
english_example: The first chapter of a book usually introduces the main character of a story.
spanish_meaning: Un capítulo es una parte de un libro con número o título.
spanish_example: El primer capítulo de un libro suele presentar al personaje principal.

english_meaning: To connect two things means to join them together.
english_example: I connected the mouse to my laptop computer.
spanish_meaning: Conectar dos cosas significa unirlas.
spanish_example: Conecté el ratón a mi portátil.

english_meaning: Etc. is short for “etcetera.” It is used to refer to other unspecified objects.
english_example: She was going to bring treats to the party: cookies, muffins, cake, etc.
spanish_meaning: Etc. es la abreviatura de “etcétera”.
spanish_example: Trajo dulces a la fiesta: galletas, muffins, pastel, etc.

english_meaning: To flip means to press a switch quickly to turn it on or off.
english_example: To turn on the lights, just flip this switch.
spanish_meaning: Pulsar significa presionar un interruptor para encender o apagar.
spanish_example: Para encender las luces, solo hay que pulsar este interruptor.

english_meaning: If you are idle, you are not doing anything.
english_example: She read a book to keep from being idle.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien está ocioso, no está haciendo nada.
spanish_example: Leía un libro para no quedarse ocioso.

english_meaning: To notify someone of something is to tell them about it.
english_example: The teacher notified us about a change to our assignment.
spanish_meaning: Notificar a alguien es informarle de algo.
spanish_example: El profesor notificó a la clase de un cambio de tarea.

english_meaning: Peas are a vegetable that is small, round, and green.
english_example: His favorite food was peas.
spanish_meaning: Un guisante es una verdura pequeña, redonda y verde.
spanish_example: Sus alimentos favoritos eran los guisantes.

english_meaning: A raisin is a dried grape.
english_example: Raisins are one of my favorite snacks.
spanish_meaning: Una pasa es una uva seca.
spanish_example: Las pasas son uno de mis bocadillos favoritos.

english_meaning: To retain something is to keep it.
english_example: Even by the afternoon, the day had retained the morning’s freshness.
spanish_meaning: Retener algo es mantenerlo o guardarlo.
spanish_example: Al mediodía, el día todavía había retenido la frescura de la mañana.

english_meaning: To state something means to say it in a definite way.
english_example: The president stated his opinion about the world’s health concerns.
spanish_meaning: Declarar algo es decirlo de forma clara y firme.
spanish_example: El presidente declaró su opinión sobre la salud mundial.

english_meaning: A tray is a fat plate used to hold food.
english_example: The waiter brought our food on a tray.
spanish_meaning: Una bandeja es un plato ancho para llevar comida.
spanish_example: El camarero trajo la comida en una bandeja.

english_meaning: If something is unfortunate, it is bad or unlucky.
english_example: It was unfortunate that Dave’s team lost, but he still had fun.
spanish_meaning: Algo desafortunado es malo o trae mala suerte.
spanish_example: Fue desafortunado que el equipo de Dave perdiera.

english_meaning: When something is vivid, it is bright and colorful.
english_example: The figures in the painting were vivid.
spanish_meaning: Algo vívido es brillante y lleno de color.
spanish_example: Las figuras en la pintura eran muy vívidas.

english_meaning: To vomit means to have food come up from one’s stomach.
english_example: It is common for women to vomit when they are pregnant.
spanish_meaning: Vomitar es hacer que el alimento salga del estómago.
spanish_example: Es común vomitar durante el embarazo.

english_meaning: Ambition is the desire to succeed and to become the best at something.
english_example: His great ambition led him to try to climb the mountain alone.
spanish_meaning: La ambición es el deseo de triunfar y ser el mejor en algo.
spanish_example: Su gran ambición lo llevó a intentar escalar la montaña solo.

english_meaning: An ambulance is a vehicle that transports people in a medical emergency.
english_example: The ambulance sped through the street to get to the accident.
spanish_meaning: Una ambulancia es un vehículo que lleva personas en emergencias médicas.
spanish_example: La ambulancia aceleró por la calle para llegar al accidente.

english_meaning: An ankle connects your leg to your foot.
english_example: The man hurt his ankle while jogging in the park.
spanish_meaning: El tobillo une la pierna con el pie.
spanish_example: El hombre se torció el tobillo al correr.

english_meaning: A cabin is a small wooden house in a forest or camping area.
english_example: The man reached the mountain cabin after a long day of hiking along the river.
spanish_meaning: Una cabaña es una casa pequeña de madera en bosque o camping.
spanish_example: El hombre llegó a la cabaña de montaña tras un día largo.

english_meaning: A calendar is a chart that shows the days, weeks, and months of a year.
english_example: Marco used an online calendar to keep track of his schedule.
spanish_meaning: Un calendario muestra los días, semanas y meses de un año.
spanish_example: Marco usó un calendario en línea para organizar su horario.

english_meaning: A calorie is a unit of heat used to measure how much energy a food will produce.
english_example: My sister is on a low-calorie diet for fitness training.
spanish_meaning: La caloría es una unidad de energía de los alimentos.
spanish_example: Mi hermana sigue una dieta baja en calorías.

english_meaning: A cave is an open space or hole underground or inside a mountain or cliff.
english_example: A crystal blue lake could be found in the underground cave.
spanish_meaning: Una cueva es un hueco o espacio subterráneo en una montaña.
spanish_example: En una cueva subterránea encontraron un lago de color azul.

english_meaning: Constantly means doing something on a continuous basis.
english_example: He was constantly checking his watch to see if he was late.
spanish_meaning: Constantemente significa de manera continua.
spanish_example: Revisaba su reloj constantemente para saber si iba tarde.

english_meaning: A contingent is a set of people that are part of a larger group.
english_example: The movie star stood in front of an entire contingent of photographers.
spanish_meaning: Un contingente es un grupo que forma parte de uno más grande.
spanish_example: La estrella de cine se enfrentó a un contingente de fotógrafos.

english_meaning: A deadline is the time by which you need to have something completed.
english_example: Marius had a short deadline in which to finish his report.
spanish_meaning: Una fecha límite es el plazo para terminar algo.
spanish_example: Marius tenía una fecha límite muy corta para su informe.

english_meaning: To exert means to use strength or ability to do something.
english_example: You exert a lot of energy when you lift weights.
spanish_meaning: Esforzarse es usar energía o fuerza para hacer algo.
spanish_example: Te esfuerzas mucho cuando levantas pesas.

english_meaning: To flank is to be positioned at the side of something or someone.
english_example: The mayor entered the room flanked by her advisers.
spanish_meaning: Flanquear significa ir a un lado de alguien.
spanish_example: La alcaldesa entró a la sala flanqueada por sus asesores.

english_meaning: To be fond of something means to cherish or like it.
english_example: The mother cow was very fond of her lovely little calf.
spanish_meaning: Ser afectuoso significa querer y cuidar con cariño.
spanish_example: La madre vaca estaba muy afectuosa con su ternero.

english_meaning: To forbid means to order someone not to do something.
english_example: After Robert used his crayons on the wall, his mother wanted to forbid him from using them at all!
spanish_meaning: Prohibir significa ordenar que no se haga algo.
spanish_example: Su madre quería prohibirle usar crayones en la pared.

english_meaning: To haul something means to carry it from place to place.
english_example: The porter said he’d haul our suitcases up to our hotel room.
spanish_meaning: Transportar algo es llevarlo de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: El personal dijo que transportaría nuestras maletas.

english_meaning: To impair something means to make it weaker or worse.
english_example: Regularly eating too much can impair your health.
spanish_meaning: Perjudicar algo significa hacerlo peor.
spanish_example: Comer demasiado puede perjudicar tu salud.

english_meaning: To be impatient means to get angry or anxious when something takes too much time.
english_example: The impatient teacher could not control the students in her classroom.
spanish_meaning: Ser impaciente es ponerse nervioso o enojado si algo tarda.
spanish_example: El profesor impaciente no pudo controlar a sus estudiantes.

english_meaning: Mid means in the middle or center of something.
english_example: Cassandra fell asleep mid-movie.
spanish_meaning: Mid significa en el centro o mitad de algo.
spanish_example: Cassandra se durmió a la mitad de la película.
notes: Uso ambiguo: en está entrada aparece como prefijo/adverbio en el contexto de mid-movie.

english_meaning: Nutrition is the process of eating the right kind of food in order to stay healthy.
english_example: The mother always made sure that her baby had good nutrition.
spanish_meaning: La nutrición es la ingesta de alimentos saludables para estar sano.
spanish_example: La madre se aseguró de que su bebé tuviera buena nutrición.

english_meaning: An optimist is someone who has a lot of hope and always sees the bright side of things.
english_example: Joe was an optimist, so he believed he would get the job that he wanted.
spanish_meaning: Un optimista tiene mucha esperanza y ve siempre el lado positivo.
spanish_example: Joe era un optimista, así que creyó que conseguiría el trabajo.

english_meaning: A background is a person’s education, family, and experience.
english_example: The new teacher had a background in science and math.
spanish_meaning: El trasfondo es la educación, familia y experiencia de alguien.
spanish_example: El nuevo profesor tenía un trasfondo en ciencias y matemáticas.

english_meaning: Bait is something used to trick a person or thing to do something.
english_example: The best bait for catching fish is a big, fat worm.
spanish_meaning: Un cebo es algo que se usa para atrapar a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: El mejor cebo para pescar era un gusano grande.

english_meaning: To chronicle an event means to record it.
english_example: The daily newspaper chronicles local and world events.
spanish_meaning: Crónica significa registrar un evento.
spanish_example: El periódico local cronicó eventos diarios y mundiales.

english_meaning: Copper is a red-brown metal often used in electric wire and pipes.
english_example: Ancient hunters melted copper to make knives and spears.
spanish_meaning: El cobre es un metal rojo-marrón usado en cables y tuberías.
spanish_example: Los antiguos cazadores fundían cobre para hacer cuchillos y lanzas.

english_meaning: A disease is an illness that causes specific problems.
english_example: He had a disease that caused him to lose his hearing.
spanish_meaning: Una enfermedad es un padecimiento que causa problemas físicos.
spanish_example: Tenía una enfermedad que le hizo perder audición.

english_meaning: Folklore is the collection of beliefs and stories of a culture.
english_example: India’s folklore has stories about great warriors written in long poems.
spanish_meaning: El folclore es el conjunto de creencias e historias de una cultura.
spanish_example: El folclore de la India tiene poemas largos sobre guerreros.

english_meaning: To infect someone means to give them an illness.
english_example: The common cold infects hundreds of millions of people each year.
spanish_meaning: Infectar a alguien significa darle una enfermedad.
spanish_example: El resfriado común infecta a cientos de millones cada año.

english_meaning: To itch means to have an unpleasant feeling that you want to scratch.
english_example: The rough fabric in his shirt made his neck itch.
spanish_meaning: La picazón es la sensación incómoda de querer rascarse.
spanish_example: La tela áspera de su camisa hizo que su cuello picara.

english_meaning: Literature is books, plays, and poetry.
english_example: Early American literature covers the poetry and stories from 1500 to 1800.
spanish_meaning: La literatura son libros, obras de teatro y poesía.
spanish_example: La literatura norteamericana temprana va de 1500 a 1800.

english_meaning: A millennium is 1000 years.
english_example: Stonehenge is believed to have been built about five millennia ago.
spanish_meaning: Un milenio son 1000 años.
spanish_example: Se cree que Stonehenge se construyó hace unos cinco milenios.

english_meaning: A myth is a traditional story that explains a culture’s history and beliefs.
english_example: In Greece, there was a myth about a woman who had snakes for hair.
spanish_meaning: Un mito es una historia tradicional que explica creencias e historia.
spanish_example: En Grecia, había un mito sobre una mujer con cabellos de serpiente.

english_meaning: To promote someone means to raise them to a higher position or rank.
english_example: After two years at the company, she was promoted to manager.
spanish_meaning: Promover a alguien significa subirlo a un puesto más alto.
spanish_example: Después de dos años, ella fue promovida a gerente.

english_meaning: To relate to something means to have a connection with it.
english_example: A company’s plan usually relates to how much profit it can make.
spanish_meaning: Relacionar algo significa conectarlo con otro algo.
spanish_example: El plan de la empresa se relacionaba con sus ganancias.

english_meaning: A religion is a belief in a god or gods.
english_example: Their religion taught that people should forgive their enemies.
spanish_meaning: La religión es la creencia en un dios o dioses.
spanish_example: Su religión enseñaba que se debe perdonar a los enemigos.

english_meaning: A sum is a specific amount of money.
english_example: He calculated the numbers to see what the sum of his bills would be.
spanish_meaning: La suma es una cantidad total de dinero.
spanish_example: Calculó las cifras para ver la suma de sus cuentas.

english_meaning: A teller is a person who works with a bank’s customers.
english_example: The teller at the bank helped Kelly put money into a savings account.
spanish_meaning: Un cajero es una persona que atiende a clientes en un banco.
spanish_example: El cajero del banco ayudó a Kelly a abrir una cuenta.

english_meaning: If someone is trustworthy, they are honest and truthful.
english_example: Mary is one of the most trustworthy people I’ve ever met.
spanish_meaning: Confiable significa honesto y en quien se puede confiar.
spanish_example: Mary es una de las personas más confiables que conozco.

english_meaning: To update something means to make it more modern.
english_example: We need to update the programs on our computers.
spanish_meaning: Actualizar algo es hacerlo más moderno.
spanish_example: Tenemos que actualizar los programas de los ordenadores.

english_meaning: A vein is a tube in the body that carries blood toward the heart.
english_example: The blue veins in my hand are just under my skin.
spanish_meaning: Una vena es un conducto de sangre en el cuerpo que llega al corazón.
spanish_example: Las venas azules de mi mano están justo bajo la piel.

english_meaning: Venom is a poisonous substance that comes from animals or plants.
english_example: A snake’s venom can be used to cure the illnesses it creates.
spanish_meaning: El veneno es una sustancia tóxica de animales o plantas.
spanish_example: El veneno de una serpiente puede causar enfermedades.

english_meaning: Charity is an act of giving help, usually money, to those who need it.
english_example: Thanks to his friends’ charity, he had enough money to pay the rent.
spanish_meaning: La caridad es ayudar a quienes lo necesitan, por lo general con dinero.
spanish_example: Gracias a la caridad de sus amigos, pudo pagar el alquiler.

english_meaning: Commerce is the activity of buying and selling things.
english_example: The new shopping mall increased the commerce in that section of town.
spanish_meaning: El comercio es la actividad de comprar y vender.
spanish_example: El nuevo centro comercial aumentó el comercio del barrio.

english_meaning: To condemn someone means to give them a specific punishment.
english_example: The judge condemned the criminal to five years in prison.
spanish_meaning: Condenar a alguien es darle un castigo.
spanish_example: El juez condenó al criminal a cinco años de prisión.

english_meaning: If something is cozy, then it is comfortable, warm, and relaxing.
english_example: The thick blanket made the bed very cozy.
spanish_meaning: Algo acogedor es cómodo, cálido y relajante.
spanish_example: La manta gruesa volvió la cama muy acogedora.

english_meaning: To deplete an amount of something means to use all of it up.
english_example: All the driving he was doing was depleting his car’s fuel supply.
spanish_meaning: Agotar algo significa usarlo casi por completo.
spanish_example: Conducir tanto estaba agotando el combustible de su coche.

english_meaning: An economy is the money and businesses of a country or region.
english_example: The factory was good for the economy because it brought jobs to the area.
spanish_meaning: La economía es el sistema de dinero y negocios de una región.
spanish_example: La fábrica benefició a la economía al dar trabajos en la zona.

english_meaning: An empire is a large group of countries ruled by an emperor or empress.
english_example: The emperor built roads to make travel easier throughout the empire.
spanish_meaning: Un imperio es un gran grupo de países gobernados por un emperador.
spanish_example: El emperador construyó carreteras para viajar mejor por su imperio.

english_meaning: Goods are anything that can be bought or sold.
english_example: Shoes, hats, dresses, and purses were the goods she wanted to buy.
spanish_meaning: Los bienes son cosas que se pueden comprar o vender.
spanish_example: Zapatos, sombreros y bolsos eran los bienes que quería comprar.

english_meaning: To heed something means to obey or follow it.
english_example: You should heed the advice on the sign and not drive so fast.
spanish_meaning: Obedecer o atender una norma es cumplirla.
spanish_example: Debes obedecer el cartel y no conducir tan rápido.

english_meaning: To hitchhike means to travel by asking for rides from passing vehicles.
english_example: She didn’t have a car, so she hitchhiked several miles to her brother’s home.
spanish_meaning: Hacer autostop significa viajar pidiendo aventones en coches.
spanish_example: No tenía coche, así que viajó con autostop varios kilómetros.

english_meaning: To mock someone means to tease them in a cruel way.
english_example: The girls mocked Nancy because she was a new student.
spanish_meaning: Burlarse de alguien significa reírse cruelmente de él.
spanish_example: Las chicas burlaron a Nancy por ser nueva.

english_meaning: If someone is neutral, then they do not help either of the two fighting sides.
english_example: The girl’s friend remained neutral while the couple was arguing.
spanish_meaning: Neutral significa no apoyar a ninguna de las partes en conflicto.
spanish_example: La amiga de la pareja se mantuvo neutral mientras discutían.

english_meaning: To persecute means to treat someone badly.
english_example: Dan felt persecuted because he was smaller than the other boys at school.
spanish_meaning: Perseguir a alguien significa tratarlo injustamente o violentamente.
spanish_example: Dan se sentía perseguido porque era más pequeño que los demás.

english_meaning: Pity is the feeling of sadness and kindness for those who are suffering.
english_example: Because she had pity for the lost boy, she helped him find his parents.
spanish_meaning: La piedad o compasión es la tristeza amable hacia quien sufre.
spanish_example: Sintió mucha piedad por el niño perdido y lo ayudó a encontrar a sus padres.

english_meaning: To reduce something means to make it smaller in size or fewer in number.
english_example: When the store reduced its prices, people wanted to shop there.
spanish_meaning: Reducir significa hacer más pequeño o menos.
spanish_example: Cuando la tienda redujo precios, más personas quisieron comprar.

english_meaning: A scribe was a person whose job was to copy written works.
english_example: In ancient Egypt, scribes recorded important events.
spanish_meaning: Un escriba era una persona que copiaba textos escritos.
spanish_example: En el Egipto antiguo, los escribas registraban eventos importantes.

english_meaning: A temper is someone’s mood or a chance that they might get angry.
english_example: She has a temper. Even the slightest mistakes make her angry.
spanish_meaning: El temperamento es el carácter o estado de ánimo de alguien.
spanish_example: Ella tiene un temperamento fuerte; los errores pequeños la enfadan.

english_meaning: A throne is the special chair in which a ruler sits.
english_example: Nobody except the king and queen sat in the thrones in the great hall.
spanish_meaning: Un trono es el asiento del gobernante.
spanish_example: Nadie, salvo el rey y la reina, se sentó en el trono.

english_meaning: Unity is the state of people working together for a certain purpose.
english_example: The project was finished early thanks to the unity of the workers.
spanish_meaning: La unidad es trabajar juntos hacia un objetivo común.
spanish_example: El proyecto se terminó pronto gracias a la unidad del equipo.

english_meaning: A victor is a group or person that wins in a contest.
english_example: At the end of the game, the blue team were the victors.
spanish_meaning: Un vencedor es quien gana una competencia.
