spanish_example: Al final del juego, los azules fueron los vencedores.

english_meaning: If something is accurate, it is completely correct.
english_example: The story in the newspaper wasn’t very accurate.
spanish_meaning: Algo preciso es totalmente correcto.
spanish_example: La información del periódico no era muy precisa.

english_meaning: To analyze something is to study it.
english_example: The scientist will analyze the blood sample.
spanish_meaning: Analizar algo significa estudiarlo con detalle.
spanish_example: El científico analizará la muestra de sangre.

english_meaning: An asteroid is a giant rock from outer space.
english_example: In 1908, a giant asteroid hit Siberia.
spanish_meaning: Un asteroide es una gran roca del espacio.
spanish_example: En 1908, un gran asteroide impactó Siberia.

english_meaning: A controversy is a dispute about something that affects many people.
english_example: There has been a lot of controversy over the judge’s decision.
spanish_meaning: Una controversia es un desacuerdo que afecta a muchos.
spanish_example: Ha habido mucha controversia sobre la decisión del juez.

english_meaning: To evolve is to change over time.
english_example: Many people think that humans evolved from animals.
spanish_meaning: Evolucionar significa cambiar con el tiempo.
spanish_example: Mucha gente piensa que los humanos evolucionaron desde otros animales.

english_meaning: A factor is something that has an effect on the way another thing happens.
english_example: Smoking is the main factor that causes lung cancer.
spanish_meaning: Un factor es algo que influye en cómo sucede algo.
spanish_example: Fumar es el principal factor que causa cáncer de pulmón.

english_meaning: If something is genetic, it is related to the genes in one’s body.
english_example: The color of one’s eyes is genetic.
spanish_meaning: Genético se refiere a los genes del cuerpo.
spanish_example: El color de ojos es genético.

english_meaning: A genome is the collection of all the genes in a living thing.
english_example: Understanding the human genome may help cure many diseases.
spanish_meaning: El genoma es el conjunto de genes de un organismo vivo.
spanish_example: Entender el genoma humano puede ayudar a curar enfermedades.

english_meaning: To be identical is to be the same as someone or something else.
english_example: James and John are identical twins.
spanish_meaning: Idéntico significa exactamente igual.
spanish_example: James y John son gemelos idénticos.

english_meaning: An intellectual is a very smart person.
english_example: We’ve always considered my Uncle Max to be the intellectual of the family.
spanish_meaning: Un intelectual es una persona muy culta o pensante.
spanish_example: Siempre hemos visto a mi tío Max como el intelectual de la familia.

english_meaning: A majority of something is more than half of the people or things in that group.
english_example: A majority of the people voted for Tom Smith in the election.
spanish_meaning: La mayoría significa más de la mitad de un grupo.
spanish_example: Una mayoría votó por Tom Smith en la elección.

english_meaning: A mammal is an animal that usually has hair and is not born from an egg.
english_example: Even though they live in water, whales are actually mammals.
spanish_meaning: Un mamífero es un animal con pelo y que no nace de huevo.
spanish_example: Aunque viven en el agua, las ballenas son mamíferos.

english_meaning: To multiply is to increase in number.
english_example: In the past year, the number of people at work has multiplied by ten percent.
spanish_meaning: Multiplicar significa aumentar en número.
spanish_example: En el año pasado, la gente del trabajo se multiplicó un diez por ciento.

english_meaning: Offspring are the children of a person or the babies of an animal.
english_example: The dog’s offspring had the same color of fur as she did.
spanish_meaning: La descendencia son los hijos de una persona o animal.
spanish_example: La descendencia del perro tenía el mismo color de pelo.

english_meaning: A pesticide is a substance used to kill insects.
english_example: The farmer sprayed his crops with a pesticide to keep bugs away.
spanish_meaning: Un pesticida es una sustancia para eliminar insectos.
spanish_example: El agricultor roció sus cultivos con un pesticida.

english_meaning: To regulate something is to control how it happens.
english_example: The bank regulates how much money people can borrow.
spanish_meaning: Regular significa controlar cómo algo sucede.
spanish_example: El banco regula cuánto dinero puede pedir préstamo la gente.

english_meaning: To reinforce something is to make it stronger.
english_example: Peter reinforced his opinion with information from a book.
spanish_meaning: Reforzar algo es hacerlo más fuerte.
spanish_example: Peter reforzó su opinión con datos de un libro.

english_meaning: If someone or something is stricken by a disease or problem, they are badly affected by it.
english_example: The pilot landed the stricken airplane with difficulty.
spanish_meaning: Afligido significa gravemente afectado por enfermedad o dolor.
spanish_example: El piloto aterrizó el avión afligido con dificultad.

english_meaning: If something is vast, it is very large.
english_example: The wealthy man bought a vast amount of land in the countryside.
spanish_meaning: Vasto significa muy grande o extenso.
spanish_example: El millonario compró una cantidad vasta de tierra.

english_meaning: A vegetarian is someone who does not eat any meat products.
english_example: I became a vegetarian because I don’t like the taste of meat.
spanish_meaning: Un vegetariano no come carnes.
spanish_example: Me hice vegetariano porque no me gusta el sabor de la carne.

english_meaning: A bowl is a dish with a round deep shape.
english_example: I put the fruit in the fruit bowl.
spanish_meaning: Un cuenco es un plato hondo y redondo.
spanish_example: Puse la fruta en el cuenco.

english_meaning: Cooking food involves making it ready to eat.
english_example: I really enjoy cooking, especially Italian food.
spanish_meaning: Cocinar es preparar la comida para comer.
spanish_example: Me encanta cocinar, sobre todo comida italiana.

english_meaning: If something is dense, it has a lot of things close together.
english_example: I easily became lost in the dense forest.
spanish_meaning: Denso significa concentrado, con muchas cosas juntas.
spanish_example: Me perdí fácilmente en el bosque denso.

english_meaning: Dignity is the ability to be calm and worthy of respect.
english_example: When his company went out of business, he faced it with dignity.
spanish_meaning: La dignidad es ser digno de respeto y mantener la calma.
spanish_example: Cuando cerró su empresa, lo aceptó con mucha dignidad.

english_meaning: To dominate someone or something is to control them.
english_example: The loud man dominated the conversation.
spanish_meaning: Dominar a alguien significa tener control sobre él.
spanish_example: El hombre más ruidoso dominó la conversación.

english_meaning: If something is earthen, it is made of clay.
english_example: The house’s roof was made of earthen tiles.
spanish_meaning: Algo de barro está hecho con arcilla.
spanish_example: El techo de la casa estaba hecho con tejas de barro.

english_meaning: If someone is hostile, they are angry and unfriendly.
english_example: We were happy to move away from our hostile neighbor.
spanish_meaning: Hostil significa enojado y poco amistoso.
spanish_example: Estábamos contentos de mudarnos lejos de nuestro vecino hostil.

english_meaning: If something is incorrect, it is wrong.
english_example: Your answer is incorrect. You need to correct it.
spanish_meaning: Incorrecto significa no correcto.
spanish_example: Tu respuesta es incorrecta; corrígela.

english_meaning: Your intake of food is the amount of food you take into your body.
english_example: The doctor said I needed to increase my intake of fruits and vegetables.
spanish_meaning: La ingesta es la cantidad de alimento que entra al cuerpo.
spanish_example: El doctor dijo que aumentara mi ingesta de frutas y verduras.

english_meaning: If someone does something likewise, they do the same thing as someone else.
english_example: If Joe is not going to school so he can go swimming, I want to do likewise.
spanish_meaning: Asimismo significa de la misma manera.
spanish_example: Si Joe no va a la escuela para ir a nadar, yo quiero hacer asimismo.

english_meaning: A machine is made by people and does work for us.
english_example: The machine was broken.
spanish_meaning: Una máquina es un artefacto con piezas que hace trabajo por nosotros.
spanish_example: La máquina se estropeó.

english_meaning: Medication is medicine or drugs given to people who are sick.
english_example: The doctor gave me medication to treat my illness.
spanish_meaning: Un medicamento es fármaco para tratar una enfermedad.
spanish_example: El médico me recetó un medicamento para mi dolencia.

english_meaning: If something is obscure, it is not well known.
english_example: The old man travels the world in search of obscure books.
spanish_meaning: Oscuro significa poco conocido o poco claro.
spanish_example: El hombre viejo viaja por el mundo buscando obras poco conocidas.

english_meaning: To oppress someone means to rule over them in a cruel and unfair way.
english_example: Free speech had been oppressed in his country.
spanish_meaning: Oprimir a alguien significa dominarlo de forma cruel e injusta.
spanish_example: La libertad de expresión había sido oprimida en su país.

english_meaning: To peel fruits and vegetables is to remove their skin.
english_example: He peeled the apple before eating it.
spanish_meaning: Pelar frutas y verduras es quitarles la piel.
spanish_example: Peleó la manzana antes de comerla.

english_meaning: A prescription is permission from a doctor to get medicine.
english_example: The doctor gave me a prescription for my medication.
spanish_meaning: Una receta médica es el permiso del médico para conseguir un medicamento.
spanish_example: El médico me dio una receta para mis medicinas.

english_meaning: When you make progress, you get better at what you are doing.
english_example: There are clear signs of progress.
spanish_meaning: Progreso significa mejorar en algo.
spanish_example: Hay señales claras de progreso.

english_meaning: Sandy means covered with sand.
english_example: I love walking on a sandy beach.
spanish_meaning: Arenoso significa cubierto de arena.
spanish_example: Me encanta caminar por una playa arenosa.

english_meaning: Shortage comes from short and means not having enough.
english_example: During the dry season, there was a shortage of water.
spanish_meaning: Escasez significa falta o insuficiencia de algo.
spanish_example: Durante la estación seca, hubo escasez de agua.

english_meaning: If someone is skillful at something, they are very good at doing it.
english_example: The carpenter is very skillful at building houses.
spanish_meaning: Ser hábil es ser muy bueno en algo.
spanish_example: Trabajó con mucha habilidad y confianza.

english_meaning: An aircraft is a vehicle that flies in the sky, such as an airplane or helicopter.
english_example: At the museum in the airport, you can see a lot of old aircraft.
spanish_meaning: Una aeronave es un vehículo que vuela, como avión o helicóptero.
spanish_example: En el museo del aeropuerto hay muchas aeronaves antiguas.

english_meaning: A celebrity is someone who is famous.
english_example: It was the highlight of the evening when the celebrities arrived.
spanish_meaning: Una celebridad es una persona famosa.
spanish_example: La fiesta fue especial cuando llegaron las celebridades.

english_meaning: Concrete is a substance made from stones.
english_example: The man covered the ground with concrete.
spanish_meaning: Concreto es una mezcla dura usada para construir.
spanish_example: El hombre cubrió el suelo con concreto.

english_meaning: If someone is decisive, they make decisions quickly.
english_example: Our boss is very decisive, so it did not take long to organize the project.
spanish_meaning: Decisivo significa tomar decisiones con rapidez.
spanish_example: Nuestro jefe es muy decisivo, así que organizamos el proyecto enseguida.

english_meaning: If someone is esteemed, many people like or respect them.
english_example: An esteemed scientist is coming to the university to talk about his discoveries.
spanish_meaning: Estimado significa muy respetado por los demás.
spanish_example: Un científico estimado vino a la universidad a hablar de sus hallazgos.

english_meaning: If something is ethical, it is the right thing to do.
english_example: Many people believe that it is ethical to help others in need.
spanish_meaning: Ético significa moralmente correcto.
spanish_example: Muchos piensan que es ético ayudar a quienes necesitan apoyo.

english_meaning: If plants or animals are extinct, there are none left.
english_example: There used to be dinosaurs all over the world, but now they are extinct.
spanish_meaning: Extinto significa que ya no existe.
spanish_example: Los dinosaurios eran comunes y ahora están extintos.

english_meaning: If a person or plant is hardy, it is strong and can live through difficult conditions.
english_example: The farmer is a hardy man and doesn’t mind working outside.
spanish_meaning: Resistente significa fuerte y capaz de soportar dificultades.
spanish_example: El granjero es un hombre resistente y no le importa trabajar fuera.

english_meaning: An institute is an organization that is interested in research or teaching.
english_example: I am going to a lecture about Ancient Rome at the Historical Institute.
spanish_meaning: Un instituto es una organización dedicada a la enseñanza o investigación.
spanish_example: Voy a una conferencia en el Instituto Histórico sobre Roma antigua.

english_meaning: Jealousy is a feeling of wanting something that somebody else has.
english_example: She felt a lot of jealousy when she saw her friend with a new car.
spanish_meaning: Los celos son el deseo de tener lo que otra persona posee.
spanish_example: Sintió celos al ver a su amiga con un coche nuevo.

english_meaning: To migrate means to move from one place to another.
english_example: Many birds migrate to warmer countries in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Migrar significa mudarse de un lugar a otro.
spanish_example: Muchos pájaros migran a lugares más cálidos en invierno.

english_meaning: To nurture something means to care for it as it grows or develops.
english_example: Robert nurtured his plants, and that is why they grow so well.
spanish_meaning: Criar algo es cuidarlo mientras crece y se desarrolla.
spanish_example: Robert crió sus plantas, y por eso crecieron tan bien.

english_meaning: If something is overhead, it is located above you.
english_example: As we sat on the top of the hill, a plane flew overhead.
spanish_meaning: Sobre algo significa encima de ti.
spanish_example: Nos sentamos en la colina y un avión voló sobre nuestras cabezas.

english_meaning: A principle is a belief about the correct way to behave.
english_example: To maintain principles, it’s vital to watch, listen, and speak carefully.
spanish_meaning: Un principio es una creencia sobre cómo actuar.
spanish_example: Mantener los principios exige escuchar, mirar y hablar con cuidado.

english_meaning: If a place is rural, it is in the countryside instead of the city.
english_example: I want to live in a small house in a rural area.
spanish_meaning: Rural significa relacionado con el campo.
spanish_example: Quiero vivir en una casa pequeña en una zona rural.

english_meaning: If a place is secluded, it is far away from any other place.
english_example: There was a secluded bench in the park.
spanish_meaning: Aislado significa lejos de otros sitios.
spanish_example: Había un banco aislado en el parque.

english_meaning: A species is a type of plant or animal.
english_example: There are 21 different species of butterfly in this forest.
spanish_meaning: Una especie es un tipo de planta o animal.
spanish_example: Hay 21 especies de mariposa en este bosque.

english_meaning: A swamp is a very wet area of land.
english_example: There are lots of wild animals living in the swamp.
spanish_meaning: Un pantano es un lugar de tierra muy húmedo.
spanish_example: Muchos animales viven en el pantano.

english_meaning: To traverse means to move or travel through an area.
english_example: The explorer traversed the desert alone on a camel.
spanish_meaning: Atravesar significa pasar por o viajar a través de un lugar.
spanish_example: El explorador atravesó el desierto sobre un camello.

english_meaning: Zoology is a subject in which people study animals.
english_example: Helen wants to study zoology because she has always liked animals.
spanish_meaning: La zoología es la ciencia que estudia animales.
spanish_example: Helen quiere estudiar zoología porque siempre le gustaron los animales.

english_meaning: If someone acts accordingly, they act in a way that is suitable.
english_example: He feels like he did a good job and his boss should pay him accordingly.
spanish_meaning: Actuar en consecuencia significa actuar de manera apropiada.
spanish_example: Hizo un buen trabajo y el jefe debería pagarle en consecuencia.

english_meaning: An anchor is a heavy object dropped from a boat to make it stay in one place.
english_example: When the ship reached its destination, the crew dropped the anchor.
spanish_meaning: Una ancla es un peso de metal o piedra que mantiene un barco en su sitio.
spanish_example: Al llegar al puerto, la tripulación arrojó el ancla.

english_meaning: When you cause something, you make it happen.
english_example: Tiredness caused the accident.
spanish_meaning: Causar algo significa hacerlo suceder.
spanish_example: El cansancio causó el accidente.

english_meaning: Context is the situations that form the background of an event.
english_example: They studied the context of the battle before giving their presentation.
spanish_meaning: El contexto es el entorno de una situación o evento.
spanish_example: Estudiaron el contexto de la batalla antes de la presentación.

english_meaning: To designate someone or something means to give them a particular description.
english_example: The famous lighthouse was designated as a historical monument.
spanish_meaning: Designar significa nombrar o señalar para un rol.
spanish_example: El faro famoso fue designado como monumento histórico.

english_meaning: To distort something means to lie about it, or pull or twist out of shape.
english_example: The man’s lawyer distorted the facts so that he would be set free.
spanish_meaning: Distorsionar algo es cambiarlo, torciéndolo o alterando la verdad.
spanish_example: El abogado distorsionó los hechos para que quedara libre.

english_meaning: A dock is an enclosed area where ships go to be loaded, unloaded, and repaired.
english_example: The huge ship pulled into the dock, and the crew unloaded the cargo.
spanish_meaning: Un muelle es el lugar donde atracan y cargan los barcos.
spanish_example: El gran barco entró en el muelle y descargaron la carga.

english_meaning: If you have a lot of energy, you have plenty of strength and can do lots of things.
english_example: If you lack energy, try eating better food.
spanish_meaning: La energía es la fuerza o capacidad para hacer muchas cosas.
spanish_example: Si no tienes energía, come mejor.

english_meaning: If something is frequent, then it happens or is done often.
english_example: While Dad was sick, the doctor made frequent visits to his house.
spanish_meaning: Frecuente significa que sucede muchas veces.
spanish_example: Mientras papá estaba enfermo, el médico hizo visitas frecuentes.

english_meaning: The gears are the part of a motor that controls the speed.
english_example: My brother has a car with four gears.
spanish_meaning: Los engranajes son las partes de una máquina que controlan la velocidad.
spanish_example: Mi hermano tiene un coche con cuatro engranajes.

english_meaning: When something is genuine, it is true or real.
english_example: After the painting was determined to be genuine, it sold for a million dollars.
spanish_meaning: Genuino significa verdadero o auténtico.
spanish_example: Tras comprobar que el cuadro era genuino, se vendió por un millón.

english_meaning: Grease is an oily substance put on moving parts so they work smoothly, or oil or fat in cooking.
english_example: When I was done working on the car, I had grease all over my hands.
spanish_meaning: La grasa es una sustancia aceitosa usada en piezas móviles o en cocina.
spanish_example: Al terminar el coche, tenía grasa en las manos.

english_meaning: Knowledge is what you know.
english_example: He has trouble putting his knowledge into practice.
spanish_meaning: El conocimiento es lo que sabes.
spanish_example: Le cuesta aplicar su conocimiento en la práctica.

english_meaning: When you omit something, you leave it out or do not do it.
english_example: I omitted some important information: he is not coming.
spanish_meaning: Omitir algo significa dejarlo fuera o no hacerlo.
spanish_example: omití información importante: él no viene.

english_meaning: To offset means to use one thing to cancel out the effect of another thing.
english_example: Increased wages are offset by higher prices for goods.
spanish_meaning: Compensar es usar algo para contrarrestar otra cosa.
spanish_example: El aumento de salarios se compensa con precios más altos.

english_meaning: To overlap something means to cover a piece of it.
english_example: The gift on top overlaps the other gift on the bottom.
spanish_meaning: Superponer algo es cubrir una parte con otra.
spanish_example: El regalo de arriba se superpone al de abajo.

english_meaning: When something is secondhand, it has been owned by someone else.
english_example: Her secondhand jeans were a bit faded in the front.
spanish_meaning: De segunda mano significa que lo usó otra persona.
spanish_example: Sus vaqueras de segunda mano estaban un poco gastadas.

english_meaning: When you have skill at doing a job, you are good at doing it.
english_example: She worked with great skill and confidence.
spanish_meaning: La habilidad es la destreza para hacer bien un trabajo.
spanish_example: Trabajó con gran habilidad y seguridad.

english_meaning: A slot is a narrow opening in a machine or container.
english_example: To operate the machine, put your coins into the slot.
spanish_meaning: Una ranura es una hendidura para meter monedas o piezas.
spanish_example: Para usar la máquina, mete las monedas en la ranura.

english_meaning: A tactic is a careful plan to achieve something.
english_example: Sam thought of a good tactic to attract more business.
spanish_meaning: Una táctica es un plan ordenado para lograr algo.
spanish_example: Sam pensó en una buena táctica para atraer más clientes.

english_meaning: If something is beneficial, it is good for you.
english_example: Drinking milk every day is beneficial for your bones.
spanish_meaning: Algo beneficioso es bueno para la salud o para la vida.
spanish_example: Beber leche todos los días es beneficioso para los huesos.

english_meaning: A birthplace is a place where a person is born or where something started.
english_example: China is the birthplace of chopsticks.
spanish_meaning: El lugar de nacimiento es donde una persona nace.
spanish_example: China es el lugar de nacimiento de los palillos.

english_meaning: The capacity of something is the amount of things that can be put in it.
english_example: The parking lot has reached its full capacity.
spanish_meaning: La capacidad es la cantidad que un espacio puede contener.
spanish_example: El estacionamiento llegó a su máxima capacidad.

english_meaning: If something is comparative, it is being judged based on something else.
english_example: The money that John has is comparative to that of most other adults.
spanish_meaning: Comparativo significa que se compara con otra cosa.
spanish_example: El dinero de John es comparativo al de la mayoría de adultos.

english_meaning: If something is comprehensive, it has all the details about something else.
english_example: The teacher gave us a comprehensive review for the exam.
spanish_meaning: Completo significa que incluye muchos detalles.
spanish_example: El profesor nos dio una revisión completa para el examen.

english_meaning: To conserve something is to protect it from being ruined or used completely.
english_example: The group worked to conserve the beauty of Europe’s national parks.
spanish_meaning: Conservar algo es protegerlo de perderse o deteriorarse.
spanish_example: El grupo trabajó para conservar la belleza de los parques nacionales.

english_meaning: If something is crucial, it is extremely important to another thing.
english_example: Clean air is crucial to the survival of humans, plants, and animals.
spanish_meaning: Crucial significa extremadamente importante.
spanish_example: El aire limpio es crucial para la vida.

english_meaning: Cumulative describes an increase by adding one after another.
english_example: The cumulative snowfall in the area is fifty centimeters per year.
spanish_meaning: Cumulativo significa que se suma poco a poco.
spanish_example: La nieve cumulativa en la zona llega a cincuenta centímetros al año.

english_meaning: To deposit something is to put it into a place or another thing.
english_example: I deposited the money into my bank account.
spanish_meaning: Depositar algo es colocarlo en un lugar, como una cuenta o caja.
spanish_example: Deposité el dinero en mi cuenta bancaria.

english_meaning: To distribute something is to give it to a number of people.
english_example: The teacher distributed crayons and markers to her students.
spanish_meaning: Distribuir significa dar o repartir algo entre varias personas.
spanish_example: La maestra distribuyo crayones y rotulos a sus estudiantes.

english_meaning: The equator is an imaginary line that splits the Earth into north and south.
english_example: The equator crosses the northern part of South America.
spanish_meaning: El ecuador es una linea imaginaria que divide la Tierra entre norte y sur.
spanish_example: El ecuador atraviesa la parte norte de America del Sur.

english_meaning: Exotic describes something unusual because it is from far away.
english_example: Rebecca tried many exotic foods on her trip to Africa.
spanish_meaning: Exotico describe algo inusual porque viene de un lugar lejano.
spanish_example: Rebecca probó muchas comidas exóticas en su viaje a Africa.

english_meaning: If something is federal, it relates to the government of a country.
english_example: Sometimes federal laws are different from state laws.
spanish_meaning: Algo federal está relacionado con el gobierno de un país.
spanish_example: A veces las leyes federales son diferentes de las estatales.

english_meaning: A formation is the way that something is made.
english_example: The formation of ice happens when water freezes.
spanish_meaning: La formacion es la manera en que algo se forma.
spanish_example: La formacion del hielo ocurre cuando el agua se congela.

english_meaning: The frequency of something is the number of times that it happens.
english_example: The frequency of rainstorms is very high, especially during the spring.
spanish_meaning: La frecuencia es el numero de veces que algo sucede.
spanish_example: La frecuencia de tormentas de lluvia es muy alta en primavera.

english_meaning: An objective is a goal or plan that someone has.
english_example: My objective this week is to finish my homework by 7:30 every night.
spanish_meaning: Un objetivo es una meta o plan.
spanish_example: Mi objetivo está semana es terminar mi tarea todos los días a las 7:30 de la noche.

english_meaning: Oxygen is a gas that all living things need to breathe.
english_example: She thinks there is more oxygen in the air in the country than in the city.
spanish_meaning: El oxígeno es un gas que los seres vivos necesitan para respirar.
spanish_example: En el campo suele haber más oxígeno en el aire que en la ciudad.

english_meaning: A rainforest is a forest that is in a place where it rains very often.
english_example: The rainforest is home to many animals.
spanish_meaning: La selva tropical es un bosque muy lluvioso.
spanish_example: La selva tropical es hogar de muchos animales.

english_meaning: A strategy is a plan for how to do something.
english_example: The team came up with a strategy to win the game.
spanish_meaning: Una estrategia es un plan para lograr algo.
spanish_example: El equipo invento una estrategia para ganar el juego.

english_meaning: If an area is wooded, it is covered with trees.
english_example: Jim and Ben decided to go hiking in the wooded area by the river.
spanish_meaning: Un área arbolada está cubierta de árboles.
spanish_example: Jim y Ben decidieron hacer senderismo en la zona arbolada junto al río.

english_meaning: Avail is help or benefit.
english_example: His studying was to no avail because he failed the test.
spanish_meaning: El provecho o la ayuda que algo proporciona.
spanish_example: Su estudio fue de poco provecho porque no aprobo el examen.

english_meaning: To define means to clearly state, show, or explain what something is.
english_example: People define success in many different ways.
spanish_meaning: Definir es expresar con claridad que cosa es algo.
spanish_example: Las personas definen el exito de muchas maneras distintas.

english_meaning: To dread is to be afraid of something that could, or is going to, happen.
english_example: I dread the possibility that I will not get into college.
spanish_meaning: Tener temor o miedo de que algo pueda pasar.
spanish_example: Temo la posibilidad de que no entre a la universidad.

english_meaning: To expand is to become bigger in size.
english_example: A balloon will expand as you blow air into it.
spanish_meaning: Expandir significa hacerse más grande.
spanish_example: Un globo se expande cuando soplas aire dentro.

english_meaning: If something is fundamental, it is a basic part of something.
english_example: The fundamental rules of basketball are easy.
spanish_meaning: Algo fundamental es una parte básica e importante.
spanish_example: Las reglas fundamentales del baloncesto son fáciles.

english_meaning: If something is horrifying, it is frightening and very unpleasant.
english_example: There was a horrifying car accident today.
spanish_meaning: Algo horripilante es aterrador y muy desagradable.
spanish_example: Hubo un accidente de auto horripilante hoy.

english_meaning: If someone is incredulous about something, they do not believe that it is true.
english_example: She was incredulous that monkeys could ever drive a car.
spanish_meaning: Es incredulo quien no cree que algo sea verdad.
spanish_example: Ella estaba incredula de que los monos pudieran conducir un auto.

english_meaning: To linger is to last for a long time.
english_example: The smell of fresh cookies lingered in the bakery.
spanish_meaning: Perdurar significa durar mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: El olor a galletas recién hechas perduro en la panaderia.

english_meaning: An organism is a living thing, especially a very small one.
english_example: We studied the organism under the microscope.
spanish_meaning: Un organismo es un ser vivo, especialmente uno pequeño.
spanish_example: Estudiamos el organismo bajo el microscopio.

english_meaning: To paraphrase is to make someone else’s writing or speech shorter.
english_example: The students were asked to paraphrase the story they had just heard.
spanish_meaning: Parafrasear es decir lo mismo con menos palabras.
spanish_example: Alumnos tuvieron que parafrasear la historia que acababan de oir.

english_meaning: A plague is a serious disease that quickly spreads to many people.
english_example: A plague in Europe killed millions of people.
spanish_meaning: Una peste es una enfermedad grave que se propaga rapidamente.
spanish_example: Una peste en Europa mato a millones de personas.

english_meaning: If something happens presently, it is happening right now.
english_example: Presently, our profits are good, but by next year we can do even better.
spanish_meaning: Actualmente significa en este momento.
spanish_example: Actualmente nuestras ganancias son buenas, pero el proximo año podemos hacerlo mejor.
notes: ambiguo entre "actualmente" y "proximamente" en otros contextos

english_meaning: If something is random, it happens without any pattern or reason.
english_example: Young children often ask random questions.
spanish_meaning: Cosas aleatorias suceden sin patrón ni razón aparente.
spanish_example: Los niños pequeños hacen preguntas aleatorias con frecuencia.

english_meaning: A riot is a crowd that reacts to bad news by violently breaking laws.
english_example: A riot broke out after the candidate lost the election.
spanish_meaning: Un disturbio es una multitud que responde con violencia y desorden.
spanish_example: después de la derrota electoral estallo un disturbio.

english_meaning: To scribble is to write something quickly without caring about how it looks.
english_example: I scribbled a rough diagram of our plan and gave it to him.
spanish_meaning: Garabatear es escribir rapido sin cuidar la letra.
spanish_example: yo garabatee un dibujo rapido de nuestro plan y se lo di.

english_meaning: A shrine is a religious building built to honor a person, event, or god.
english_example: He prayed at the shrine for an hour.
spanish_meaning: Un santuario es un edificio religioso de devocion.
spanish_example: Oró durante una hora en el santuario.

english_meaning: Solitude is the state of being totally alone.
english_example: John lives a life of solitude because he doesn’t get along well with people.
spanish_meaning: La soledad es estar completamente solo.
spanish_example: John vive una vida de soledad porque no se lleva bien con la gente.

english_meaning: If something is stark, then it is severe or clear in appearance or outline.
english_example: There is a stark contrast between their test scores.
spanish_meaning: Algo extremo/severo es muy marcado.
spanish_example: Hay un contraste extremo entre sus puntajes.

english_meaning: To summon a person is to ask them to come to you.
english_example: We summoned the doctor as soon as we noticed she was sick.
spanish_meaning: Convocar significa llamar a alguien para que venga.
spanish_example: Convocamos al doctor apenas notamos que estaba enferma.

english_meaning: To worsen is to get worse.
english_example: The weather suddenly worsened, and we had to stay inside.
spanish_meaning: Empeorar es volverse peor.
spanish_example: El clima empeoro de repente y tuvimos que quedarnos adentro.

english_meaning: An automobile is a car.
english_example: The first automobiles were very different from the ones that exist today.
spanish_meaning: Un automóvil es un coche.
spanish_example: Los primeros automóviles eran muy diferentes a los de hoy.

english_meaning: A candidate is a person who is competing to win something such as a job.
english_example: Alice is the best candidate for the job.
spanish_meaning: Un candidato compite por ganar algo, como un empleo.
spanish_example: Alice es la mejor candidata para el puesto.

english_meaning: If something is confidential, it must be kept secret.
english_example: The information from the meeting is confidential.
spanish_meaning: Confidencial significa que debe mantenerse en secreto.
spanish_example: La informacion de la reunion es confidencial.

english_meaning: If something is corporate, it is related to a large business.
english_example: Tom enjoys working in the corporate world.
spanish_meaning: Cosas corporativas pertenecen a una gran empresa.
spanish_example: A Tom le gusta trabajar en el mundo corporativo.

english_meaning: To enhance something is to make it better.
english_example: Amy’s blue shirt really enhances the color of her eyes.
spanish_meaning: Mejorar significa hacer algo más bueno.
spanish_example: La camisa azul de Amy mejora mucho el color de sus ojos.

english_meaning: An era is a period of time that has something special about it.
english_example: During the medieval era, knights wore protective armor.
spanish_meaning: Una era es un período histórico con características propias.
spanish_example: En la era medieval, los caballeros usaban armadura.

english_meaning: A guideline is a rule about how to do something.
english_example: Before they began the project, the teacher gave them some guidelines.
spanish_meaning: Una guia es una regla o norma para actuar.
spanish_example: Antes de iniciar el proyecto, el profesor dio algunas directrices.

english_meaning: To incorporate is to add something to another thing.
english_example: I decided to incorporate a new ingredient into my cake recipe.
spanish_meaning: Incorporar es agregar una cosa a otra.
spanish_example: Decidi incorporar un nuevo ingrediente en mi receta de pastel.

english_meaning: To interact is to talk to or do something with another person.
english_example: The kids began to interact when the adults left the room.
spanish_meaning: Interactuar es comunicarte o actuar con otra persona.
spanish_example: Los niños comenzaron a interactuar cuando los adultos se fueron.

english_meaning: An interval is the time between two things happening.
english_example: Tony rested for brief intervals while he worked in the yard.
spanish_meaning: El intervalo es el tiempo entre dos momentos.
spanish_example: Tony descanso en intervalos cortos mientras trabajaba en el patio.

english_meaning: If something is mobile, it can be moved easily.
english_example: Mobile phones are convenient because you can use them anywhere.
spanish_meaning: Móvil significa que puede trasladarse o estar disponible.
spanish_example: Los celulares móviles son prácticos porque se pueden usar en cualquier lugar.

english_meaning: To modify something is to change it a little bit.
english_example: I modified my outfit by adding a belt to it.
spanish_meaning: Modificar es cambiar algo levemente.
spanish_example: Modifique mi atuendo al agregarle un cinturón.

english_meaning: If two things are parallel, they are the same distance away from each other (along their entire length).
english_example: There are two yellow parallel lines dividing both sides of traffic.
spanish_meaning: Paralelo significa que corre al mismo nivel o distancia.
spanish_example: En la carretera hay dos lineas paralelas amarillas en ambos sentidos.

english_meaning: A phenomenon is something that can be seen as it is happening.
english_example: I thought the lights were a strange phenomenon, but they were just shooting stars.
spanish_meaning: Un fenomeno es algo observable que ocurre.
spanish_example: Pensé que las luces eran un fenomeno raro, pero eran simples estrellas fugaces.

english_meaning: To pollute means to make air, water, or land dirty, unclean, or foul.
english_example: The careless factory polluted the river with chemicals.
spanish_meaning: Contaminar es ensuciar aire, agua o tierra.
spanish_example: La fábrica descuidada contaminó el río con químicos.

english_meaning: To ridicule is to make fun of something in a mean way.
english_example: The other students ridicule Peter’s foreign accent.
spanish_meaning: Ridiculizar es burlarse de forma cruel.
spanish_example: Los otros alumnos ridiculizaron el acento extranjero de Peter.

english_meaning: If something is solar, it is related to the sun.
english_example: Using solar energy is good for the environment.
spanish_meaning: Solar se refiere al sol.
spanish_example: Usar energia solar es bueno para el medio ambiente.

english_meaning: A territory is a piece of land that belongs to a country but isn’t a state.
english_example: Gibraltar is a territory of Great Britain.
spanish_meaning: Territorio es una zona de tierra que pertenece a un país.
spanish_example: Gibraltar es un territorio del Reino Unido.

english_meaning: A tournament is a competition, usually with many people participating.
english_example: My dad is playing in a golf tournament tomorrow.
spanish_meaning: Un torneo es una competencia con muchos participantes.
spanish_example: Mi padre jugará en un torneo de golf mañana.

english_meaning: Transportation is any type of vehicle that can carry people or things.
english_example: I don’t have a car, so my normal transportation is the train.
spanish_meaning: El transporte es cualquier medio para llevar personas o cosas.
spanish_example: Como no tengo auto, mi transporte normal es el tren.

english_meaning: A bill is a statement of money owed for goods or for a service.
english_example: I have so many bills that I do not know how to pay them all.
spanish_meaning: Una factura o cuenta es dinero que se debe pagar.
spanish_example: Tengo tantas facturas que no se pagar todas.

english_meaning: A boundary is the line where one area of land stops and another begins.
english_example: This fence shows the boundary between our yard and yours.
spanish_meaning: La frontera es la linea que separa dos zonas.
spanish_example: La cerca muestra la frontera entre nuestro patio y el tuyo.

english_meaning: Chaos is a situation that is confusing and not ordered.
english_example: His presentation was in chaos. I couldn’t understand what he meant.
spanish_meaning: El caos es una situación confusa y desordenada.
spanish_example: Su exposición estaba en un caos y no pude entenderlo.

english_meaning: If a person is consistent, they always have the same behavior or attitude.
english_example: Sara comes in every day and is our most consistent worker.
spanish_meaning: Consistente significa constante y sin cambios.
spanish_example: Sara llega todos los días y es nuestra trabajadora más consistente.

english_meaning: A cyclone is a large storm with heavy rain and winds that spin in a circle.
english_example: Hundreds of homes were damaged by the cyclone.
spanish_meaning: Un ciclón es una tormenta muy fuerte con lluvia y vientos circulares.
spanish_example: Cientos de hogares se dañaron por el ciclón.

english_meaning: If someone or something is doomed, they are going to fail or be destroyed.
english_example: Since I spent all my money, my date with Jane is doomed.
spanish_meaning: Condenado significa destinado al fracaso o destruccion.
spanish_example: Mi cita con Jane está condenada desde que gaste todo mi dinero.

english_meaning: An heir is a person who receives the money or property of someone who dies.
english_example: The princess was the heir to the king and queen’s throne.
spanish_meaning: Un heredero recibe bienes o propiedades de alguien fallecido.
spanish_example: La princesa era la heredera del trono del rey y la reina.

english_meaning: If something is martial, it is related to fighting or war.
english_example: Karate is a martial art that began many years ago in Japan.
spanish_meaning: Marcial se relaciona con la guerra o la lucha.
spanish_example: El karate es un arte marcial que comenzo hace muchos años en Japon.

english_meaning: If food is organic, it is grown without adding chemicals to it.
english_example: The organic carrots are more expensive, but they’re better for you.
spanish_meaning: orgánico indica cultivado sin químicos.
spanish_example: Las zanahorias orgánicas son más caras pero son mejores para la salud.

english_meaning: Poultry is a bird, such as a chicken, that is used for meat and eggs.
english_example: He raises poultry and sells their meat for extra money.
spanish_meaning: Las aves de corral son aves criadas para carne y huevos.
spanish_example: Cria aves de corral y vende su carne para ganar dinero extra.

english_meaning: To scramble is to move somewhere quickly and desperately.
english_example: The hikers scrambled down the side of the hill.
spanish_meaning: Apresurarse es moverse rapido y con urgencia.
spanish_example: Los excursionistas se apresuraron cuesta abajo de la colina.

english_meaning: A sergeant is a soldier or police officer of middle rank.
english_example: He was promoted to sergeant after a year in the army.
spanish_meaning: Un sargento es un soldado o policia de rango medio.
spanish_example: Fue ascendido a sargento después de un año en el ejercito.

english_meaning: If you describe something as sheer, it is complete and total.
english_example: I was impressed by her sheer dedication to jogging.
spanish_meaning: Absoluto significa completo o total.
spanish_example: Quede impresionado por su dedicacion absoluta al trote.

english_meaning: A stance is an attitude about an issue that someone states clearly.
english_example: My stance is that using oil and gas is bad for the environment.
spanish_meaning: La postura es una posición o actitud clara ante un tema.
spanish_example: Mi postura es que usar petróleo y gas es malo para el ambiente.

english_meaning: A telegraph is a method of sending electric messages on wires.
english_example: In the 1900s, the telegraph was the fastest way to send a message.
spanish_meaning: El telégrafo es un sistema de mensajes por cable.
spanish_example: A comienzos del siglo XX, el telégrafo era la forma más rápida de enviar mensajes.

english_meaning: Textile is cloth that has been woven or knitted.
english_example: The blue textile was going to be used to make blouses.
spanish_meaning: Lo textil es tela fabricada por tejido o tejido de punto.
spanish_example: La tela azul se iba a usar para hacer blusas.

english_meaning: A tornado is a tube-shaped formation of air that spins very quickly.
english_example: During a tornado, the safest place to be is underground.
spanish_meaning: Un tornado es una tormenta circular muy intensa.
spanish_example: Durante un tornado, el lugar más seguro es estar bajo tierra.

english_meaning: A typhoon is a large tropical storm that moves in circles.
english_example: Thousands of people lost electricity after a typhoon hit Australia.
spanish_meaning: Un tifón es una gran tormenta tropical que gira en espiral.
spanish_example: Miles de personas se quedaron sin electricidad tras el tifón en Australia.

english_meaning: To wail is to show sadness by crying loudly.
english_example: The baby wailed because it was hungry.
spanish_meaning: Llorar a gritos expresa tristeza intensa.
spanish_example: El bebé lloraba a gritos porque tenía hambre.

english_meaning: A wardrobe is the collection of all of a person’s clothing.
english_example: She bought some new clothes to expand her wardrobe.
spanish_meaning: El guardarropa es el conjunto de ropa de una persona.
spanish_example: Compro ropa nueva para ampliar su guardarropa.

english_meaning: Something that has an adverse effect can be harmful, dangerous, or unfavorable.
english_example: I worry that the tornado will have an adverse effect on the farm.
spanish_meaning: Adverso es negativo o perjudicial.
spanish_example: Me preocupa que el tornado tenga un efecto adverso en la granja.

english_meaning: An alternate option is a different option.
english_example: Taking the bus and driving to work are alternate ways to travel.
spanish_meaning: Alternativo significa una opcion distinta.
spanish_example: Ir en autobus o conducir al trabajo son formas alternativas de viajar.

english_meaning: Materials that are biodegradable break down naturally into substances that do not harm the environment.
english_example: I use biodegradable compost to feed my garden.
spanish_meaning: Biodegradable significa que se descompone naturalmente.
spanish_example: Usamos compost compostable para mi huerto.

english_meaning: When something is boxed, it is inside a package.
english_example: Justine brought a boxed gift to the birthday party.
spanish_meaning: En caja significa dentro de un empaque.
spanish_example: Justine trajo un regalo en caja a la fiesta de cumpleanos.

english_meaning: If you choke on something, it stops you from breathing.
english_example: The gum Malinda swallowed made her choke.
spanish_meaning: Atragantarse es dejar de respirar bien al tragar algo.
spanish_example: Malada se atraganto después de tragar un chicle.

english_meaning: When something is convenient, it saves you time or effort.
english_example: Walking through the park is a convenient way to exercise on the way to work.
spanish_meaning: Conveniente significa facil, sin esfuerzo.
spanish_example: Caminar por el parque es una forma conveniente de hacer ejercicio.

english_meaning: To discard something is to throw it away.
english_example: After repairing the window, discard any broken glass.
spanish_meaning: Deshacerse de algo es tirarlo.
spanish_example: después de reparar la ventana, deseche cualquier vidrio roto.

english_meaning: A dolphin is a large sea mammal that breathes air.
english_example: Intelligent dolphins have learned to communicate with humans.
spanish_meaning: El delfin es un mamifero marino grande.
spanish_example: Delfines inteligentes aprendieron a comunicarse con humanos.

english_meaning: Ecologically means that something is done in a way that concerns living organisms and the environment.
english_example: We planted our garden according to ecologically correct practices.
spanish_meaning: Ecologicamente significa de forma cuidadosa con el medio ambiente.
spanish_example: Plantamos el jardin siguiendo practicas ecologicamente correctas.

english_meaning: Something that is fatal results in someone’s death.
english_example: Being shot by the attacker proved fatal to the victim.
spanish_meaning: Fatal significa mortal o que causa la muerte.
spanish_example: Ser herido por el atacante fue fatal para la victima.

english_meaning: The number of times something happens is the incidence of the event.
english_example: We need to increase the incidence of success in school.
spanish_meaning: La incidencia es el numero de veces que ocurre algo.
spanish_example: Debemos aumentar la incidencia del exito en la escuela.

english_meaning: Municipal means that something belongs to a city or local government.
english_example: The municipal parking lot downtown can fit one hundred cars.
spanish_meaning: Municipal se refiere al ayuntamiento o gobierno local.
spanish_example: El estacionamiento municipal del centro cabe cien autos.

english_meaning: Overuse occurs when something is utilized too many times.
english_example: Rochelle’s overuse of the exercise bike caused the gears to break.
spanish_meaning: El sobreuso es usar algo demasiadas veces.
spanish_example: El sobreuso de la bicicleta estatica rompió los engranajes.

english_meaning: A pond is a freshwater body that is smaller than a lake.
english_example: I learned to swim at the pond behind the recreation center.
spanish_meaning: Pozo/estanque es un cuerpo pequeño de agua dulce.
spanish_example: Aprendi a nadar en el estanque detras del centro recreativo.

english_meaning: When you recycle an object, you use its parts to make something else.
english_example: We recycle our newspapers so that they can be made into packing boxes.
spanish_meaning: Reciclar es reutilizar materiales para crear algo nuevo.
spanish_example: Reciclamos nuestros periodicos para hacer cajas de empaque.

english_meaning: An object that is reusable can be utilized over and over again.
english_example: Saburo keeps his empty jelly jars because they are reusable for storing sewing supplies.
spanish_meaning: Reutilizable significa que puede usarse muchas veces.
spanish_example: Saburo guarda frascos vacios porque sirven reutilizables para guardar herramientas.

english_meaning: When something is stuffed, it is pushed into a small space.
english_example: The newspapers were all stuffed into the drawer.
spanish_meaning: apretado o lleno.
spanish_example: Los periodicos estaban todos metidos y aplastados en el cajon.

english_meaning: A tragic event causes sadness because it might involve death or suffering.
english_example: The entire community attended the memorial service for the victims of the tragic fire.
spanish_meaning: Tragico describe algo triste o doloroso.
spanish_example: Toda la comunidad asistio al homenaje por las victimas del incendio tragico.

english_meaning: To utilize something is to use it for a specific purpose.
english_example: The team can utilize the lab equipment to complete the experiment.
spanish_meaning: Utilizar es usar algo para un fin especifico.
spanish_example: El equipo puede utilizar el equipo de laboratorio para completar el experimento.

english_meaning: A whale is a very large mammal that lives in the ocean.
english_example: We were surprised when a whale surfaced next to our boat.
spanish_meaning: La ballena es un gran mamifero marino.
spanish_example: Nos sorprendio ver una ballena asomarse junto a nuestro bote.

english_meaning: An amateur is a person who does something for fun and isn’t paid for it.
english_example: The amateur took pictures just as well as the person who worked for money.
spanish_meaning: Un aficionado practica por gusto y sin pago.
spanish_example: El aficionado tomo fotos tan bien como el profesional pagado.

english_meaning: If something is ambiguous, it is not entirely clear.
english_example: It’s ambiguous as to whether a newborn baby looks like a girl or a boy.
spanish_meaning: Ambiguo significa no completamente claro.
spanish_example: No está claro si un recién nacido parece más chico o niña.

english_meaning: If someone is anonymous, no one knows who he or she is.
english_example: An anonymous donor gave a thousand dollars to the museum.
spanish_meaning: Anónimo significa que nadie conoce la identidad.
spanish_example: Un donante anonimo dono mil dolares al museo.

english_meaning: To attain something is to succeed at something or to get something you want.
english_example: If you want to attain a healthy body, you must exercise every day.
spanish_meaning: Alcanzar es lograr o conseguir algo.
spanish_example: Si quieres alcanzar un cuerpo sano, debes ejercitarte cada dia.

english_meaning: Autonomy is another word for freedom or independence.
english_example: In the 1800s, the people of India fought for autonomy from Britain.
spanish_meaning: La autonomia es independencia o libertad.
spanish_example: En el siglo XIX, el pueblo de la India lucho por autonomia de Gran Bretaña.

english_meaning: A concession is something that one person gives up to another.
english_example: China gave Hong Kong to Britain as a concession after the war.
spanish_meaning: Concesion es una cedencia o derecho otorgado.
spanish_example: China cedio Hong Kong a Gran Bretaña como concesion después de la guerra.

english_meaning: Decay is the result of something slowly being broken down or destroyed naturally.
english_example: The decay in the old building was obvious.
spanish_meaning: La decadencia es el deterioro lento y natural.
spanish_example: La decadencia del edificio viejo era evidente.

english_meaning: To dwell somewhere means to live there.
english_example: Before he was a successful writer, Mark Twain dwelled in the city of Hannibal, Missouri.
spanish_meaning: Habitar significa vivir en un lugar.
spanish_example: Antes de ser escritor de exito, Mark Twain habitaba en la ciudad de Hannibal, Missouri.

english_meaning: To enlighten someone is to teach them about something.
english_example: Greek philosophers wanted to enlighten the people of Athens with their ideas.
spanish_meaning: Iluminar/enseñar es ayudar a comprender.
spanish_example: Filosofo griegos quisieron iluminar al pueblo de Atenas con sus ideas.

english_meaning: To enrich means to make someone rich or increase their wealth.
english_example: Taxes on people’s income can be used to enrich the government.
spanish_meaning: Enriquecer significa hacer más rico a alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Los impuestos sobre ingresos pueden usarse para enriquecer al gobierno.

english_meaning: To flourish means to do very well and be in an excellent condition.
english_example: The Roman Empire flourished in Europe two thousand years ago.
spanish_meaning: Florecer significa prosperar o tener exito.
spanish_example: El imperio romano florecio en Europa hace dos mil años.

english_meaning: Geometry is the study of shapes and how to measure them.
english_example: If you want to be able to calculate the area of a circle, you must study geometry.
spanish_meaning: La geometría estudia las formas y sus medidas.
spanish_example: Para calcular el área de un círculo, debes estudiar geometría.

english_meaning: To gleam means to sparkle and shine.
english_example: The waves of the ocean would gleam every night at sunset.
spanish_meaning: Brillar significa resplandecer o reflejar luz.
spanish_example: Las olas del oceano brillaban cada noche al atardecer.

english_meaning: Greed is wanting to have more of something than you need or should have.
english_example: The story of King Midas is a story of greed.
spanish_meaning: La codicia es querer más de lo necesario.
spanish_example: La historia de Midas es la historia de la codicia.

english_meaning: Harmony is a feeling that everything is peaceful, balanced, and in agreement.
english_example: The United Nations is struggling to bring peace and harmony to the world.
spanish_meaning: La armonia es paz y equilibrio entre personas o cosas.
spanish_example: Las Naciones Unidas busca traer paz y armonia al mundo.

english_meaning: Indigenous means that something or someone exists naturally in an environment or area.
english_example: The Pueblo people were an indigenous tribe in Arizona who lived in adobe homes.
spanish_meaning: Indigena indica originario o nativo de un lugar.
spanish_example: El pueblo pueblo era una tribu indigena de Arizona que vivia en viviendas de adobe.

english_meaning: Jurisdiction means the power or right to make judgments about the law and how it is upheld.
english_example: We did not have jurisdiction to bring the criminal to trial.
spanish_meaning: La jurisdiccion es la autoridad legal para juzgar y aplicar leyes.
spanish_example: No teniamos jurisdiccion para llevar al criminal a juicio.

english_meaning: A parade is a series of things or people that come or are shown one after another.
english_example: This parade of elephants is part of an open-air exhibit to honor these majestic animals.
spanish_meaning: Un desfile es una muestra o marcha de personas o cosas seguidas.
spanish_example: Este desfile de elefantes forma parte de una exhibicion al aire libre.

english_meaning: A statue is a three-dimensional work of art, usually made of clay, marble, or metal.
english_example: The Venus de Milo is a famous ancient Greek statue.
spanish_meaning: Una estatua es una obra de arte tridimensional.
spanish_example: La Venus de Milo es una estatua griega antigua famosa.

english_meaning: A virgin is someone who has never had sex.
english_example: The virgin goddess of the moon was known as Diana.
spanish_meaning: Una virgen nunca ha tenido relaciones sexuales.
spanish_example: La diosa virgen de la luna era conocida como Diana.

english_meaning: If something is abnormal, it is different from normal or average.
english_example: I felt abnormal this morning, like I was sick.
spanish_meaning: Anormal significa fuera de lo normal.
spanish_example: Esta mañana me sentía anormal, como si estuviera enfermo.

english_meaning: If someone or something is absent, they are missing or not in the place they are expected to be.
english_example: I was absent from school yesterday because I went on a trip with my father.
spanish_meaning: Una persona ausente no está presente en el lugar esperado.
spanish_example: Estuve ausente de la escuela ayer porque viajé con mi padre.

english_meaning: When something is adjacent, it is next to or adjoining something else.
english_example: My apartment is adjacent to a lovely park and playground.
spanish_meaning: Adyacente significa que está al lado de algo.
spanish_example: Mi departamento está adyacente a un parque y una plaza.

english_meaning: Aluminum is a chemical element that is a light silver-coloured metal.
english_example: I used the aluminum foil to wrap the food.
spanish_meaning: El aluminio es un metal ligero de color plata.
spanish_example: Use la hoja de aluminio para envolver la comida.

english_meaning: If something is applicable to a person or thing, it is relevant to them.
english_example: I discovered that my old password was no longer applicable to the website.
spanish_meaning: Aplicable significa que sirve o corresponde a una situación.
spanish_example: Descubrí que mi antigua contraseña ya no era aplicable al sitio web.

english_meaning: If something is artificial, it was not made naturally but mimics something natural.
english_example: The artificial Christmas tree was made of plastic.
spanish_meaning: Artificial significa hecho por el hombre, no natural.
spanish_example: El árbol navideño artificial estaba hecho de plástico.

english_meaning: A bicycle is a two-wheeled vehicle powered by pedaling.
english_example: I rode my bicycle down the mountain road.
spanish_meaning: Una bicicleta es un vehiculo de dos ruedas con pedales.
spanish_example: Baje por el camino de la montaña en mi bicicleta.

english_meaning: To broker is to arrange or negotiate the details of something for others.
english_example: The lawyer will broker our agreement.
spanish_meaning: Un corredor o mediador negocia acuerdos.
spanish_example: El abogado negociara nuestro acuerdo.

english_meaning: A bureaucracy is a group of people who work together to help manage a large business or run a country.
english_example: The members of the bureaucracy were flooded by too many petitions.
spanish_meaning: La burocracia es la administracion formal y extensa de una organizacion.
spanish_example: Los miembros de la burocracia recibieron demasiadas peticiones.

english_meaning: To configure something means to set it up and arrange it.
english_example: The engineer helped to configure my new computer.
spanish_meaning: Configurar es preparar o ajustar un sistema.
spanish_example: El ingeniero me ayudo a configurar mi computadora nueva.

english_meaning: To consolidate means to join or bring together into one thing.
english_example: When we moved in together, we had to consolidate our belongings.
spanish_meaning: Consolidar es unir cosas en una sola.
spanish_example: Cuando nos mudamos juntos, tuvimos que consolidar nuestras pertenencias.

english_meaning: If something is done for your convenience, it allows you to do something easily or without any trouble.
english_example: I love the convenience of having a swimming pool in my back yard.
spanish_meaning: La conveniencia facilita algo y ahorra esfuerzo.
spanish_example: Me encanta la conveniencia de tener una piscina en mi patio trasero.

english_meaning: To deduct means to subtract something.
english_example: I had to deduct my expenses from my checkbook.
spanish_meaning: Deducir es restar una cantidad.
spanish_example: Tuve que deducir mis gastos de mi libreta de cheques.

english_meaning: To deem means to consider something.
english_example: I deemed the ice cream to be very delicious.
spanish_meaning: Considerar es juzgar o creer algo.
spanish_example: Considere que el helado era muy delicioso.

english_meaning: An entrepreneur is someone who starts a new business or organization in order to make money.
english_example: The entrepreneur made a success out of his new business.
spanish_meaning: Un emprendedor inicia un negocio propio.
spanish_example: El emprendedor tuvo exito con su nuevo negocio.

english_meaning: If something is evenly spread or spaced, it is divided equally into amounts, numbers, or values.
english_example: Sprinkle the sugar evenly all over the cookies.
spanish_meaning: De manera uniforme significa repartido de forma igual.
spanish_example: Esparce el azucar de forma uniforme sobre las galletas.

english_meaning: When something is fiscal, it is related to money or finances, especially that of a government or business.
english_example: Did the company show fiscal growth this year?
spanish_meaning: La palabra fiscal se refiere al dinero o las finanzas, especialmente de un gobierno o una empresa.
spanish_example: ¿La empresa mostró crecimiento fiscal este año?

english_meaning: A franchise is the right to sell another company’s products or services in a particular area.
english_example: The fast food restaurant has a franchise near my home.
spanish_meaning: Una franquicia es el derecho a vender productos de otra empresa.
spanish_example: Hay una franquicia de comida rápida cerca de mi casa.

english_meaning: If something is ideological, it is based on a system of beliefs or ideals, especially those that relate to a government or economy.
english_example: The citizens of England had ideological differenes about its government.
spanish_meaning: Ideologico significa basado en ideas o creencias.
spanish_example: Los ciudadanos ingleses tenian diferencias ideologicas sobre su gobierno.

english_meaning: A robot is a machine that can do the work of a person and operates automatically or is controlled by a computer.
english_example: I bought a robot that can clean the floor on its own.
spanish_meaning: Un robot es una máquina que hace trabajo automático.
spanish_example: compré un robot que puede limpiar el piso solo.

english_meaning: An abbey is a house, or group of houses, where monks or nuns live.
english_example: When the monk returned to the abbey, he went immediately to his bedroom.
spanish_meaning: Una abbadia es una comunidad donde viven monjes o monjas.
spanish_example: Cuando el monje regreso al monasterio, fue directo a su cuarto.

english_meaning: If something is abundant, then it is available in large quantities.
english_example: Cakes, cookies, and candy were so abundant that the child was very happy.
spanish_meaning: Abundante significa disponible en grandes cantidades.
spanish_example: Había tantas tartas, galletas y dulces que el niño estaba muy feliz.

english_meaning: To adjoin something means to be next to or attached to something else.
english_example: She can listen to her brother’s conversations because her room adjoins his.
spanish_meaning: Colindante significa junto a otra zona o unido.
spanish_example: Escucha las conversaciones de su hermano porque su habitacion está colindante a la suya.

english_meaning: If something is ample, then it is enough or more than enough.
english_example: There was an ample supply of oats to feed the horses.
spanish_meaning: o abundante significa que alcanza o sobra.
spanish_example: Había una cantidad suficiente de avena para alimentar los caballos.

english_meaning: If a place is arid, then it is hot and dry and gets very little or no rain.
english_example: Not many plants grow in the arid desert.
spanish_meaning: Arido significa seco y con poca lluvia.
spanish_example: En el desierto arido no crecen muchas plantas.

english_meaning: A cathedral is an important, and often large and beautifully-built, church.
english_example: The large cathedral is full of people on Sunday mornings.
spanish_meaning: Una catedral es una iglesia grande e importante.
spanish_example: La catedral grande está llena de gente los domingos por la mañana.

english_meaning: A crisis is a difficult time when things are going to either get worse or better.
english_example: The crisis was over and things returned to normal.
spanish_meaning: Una crisis es un momento difícil y decisivo.
spanish_example: La crisis terminó y las cosas volvieron a la normalidad.

english_meaning: To deprive someone of something means to not let them have it.
english_example: Because the child was bad, she was deprived of her dessert after dinner.
spanish_meaning: Privar significa quitar a alguien algo.
spanish_example: Como la niña se porto mal, se la privó de su postre después de cenar.

english_meaning: A drought is a long period of time in which little or no rain falls.
english_example: After three months of drought, the vegetation and trees started dying.
spanish_meaning: Una sequía es una falta prolongada de lluvia.
spanish_example: Después de tres meses de sequía, la vegetación y los árboles empezaron a morir.

english_meaning: If someone is eligible, then they are permitted to do or have something.
english_example: Only people who bought tickets were eligible to win a prize.
spanish_meaning: Elegible significa que cumple las condiciones para participar o recibir algo.
spanish_example: Solo quienes compraron entradas eran elegibles para ganar un premio.

english_meaning: To fast means to go without food or drink for a period of time.
english_example: In her religion, they fast for five days and then have a big feast.
spanish_meaning: Hacer ayuno es pasar tiempo sin comer ni beber.
spanish_example: En su religion, ayunan durante cinco días y luego hacen una gran fiesta.

english_meaning: To grumble means to complain.
english_example: He grumbled about having to work late on Friday.
spanish_meaning: Quejarse es expresar descontento.
spanish_example: Se quejo por tener que trabajar tarde el viernes.

english_meaning: If someone goes inland, they travel into the center of a country or land.
english_example: The river curved inland near the campground.
spanish_meaning: Ir tierra adentro significa alejarse de la costa hacia el interior.
spanish_example: El río se desviaba hacia el interior cerca del campamento.

english_meaning: Moisture is small drops of water in the air or on a surface.
english_example: If you breathe on a window, moisture from your breath collects on the glass.
spanish_meaning: La humedad es agua en forma de vapor o gotitas.
spanish_example: Si respires sobre una ventana, la humedad de tu aliento se recoge en el vidrio.

english_meaning: If something happens nonetheless, then it occurs despite some other thing.
english_example: She tried to keep the dog out of the mud, but it got dirty nonetheless.
spanish_meaning: Aun así significa que algo ocurre pese a otra cosa.
spanish_example: Intentó mantener al perro fuera del lodo, pero aun así se ensució.

english_meaning: An oath is a formal, often public, promise.
english_example: Judges must take an oath to be fair to everyone in court.
spanish_meaning: Un juramento es una promesa formal y solemne.
spanish_example: Los jueces deben hacer un juramento de ser justos con todos en la corte.

english_meaning: A prairie is a large flat area of grassland.
english_example: The prairie was perfect for a farm because there were hills and trees.
spanish_meaning: Una pradera es una gran área de pasto llano.
spanish_example: La pradera era perfecta para una granja.

english_meaning: If an area of land is rugged, then it is rocky and difficult to travel through.
english_example: Their car couldn’t make it far along the rugged roads.
spanish_meaning: Terreno accidentado es difícil de transitar.
spanish_example: El coche no pudo seguir mucho en los caminos accidentados.

english_meaning: If something is scarce, then there is a very small amount of it.
english_example: When gasoline was scarce, we rode our bike to school instead of driving.
spanish_meaning: Escaso significa poca cantidad disponible.
spanish_example: Cuando la gasolina era escasa, fuimos en bicicleta a la escuela.

english_meaning: To speculate means to guess about something.
english_example: My sister looked at the sky and speculated that it would rain tomorrow.
spanish_meaning: Especular es conjeturar sin certeza.
spanish_example: Mi hermana miro al cielo y especulo que lloveria mañana.

english_meaning: If a plant or animal is aquatic, it lives or grows in water.
english_example: The dolphin is an aquatic mammal.
spanish_meaning: Acuatico significa que vive en el agua.
spanish_example: El delfin es un mamifero acuatico.

english_meaning: The biosphere is the Earth’s surface and atmosphere where there are living things.
english_example: Birds, trees, and worms all thrive in the biosphere.
spanish_meaning: La biosfera es la zona de la Tierra donde existen seres vivos.
spanish_example: Las aves, árboles y lombrices viven en la biosfera.

english_meaning: When something is bizarre, it is very strange.
english_example: My bizarre dreams make no sense to me when I am awake.
spanish_meaning: Extrano significa raro o inusual.
spanish_example: Mis sueños extraños no tienen sentido cuando estoy despierto.

english_meaning: Celsius is a scale for measuring temperature.
english_example: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.
spanish_meaning: El Celsius es una escala para medir la temperatura.
spanish_example: El agua se congela a cero grados Celsius.

english_meaning: If something is coarse, that means it has a rough texture.
english_example: The coarse sweater made my skin itch.
spanish_meaning: Grueso o áspero indica textura rugosa.
spanish_example: El suéter áspero me hizo picar la piel.

english_meaning: A companion is a person that someone spends a lot of time with.
english_example: I always walk to school with my companion Frank.
spanish_meaning: Un companero es alguien con quien pasas mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: Siempre voy a la escuela con mi companero Frank.

english_meaning: To digest means to swallow food and pass it through the body.
english_example: Allow some time for food to be digested before going swimming.
spanish_meaning: Digerir es descomponer los alimentos en el cuerpo.
spanish_example: Dale tiempo al cuerpo para digerir la comida antes de nadar.

english_meaning: The duration of an event is the time during which it happens.
english_example: The girls watched television for the duration of the evening.
spanish_meaning: La duracion es el tiempo que dura un evento.
spanish_example: Las niñas vieron television durante toda la duracion de la tarde.

english_meaning: Ecology is the study of the environment and living things.
english_example: We study ecology to learn how to help improve the Earth.
spanish_meaning: La ecologia estudia el medio ambiente y los seres vivos.
spanish_example: Estudiamos ecologia para aprender a mejorar la Tierra.

english_meaning: A feat is an impressive or difficult achievement or action.
english_example: The elephant’s standing up on one leg was a feat.
spanish_meaning: Una hazaña es un logro difícil e impresionante.
spanish_example: Terminar el maratón fue una hazaña difícil.

english_meaning: If something is infinite, it has no limit or end.
english_example: Many scientists believe that the universe is infinite.
spanish_meaning: Algo infinito no tiene fin.
spanish_example: Muchos científicos creen que el universo es infinito.

english_meaning: The nucleus is the central part of an atom or cell.
english_example: The nucleus is made up of many tiny particles.
spanish_meaning: El núcleo es la parte central de un átomo o célula.
spanish_example: El núcleo está compuesto por muchas partículas pequeñas.

english_meaning: A parasite is a tiny animal or plant that attaches to another animal to get food.
english_example: The sick dog was covered in parasites.
spanish_meaning: Un parásito es un organismo pequeño que vive de otro.
spanish_example: El perro enfermo estaba lleno de parásitos.

english_meaning: When something is prominent, it is important and well known.
english_example: Queen Victoria was a prominent person in history.
spanish_meaning: Prominente significa importante y conocido.
spanish_example: La reina Victoria fue una persona prominente en la historia.

english_meaning: When something is repetitive, it is repeated many times and becomes boring.
english_example: Working on an assembly line making cars every day is a repetitive job.
spanish_meaning: Repetitivo es algo que se repite y aburre.
spanish_example: Trabajar cada dia en una linea de ensamblaje es un empleo repetitivo.

english_meaning: If something is reproductive, it is a living thing which can produce young.
english_example: The reproductive system of a plant is simple.
spanish_meaning: Reproductivo se refiere a la capacidad de dar vida nueva.
spanish_example: El sistema reproductivo de una planta es simple.

english_meaning: When a place is temperate, it never gets too hot or cold.
english_example: In Peru, the weather is temperate and rarely gets too hot or cold.
spanish_meaning: Templado significa no extremo en temperatura.
spanish_example: En Peru, el clima es templado y rara vez muy caliente o frio.

english_meaning: Tolerance is the ability to accept something unfavorable or to allow the freedom of choice for others.
english_example: Boxers have a high tolerance for pain.
spanish_meaning: La tolerancia es aceptar diferencias o soportar dolor.
spanish_example: Los boxeadores tienen alta tolerancia al dolor.

english_meaning: To undergo an action means to have it happen to you.
english_example: The cancer patient undergoes treatments twice a week.
spanish_meaning: Significa someterse a una acción o tratamiento.
spanish_example: El paciente con cancer se somete a tratamientos dos veces por semana.

english_meaning: When someone is vulnerable, they are weak and without protection.
english_example: He felt very vulnerable when he was stranded in the desert.
spanish_meaning: Vulnerable significa estar expuesto y en riesgo.
spanish_example: Se sintio muy vulnerable cuando se quedó atrapado en el desierto.

english_meaning: If something is archaic, it is very old or outdated.
english_example: To be competitive, we must update our archaic equipment.
spanish_meaning: Arcaico significa viejo y fuera de uso.
spanish_example: Para competir, debemos actualizar nuestro equipo arcaico.

english_meaning: Brass is a metal that is used to make musical instruments and ornaments.
english_example: Brass is used to make musical instruments like trumpets.
spanish_meaning: El laton es un metal para instrumentos y adornos.
spanish_example: El laton se usa para fabricar instrumentos como las trompetas.

english_meaning: Capitalism is an economic system where private companies make goods for profit.
english_example: Most economies in the world today are based on capitalism.
spanish_meaning: El capitalismo es una economia de propiedad privada y lucro.
spanish_example: La mayoria de las economias actuales se basan en el capitalismo.

english_meaning: A component is a part of a larger machine.
english_example: Computers have many different components, so they are complicated to build.
spanish_meaning: Componente es una parte de un conjunto o máquina.
spanish_example: Las computadoras tienen muchos componentes, por eso son difíciles de armar.

english_meaning: Dependence is a situation in which somebody relies on something else.
english_example: Young children have a dependence on their parents.
spanish_meaning: La dependencia es necesidad de apoyo en otra persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Los niños pequeños tienen dependencia de sus padres.

english_meaning: To diminish means to reduce or get smaller.
english_example: As the economy got worse, my savings diminished.
spanish_meaning: Disminuir es hacer menor la cantidad o intensidad.
spanish_example: Mis ahorros disminuyeron a medida que empeoro la economia.

english_meaning: A drawback is a disadvantage.
english_example: The drawback of having a car is that it is very expensive to maintain.
spanish_meaning: Una desventaja es un aspecto negativo.
spanish_example: El gran problema de tener auto es que cuesta caro mantenerlo.

english_meaning: To impose means to interrupt or force your ideas on other people.
english_example: He imposes on his wife every morning by expecting her to make his breakfast.
spanish_meaning: Imponer es forzar algo sobre otros.
spanish_example: Impone su opinión a su esposa todas las mañanas pidiéndole desayuno.

english_meaning: Industry is a kind of business that produces services or things for sale.
english_example: The tourist industry is doing well all around the world.
spanish_meaning: La industria es actividad productiva de bienes o servicios.
spanish_example: La industria turistica va bien en todo el mundo.
