english_meaning: Managerial describes something related to a manager or management.
english_example: Nancy has a managerial position at the bank.
spanish_meaning: Lo gerencial está relacionado con la gerencia.
spanish_example: Nancy tiene un cargo gerencial en el banco.

english_meaning: If something is medieval, it comes from the period between 650 and 1500 CE.
english_example: We visited a castle that was built during medieval times.
spanish_meaning: Medieval se refiere a la edad media.
spanish_example: Visitamos un castillo construido en tiempos medievales.

english_meaning: If something is obsolete, it is not used anymore because something better exists.
english_example: Since computers became inexpensive, typewriters have become obsolete.
spanish_meaning: Obsoleto es algo en desuso.
spanish_example: Desde que los computadores se abaratarn, las maquinas de escribir quedaron obsoletas.

english_meaning: When you are oriented towards something, you are faced in that direction.
english_example: He is living a money-oriented lifestyle.
spanish_meaning: Una persona orientada está dirigida hacia algo.
spanish_example: Vive un estilo orientado al dinero.

english_meaning: A peninsula is a large piece of land that is surrounded by the sea on three sides.
english_example: The state of Florida is an example of a peninsula.
spanish_meaning: Una península es una extensión de tierra rodeada por agua en tres lados.
spanish_example: Florida es un ejemplo de península.

english_meaning: If a person has prestige, people admire or respect them.
english_example: The young actress gained much prestige after she won an award.
spanish_meaning: El prestigio es respeto y reputacion.
spanish_example: La joven actriz gano mucho prestigio al ganar un premio.

english_meaning: A proportion is an amount that shows the link between the parts and the whole.
english_example: Only a small proportion of the people in this town actually work here.
spanish_meaning: La proporción muestra la relación entre parte y todo.
spanish_example: Solo una pequeña proporción de la gente de este pueblo trabaja aquí.

english_meaning: If something is radical, it is very new or different.
english_example: The president is planning to make some radical changes to the law.
spanish_meaning: Radical significa profundo o extremo en cambios.
spanish_example: El presidente planea cambios radicales a la ley.

english_meaning: To refute something means to prove that it is false or incorrect.
english_example: The bank manager has refuted the claims that he lied to his customers.
spanish_meaning: Refutar es demostrar que algo es falso.
spanish_example: El gerente del banco ha refutado que hubiera engañado clientes.

english_meaning: If something is spectacular, it looks or sounds very impressive.
english_example: There was a spectacular fireworks display in the park at New Year.
spanish_meaning: Espectacular significa muy llamativo o impresionante.
spanish_example: Hubo un show espectacular de fuegos artificiales en el parque en Año Nuevo.

english_meaning: To weave means to make cloth using horizontal and vertical threads.
english_example: We saw a woman weave a blanket on our vacation to South America.
spanish_meaning: Tejer es formar tela entre hilos cruzados.
spanish_example: Vimos a una mujer tejer una manta en nuestro viaje.

english_meaning: An accountant is a person whose job is to keep financial accounts for a business.
english_example: The accountant helped us keep track of our spending.
spanish_meaning: Un contador se encarga de las cuentas financieras.
spanish_example: El contador nos ayudo a llevar control de gastos.

english_meaning: A capitalist is a business person who invests in trade and industry for profit.
english_example: The capitalist invested in a factory that made wheat into cereal.
spanish_meaning: Un capitalista invierte en negocios con fines de lucro.
spanish_example: El capitalista invirtio en una fábrica que transformaba trigo en cereal.

english_meaning: Contempt is the feeling of having no respect for something.
english_example: The judge had contempt for the wicked criminal.
spanish_meaning: Desprecio es falta total de respeto.
spanish_example: El juez sentía desprecio por el criminal.

english_meaning: A crop is something produced by the land.
english_example: I had a good crop of onions this year.
spanish_meaning: La cosecha es lo que produce la tierra.
spanish_example: Este año tuvimos buena cosecha de cebollas.

english_meaning: To dedicate oneself to something means to put a lot of time and effort into it.
english_example: The nun dedicated herself to helping people in need.
spanish_meaning: A una persona dedicada le pone empeño a una causa.
spanish_example: La monja se dedicó a ayudar a los necesitados.

english_meaning: A ditch is a narrow hole cut into the ground by a road or a field.
english_example: When the car slid off of the road, it fell into the ditch.
spanish_meaning: Una zanja es un corte estrecho en el suelo.
spanish_example: El coche se deslizo y cayo en la zanja.

english_meaning: An enterprise is a company or business.
english_example: My father owns an advertising enterprise.
spanish_meaning: Una empresa es una organizacion comercial.
spanish_example: Mi padre tiene una agencia de publicidad.

english_meaning: To finance someone or something means to provide money for them.
english_example: The government financed the scientist’s experiments with new weapons.
spanish_meaning: Financiar es aportar dinero para algo.
spanish_example: El gobierno financio los experimentos del cientifico con nuevas armas.

english_meaning: When someone is indifferent toward something, they have a lack of interest in it.
english_example: Lisa is indifferent toward school. She doesn’t care what her final grades are.
spanish_meaning: Indiferente significa no interesarse por algo.
spanish_example: Lisa es indiferente a la escuela y no le importan sus notas finales.

english_meaning: To irrigate means to supply water to land so that crops can grow.
english_example: In dry climates, it is important to irrigate fields of crops.
spanish_meaning: Regar significa suministrar agua a la tierra para que puedan crecer los cultivos.
spanish_example: En climas secos, es importante regar los campos de cultivos.

english_meaning: To maximize something is to make it as great as possible in amount, size, or importance.
english_example: You should exercise regularly to maximize a healthy lifestyle.
spanish_meaning: Maximizar significa hacer que algo sea lo más grande posible en cantidad, tamaño o importancia.
spanish_example: Debes hacer ejercicio regularmente para maximizar un estilo de vida saludable.

english_meaning: When something is monetary, it relates to money.
english_example: A strong monetary policy is important for a country to be successful.
spanish_meaning: Monetario se refiere al dinero.
spanish_example: Una política monetaria fuerte es importante para que un país sea exitoso.

english_meaning: A precaution is an action that is meant to stop something bad from happening.
english_example: As a precaution, you should put on a heavy coat before going out in cold weather.
spanish_meaning: Una precaución es una acción para evitar que ocurra algo malo.
spanish_example: Como precaución, ponte un abrigo grueso antes de salir con frío.

english_meaning: Preliminary describes something that happens before a more important event.
english_example: The runners must do well in the preliminary races to qualify for the final race.
spanish_meaning: Preliminar describe algo que ocurre antes de un evento más importante.
spanish_example: Los corredores deben rendir bien en las rondas preliminares para clasificar a la final.

english_meaning: To saturate something means to completely soak it with a liquid.
english_example: The sponge was saturated with soapy water and dripped all over the floor.
spanish_meaning: Saturar algo significa empaparlo completamente con un líquido.
spanish_example: La esponja quedó saturada de agua jabonosa y chorreaba por el suelo.

english_meaning: The simplicity of something is the fact that it is easy to do or understand.
english_example: We were able to find the house thanks to the simplicity of the directions.
spanish_meaning: La simplicidad es que algo sea fácil de hacer o de entender.
spanish_example: Encontramos la casa gracias a la simplicidad de las indicaciones.

english_meaning: To sow seeds means to plant them in the ground.
english_example: He always sows his garden seeds in the springtime.
spanish_meaning: Sembrar significa plantar semillas en el suelo.
spanish_example: Él siempre siembra las semillas de su jardín en primavera.

english_meaning: A spade is a tool used for digging.
english_example: The gardener used her spade to make a hole for the new plant.
spanish_meaning: Una pala es una herramienta para cavar.
spanish_example: La jardinera usó su pala para hacer un hoyo para una planta nueva.

english_meaning: A tomato is a round red fruit, but usually eaten as a vegetable. It’s often used in salads or pasta sauces.
english_example: I like tomato sandwiches.
spanish_meaning: El tomate es una fruta redonda y roja, pero suele comerse como verdura.
spanish_example: Me encantan los sándwiches de tomate.

english_meaning: When something is upcoming, that means it will happen in the near future.
english_example: The kids were worried about their upcoming exam.
spanish_meaning: Próximo significa que algo ocurrirá en un futuro cercano.
spanish_example: Los niños estaban preocupados por su examen próximo.

english_meaning: Anthropology is the study of people, society, and culture.
english_example: In anthropology class, I learned about simple tools that ancient cultures used.
spanish_meaning: La antropología es el estudio de las personas, la sociedad y la cultura.
spanish_example: En antropología aprendí sobre herramientas simples usadas en culturas antiguas.

english_meaning: To applaud means to clap in order to show approval.
english_example: Everyone cheered and applauded Manny’s efforts.
spanish_meaning: Aplaudir significa aplaudir para mostrar aprobación.
spanish_example: Todo el mundo aplaudió el esfuerzo de Manny.

english_meaning: To appoint someone to a job means to give the job to them.
english_example: Two students were appointed to help the scientists with their research.
spanish_meaning: Nombrar a alguien para un trabajo significa darle ese trabajo.
spanish_example: Dos estudiantes fueron nombrados para ayudar a los científicos en su investigación.

english_meaning: When things are compatible, they work well or exist together successfully.
english_example: Jan and Fred are too different. They will never be compatible.
spanish_meaning: Cuando algo es compatible, funciona bien o convive con otra cosa.
spanish_example: Jan y Fred son muy distintos; nunca serán compatibles.

english_meaning: Competence is the ability to do something well or effectively.
english_example: The job was easy because the group had enough competence to do it well.
spanish_meaning: La competencia es la capacidad de hacer algo bien o eficazmente.
spanish_example: El trabajo fue fácil porque el grupo tenía suficiente competencia para hacerlo bien.

english_meaning: To confer with someone means to discuss something with them to make a decision.
english_example: I will have to confer with my wife before I can purchase a new car.
spanish_meaning: Conferir con alguien significa discutir algo para tomar una decisión.
spanish_example: Tengo que consultar con mi esposa antes de comprar un coche nuevo.

english_meaning: When things are consecutive, they happen one after another without interruption.
english_example: The king ruled for ten consecutive years.
spanish_meaning: Consecutivo significa que algo sucede uno después de otro sin interrupción.
spanish_example: El rey reinó durante diez años consecutivos.

english_meaning: When something is crude, it is not exact or detailed but can still be useful.
english_example: She drew crude hearts on the ground to show how much she loved him.
spanish_meaning: Tosco significa poco preciso o sin detalles, aunque útil.
spanish_example: Ella dibujó corazones toscos en el suelo para mostrar cuánto lo amaba.

english_meaning: A cube is a solid object with six square surfaces that are all the same size.
english_example: Please get me some ice cubes to put in my soda.
spanish_meaning: Un cubo es un sólido con seis caras cuadradas del mismo tamaño.
spanish_example: Por favor tráeme algunos cubos de hielo para mi refresco.

english_meaning: Feedback is comments to a person about how they are doing something.
english_example: I asked my boss for feedback on my work.
spanish_meaning: La retroalimentación son comentarios sobre cómo hace algo una persona.
spanish_example: Le pedí retroalimentación sobre mi trabajo a mi jefe.

english_meaning: Ignorance of something is lack of knowledge about it.
english_example: When he failed the test, his ignorance of math was obvious.
spanish_meaning: La ignorancia es la falta de conocimiento sobre algo.
spanish_example: Cuando suspenció el examen, su ignorancia de matemáticas fue evidente.

english_meaning: When something is masculine, it is a quality or thing related to men.
english_example: American football is usually considered a masculine sport.
spanish_meaning: Masculino es una cualidad o cosa relacionada con los hombres.
spanish_example: El fútbol americano suele considerarse un deporte masculino.

english_meaning: A monument is a structure that is built to remind people of a person or event.
english_example: A large monument was built to honor the brave soldiers.
spanish_meaning: Un monumento es una estructura construida para recordar a una persona o suceso.
spanish_example: Se construyó un gran monumento para honrar a los valientes soldados.

english_meaning: When someone is muscular, they are very fit and strong.
english_example: He exercised regularly so that his body could become muscular.
spanish_meaning: Musculoso significa muy fuerte o en buena forma física.
spanish_example: Él se ejercitaba para que su cuerpo se volviera más musculoso.

english_meaning: A person’s posture is the manner in which they stand or sit.
english_example: Your back will feel better if you improve your posture.
spanish_meaning: La postura es la manera en que alguien se para o se sienta.
spanish_example: Tu espalda se sentirá mejor si mejoras tu postura.

english_meaning: To situate something means to place or build it in a certain place.
english_example: The road was situated between the forest and the lake.
spanish_meaning: Situar algo significa colocarlo en un lugar concreto.
spanish_example: El camino estaba situado entre el bosque y el lago.

english_meaning: To supervise something means to make sure that it is done correctly.
english_example: Allen supervised the construction workers to ensure everyone’s safety.
spanish_meaning: Supervisar significa asegurarse de que algo se haga correctamente.
spanish_example: Allen supervisó a los trabajadores de la construcción para que todos fueran seguros.

english_meaning: Symmetry is the state of having two halves that are exactly the same.
english_example: The artist made sure to use perfect symmetry when painting the butterfly.
spanish_meaning: La simetría es tener dos mitades exactamente iguales.
spanish_example: La artista usó una simetría perfecta al pintar la mariposa.

english_meaning: A tattoo is a design that is drawn permanently on the skin with needles.
english_example: The surfer had tattoos on both his arms.
spanish_meaning: Un tatuaje es un dibujo dibujado permanentemente en la piel con agujas.
spanish_example: El surfista tenía tatuajes en ambos brazos.

english_meaning: An undergraduate is a student at a college who is studying for a bachelor’s degree.
english_example: She was excited to finish high school and enroll as an undergraduate in the fall.
spanish_meaning: Un estudiante de pregrado estudia en la universidad para obtener una licenciatura.
spanish_example: Ella estaba emocionada de terminar la secundaria e inscribirse como estudiante de pregrado en otoño.

english_meaning: To cater to someone means to provide them with all the things they need or want.
english_example: Bill was too sick to get out of bed, so his nurse catered to his needs.
spanish_meaning: Atender significa proveer lo que alguien necesita o desea.
spanish_example: Bill estaba muy enfermo para levantarse y su enfermera lo atendía en todo lo que necesitaba.

english_meaning: When someone is considerate, they pay attention to the needs of others.
english_example: The considerate boy gave his girlfriend a present to cheer her up when she was sad.
spanish_meaning: Considerado significa atento a las necesidades de los demás.
spanish_example: El chico considerado le dio un regalo a su novia para animarla.

english_meaning: The consumption of food or drink is the act of eating or drinking it.
english_example: These apples are too rotten for consumption.
spanish_meaning: El consumo de comida o bebida es el acto de comer o beberla.
spanish_example: Esas manzanas estaban demasiado podridas para su consumo.

english_meaning: Criteria are factors on which a person judges or decides something.
english_example: Before she got the job, she had to meet all the necessary criteria.
spanish_meaning: Los criterios son los factores con los que alguien juzga o decide algo.
spanish_example: Antes de obtener el trabajo, ella tuvo que cumplir todos los criterios necesarios.

english_meaning: Crust is the tough outer part of a loaf of bread.
english_example: The little boy never ate the crust of his pizza.
spanish_meaning: La corteza es la parte dura externa de un pan.
spanish_example: El niño nunca se comía la corteza de su pizza.

english_meaning: To entitle someone means to give them the right to have or do something.
english_example: His golden ticket entitled him to sit in the front row at the concert.
spanish_meaning: Otorgar alguien significa darle el derecho de tener o hacer algo.
spanish_example: Su boleto dorado le otorgaba sentarse en la primera fila del concierto.

english_meaning: To escort people means to safely accompany them to a place.
english_example: Her bodyguards escorted her to the movie theater.
spanish_meaning: Escoltar significa acompañar a alguien de manera segura a un lugar.
spanish_example: Sus guardaespaldas la escoltaron al cine.

english_meaning: When something is external, it is connected to an outer part.
english_example: It is warm inside my house, but the external temperature is freezing.
spanish_meaning: Externo se relaciona con una parte exterior.
spanish_example: Hace calor en casa, pero la temperatura externa está helada.

english_meaning: A facility is a building that exists for a particular purpose.
english_example: There are many educational facilities in big cities.
spanish_meaning: Una instalación es un edificio para un propósito específico.
spanish_example: Hay muchas instalaciones educativas en ciudades grandes.

english_meaning: A faculty is a mental or physical ability.
english_example: The boy’s mental faculties impressed all of his teachers.
spanish_meaning: Las facultades son habilidades mentales o físicas.
spanish_example: Las facultades mentales del niño impresionaron a todos sus profesores.

english_meaning: A heap of things is a large pile of them.
english_example: After the building was torn down, all that was left was a heap of bricks.
spanish_meaning: Un montón es una gran pila de cosas.
spanish_example: Después de demoler el edificio, quedó un montón de ladrillos.

english_meaning: A hemisphere is one half of the Earth.
english_example: In the northern hemisphere, the weather is usually warmest in July and August.
spanish_meaning: Un hemisferio es la mitad de la Tierra.
spanish_example: En el hemisferio norte el tiempo suele ser más cálido en julio y agosto.

english_meaning: A hound is a type of dog that is often used for racing or hunting.
english_example: The men took their hounds with them when they went on the hunting trip.
spanish_meaning: Un sabueso es un tipo de perro usado para cazar.
spanish_example: Los hombres llevaron a sus sabuesos al viaje de caza.

english_meaning: If something is impersonal, it is not friendly and makes people feel unimportant.
english_example: The boy felt scared on his first day at the big, impersonal high school.
spanish_meaning: Impersonal es algo poco amigable que no hace sentir importante a la gente.
spanish_example: El chico se sintió asustado en la gran escuela impersonal.

english_meaning: When you lick something, you pass your tongue over it.
english_example: He licked his ice cream before it melted.
spanish_meaning: Lamer algo significa pasar la lengua por él.
spanish_example: Él lamió su helado antes de que se derritiera.

english_meaning: An ornament is an attractive object that people display in their homes.
english_example: The woman kept some colorful ornaments on the shelves.
spanish_meaning: Un adorno es un objeto decorativo.
spanish_example: La mujer guardó unos adornos coloridos en las estanterías.

english_meaning: A pedestrian is a person who is walking on a street.
english_example: Drivers should be careful when pedestrians are walking around.
spanish_meaning: Un peatón es una persona que camina por la calle.
spanish_example: Los conductores deben tener cuidado cuando hay peatones alrededor.

english_meaning: A sanctuary is a place where people in danger can go to be safe.
english_example: The church was made into a sanctuary for homeless people in the winter.
spanish_meaning: Un santuario es un lugar seguro para personas en peligro.
spanish_example: La iglesia fue un santuario para personas sin hogar en invierno.

english_meaning: A spectator is someone who watches something, for example, a sports event.
english_example: There were thousands of spectators at the big game.
spanish_meaning: Un espectador es alguien que mira un espectáculo o evento.
spanish_example: Había miles de espectadores en el gran juego.

english_meaning: When you yell at someone, you shout at them.
english_example: Someone yelled out his name.
spanish_meaning: Gritar significa llamar o hablar muy fuerte.
spanish_example: Alguien gritó su nombre.

english_meaning: An accessory is a thing that is added to another thing to make it look better.
english_example: The store sold colorful accessories like bags, sunglasses, and makeup.
spanish_meaning: Un accesorio es algo añadido para que algo se vea mejor.
spanish_example: La tienda vendía accesorios como bolsos, gafas de sol y maquillaje.

english_meaning: An acquisition is something that a person buys or gets in some way.
english_example: Marty was happy with his new acquisition: a very fast bicycle.
spanish_meaning: Una adquisición es algo que una persona compra o consigue.
spanish_example: Marty estaba feliz con su adquisición: una bicicleta muy rápida.

english_meaning: Cardboard is a material made out of stiff paper. It is often used to make boxes.
english_example: We packed our things into cardboard boxes and moved to our new home.
spanish_meaning: El cartón es un material de papel rígido.
spanish_example: Empaquetamos nuestras cosas en cajas de cartón y nos mudamos.

english_meaning: A dilemma is a difficult situation in which a choice has to be made.
english_example: Choosing either the tastier or healthier drink proved to be quite a dilemma.
spanish_meaning: Un dilema es una situación difícil en la que hay que elegir.
spanish_example: Elegir entre la bebida más rica o la más saludable fue todo un dilema.

english_meaning: When something is elaborate, it contains a lot of details.
english_example: She gave the teacher an elaborate explanation of her project.
spanish_meaning: Elaborado significa lleno de muchos detalles.
spanish_example: Ella dio una explicación muy elaborada de su proyecto.

english_meaning: Exact means correct in every detail.
english_example: I know the exact location of the restaurant you mentioned before.
spanish_meaning: Exacto significa correcto en cada detalle.
spanish_example: Conozco la ubicación exacta del restaurante que mencionaste.

english_meaning: To facilitate something is to make it easier.
english_example: To facilitate the meeting, Melissa used a simple computer program.
spanish_meaning: Facilitar significa hacer algo más fácil.
spanish_example: Para facilitar la reunión, Melissa usó un programa simple.

english_meaning: A fleet is a group of ships.
english_example: The fleet of ships spent a few days at the dock.
spanish_meaning: Una flota es un grupo de barcos.
spanish_example: La flota de barcos estuvo unos días en el muelle.

english_meaning: A grid is a pattern of squares with numbers and letters to find places on a map.
english_example: We located our town using the grid.
spanish_meaning: Una cuadrícula es un patrón de cuadrados con letras y números para ubicar lugares en mapas.
spanish_example: Encontramos nuestra ciudad usando la cuadrícula.

english_meaning: To import means to bring in a product from another country.
english_example: Foods that have been imported are usually more expensive.
spanish_meaning: Importar significa traer un producto de otro país.
spanish_example: Los alimentos importados suelen ser más caros.

english_meaning: To infer something is to decide it is true based on other information one has.
english_example: By the position of the sun in the sky, she inferred that it was noon.
spanish_meaning: Inferir algo significa deducir que es cierto por otra información.
spanish_example: Por la posición del sol, ella dedujo que era mediodía.

english_meaning: To inflate something means to fill it up with air.
english_example: I helped him inflate the balloons.
spanish_meaning: Inflar significa llenarlo con aire.
spanish_example: Le ayudé a inflar los globos.

english_meaning: When something is innate, it is something that one is born with and was not learned.
english_example: He had the innate desire to please his teachers.
spanish_meaning: Algo innato es con lo que se nace y no se aprende.
spanish_example: Él tenía el deseo innato de agradar a sus profesores.

english_meaning: Marble is a type of rock that feels cold and is smooth when cut.
english_example: The large house had floors made of marble.
spanish_meaning: El mármol es una roca lisa y fría cuando se talla.
spanish_example: La casa grande tenía suelos de mármol.

english_meaning: A mast is a long pole on a ship that holds the sail.
english_example: The mast held both sails of the ship upright.
spanish_meaning: Un mástil es un poste largo de un barco que sostiene las velas.
spanish_example: El mástil sostenía ambas velas en su lugar.

english_meaning: Nausea is the feeling of being sick to your stomach.
english_example: The doctor said the medicine would help get rid of her nausea.
spanish_meaning: La náusea es el malestar del estómago.
spanish_example: El médico dijo que el medicamento quitaría su náusea.

english_meaning: When something is naval, it relates to a country’s navy or military ships.
english_example: The country sent all of its naval forces to protect them.
spanish_meaning: Naval se refiere a la armada militar.
spanish_example: El país envió sus fuerzas navales para proteger la zona.

english_meaning: A pouch is a small, flexible bag that is usually made of soft material.
english_example: I keep my money in a small pouch.
spanish_meaning: Una bolsita es una pequeña bolsa flexible.
spanish_example: Guardo mi dinero en una bolsita pequeña.

english_meaning: If something is saturated, it is completely wet.
english_example: Leigh’s hair became saturated in the rainstorm.
spanish_meaning: Algo saturado está completamente mojado.
spanish_example: El pelo de Leigh se saturó de agua en la tormenta.

english_meaning: An update is an act of making something more modern or current.
english_example: My phone is downloading a software update.
spanish_meaning: Una actualización es la acción de poner algo más moderno o actual.
spanish_example: Mi teléfono está descargando una actualización de software.

english_meaning: An attorney is one who gives others advice about the law.
english_example: The attorney appeared in front of the judge for me.
spanish_meaning: Un abogado es quien asesora en temas de ley.
spanish_example: El abogado apareció frente al juez en mi lugar.

english_meaning: When something is chronic, it happens over and over again for a long time.
english_example: He had chronic pain in his chest and needed to see a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Crónico significa que algo ocurre repetidamente durante mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: Él tenía dolor crónico en el pecho y necesitó ver al médico.

english_meaning: Discipline is training that helps people follow the rules.
english_example: One of the teacher’s jobs is to teach her students discipline.
spanish_meaning: La disciplina es entrenamiento para seguir reglas.
spanish_example: Una de las tareas de la maestra es enseñar disciplina a sus alumnos.

english_meaning: A donor is somebody who gives something to an organization.
english_example: He was proud to be a blood donor.
spanish_meaning: Un donante es alguien que da algo a una organización.
spanish_example: Él estaba orgulloso de ser donante de sangre.

english_meaning: A fellow is someone who shares a job or quality with someone else.
english_example: All of my fellow patients at the hospital have also complained about the food.
spanish_meaning: Un compañero es alguien que comparte una profesión o cualidad.
spanish_example: Mis compañeros de pacientes del hospital también se quejaron de la comida.

english_meaning: Gossip is information that might be untrue but is still discussed anyway.
english_example: The friends exchanged gossip about the people they knew in school.
spanish_meaning: El chisme son rumores o noticias discutidas, a veces falsas.
spanish_example: Las amigas intercambiaron chismes sobre personas de su escuela.

english_meaning: To graduate from a school means to complete and pass all courses of study there.
english_example: At the end of the spring, my friends and I will graduate from high school.
spanish_meaning: Graduarse es terminar y aprobar una escuela.
spanish_example: Al final de la primavera, mis amigos y yo nos graduaremos de secundaria.

english_meaning: Graffiti is words or drawings in public places.
english_example: The wall was covered with colorful graffiti.
spanish_meaning: El grafiti son palabras o dibujos en lugares públicos.
spanish_example: La pared estaba cubierta de grafiti colorido.

english_meaning: A guardian is someone who protects somebody or something.
english_example: The librarians are the guardians of the books.
spanish_meaning: Un guardián es alguien que protege algo o a alguien.
spanish_example: Los bibliotecarios son guardianes de los libros.

english_meaning: To implicate someone is to show that they have done a crime or something bad.
english_example: The man was implicated in the theft at the store.
spanish_meaning: Implicar a alguien es mostrar que esa persona hizo algo malo.
spanish_example: El hombre fue implicado en el robo de la tienda.

english_meaning: Kin is a person’s family and relatives.
english_example: His kin were all farmers.
spanish_meaning: Los parientes de una persona son su familia cercana.
spanish_example: Sus parientes eran todos agricultores.

english_meaning: A referee is a person who makes sure that the rules are followed in sports.
english_example: The soccer player didn’t agree with the referee.
spanish_meaning: Un árbitro asegura que se cumplan las reglas en los deportes.
spanish_example: Al futbolista no le gustó la decisión del árbitro.

english_meaning: To sever something is to cut through it completely.
english_example: He severed the string using scissors.
spanish_meaning: Cortar algo por completo significa separar totalmente.
spanish_example: Cortó por completo la cuerda con las tijeras.

english_meaning: A shaft is a handle of a tool or weapon.
english_example: The golf club had a long wooden shaft that he held in his hands.
spanish_meaning: El mango es la empuñadura de una herramienta o arma.
spanish_example: Sostuvo el mango de la pala en su mano.

english_meaning: To stab means to cut with the end of a sharp object like a knife.
english_example: He stabbed the fork into the potato and passed it to his daughter.
spanish_meaning: Apuñalar significa herir con la punta de un objeto cortante.
spanish_example: Ella apuñaló el tenedor en la papa y se lo dio a su hija.

english_meaning: A stimulus is something that causes growth or activity.
english_example: Having a lot of money is a stimulus for people to buy more things.
spanish_meaning: Un estímulo es algo que provoca crecimiento o actividad.
spanish_example: Tener mucho dinero puede ser un estímulo para comprar más cosas.

english_meaning: A suspicion is a feeling that something is possible or true in a crime.
english_example: The police had a suspicion that the driver had stolen the purse.
spanish_meaning: Sospecha es una sensación de que algo es posible o verdadero en un crimen.
spanish_example: La policía tenía sospechas de que el conductor había robado el bolso.

english_meaning: To terminate something means to stop or end it.
english_example: The trip was terminated after the car broke down.
spanish_meaning: Terminar algo significa detenerlo o darle fin.
spanish_example: El viaje terminó cuando se rompió el coche.

english_meaning: A theme is the main subject of a book, movie, or painting.
english_example: The students discussed the book’s theme in class.
spanish_meaning: El tema es la idea principal de un libro, película o pintura.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes discutieron el tema del libro en clase.

english_meaning: Tuition is the amount of money paid to go to a school.
english_example: University tuitions have increased by 50 percent in the last five years.
spanish_meaning: La matrícula es la cantidad de dinero que se paga para ir a una escuela.
spanish_example: Las matrículas universitarias han subido 50 por ciento en cinco años.

english_meaning: If someone is aggressive, then they constantly want to fight or argue.
english_example: Nobody liked to play games with him because he was always too aggressive.
spanish_meaning: Alguien agresivo tiende a pelear o discutir seguido.
spanish_example: Nadie quería jugar con él porque siempre era muy agresivo.

english_meaning: Amnesty is a pardon given to prisoners of war.
english_example: She was denied amnesty for her involvement in the war.
spanish_meaning: La amnistía es un perdón dado a personas implicadas en una guerra.
spanish_example: Le negaron la amnistía por su participación en la guerra.

english_meaning: An arena is a building where people can watch sports and concerts.
english_example: The new arena was all set to hold the championship match.
spanish_meaning: Una arena es un edificio donde se ven deportes o conciertos.
spanish_example: La nueva arena estaba lista para albergar el campeonato.

english_meaning: An auditorium is a large building used for public events.
english_example: People have gathered at the school auditorium to watch the play.
spanish_meaning: Un auditorio es un gran edificio usado para eventos públicos.
spanish_example: La gente se reunió en el auditorio de la escuela para ver la obra.

english_meaning: A captive is a prisoner.
english_example: The guards told the captive that there was no way he could escape the prison.
spanish_meaning: Un cautivo es una persona prisionera.
spanish_example: Los guardias le dijeron al cautivo que no había forma de escapar.

english_meaning: Combat is fighting between two people or groups.
english_example: The two warriors were locked in combat.
spanish_meaning: El combate es una lucha entre dos personas o grupos.
spanish_example: Los dos guerreros estaban en combate.

english_meaning: If something is commonplace, then it is ordinary.
english_example: There is nothing commonplace about the way Morris dresses.
spanish_meaning: Común significa ordinario o normal.
spanish_example: No hay nada común en la forma de vestir de Morris.

english_meaning: A compound is an enclosed area such as a prison or factory.
english_example: The workers waited outside the compound for the gates of the factory to open.
spanish_meaning: Un recinto es un área cerrada como prisión o fábrica.
spanish_example: Los trabajadores esperaban fuera del recinto para que se abriera la fábrica.

english_meaning: A corps is a division of a military force.
english_example: The army had a corps of archers who trained apart from the regular soldiers.
spanish_meaning: Un cuerpo es una unidad de un cuerpo militar.
spanish_example: El ejército tenía un cuerpo de arqueros separados.

english_meaning: To distract someone means to stop them from concentrating on something.
english_example: The phone call distracted him so much that he forgot all about his homework.
spanish_meaning: Distraer a alguien significa evitar que se concentre en algo.
spanish_example: La llamada distrajo tanto a Juan que se olvidó de la tarea.

english_meaning: If someone is dumb, they are unable to speak.
english_example: She did not share the secret. She remained as silent as if she were dumb.
spanish_meaning: Si alguien es mudo, no puede hablar.
spanish_example: Ella guardó el secreto. Se quedó callada como si fuera muda.

english_meaning: A foe is an enemy or opponent.
english_example: It was hard to believe that anyone could be his foe.
spanish_meaning: Un enemigo o rival es alguien que se opone.
spanish_example: Era difícil creer que alguien pudiera ser su enemigo.

english_meaning: To hack something means to cut it into uneven pieces.
english_example: My uncle used the ax to hack the tree into many logs.
spanish_meaning: Hender significa cortar algo en pedazos irregulares.
spanish_example: Mi tío usó el hacha para hender el árbol en varios troncos.

english_meaning: To meditate means to focus or think deeply in silence.
english_example: She liked to meditate for several hours each day.
spanish_meaning: Meditar significa reflexionar en silencio o concentrarse profundamente.
spanish_example: Le gustaba meditar durante varias horas cada día.

english_meaning: To nick something or someone means to cut them slightly with a sharp object.
english_example: While cutting the carrots, the cook nicked his finger with the edge of his knife.
spanish_meaning: Cortar algo levemente con un objeto afilado es nickearlo.
spanish_example: Mientras cortaba las zanahorias, el cocinero se cortó un poco con el cuchillo.

english_meaning: To provoke someone means to annoy them on purpose to cause violence.
english_example: The older boy provoked Paul by calling him mean names.
spanish_meaning: Provocar a alguien es molestarlo a propósito para causar violencia.
spanish_example: El chico mayor provocó a Paul llamándolo con apodos crueles.

english_meaning: A realm is any area of activity or interest.
english_example: He was not very active in the realm of business.
spanish_meaning: Un ámbito o esfera es un área de actividad o interés.
spanish_example: Él no era muy activo en el ámbito empresarial.

english_meaning: A reign is the period of time in which a ruler rules.
english_example: The emperor’s reign lasted for only two years.
spanish_meaning: El reinado es el período en que manda un gobernante.
spanish_example: El reinado del emperador duró solo dos años.

english_meaning: Rust is a red and brown coating on iron objects caused by water and air.
english_example: The old metal gate would not swing because the hinges were covered in rust.
spanish_meaning: El óxido es una capa rojiza en hierro causada por agua y aire.
spanish_example: La vieja puerta de metal no se movía por la herrumbre en las bisagras.

english_meaning: If something is sacred, then it is worshipped and respected.
english_example: One religion in India will not harm cows because it believes that they are sacred.
spanish_meaning: Sagrado significa venerado y respetado.
spanish_example: Hay una religión en India que considera sagradas a las vacas.

english_meaning: If something is aesthetic, then it is concerned with a love of beauty.
english_example: The dresses were noteworthy for their aesthetic design.
spanish_meaning: Estético se refiere al gusto por la belleza.
spanish_example: Los vestidos destacaban por su diseño estético.

english_meaning: If someone is arrogant, they think that they are more important than others.
english_example: He is very arrogant. Even though he’s not the boss, he tells everyone what to do.
spanish_meaning: Arrogante significa creer que uno vale más que los demás.
spanish_example: Es muy arrogante; aunque no sea jefe, le dice a todos qué hacer.

english_meaning: A bias is a person’s likelihood to like one thing more than another thing.
english_example: The mothers had a natural bias for their own child’s picture.
spanish_meaning: Sesgo es la tendencia de una persona a preferir una cosa más que otra.
spanish_example: Las madres tenían un sesgo natural por la foto de su propio hijo.

english_meaning: A canyon is a narrow valley with steep walls through which a river often flows.
english_example: The canyon was so deep that the ground inside was covered in shadow.
spanish_meaning: Un cañón es un valle estrecho con paredes empinadas.
spanish_example: El cañón era tan profundo que el suelo estaba en sombra.

english_meaning: A creek is a stream or small river.
english_example: Only small fish lived in the shallow waters of the creek.
spanish_meaning: Un arroyo es un pequeño río o corriente.
spanish_example: Solo vivían peces pequeños en las aguas bajas del arroyo.

english_meaning: A drill is a tool with a point that spins in order to make a hole.
english_example: The carpenter used the drill to make several holes in the wood.
spanish_meaning: Un taladro es una herramienta con una punta giratoria para hacer agujeros.
spanish_example: El carpintero usó el taladro para hacer varios agujeros en la madera.

english_meaning: An executive is the top manager of a business.
english_example: After twenty years at the company, he finally became the executive.
spanish_meaning: Un ejecutivo es el alto directivo de una empresa.
spanish_example: Después de veinte años, finalmente se convirtió en ejecutivo.

english_meaning: Fatigue is a feeling of extreme tiredness.
english_example: After three days with little sleep, she was feeling a lot of fatigue.
spanish_meaning: Fatiga es cansancio extremo.
spanish_example: Después de tres días sin dormir, sentía mucha fatiga.

english_meaning: An incline is a sharp rise in something, especially a hill or mountain.
english_example: This mountain has one of the steepest inclines in the world.
spanish_meaning: Una pendiente es una subida pronunciada, especialmente en una colina.
spanish_example: Esta montaña tiene una de las pendientes más empinadas del mundo.

english_meaning: If something is nasty, then it is not nice or pleasant.
english_example: The rotten apple left a nasty taste inside her mouth.
spanish_meaning: Algo desagradable no es ni bonito ni agradable.
spanish_example: La manzana podrida dejó un sabor desagradable en su boca.

english_meaning: To perceive something means to be aware of it.
english_example: He was talking loudly, so he did not perceive that the music had stopped.
spanish_meaning: Percibir algo significa ser consciente de ello.
spanish_example: Hablaba tan fuerte que no percibió que la música se había detenido.

english_meaning: A primate is a type of mammal that includes monkeys, apes, and humans.
english_example: Primates use their hands for such tasks as swinging from branches.
spanish_meaning: Un primate es un mamífero como monos, simios y humanos.
spanish_example: Los primates usan sus manos para tareas como columpiarse de las ramas.

english_meaning: If something is primitive, then it is simple, basic, and not very developed.
english_example: The computers of the 1980s are primitive compared to those of today.
spanish_meaning: Primitivo significa simple, básico y poco desarrollado.
spanish_example: Las computadoras de los años 80 eran primitivas comparadas con las actuales.

english_meaning: A stereotype is a general but often incorrect idea about a person or thing.
english_example: There’s a stereotype that pigs are dirty animals. But they are rather clean.
spanish_meaning: Un estereotipo es una idea general e incorrecta sobre una persona o cosa.
spanish_example: Hay un estereotipo de que los cerdos son sucios, pero no siempre es así.

english_meaning: If something is sticky, then it is covered with a substance that things stick to.
english_example: Place the sticky part of the tape against the paper, so it will cling to the wall.
spanish_meaning: Pegajoso significa cubierto de algo a lo que se pega.
spanish_example: Pon la parte pegajosa de la cinta contra el papel para que se adhiera a la pared.

english_meaning: A termite is an insect that lives in groups and feeds on wood.
english_example: The wood we found was full of termites.
spanish_meaning: Una termita es un insecto que vive en grupos y come madera.
spanish_example: La madera que hallamos estaba llena de termitas.

english_meaning: If we say that thereby something happens, we mean it is a result of something else.
english_example: He didn’t score a goal, thereby ending his chance at setting a record.
spanish_meaning: Por ello significa que algo ocurre como resultado de algo más.
spanish_example: No metió un gol, por ello terminó su oportunidad de romper el récord.

english_meaning: A trail is a path for travelling by foot through a wild area.
english_example: A narrow trail cut through the field and over the hills.
spanish_meaning: Un sendero es un camino para caminar por una zona natural.
spanish_example: Un sendero estrecho atravesaba el campo y subía por las colinas.

english_meaning: A twig is a short and thin branch from a tree or bush.
english_example: They started the fire with a handful of dry twigs.
spanish_meaning: Una rama es una ramita corta y delgada de un árbol o arbusto.
spanish_example: Encendieron el fuego con un puñado de ramas secas.

english_meaning: Welfare is the health and happiness of a person or group.
english_example: Having plenty of clean water is necessary for the welfare of people.
spanish_meaning: El bienestar es la salud y felicidad de una persona o grupo.
spanish_example: Tener agua limpia es necesaria para el bienestar de la gente.

english_meaning: If something is done on one’s behalf, it is done for that person by another.
english_example: The original speaker was sick, so his son gave the speech on his behalf.
spanish_meaning: Hacer algo en nombre de alguien es hacerlo por esa persona a través de otro.
spanish_example: El hablante original estaba enfermo y su hijo habló en su nombre.

english_meaning: To flap means to move quickly up and down or from side to side.
english_example: The tiny bird flapped its wings and ate from the flowers.
spanish_meaning: Aletear o agitar significa mover rápidamente de arriba abajo o de lado a lado.
spanish_example: El pajarito agitación sus alas y se posó en las flores.

english_meaning: A glacier is a large piece of ice that moves very slowly.
english_example: The North Pole is covered by a huge glacier.
spanish_meaning: Un glaciar es una gran masa de hielo que se mueve muy lentamente.
spanish_example: El Polo Norte está cubierto por enormes glaciares.

english_meaning: The globe refers to the Earth.
english_example: Water covers most of the globe.
spanish_meaning: El globo terráqueo se refiere a la Tierra.
spanish_example: El agua cubre la mayor parte del globo terráqueo.

english_meaning: When something is horizontal, it is fat and level with the ground.
english_example: The Russian fag has three horizontal stripes of white, blue, and red.
spanish_meaning: Algo horizontal es plano y paralelo al suelo.
spanish_example: La bandera rusa tiene tres franjas horizontales de blanco, azul y rojo.

english_meaning: To hum means to make a low, continuous noise.
english_example: The man hummed his favorite song.
spanish_meaning: Zumbar significa hacer un ruido bajo y continuo.
spanish_example: El hombre tarareó su canción favorita.

english_meaning: An inventory is a supply of something.
english_example: Gwen was checking the inventory to make sure we had what we needed.
spanish_meaning: Un inventario es un registro de lo que se tiene.
spanish_example: Gwen revisaba el inventario para asegurar que tuviéramos lo necesario.

english_meaning: If a thought or feeling is inward, it is not expressed or shown to others.
english_example: She had an inward feeling of guilt when she lied to her mother.
spanish_meaning: Interno significa no expresado ni mostrado a los demás.
spanish_example: Tenía un sentimiento interno de culpa cuando le mintió a su madre.

english_meaning: A loaf of bread is bread shaped and baked in one piece.
english_example: Could you please buy a loaf of bread for sandwiches?
spanish_meaning: Una hogaza es un pan redondo y grande.
spanish_example: ¿Puedes comprar una hogaza de pan para los sándwiches?

english_meaning: An oracle is a person who speaks with gods and gives advice about the future.
english_example: The king went to the oracle to ask if going to war was a good idea.
spanish_meaning: Un oráculo es una persona que habla con dioses y aconseja sobre el futuro.
spanish_example: El rey consultó al oráculo para preguntar si convenía ir a la guerra.

english_meaning: To orbit something means to move around it in a continuous, curving path.
english_example: The Moon orbits the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Orbitar algo significa moverse alrededor suyo en trayectoria curva.
spanish_example: La Luna orbita la Tierra.

english_meaning: An overview is a general description of a situation.
english_example: My brother gave me an overview of the important parts of the book.
spanish_meaning: Una visión general es una descripción general de una situación.
spanish_example: Mi hermano me dio una visión general de las partes importantes del libro.

english_meaning: A preview is an opportunity to see something before it is available to the public.
english_example: The band played us a preview of their new song.
spanish_meaning: Un avance es una vista previa de algo antes de que esté disponible.
spanish_example: La banda nos tocó un avance de su nueva canción.

english_meaning: If something is previous, then it happened earlier in time or order.
english_example: He turned back to the previous page to read the paragraph again.
spanish_meaning: Anterior significa que ocurrió antes, en el tiempo o en el orden.
spanish_example: Volvió a la página anterior para releer el párrafo.

english_meaning: To provide something means to supply it.
english_example: Each student was provided with a test and three sharp pencils.
spanish_meaning: Proporcionar significa suministrar algo.
spanish_example: Cada estudiante recibió una prueba y tres lápices bien afilados.

english_meaning: To recur means to happen more than once.
english_example: Burglaries seem to recur over and over in our neighborhood.
spanish_meaning: Repetirse significa suceder más de una vez.
spanish_example: Los robos parecen repetirse una y otra vez en nuestro barrio.

english_meaning: When something is relevant, it is important to a certain person or situation.
english_example: The 30-year-old book about politics is still relevant to our society today.
spanish_meaning: Algo relevante es importante para una persona o situación.
spanish_example: El libro de hace 30 años aún es muy relevante para nuestra sociedad.

english_meaning: A rite is a traditional ceremony carried out by a particular group or society.
english_example: Special masks are worn during the rite when a new baby is born.
spanish_meaning: Un rito es una ceremonia tradicional de un grupo.
spanish_example: Se usan máscaras durante el rito cuando nace un bebé.

english_meaning: To stall means to stop a process and continue it at a later time.
english_example: If you give the car a push, it won’t stall.
spanish_meaning: Detener o retrasar algo para continuarlo después es frenar.
spanish_example: Si empujas el coche, no se frenará.

english_meaning: If something is supernatural, it is not real or explainable by laws of nature.
english_example: The dragon had supernatural powers, such as flying and breathing fire.
spanish_meaning: Sobrenatural es algo que no existe según las leyes naturales.
spanish_example: El dragón tenía poderes sobrenaturales, como volar y escupir fuego.

english_meaning: An aquarium is a place where fish and underwater animals are kept.
english_example: We took a trip to the aquarium and saw a scary shark.
spanish_meaning: Un acuario es un lugar donde se mantienen peces y animales acuáticos.
spanish_example: Fuimos al acuario y vimos un tiburón asombroso.

english_meaning: If something is arbitrary, it is not based on any plan or system, so it seems random.
english_example: The classroom had many arbitrary rules that confused me.
spanish_meaning: Arbitrario significa sin un sistema, por eso parece aleatorio.
spanish_example: La clase tenía reglas arbitrarias que me confundían.

english_meaning: An autobiography is a true story of a person’s life written by that person.
english_example: I read an autobiography about my favorite entertainer.
spanish_meaning: Una autobiografía es la historia real de una vida escrita por esa persona.
spanish_example: Leí una autobiografía de mi artista favorito.

english_meaning: A convention is behavior that is considered to be common or polite.
english_example: In the US, a popular convention is to shake hands when you meet someone.
spanish_meaning: Una convención son normas o costumbres sociales comunes.
spanish_example: En Estados Unidos, una convención popular es saludar con la mano al conocerse.

english_meaning: If someone is gracious, then they are kind and helpful to those who need it.
english_example: The operator was gracious enough to help me find the number.
spanish_meaning: Una persona cortés es amable y servicial con quien lo necesita.
spanish_example: El operador fue tan cortés que me ayudó a encontrar el número.

english_meaning: To improve something means to make it better.
english_example: He studied hard to improve his test scores from the previous year.
spanish_meaning: Mejorar algo significa hacerlo mejor.
spanish_example: Estudió mucho para mejorar sus calificaciones de examen respecto al año anterior.

english_meaning: To insulate something means to protect it from heat, cold, or noise.
english_example: People can conserve energy by insulating their houses.
spanish_meaning: Aislar significa proteger de calor, frío o ruido.
spanish_example: La gente puede ahorrar energía aislando sus casas.

english_meaning: To intrigue means to cause an interest in something or someone.
english_example: Her mysterious past intrigued her new friend.
spanish_meaning: Intrigar significa causar interés en alguien o algo.
spanish_example: Su pasado misterioso intrigó a su nuevo amigo.

english_meaning: Longevity is the ability to live for a long time.
english_example: Sea turtles have an amazing longevity.
spanish_meaning: La longevidad es la capacidad de vivir mucho tiempo.
spanish_example: Las tortugas marinas tienen una longevidad asombrosa.

english_meaning: To misplace something means to lose it.
english_example: I misplaced my wallet, and I didn’t find it until a week later.
spanish_meaning: Extraviar algo significa perderlo.
spanish_example: Extravié mi billetera y no la encontré hasta una semana después.

english_meaning: When children are naughty, they behave badly or do not do what they are told.
english_example: The boy had to go to his room because he was being naughty.
spanish_meaning: Naughty significa comportarse mal o no obedecer.
spanish_example: El chico tuvo que ir a su cuarto porque era travieso.

english_meaning: A norm is a way of behaving that is considered normal in a particular society.
english_example: Wearing a heavy coat all summer is not considered a norm.
spanish_meaning: Una norma es una forma de comportamiento considerada normal en una sociedad.
spanish_example: Usar abrigo grueso todo el verano no es una norma.

english_meaning: An orangutan is a large ape with red and brown hair and long arms.
english_example: Orangutans use their long arms to swing from trees.
spanish_meaning: Un orangután es un gran simio de pelo rojo-marrón y brazos largos.
spanish_example: Los orangutanes usan sus largos brazos para columpiarse de los árboles.

english_meaning: To overload something means to put more things into it than it is meant to hold.
english_example: If you overload the truck, it might crash.
spanish_meaning: Sobrecargar algo significa meter más de lo que puede sostener.
spanish_example: Si sobrecargas el camión, podría accidentarse.

english_meaning: Philanthropy is the act of helping others without wanting anything in return.
english_example: The wealthy business owner is well known for his acts of philanthropy.
spanish_meaning: La filantropía es ayudar a los demás sin esperar nada a cambio.
spanish_example: El empresario rico es conocido por sus actos de filantropía.

english_meaning: To probe into something means to ask questions to discover facts about it.
english_example: The bank probed into his financial history to see if he qualified for a loan.
spanish_meaning: Sondear algo significa investigar preguntando para descubrir hechos.
spanish_example: El banco sondeó su historial financiero para saber si calificaba para un préstamo.

english_meaning: A recipient of something is the person who receives it.
english_example: I was the recipient of four phone calls today.
spanish_meaning: Un receptor es la persona que recibe algo.
spanish_example: Fui receptor de cuatro llamadas telefónicas hoy.

english_meaning: A reptile is a cold-blooded animal that lays eggs and has skin covered with scales.
english_example: Lizards are my favorite type of reptile.
spanish_meaning: Un reptil es un animal de sangre fría con escamas y huevos.
spanish_example: Las lagartijas son mi tipo favorito de reptiles.

english_meaning: To thrive means to do well and be successful, healthy, or strong.
english_example: He may be an old man, but he continues to thrive.
spanish_meaning: Prosperar significa hacerlo bien y mantenerse exitoso, sano o fuerte.
spanish_example: Aunque es mayor, él sigue prosperando.

english_meaning: When something is ultimate, it is the final result or aim of a long series of events.
english_example: By trying hard in school, I will reach my ultimate goal of becoming a doctor.
spanish_meaning: Definitivo es el resultado o meta final de una secuencia larga.
spanish_example: Con esfuerzo en la escuela, alcanzaré mi meta definitiva: ser médico.

english_meaning: If something is antique, it is very old and rare, and therefore valuable.
english_example: My grandmother’s antique rocking chair is worth a lot of money.
spanish_meaning: Antiguo significa muy viejo y por eso valioso.
spanish_example: La mecedora antigua de mi abuela vale mucho dinero.

english_meaning: An applicant is someone who writes a request to be considered for a job or prize.
english_example: Lots of applicants came into the store when the job position became available.
spanish_meaning: Un solicitante pide ser considerado para un trabajo o premio.
spanish_example: Muchos solicitantes llegaron a la tienda cuando se abrió la vacante.

english_meaning: An artifact is an old object made by humans that is historically interesting.
english_example: We studied artifacts from an ancient Chinese settlement.
spanish_meaning: Un artefacto es un objeto antiguo hecho por humanos y de interés histórico.
spanish_example: Estudiamos artefactos de un antiguo asentamiento chino.

english_meaning: When something is authentic, it is not false or a copy of the original.
english_example: We ate authentic Italian food on our vacation to Rome.
spanish_meaning: Algo auténtico no es falso ni una copia.
spanish_example: Comimos auténtica comida italiana durante nuestras vacaciones en Roma.

english_meaning: The chronology of a series of past events is when they happened.
english_example: We learned the chronology of World War II in history class.
spanish_meaning: La cronología de eventos pasados es el orden en que ocurrieron.
spanish_example: Aprendimos la cronología de la Segunda Guerra Mundial en historia.

english_meaning: A diplomat is a representative of a country who works with another country.
english_example: The Spanish diplomat discussed trade issues with officials in Peru.
spanish_meaning: Un diplomático es un representante de su país que trabaja con otro país.
spanish_example: El diplomático español discutió temas comerciales con funcionarios de Perú.

english_meaning: An epic is a long book, poem, or movie about a period of time or a great event.
english_example: The poet wrote an epic about the great discoveries of the past thousand years.
spanish_meaning: Un texto épico es un poema o película larga sobre grandes hechos.
spanish_example: El poeta escribió un texto épico sobre los grandes descubrimientos del pasado.

english_meaning: An excerpt is a short piece of writing or music taken from a larger piece.
english_example: I didn’t listen to the entire symphony online, but I did play an excerpt.
spanish_meaning: Un extracto es un fragmento corto de un texto o música.
spanish_example: No escuché toda la sinfonía, pero sí un extracto.

english_meaning: A fossil is the hard remains of a prehistoric animal or plant.
english_example: The expert arranged the fossils to build the skeleton of the dinosaur.
spanish_meaning: Un fósil es el resto endurecido de un animal o planta prehistórica.
spanish_example: El experto ordenó fósiles para reconstruir el esqueleto del dinosaurio.

english_meaning: To humiliate someone means to make them feel ashamed and embarrassed.
english_example: I was humiliated when I tripped and fell down in front of the whole school.
spanish_meaning: Humillar a alguien significa hacerlo sentir avergonzado.
spanish_example: Me sentí humillado cuando me tropecé frente a toda la escuela.

english_meaning: When a poem is considered lyric, it expresses a lot of emotion.
english_example: I enjoy reading and creating my own lyric poetry.
spanish_meaning: Lírico describe un poema con mucha emoción.
spanish_example: Me gusta leer y crear mis propios poemas líricos.

english_meaning: Majesty is supreme greatness or authority.
english_example: The majesty of the old castle amazed the tourists who came to see it.
spanish_meaning: Majestad es grandeza o autoridad suprema.
spanish_example: La majestuosidad del castillo antiguo sorprendió a los turistas.

english_meaning: The monarch of a country is someone who rules a country through hereditary right.
english_example: The monarch lived in a beautiful palace with a grand gate.
spanish_meaning: Un monarca es quien gobierna un país por herencia.
spanish_example: El monarca vivía en un hermoso palacio con una gran puerta.

english_meaning: To precede something means to come before it.
english_example: The hurricane was preceded by a moment of still wind and clear sky.
spanish_meaning: Preceder algo significa venir antes.
spanish_example: El huracán fue precedido por un momento de calma y cielo despejado.

english_meaning: When someone is punctual, they do something or arrive at the right time.
english_example: My mother hates being late. She is the most punctual person I know.
spanish_meaning: Puntual significa hacer o llegar a la hora correcta.
spanish_example: Mi mamá odia llegar tarde; es muy puntual.

english_meaning: To recruit people means to select them to join or work for an organization.
english_example: We successfully recruited someone to be the new manager.
spanish_meaning: Reclutar personas significa elegirlas para unirse a una organización.
spanish_example: Reclutamos a alguien que sería el nuevo gerente.

english_meaning: A refund is money given back to a person when an item is returned to a store.
english_example: I asked for a refund because the shoes I bought were too tight.
spanish_meaning: Un reembolso es dinero que se devuelve al devolver un producto.
spanish_example: Pedí un reembolso porque los zapatos eran demasiado estrechos.

english_meaning: A register is an official list or record of people or things.
english_example: At a wedding, there is register for all of the guests to sign.
spanish_meaning: Un registro es una lista oficial de personas o cosas.
spanish_example: En una boda, hay un registro donde firman todos los invitados.

english_meaning: Renown is the quality of being well known due to having done good things.
english_example: Michael is a singer of great renown in New Zealand.
spanish_meaning: Renombre es ser muy conocido por hacer cosas buenas.
spanish_example: Michael es un cantante de gran renombre en Nueva Zelanda.

english_meaning: A tusk is a long, curved, pointed tooth of an elephant, boar, or walrus.
english_example: Sadly, some people hunt elephants and remove their tusks to sell them.
spanish_meaning: Un colmillo es un diente largo y curvado de elefantes, jabalíes o morsas.
spanish_example: Algunas personas cazan elefantes y extraen sus colmillos para venderlos.

english_meaning: To coexist with something means to exist with it in the same time and place.
english_example: Our pets coexist in our home with few or no problems.
spanish_meaning: Coexistir con algo significa existir en el mismo tiempo y lugar.
spanish_example: Nuestras mascotas conviven en casa con muy pocos problemas.

english_meaning: To conceive something means to be able to imagine or believe it.
english_example: The child could not conceive the actual size of the Earth.
spanish_meaning: Concebir algo significa imaginarlo o creerlo posible.
spanish_example: El niño no podía concebir el tamaño real de la Tierra.

english_meaning: When something or someone is dubious, they are not considered honest.
english_example: The police thought that the man’s description of the crime was dubious.
spanish_meaning: Dudoso significa poco fiable o no considerado honesto.
spanish_example: La policía pensó que la descripción del hombre era dudosa.

english_meaning: An ego is a person’s sense of their own worth.
english_example: Kelly’s ego made her think that she was some kind of a superhero.
spanish_meaning: El ego es la idea de alguien sobre su propio valor.
spanish_example: El ego de Kelly la hacía pensar que era una superhéroe.

english_meaning: Elastic is a rubber that stretches when it is pulled.
english_example: He attached the elastic bungee to his legs before he jumped of the platform.
spanish_meaning: Elástico es un tipo de goma que se estira al tirar.
spanish_example: Ató elástico bungee a sus piernas antes de saltar de la plataforma.

english_meaning: An endeavor is an attempt to do something, especially something new, or original.
english_example: The company’s new advertising endeavor ended in a horrible failure.
spanish_meaning: Un esfuerzo o intento es una tentativa de hacer algo, especialmente algo nuevo u original.
spanish_example: El nuevo esfuerzo publicitario de la empresa terminó en un fracaso horrible.

english_meaning: To engrave means to cut a design, or words, into the surface of something.
english_example: The couple engraved their names onto the old pine tree.
spanish_meaning: Grabar significa cortar un diseño o palabras en la superficie de algo.
spanish_example: La pareja grabó sus nombres en un árbol de pino.

english_meaning: To excavate means to dig on land and remove dirt to look for something.
english_example: The team wishes to excavate the site in hope of finding fossils.
spanish_meaning: Excavar significa cavar y quitar tierra para buscar algo.
spanish_example: El equipo quiso excavar el sitio con la esperanza de hallar fósiles.

english_meaning: When something is jagged, it has a tough, uneven shape or edge.
english_example: The swimmer was hurt when he fell on the jagged rocks.
spanish_meaning: Irregular significa tener forma o borde áspero y desigual.
spanish_example: El nadador se hirió al caer sobre las rocas irregulares.

english_meaning: A locale is a small area or place where something specific happens.
english_example: The spa was the perfect locale for my mother to relax and enjoy her vacation.
spanish_meaning: Un lugar es un sitio pequeño o específico.
spanish_example: El spa fue el lugar perfecto para que mi madre se relajara.
notes: Puede entenderse también como “lugar” o “local” según contexto.

english_meaning: Magnitude refers to large size.
english_example: This was a discovery of great magnitude.
spanish_meaning: La magnitud es el tamaño o importancia de algo.
spanish_example: El descubrimiento fue de gran magnitud.

english_meaning: A mold is a hollow container that is used to make certain shapes.
english_example: Using a cupcake mold will ensure that they come out just right.
spanish_meaning: Un molde es un contenedor que da forma a un objeto.
spanish_example: Usar un molde ayuda a que salgan bien.

english_meaning: When something is outright, it is open and direct.
english_example: Everyone knew that the boy’s story was an outright lie.
spanish_meaning: Directo significa abierto y sin rodeos.
spanish_example: Todos sabían que la historia del chico era una mentira directa.

english_meaning: The periphery of an area, place, or thing is the edge of it.
english_example: A beautiful floral pattern is on the periphery of the stationery.
spanish_meaning: La periferia es el borde de una zona, lugar o cosa.
spanish_example: Había un patrón floral en la periferia de la papelería.

english_meaning: Plaster is a smooth paste that gets hard when it dries.
english_example: He used plaster to fill in the cracks in the old walls.
spanish_meaning: El yeso es una pasta que se endurece al secarse.
spanish_example: Usó yeso para rellenar las grietas de las paredes viejas.

english_meaning: A shovel is a tool with a long handle that is used for digging.
english_example: The boy grabbed his shovel and got all of the snow off of the sidewalk.
spanish_meaning: La pala es una herramienta con mango largo para cavar.
spanish_example: El chico tomó su pala y limpió la nieve de la acera.

english_meaning: When something is skeletal, it relates to bones in the body.
english_example: We studied the skeletal system in anatomy class.
spanish_meaning: Se usa para algo relacionado con los huesos.
spanish_example: Estudiamos el sistema esquelético en la clase de anatomía.

english_meaning: When you stumble, you move in an awkward, unplanned way.
english_example: She stumbles a lot when she is drunk.
spanish_meaning: Tropezar es moverse de forma torpe y sin planificación.
spanish_example: Ella tropezó mucho cuando estaba ebria.

english_meaning: Your thigh is the upper half of your leg.
english_example: He suffered from a serious thigh injury.
spanish_meaning: El muslo es la parte superior de la pierna.
spanish_example: Se rompió gravemente el muslo.

english_meaning: When something or someone is vicious, they are violent and cruel.
english_example: The vicious dog tried to bite the small child.
spanish_meaning: Cruel es ser violento o despiadado.
spanish_example: El perro cruel intentó morder al niño.

english_meaning: An attic is a room just below a house’s roof.
english_example: There were two windows in their attic.
spanish_meaning: Un ático es una habitación bajo el tejado de una casa.
spanish_example: El ático tenía dos ventanas.

english_meaning: A chunk is a thick, solid piece of something.
english_example: When they broke open the rock, they saw it was filled with chunks of gold.
spanish_meaning: Un bloque es una pieza gruesa y sólida de algo.
spanish_example: Al romper la roca, vieron que estaba llena de bloques de oro.

english_meaning: If something is civic, then it is related to a town or city, in particular to its government.
english_example: Most of the important civic buildings are located downtown.
spanish_meaning: Cívico se relaciona con una ciudad o su gobierno.
spanish_example: La mayoría de los edificios cívicos están en el centro.

english_meaning: A descent is a movement downwards.
english_example: During the space shuttle’s descent, the Earth appeared larger and larger.
spanish_meaning: Un descenso es un movimiento hacia abajo.
spanish_example: Durante el descenso del transbordador, la Tierra se veía más grande.

english_meaning: A din is loud, unpleasant, and extended noise.
english_example: The din of the rusty machinery made the factory a horrible place to work.
spanish_meaning: El estruendo es un ruido fuerte y desagradable.
spanish_example: El estruendo de la maquinaria hizo ese lugar horrible para trabajar.

english_meaning: To dissatisfy someone means to fail to please them.
english_example: He was dissatisfied with his meal.
spanish_meaning: Descontentar a alguien es no satisfacerlo suficientemente.
spanish_example: Él estaba descontento con su comida.

english_meaning: A fuss is excited or annoyed behavior that is not useful in any way.
english_example: The child made an awful fuss because she didn’t want to go to bed.
spanish_meaning: El alboroto es conducta molesta o exagerada.
spanish_example: La niña hizo un gran alboroto porque no quería dormir.

english_meaning: A glamorous occasion is one full of beauty and excitement.
english_example: There is nothing glamorous about where we live. It is very ordinary.
spanish_meaning: Glamoroso es elegante y llamativo.
spanish_example: No hay nada glamoroso en donde vivimos.

english_meaning: If food is gourmet, then it is nicer and more expensive than regular food.
english_example: In order to get a gourmet meal, you have to eat at an expensive restaurant.
spanish_meaning: Gourmet se refiere a comida más fina y cara.
spanish_example: Para probar un plato gourmet hay que ir a un restaurante caro.

english_meaning: The word hence shows that something is a result of something else.
english_example: Mary forgot the key. Hence, we couldn’t open the door.
spanish_meaning: Por lo tanto expresa una consecuencia.
spanish_example: María olvidó la llave, por lo tanto no pudimos abrir la puerta.

english_meaning: If something is intrinsic, then it is related to the basic nature of that thing.
english_example: Paper money has no intrinsic value. It is useful simply because society says it is.
spanish_meaning: Intrínseco es lo propio de la naturaleza de algo.
spanish_example: El dinero de papel no tiene valor intrínseco.

english_meaning: A kettle is a large metal pot used for boiling liquids or cooking food.
english_example: The soup was being cooked in a large kettle.
spanish_meaning: La tetera es una olla de metal para calentar líquidos o cocinar.
spanish_example: La sopa se cocinó en una gran tetera.

english_meaning: A ministry is a government department.
english_example: The Ministry of Trade is responsible for taxing imports and exports.
spanish_meaning: Un ministerio es un departamento del gobierno.
spanish_example: El ministerio de Comercio se encarga de impuestos de importación y exportación.

english_meaning: An ordeal is a bad experience.
english_example: Driving in the snowstorm was an ordeal she’d never forget.
spanish_meaning: Una ordeal es una experiencia muy difícil o dolorosa.
spanish_example: Conducir en la tormenta de nieve fue una auténtica prueba.

english_meaning: If someone is outspoken, then they are not afraid to say what they think.
english_example: She was an outspoken critic about the new banking laws.
spanish_meaning: Franco significa expresar abiertamente lo que se piensa.
spanish_example: Ella fue franca al criticar las nuevas leyes.

english_meaning: To overwork someone means to make them tired with too much work.
english_example: After working for three weeks with only one day of rest, Judy was overworked.
spanish_meaning: Sobretrabajar significa exigir demasiado a alguien con trabajo.
spanish_example: Tras tres semanas con un día de descanso, Judy estaba sobrecargada.

english_meaning: If something is particular, then it is a single, important part of a group of things.
english_example: The dress shop didn’t have the particular dress she was looking for.
spanish_meaning: Particular significa un elemento específico de un grupo.
spanish_example: La tienda no tenía la prenda particular que buscaba.

english_meaning: To snore means to make a loud noise each time a sleeping person breathes.
english_example: It was impossible to get to sleep because my husband snored.
spanish_meaning: Roncar es hacer ruido al respirar al dormir.
spanish_example: No podía dormir porque mi esposo roncaba.

english_meaning: If something is done soundly, then it is done in the best or most complete way.
english_example: The nearest opponent was ten meters behind. She won the race soundly.
spanish_meaning: Sólidamente significa de manera completa y clara.
spanish_example: Ganó la carrera sólidamente, con mucha ventaja.

english_meaning: A superintendent is a person who runs a certain department or building.
english_example: The superintendent decided to close the schools because of the weather.
spanish_meaning: Un superintendente dirige un departamento o edificio.
spanish_example: El superintendente cerró las escuelas por el clima.

english_meaning: If something is congested, it is full or blocked.
english_example: Tom didn’t get home until after dark because the road was so congested.
spanish_meaning: Congestionado significa lleno o bloqueado.
spanish_example: No llegó a casa hasta tarde porque la carretera estaba congestionada.

english_meaning: A courier is someone who takes and delivers mail or packages.
english_example: Before trains, most couriers used horses to travel.
spanish_meaning: El mensajero lleva y entrega cartas o paquetes.
spanish_example: Antes de los trenes, la mayoría de mensajeros usaba caballos.

english_meaning: To deform something means to change it from its correct or original shape.
english_example: The computer program deformed the building’s picture into an unreal sight.
spanish_meaning: Deformar es cambiar la forma correcta de algo.
spanish_example: La computadora deformó la imagen del edificio.

english_meaning: Etiquette is the group of rules about how to be polite.
english_example: When in Asian countries, bowing is a form of etiquette.
spanish_meaning: La etiqueta es el conjunto de reglas de cortesía.
spanish_example: En Japón, la reverencia es una forma de etiqueta.

english_meaning: If something is exclusive, it is expensive and only for rich people.
english_example: The golf course was so exclusive that most people hadn’t even heard of it.
spanish_meaning: Exclusivo significa caro y para pocos privilegiados.
spanish_example: El campo de golf era tan exclusivo que casi nadie lo conocía.

english_meaning: Freight is a set of items carried on a train, boat, or airplane.
english_example: Trade ships only carried valuable freight like silk and spices.
spanish_meaning: La carga es el conjunto de mercancías transportadas.
spanish_example: Los barcos solo llevaban carga valiosa como seda y especias.

english_meaning: A garment is a piece of clothing.
english_example: The businessman had all of his garments cleaned before the important meeting.
spanish_meaning: Una prenda es una pieza de ropa.
spanish_example: El empresario limpió todas sus prendas antes de la reunión.

english_meaning: Insomnia is a condition in which a person has difficulty sleeping.
english_example: Nate’s insomnia prevented him from getting enough rest.
spanish_meaning: Insomnio es la dificultad para dormir.
spanish_example: El insomnio de Nate no le permitió descansar lo suficiente.

english_meaning: Intuitive is knowing about something without having support or proof.
