english_example: Rhonda had an intuitive feeling that Shane wasn’t coming to school today.
spanish_meaning: Intuitivo es saber algo sin prueba o apoyo.
spanish_example: Rhonda tenía una intuición de que Shane no iría a la escuela.

english_meaning: If something is liable to happen, it is very likely that it will happen.
english_example: During the summer months, hikers in the forest are liable to see deer and elk.
spanish_meaning: Propenso significa probable que ocurra.
spanish_example: En verano, los excursionistas son propensos a ver ciervos y alces.

english_meaning: To obsess about something means to think about it all of the time.
english_example: After watching the Star Wars movies, Ike obsessed about becoming a Jedi.
spanish_meaning: Obsesionarse es pensar continuamente en algo o alguien.
spanish_example: Después de ver Star Wars, Ike se obsesionó con ser Jedi.

english_meaning: When something is overboard, it is over the side of a boat and in the water.
english_example: Tom and Gary slipped on the wet floor and fell overboard.
spanish_meaning: Por la borda es terminar fuera de un barco y en el agua.
spanish_example: Tom y Gary resbalaron y cayeron por la borda.

english_meaning: A premium is a payment that is higher than average.
english_example: Tony paid a premium for the gas because it made his car run better.
spanish_meaning: Una prima es un pago más alto que la media.
spanish_example: Tony pagó una prima por la gasolina para que el coche anduviera mejor.

english_meaning: A privilege is a special right given only to a certain person or group of people.
english_example: Only the best employee had the privilege of parking in that spot.
spanish_meaning: Privilegio es un derecho especial de una persona o grupo.
spanish_example: Solo el mejor empleado tenía el privilegio de aparcar ahí.

english_meaning: To propel something means to push or move it somewhere.
english_example: The strong wind propelled the leaf through the air and across the street.
spanish_meaning: Impulsar es empujar para mover algo.
spanish_example: El viento impulsó la hoja por el aire y la cruzó la calle.

english_meaning: To socialize is to have a good time with people.
english_example: I like to socialize with my classmates after school.
spanish_meaning: Socializar es pasar un buen momento con otras personas.
spanish_example: Me gusta socializar con mis compañeros después de la escuela.

english_meaning: To suppress something means to prevent it from happening.
english_example: She suppressed her urge to scream because she didn’t want to be noticed.
spanish_meaning: Reprimir significa impedir que algo ocurra.
spanish_example: Reprimió su impulso de gritar para no ser visto.

english_meaning: A tram is a vehicle like a streetcar that runs on electricity above ground.
english_example: I took the tram to Eighth Avenue.
spanish_meaning: Un tranvía es un vehículo sobre vías impulsado eléctricamente.
spanish_example: Tomé el tranvía hasta Octava Avenida.

english_meaning: To unsettle someone means to make them anxious or worried.
english_example: The dark clouds in the sky unsettled Beth.
spanish_meaning: Inquietar significa causar ansiedad o preocupación.
spanish_example: Las nubes oscuras inquietaron a Beth.

english_meaning: To warp means to become bent into the wrong shape.
english_example: The woman put the clock above the fireplace, and the heat warped it.
spanish_meaning: Deformarse es doblarse hasta quedar de forma incorrecta.
spanish_example: El calor del hogar deformó el reloj.

english_meaning: To accumulate something is to collect a lot of it over time.
english_example: The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.
spanish_meaning: Acumular es reunir mucha cantidad con el tiempo.
spanish_example: El correo se acumuló en su buzón mientras estaban de vacaciones.

english_meaning: When something is aerial, it relates to being in the air or flying.
english_example: The aerial photographer took pictures from the air balloon.
spanish_meaning: Aéreo se relaciona con estar en el aire o volar.
spanish_example: El fotógrafo aéreo tomó fotos desde un globo aerostático.

english_meaning: An apparatus is a device used for a particular purpose.
english_example: The campers had an apparatus that showed them their exact location.
spanish_meaning: Un aparato es un dispositivo usado para un propósito específico.
spanish_example: Los campistas tenían un aparato para conocer su ubicación exacta.

english_meaning: An avalanche is a large amount of snow, ice, and rock falling off a mountain.
english_example: The avalanche destroyed the mountain village.
spanish_meaning: La avalancha es una gran cantidad de nieve, hielo y rocas cayendo de una montaña.
spanish_example: La avalancha destruyó el pueblo de montaña.

english_meaning: Consistency is the state of always behaving in the same way.
english_example: Her consistency in archery meant that our team had a chance to win.
spanish_meaning: La consistencia es la constancia de conducta o estado.
spanish_example: La consistencia de ella en arquería nos dio opciones de ganar.

english_meaning: To discharge someone is to allow them to leave from a place, usually a hospital.
english_example: I was discharged from the hospital after three days.
spanish_meaning: Dar de alta es permitir salir de un lugar como un hospital.
spanish_example: Me dieron de alta del hospital después de tres días.

english_meaning: An episode is something that happens as part of a series of events.
english_example: We watched the final exciting episode of the TV series.
spanish_meaning: Un episodio es un suceso dentro de una serie de eventos.
spanish_example: Vimos el episodio final emocionante de la serie de televisión.

english_meaning: When something is intact, it is complete and not damaged.
english_example: Despite being over 30 years old, my father’s model ship is still intact.
spanish_meaning: Intacto significa completo y sin daños.
spanish_example: A pesar de sus 30 años, el barco de mi padre sigue intacto.

english_meaning: When a person is mortal, they cannot live forever.
english_example: All people are mortal.
spanish_meaning: Mortal significa que algo no puede vivir para siempre.
spanish_example: Todos los seres humanos son mortales.

english_meaning: An omen is a sign of what will happen in the future.
english_example: He thought that seeing a black cat in the street was an omen of bad luck.
spanish_meaning: El presagio es una señal de lo que ocurrirá en el futuro.
spanish_example: El gato negro fue visto como un presagio de mala suerte.

english_meaning: When the sky is overcast, it is full of clouds and is not sunny.
english_example: The sky was overcast in the morning, but by noon it was bright again.
spanish_meaning: Encapotado significa lleno de nubes, sin sol.
spanish_example: El cielo estaba encapotado por la mañana, pero luego se aclaró.

english_meaning: When something is poignant, it causes a very strong feeling of sadness.
english_example: The girls cried at the end of the poignant movie.
spanish_meaning: Conmovedor es aquello que causa tristeza intensa.
spanish_example: Las chicas lloraron al final de la película conmovedora.

english_meaning: A ranger is a person who protects forests or parks.
english_example: Peter wanted to be a ranger because he liked spending time outside.
spanish_meaning: El guardabosques protege bosques o parques.
spanish_example: Peter quería ser guardabosques porque le gustaba el exterior.

english_meaning: Rubble is piles of broken stone and wood created after a building is destroyed.
english_example: There was rubble all over the city after the earthquake.
spanish_meaning: Los escombros son restos rotos de piedra y madera tras una destrucción.
spanish_example: Había escombros por toda la ciudad tras el terremoto.

english_meaning: To seclude someone means to keep them away from other people.
english_example: She was secluded on an island for over a year.
spanish_meaning: Aislar es mantener a alguien lejos de los demás.
spanish_example: Ella estuvo aislada en una isla durante más de un año.

english_meaning: If something moves sideways, then it moves to or from the side.
english_example: Jim turned sideways in order to slow down and stop his snowboard.
spanish_meaning: De lado significa moverse hacia la parte lateral.
spanish_example: Jim se inclinó de lado para frenar y detener el snowboard.

english_meaning: To sob is to cry loudly.
english_example: I sobbed when my youngest daughter got married.
spanish_meaning: Sollozar es llorar con fuerza y voz alta.
spanish_example: Lloré cuando mi hija menor se casó.

english_meaning: When something or someone is sober, they are serious and calm.
english_example: After the funeral, everybody felt very sober.
spanish_meaning: Sobrio significa serio y tranquilo, sin emociones extremas.
spanish_example: Tras el funeral, todo el mundo se sentía muy sobrio.

english_meaning: A speck is a very small mark or amount.
english_example: A speck of blood appeared where the mosquito had bit him.
spanish_meaning: Una mancha o puntito es una cantidad mínima.
spanish_example: Apareció una mancha de sangre donde lo picó el mosquito.

english_meaning: An upbringing is the way that someone is taught to behave by their parents.
english_example: He had a strict upbringing and was never allowed to watch television.
spanish_meaning: La crianza es la forma en que uno aprende a comportarse.
spanish_example: Su crianza fue muy estricta y nunca vio televisión.

english_meaning: Bliss is a state of complete happiness.
english_example: Every time he sees his girlfriend, he feels a sense of bliss.
spanish_meaning: La dicha es una sensación de felicidad completa.
spanish_example: Cada vez que ve a su novia, siente mucha dicha.

english_meaning: A butler is the most important male servant in a wealthy house.
english_example: Whenever he needed something, he rang a bell, and the butler appeared.
spanish_meaning: El mayordomo es el sirviente principal en una casa rica.
spanish_example: Cada vez que necesitaba algo, sonaba una campana y venía el mayordomo.

english_meaning: A cramp is a strong pain caused by a muscle after a lot of physical use.
english_example: After the marathon, Jenny got a terrible cramp in her calf muscle.
spanish_meaning: El calambre es un dolor fuerte por esfuerzo muscular.
spanish_example: Después del maratón, Jenny tuvo un calambre terrible en la pantorrilla.

english_meaning: When you decorate a room, you make it more attractive by adding beautiful things to it.
english_example: Have you decorated the room for Christmas yet?
spanish_meaning: Decorar es hacer un lugar más bonito con objetos.
spanish_example: ¿Ya decoraste la habitación para Navidad?

english_meaning: When a building is dilapidated, it is old and in bad condition.
english_example: Paint peeled off of the old dilapidated apartment building.
spanish_meaning: Deteriorado significa viejo y en mal estado.
spanish_example: La pintura se pelaba del edificio deteriorado.

english_meaning: To evoke a memory or emotion means to make it occur.
english_example: The picture evoked memories of when she was a young girl.
spanish_meaning: Evocar es hacer que surja un recuerdo o emoción.
spanish_example: La foto evocó recuerdos de su infancia.

english_meaning: A farewell is an instance of saying goodbye or a way to say it.
english_example: She got on the plane after we said our farewells.
spanish_meaning: La despedida es un adiós o la forma de decirlo.
spanish_example: Dijo adiós con una despedida antes de subir al avión.

english_meaning: A faucet is a device that controls the flow of a liquid or gas.
english_example: Turn off the faucet when you are done brushing your teeth.
spanish_meaning: El grifo controla el flujo de agua o gas.
spanish_example: Cierra el grifo cuando termines de cepillarte.

english_meaning: Filth is dirt or dirty things that disgust you.
english_example: There was tons of filth and trash on the shore of the river.
spanish_meaning: La suciedad son cosas sucias que repugnan.
spanish_example: Había mucha suciedad y basura en la orilla del río.

english_meaning: A flaw is a mistake in something that causes it to be less effective or correct.
english_example: We discovered a major flaw in the metal chain.
spanish_meaning: Un defecto es un error que reduce la eficacia de algo.
spanish_example: Encontramos un defecto importante en la cadena de metal.

english_meaning: To grin means to smile broadly.
english_example: That joke makes me grin every time I hear it.
spanish_meaning: Sonreír significa mostrar una sonrisa amplia.
spanish_example: Ese chiste me hace sonreír cada vez que lo oigo.

english_meaning: Housekeeping is the maintenance of a house or an establishment like a hotel.
english_example: Housekeeping is not much fun, but it has to be done.
spanish_meaning: La limpieza del hogar es el mantenimiento de una casa o hotel.
spanish_example: La limpieza del hogar no es divertida, pero hay que hacerla.

english_meaning: A mound of something is a large pile of it.
english_example: There was a mound of clothes on the teenager’s messy floor.
spanish_meaning: Un montículo es una gran pila de algo.
spanish_example: Había un montículo de ropa en el piso de la habitación.

english_meaning: When a body part is numb, it does not have any feeling.
english_example: After holding my hand under the icy water, my fingers were numb.
spanish_meaning: Entumecido significa sin sensibilidad.
spanish_example: Tras el agua helada, mis dedos quedaron entumecidos.

english_meaning: When people are reckless, they act in an unsafe way.
english_example: The reckless driver posed a threat to everyone else on the road.
spanish_meaning: Imprudente significa actuar de forma peligrosa e insegura.
spanish_example: El conductor imprudente ponía en riesgo a todos.

english_meaning: Slate is a dark grey rock that can easily be split into layers.
english_example: The roof of the church is made of slate.
spanish_meaning: La pizarra es una roca oscura usada en capas.
spanish_example: El techo de la iglesia está hecho de pizarra.

english_meaning: A stool is a seat with legs but no support for a person’s arms and back.
english_example: I don’t find stools very comfortable to sit on.
spanish_meaning: El taburete es un asiento sin respaldo ni apoyabrazos.
spanish_example: No me gusta sentarme en esos taburetes.

english_meaning: A testament to something shows that it exists or is true.
english_example: The beautiful performance was a testament to the singer’s natural talents.
spanish_meaning: Un testimonio es prueba de que algo existe o es verdadero.
spanish_example: La actuación fue un testamento de su talento natural.

english_meaning: Timber is wood that is used for building houses and making furniture.
english_example: Trees in this area are grown specifically to be used for timber.
spanish_meaning: La madera es el material obtenido de los árboles para construir.
spanish_example: Los árboles de está zona se cultivan para madera.

english_meaning: A valve is a device attached to a pipe and controls the flow of liquid or air.
english_example: The mechanic removed the dirt to clear the engine valve.
spanish_meaning: La válvula controla el paso de un fluido en una tubería.
spanish_example: El mecánico limpió la válvula del motor.

english_meaning: Camouflage is something used to hide people and things.
english_example: The green and brown camouflage was best used for hiding in forests and jungles.
spanish_meaning: El camuflaje sirve para ocultar personas y objetos.
spanish_example: El camuflaje verde y marrón fue el más efectivo.

english_meaning: When you confront someone, you meet them face to face to deal with a problem.
english_example: I confronted him as he left the meeting and told him I thought he was wrong.
spanish_meaning: Enfrentar significa tratar un problema cara a cara.
spanish_example: La confronté al salir de la reunión.

english_meaning: To contemplate something means to think about it.
english_example: Mark took a moment to contemplate the math problem before solving it.
spanish_meaning: Contemplar es pensar detenidamente sobre algo.
spanish_example: Tomó un momento para contemplar el problema.

english_meaning: To contend with something means to struggle to overcome it.
english_example: Stacy had to contend with a learning disability throughout high school.
spanish_meaning: Luchar significa esforzarse para superar algo.
spanish_example: Stacy tuvo que luchar con una discapacidad de aprendizaje en la escuela.

english_meaning: A cot is a small portable bed.
english_example: At the camp, the boys’ cabin was lined with cots.
spanish_meaning: Un catre es una cama pequeña y portátil.
spanish_example: En el campamento, la cabaña tenía muchos catres.

english_meaning: To enlist means to join the military.
english_example: In their final year at school, the students were asked to enlist in the military.
spanish_meaning: Alistarse es unirse al ejército o a una fuerza.
spanish_example: En su último año les pidieron alistarse en el ejército.

english_meaning: A frontier is a border between two regions or countries.
english_example: A fence was built along the frontier where the river curved.
spanish_meaning: La frontera es la línea entre dos regiones o países.
spanish_example: Se construyó una cerca en la frontera donde el río doblaba.

english_meaning: A guerilla is a person who fights as part of an unofficial army.
english_example: Guerilla warfare involves a lot of hit-and-run fighting.
spanish_meaning: Un guerrillero participa en luchas militares irregulares.
spanish_example: La guerrilla usa tácticas de guerra de guerrilla.

english_meaning: A handbook is material that gives specific information or instructions.
english_example: If you look at the handbook, it will tell you which wires to connect to the TV.
spanish_meaning: Un manual es una guía con instrucciones.
spanish_example: El manual te dirá qué cables conectar al televisor.

english_meaning: If someone is hesitant, then they are not sure or are slow to act or speak.
english_example: Although he knew the answer, he was hesitant to say it because he might be wrong.
spanish_meaning: Vacilante es ser inseguo o dudar antes de actuar.
spanish_example: Era vacilante para decir la respuesta por miedo a equivocarse.

english_meaning: If something is lush, then it is full of a variety of large, healthy plants.
english_example: The lush jungle was filled with plants, trees, and vines.
spanish_meaning: Exuberante describe vegetación abundante y viva.
spanish_example: La jungla era exuberante y llena de plantas.

english_meaning: An outfit is a set of clothes worn together, often for a certain job or event.
english_example: Kelly’s new outfit made her look so glamourous.
spanish_meaning: Un atuendo es un conjunto de ropa para una ocasión.
spanish_example: El nuevo atuendo de Kelly la hacía lucir glamorosa.

english_meaning: A paw is an animal’s foot that has claws or soft pads.
english_example: The kitten cleaned its paws with its tongue.
spanish_meaning: La pata es el pie de un animal con garras o almohadillas.
spanish_example: El gatito limpió sus patas con la lengua.

english_meaning: If something or someone is splendid, then they are very good.
english_example: From his head down to his shoes, his clothes looked splendid.
spanish_meaning: Espléndido describe algo muy bueno o impresionante.
spanish_example: Su ropa se veía espléndida de la cabeza a los zapatos.

english_meaning: To stray means to go in a wrong direction and become lost.
english_example: He found himself lost because he had strayed from the tour group.
spanish_meaning: Desviarse es apartarse del camino correcto.
spanish_example: Se perdió porque se desvió del grupo.

english_meaning: If something is substantial, then it is of great importance, size, or value.
english_example: The bank said that he owed it a substantial amount of money.
spanish_meaning: Sustancial significa importante o de gran valor.
spanish_example: El banco dijo que debía una cantidad sustancial.

english_meaning: A torch is a stick with one end on fire that can be carried in order to give light.
english_example: He grabbed a piece of wood and stuck it in the fire in order to make a torch.
spanish_meaning: La antorcha es un palo con fuego para dar luz.
spanish_example: Tomó una rama y la encendió para hacer una antorcha.

english_meaning: A tract is a large area of land.
english_example: On the other side of the mountains, there was a long tract of forest.
spanish_meaning: La extensión es una gran área de terreno.
spanish_example: Había una extensa extensión de bosque.

english_meaning: A vigil is a period of watchful attention at night for a specific purpose.
english_example: He had a vigil in front of the tomb for three days.
spanish_meaning: La vigilia es quedarse despierto vigilando.
spanish_example: Estuvo en vigilia frente a la tumba durante tres días.

english_meaning: If someone is weary, then they are tired.
english_example: Jane was weary after a long day of work.
spanish_meaning: Cansado es sentirse exhausto.
spanish_example: Jane estaba cansada después de un día largo de trabajo.

english_meaning: If something is amid something else, then it is in the middle of it.
english_example: The bee was busily flying amid the flowers in the garden.
spanish_meaning: En medio de algo significa dentro o entre ello.
spanish_example: La abeja volaba en medio de las flores del jardín.

english_meaning: If something happens backstage, it occurs behind a theater’s stage.
english_example: After the show, the director went backstage and thanked the actors.
spanish_meaning: Detrás del escenario ocurren los preparativos de una función.
spanish_example: Tras el espectáculo, el director fue detrás del escenario.

english_meaning: A billionaire is someone who has at least one billion dollars.
english_example: The sale of his inventions made the inventor a billionaire.
spanish_meaning: Multimillonario describe alguien con mucha riqueza.
spanish_example: La venta de sus inventos lo volvió multimillonario.

english_meaning: A brute is someone who behaves or looks like a violent animal.
english_example: My older brother can act like a brute when he doesn’t get his way.
spanish_meaning: Bruto describe una persona violenta o tosca.
spanish_example: Mi hermano puede actuar como bruto cuando se molesta.

english_meaning: If someone is clumsy, then they are awkward in handling things.
english_example: The businessman was clumsy and dropped his work files.
spanish_meaning: Torpe significa con poca destreza.
spanish_example: El hombre fue torpe y dejó caer sus archivos.

english_meaning: To collide with something means to hit it while moving.
english_example: The two cars collided with each other because their drivers were not careful.
spanish_meaning: Colisionar es chocar con algo.
spanish_example: Los dos autos colisionaron por descuido.

english_meaning: A culprit is someone who has committed a crime or other bad deed.
english_example: The police were still searching for the culprit from the robbery.
spanish_meaning: El culpable es quien cometió un delito.
spanish_example: La policía buscaba al culpable del robo.

english_meaning: To evacuate means to leave a place of danger to a place of safety.
english_example: During the flood, many families were evacuated to higher ground.
spanish_meaning: Evacuar significa retirar gente a un lugar seguro.
spanish_example: Durante la inundación, muchas familias fueron evacuadas.

english_meaning: If something is flammable, then it is able to catch on fire.
english_example: Be careful with that blanket near the candle. It is extremely flammable.
spanish_meaning: Inflamable significa que puede arder.
spanish_example: La manta es muy inflamable cerca de la llama.

english_meaning: A mob is a large crowd of people that often wants to cause violence.
english_example: The copier was destroyed by a mob of angry workers.
spanish_meaning: La turba es una multitud grande y hostil.
spanish_example: Los trabajadores formaron una turba y destruyeron la copiadora.

english_meaning: If something is premature, then it is done too early or before the proper time.
english_example: Mark’s celebration was premature because the ball hadn’t fallen in the hole.
spanish_meaning: Prematuro significa demasiado temprano.
spanish_example: La celebración era prematura porque la bola no había caído.

english_meaning: To resent something means to have bad feelings about it.
english_example: He resented the fact that he had never been able to play an instrument.
spanish_meaning: Resentir es sentir malos sentimientos por algo.
spanish_example: Ella resentía no haber podido tocar un instrumento.

english_meaning: A satire is a work of art that uses humor and irony to make fun of something.
english_example: This book is a satire of what life was like in the army.
spanish_meaning: La sátira usa humor para criticar.
spanish_example: Este libro es una sátira del ejército.

english_meaning: Scrutiny is the careful examination of something.
english_example: A scientist should always practice scrutiny with their work.
spanish_meaning: El escrutinio es revisión cuidadosa.
spanish_example: Un científico debe practicar escrutinio en su trabajo.

english_meaning: To segregate something means to place it in a group apart from other things.
english_example: In gym class, the children were segregated into two groups: boys and girls.
spanish_meaning: Segregar significa separar en grupos distintos.
spanish_example: En clase separaron a niños y niñas.

english_meaning: To subject someone to something means to force them to do or experience it.
english_example: The officers subjected everyone to a careful search before they left the plane.
spanish_meaning: Someter es forzar a alguien a sufrir algo.
spanish_example: Los oficiales sometieron a todos a revisión.

english_meaning: To testify means to give evidence as a witness.
english_example: The judge listened while the victim testified about the robbery.
spanish_meaning: Testificar es declarar como testigo.
spanish_example: La víctima testificó sobre el robo.

english_meaning: A tumult is a loud and confused noise made by a large crowd of people.
english_example: She couldn’t hear her friend over the tumult of the other excited guests.
spanish_meaning: Un tumulto es ruido y confusión de mucha gente.
spanish_example: No oí a mi amigo por el tumulto de invitados.

english_meaning: To underestimate something or someone means to think less of them than they really are.
english_example: We lost the game because we underestimated the other team’s skill.
spanish_meaning: Subestimar es pensar menos de lo que vale.
spanish_example: Perdimos porque subestimamos su nivel.

english_meaning: Uproar is loud noise caused by people who are very angry or upset.
english_example: The fans made a great uproar when their team lost the game.
spanish_meaning: Un alboroto es un ruido fuerte colectivo.
spanish_example: Hubo alboroto cuando su equipo perdió.

english_meaning: To accelerate means to increase in speed.
english_example: When he stepped on the gas pedal, the motorcycle accelerated.
spanish_meaning: Acelerar es aumentar la velocidad.
spanish_example: Aceleró al pisar el acelerador de la moto.

english_meaning: If you do something anew, you do it again, possibly in a different way.
english_example: Although he had failed his driving test, he decided to try it anew.
spanish_meaning: De nuevo significa otra vez.
spanish_example: Falló el examen de conducir, pero lo intentará de nuevo.

english_meaning: A defect is a part of something that is wrong or missing.
english_example: All these bottles have a defect and must be sent back to the warehouse.
spanish_meaning: Un defecto es una falla o imperfección.
spanish_example: Todas las botellas tenían un defecto.

english_meaning: If something is dreary, then it is dull, dark, and lifeless.
english_example: After the fire, this section of forest is rather dreary.
spanish_meaning: Desolador describe algo triste y triste.
spanish_example: La zona del bosque era desoladora.

english_meaning: To duplicate something means to copy it.
english_example: She duplicated her friend’s movements like she was in front of a mirror.
spanish_meaning: Duplicar es copiar.
spanish_example: Ella duplicó los movimientos de su amiga.

english_meaning: If something is electromagnetic, it is related to electricity and magnetic fields.
english_example: Different colors of light come from different levels of electromagnetic energy.
spanish_meaning: Electromagnético se relaciona con electricidad y magnetismo.
spanish_example: Es energía electromagnética la que produce la luz.

english_meaning: An electron is a particle in all atoms that has a negative electric charge.
english_example: The number of electrons in an atom determines its charge.
spanish_meaning: El electrón es una partícula cargada negativamente.
spanish_example: La cantidad de electrones determina la carga.

english_meaning: To glide means to fly on extended wings with little or no effort.
english_example: When the wind is blowing, birds can glide easily through the sky.
spanish_meaning: Deslizar es moverse suavemente.
spanish_example: Con viento los pájaros pueden deslizar por el cielo.

english_meaning: If someone is ingenious, then they are very smart.
english_example: Charles was the only person ingenious enough to repair the plane’s engines.
spanish_meaning: Ingenioso significa muy inteligente.
spanish_example: Charles fue el único ingenioso para reparar los motores.

english_meaning: An innovation is a product, or an idea, that is new or very original.
english_example: Mrs. Johnson made a great innovation to the company’s business plan.
spanish_meaning: Una innovación es algo nuevo y original.
spanish_example: Ella creó una innovación en el plan de negocio.

english_meaning: If something or someone is innovative, they can think in creative ways.
english_example: Since Peter was so innovative, he was chosen to lead the science team.
spanish_meaning: Innovador es muy creativo.
spanish_example: Peter fue elegido líder por ser innovador.

english_meaning: To launch something means to make it go into motion.
english_example: The boat launched from the dock and foated down the river.
spanish_meaning: Lanzar es poner en movimiento.
spanish_example: El barco fue lanzado desde el muelle.

english_meaning: If something is meteorological, it is concerned with the science of weather.
english_example: The thunderstorm was so large that it became a great meteorological event.
spanish_meaning: Meteorológico se refiere al tiempo y el clima.
spanish_example: La tormenta fue un gran evento meteorológico.

english_meaning: Meteorology is the science that studies the weather.
english_example: In order to understand the weather better, you should study meteorology.
spanish_meaning: La meteorología estudia el clima.
spanish_example: Para entender el clima estudias meteorología.

english_meaning: To penetrate something means to enter into it.
english_example: The knife easily penetrated the surface of the orange.
spanish_meaning: Penetrar es entrar en algo.
spanish_example: El cuchillo penetró la naranja con facilidad.

english_meaning: A persistent person does not give up and keeps on working.
english_example: They made persistent attempts to get him to eat.
spanish_meaning: Persistente es no rendirse.
spanish_example: Hicieron intentos persistentes para que comiera.

english_meaning: Propulsion is the force that moves something forward.
english_example: The propulsion lifted the rocket into the sky.
spanish_meaning: La propulsión es fuerza que empuja hacia adelante.
spanish_example: La propulsión levantó el cohete al cielo.

english_meaning: To simulate something means to copy its actions or characteristics.
english_example: The French language teacher could simulate the accent of a French citizen.
spanish_meaning: Simular es copiar una acción o manera.
spanish_example: El profesor simuló el acento francés.

english_meaning: To spur someone means to urge them into action.
english_example: The coach’s speech spurred her team into playing the best game of their lives.
spanish_meaning: Incitar es impulsarlo a actuar.
spanish_example: El discurso del entrenador incitó al equipo.

english_meaning: To stimulate something means to cause or to increase activity in it.
english_example: Doctors sometimes use electric shock to stimulate a patient’s heartbeat.
spanish_meaning: Estimular es aumentar actividad.
spanish_example: La descarga eléctrica puede estimular el corazón.

english_meaning: If something is done beforehand, then it is done in advance.
english_example: He packed his luggage beforehand, so he was able to leave right away.
spanish_meaning: De antemano significa con anticipación.
spanish_example: Empacó su equipaje de antemano.

english_meaning: Something blurred is not seen clearly.
english_example: There was blurred photographs of what was supposed to be a flying saucer.
spanish_meaning: Borroso es lo que no se ve claro.
spanish_example: Las fotos estaban borrosas.

english_meaning: If a temperature is given in centigrade, it is the same as the temperature in Celsius.
english_example: During the spring time, the temperature gets as warm as 26 degrees centigrade.
spanish_meaning: Centígrado es la escala de temperatura igual a Celsius.
spanish_example: La temperatura subió a 26 grados centígrados.

english_meaning: To chatter means to talk quickly about unimportant things.
english_example: The children chattered in the back of the classroom.
spanish_meaning: Parlotear es hablar rápido de cosas pequeñas.
spanish_example: Los niños chateaban en voz alta.

english_meaning: A concerto is music played with an orchestra but features a solo instrument.
english_example: The piano concerto has a section in which only the piano plays.
spanish_meaning: El concierto es música con orquesta y solista.
spanish_example: El concierto de piano tiene un solo de piano.

english_meaning: To condense a gas means to make it a liquid.
english_example: The cool air made tiny drops of water condense on the tops of the grass.
spanish_meaning: Condensar es hacer líquido un gas.
spanish_example: El aire frío hizo que se condensara agua en la hierba.

english_meaning: To deteriorate means to become steadily worse.
english_example: The nation’s economy continued to deteriorate despite the politicians’ efforts.
spanish_meaning: Deteriorarse es empeorar con el tiempo.
spanish_example: La economía seguía deteriorándose.

english_meaning: Degree is a unit for measuring temperature.
english_example: The thermometer recorded a temperature of 38 degrees Celsius.
spanish_meaning: Grado es la unidad de medida de temperatura.
spanish_example: El termómetro marcó 38 grados Celsius.

english_meaning: An exterior is the outside surface of something.
english_example: The exterior of the nut was hard and woody, but the inside was soft and delicious.
spanish_meaning: El exterior es la superficie de fuera.
spanish_example: El exterior de la nuez era duro.

english_meaning: If someone or something is hearty, then they are loud and happy.
english_example: The grandmother ended her story with a hearty laugh that pleased her grandchild.
spanish_meaning: Entusiasta es alegre y enérgico.
spanish_example: La abuela terminó la historia con una carcajada entusiasta.

english_meaning: If someone is hospitable, they are friendly to strangers.
english_example: At dinner, my dad was very hospitable to my friends.
spanish_meaning: Hospitalario significa amable con extraños.
spanish_example: Mi padre fue muy hospitalario con mis amigos.

english_meaning: Humor can be something that makes you laugh, and it can also refer to your feelings.
english_example: He woke up in an ill humor, angry at everyone.
spanish_meaning: El humor puede hacerte reír o indicar el estado de ánimo.
spanish_example: Despertó de mal humor y estaba irritable.

english_meaning: A manor is a large house with many rooms.
english_example: The manor had over forty rooms and beautiful gardens.
spanish_meaning: La mansión es una casa grande con muchas habitaciones.
spanish_example: La mansión tenía más de cuarenta habitaciones.

english_meaning: A monastery is a building in which monks live.
english_example: The monks at this monastery are famous for their bread and music.
spanish_meaning: Un monasterio es la casa de monjes.
spanish_example: Los monjes de este monasterio son famosos por su pan.

english_meaning: A nursery is a room where babies and children sleep, play, or are cared for.
english_example: The children kissed their parents goodnight and went upstairs to the nursery.
spanish_meaning: La guardería es un cuarto o centro para niños y bebés.
spanish_example: Los niños subieron a la guardería en silencio.

english_meaning: If something is outstretched, then it is extended to its full length.
english_example: The cat stood on its back legs with its front legs outstretched.
spanish_meaning: Extendido es alargado al máximo.
spanish_example: El gato se paró con las patas delanteras extendidas.

english_meaning: A parcel is a package of things to be carried or mailed somewhere.
english_example: The parcel looked like it had been stepped on by someone.
spanish_meaning: El paquete es un bulto para enviar.
spanish_example: El paquete parecía pisado al llegar.

english_meaning: A profile is an outline of a face, usually as seen from the side.
english_example: The drawing of Olivia’s profile came out very well.
spanish_meaning: El perfil es la silueta de un rostro.
spanish_example: El dibujo del perfil de Olivia quedó bien.

english_meaning: If something is winding, then it follows a twisting course.
english_example: The winding river turned and looped around the hills.
spanish_meaning: Sinuoso describe un camino con muchas curvas.
spanish_example: El río sinuoso dio vueltas entre las colinas.

english_meaning: To zip something means to close it with a zipper.
english_example: She zipped her backpack closed after putting her books inside it.
spanish_meaning: Cerrar con cremallera usa el cierre de una prenda.
spanish_example: Ella cerró la cremallera de la mochila.

english_meaning: To abolish something means to put an end to it, such as a system or law.
english_example: President Lincoln abolished slavery in the US.
spanish_meaning: Abolir es poner fin a una práctica.
spanish_example: Lincoln abolió la esclavitud en Estados Unidos.

english_meaning: To amend something means to change it to improve or make it accurate.
english_example: The countries were in agreement that the treaty needed to be amended.
spanish_meaning: Enmendar es corregir o mejorar.
spanish_example: El tratado debía enmendarse.

english_meaning: To aspire means to have a strong desire to achieve or do something.
english_example: George aspired to be a doctor from a young age.
spanish_meaning: Aspirar es desear con fuerza.
spanish_example: George aspiraba a ser médico desde niño.

english_meaning: To censor information means to remove it if it is dangerous, rude, or rebellious.
english_example: To protect innocent people, the location of the bomb was censored.
spanish_meaning: Censurar es quitar contenido peligroso o ofensivo.
spanish_example: La ubicación de la bomba fue censurada.

english_meaning: A charter is a document that describes the rights of an organization or group.
english_example: The company charter explained that all employees had to pay a tax.
spanish_meaning: La carta constitutiva describe derechos de una organización.
spanish_example: El estatuto decía que todos pagaban impuesto.

english_meaning: A constitution is a document of principles for a government.
english_example: The country’s constitution said a prime minister could only serve three terms.
spanish_meaning: La constitución es la norma suprema de un país.
spanish_example: La constitución limita a un máximo de tres mandatos.

english_meaning: When a place is cosmopolitan, it is full of people from many different places.
english_example: There are dozens of different types of restaurants in a cosmopolitan city.
spanish_meaning: Cosmopolita es una ciudad con muchas culturas.
spanish_example: Hay muchos restaurantes en una ciudad cosmopolita.

english_meaning: To disseminate information or knowledge means to distribute it.
english_example: The organization disseminates information about the dangers of smoking.
spanish_meaning: Diseminar significa difundir.
spanish_example: La organización difunde información sobre el tabaquismo.

english_meaning: To fatter people means to praise them in an effort to please them.
english_example: He was just flattering me when he said that my new dress looked gorgeous.
spanish_meaning: Halagar es decir cumplidos para agradar.
spanish_example: Solo me halagaba al decir que mi vestido era hermoso.
notes: El texto fuente muestra “fatter”; aquí se traduce a “halagar”.

english_meaning: When someone is infamous, they are well known for something bad.
english_example: That news channel is infamous for presenting biased information.
spanish_meaning: Infame es conocido por algo malo.
spanish_example: Ese canal es infame por información sesgada.

english_meaning: If one is lame, they cannot walk properly due to an injury to the leg or foot.
english_example: The terrible accident left many people dead and several others lame.
spanish_meaning: Cojo significa caminar con dificultad.
spanish_example: Tras el accidente, los heridos quedaron cojos.

english_meaning: To limp means to walk with difficulty because someone’s leg or foot is hurt.
english_example: After the injury, the player limped off of the field.
spanish_meaning: Cojear es andar con dolor en pierna o pie.
spanish_example: El jugador cojeara al dejar la cancha.

english_meaning: An outburst is a sudden, strong expression of an emotion.
english_example: There was an outburst of cheers when the comedian took the stage.
spanish_meaning: Un estallido es una reacción súbita y fuerte.
spanish_example: Hubo un estallido de aplausos al subir el cómico.

english_meaning: When a behavior is pathological, it is extreme, unacceptable, and sometimes a symptom of disease.
english_example: The pathological liar could not even tell the truth about unimportant matters.
spanish_meaning: Patológico describe algo extremo o enfermizo.
spanish_example: El mentiroso patológico no puede decir la verdad.

english_meaning: When something is phenomenal, it is unusually great.
english_example: The child’s ability to play the piano is nothing short of phenomenal.
spanish_meaning: Fenomenal describe algo extraordinario.
spanish_example: Su talento para el piano era fenomenal.

english_meaning: A poll is a survey in which people give their opinions about important things.
english_example: The poll showed that many people support the plan to stop gang violence.
spanish_meaning: La encuesta recoge opiniones públicas.
spanish_example: La encuesta mostró apoyo al plan antiviolencia.

english_meaning: Remorse is a strong feeling of sadness and regret.
english_example: When I realized what I had done, I felt remorse for my actions.
spanish_meaning: El remordimiento es arrepentimiento intenso.
spanish_example: Sentí remordimiento por lo que hice.

english_meaning: Secrecy is the behavior of keeping things secret.
english_example: The secrecy of the big organization made the government nervous.
spanish_meaning: El secreto significa ocultar información.
spanish_example: El secreto de la organización generó nervios.

english_meaning: To tackle something means to deal with it in a determined and efficient way.
english_example: Such social problems need to be tackled right away.
spanish_meaning: Abordar es enfrentar un problema.
spanish_example: Esas cuestiones sociales deben abordarse enseguida.

english_meaning: A trance is a state where people seem asleep and have no control of themselves.
english_example: The woman’s powerful eyes often put men in a trance.
spanish_meaning: El trance es un estado de desconexión.
spanish_example: La mujer llevaba a los hombres a un trance con su mirada.

english_meaning: A coward is a person who lacks courage to do risky or dangerous things.
english_example: A firefighter cannot be a coward. They have to be able to act quickly.
spanish_meaning: Cobarde se refiere a quien tiene miedo de arriesgarse.
spanish_example: Un bombero no puede ser un cobarde.

english_meaning: To delete something means to remove or erase written material.
english_example: Several lines had been deleted from her speech.
spanish_meaning: Eliminar es borrar algo escrito.
spanish_example: Varias líneas habían sido eliminadas del discurso.

english_meaning: If someone is earnest, then they are honest.
english_example: The child was very earnest when she told her mother how she broke the dish.
spanish_meaning: Sincero significa honesto y auténtico.
spanish_example: La niña fue sincera al contar lo que rompió.

english_meaning: If something is ethnic, then it is related to a group with a similar culture.
english_example: Many sections of the city are home to different ethnic communities.
spanish_meaning: Étnico se refiere a un grupo cultural.
spanish_example: La ciudad tiene comunidades étnicas.

english_meaning: To exclude someone means to not accept them into a group.
english_example: Carol was excluded from the contest because her friend was a judge.
spanish_meaning: Excluir es no aceptar en un grupo.
spanish_example: Carol fue excluida del concurso.

english_meaning: If something is firsthand, then it is from an original source.
english_example: If you want firsthand knowledge, ask someone who saw it.
spanish_meaning: De primera mano es de una fuente directa.
spanish_example: Para saber de primera mano, pregunta a quien estuvo allí.

english_meaning: If someone is fluent in a language, then they are able to speak it very well.
english_example: She was so fluent in German that you’d have thought she was from Germany.
spanish_meaning: Fluido significa hablar con soltura.
spanish_example: Ella es fluida en alemán.

english_meaning: If something is imperial, then it is related to an empire.
english_example: These old imperial coins were once used in the Roman Empire.
spanish_meaning: Imperial se relaciona con un imperio.
spanish_example: Estas monedas imperiales venían del Imperio romano.

english_meaning: If something is inclusive, then it is open to all groups and people in society.
english_example: A more inclusive event would have allowed children to attend.
spanish_meaning: Inclusivo significa abierto a todos.
spanish_example: Un evento más inclusivo permitiría asistir a niños.

english_meaning: A legislature is the section of a government that makes laws.
english_example: The senator had served ten years in the national legislature.
spanish_meaning: La legislatura es la sección del gobierno que hace leyes.
spanish_example: El senador había servido diez años en la legislatura nacional.

english_meaning: If something is linguistic, then it is concerned with language.
english_example: A linguistic way of studying culture focuses on words within that culture.
spanish_meaning: Lo lingüístico está relacionado con el lenguaje.
spanish_example: Un enfoque lingüístico para estudiar una cultura se centra en las palabras de esa cultura.

english_meaning: If someone is monolingual, then they speak only one language.
english_example: In today’s global economy, being monolingual limits your opportunities.
spanish_meaning: Una persona monolingüe solo habla un idioma.
spanish_example: En la economía global actual, ser monolingüe limita tus oportunidades.

english_meaning: Nationality is an identity based on the nation from which you come.
english_example: His nationality is German, but he speaks French, Spanish, and Korean.
spanish_meaning: La nacionalidad es una identidad basada en la nación de la que vienes.
spanish_example: Su nacionalidad es alemana, pero habla francés, español y coreano.

english_meaning: A patriot is someone who loves, supports, and defends their country.
english_example: Every year, young patriots join their countries’ militaries.
spanish_meaning: Un patriota es alguien que ama, apoya y defiende su país.
spanish_example: Cada año, jóvenes patriotas se unen a los ejércitos de sus países.

english_meaning: To prosecute someone means to take legal action against them.
english_example: They were prosecuted for fishing in the river without a permit.
spanish_meaning: Procesar a alguien significa emprender acción legal contra él o ella.
spanish_example: A esa persona la procesaron por pescar en el río sin permiso.

english_meaning: If something is racial, then it is related to a race or races.
english_example: The differences between racial groups are physical characteristics and culture.
spanish_meaning: Lo racial está relacionado con una raza o razas.
spanish_example: Las diferencias entre los grupos raciales son de rasgos físicos y cultura.

english_meaning: If something is solemn, then it is serious and honest.
english_example: The professor preferred a solemn relationship between him and his students.
spanish_meaning: Algo solemne es serio y serio con honestidad.
spanish_example: El profesor prefirió una relación solemne con sus alumnos.

english_meaning: Solidarity is a union formed from common responsibilities or interests.
english_example: All the citizens came together in a show of solidarity to create change.
spanish_meaning: La solidaridad es una unión formada por responsabilidades o intereses comunes.
spanish_example: Todos los ciudadanos se unieron en solidaridad para impulsar el cambio.

english_meaning: Tact is the ability to avoid offending people when dealing with problems.
english_example: Since both sides would not agree, it required someone with tact to make peace.
spanish_meaning: El tacto es la habilidad de evitar ofender a las personas al tratar problemas.
spanish_example: Como ambos bandos no se ponían de acuerdo, hizo falta alguien con tacto para lograr la paz.

english_meaning: To undermine someone means to betray them or weaken their efforts or authority.
english_example: The documents helped to undermine the workers’ trust in their bosses’ honesty.
spanish_meaning: Socavar significa traicionar o debilitar sus esfuerzos o autoridad.
spanish_example: Los documentos ayudaron a socavar la confianza de los trabajadores en la honestidad de sus jefes.

english_meaning: To constrain something means to limit its development.
english_example: Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.
spanish_meaning: Constrain significa limitar su desarrollo.
spanish_example: Jim no puede unirse a nosotros porque está limitado por planes previos.

english_meaning: A depot is a bus or train station.
english_example: He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot.
spanish_meaning: La terminal (estación) es una terminal de autobuses o trenes.
spanish_example: Él esperó en la terminal a que llegara su madre.

english_meaning: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired.
english_example: As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.
spanish_meaning: Emular a alguien significa imitarlo porque se le admira mucho.
spanish_example: Cuando era niño, siempre intentó emular a su hermano mayor.

english_meaning: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger.
english_example: He shouted, “There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger.
spanish_meaning: El dedo índice es el dedo entre el pulgar y el dedo medio.
spanish_example: Gritó “¡Ahí está!” y señaló con el dedo índice.

english_meaning: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal.
english_example: The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts.
spanish_meaning: Las vísceras son todos los órganos internos de una persona o un animal.
spanish_example: El médico puede explicar todos los procesos que ocurren en las vísceras.

english_meaning: When something is inherent, it is a natural part of something else.
english_example: Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.
spanish_meaning: Algo inherente es una parte natural de otra cosa.
spanish_example: El sudor es una función corporal inherente al hacer ejercicio.

english_meaning: To intimidate means to frighten others.
english_example: My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.
spanish_meaning: Intimidar significa asustar a otros.
spanish_example: Mi papá intimida a mis amigos cuando vienen de visita.

english_meaning: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building.
english_example: The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.
spanish_meaning: Un conserje repara y cuida un edificio.
spanish_example: El conserje de la escuela limpió la cafetería desordenada.

english_meaning: When something is moist, it is slightly wet.
english_example: The ground is still moist from the rain last night.
spanish_meaning: Algo húmedo está ligeramente mojado.
spanish_example: El suelo sigue húmedo por la lluvia de anoche.

english_meaning: Nope is an informal way of saying “no.”
english_example: He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “Nope.”
spanish_meaning: “Nope” es una forma informal de decir “no”.
spanish_example: Me preguntó si tenía dinero y tuve que decir “No”.

english_meaning: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object.
english_example: The bully prodded me in the chest with her finger.
spanish_meaning: Pinchar o empujar a alguien con el dedo o un objeto puntiagudo.
spanish_example: La acosadora me pinchó en el pecho con el dedo.

english_meaning: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free.
english_example: He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as a ransom.
spanish_meaning: El rescate es una suma de dinero pagada al secuestrador para liberar a una persona.
spanish_example: Secuestró al príncipe y exigió un millón de dólares como rescate.

english_meaning: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them.
english_example: Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.
spanish_meaning: Contener a alguien o algo significa usar fuerza física para detenerlo.
spanish_example: Mike contuvo a Allen para que no llegara a la puerta.

english_meaning: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion.
english_example: The baby could not keep the saliva from dripping out of its mouth.
spanish_meaning: La saliva es el líquido acuoso de la boca que ayuda a la digestión.
spanish_example: El bebé no pudo evitar que la saliva se le saliera de la boca.

english_meaning: To spit means to force liquid from one’s mouth.
english_example: He emerged from the pool and spit water from his mouth.
spanish_meaning: Escupir significa expulsar líquido de la boca.
spanish_example: Salió de la piscina y escupió agua de la boca.

english_meaning: To sprint means to run very fast over a short distance.
english_example: The kids didn’t want to be late to class, so they sprinted to the bus stop.
spanish_meaning: Esprintar significa correr muy rápido durante una distancia corta.
spanish_example: Los niños esprintaron a la parada del autobús para no llegar tarde.

english_meaning: A stunt is something that is done in order to get attention or publicity.
english_example: The man jumped over the cars as a promotional stunt.
spanish_meaning: Un stunt o truco es algo que se hace para llamar la atención o ganar publicidad.
spanish_example: El hombre saltó sobre los autos como truco promocional.

english_meaning: When people are tolerant, they are respectful of others’ rights and beliefs.
english_example: The tolerant leader thought that everyone was equal regardless of race.
spanish_meaning: Una persona tolerante respeta los derechos y creencias de los demás.
spanish_example: El líder tolerante pensaba que todos eran iguales sin importar la raza.

english_meaning: A vampire is a fictional monster that sleeps in a coffin and sucks people’s blood.
english_example: The vampire snuck up on the woman and bit her neck.
spanish_meaning: Un vampiro es un monstruo ficticio que duerme en un ataúd y chupa sangre.
spanish_example: El vampiro se acercó sigilosamente a la mujer y le mordió el cuello.

english_meaning: To yawn means to open one’s mouth wide and breathe in air.
english_example: The child yawned and stretched her arms before going to bed for the night.
spanish_meaning: Bostezar significa abrir mucho la boca e inhalar aire.
spanish_example: El niño bostezó y se estiró los brazos antes de ir a dormir.

english_meaning: If something is adjoining, it is next to or joined with a building or room.
english_example: I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.
spanish_meaning: Adyacente significa que está al lado o unido a un edificio o habitación.
spanish_example: No pude dormir porque la gente de la habitación adyacente hacía mucho ruido.

english_meaning: To allege something is to say that it is true without offering proof.
english_example: The little girl had alleged that her older brother hid her favorite doll.
spanish_meaning: Alegar algo es decir que es verdad sin presentar pruebas.
spanish_example: La niña alegó que su hermano mayor había escondido su muñeca favorita.

english_meaning: An arch is a curved opening formed under a structure such as a bridge or doorway.
english_example: The arch of the bridge was not high enough for the tall boat to pass underneath.
spanish_meaning: Un arco es una apertura curva bajo una estructura como un puente o puerta.
spanish_example: El arco del puente no era lo suficientemente alto para que pasara el bote.

english_meaning: To assemble means to get together in one place.
english_example: The parents assembled to discuss ways to improve their children’s education.
spanish_meaning: Reunirse significa juntarse en un mismo lugar.
spanish_example: Los padres se reunieron para discutir cómo mejorar la educación de sus hijos.

english_meaning: A casualty is a person killed or injured in a war or an accident.
english_example: The only casualty in the car accident was a woman who broke her arm.
spanish_meaning: Una víctima es una persona muerta o herida en una guerra o accidente.
spanish_example: La única víctima del accidente de auto fue una mujer que se rompió el brazo.

english_meaning: To erect something means to build it.
english_example: The king erected two towers on the north and south sides of his castle.
spanish_meaning: Erigir algo significa construirlo.
spanish_example: El rey erigió dos torres al norte y al sur de su castillo.

english_meaning: If something is foul, then it is very unpleasant.
english_example: He wouldn’t let his dog drink from the water because it had a foul smell.
spanish_meaning: Algo fétido es algo muy desagradable.
spanish_example: No dejó que su perro bebiera agua porque olía a podrido.

english_meaning: A hectare is a unit of measure equal to 10,000 square meters.
english_example: His family farm covered many hectares.
spanish_meaning: La hectárea es una medida de 10,000 metros cuadrados.
spanish_example: La granja de su familia cubría muchas hectáreas.

english_meaning: To heighten an emotion means to increase the intensity of it.
english_example: The pleasant music heightened their enjoyment of the wonderful dinner.
spanish_meaning: Intensificar una emoción significa aumentar su intensidad.
spanish_example: La música agradable intensificó su disfrute de la cena.

english_meaning: Hospitality is friendly behavior and entertainment, shown to guests or strangers.
english_example: The travelers were amazed at the hospitality given them by the hotel’s staff.
spanish_meaning: La hospitalidad es un comportamiento amable y acogedor con invitados o desconocidos.
spanish_example: Los viajeros se sorprendieron por la hospitalidad del personal del hotel.

english_meaning: A mansion is a large and expensive home.
english_example: The mansion had thirty bedrooms, two kitchens, and a pool.
spanish_meaning: Una mansión es una casa grande y lujosa.
spanish_example: La mansión tenía treinta dormitorios, dos cocinas y una piscina.

english_meaning: To outnumber a group means to have a greater number than it.
english_example: The girls outnumbered the boys at the school by four to one.
spanish_meaning: Superar en número significa tener más integrantes que otro grupo.
spanish_example: Las niñas superaban en número a los niños de la escuela por cuatro a uno.

english_meaning: If someone is overjoyed, then they are extremely happy.
english_example: He was overjoyed by the news of his promotion.
spanish_meaning: Estar eufórico significa estar extremadamente feliz.
spanish_example: Él estaba eufórico con la noticia de su ascenso.

english_meaning: A pasture is an area of land covered with grass for animals to use as food.
english_example: The sheep were taken to a pasture where there was more grass.
spanish_meaning: Un pastizal es un terreno con pasto para alimentar animales.
spanish_example: Las ovejas fueron llevadas a un pastizal con más pasto.

english_meaning: A petition is a written request asking an authority to do something.
english_example: The citizens all signed a petition asking the mayor to repair the sidewalks.
spanish_meaning: Una petición es una solicitud escrita dirigida a una autoridad.
spanish_example: Todos los ciudadanos firmaron una petición pidiendo al alcalde que reparara las aceras.

english_meaning: To renovate a building means to repair it, or to build new structures on it.
english_example: The old fire station was renovated into an apartment building.
spanish_meaning: Renovar un edificio significa repararlo o construir nuevas estructuras en él.
spanish_example: La antigua estación de bomberos fue renovada para convertirse en un edificio de apartamentos.

english_meaning: To revise something means to change it, or update it to make it better.
english_example: When the editor discovered certain facts were wrong, he revised the book.
spanish_meaning: Revisar algo significa cambiarlo o actualizarlo para hacerlo mejor.
spanish_example: Cuando el editor descubrió que algunos datos eran falsos, revisó el libro.

english_meaning: A slab is a large, thick, flat piece of stone, concrete, metal, or wood.
english_example: I looked at various slabs of stone to decorate my house.
spanish_meaning: Una losa es una pieza grande, gruesa y plana de piedra, hormigón, metal o madera.
spanish_example: Miré varias losas de piedra para decorar mi casa.

english_meaning: A terrace is an open area that is connected to a house or an apartment.
english_example: In the afternoons, she liked to sit on the terrace and check her email.
spanish_meaning: La terraza es un área abierta conectada a una casa o un apartamento.
spanish_example: Por la tarde, le gustaba sentarse en la terraza y revisar su correo.

english_meaning: Turf is a section of grass and the dirt in which it grows.
english_example: After the game, the turf looked ragged.
spanish_meaning: El césped (turf) es una zona de hierba con su tierra de crecimiento.
spanish_example: Después del partido, el césped estaba hecho jirones.

english_meaning: If something is analogous to another thing, then it is like it in certain ways.
english_example: The relationship with his teacher was analogous to that of a son and mother.
spanish_meaning: Algo análogo es parecido en ciertos aspectos.
spanish_example: La relación con su profesor era análoga a la de un hijo y una madre.

english_meaning: Binoculars are a device used for seeing things that are far away.
english_example: He could see the ship on the horizon only if he used his binoculars.
spanish_meaning: Los binoculares son un dispositivo para ver cosas lejanas.
spanish_example: Solo pudo ver el barco en el horizonte cuando usó sus binoculares.

english_meaning: The bulk of something is its size.
english_example: The large elephant moved its bulk with legs as strong as tree trunks.
spanish_meaning: La masa es el tamaño de algo.
spanish_example: El elefante grande movía su enorme masa con patas tan fuertes como troncos de árbol.

english_meaning: If something comprises something else, it consists of or is made up of it.
english_example: Our school’s football team is mostly comprised with seniors.
spanish_meaning: Si algo consta de algo más, significa que se compone o está formado por ello.
spanish_example: Nuestro equipo de fútbol escolar está compuesto en su mayoría por alumnos de último curso.

english_meaning: To depict something means to show or portray it, often using art.
english_example: The statue’s face depicted the general’s determination and courage.
spanish_meaning: Representar algo significa mostrarlo o retratarlo, a menudo con arte.
spanish_example: La cara de la estatua retrataba la determinación y el coraje del general.

english_meaning: If something is dual, then it is made up of two parts.
english_example: The room had a dual function. It was a living room, but at night it was a bedroom.
spanish_meaning: Dual significa que está formado por dos partes.
spanish_example: La habitación tenía una función dual: era sala de estar y de noche dormitorio.

english_meaning: Fahrenheit is a temperature scale where water freezes at 32° and boils at 212°.
english_example: When the temperature dropped to 32° Fahrenheit, it started snowing.
spanish_meaning: Fahrenheit es una escala de temperatura donde el agua congela a 32° y hierve a 212°.
spanish_example: Cuando la temperatura bajó a 32 grados Fahrenheit, empezó a nevar.

english_meaning: To fulfill something means to achieve or finish it.
english_example: The professor did not fulfill his promise not to miss a single class.
spanish_meaning: Cumplir algo significa lograrlo o terminarlo.
spanish_example: El profesor no cumplió su promesa de no faltar a ninguna clase.

english_meaning: A grove is a small group of trees.
english_example: All the trees in this grove are apple trees.
spanish_meaning: Una arboleda es un pequeño grupo de árboles.
spanish_example: Todos los árboles de está arboleda son manzanos.

english_meaning: Ore is the raw form of rock or material from which a valuable metal is taken.
english_example: The factory melted the ore and used it to make iron products.
spanish_meaning: La mena es la materia prima de roca o material del que se extrae un metal valioso.
spanish_example: La fábrica fundió la mena y la usó para hacer productos de hierro.

english_meaning: The outback is the wild inland region of Australia where very few people live.
english_example: Many Australian farmers use the outback to raise cattle.
spanish_meaning: El outback es la región interior de Australia donde vive muy poca gente.
spanish_example: Muchos granjeros australianos usan el outback para criar ganado.

english_meaning: To outweigh something means to exceed it in value, amount, or importance.
english_example: Finding a warm place to sleep outweighed the need to find something to eat.
spanish_meaning: Prevalecer significa ser más importante o tener más peso que otra necesidad.
spanish_example: Encontrar un lugar cálido para dormir fue más importante que encontrar algo para comer.

english_meaning: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical.
english_example: The paradox of her work was that the less she worked, the more she got done.
spanish_meaning: Una paradoja es una afirmación o hecho verdadero que parece ilógico.
spanish_example: La paradoja de su trabajo era que cuanto menos trabajaba, más terminaba.

english_meaning: A pier is a structure that extends into a body of water.
english_example: If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier.
spanish_meaning: Un muelle es una estructura que se extiende hacia una masa de agua.
spanish_example: Si quieres pescar peces más grandes, ve al final del muelle.

english_meaning: A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
english_example: We got to the house early because we took a shortcut through the forest.
spanish_meaning: Un atajo es una ruta más corta que la ruta principal.
spanish_example: Llegamos temprano a casa porque tomamos un atajo por el bosque.

english_meaning: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
english_example: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.
spanish_meaning: Un arancel es un impuesto o tarifa sobre ciertas importaciones o exportaciones.
spanish_example: Nuestro arancel sobre la lana importada encarece la lana extranjera.

english_meaning: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.
english_example: The thermometer outside the window indicated that it was a hot day today.
spanish_meaning: El termómetro es un aparato que mide la temperatura.
spanish_example: El termómetro de la ventana indicaba que hoy hacía un día muy caluroso.

english_meaning: To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.
english_example: She tilted her glass and almost spilled some of the wine inside.
spanish_meaning: Inclinar algo significa ponerlo en una posición inclinada.
spanish_example: Ella inclinó su vaso y casi derramó algo de vino.

english_meaning: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
english_example: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa.
spanish_meaning: “Viceversa” significa invertir el orden de las dos ideas principales.
spanish_example: Los estudiantes aprenden de sus profesores, y viceversa.

english_meaning: The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
english_example: The police looked for the lost dog, but its whereabouts were still unknown.
spanish_meaning: El paradero de alguien o algo es el lugar donde está.
spanish_example: La policía buscó al perro perdido, pero su paradero seguía siendo desconocido.
